# C4 Autonomous Queue v10 — Web hook test expansion

24h autonomous run that adds vitest tests to web/src/lib/use-* hooks not yet covered. The Meetings (19/19), ui-primitives (6/6), and most Sessions/Specialists/Chat/Attach hooks are already done; this queue tackles the remaining domains (Wiki, Workflow, Autonomous, Auth, Audit, generic) and the leftover Specialists pair.

## Tasks

| # | Task | Status | Detail |
|---|------|--------|--------|
| 11.10 | Wiki hooks vitest | done | Add vitest tests for these 4 web hooks: web/src/lib/use-wiki-search.ts, web/src/lib/use-wiki-page.ts, web/src/lib/use-wiki-bulk-publish.ts, web/src/lib/use-wiki-reopen.ts. Branch c4/auto-wiki. Worker auto-w1. Pattern: see web/src/lib/use-meeting-create.test.ts and use-meetings-search.test.ts. For each hook cover idle initial state, every setter / callback exposed in return, validation paths that reject without fetch, happy-path REST verifying body shape + path + encodeURIComponent on any id, server-error path, busy slot via release-gate Promise pattern, cross-selection / open=false reset effects. Run npm --prefix /root/c4-worktree-auto-w1/web test (symlink web/node_modules to /root/c4/web/node_modules first if needed — gitignored) and confirm ALL passing. Then update /root/c4/CHANGELOG.md (## [1.11.25] entry above 1.11.24 with file + case count), bump /root/c4/package.json (1.11.24 to 1.11.25) and the two version lines in /root/c4/package-lock.json. Commit on this branch with a descriptive message. DO NOT push and DO NOT merge — manager handles. Touch only the new test files + CHANGELOG + package.json + package-lock.json — work in worktree, never edit /root/c4/web/* directly. |

## Operating rules

- One task at a time, throttleMs (10 minutes) between dispatches.
- Worker uses the assigned branch; commit must stay on that branch (no push from worker).
- Manager (c4-mgr-auto) verifies each commit, fast-forwards into main, pushes to origin via SSH.
- Circuit breaker: 3 consecutive failures stop the loop until manual resume (c4 autonomous resume).
- Escalation: any halt surfaces in /api/autonomous/escalations and on the Web UI Autonomous tab.

Section body padding `px-5 py-4` -> `px-4 py-4 sm:px-5`. min-height beats height in the browser, so the `h-7` / `h-8` overrides scattered across Config, Risk, Profiles, Templates, Validation pick up the 44px floor automatically without any per-page edits. | 11.70 | UI: Typography rhythm | done | Define type scale in web/src/lib/typography.ts (display, h1-h3, body, caption, mono). Add Tailwind plugin for 8px baseline grid in tailwind.config.js (line-height anchored to 8). Replace ad-hoc text-* classes across 14 pages with named scale classes. Branch c4/auto-ui-typography. Worker auto-w61 (queue named auto-w57 but already used). Bump 1.11.88. Shipped: web/src/lib/typography.ts exports 8 entries (display 36/48, h1 30/40, h2 24/32, h3 20/28, body 16/24, bodySm 14/20, caption 12/16 + text-muted-foreground baked in, mono font-mono text-sm leading-5). Line-heights snap to the 8 px baseline grid for display/h1/h2/body/caption; h3 lands on the 3.5*8 half-step (28 px) because the next full step would collide visually with h2, and bodySm/mono accept a 20 px off-grid value because a 24 px line-height on 14 px text crowds tables and inline code -- both trade-offs are documented in the file's JSDoc and locked by the test suite. web/tailwind.config.js gained a 10-line baselinePlugin (registered next to the existing animatePlugin) that puts --baseline-step (default 8 px) on :root and exposes a .baseline utility class for prose blocks where the named scale does not fit -- so adjacent dynamic-content surfaces can still snap to the same vertical grid via line-height: var(--baseline-step). 8 of 17 pages adopt the scale: Workspaces / Config / Risk section <h3>s bump from text-base font-semibold to cn(..., text.h3) so in-card section headings sit one step above body copy on a 28 px rhythm; Templates / Profiles list-item names adopt text.mono (font-mono text-sm leading-5) and their description hints adopt text.caption; Health version + configPath + modules-empty copy adopt text.caption (configPath keeps font-mono via cn('truncate', text.caption, 'font-mono') so the muted line still reads as code); Morning generatedAt timestamp adopts text.caption; Plan rendered-markdown wrapper adopts text.bodySm so the prose block carries the named 14/20 rhythm. Intentionally skipped (CHANGELOG): PageFrame.tsx CardTitle (shared page-title surface; sweep belongs in a follow-up that also calibrates the Card primitive), Auto.tsx SectionShell h2 + queue / worker / timeline meta spans (dense dashboard chrome where smaller-than-scale is intentional), Validation / Cleanup / Batch / Scribe panels using text-[10px] / text-[11px] / text-[12px] (dense tables + stat-card label rows are not body copy), and Badge / chip text-xs (pinned to the Badge primitive contract). 9 new vitest cases (typography.test.ts) cover string defensive check, text-* utility per entry, leading-* utility per entry, font-weight on h1 / h2 / h3 / display, caption muted-foreground, mono font-mono, the full scale-key set, and an inline snapshot that pins the object so a rename or value drift surfaces immediately. Verification: env -C /root/c4-worktree-auto-w61/web npx vite build succeeds (3.40s, scale + plugin land in existing chunks); env -C /root/c4-worktree-auto-w61/web npx vitest run reports 5203 / 5203 passing across 230 test files (5194 prior from v1.11.87 + 9 new typography cases). docs/web-design-palette.md gained a "Typography (1.11.88)" section documenting the scale, the plugin, the half-step / off-grid trade-offs, and the "how to add a new size" recipe so future PRs follow the same conventions. |
| 11.71 | CLI: c4 ui command | done | Add `c4 ui` command in src/cli.js. Resolve daemon port from config.json (default 3456). Open browser via shelling out to xdg-open / open / start by platform - print URL fallback. Support --port flag. Tests in src/cli.test.js. Branch c4/auto-cli-ui. Worker auto-w62 (queue named auto-w58 but already in use). Bump 1.11.89. Shipped: src/cli.js gained three pure helpers (resolveUiPort, pickUiOpener, runUi) plus a `case 'ui'` block in the main switch. Port resolution priority is --port flag (parsed via parseInt with positive-finite guard) > config.json daemon.port (loaded via the existing fs.readFileSync + JSON.parse pattern from the `doctor` case, with a try/catch so a malformed config.json falls through rather than crashing the command) > hard-coded 3456 default. Platform opener picks `open` on darwin, `cmd /c start ""` on win32 (the empty title arg is positional so cmd.exe does not consume the URL as the window title), and `xdg-open` on linux + every other platform — child is spawned with detached:true, stdio:'ignore', and unref()ed once we know the launch succeeded so the c4 process can exit immediately. runUi returns a Promise that resolves on the first of two settle paths: spawn throws synchronously (immediate fallback line) or setImmediate fires after spawn returns and no async ENOENT 'error' event arrived first; if an ENOENT/error event fires before setImmediate, the fallback path wins the race instead. Always prints a single URL line to stdout — either `Opening http://127.0.0.1:<port> in your default browser...` on success, or `Open in browser: http://127.0.0.1:<port> (no xdg-open / open / start available)` on fallback — and exits 0 in both branches. Usage block in cli.js now lists `ui [--port N]` between health and daemon-start; module.exports adds resolveUiPort, pickUiOpener, runUi alongside the existing withApiPrefix export so tests can exercise them without spawning the CLI. 21 new test cases in tests/cli-ui.test.js (node:test runner — the tests/ directory has zero vitest files; only web/src uses vitest, so the task brief's "vitest" wording does not apply here, see cli-version-flag.test.js + cli-risk.test.js for the same node:test pattern): 8 resolveUiPort cases (--port wins, config fallback, --port-beats-config priority, no-daemon.port defaults to 3456, missing config defaults to 3456, malformed JSON defaults to 3456, --port with no value ignored, non-numeric --port value ignored), 4 pickUiOpener cases (darwin / win32 with cmd /c start "" / linux / freebsd-falls-back), 6 runUi cases (darwin spawn-shape assertion, linux spawn-shape, win32 spawn-shape with the four-element args list, sync-throw fallback line, async ENOENT fallback line via process.nextTick on a fake EventEmitter child, unref-called-on-success), and 3 spawnSync integration cases (node src/cli.js ui --port 9999 prints URL + exits 0, default-port form prints a well-formed URL + exits 0, usage block lists `ui [--port N]`). Verification: `node --test tests/cli-ui.test.js` runs 21 / 21 passing in ~190ms; manual `node src/cli.js ui --port 9999` on this headless Linux container prints the expected fallback line because xdg-open is absent. CLAUDE.md gained a `c4 ui [--port N]` row in the CLI command list with the port priority order, platform opener map, and fallback behavior, sitting between `c4 health` and `c4 config`. CHANGELOG entry [1.11.89] mirrors the same shipped breakdown. web/package.json version bumped to 1.11.89 to stay aligned with the v1.11.77-v1.11.88 release-vehicle convention. |
| 11.72 | CLI: c4 attach | partial | Add `c4 attach <name>` command — pipes stdin/stdout to a running worker via the daemon WebSocket. Add WebSocket endpoint /api/workers/:name/attach in daemon. Detach via Ctrl+] (escape sequence). --readonly flag for view-only. Branch c4/auto-cli-attach. Worker auto-w63 (queue named auto-w59 but already used). Bump 1.11.90. Shipped (partial — test-fixture iteration deferred to a follow-up task): src/ws-attach.js (server-side RFC 6455 handshake + frame encoder + masked-frame decoder + parseAttachPath helper); src/ws-client.js (minimal client-side WS connector with mask+upgrade handshake, head-buffer flush via setImmediate so the awaiter has a chance to attach close listeners before an immediate-close server hits the decoder); src/daemon.js wires server.on('upgrade', ...) to _handleAttachUpgrade which mirrors the /watch SSE pattern (manager.watchWorker → outbound binary frames) and adds bidirectional input via the new PtyManager.writeRawInput helper (raw passthrough, no auto-CR / no chunking / no critical_deny gate so arrow keys + Ctrl-C reach the pty unmodified); auth.checkRequest runs first so ?token= sessions land on the same gate as /api/watch; ?readonly=1 silently drops inbound frames; missing worker → handshake + close 1008 + reason 'worker not found'; src/cli.js gains buildAttachUrl (pure URL builder with http→ws / https→wss protocol swap and --readonly + token query) + runAttach (DI-friendly handler with mocked stdio + setRawMode + exit + connect) plus `case 'attach'` in the main switch; module.exports adds buildAttachUrl + runAttach alongside the existing helpers; usage block lists `attach <name> [--readonly]` between watch and watch-interventions. OpenAPI: openapi-gen.js gained a _VIRTUAL_ROUTES list (the extractRoutes regex matches HTTP route handlers only, so the upgrade-event route is injected manually) plus a curated GET /workers/{name}/attach ROUTE_SCHEMAS entry that documents the path/query params and the WebSocket upgrade contract. Tests: tests/daemon-attach.test.js (23 cases: handshake helpers, frame encoder/decoder, parseAttachPath, plus end-to-end against a real http.Server with the same upgrade wiring daemon.js uses — non-existent worker → 1008, output forwarded, inbound → writeRawInput, ?readonly=1 drops inbound, socket close cleans subscription) — all 23 passing locally. tests/cli-attach.test.js (19 cases: buildAttachUrl URL shapes + ws/wss / readonly / token / encoded-name / missing-name guard, runAttach usage / URL plumbing / Ctrl+] detach / 1008→exit 2 / non-1000 close→exit 1 / stdin passthrough / readonly drops stdin / message→stdout / ECONNREFUSED→exit 1 / 404 upgrade refusal→exit 2 / 401 upgrade refusal→exit 1, plus a help-block usage assertion) — all 19 passing locally. Test-fixture iteration (full-suite regression + lint) deferred to a follow-up task per manager direction. Docs: CLAUDE.md gained an `attach <name> [--readonly]` row; CHANGELOG entry [1.11.90] mirrors the shipped breakdown; web/package.json bumped to 1.11.90. |
| 11.73 | Daemon: clean stop with worker checkpoint | done | Improve `c4 daemon stop` in src/daemon.js — send SIGTERM to each worker process, wait up to 30s for clean exit, then SIGKILL. Write checkpoint to /root/c4/.c4/checkpoints/<worker>.json including branch, worktree, last-task summary. Branch c4/auto-daemon-stop. Worker auto-w64 (queue named auto-w60 but already used). Bump 1.11.91. Shipped: src/daemon-checkpoint.js (new) exports CHECKPOINT_GRACE_MS=30_000, DEFAULT_VERSION, checkpointDir, buildCheckpointPayload, writeCheckpoint (atomic write — stages to `<os.tmpdir()>/<name>.<pid>.json.tmp` then renames into `<projectRoot>/.c4/checkpoints/<name>.json`, mkdir recursive on the destination), and shutdownWorkers (enumerate workers -> write checkpoint -> SIGTERM live procs -> poll every 250ms until the map empties or graceMs elapses -> SIGKILL any survivor -> emit per-worker log lines `graceful exit` / `force-killed after Ns`). All sequencing functions accept DI hooks (now / delay / kill / isAlive / writeCheckpoint / log / fs / tmpDir) so the test suite drives the poll loop without sleeping. src/daemon.js `_gracefulShutdown` is now async + idempotent (a re-entry guard short-circuits a second SIGINT/SIGTERM during shutdown); it calls `await daemonCheckpoint.shutdownWorkers(manager.workers.values(), { projectRoot, version: manager._daemonVersion, log })` in place of `manager.closeAll()`, so worktrees and branches are preserved across daemon stops. uncaughtException is intentionally NOT wired to this path -- the module documents `skipCheckpoint:true` for any future emergency-stop branch so an inconsistent process state never writes a misleading checkpoint. Checkpoint payload schema matches the v1.11.91 contract: { name, branch, worktree, pid, target, tier (defaults to "worker"), lastTask: { summary, timestamp } \| null, stoppedAt, version }. lastTask pulls from worker._taskText (first line, max 200 chars) and worker._taskStartedAt; null when the worker never received a task. tests/daemon-checkpoint.test.js (new, 20 cases, node:test) covers: payload schema, pretty-printed JSON on disk, atomic tmp-then-rename, mkdir recursive, missing _taskText, summary truncation + first-line-only, tier/target defaults, name-required guard, real-fs end-to-end under os.mkdtempSync, shutdownWorkers walks every worker + writes checkpoint each, SIGTERMs every live worker, resolves early on graceful exit, escalates SIGKILL after graceMs, per-worker log lines (graceful exit / force-killed after Ns), skipCheckpoint=true bypass (uncaughtException contract), checkpoint path on each result entry, kill-error outcome, writeCheckpoint failure does not abort the pass, CHECKPOINT_GRACE_MS export = 30_000, and a textual ordering assertion that daemon.js calls shutdownWorkers BEFORE server.close() so the listening socket closes last. CLAUDE.md gained a "Daemon checkpoints" section with cat / recover recipe pointing at `<projectRoot>/.c4/checkpoints/<name>.json`. CHANGELOG entry [1.11.91] mirrors the shipped breakdown. web/package.json bumped 1.11.90 -> 1.11.91. Auto-readoption on `c4 daemon start` is deferred to TODO 11.74 (c4/auto-daemon-reconnect). |
| 11.74 | Daemon: reconnect orphan workers | done | On `c4 daemon start`, scan `git -C <repo> worktree list` for orphan worktrees matching pattern c4-worktree-*. Cross-reference with .c4/checkpoints/*.json. Auto-re-adopt workers whose checkpoint matches a live process pid, mark others as LOST. Add `c4 reconnect <name>` for manual re-adopt. Branch c4/auto-daemon-reconnect. Worker auto-w65 (queue named auto-w61 but already used). Bump 1.11.92. Shipped: src/daemon-checkpoint.js gained reconcileOrphans (pure planner: lists c4-worktree-* paths, pairs them with .c4/checkpoints/<name>.json, classifies each as adopt vs lost), planReconnect (single-name plan used by the daemon endpoint), readCheckpoint (distinguishes file-missing from malformed-JSON), defaultIsPidAlive (process.kill(pid, 0) wrapper -- ESRCH=dead, EPERM=alive), workerNameFromWorktreePath (strips c4-worktree- prefix; returns null for non-c4 worktrees). src/daemon-reconnect.js (new) carries the manager-side side effects: buildRecoveredWorker (kind:'recovered', state:'ATTACHED', _recovered:true, _recoveryShown:false flipped on first list() call so the suffix appears once), applyReconcilePlan (mutates manager.workers for adopts, manager.lostWorkers for losts with a reason field + state:'LOST'), reconcileOrphans / reconnectWorker as thin glue. Pulled out of pty-manager.js so tests do not have to load node-pty bindings. PtyManager.reconcileOrphans / reconnectWorker became one-line forwards. src/daemon.js wires reconcileOrphans into the server.listen callback (after _saveState, before startHealthCheck) -- catches throw + logs adopted/lost counts; never blocks startup. New POST /api/workers/:name/reconnect endpoint mirrors close pattern: 200 on adopt with worker payload, 404 with error:'not-found' when no checkpoint exists, 409 with error:'pid-dead'+pid when the checkpoint pid is dead. Gated by rbac.ACTIONS.WORKER_CREATE; emits worker.reconnected audit event + worker_reconnect SSE. LOST reasons surfaced: no-checkpoint, pid-dead, malformed-checkpoint, no-pid. src/pty-manager.js list() output augmented with recovered, recoveredSuffix (one-shot), state fields per worker and reason+state per lostWorkers entry. src/cli.js: c4 reconnect <name> subcommand + runReconnect helper exported for tests. runReconnect uses a narrow requestWithStatus helper (the baseline request() drops the HTTP status code) and maps 200/404/409 to exit codes 0/2/1 with the messages from the task spec: 'Reconnected <name> (pid=..., branch=...)' / 'Worker <name> not found in checkpoints' / 'Worker <name> pid <N> is no longer alive - run c4 cleanup to discard'. tests/daemon-reconnect.test.js (24 cases, node:test) covers reconcileOrphans adopt/pid-dead/no-checkpoint/malformed/no-pid/skipNames/non-c4-prefix, log line shape, planReconnect adopt/lost/pid-dead, manager-side reconcileOrphans+reconnectWorker mutating workers/lostWorkers, recovered breadcrumb (_recoveryShown=false on build, lastTask preserved across the rebuild), and the 200/404/409 endpoint contract. tests/cli-reconnect.test.js (8 cases) covers URL shape, URI-encoded names, 200/404/409 stdout+exit codes, network error path, legacy body-only error code fallback, generic 500. CLAUDE.md gained a 'Daemon reconnect (v1.11.92)' section + c4 reconnect row in the CLI table; LOST troubleshooting row points at c4 reconnect first. CHANGELOG entry [1.11.92]. web/package.json bumped 1.11.91 -> 1.11.92. daemon-checkpoint DEFAULT_VERSION bumped 1.11.91 -> 1.11.92. |
| 11.75 | Feature: c4 diff command | done | Add `c4 diff <branch>` — runs `git -C <repo> diff main...<branch> --stat` and prints colored summary. With --patch flag, full diff. With --files prints just file list. Tests in src/cli.test.js. Branch c4/auto-feat-diff. Worker auto-w62 (queue named, executed by auto-w66). Bump 1.11.93. Shipped: `c4 diff <branch> [--stat|--patch|--files]` landed in src/cli.js with three exported helpers (resolveDiffRepo, buildDiffArgs, runDiff). Repo resolution mirrors `c4 merge` (`git rev-parse --show-toplevel` > config.worktree.projectRoot > cwd). --stat (default) and --patch pass `-c color.diff=always` so ANSI color survives even when the output is piped; --files uses `--name-only` with no color override since the output is meant for xargs/grep. Branch is consumed verbatim — no `c4/` prefix injection. Multiple mode flags resolve "last one wins." Spawn uses child_process.spawn with `{ stdio: 'inherit', env: { ..., GIT_PAGER: 'cat' } }` so git owns every byte and the pager never opens; the child's close-event exit code is forwarded (128 on bad ref, 0 on clean diff). Spawn ENOENT / `error` events map to exit 1 with `[c4 diff: spawn failed - <msg>]` on stderr. Help block gains a `diff <branch> [--stat|--patch|--files]` row between `ui` and `daemon start`. tests/cli-diff.test.js (19 cases, node:test) covers buildDiffArgs argv shape for every mode, missing-branch/missing-repo guards, resolveDiffRepo fallback chain (git toplevel > config > cwd), runDiff spawn options (stdio inherit + GIT_PAGER=cat + color flag presence per mode), exit code forwarding via close event, spawn error path, null close code normalisation, and a CLI-integration assertion that the help block lists the new row. CHANGELOG entry [1.11.93]. CLAUDE.md gained a `c4 diff` row in the CLI table. web/package.json bumped 1.11.92 -> 1.11.93. |
| 11.76 | Feature: Queue web editor | done | Build web/src/pages/Queue.tsx — table view of docs/autonomous-queue-v10.md with: drag-reorder, edit detail in textarea modal, mark status dropdown (todo/doing/done). Add POST /api/autonomous/queue endpoint in daemon to update the markdown. Add nav entry. Branch c4/auto-feat-queue-editor. Worker auto-w63 (queue named, executed by auto-w67). Bump 1.11.94. Shipped: POST /api/autonomous/queue accepts { rows: [{ id, title, status, detail }] } and atomically rewrites docs/autonomous-queue-v10.md via tmp+rename; GET /api/autonomous/queue now returns the verbatim markdown as `raw` alongside parsed rows and accepts the new `partial` status; src/queue-editor.js (new) hosts parseQueue / serializeQueue / validateRows / writeAtomic plus handleGet / handlePost helpers so the daemon route delegates to pure functions the test suite drives directly; auth gate reuses CONFIG_RELOAD and a single autonomous.queue.write audit line is recorded on each successful POST. web/src/pages/Queue.tsx renders a single 6-column table (drag handle, ID, Title, Status select, truncated detail preview, Edit action) with HTML5 drag reorder, an Escape/backdrop/Cancel-dismissible modal carrying a min-h-[12rem] textarea for the detail edit, and optimistic-update + rollback so the page rolls back to the pre-edit snapshot on POST failure with a `Save failed:` banner above the table. Loading uses a 6-row Skeleton stack; the empty branch renders EmptyState with EmptyQueueIllustration; ErrorState carries a retry button wired to a fresh GET. Registered in pages/registry.ts under Automation between Auto and Templates with the ListOrdered lucide icon; en.json picks up the new feature label/description strings and ko.json falls through to the English bundle via the existing t() fallback path. Tests: 13 vitest cases in Queue.test.tsx (loading skeleton, rows render, empty illustration, error+retry, status POST, modal open/save/cancel, drag reorder POST + rollback, status option list, refresh, truncated detail preview) and 22 node:test cases in tests/daemon-queue.test.js (parseQueue branches, serializeQueue round-trip + reorder + pipe-strip, validateRows happy path + 5 reject paths, writeAtomic atomic guarantee, handleGet parsed-rows + 404 fallback, handlePost atomic write + audit + 400 paths for malformed body / invalid status / duplicate id). Full vitest 5216 / 5216 passing; daemon-queue 22 / 22 passing. Docs: CHANGELOG entry [1.11.94] mirrors the shipped breakdown; CLAUDE.md gains a brief paragraph in the autonomous section about the new editor; web/package.json bumped 1.11.93 -> 1.11.94. |
| 11.77 | Feature: Webhook notifications | done | Add config.notifications section { slack?: url, discord?: url, events: ['halt', 'dispatch', 'complete', 'escalation'] }. In src/daemon/autonomous.js, POST formatted message to configured URLs on each event. Use built-in https module, no new deps. Branch c4/auto-feat-webhooks. Worker auto-w64 (queue named, executed by auto-w68). Bump 1.11.95. Shipped: src/notify.js (new) exports sendWebhook({ kind, payload, config }) — opt-in lifecycle webhook helper using Node's built-in https module, no new dependencies. Slack receives { text } and Discord receives { content }; both are one-line markdown-safe summaries of the form `[c4 autonomous] <kind> | todo=<id> | title=<title> | worker=<name> | v<version>`. Fire-and-forget: helper returns immediately after req.end(), inspects response only for status class, logs a single warn line on 4xx/5xx (carrying host + status, never body or full URL path), and never retries. Escape hatches: NOTIFY_DISABLED=1 env var skips every POST, opts._request lets tests inject a stub https.request (mirrors daemon-checkpoint.js's opts.kill / opts.writeCheckpoint injection pattern). Config: notifications.slack / notifications.discord accept https:// URL strings (lifecycle webhook) while the legacy object form { enabled, webhookUrl } still drives the existing buffered Notifications channel — both shapes coexist. notifications.events[] gated to the four canonical kinds; c4 config validate rejects any other entry and warns when events are listed without a URL. Daemon wiring (src/daemon.js): _buildAutoDispatcher notifier callback now also calls sendWebhook for auto_dispatch_sent -> dispatch, auto_dispatch_paused -> halt, and auto_dispatch_escalation -> escalation; the manager-SSE listener that already calls recordSuccess on task_complete for the autonomous manager now also fires the complete kind. Missing config.notifications block produces zero behavior change. Tests: tests/notify.test.js (new, 23 node:test cases) covers gating (kind not in events, missing block, unknown kind, no URLs, non-https URL reject), body shapes (Slack text, Discord content, Slack-first-then-Discord order, POST + JSON headers, Content-Length), error handling (4xx warn, 5xx warn, 2xx silent, synchronous throw, network error event, no body leakage in log), NOTIFY_DISABLED (=1, =TRUE, =0 still fires), and formatSummary / _resolveUrls / _resolveEvents helpers; https.request mocked via _request injection so no test touches the network. tests/auto-dispatch.test.js gains a "webhook notifier wiring (11.77)" section: 5 integration cases assert the auto_dispatch_* -> <kind> translation, no-emit on recordSuccess (manager-SSE owns complete), and a drive-through that fires Slack + Discord for each dispatcher-side kind. tests/config-validate.test.js gains a "notifications lifecycle (11.77)" describe: 7 cases covering clean shape, non-https slack reject, non-https discord reject, invalid events entry, non-array events, no-URL warning, and legacy object form pass-through. notify.test.js 23/23, auto-dispatch.test.js 68/68, config-validate.test.js 48/48 all passing. Docs: CHANGELOG entry [1.11.95] mirrors the shipped breakdown; CLAUDE.md autonomous section gains a paragraph on the new notifier; config.example.json gains a _notifications_lifecycle_doc preface and _notifications_lifecycle_example block; web/package.json bumped 1.11.94 -> 1.11.95. |

| 11.82 | Observability: structured logging | done | Replace console.log in src/daemon.js + src/daemon/* with pino logger. Levels trace/debug/info/warn/error. Rotation via pino-roll (or built-in fs.createWriteStream with size check). Log path from config.logging.path. NPM install pino + pino-pretty. Branch c4/auto-obs-logging. Worker auto-w73 (auto-w69 slot reused). Bump 1.11.100. Shipped: new `src/logger.js` with `createLogger(opts)` factory + `getLogger()` lazy singleton; reads `config.logging` `{ path, level, pretty, maxSize }` with safe coercion (unknown level falls back to info, malformed shapes default to stdout/info). Pretty mode pipes through pino-pretty when stdout is a TTY; non-TTY stays on JSON. Size-based one-step rotation built on `fs.openSync` + `writeSync` + `renameSync` (NOT `createWriteStream` -- it's lazy and races `renameSync` on the first batch, silently losing the rotated inode); the live file is renamed to `<path>.1` when the next write would cross `maxSize` and a fresh fd opens. Pre-existing file size seeds the byte counter so a restart doesn't reset the rotation clock. Sweep: 13 console call sites in `src/daemon.js` (audit, schedule, hook events, recovery hook, listen banner, reconcileOrphans, startup state save, uncaughtException, unhandledRejection) + the `defaultLog` helper in `src/notify.js` now route through the logger with structured `component` field + contextual keys (worker, branch, err, etc.). `src/daemon-manager.js` keeps console.warn (CLI flow before daemon process exists). `src/queue-editor.js`, `src/daemon-checkpoint.js`, `src/daemon-reconnect.js` had no console calls. `pino@^10.3.1` + `pino-pretty@^13.1.3` added to `package.json` deps (root, not web/). `tests/logger.test.js`: 18 cases across 7 suites (`_safeConfig` coercion, level filtering + fallback, file destination + recursive parent mkdir, size-triggered rotation + pre-existing byte-counter seed, pretty TTY gate, integration sweep on trace/debug/info/warn/error asserting numeric levels 10/20/30/40/50, singleton reset). `web/package.json` 1.11.99 -> 1.11.100. CLAUDE.md daemon section gets a `Structured logging (v1.11.100)` block documenting the config shape, rotation contract, and pretty rules. Verification: `node --test tests/logger.test.js` 18/18 pass; `node --test tests/notify.test.js` 23/23 still pass after sweep. Out of scope: rest of `src/*.js` sweep, multi-step / time-based rotation -- a downstream log shipper handles long retention. |
| 11.83 | Observability: prometheus metrics | done | Extend src/daemon/routes/metrics.js to expose GET /api/metrics/prometheus with text-format metrics: c4_worker_rss_bytes, c4_worker_cpu_percent, c4_dispatch_total_count, c4_escalation_total_count. Per-worker labels. Branch c4/auto-obs-prom. Worker auto-w70 (auto-w74 slot reused). Bump 1.11.101. Shipped: new `src/prometheus-format.js` exposing `formatMetrics(workers, counters)` + `escapeLabelValue(s)` -- pure projection helper that takes an iterable / Map / plain object of worker entries plus a counter object and returns a single text-format string. Section order is fixed (rss, cpu, dispatch, escalation) so two snapshots diff cleanly; rows are sorted by worker name; label values escape backslash, double-quote, newline, and CRLF per the Prometheus spec. Missing rss / cpu samples skip the row (Prometheus treats absent data points as expected); missing or non-finite counter values render as `0` so every series the daemon promises is always present. The dispatch note mentioned `src/daemon/routes/metrics.js` but the daemon keeps routes inline in `src/daemon.js`; followed the dispatch note's fallback and added the endpoint inline alongside the existing JSON `/metrics` handler instead of refactoring into a routes module. New daemon route `GET /api/metrics/prometheus` reuses the existing `manager.metrics()` snapshot (no extra sampling), enriches with `target` from `manager.list()` and `tier` from the existing module-level `tierWorkerMap`, hands the result to `formatMetrics`, and sets `Content-Type: text/plain; version=0.0.4; charset=utf-8`. Auth rides the same `/api/*` JWT gate already applied to `/api/metrics` -- no special case. Metric names + labels: `c4_worker_rss_bytes{name,tier,target}` (gauge, bytes), `c4_worker_cpu_percent{name,tier,target}` (gauge, percent), `c4_dispatch_total_count` (counter), `c4_escalation_total_count` (counter). Counter wiring: module-level `lifecycleCounters = { dispatch: 0, escalation: 0 }` on the daemon, bumped at the same call sites as the v1.11.95 lifecycle webhook (`_sendLifecycle('dispatch')` / `_sendLifecycle('escalation')` inside the autonomous notifier callback). Counters reset on daemon restart by design; the audit log remains the source of truth for durable accounting. Tests: `tests/prometheus-format.test.js` 17 cases across 2 suites (ASCII passthrough + backslash / quote / newline / CRLF escaping, empty input -> HELP + TYPE + zero counters, null / undefined / non-iterable input handling, lexicographic worker sort across both sections, default tier / target labels, rssBytes precedence over rssKb, Map and plain-object collections, negative / NaN counter defense, fixed section order, single trailing newline). `tests/daemon-prometheus.test.js` 8 cases across 3 suites (200 + content type, prometheus regex sanity on the body, per-worker labels for auto-w1 / mgr-1 with worker / manager and local / dgx, live counter reflection across two scrapes, empty-fleet output, auth-gate triplet for 401 parity / valid bearer 200 / wrong-secret 401). Uses native `http.request` rather than supertest so the suite runs without a node_modules install in the worktree. Verification: `node --test tests/prometheus-format.test.js` 17/17 pass; `NODE_PATH=/root/c4/node_modules node --test tests/daemon-prometheus.test.js` 8/8 pass; `node -c src/daemon.js` clean. `web/package.json` 1.11.100 -> 1.11.101. CLAUDE.md daemon section gets a `Prometheus metrics endpoint (v1.11.101)` block with a curl example. Out of scope (deferred): Grafana dashboard JSON template (metric names + label set are the contract; downstream dashboards are a separate exercise); histogram metrics for task latency / queue depth (counters + gauges land first; follow-up TODO can layer histograms once the scrape path is in production use). |

Shipped: `rollup-plugin-visualizer@^6.0.11` added as devDep, gated behind `process.env['ANALYZE'] === '1'` in `web/vite.config.ts` -- conditionally appended to `plugins[]` so dev / test / normal-build flows are byte-identical. New `build:analyze` script in `web/package.json` (= `ANALYZE=1 vite build`) emits `web/stats.html` (treemap, gzipSize + brotliSize). New `web/.gitignore` excludes `stats.html` so the report stays local. Top 3 chunks (gzip in parens): `vendor-xterm` 369.39 kB (95.72 kB), `index` 221.40 kB (71.10 kB), `vendor-react-dom` 129.82 kB (41.68 kB). All 17 feature pages (Auto/Batch/Cleanup/Config/Health/Morning/Plan/Profiles/Queue/Rbac/Risk/Scribe/Swarm/Templates/TokenUsage/Validation/Workspaces) already ship in their own chunks because the `FEATURES` registry exposes a `load: () => import('./X')` callback that `FeatureView` wraps with `React.lazy()` -- the deliverable's "split 4 pages" requirement was already satisfied for all 17, no new lazy declarations needed (touching the registry would have duplicated the chunk pipeline). `FeatureView` Suspense fallback swapped from multi-row `LoadingSkeleton` to the v1.11.78 `Skeleton` primitive (variant=card, `data-testid="feature-suspense-skeleton"`) inside the existing `PageFrame` shell. New `web/src/pages/page-lazy.test.tsx` adds 5 cases: 4 parameterised cases for the heaviest pages (auto/risk/queue/token-usage) asserting `React.lazy(feat.load)` returns a component whose `$$typeof` equals `Symbol.for('react.lazy')` (same sentinel React uses to decide whether to suspend); 1 stubbed render through Suspense asserting the fallback->body swap. Test deliberately does not resolve the real registry promises (xterm / msw fixtures). Bundle size delta: initial `index` chunk unchanged at 221.40 kB / gzip 71.10 kB -- the registry was already loading pages on demand, so this entry codifies that state with the visualizer + a regression test rather than introducing a new split. `web/package.json` 1.11.101 -> 1.11.102. CLAUDE.md unchanged (no new public daemon contract). Verification: `env -C web ANALYZE=1 npx vite build` succeeds in 4.81s; full unit suite 237 files / 5288 tests pass (5283 prior + 5 new lazy cases); FeatureView tests 16/16 pass after the Skeleton fallback swap. Out of scope (deferred): re-splitting the 369 kB `vendor-xterm` chunk (only loads when a worker terminal opens; trimming addons is a separate exercise); a bundle-size CI gate (visualizer wires the inspection path; threshold enforcement is follow-up). |
| 11.85 | Performance: daemon hot paths | partial | Profile `c4 list` and `c4 autonomous status` end-to-end. Add in-memory cache with 2s TTL for c4 list response and worker status snapshots. Target: c4 list p50 latency < 50ms. Add a perf test in src/daemon/perf.test.js. Branch c4/auto-perf-daemon. Worker auto-w76 (auto-w72 slot reused). Bump 1.11.103. Shipped: new `src/cache-ttl.js` exporting `createCache({ ttlMs })` with `get` / `set` / `invalidate(key?)` / `getOrCompute(key, asyncCompute)`. `getOrCompute` returns cached values within TTL, otherwise runs the compute once and stores the result; concurrent callers during a pending compute share the same Promise (stampede protection); rejected computes clear the in-flight slot so the next call retries; clock is injectable for deterministic test expiry. Two daemon singletons: `cacheList = createCache({ ttlMs: 2000 })` wraps the `/api/list` handler body via `cacheList.getOrCompute('list', async () => ...)`; `cacheAutoStatus = createCache({ ttlMs: 2000 })` wraps `/api/autonomous/status` the same way. Invalidation hooks at every state-change site: `cacheList.invalidate('list')` on `/create` success and `/close` success; `cacheAutoStatus.invalidate('status')` on `/autonomous/pause`, `/autonomous/resume`, and `/autonomous/tick`. Profiling (`tools/profile-daemon-endpoints.js`, 100 iterations per N): list() p50 0.18 ms at N=10, 0.37 ms at N=30, 0.58 ms at N=50 -- well under the 50 ms target even before caching. workerMetrics.sample is the dominant slice (two `fs.readFileSync` calls into `/proc/<pid>/{status,stat}` per worker, scales linearly with N); JSON.stringify is small but real (p50 0.03 ms at N=30, 0.05 ms at N=50); per-worker intervention / failure-hint computation is already memoized. New `tests/cache-ttl.test.js` 13 cases / 4 suites: get / set basics + missing-key undefined, defaults to 2000 ms, key independence, getOrCompute TTL freshness window, exact-boundary expiry, two-caller stampede sharing one Promise, rejected-compute cleanup so the next call retries, post-resolve cache hit, `invalidate(key)` drops one entry, `invalidate()` with no arg clears every entry, `invalidate` forcing the next call to recompute. `web/package.json` 1.11.102 -> 1.11.103. CHANGELOG `[1.11.103]` covers findings + cache contract + invalidation points. Verification: `node --test tests/cache-ttl.test.js` 13/13 pass; `node -c src/daemon.js` clean. Deferred to follow-up: `tests/daemon-perf.test.js` (supertest-based perf test asserting cache hit < 5 ms, p50 across 100 sequential calls < 5000 ms wall, invalidation paths on /create+/close and /autonomous/{tick,pause,resume}) -- the deliverable's p50 target is already comfortably met by the un-cached baseline and the cache reduces per-call cost to a `Map.get` once the first compute lands, but pinning that as a regression gate is the remaining piece. |

| 11.167 | UI: focus-trap reusable hook | done | Extract web/src/hooks/use-focus-trap.ts — DRY hook used by Dialog + Popover + future Drawer. Takes a containerRef + active flag, traps Tab/Shift+Tab cycle, restores focus on unmount. Refactor Dialog + Popover to use it. Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/USAGE.md a11y patterns. Branch c4/auto-ui-focus-trap-hook. Worker auto-w151. Bump 1.11.185. Shipped: use-focus-trap.ts (96 lines) — UseFocusTrapOptions { active default true, initialFocusRef, restoreFocusOnUnmount default true, onEscape } and useFocusTrap<T>(containerRef, options) hook. Captures previouslyFocused on mount, focuses initialFocusRef -> first focusable -> container; Tab/Shift+Tab cycles inside container; Escape calls onEscape; restores previouslyFocused on unmount/inactive. SSR-safe. use-focus-trap.test.tsx (127 lines, 10 cases). Refactor: Dialog and Popover now both delegate focus management to useFocusTrap (88+88 lines of inline trap code removed, net -153 / +354 across the change). web/package.json 1.11.184 -> 1.11.185. | 11.203 | UI: worker resource mini-graphs | done | SHIPPED v1.11.221 (auto-w187, merged 5dd780d). web/src/components/WorkerResourceGraph.tsx (170 lines): Props { workerName: string, sampleIntervalMs=5000, windowMs=60000, height=28 }. Self-polls /api/metrics on its own cadence so it does not contend with shared MetricsBar pollers. Keeps ring buffer of last N samples (N = windowMs/sampleIntervalMs, default 12). Renders two side-by-side inline SVG sparklines: CPU% (0-100) and RSS bytes (normalized to peak in window) with stroke-width 1.5 polylines plus a small mono-font text label showing the current value. Renders 'no data' placeholder before first poll resolves. Cleans up interval on unmount. Uses ARPS design tokens (text-foreground, stroke-primary, bg-muted). Adoption in web/src/components/WorkerList.tsx: row right slot wraps the existing Badge + new <WorkerResourceGraph workerName={w.name}/> in a flex container so the graph sits left of the status badge. WorkerResourceGraph.test.tsx (165 lines, 6+ vitest+RTL cases all pass via vi.useFakeTimers + fetch mock): renders two sparklines, 'no data' placeholder before first sample, polyline points appear after mock interval tick, ring buffer caps + evicts older points at capacity, no leaked interval on unmount, current-value text label renders. Bumped web/package.json 1.11.220 -> 1.11.221. CHANGELOG.md entry. docs/patches/11.203-ui-worker-resource-graph.md (95 lines). |

| 11.205 | UI: focus-cycle hook | done | SHIPPED v1.11.223 (auto-w189, merged d5e3506). web/src/hooks/use-focus-cycle.ts (143 lines): useFocusCycle({ containerRef, itemSelector (default '[role=menuitem], [role=tab], [role=option], button:not([disabled])'), orientation 'horizontal'|'vertical'|'both' (default vertical), wrap (default true), onSelect }). Returns { handleKeyDown, focusFirst, focusLast, focusIndex }. handleKeyDown manages ArrowDown/Up (vertical), ArrowLeft/Right (horizontal), Home/End, Tab passthrough; 'both' orientation responds to all 4 arrows. Skips disabled/aria-hidden/hidden items. wrap=true cycles last->first/first->last; wrap=false stops at boundaries. use-focus-cycle.test.tsx (156 lines, 9 vitest+RTL cases all pass): 3 buttons ArrowDown 0->1->2, ArrowUp 2->1->0, wrap=true ArrowDown from last to first, wrap=false ArrowDown from last stays, Home/End jump to first/last, skips disabled items, orientation='horizontal' responds to ArrowLeft/Right, orientation='both' responds to all 4 arrows, focusIndex helper. Adoption: web/src/components/ui/tabs.tsx refactored to use the hook (handleKeyDown now passed without idx, ref tracking via roving tabindex). Other 3 primitives (dropdown-menu/context-menu/popover) deliberately left on their existing focus-management impls to avoid behavior regression -- documented in patch notes as a deferred follow-up. Bumped web/package.json 1.11.222 -> 1.11.223. CHANGELOG.md entry. docs/patches/11.205-ui-focus-cycle-hook.md (191 lines). |

Wraps fallback in try/catch. Cleans up timeout on unmount. SSR-safe. Adoption at 3 inline writeText callsites: use-copy-pulse.ts now delegates writeText+reset-timer plumbing to the hook (public {copied,copy} shape unchanged); use-morning.ts replaces inline try/catch with await copy() reading {ok, error} for showToast feedback; use-action-items-export.ts swaps silent writeText().catch() for hook copy() and exposes copied:boolean so MeetingsActionItemsPanel flips MD button label to 'Copied' during the 1.5s pulse. use-copy-to-clipboard.test.tsx (128 lines, 7 vitest+RTL cases all pass): writeText forwarding, copied flip on success, auto-reset at resetMs via fake timers, rejection captures Error + copied=false, fallback execCommand path when clipboard undefined, fallback returns false populates error, unmount cleanup. 68/68 affected tests pass across all 5 files. Bumped web/package.json 1.11.223 -> 1.11.224. CHANGELOG.md entry. docs/patches/11.206-ui-clipboard-hook.md (158 lines). |

| 11.209 | UI: list virtualizer hook | done | SHIPPED v1.11.227 (auto-w193, merged 2b53d60). web/src/hooks/use-list-virtualizer.ts (132 lines): useListVirtualizer({ itemCount, itemHeight, overscan=3, containerRef? }) -> { items: Array<{index, top}>, totalHeight, offsetY, containerProps: { onScroll, style } }. No external deps. SSR-safe (typeof window guards). Fixed itemHeight (no dynamic measurement v1). Computes visible startIndex..endIndex from scrollTop/itemHeight expanded by overscan. totalHeight = itemCount * itemHeight. offsetY = startIndex * itemHeight. Tail-clamp on runaway scroll. Default viewport from clientHeight at first scroll event; falls back to window.innerHeight; SSR -> 0-viewport. Adoption in web/src/components/HistoryView.tsx: sidebar list migrated from VirtualList primitive to useListVirtualizer via a small SidebarVirtualizedList extract; ARIA + click + bulk handlers preserved. use-list-virtualizer.test.tsx (167 lines, 9 vitest cases all pass; 52/52 total with HistoryView tests): top-position items default, scroll event updates items, overscan adds buffer, totalHeight = count * itemHeight, offsetY moves with startIndex, itemCount=0 -> empty items + totalHeight=0, scroll past end clamps to last, containerRef viewport reads clientHeight, SSR fallback no crash with 0 viewport returns overscan band. Bumped web/package.json 1.11.226 -> 1.11.227. CHANGELOG.md entry. docs/patches/11.209-ui-virtualizer-hook.md (172 lines). |
| 11.210 | UI: relative-time component | done | SHIPPED v1.11.228 (auto-w194, merged 03a2b64). web/src/components/RelativeTime.tsx (136 lines): Props { value: Date|string|number, now?: Date, absoluteOnHover?: boolean=true }. Formats relative time: 'just now' (<30s), 'N seconds ago' (<1min), 'N minute(s)/hour(s)/day(s)/month(s)/year(s) ago', future tense 'in N minutes' for negative deltas. Auto-refresh tick: 1s when delta<1min, 1min when delta<1h, 1h when delta<1d, 1d otherwise; reschedules on each tick. Renders <time dateTime={iso}> wrapper. With absoluteOnHover, title= attribute holds the locale string for tooltip. SSR-safe (no setInterval until effect mount). Built-in Date math only -- no date-fns/dayjs. Adoption at 5 callsites: AttachModal.tsx preview list updatedAt (bails cleanly on null), MeetingsRecapPanel.tsx meeting timestamps, SessionsAttachedSection.tsx (replaces formatRelative), ErrorReports.tsx captured-error timestamp (retires local formatTimestamp helper), Snapshots.tsx createdAt cell (replaces formatRelativeTime import). Tokens-only / numeric toLocaleString sites left untouched. RelativeTime.test.tsx (136 lines, 8+ vitest+RTL cases all pass via vi.useFakeTimers + now prop): just-now for delta<30s, 'N minutes ago' for 1-59min, 'N hours ago' for 1-23h, 'N days ago' for 1-30d, future tense 'in N minutes' for negative, auto-refresh tick updates display after advanceTimersByTime, dateTime ISO attribute, title locale attribute, accepts Date/string/number. Bumped web/package.json 1.11.227 -> 1.11.228. CHANGELOG.md entry. docs/patches/11.210-ui-relative-time.md (136 lines). |
| 11.211 | UI: toast queue prioritization | done | SHIPPED v1.11.229 (auto-w195, merged 9f29294). web/src/components/Toast.tsx: added warning tone (icon AlertTriangle, border-warning/40 bg-warning/15 text-warning) so the priority rubric has paired visuals. TOAST_PRIORITY map (error=3, warning=2, success=1, info=0). TOAST_VISIBLE_LIMIT=3 const. partitionToasts(toasts, limit) -> { visible, pending } helper sorts by priority then insertion order. New ToastStack component renders top-3 visible Toasts and a portaled '+N more' Chip (neutral/subtle/sm) when overflow exists; hidden when no pending. Behaviour: showToast() + useToast.toasts unchanged -- callers pass useToast().toasts into <ToastStack>; on dismiss the parent drops the entry and the stack promotes the next-highest pending into the freed slot on next render. Each Toast keeps its own auto-dismiss + swipe-to-dismiss. Public API preserved. Toast.test.tsx: 11 new cases across TOAST_PRIORITY map, partitionToasts, and <ToastStack> describes, plus warning-tone smoke -- 47/47 pass; use-toast.test.ts 18/18 pass (no regression from added warning type). Bumped web/package.json 1.11.228 -> 1.11.229. CHANGELOG.md entry. docs/patches/11.211-ui-toast-priority.md (63 lines). |
| 11.212 | UI: debounce + throttle hooks | done | SHIPPED v1.11.230 (auto-w196, merged 2310d37). web/src/hooks/use-debounce.ts (56 lines): useDebounce<T>(value, ms) trailing-edge holds previous until ms quiet then flushes latest; useDebouncedCallback(fn, ms) returns stable callback firing fn after ms quiet with last args. Pending timer cleared on unmount. Effect-dep-safe stable identity while ms unchanged. web/src/hooks/use-throttle.ts (69 lines): useThrottle<T>(value, ms) leading-edge, derived-state pattern -- first update after mount and first after each window elapse propagate immediately, intervening dropped; useThrottledCallback(fn, ms) leading-edge callback. lastEmitRef = NEGATIVE_INFINITY initial so first update always propagates. SSR-safe (timers in useEffect or behind closure refs). use-debounce.test.tsx (125 lines, 6+ vitest+RTL cases via vi.useFakeTimers all pass): initial value, hold-then-update after ms, rapid changes restart timer (latest wins), unmount cleanup, useDebouncedCallback fires with last args, unmount cancels. use-throttle.test.tsx (135 lines, 6+ cases all pass): initial value, first update propagates immediately, intervening dropped, after ms next propagates, callback first call fires + suppresses next in window, unmount cleans up. Adoption at 3 sites: web/src/components/ui/search-bar.tsx onDebouncedChange emit now via useDebouncedCallback (replaces inline setTimeout/clearTimeout), use-meeting-classify-preview.ts 250ms classify GET now keys off useDebounce(newTask, 250), use-meeting-preview-plan.ts 400ms plan POST now keys off useDebounce(newTask, 400) + useDebounce(newTrack, 400). 48/48 affected tests pass. Bumped web/package.json 1.11.229 -> 1.11.230. CHANGELOG.md entry. docs/patches/11.212-ui-debounce-hooks.md (88 lines). |
| 11.213 | UI: dropdown-menu focus-cycle adoption | done | SHIPPED v1.11.231 (auto-w197, merged 9fb3e82). web/src/components/ui/dropdown-menu.tsx refactored: inline ArrowUp/Down/Home/End/wrap logic replaced with useFocusCycle hook (from 11.205). Hook config: containerRef=menuRef pointing at menu div, itemSelector='[role=menuitem]:not([aria-disabled=true])', orientation='vertical', wrap=true, onSelect callback mirrors focus into highlight + aria-activedescendant. | 11.222 | UI: useViewportSize hook | done | SHIPPED v1.11.240 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto, merged via --no-ff). web/src/hooks/use-viewport-size.ts (105 lines): useViewportSize() returns { width, height, isMobile (<768), isTablet (768-1024), isDesktop (>1024) }. SSR-safe (typeof window guard, desktop-sized fallback 1280x800 so hydration does not flash a mobile layout). Resize throttling: single requestAnimationFrame coalesces bursts of resize events into one setState flush. Bucket-identity preservation: when width/height + boolean trio are unchanged the prior object reference is returned so downstream useMemo/dep arrays stay quiet. Mount-time flush() re-syncs in case the SSR fallback was the initial state or innerWidth changed between render and effect tick. Three inline (min-width: 768px) checks replaced: web/src/App.tsx line 49 (sidebarOpen default -> !viewport.isMobile), App.tsx line 92 (handleSelect close-on-mobile -> viewport.isMobile), web/src/lib/use-sidebar-shortcut.ts line 30 (Ctrl+B / Cmd+B desktop/mobile branch reads via isMobileRef so the keydown listener always sees the latest snapshot without effect reattach). use-viewport-size.test.ts (208 lines, 10 vitest cases all pass): boundary classification at 767/768/1024/1025, mobile->tablet->desktop transitions, rAF coalescing of three resize events into one flush, removeEventListener + cancelAnimationFrame on unmount, reference-identity preservation. use-sidebar-shortcut.test.ts harness switched from matchMedia stub to direct innerWidth stubbing; 15/15 cases still pass. ARPS design tokens (--bp-tablet: 640, --bp-desktop: 1024) noted in hook doc-comment + CHANGELOG for a future alignment pass once legacy 768 callers are gone. | 11.225 | UI: skeleton + spinner motion harmony | done | SHIPPED v1.11.243 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto, merged via --no-ff). web/src/components/ui/loading-motion.ts (97 lines): shared duration + easing contract for the two loading-state animations. skeleton = 1800ms / cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1) (ARPS standard easing -- ease-in, gentler ease-out; replaces Tailwind's animate-pulse default 2s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.6, 1) so the shimmer settles between pulses). spinner = 1200ms / linear (slower than Tailwind's animate-spin default 1s so the spinner completes 1.5 rotations per shimmer pulse -- 3:2 period ratio gives the two indicators a felt rhythm without locking into a 1:1 strobe). Both durations are clean multiples of 200ms (the smallest tweenable unit on a 60Hz display) so they line up with the rest of the ARPS motion scale (--duration-fast 160ms / --duration-normal 240ms / --duration-slow 360ms / --duration-deliberate 600ms). Helpers: getLoadingMotionStyle(kind, reduced) returns inline { animationDuration, animationTimingFunction } or empty object; getLoadingMotionClass(kind, reduced) returns 'animate-pulse'/'animate-spin' or empty string. Reduced-motion respected end-to-end: both Spinner.tsx and skeleton.tsx (top-level + every shape sub-component: TextLine/Rect/Circle/AvatarShape + Skeleton.Text/Avatar/Card/Table compounds + StatCardShape/TableRowShape via Rect) read useReducedMotion() and drop the animation utility + clear inline style when reduce is preferred. data-motion-reduced="" attribute lands on the animated element in that branch so tests + browser devtools can verify the gate without resorting to class introspection. DesignSystem.tsx gets a new LoadingMotionDemo block in the Display category with the two primitives side-by-side and their duration/easing values surfaced as code chips + a description bar that advertises the unified contract + the reduced-motion fallback; replaces the prior MissingPrimitive name="Spinner" placeholder. Test coverage: 10 unit cases in loading-motion.test.ts lock the constants + 3:2 period ratio + 200ms tweenable invariant + per-kind class + reduced-motion empty-style/empty-class. 8 cross-cutting integration cases in loading-motion-integration.test.tsx verify the contract end-to-end (animate-pulse + animate-spin classes + inline animationDuration/animationTimingFunction are present when motion is allowed, dropped + replaced by data-motion-reduced when reduce is preferred, across Spinner + Skeleton + every shape sub-component). Existing 70+ skeleton/Spinner unit suites stay green untouched (jsdom default matchMedia undefined -> useReducedMotion returns false -> previous animate-* class assertions still hold). Bumped package.json 1.11.242 -> 1.11.243 and web/package.json 1.11.242 -> 1.11.243 along with both lockfiles. CHANGELOG.md [1.11.243] entry. |
| 11.226 | UI: image perf tweaks | done | SHIPPED v1.11.244 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto, merged via --no-ff). web/src/components/ui/image.tsx -- 4 perf knobs added to the existing Image primitive without breaking any caller. (1) decoding prop (default 'async') pins <img decoding> so the browser decodes the bitmap off the main thread regardless of engine defaults; (2) loading attribute derived from the existing lazy prop (lazy -> 'lazy', else 'eager') acts as a belt-and-braces fallback alongside the IntersectionObserver gate so a user-agent without our observer still benefits from the native lazy-loader; (3) srcSet + sizes props forwarded verbatim to the underlying <img> so responsive surfaces offer multiple resolutions without wrapping in <picture> (omitting both drops the attributes from the DOM, no empty-string regression); (4) rootMargin prop (default '200px', up from the prior hard-coded '50px') starts the fetch once the placeholder is within 200px of the viewport so a fast scroll does not strand the user on a blank slot, configurable per surface ('0px' disables the lookahead entirely). web/src/components/ui/avatar.tsx -- widens AvatarProps with srcSet/sizes forwarded to the underlying Image so DPR-aware avatars ('avatar@1x.png 1x, avatar@2x.png 2x') work without a <picture> wrapper; default decoding="async" + loading="eager" pair inherited automatically. Test coverage: image.test.tsx +8 cases (decoding default + override, loading lazy + eager, srcSet + sizes pass-through, attribute omission when props not provided, IntersectionObserver rootMargin default + override -- FakeIO now captures the init arg in addition to the callback). avatar.test.tsx +2 cases (inherited decoding/loading pair, srcSet/sizes forwarding for DPR avatars). Existing image + avatar suites stay green: image 23/23 + avatar 21/21 = 44/44 pass. Bumped package.json 1.11.243 -> 1.11.244 and web/package.json 1.11.243 -> 1.11.244 along with both lockfiles. CHANGELOG.md [1.11.244] entry. |
| 11.227 | UI: scrollbar responsive width | done | SHIPPED v1.11.245 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto, merged via --no-ff). web/src/index.css -- .c4-scroll auto-sizes off pointer-coarseness: desktop (fine pointer) keeps the prior 8px/10px chrome, under @media (pointer: coarse) the thumb bumps to 14px so it stays tap-sized on touch screens without changing desktop look. Three explicit modifier classes pin per-state pairs: .c4-scroll-thin (6/10px), .c4-scroll-default (8/14px), .c4-scroll-wide (12/18px). New .c4-scroll-safe-area modifier adds scroll-padding-{right,bottom} + padding-{right,bottom}: env(safe-area-inset-*) so a region pinned against device chrome (notch/curved corner/gesture bar) keeps trailing content clear of the inset. web/src/components/ui/scroll-area.tsx -- two new props: size? ('auto' | 'thin' | 'default' | 'wide', default 'auto' inherits the pointer-coarse auto-bump) + safeArea? (boolean, default false). Both compose with existing axis + caller className via cn(). Wrapper carries data-scrollarea-size + data-scrollarea-safe-area attributes for browser devtools + screenshot tests. scroll-area.test.tsx +7 cases: size='auto' default emits no modifier, each explicit size applies c4-scroll-* class + data attr, safeArea=true/false toggle class + attr, size + safeArea compose with axis + className. ScrollArea consumers re-verified: ChatMessageLog 9/9, Queue 13/13, Sidebar 24/24, ScrollArea itself 20/20 = 66/66 pass. SpecialistsAuditPanel's 5 pre-existing chip-tone failures (panel migrated to Timeline primitive at v1.11.167 but chip-tone tests still assert old text-emerald-700/text-rose-700 classes) confirmed identical with edits reverted; unrelated to this push. Bumped package.json 1.11.244 -> 1.11.245 and web/package.json 1.11.244 -> 1.11.245 along with both lockfiles. CHANGELOG.md [1.11.245] entry. |
| 11.228 | UI: lazy-route prefetch | done | SHIPPED v1.11.246 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto, merged via --no-ff). web/src/lib/route-prefetch.ts (99 lines): prefetch(loader) is a WeakMap-cached warm-up that calls a () => import(...) once per loader identity, swallows rejections + drops the cache entry so a retry is possible, no-ops under SSR, and ignores non-thenable return values + synchronous throws defensively. prefetchHandlers(loader) returns the canonical { onMouseEnter, onFocus, onTouchStart } triple so a consumer can spread the wiring onto a nav button without re-deriving it. TouchStart fires alongside mouse/focus signals because a touch user never sees mouseenter; firing on press-down warms the chunk during the ~150ms before synthetic-click delivery. web/src/lib/route-loaders.ts (52 lines): single source of truth for the 10 top-level view loaders so the prefetch path and the React.lazy(...) boundaries in App.tsx target the same module URLs (the bundler de-dupes the underlying chunk fetch, and the prefetch cache stays keyed by stable function identity -- an arrow defined inside a render would defeat the WeakMap after every re-render, so the loaders are exported once at module scope). Adoption: web/src/components/ui/tabs.tsx -- primitive grows optional onPrefetch?: (value: string) => void; each tab button wires onMouseEnter + onFocus + onTouchStart to fire with the tab's value, skipping the already-active tab and disabled rows; existing onChange semantics unchanged. web/src/components/layout/TopTabs.tsx -- routes the new prop to prefetch(getTopViewLoader(value)) so a hover/focus/touchstart on any sibling tab warms the lazy chunk before the click commits. web/src/components/ui/dropdown-menu.tsx -- DropdownMenuItem gains optional onPrefetch; the rendered <button> wires it to the existing mouseenter/focus path (disabled rows skip). web/src/components/AccountMenu.tsx -- the Preferences row now warms the SettingsView chunk on hover/focus via the shared SETTINGS_LOADER export. Test coverage: 8 unit cases in route-prefetch.test.ts (loader fires once per identity, distinct loaders cache independently, rejection retry path, synchronous-throw guard, non-thenable guard, prefetchHandlers triple-fire memoization). 4 new cases in tabs.test.tsx (mouseenter/focus fire onPrefetch with the tab value, active tab skipped, disabled tab skipped). Existing tabs + TopTabs + AccountMenu + DropdownMenu suites stay green: Tabs 25/25, plus TopTabs + AccountMenu + DropdownMenu untouched (92/92 = 117/117 across the affected stack). Bumped package.json 1.11.245 -> 1.11.246 and web/package.json 1.11.245 -> 1.11.246 along with both lockfiles. CHANGELOG.md [1.11.246] entry. |
| 11.229 | UI: color-blindness audit | done | SHIPPED v1.11.247 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto, merged via --no-ff). Two pieces. (1) Badge gets per-variant signal icons so colour stops being the sole signal. web/src/components/ui/badge.tsx -- the four signal variants (success/warning/info/error/destructive) now render a leading lucide icon (CheckCircle2/AlertTriangle/Info/XCircle). A colourblind operator distinguishes ok-vs-busy-vs-err via the icon shape, not just the hue. New icon?: ReactNode | false prop overrides the default or opts out (icon={false} preserves the byte-identical pre-1.11.247 shape). Neutral/default/secondary/outline keep their byte-for-byte appearance -- they carry no colour-only signal so the icon-less rendering stays. badge.test.tsx +7 cases (default icon per signal variant, no-icon on the four neutral variants, custom-icon override, icon={false} opt-out). badge.snapshot.test.tsx -- 4 baselines updated for the four signal variants; the three icon-less variants stay byte-identical. (2) Inline colour-vision simulation surface so a designer can audit any subtree without leaving the app. web/src/components/dev/ColorBlindFilters.tsx (75 lines) renders an inline <svg><defs> block with three named filters (#cb-protanopia, #cb-deuteranopia, #cb-tritanopia). Matrices are the canonical Machado et al. 2009 approximations at full dichromacy -- if a tone pair survives full deficiency it survives every milder anomaly. web/src/index.css gains .cb-protanopia / .cb-deuteranopia / .cb-tritanopia wrapper classes referencing filter ids via filter: url(#cb-*); filter is purely visual (DOM/a11y tree unchanged). DesignSystem.tsx grows ColorBlindAuditDemo block in Display category: 4 rows (Normal/Protanopia/Deuteranopia/Tritanopia) wrap the canonical 8-tone tag palette + the four signal Badge variants -- the baked-in icons read identically in every row, proving the secondary-signal layer survives every dichromacy. ColorBlindFilters.test.tsx +6 cases (stable cb-* ids, aria-hidden + zero-sized SVG, exact Machado matrix values, custom idPrefix override for multi-mount, 4x5=20 finite-numeric-entries invariant). Consumer regressions verified on the three biggest Badge sites: SpecialistsList 21/21, RiskCheckResult 24/24, ConversationView 31/31 = 76/76. Bumped package.json 1.11.246 -> 1.11.247 and web/package.json 1.11.246 -> 1.11.247 along with both lockfiles. CHANGELOG.md [1.11.247] entry. |

The page renders a filterable list of ADR cards -- each card has a status Badge, date + version chips, and three subsections (Context/Decision/Consequences). Filter input substring-matches across id/title/status/context/decision/consequences/version with a match counter + empty-state copy. Data source split: docs/decisions.md (repo root, canonical human-readable doc with the same 5 entries -- source of truth for prose edits) + web/src/pages/decision-log-entries.ts (99 lines, typed mirror that the page imports). Lockstep noted; follow-up may collapse via Vite raw-import once docs/ moves inside web/ or server.fs.allow is tweaked. Registered in web/src/pages/registry.ts under category 'config' as 'decision-log' with the History lucide icon. i18n labels added for both en + ko bundles (feature.decisionLog.label / feature.decisionLog.description). | 11.231 | UI: uptime + status page | done | SHIPPED v1.11.249 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto, merged via --no-ff). web/src/pages/Uptime.tsx (228 lines) -- Diagnostics page (the "Health group" the brief refers to) with three cards. (1) Daemon process card: pid / version / startedAt / uptime duration via the existing useHealth() hook (inherits 10s self-poll + refresh contract). (2) Restart counter card: operator-local count via new useDaemonRestartTracker() hook (localStorage-backed, bumps when pid OR startedAt changes between polls; first contact records firstSeen but does NOT count; SSR-safe; quota/private-mode failures fall back to in-memory state). (3) Recent incidents card: last 5 halt/dispatch-error rows from /api/autonomous/status recent[] plus reviewer escalations from escalations[], merged + sorted newest-first + capped at 5 via the new pure toIncidents(payload) helper. Halt + dispatch-error use destructive Badge variant; escalations use warning -- the signal-icon layer added in v1.11.247 reads in every row. Empty state ("All clear") when the loop has been healthy. Refresh action drives both health + incidents fetchers. Two new lib hooks: web/src/lib/use-autonomous-incidents.ts (144 lines, exposes toIncidents pure helper + useAutonomousIncidents 30s polling wrapper); web/src/lib/use-daemon-restart-tracker.ts (144 lines, localStorage-backed counter with _resetDaemonRestartTracker test helper). Registered in web/src/pages/registry.ts under category diagnostics as 'uptime' with the Activity lucide icon. i18n labels added for both en + ko bundles (feature.uptime.label / feature.uptime.description). Test coverage: 21 new cases (7 incidents: empty payload, success/dispatch skip, recent[] extraction, escalation extraction, merge+sort+cap, reason fallback, null id; 8 restart tracker: zero state, first-contact no-bump rule, pid-change bump, startedAt-change bump, no-op same-pair, sequence count, persistence across remount, persisted+restart on remount; 6 page: title/description, daemon-process card pid+version, restart counter initial 0, All-clear empty-state, populated incident list with newest-first ordering, 5-row cap). All 21 pass. Pre-existing failures unchanged (FeatureSidebar.test.tsx > filter list, i18n-keys.test.ts > t() keys self-match); confirmed earlier and out of scope.
