| 11.536 | UI: chart-line-pareto primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.554 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-pareto.tsx` ships `<ChartLinePareto>` -- pure-SVG **Pareto chart in line form**, categorical inputs ranked by value descending, rendered as a primary value line against the LEFT axis with a cumulative-percentage overlay against the RIGHT axis. A dashed horizontal reference at the configurable threshold (default 80% -- the canonical 80-20 rule) calls out the threshold, and a dashed vertical "crossover" line marks the rank where the cumulative percentage first reaches the threshold. Vital-few items (those at or before the crossover) are tinted green and the rectangle behind them is shaded; trivial-many items are tinted gray. A vital-few badge in the top-left tells operators "N vital few drive 80%". The canonical "80-20 rule" / "vital few vs trivial many" visualisation used in root-cause analysis, defect prioritisation, inventory ABC analysis, etc. Distinct from `<ChartPareto>` (catalog earlier; bar-form of the Pareto analysis -- this primitive is the LINE-form with the cumulative on a SEPARATE line on a dual axis), `<ChartLineCumulative>` (11.520; simple cumulative SUM rising over time -- Pareto's cumulative is RANKED-descending + NORMALISED to 100% with canonical 80% threshold + vital-few crossover), `<ChartLineTarget>` (11.518; vs arbitrary user target -- Pareto's reference is the standardized 80% rule with built-in vital-few identification), `<ChartLineThreshold>` (11.503; arbitrary user-defined horizontal thresholds + zones -- Pareto's threshold is the canonical Pareto rule with semantic vital-few/trivial-many tinting), `<ChartLineBaseline>` (11.510; single flat reference -- Pareto has TWO coordinated references plus the dual cumulative overlay), and `<ChartLineSlope>` (catalog earlier; connects two categorical points -- Pareto handles N ranked categories with cumulative tracking). API: `data: readonly ChartLineParetoItem[]` (`{category, value}` with `value >= 0`; non-finite / negative / non-string category dropped); `threshold` (default 80; clamped to [0, 100]); `valueColor` / `cumulativeColor` / `thresholdColor` / `vitalColor` / `trivialColor`; `thresholdDashArray` (default '6 4') / `crossoverDashArray` (default '4 3'); `fillOpacity` (default 0.12); toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots`/`showLegend`/`showTooltip`/`showVitalFewBadge`/`showVitalFewFill`/`showCrossover`/`showThresholdLine`/`animate`; standard sizing / format / a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatPercent` / `formatCategory`; `xLabel` / `yLabel` / `percentLabel` (right axis) / `valueLabel` / `cumulativeLabel`; `onPointClick({point})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_PARETO_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/CUMULATIVE_STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/THRESHOLD/THRESHOLD_DASH/CROSSOVER_DASH/FILL_OPACITY/VALUE_COLOR/CUMULATIVE_COLOR/THRESHOLD_COLOR/VITAL_COLOR/TRIVIAL_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineParetoFiniteItems` (filters to finite non-negative + string category); `normaliseLineParetoThreshold` (clamps to [0, 100]; non-finite -> 80); `rankLineParetoItems(items, threshold?)` -- canonical Pareto ranking helper; sorts by value descending; assigns rank (0-based); computes cumulativeValue, cumulativePercent (0-100), share (per-item percent), and stratum ('vital' | 'trivial'); vital classification: items whose cumulative percent (inclusive) is `<= threshold` are vital, the FIRST item that crosses the threshold is also vital (predecessor was below threshold so removing it would drop cumulative below threshold), subsequent items are trivial; threshold <= 0 -> all trivial; preserves originalIndex from input array; `findLineParetoCrossover(ranked, threshold?)` -- returns `{rank, cumulativePercent}` of first item reaching threshold; empty -> `{-1, 0}`; threshold <= 0 -> rank 0; never reached -> last rank; `computeLineParetoLayout({data, ...})` -- returns `{ok, ..., ranked, valuePath, cumulativePath, vitalFillPath, thresholdPy, crossoverRank, crossoverPx, vitalFewCount, trivialManyCount, finiteCount, totalCount, totalValue, threshold}`; dual y-axes span `[0, maxValue]` LEFT and `[0, 100]%` RIGHT sharing same inner panel; per-point carries `{rank, category, value, px, valuePy, cumulativePy, cumulativeValue, cumulativePercent, share, stratum, originalIndex}`; degenerate canvas / empty data -> ok=false; vitalFillPath is a rectangle from padding to crossoverPx; `describeLineParetoChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-pareto-aria-desc"` span; value path + cumulative path + threshold line + every value dot `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; cumulative dots decorative (role=graphics-symbol + aria-hidden); crossover vertical `role="graphics-symbol"` with aria-label including rank; dot aria-label includes category + rank + value + cumulative + stratum. data-section on every node (chart-line-pareto / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -x-label / -y-label / -percent-label / -value-path / -cumulative-path / -threshold / -crossover / -vital-fill / -point-group / -dot / -tooltip / -tooltip-label / -tooltip-rank / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-cumulative / -tooltip-stratum / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-total / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-count / -badge-label); root mirrors `data-total-points`, `data-vital-few-count`, `data-trivial-many-count`, `data-crossover-rank`, `data-threshold`, `data-total-value`, `data-animate`; value path `data-kind="value"`; cumulative path `data-kind="cumulative"`; dots `data-kind` ('value' / 'cumulative') + `data-rank`, `data-category`, `data-value`, `data-cumulative-percent`, `data-share`, `data-stratum`. Tooltip surfaces category in value-color, `rank: <n>`, bold `value: <v> (share <p>%)`, `cumulative: <p>%`, and a coloured `vital few` / `trivial many` row. Vital-few badge in the top-left calls out the count with ★ icon + threshold label. Legend has value (solid swatch) + cumulative (solid swatch) + vital (solid swatch with count) items + total items / total value. 65 vitest cases (defaults, helpers incl. rankLineParetoItems descending sort + originalIndex + cumulative reaches 100 + classifies vital-few incl. boundary crosser + boundary crosser vital when previous below + threshold=0 all trivial + flat distribution + share sums to 100, findLineParetoCrossover empty / first reaching / last when never reached / threshold=0, computeLineParetoLayout empty / degenerate / rank order / vital count / crossover rank / value + cumulative paths / vital-few fill / no fill at threshold=0 / threshold py monotonic with threshold value / valueMin/valueMax overrides / drops non-finite / totalValue / crossover px > padding / per-point stratum / percent ticks, describeLineParetoChart No data + summary, component render incl. empty / value path kind=value / cumulative path kind=cumulative / dashed threshold + hide via prop / crossover vertical + hide / vital-few fill + hide / badge + hide / value + cumulative dots per rank / value dot data-stratum + cumulative-percent + share / hide dots / ARIA / root data-* / tooltip rows incl. vital / hide on leave + omit via prop / onPointClick payload / legend value + cumulative + vital items + omit / animate flag + class / vital color default for vital dot / ref / displayName) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for the new files. Exported via components/ui barrel. Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/c4-mgr-auto. Worker auto-w518. Bump 1.11.554. |
| 11.537 | UI: chart-line-bollinger primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.555 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-bollinger.tsx` ships `<ChartLineBollinger>` -- pure-SVG **Bollinger Bands** technical indicator (John Bollinger, 1980s). Each series renders against three coordinated **rolling** rules driven by a sliding window of size `window` (default 20): the middle band is the rolling SMA (dashed) and the upper / lower bands are at `SMA +- kSigma * rolling_std` (default k = 2 sigma, the canonical Bollinger configuration). The region between upper and lower is shaded with the band color, and points that pierce the bands ("breakouts") are tinted green when above or red when below at a slightly larger radius. A breakout badge in the top-left tells operators how many points sit outside the envelope. The canonical trader's volatility indicator -- showing where "normal" lies given RECENT history, so breakouts mean a regime shift, not just deviation from a global mean. Distinct from `<ChartLineConfidence>` (11.502; per-point upper/lower bounds supplied EXTERNALLY -- Bollinger DERIVES the bounds statistically from a ROLLING window), `<ChartLineMovingAvg>` (11.513; SMA overlay only -- Bollinger adds the +/- kSigma envelope around the SMA), `<ChartLinePercentile>` (11.514; per-x-bucket distribution percentile bands requiring multi-sample input -- Bollinger uses single-sample input with rolling stats), `<ChartLineAnomaly>` (11.524; per-point z-score outliers with optional rolling window -- Bollinger surfaces the BANDS as a visible envelope and tags every breakout as part of a continuous signal), and `<ChartLineControl>` (11.534; SPC chart with GLOBAL mean +/- k*sigma over the whole series -- Bollinger is ROLLING, so the bands track LOCAL volatility and the same threshold means different things in different regimes). Single-spike breakout math: for sliding window N + k sigma, a single outlier breaks out iff `sqrt(N-1) > k` -- with canonical k=2 that means N > 5. API: `series: readonly ChartLineBollingerSeries[]` (`{id, label, data, color?, window?, kSigma?, bandColor?}`; per-series window/kSigma always beats chart-level); `window` (default 20; clamped to >= 2); `kSigma` (default 2; clamped to > 0); `breakoutUpColor` / `breakoutDownColor` / `insideColor`; `bandColor`; `bandOpacity` (default 0.16); `middleDashArray` (default '4 3'); toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots`/`showLegend`/`showTooltip`/`showBreakoutBadge`/`showBand`/`showMiddle`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility; `formatValue` / `formatX` / `formatPercent`; `onPointClick({series, point})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_BOLLINGER_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/BAND_STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/WINDOW/K_SIGMA/BAND_OPACITY/MIDDLE_DASH/BAND_COLOR/BREAKOUT_UP_COLOR/BREAKOUT_DOWN_COLOR/INSIDE_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR/PALETTE`; `getLineBollingerDefaultColor` (10-color cycle); `getLineBollingerFinitePoints`; `normaliseLineBollingerWindow` (clamps >= 2; floors fractional; non-finite -> 20); `normaliseLineBollingerKSigma` (clamps > 0; non-finite / non-positive -> 2); `computeRollingMean(values, window)` -- per-index rolling mean over trailing window samples; first window-1 entries null; non-finite excluded from sum; `computeRollingStd(values, window, means?)` -- per-index rolling POPULATION std; first window-1 entries null; reuses pre-computed means when supplied; `classifyLineBollingerState(y, upper, lower)` -- `'inside' | 'above' | 'below'`; bands null -> inside; non-finite y -> inside; `computeLineBollingerBands(points, {window?, kSigma?})` -- canonical Bollinger helper; sorts ascending; drops non-finite; returns per-point `{index, x, y, middle, upper, lower, sigma, bandwidth, percentB, state}` where `bandwidth = (upper - lower) / |middle|` (null when middle=0 or non-finite) and `percentB = (y - lower) / (upper - lower)` (null when bands collapse); first window-1 entries have null bands; `computeLineBollingerLayout({series, ...})` -- returns `{series, xTicks, yTicks, xMin, xMax, yMin, yMax, innerWidth, innerHeight, totalPoints, visibleSeriesCount}`; y range expanded to cover bands; per-series carries `{id, label, color, bandColor, window, kSigma, points, path, middlePath, upperPath, lowerPath, bandPath, finiteCount, totalCount, bandValidCount, aboveCount, belowCount, insideCount, latestPercentB, latestBandwidth, latestState}`; per-point carries projected `{...sample, px, py, middlePy, upperPy, lowerPy}`; paths built as contiguous-run segmented SVG paths so null gaps preserve correct line continuity; degenerate canvas / empty / all-hidden -> empty layout; `describeLineBollingerChart(series, options?)` -- ARIA summary (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-bollinger-aria-desc"` span; upper / middle / lower band lines `role="graphics-symbol"` with descriptive aria-label (including window + k); price path + every dot `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; dot aria-label includes state and %B when available. data-section on every node (chart-line-bollinger / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -x-label / -y-label / -series / -series-group / -band / -band-line / -path / -dot / -tooltip / -tooltip-label / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-y / -tooltip-middle / -tooltip-upper / -tooltip-lower / -tooltip-state / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -legend-total-points / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-count / -badge-label); root mirrors `data-series-count`, `data-visible-series-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-window`, `data-k-sigma`, `data-breakout-count`, `data-dominant-state`, `data-animate`; price path `data-kind="price"`; band lines `data-kind` ('upper'/'middle'/'lower'); dots `data-middle`, `data-upper`, `data-lower`, `data-percent-b`, `data-bandwidth`, `data-state`; series groups `data-series-id`, `data-series-color`, `data-series-band-color`, `data-series-window`, `data-series-k-sigma`, `data-series-finite-count`, `data-series-band-valid-count`, `data-series-above-count`, `data-series-below-count`, `data-series-inside-count`, `data-series-latest-state`. Tooltip surfaces label, x, bold y, three reference rows (`middle`, `upper`, `lower` each n/a when window unfilled), and a coloured `inside bands` / `breakout above` / `breakout below` row tinted by state with the point's %B. Sign badge in top-left calls out total breakout count with icon ▲/▼/◆ and dominant state. Legend shows per-series `BB(window, k); N breakouts`. 71 vitest cases (defaults, helpers incl. rolling mean + std + classify state + computeLineBollingerBands incl. leading nulls / breakout long-window verified fixture / %B / bandwidth positive / bandwidth null when middle=0 / sort ascending, computeLineBollingerLayout incl. per-series band paths / counts / y range expands / projected pys null leading / hidden drop / bounds overrides / window override / latest state / empty band path for short series, describeLineBollingerChart No data + window + k + latest %B, component render incl. empty / price kind=price / upper+middle+lower band lines + middle dashed / band polygon + hide / hide middle / dots with state attrs / inside dots state=inside / hide dots / breakout badge dominant + omit / ARIA / root data-* / group data-* / tooltip middle+upper+lower rows + n/a for leading + hide on leave + omit via prop / onPointClick payload / legend BB(window, k) + breakouts + toggle + omit / animate flag + class / ref / displayName) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. | 11.538 | UI: chart-line-kalman primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.556 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-kalman.tsx` ships `<ChartLineKalman>` -- pure-SVG **1D Kalman filter** smoothing overlay. Each series takes noisy observations `{x, y}`, runs a one-dimensional random-walk Kalman filter forward, and renders three coordinated elements on the same axes: the dimmed raw observations line, the smoothed estimate line in the series color, and an uncertainty band at the posterior `+/- kSigma * sqrt(P_k)`. Per-point dots are tinted along a gradient from `gainLowColor` (low gain -- filter trusting its model) to `gainHighColor` (high gain -- filter trusting the measurement). A Kalman-gain badge in the top-left calls out the final gain of the series with the strongest absolute gain. Filter equations per step k: **Predict**: x_pred = x_{k-1}, P_pred = P_{k-1} + Q; **Gain**: K = P_pred / (P_pred + R); **Update**: x_k = x_pred + K * (z_k - x_pred), P_k = (1 - K) * P_pred. The canonical recursive-Bayesian smoother used in sensor fusion, signal tracking, and any context where the question is "what is the underlying state given noisy observations?". Distinct from `<ChartLineMovingAvg>` (11.513; linear SMA smoothing with fixed window -- Kalman is recursive Bayesian filtering with posterior covariance that adapts each step), `<ChartLineConfidence>` (11.502; per-point bounds EXTERNAL -- Kalman's band is DERIVED from the filter's posterior variance P_k), `<ChartLineBollinger>` (11.537; rolling SMA +/- k*rolling_std over fixed window -- Kalman is recursive and depends on all past samples through Q/R model, not windowed), `<ChartLineControl>` (11.534; global mean +/- k*sigma SPC -- Kalman bounds are per-step adaptive shrinking as filter gains certainty), `<ChartLineAnomaly>` (11.524; per-point z-score outliers -- Kalman tracks the optimal Bayesian estimate; innovation is exposed per-point so callers can build their own outlier rules), and `<ChartLineForecast>` (11.519; historical/forecast split on time axis -- Kalman smooths observed data without splitting timeline). Filter behaviour: with **Q = 0** the posterior variance collapses asymptotically toward 0 and gain approaches 0 (filter becomes running average); with **Q > 0** the variance reaches non-zero steady state and gain stabilises depending on Q/R ratio; **innovation** `obs - predicted` is exposed per-point; when `initialEstimate` is unspecified the filter seeds with the first observation so `innovation[0] = 0` and first estimate equals first observation regardless of gain. API: `series: readonly ChartLineKalmanSeries[]` (`{id, label, data, color?, processNoise?, measurementNoise?, initialEstimate?, initialVariance?, kSigma?}`; per-series Q/R/P_0/x_0/k always beats chart-level defaults); `processNoise` Q (default 0.01); `measurementNoise` R (default 1); `initialEstimate` x_0 (defaults to first observation per-series); `initialVariance` P_0 (default 1); `kSigma` (default 2); `gainHighColor` / `gainLowColor` (per-point dot gradient endpoints); `obsColor`; `bandColor`; `bandOpacity` (default 0.18); `obsOpacity` (default 0.55); toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots`/`showLegend`/`showTooltip`/`showGainBadge`/`showBand`/`showObservations`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility; `formatValue` / `formatX` / `formatGain`; `onPointClick({series, point})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_KALMAN_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/OBS_STROKE_WIDTH/EST_STROKE_WIDTH/BAND_STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PROCESS_NOISE/MEASUREMENT_NOISE/INITIAL_VARIANCE/K_SIGMA/BAND_OPACITY/OBS_OPACITY/PALETTE/OBS_COLOR/BAND_COLOR/GAIN_HIGH_COLOR/GAIN_LOW_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineKalmanDefaultColor`, `getLineKalmanFinitePoints`, `normaliseLineKalmanNoise` (non-finite / negative -> fallback; zero allowed), `normaliseLineKalmanKSigma` (non-finite / non-positive -> 2), `runLineKalmanFilter(points, options?)` (canonical filter pass; sorts ascending; drops non-finite; returns per-point `{index, x, observation, predicted, predictedVariance, estimate, variance, gain, innovation, upper, lower}`; assumes random-walk process model `x_k = x_{k-1} + w`; empty / null -> []), `computeLineKalmanLayout({series, ...})` -- returns `{series, xTicks, yTicks, xMin, xMax, yMin, yMax, innerWidth, innerHeight, totalPoints, visibleSeriesCount}`; y range expanded to cover upper/lower band; per-series carries `{id, label, color, processNoise, measurementNoise, initialVariance, initialEstimate, kSigma, points, obsPath, estPath, bandPath, finiteCount, totalCount, meanGain, maxGain, minGain, finalVariance, finalEstimate, finalGain, rmseObservation}`; per-point carries projected `{..., px, obsPy, estPy, upperPy, lowerPy}`; degenerate canvas / empty / all-hidden -> empty layout; `describeLineKalmanChart(series, options?)` -- ARIA summary (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-kalman-aria-desc"` span; observation path + estimate path + every dot `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; dot aria-label includes observation, estimate, and gain. data-section on every node (chart-line-kalman / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -x-label / -y-label / -series / -series-group / -band / -obs-path / -est-path / -dot / -tooltip / -tooltip-label / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-observation / -tooltip-estimate / -tooltip-variance / -tooltip-gain / -tooltip-innovation / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -legend-total-points / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-value / -badge-label); root mirrors `data-series-count`, `data-visible-series-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-process-noise`, `data-measurement-noise`, `data-initial-variance`, `data-k-sigma`, `data-dominant-gain`, `data-animate`; obs path `data-kind="observation"`; est path `data-kind="estimate"`; dots `data-observation`, `data-estimate`, `data-variance`, `data-gain`, `data-innovation`; series groups `data-series-id`, `data-series-color`, `data-series-process-noise`, `data-series-measurement-noise`, `data-series-initial-variance`, `data-series-k-sigma`, `data-series-finite-count`, `data-series-mean-gain`, `data-series-max-gain`, `data-series-min-gain`, `data-series-final-gain`, `data-series-final-estimate`, `data-series-final-variance`, `data-series-rmse`. Tooltip surfaces label, x, `obs` row, bold `est` row, `var` row, gain-coloured `gain` row, and `innov` row (per-point innovation). Sign badge in top-left calls out final gain with icon `K`. Legend shows `Q <q>, R <r>; gain <mean gain>` per series. 60 vitest cases (defaults, helpers, runLineKalmanFilter incl. first estimate moves from initial toward observation by gain (verified Kalman math at Q=0, R=1, P_0=1 -> K=0.5, est=5) / gain decreases when prior variance shrinks at Q=0 / Q>0 makes gain converge to non-zero steady state / constant observations converge to observed value / initial estimate falls back to first observation when unspecified / upper/lower bands from k*sqrt(variance) / sort ascending / drops non-finite / innovation is obs - predicted, computeLineKalmanLayout empty / degenerate / per-series obs+est+band paths / gain stats / RMSE / y range expanded / hidden / bounds / per-series noise override / visibleSeriesCount + total / per-point projected pys diverge after first sample, describeLineKalmanChart No data + Q + R + final est + gain, component render incl. empty / obs path kind=observation / est path kind=estimate / uncertainty band + hide / hide obs path / dots with gain + variance + innovation + estimate + observation attrs / hide dots / gain badge + omit / ARIA / root data-* / group data-* / tooltip obs + est + variance + gain + innovation rows + hide on leave + omit via prop / onPointClick payload / legend Q + R + mean gain + toggle + omit / animate flag + class / ref / displayName) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for the new files. Exported via components/ui barrel. Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/c4-mgr-auto. Worker auto-w520. Bump 1.11.556. |
| 11.539 | UI: chart-line-ewma primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.557 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-ewma.tsx` ships `<ChartLineEwma>` -- pure-SVG **exponentially-weighted moving average (EWMA)** overlay using the canonical recursive formula `ewma[k] = alpha * raw[k] + (1 - alpha) * ewma[k-1]`. Renders three coordinated elements on the same axes: the dimmed raw observations line, the smoothed EWMA line in the series color, and (when enabled) per-point residual sticks linking raw to smoothed colored by sign (green when raw > ewma, red when raw < ewma). A half-life badge in the top-left calls out the canonical interpretation: `ln(2) / -ln(1 - alpha)` is the number of samples back that hold half the smoothing weight. The canonical recursive smoother used in pandas (`Series.ewm`), monitoring (RRDtool's exponential averaging, many Prometheus recording rules), and any context where the question is "what is the running level of this signal with a specific time-scale of memory?". Distinct from `<ChartLineMovingAvg>` (11.513; SMA with fixed window of equal weights -- EWMA gives EXPONENTIALLY DECREASING weights over ALL past samples with no window), `<ChartLineBollinger>` (11.537; rolling SMA + envelope -- EWMA is recursive and uses all past samples with exponential decay), `<ChartLineKalman>` (11.538; recursive Bayesian filter with separate process and measurement noise variances -- EWMA is DETERMINISTIC with a single parameter, no uncertainty model, no posterior covariance), `<ChartLineControl>` (11.534; SPC global mean+/-k*sigma -- EWMA is a smoother, not a control rule), and `<ChartLineTrend>` (11.512; one global linear regression line per series -- EWMA is per-point recursive). Behaviour: alpha=1 -> no smoothing (ewma==raw at every step); alpha->0 -> infinite memory (ewma approaches the initial estimate, never updates); first sample's residual is 0 when `initialEstimate` defaults to the first observation; span and alpha are interchangeable (passing `span=9` is identical to passing `alpha=0.2` within float precision). API: `series: readonly ChartLineEwmaSeries[]` (`{id, label, data, color?, alpha?, span?, initialEstimate?, ewmaDashArray?}`; per-series overrides always beat chart-level); `alpha` (default 0.3; clamped to (0, 1]); `span` (alternative parameterization `alpha = 2/(span+1)`; used only when alpha not explicit); `initialEstimate` (defaults to first observation per-series); `rawColor`; `residualPosColor` / `residualNegColor`; `ewmaDashArray`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots`/`showLegend`/`showTooltip`/`showHalfLifeBadge`/`showRaw`/`showResidualSticks`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility; `formatValue` / `formatX` / `formatAlpha`; `onPointClick({series, point})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_EWMA_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/RAW_STROKE_WIDTH/EWMA_STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/ALPHA/RAW_OPACITY/RESIDUAL_OPACITY/EWMA_DASH/PALETTE/RAW_COLOR/RESIDUAL_POS_COLOR/RESIDUAL_NEG_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineEwmaDefaultColor` (10-color cycle); `getLineEwmaFinitePoints`; `normaliseLineEwmaAlpha` (clamps to (0, 1]; non-finite -> 0.3); `lineEwmaSpanToAlpha(span)` -- `2 / (span + 1)`; non-positive / non-finite -> default; `lineEwmaAlphaToSpan(alpha)` -- `2 / alpha - 1`; degenerate (alpha <= 0 or alpha >= 1) -> Infinity; `lineEwmaHalfLife(alpha)` -- `ln(2) / -ln(1 - alpha)`; alpha=0 -> Infinity; alpha=1 -> 0; `classifyLineEwmaResidual(residual)` -- `'positive' | 'negative' | 'zero'`; non-finite -> zero; `resolveLineEwmaAlpha(options?)` -- explicit alpha beats span; default when neither; `runLineEwma(points, options?)` -- canonical EWMA pass; sorts ascending; drops non-finite; returns per-point `{index, x, raw, ewma, residual, residualSign}`; first sample residual is 0 when initialEstimate falls back to first observation; empty / null -> []; `computeLineEwmaLayout({series, ...})` -- returns `{series, xTicks, yTicks, xMin, xMax, yMin, yMax, innerWidth, innerHeight, totalPoints, visibleSeriesCount}`; y range covers both raw and ewma; per-series carries `{id, label, color, alpha, effectiveSpan, halfLife, initialEstimate, ewmaDashArray, points, rawPath, ewmaPath, residualSegments, finiteCount, totalCount, finalEwma, rmseResidual, positiveResidualCount, negativeResidualCount, zeroResidualCount, maxAbsResidual}`; per-point carries projected `{..., px, rawPy, ewmaPy}`; degenerate canvas / empty / all-hidden -> empty layout; `describeLineEwmaChart(series, options?)` -- ARIA summary (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-ewma-aria-desc"` span; raw path + EWMA path + every dot `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; EWMA aria-label includes alpha + half-life; dot aria-label includes raw + ewma + residual + sign. data-section on every node (chart-line-ewma / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -x-label / -y-label / -series / -series-group / -raw-path / -ewma-path / -residual-stick / -dot / -tooltip / -tooltip-label / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-raw / -tooltip-ewma / -tooltip-residual / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -legend-total-points / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-alpha / -badge-label); root mirrors `data-series-count`, `data-visible-series-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-alpha`, `data-dominant-alpha`, `data-dominant-half-life`, `data-animate`; raw path `data-kind="raw"`; ewma path `data-kind="ewma"`; dots `data-raw`, `data-ewma`, `data-residual`, `data-residual-sign`; residual sticks `data-sign`; series groups `data-series-id`, `data-series-color`, `data-series-alpha`, `data-series-half-life`, `data-series-effective-span`, `data-series-finite-count`, `data-series-positive-residual-count`, `data-series-negative-residual-count`, `data-series-zero-residual-count`, `data-series-max-abs-residual`, `data-series-rmse`, `data-series-final-ewma`. Tooltip surfaces label, x, `raw` row, bold `ewma` row, and a coloured signed `residual` row (with explicit + / - prefix). Half-life badge in top-left calls out dominant series' alpha with icon α + half-life label. Legend shows `α <a>; hl <half-life>` per series. 75 vitest cases (defaults, helpers, runLineEwma incl. canonical formula at alpha=0.5 (init=0, raw=10 -> ewma=5) / flat series converges / observation[0] as initial -> residual[0]=0 / alpha=1 -> ewma==raw / smaller alpha larger RMSE / sort ascending / drops non-finite / sign reflects raw vs ewma / span yields same as derived alpha, span-to-alpha + alpha-to-span round-trip + pandas convention + degenerate Infinity, halfLife at alpha 1/0.5/0 + smaller alpha longer hl, classifyResidual all signs, resolveLineEwmaAlpha alpha-beats-span + span-fallback + default, computeLineEwmaLayout incl. per-series paths + alpha + half-life + effective span / residual counts / RMSE / hidden / bounds / per-series alpha + span override / total + visible counts / per-point pys, describeLineEwmaChart No data + alpha + half-life + final EWMA, component render incl. empty / raw kind=raw / ewma kind=ewma / hide raw / residual sticks when shown + omit by default / dots with raw + ewma + residual + sign attrs / hide dots / half-life badge + omit / ARIA / root data-* / group data-* / tooltip raw + ewma + residual rows + hide on leave + omit via prop / onPointClick payload / legend α + hl + toggle + omit / animate flag + class / ref / displayName) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for the new files. Exported via components/ui barrel. Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/c4-mgr-auto. Worker auto-w521. Bump 1.11.557. |
| 11.540 | UI: chart-line-fft primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.558 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-fft.tsx` ships `<ChartLineFft>` -- pure-SVG chart that pairs a **time-domain line** on the left with a **frequency-domain spectrum** on the right, computed via a direct Discrete Fourier Transform. The component is the catalog's ONLY **frequency-domain** primitive -- the side panel surfaces the dominant cycle (period = 1 / dominant frequency) so operators can spot periodic structure hidden in the raw time series. DFT formula `X[k] = sum y[n] * exp(-2*pi*i*k*n/N)` computed for half-spectrum `k = 0..N/2` (real-valued input has conjugate-symmetric DFT). Frequency at bin k is `k / (N * sampleStep)` where `sampleStep = (xN-1 - x0) / (N-1)` so units match the sample grid. Period at bin k is `1 / frequency[k]`; bin 0 (DC) has infinite period and is excluded from the dominant search by default. Direct DFT is O(N^2) which is fast at chart-scale and avoids the power-of-two length constraint of radix-2 FFT, letting every input length work without padding. Distinct: catalog's ONLY frequency-domain primitive -- distinct from every other smoother / filter: `<ChartLineMovingAvg>` (11.513; SMA), `<ChartLineEwma>` (11.539; exponential), `<ChartLineKalman>` (11.538; recursive Bayesian), `<ChartLineBollinger>` (11.537; rolling envelope), `<ChartLineControl>` (11.534; SPC global) are all time-domain only; also distinct from `<ChartLineMomentum>` (11.533; sub-panel oscillator with DERIVATIVE, not spectrum). API: `series: readonly ChartLineFftSeries[]` (`{id, label, data, color?, detrend?, excludeDc?}`; per-series detrend / excludeDc always beats chart-level); `detrend` (default true) -- subtract mean before computing DFT; `excludeDc` (default true) -- skip bin 0 when picking dominant; `timePanelRatio` (default 0.6; clamped to [0.1, 0.9]); `gap` (default 20); `spectrumAsBars` (default true) -- bars vs line on spectrum panel; `spectrumColor`; `dominantColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots`/`showLegend`/`showTooltip`/`showDominantBadge`/`showSpectrum`/`showDominantMarker`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility; standard sizing / format / a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX` / `formatFrequency` / `formatPeriod` / `formatMagnitude`; `onPointClick({series, point})` (time-domain dots) / `onBinClick({series, bin})` (spectrum bars/dots). Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_FFT_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/TICK_COUNT/TIME_PANEL_RATIO/STROKE_WIDTH/SPECTRUM_BAR_WIDTH_RATIO/DOT_RADIUS/PALETTE/SPECTRUM_COLOR/DOMINANT_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineFftDefaultColor`, `getLineFftFinitePoints`, `normaliseLineFftPanelRatio` (clamps to [0.1, 0.9]), `detrendLineFftValues` (subtracts mean of finite values; non-finite -> 0), `computeLineFftDft(values)` -- direct DFT; returns `{real, imag}[]` of length floor(N/2)+1; non-finite skipped, `computeLineFftSpectrum(points, {detrend?, excludeDc?})` -- canonical spectrum helper; sorts ascending; drops non-finite; optional detrend (default true); excludeDc (default true) controls dominant search; returns `{bins, dominantBin, totalSamples, detrended, excludedDc, meanValue}` with each bin carrying `{k, frequency, period, real, imag, magnitude, normalisedMagnitude}`; period for k=0 is Infinity; empty / single-point -> empty bins + null dominantBin; `findLineFftDominantBin(bins, excludeDc?)` -- max-magnitude bin; excludes DC by default; empty / null -> null; `computeLineFftLayout({series, ...})` -- splits canvas into time panel + spectrum panel; returns `{series, timePanel, spectrumPanel, timeXTicks, timeYTicks, spectrumXTicks, spectrumYTicks, xMin, xMax, yMin, yMax, freqMin, freqMax, magMin, magMax, totalPoints, visibleSeriesCount}`; per-series carries `{id, label, color, detrend, excludeDc, timePoints, timePath, spectrum, spectrumBins, spectrumPath, dominantFrequency, dominantPeriod, dominantMagnitude, finiteCount, totalCount}`; spectrum bins projected to bar geometry `{px, py, barX, barWidth, barHeight, isDominant}`; degenerate canvas / empty / all-hidden -> empty layout; `describeLineFftChart(series, options?)` -- ARIA summary (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-fft-aria-desc"` span; time path + every time dot + every spectrum bar/dot `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; spectrum aria-label includes bin k + frequency + period + magnitude + dominant. data-section on every node (chart-line-fft / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -x-label / -y-label / -spectrum-x-label / -spectrum-y-label / -series / -series-group / -time-path / -time-dot / -spectrum-path / -bin-bar / -bin-dot / -tooltip / -tooltip-label / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-y / -tooltip-frequency / -tooltip-period / -tooltip-magnitude / -tooltip-dominant / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -legend-total-points / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-frequency / -badge-period); `data-panel="time"` or `"spectrum"` on axis/tick nodes; root mirrors `data-series-count`, `data-visible-series-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-detrend`, `data-exclude-dc`, `data-dominant-frequency`, `data-dominant-period`, `data-animate`; time path `data-kind="time"`; spectrum bars `data-bin-k`, `data-frequency`, `data-period`, `data-magnitude`, `data-normalised-magnitude`, `data-dominant`; series groups expose detrend + excludeDc + dominant freq/period/magnitude + bin count + finite count. Tooltip on time-domain dots shows label, x, bold y; on spectrum bins shows label + bin number, frequency, period, bold magnitude, and (when dominant) a coloured "dominant" row. Dominant badge in top-left calls out the strongest series' frequency + period with icon `f`. Legend shows `f=<freq>; T=<period>` per series. 70 vitest cases (defaults, helpers, computeLineFftDft incl. DC = sum, period-4 cosine concentrates at bin 4 with N=16 (canonical verified math), output length N/2+1, computeLineFftSpectrum incl. period-4 cosine detected at frequency 0.25 with period 4, detrend default true + excludeDc default true skips bin 0, two-tone signal picks larger amplitude (period 8), normalisedMag maxes at 1, sort ascending, drops non-finite, records meanValue, findLineFftDominantBin excludes DC default + includes when excludeDc=false, computeLineFftLayout splits canvas + per-series timePath + spectrumBins + dominant mark + bounds overrides + per-series detrend/excludeDc override + total + visible counts + projected bar geometry, describeLineFftChart No data + dominant frequency + period, component render incl. empty / time path kind=time / spectrum bin bars by default / dots when spectrumAsBars=false + spectrum path / hide spectrum / dominant data-dominant=true / time dots 16 + hide / dominant badge with freq + period + omit / ARIA / root data-* / group data-* / tooltip on time dot (x + y) / tooltip on bin bar (f + T + |X| + dominant row) + hide on leave + omit via prop / onPointClick for time dots + onBinClick for spectrum bars / legend f= and T= + toggle + omit / animate flag + class / ref / displayName) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for the new files. Exported via components/ui barrel. Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/c4-mgr-auto. Worker auto-w522. Bump 1.11.558. |
| 11.541 | UI: chart-line-resample primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.559 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-resample.tsx` ships `<ChartLineResample>` -- pure-SVG line chart with a runtime **downsample / upsample toggle** for handling dense (or sparse) data. Renders the raw series as a dimmed reference + the resampled series in the series color on top, plus a 5-button mode toggle (`LTTB`, `Stride`, `Mean`, `Min/Max`, `Upsample`) so operators can pick the algorithm that best preserves the visual structure of their data. Five canonical algorithms: **LTTB** (Largest Triangle Three Buckets; canonical visual downsampling -- picks the sample from each bucket that forms the largest triangle with the previously kept sample and the average point of the next bucket; best shape preservation for line charts; first + last always kept), **Stride** (every-Nth sample at `k = (N-1)/(T-1)`; simple but loses extrema), **Mean** (bucket average; smooths the data; synthesizes), **Min/Max** (preserves extrema; emits both min and max per bucket ordered by x for path continuity; OHLC-friendly), **Linear upsample** (linearly interpolates between samples to produce target evenly-spaced output points along the x range; passthrough when input >= target; endpoints non-synthesized). All algorithms preserve `originalIndex` so callers can map resampled points back to source rows; LTTB / Stride / Min/Max keep ORIGINAL samples (synthesized=false), Mean and Linear-upsample emit synthesized samples (synthesized=true). The canonical "this is too many points to render" / "I need to interpolate this sparse series" primitive used in observability dashboards (Prometheus / Grafana LTTB), financial charts, and any context where the question is "keep the shape, change the point count". Distinct from `<ChartLineMovingAvg>` (11.513; SMA smoothing preserves count modifies values -- resample CHANGES the count), `<ChartLineEwma>` (11.539; exponential smoothing -- same count), `<ChartLineKalman>` (11.538; recursive Bayesian filter -- same count), `<ChartLineBollinger>` (11.537; rolling envelope -- same count), `<ChartLineZoom>` (catalog earlier; viewport zoom on same data without changing count), and `<ChartLineFft>` (11.540; frequency domain -- resample stays in time domain). API: `series: readonly ChartLineResampleSeries[]` (`{id, label, data, color?, mode?, target?}`; per-series mode / target always beats chart-level); `mode` (controlled) / `defaultMode` (uncontrolled; default 'lttb') -- canonical mode toggle; `onModeChange(mode)`; `target` (default 100; clamped to >= 2); `rawColor`; `rawOpacity` (default 0.3); toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots`/`showLegend`/`showTooltip`/`showReductionBadge`/`showRaw`/`showModeToggle`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility; `formatValue` / `formatX` / `formatRatio`; `onPointClick({series, point})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_RESAMPLE_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/RAW_STROKE_WIDTH/RESAMPLED_STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/RAW_OPACITY/TARGET/PALETTE/RAW_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `LINE_RESAMPLE_MODES` (the 5 canonical modes); `getLineResampleDefaultColor`; `getLineResampleFinitePoints` (preserves originalIndex); `normaliseLineResampleTarget` (clamps to >= 2; floors fractional; non-finite -> 100); `normaliseLineResampleMode` (identity for valid; default 'lttb'); `lineResampleStride(points, target)` -- every-Nth; `lineResampleMean(points, target)` -- bucket average synthesized; `lineResampleMinMax(points, target)` -- min + max per bucket ordered by x; `lineResampleLttb(points, target)` -- canonical LTTB; first + last always kept; target < 3 falls back to stride; `lineResampleLinearUpsample(points, target)` -- linear interpolation; passthrough when input >= target; endpoints non-synthesized; single-point handled; `applyLineResample(points, mode, target)` -- dispatcher; `computeLineResampleLayout({series, ...})` -- returns `{series, xTicks, yTicks, xMin, xMax, yMin, yMax, innerWidth, innerHeight, totalRawPoints, totalResampledPoints, visibleSeriesCount}`; per-series carries `{id, label, color, mode, target, rawPoints, resampledPoints, rawPath, resampledPath, rawCount, resampledCount, finiteCount, totalCount, reductionRatio}`; per-point carries `{...sample, index, px, py}` including synthesized + originalIndex; degenerate canvas / empty / all-hidden -> empty layout; `describeLineResampleChart(series, options?)` -- ARIA summary (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-resample-aria-desc"` span; raw path + resampled path + every dot `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; raw aria-label includes raw count; resampled aria-label includes mode + resampled/raw counts; mode toggle is `role="radiogroup"` with per-button `role="radio"` + `aria-checked`. data-section on every node (chart-line-resample / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -x-label / -y-label / -series / -series-group / -raw-path / -resampled-path / -dot / -tooltip / -tooltip-label / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-y / -tooltip-source / -controls / -mode-toggle / -mode-button / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-mode / -badge-counts / -badge-ratio); root mirrors `data-series-count`, `data-visible-series-count`, `data-total-raw-points`, `data-total-resampled-points`, `data-mode`, `data-target`, `data-reduction-ratio`, `data-animate`; raw path `data-kind="raw"`; resampled path `data-kind="resampled"`; dots `data-original-index`, `data-synthesized`; series groups `data-series-id`, `data-series-color`, `data-series-mode`, `data-series-target`, `data-series-raw-count`, `data-series-resampled-count`, `data-series-reduction-ratio`, `data-series-finite-count`; mode buttons `data-mode`, `data-active`. Tooltip surfaces label, x, bold y, and a source row indicating either `original #<i>` (kept input sample) or `synthesized (<mode>)` (bucket average or interpolated). Reduction badge in top-left calls out mode + counts + ratio with icon ↓. Legend shows per-series `(resampledCount/rawCount)`. 77 vitest cases (defaults incl. 5 canonical modes, helpers, lineResampleStride incl. endpoints preserved + target clamp, lineResampleMean incl. bucket mean verified at 1.5 and 3.5, lineResampleMinMax incl. preserves min + max + orders by x within bucket, lineResampleLttb incl. emits exactly target / endpoints preserved / all non-synthesized / target < 3 falls back to stride, lineResampleLinearUpsample incl. linear interpolation verified midpoint y=1 between (0,0)-(1,2) at target=5 + endpoints non-synthesized + single-point input, applyLineResample dispatcher, computeLineResampleLayout incl. raw + resampled paths / reduction ratio / per-series mode + target override / hidden / bounds overrides / total counts / upsamples sparse data, describeLineResampleChart No data + per-series with mode + counts, component render incl. empty / raw path kind=raw / resampled path kind=resampled / hide raw / dots with synthesized + originalIndex attrs / hide dots / reduction badge with mode + counts + hide / mode toggle has 5 buttons matching LINE_RESAMPLE_MODES / active mode aria-checked=true + data-active=true / uncontrolled click switches active / controlled mode does not update internal / hide mode toggle / ARIA / root data-* / tooltip synthesized vs original source row + hide on leave + omit via prop / onPointClick payload / legend resampled/raw counts + toggle + omit / animate flag + class / ref / displayName) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. | 11.542 | UI: chart-line-rangefocus primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.560 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-rangefocus.tsx` ships `<ChartLineRangeFocus>` -- pure-SVG line chart implementing the canonical **focus + context** pattern (also known as "overview + detail" or "brush + zoom"). Canvas splits vertically into a large **detail** panel (top) that renders only the points within the selected sub-range projected to fill the panel, and a small **overview** panel (bottom) that renders the entire data range dimmed with a draggable **brush** rectangle marking the selected sub-range. Three pointer-driven affordances: drag the body to MOVE the window; drag either edge handle to RESIZE; click anywhere on the overview to RECENTER. The canonical "navigate dense time-series" UI used in Grafana (time-range bar), D3 brush examples, and any context where the question is "drill into a sub-window without losing the big picture". Distinct from `<ChartLineZoom>` (catalog earlier; single-chart viewport zoom on one panel -- range-focus uses TWO panels with a brush on the overview), `<ChartLineResample>` (11.541; changes underlying sample COUNT -- range-focus keeps full data and zooms x-window), `<ChartLineForecast>` (11.519; semantic split of time axis between historical / forecast -- range-focus is a UI affordance for navigation), and `<ChartLineFft>` (11.540; split panel pairing time + frequency domains -- range-focus's two panels are BOTH time domain at different x-resolutions). API: `series: readonly ChartLineRangeFocusSeries[]` (`{id, label, data, color?}`); `range` (controlled) / `defaultRange` (uncontrolled); `onRangeChange(range)`; `minRangeWidth` -- prevents brush narrower than this; `overviewRatio` (default 0.28; clamped to [0.1, 0.5]); `gap` (default 24); `brushHandleWidth` (default 6); `brushColor`, `brushOpacity`, `outsideOpacity`, `overviewLineColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots`/`showLegend`/`showTooltip`/`showRangeBadge`/`showOverview`/`showOverviewBrush`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility; standard sizing / format / a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX` / `formatRangeWidth`; `onPointClick({series, point})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_RANGEFOCUS_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/GAP/OVERVIEW_RATIO/BRUSH_HANDLE_WIDTH/STROKE_WIDTH/OVERVIEW_STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/BRUSH_OPACITY/OUTSIDE_OPACITY/PALETTE/BRUSH_COLOR/OVERVIEW_LINE_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineRangeFocusDefaultColor`; `getLineRangeFocusFinitePoints`; `normaliseLineRangeFocusOverviewRatio` (clamps to [0.1, 0.5]; non-finite -> 0.28); `clampLineRangeFocusRange(range, fullXMin, fullXMax, minWidth?)` -- enforces x0 <= x1 (swaps reversed), x0 >= fullXMin + x1 <= fullXMax, x1 - x0 >= minWidth (expanded around centre; pinned to bounds when centre would go past), non-finite bounds -> defaults; `computeLineRangeFocusLayout({series, range, ...})` -- splits canvas; returns `{series, detailPanel, overviewPanel, brushRect, brushLeftHandle, brushRightHandle, detailXTicks, detailYTicks, overviewXTicks, fullXMin, fullXMax, detailXMin, detailXMax, fullYMin, fullYMax, detailYMin, detailYMax, range, totalPoints, totalInRange, visibleSeriesCount, ok}`; per-series carries `{id, label, color, detailPoints, detailPath (in-range only), overviewPoints, overviewPath (full), inRangeCount, finiteCount, totalCount}`; each detail point has `{index, x, y, px, py, inRange}`; degenerate canvas / empty / all-hidden -> ok=false; `describeLineRangeFocusChart(series, options?)` -- ARIA summary (No data when missing). Brush interaction model: pointer-captured drag with pointermove + pointerup listeners on window so drags continue when cursor leaves SVG; delta-x in pixels converted to delta-x in data via overview panel's projection; move drags pin against full bounds when window would slide off either side; edge drags extend or shrink corresponding end independently; every mutation goes through `clampLineRangeFocusRange` so final state is always valid. Range badge in top-left calls out [x0, x1] + width with icon ↔. Brush group is `role="slider"` with `aria-valuemin`/`aria-valuemax`/`aria-valuenow`/`aria-valuetext`. ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-rangefocus-aria-desc"` span; detail line + detail dots `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; overview line `role="graphics-symbol"` (decorative; not focusable); brush group `role="slider"`. data-section on every node (chart-line-rangefocus / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -x-label / -y-label / -detail-series / -detail-series-group / -detail-path / -detail-dot / -overview / -overview-path / -brush / -brush-bg / -brush-rect / -brush-handle / -tooltip / -tooltip-label / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-y / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -legend-total-points / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-range / -badge-width); root mirrors `data-series-count`, `data-visible-series-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-total-in-range`, `data-range-x0`, `data-range-x1`, `data-range-ratio`, `data-animate`; detail line `data-kind="detail"`; overview line `data-kind="overview"`; brush handles `data-kind` ('left' / 'right'); series groups `data-series-id`, `data-series-color`, `data-series-in-range-count`, `data-series-finite-count`. Tooltip surfaces label, x, bold y on detail-dot hover. Legend shows per-series `(inRangeCount/finiteCount)`. 57 vitest cases (defaults, helpers, clampLineRangeFocusRange incl. clamp to full bounds + null -> full + swap reversed + expand to minWidth + collapse + non-finite + pin minWidth to lo, computeLineRangeFocusLayout incl. empty / degenerate / splits canvas / detail path only in-range / overview has all / brush rect width matches projected range / records inRange counts / drops hidden / bounds overrides / clamps range / totalInRange sums per-series / handles flank rect at handle-width/2, describeLineRangeFocusChart No data + range + in-range count, component render incl. empty / detail path kind=detail / overview path kind=overview / hide overview via prop / brush rect + 2 handles / role=slider + aria-value* / hide brush via prop / detail dots only in-range / hide dots / range badge with x0/x1 + hide / ARIA / root data-* / controlled range stays controlled / tooltip on detail dot (x+y) + hide on leave + omit via prop / onPointClick payload / legend in-range/finite counts + toggle + omit / animate flag + class / ref / displayName) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for the new files. Exported via components/ui barrel. Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/c4-mgr-auto. Worker auto-w524. Bump 1.11.560. |
| 11.543 | UI: chart-line-decompose primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.561 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-decompose.tsx` ships `<ChartLineDecompose>` -- pure-SVG **additive seasonal decomposition** chart. Takes one time series and splits it into three coordinated components: **trend** (centered moving average of window=period; canonical 2xN half-weight formula for even periods), **seasonal** (per-phase average of detrended values, then centered so the seasonal pattern has zero mean), and **residual** (observed - trend - seasonal). Rendered as a stacked 4-panel chart (Observed / Trend / Seasonal / Residual) sharing the x-axis, with per-panel y-scales and dashed zero reference lines on the seasonal + residual panels. Seasonal and residual y ranges are pinned symmetric around zero when both signs are present so the reference line lands in the middle of the panel. Matches pandas' `seasonal_decompose(model='additive', period=p)` output (centered moving-average convention). The canonical statsmodels / pandas seasonal-decompose 4-pane output -- used for visual diagnosis of seasonality vs trend vs noise in any periodic time series (sales, weather, web traffic, etc.). Distinct from `<ChartLineMovingAvg>` (11.513; SMA only -- decompose extracts THE trend component but ALSO produces seasonal + residual splits), `<ChartLineResidual>` (11.525; observed minus EXTERNALLY-supplied predicted -- decompose derives the predicted (trend + seasonal) internally from the same input series), `<ChartLineFft>` (11.540; frequency-domain transform -- decompose stays in time domain and extracts ONE pre-specified period; use FFT to IDENTIFY the dominant period, then decompose to SPLIT around that period), `<ChartLineBollinger>` (11.537; rolling envelope -- decompose is a 4-panel split), and `<ChartLineKalman>` (11.538; recursive Bayesian filter -- decompose is a deterministic windowed decomposition). API: `data: readonly ChartLineDecomposePoint[]` (`{x, y}`); `period` (default 12; clamped to >= 2); `observedColor` / `trendColor` / `seasonalColor` / `residualColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false; dense paths usually enough)/`showLegend`/`showTooltip`/`showPeriodBadge`/`showResidualZero`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled component visibility via `hiddenComponents` / `defaultHiddenComponents` / `onHiddenComponentsChange`; standard sizing / format / a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({panel, point, sample})` (sample includes full per-sample decomposition). Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_DECOMPOSE_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/GAP/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/OBSERVED_COLOR/TREND_COLOR/SEASONAL_COLOR/RESIDUAL_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR/RESIDUAL_ZERO_DASH`; `LINE_DECOMPOSE_COMPONENT_KINDS` (the 4 canonical kinds); `getLineDecomposeFinitePoints`; `normaliseLineDecomposePeriod` (clamps >= 2; floors fractional; non-finite -> 12); `computeCenteredMovingAverage(values, period)` -- canonical centered MA; odd: `mean(y[i-half..i+half])`; even: `(0.5*y[i-half] + y[i-half+1] + ... + y[i+half-1] + 0.5*y[i+half]) / p`; edges null; non-finite skipped from window sum; `computeLineDecomposeSeasonalPattern(detrended, period)` -- length-period array of phase means with overall mean subtracted (centers pattern); skips null entries; `computeLineDecomposition(points, period)` -- canonical decomposition; sorts ascending; drops non-finite; returns `{samples, period, trendValidCount, residualValidCount, seasonalPattern, ok}`; each sample carries `{index, x, observed, trend, seasonal, residual, phase}`; reconstructs `observed = trend + seasonal + residual` exactly where trend is valid (verified by test); `computeLineDecomposeLayout({data, ...})` -- stacks enabled panels evenly with gap separators; returns `{ok, width, height, panels, panelMap, xTicks, xMin, xMax, innerWidth, innerHeight, totalPoints, finiteCount, period, decomposition}`; each panel projects its own y range (seasonal/residual symmetric around 0); per-panel `points[i].py` null where decomposition is null at trend edges; observed panel never has null pys; `describeLineDecomposeChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-decompose-aria-desc"` span; per-panel line paths `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; dots (when shown) also focusable; legend is `role="group"` with per-button `aria-pressed`. data-section on every node (chart-line-decompose / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis-group / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -x-ticks / -x-label / -panel-label / -panels / -panel / -path / -zero-line / -dot / -tooltip / -tooltip-label / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-observed / -tooltip-trend / -tooltip-seasonal / -tooltip-residual / -tooltip-phase / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-period / -badge-counts); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-total-points`, `data-trend-valid-count`, `data-residual-valid-count`, `data-panel-count`, `data-hidden-count`, `data-animate`; per-panel group `data-panel` (observed / trend / seasonal / residual), `data-color`, `data-valid-count`; per-panel path `data-kind` matching component kind; zero reference lines `data-panel`. Tooltip surfaces the full per-sample breakdown: panel label, x, obs, trend (n/a at edges), seasonal, residual (n/a at edges), phase. Period badge in top-left with ⌬ icon + period + sample count. Legend has 4 toggle buttons (one per component) with `aria-pressed` flag. 61 vitest cases (defaults, helpers, computeCenteredMovingAverage odd + even formulas verified at known values incl. half-weight endpoints at period 4 (test verified output at i=2 = 3 for [1,2,3,4,5,6]), computeLineDecomposeSeasonalPattern centers to zero verified by sum, computeLineDecomposition empty / per-sample / trend null at edges / seasonal repeats / **VERIFIED reconstruction identity observed = trend + seasonal + residual** / trendValidCount === residualValidCount / default period fallback / sort ascending, computeLineDecomposeLayout empty / degenerate / 4 panels canonical order / enabledComponents filter / residual range centered on 0 / residual zero py / trend edges null pys / observed never null / totalPoints + period, describeLineDecomposeChart No data + period + samples + trend-valid summary, component render incl. empty / 4 panel paths in canonical order / hide via hiddenComponents (controlled) / zero reference line on residual + seasonal + hide via prop / per-panel label rendered / period badge + omit / dots only when showDots=true / ARIA / root data-* / tooltip breakdown rows + hide on leave + omit via prop / onPointClick payload with panel kind / legend 4 toggle buttons + uncontrolled toggle + omit / animate flag + class / ref / period > samples = all trend null / displayName) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for the new files. Exported via components/ui barrel. Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/c4-mgr-auto. Worker auto-w525. Bump 1.11.561. |
| 11.544 | UI: chart-line-fft-window primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.562 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-fft-window.tsx` ships `<ChartLineFftWindow>` -- pure-SVG **windowed-FFT** chart that applies a tapering window (Rectangular / Hann / Hamming / Blackman, toggleable at runtime) to the time series BEFORE running the DFT. Pairs a time-domain panel (left) with a frequency-spectrum panel (right), and overlays a dashed window envelope on the time panel so operators can SEE the taper being applied. The canonical "I need clean spectral peaks instead of smeared leakage" frequency-domain primitive used in audio analysis, vibration diagnostics, and any context where the question is "what's the true dominant frequency, not the leakage ghosts?". Distinct from `<ChartLineFft>` (11.540; no window -- this is the windowed equivalent with 4 canonical window options + runtime toggle + per-bin gain reporting), `<ChartLineDecompose>` (11.543; time-domain seasonal decomposition -- FFT-window is frequency-domain), and `<ChartLineMomentum>` (11.533; sub-panel oscillator with DERIVATIVE -- not a spectrum). Window formulas (verified by test): Hann `0.5 * (1 - cos(2*pi*n/(N-1)))` with endpoints 0 + center 1; Hamming `0.54 - 0.46*cos(2*pi*n/(N-1))` with endpoints 0.08; Blackman `0.42 - 0.5*cos(arg) + 0.08*cos(2*arg)` with endpoints 0 + center 1; Rectangular `1.0`. Reports per-spectrum **coherent gain** = sum(w)/N (amplitude scale factor; multiply un-windowed amplitude by 1 / coherent gain to recover the original tone amplitude) and **processing gain** = sum(w^2)/N (noise power scale factor). API: `series: readonly ChartLineFftWindowSeries[]` (`{id, label, data, color?, windowMode?, detrend?, excludeDc?}`; per-series overrides always beat chart-level); `windowMode` (controlled) / `defaultWindowMode` (uncontrolled; default 'hann') + `onWindowModeChange`; `detrend` (default true); `excludeDc` (default true); `timePanelRatio` (default 0.6; clamped to [0.1, 0.9]); `gap` (default 20); `spectrumColor` / `dominantColor` / `windowColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots`/`showLegend`/`showTooltip`/`showDominantBadge`/`showSpectrum`/`showDominantMarker`/`showWindowEnvelope`/`showWindowedTime`/`showRawTime`/`showWindowToggle`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility; `formatValue` / `formatX` / `formatFrequency` / `formatPeriod` / `formatMagnitude` / `formatGain`; `onPointClick({series, point})` / `onBinClick({series, bin})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_FFT_WINDOW_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/TICK_COUNT/TIME_PANEL_RATIO/STROKE_WIDTH/WINDOW_STROKE_WIDTH/BAR_WIDTH_RATIO/DOT_RADIUS/PALETTE/SPECTRUM_COLOR/DOMINANT_COLOR/WINDOW_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `LINE_FFT_WINDOW_MODES` (the 4 canonical modes); `getLineFftWindowDefaultColor`; `getLineFftWindowFinitePoints`; `normaliseLineFftWindowPanelRatio` (clamps [0.1, 0.9]); `normaliseLineFftWindowMode` (identity for valid; default 'hann'); `computeLineFftWindowCoefficients(N, mode)` -- canonical window generator; N <= 0 / non-finite -> []; N=1 -> [1]; `applyLineFftWindowToValues(values, coefficients)` -- element-wise multiply; non-finite slots emit 0; uses min length; `computeLineFftWindowDft(values)` -- canonical real-input half-spectrum DFT; returns `{real, imag}[]` of length floor(N/2)+1; non-finite skipped; `computeLineFftWindowSpectrum(points, {windowMode?, detrend?, excludeDc?})` -- canonical pipeline; sorts ascending; drops non-finite; default detrend + excludeDc true; returns `{bins, dominantBin, totalSamples, detrended, excludedDc, meanValue, windowMode, windowCoherentGain, windowProcessingGain, windowedValues, windowCoefficients}`; `findLineFftWindowDominantBin(bins, excludeDc?)` -- excludes DC by default; `computeLineFftWindowLayout({series, ...})` -- splits canvas into time + spectrum panels; returns `{ok, width, height, series, timePanel, spectrumPanel, timeXTicks, timeYTicks, spectrumXTicks, spectrumYTicks, xMin, xMax, yMin, yMax, freqMin, freqMax, magMin, magMax, totalPoints, visibleSeriesCount}`; per-series carries `{id, label, color, windowMode, detrend, excludeDc, timePoints, rawTimePath, windowedTimePath, windowEnvelopePath, spectrum, spectrumBins, dominantFrequency, dominantPeriod, dominantMagnitude, finiteCount, totalCount}`; per-point carries `{index, x, rawY, windowedY, windowWeight, px, rawPy, windowedPy, windowPy}`; bin bars projected to `{px, py, barX, barWidth, barHeight, isDominant}`; degenerate canvas / empty / all-hidden -> empty layout; `describeLineFftWindowChart(series, options?)` -- ARIA summary (No data when missing). Window mode toggle is `role="radiogroup"` with 4 `role="radio"` buttons + `aria-checked` flag. ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-fft-window-aria-desc"` span; raw path + windowed path + envelope path + every time dot + every spectrum bar all `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; spectrum bar aria-label includes bin k + frequency + period + magnitude + dominant. data-section on every node (chart-line-fft-window / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -x-label / -y-label / -spectrum-x-label / -spectrum-y-label / -series / -series-group / -raw-path / -windowed-path / -envelope-path / -time-dot / -bin-bar / -tooltip / -tooltip-label / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-raw / -tooltip-windowed / -tooltip-weight / -tooltip-frequency / -tooltip-period / -tooltip-magnitude / -tooltip-gain / -tooltip-dominant / -controls / -mode-toggle / -mode-button / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -legend-total-points / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-frequency / -badge-period / -badge-window); `data-panel="time"` or `"spectrum"` on axis/tick nodes; root mirrors `data-window-mode`, `data-detrend`, `data-exclude-dc`, `data-dominant-frequency`, `data-dominant-period`, `data-dominant-coherent-gain`, `data-total-points`, `data-series-count`, `data-visible-series-count`, `data-animate`; raw path `data-kind="raw"`; windowed path `data-kind="windowed"`; envelope path `data-kind="envelope"`; spectrum bars `data-bin-k`, `data-frequency`, `data-period`, `data-magnitude`, `data-normalised-magnitude`, `data-dominant`; time dots `data-raw-y`, `data-windowed-y`, `data-window-weight`; series groups `data-series-window-mode`, `data-series-coherent-gain`, `data-series-processing-gain`, `data-series-dominant-frequency`, `data-series-dominant-period`, `data-series-dominant-magnitude`, `data-series-bin-count`, `data-series-finite-count`, `data-series-detrend`, `data-series-exclude-dc`. Tooltip on time dots shows label, x, raw, bold windowed value, and the per-point window coefficient. Tooltip on spectrum bins shows label + bin number, frequency, period, bold magnitude, window mode + coherent gain row, and (when dominant) a coloured "dominant" row. Dominant badge in top-left with `f` icon + frequency + period + window mode label. Legend shows per-series `(f=<freq>; T=<period>; <window> CG <gain>)`. 90 vitest cases (defaults incl. 4 canonical modes, helpers, computeLineFftWindowCoefficients incl. | 11.545 | UI: chart-line-spectrogram primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.563 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-spectrogram.tsx` ships `<ChartLineSpectrogram>` -- pure-SVG **STFT spectrogram** chart. Pairs a time-domain line on top with a 2D frequency-vs-time heatmap below, where each column is one Short-Time Fourier Transform frame, each row is a frequency bin, and the cell color encodes the magnitude. A dominant-frequency track overlays the heatmap as a dashed line showing how the dominant frequency evolves across frames. The canonical librosa / matplotlib / Audacity spectrogram output -- used for visualising audio, vibration, EEG, or any signal where the dominant frequency shifts over time. STFT algorithm: for each window position start=0, hop, 2*hop, ..., extract samples y[start..start+W-1] (optionally pre-detrended), multiply element-wise by window coefficients w[0..W-1], compute half-spectrum DFT (length W/2+1), take per-bin magnitude. Frames count = `floor((N - W) / hop) + 1`. Frequency at bin k = `k / (W * sampleStep)`. Bin 0 (DC) dropped from heatmap by default. Distinct from `<ChartLineFft>` (11.540; single 1D spectrum of whole signal with no windowing -- spectrogram is a SEQUENCE of windowed spectra over sliding windows), `<ChartLineFftWindow>` (11.544; single windowed 1D spectrum of whole signal -- spectrogram applies the same windowing PER WINDOW POSITION and stacks results into a 2D heatmap), and `<ChartLineDecompose>` (11.543; time-domain seasonal split -- spectrogram is frequency-domain and TIME-VARYING). API: `data: readonly ChartLineSpectrogramPoint[]` (`{x, y}` samples); `windowSize` (default 32; clamped to >= 2); `hopSize` (default windowSize/2; clamped to [1, windowSize]); `windowMode` (controlled) / `defaultWindowMode` (uncontrolled; default 'hann') + `onWindowModeChange`; `detrend` (default true); `excludeDc` (default true); `linePanelRatio` (default 0.32; clamped to [0.1, 0.6]); `lowColor` / `midColor` / `highColor` -- 3-stop heatmap color scale; `dominantColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots`/`showLegend`/`showTooltip`/`showDominantBadge`/`showDominantTrack`/`showLine`/`showWindowToggle`/`showColorScale`/`animate`; standard sizing / format / a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX` / `formatFrequency` / `formatMagnitude`; `onCellClick({cell})` / `onPointClick({point})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_SPECTROGRAM_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/TICK_COUNT/LINE_PANEL_RATIO/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/WINDOW_SIZE/HOP_RATIO/LINE_COLOR/DOMINANT_COLOR/LOW_COLOR/MID_COLOR/HIGH_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `LINE_SPECTROGRAM_WINDOW_MODES` (the 4 canonical modes); `getLineSpectrogramFinitePoints`; `normaliseLineSpectrogramWindowSize` (clamps >= 2; floors fractional; non-finite -> 32); `normaliseLineSpectrogramHopSize(value, windowSize)` (default half window; clamps to [1, windowSize]); `normaliseLineSpectrogramWindowMode` (identity for valid; default 'hann'); `normaliseLineSpectrogramPanelRatio` (clamps to [0.1, 0.6]); `computeLineSpectrogramWindowCoefficients(N, mode)` -- canonical window weights identical to 11.544; `getLineSpectrogramScaleColor(t, low, mid, high)` -- 3-stop linear interpolation in RGB; clamps t to [0, 1]; non-finite -> low color; `computeLineSpectrogram(points, {windowSize?, hopSize?, windowMode?, detrend?, excludeDc?})` -- canonical STFT pipeline; sorts ascending; drops non-finite; returns `{ok, frames, windowSize, hopSize, windowMode, binCount, maxMagnitude, frequencies, totalSamples, sampleStep}`; each frame carries `{frameIndex, centerSampleIndex, centerX, startX, endX, magnitudes, dominantBin, dominantFrequency, dominantMagnitude}`; input shorter than window -> ok=false; `computeLineSpectrogramLayout({data, ...})` -- splits canvas into line + spectrogram panels; projects time path + heatmap cells + dominant-track polyline; returns `{ok, width, height, linePanel, spectrogramPanel, timePoints, timePath, cells, cellsByFrame, dominantTrack, dominantTrackPath, xTicks, yTicks, freqTicks, xMin, xMax, yMin, yMax, freqMin, freqMax, magMin, magMax, totalPoints, spectrogram}`; cells.length = frames * (binCount - 1 with default excludeDc); higher frequency bins rendered at top of panel (smaller py); per-cell color from `getLineSpectrogramScaleColor(normalisedMagnitude)`; `describeLineSpectrogramChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary (No data when missing). Color scale 3-stop gradient (low / mid / high) shown below the chart with the maximum magnitude label. Tooltip on cells shows frame number, bin, frequency, bold magnitude, and (when dominant) a coloured "dominant" row; on time dots shows x and bold y. Dominant badge in top-left calls out frame count + window mode + window size + peak frequency with icon ▦. Window mode toggle is `role="radiogroup"` with 4 `role="radio"` buttons + `aria-checked` flag. ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-spectrogram-aria-desc"` span; time path + time dots + every cell `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; dominant track decorative `role="graphics-symbol"`; cell aria-label includes frame + bin + frequency + magnitude + dominant flag. data-section on every node (chart-line-spectrogram / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -x-label / -y-label / -spectrogram-y-label / -time-path / -time-dot / -cells / -cell / -dominant-track / -tooltip / -tooltip-frame / -tooltip-bin / -tooltip-frequency / -tooltip-magnitude / -tooltip-dominant / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-y / -controls / -window-toggle / -window-button / -color-scale / -color-scale-low / -color-scale-gradient / -color-scale-high / -legend / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-frames / -badge-window / `data-total-points`, `data-max-magnitude`, `data-peak-frame-index`, `data-peak-frequency`, `data-detrend`, `data-exclude-dc`, `data-animate`; cells `data-frame-index`, `data-bin-index`, `data-frequency`, `data-magnitude`, `data-normalised-magnitude`, `data-dominant`; dominant track `data-kind="dominant"`; time path `data-kind="time"`; time dots `data-x`, `data-y`; window buttons `data-mode`, `data-active`; badge `data-window-mode` + peak frequency + magnitude + frame index. 84 vitest cases (defaults + 4 canonical window modes, helpers, computeLineSpectrogramWindowCoefficients incl. canonical formulas (Hann endpoints 0 + center 1 / Hamming endpoints 0.08 / Blackman endpoints 0 + center 1 / Rectangular all 1s / non-positive -> []), getLineSpectrogramScaleColor incl. t=0/0.5/1 verified at #000000/#888888/#ffffff + clamps below/above + non-finite -> low, computeLineSpectrogram incl. ok=false when input < window + multiple frames at 50% hop default + windowSize/hopSize reflected + binCount = W/2+1 + frequencies length matches + **chirp dominant bin shifts from 4 (period-4 early) to 8 (period-2 late)** + steady cosine same dominant in every frame + drops non-finite + sort ascending + maxMagnitude > 0 + rectangular mode honored + frame centerX in [startX, endX], computeLineSpectrogramLayout incl. empty / degenerate / splits canvas / time path + cells / cells.length = frames * (binCount-1 default) / per-frame cell count / one dominant cell per frame / dominant track length = frames + path / cells have rgb colors / normalisedMagnitude in [0,1] / **higher frequency cells appear higher on screen** verified, describeLineSpectrogramChart No data + STFT frames + window + bins, component render incl. empty / time path kind=time / hide line via prop / cells / dominant cell data-dominant=true / dominant track kind=dominant + hide via prop / badge with frame count + peak frequency + hide via prop / window toggle 4 buttons + aria-checked active + uncontrolled click switches via onWindowModeChange + controlled stays + hide toggle / color scale gradient + hide via prop / ARIA / root data-* / tooltip on cell (frame + bin + freq + magnitude +
