| 11.554 | UI: chart-line-cusum primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.572 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-cusum.tsx` ships `<ChartLineCusum>` -- pure-SVG two-panel line chart with a **CUSUM (cumulative sum) drift detector** and **reset-on-trigger markers**. Main panel (top) renders the raw series plus a dashed target reference line; the CUSUM sub-panel (bottom) renders the two cumulative sums with their decision thresholds; drift triggers are marked on both panels. The two-sided **tabular CUSUM** is the canonical sequential change-detection method. For a target mean T and reference allowance k it tracks two non-negative running sums: `C+_t = max(0, C+_{t-1} + (y_t - T) - allowance)` accumulates persistent UPWARD drift; `C-_t = max(0, C-_{t-1} - (y_t - T) - allowance)` accumulates persistent DOWNWARD drift. Both are floored at 0 by the max so a brief excursion that reverts decays back toward 0 rather than persisting. An alarm fires when either sum crosses the decision interval h. Slack and threshold are expressed in sigma units: `allowance = slack * sigma`, `decisionInterval = threshold * sigma`, with defaults slack = 0.5 and threshold = 5 (the textbook k = 0.5 sigma, h = 5 sigma tuning that catches a 1-sigma sustained shift quickly with a low false-alarm rate). **Reset-on-trigger**: when a sum crosses its decision interval the chart records the crossing value (so the triggering spike is visible) then RESETS that sum to 0, so detection of the next shift starts fresh -- a process that drifts, is corrected, and drifts again produces a separate trigger each time. **Verified properties: a series constant at the target produces C+ = C- = 0 throughout and zero triggers; an upward shift accumulates C+ step by step (3 -> 6 -> 9 -> 12) and triggers when it crosses h = 10; a downward shift accumulates C- symmetrically; after a single huge spike triggers, the very next sample's C+ is back at 0 (reset confirmed); a threshold of 0 disables triggering; both sums are always non-negative.** Distinct from `<ChartLineControl>` (11.534; a Shewhart SPC chart with CL/UCL/LCL at mean +- k*sigma -- the control chart is MEMORYLESS, each point judged independently, so it only catches LARGE single excursions, whereas CUSUM has MEMORY and accumulates small deviations so a small PERSISTENT shift is caught far sooner -- CUSUM is the standard answer to "the control chart is too slow to notice a 1-sigma drift"), `<ChartLineChangepoint>` (11.546; detects VARIANCE shifts via a windowed variance-ratio score -- CUSUM detects MEAN shifts via the cumulative sum and signals sequentially rather than scoring every index), `<ChartLineCumulative>` (11.520; a plain running sum of the raw values -- CUSUM is the running sum of DEVIATIONS from a target minus a slack, floored at 0, with reset -- a detector not an accumulator), and `<ChartLineAnomaly>` (11.524; per-point z-score outlier flags, each point independent -- CUSUM is sequential and cumulative). Control charts and anomaly detection answer "is THIS POINT unusual?"; CUSUM answers "has the PROCESS MEAN shifted, and when?". API: `series: readonly ChartLineCusumSeries[]` (`{id, label, data, color?, target?, sigma?}`; per-series target / sigma overrides beat chart-level); `target` (expected mean; defaults to the series mean); `sigma` (scale for slack + threshold; defaults to the population standard deviation); `slack` (default 0.5; sigma units; clamped to >= 0); `threshold` (default 5; sigma units; clamped to >= 0; 0 disables triggering); `subHeightRatio` (default 0.4; clamped to [0.1, 0.9]); `targetColor` / `thresholdColor` / `triggerColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots`/`showLegend`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showTargetLine`/`showThresholds`/`showTriggers`/`showTriggerLines`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility; standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX` / `formatCusum`; `onPointClick({series, point})` + `onTriggerClick({series, trigger})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_CUSUM_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/TICK_COUNT/SUB_HEIGHT_RATIO/RAW_STROKE_WIDTH/CUSUM_STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/SLACK/THRESHOLD/PALETTE/TARGET_COLOR/THRESHOLD_COLOR/TRIGGER_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineCusumDefaultColor` (10-color cycle); `getLineCusumFinitePoints`; `normaliseLineCusumSlack` (>= 0; non-finite -> 0.5); `normaliseLineCusumThreshold` (>= 0; non-finite -> 5); `normaliseLineCusumSubHeightRatio` (clamps to [0.1, 0.9]); `computeLineCusumStats(values, options?)` -- the reference statistics; returns `{mean, computedSigma, sigma, target, slack, threshold, allowance, decisionInterval, ok}`; default target = mean; sigma defaults to population std; ok = sigma > 0; `classifyLineCusumTriggerSide(upper, lower)` -- 'upper' \| 'lower' \| 'both' \| 'none'; `runLineCusum(points, options?)` -- the full CUSUM pipeline; sorts ascending; drops non-finite; runs the two-sided recursion with reset-on-trigger; returns `{samples, stats, triggerCount, upperTriggerCount, lowerTriggerCount, maxCusumPos, maxCusumNeg, totalSamples}`; per-sample `{index, x, raw, deviation, cusumPos, cusumNeg, upperTriggered, lowerTriggered, triggered, triggerSide}`; `computeLineCusumLayout({series, ...})` -- projects the main + sub panels, raw + CUSUM paths, target line, thresholds, and the trigger list; per-series carries `{id, label, color, stats, points, rawPath, cusumPosPath, cusumNegPath, targetPy, triggers, triggerCount, upperTriggerCount, lowerTriggerCount, maxCusumPos, maxCusumNeg, finiteCount, totalCount}`; per-point projected `{...sample, px, rawPy, cusumPosPy, cusumNegPy}`; per-trigger `{index, x, px, side, cusumPos, cusumNeg, upperPy, lowerPy}`; sub-panel y range symmetric around zero; degenerate canvas / empty / all-hidden -> ok=false; `describeLineCusumChart(series, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions CUSUM + target + slack + threshold + drift triggers (No data when missing). Layout: two stacked SVG panels sharing the x axis -- main panel (top, ~60%) with the raw series line + a dashed horizontal target reference line; CUSUM sub-panel (bottom, ~40%) with C+ plotted upward from a centred zero line, C- plotted downward (negated), dashed decision-interval thresholds at +h and -h. Drift triggers render as diamond markers on the sub-panel (at the crossing CUSUM value) plus a dashed vertical trigger line through the main panel at the drift x. ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-cusum-aria-desc"` span; raw path + both CUSUM paths + trigger markers + every dot `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; trigger marker aria-label includes the x + side. data-section on every node (chart-line-cusum / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -x-label / -y-label / -sub-label / -zero-line / -series / -series-group / -target-line / -thresholds / -threshold-line / -raw-path / -cusum-pos-path / -cusum-neg-path / -trigger-line / -trigger-marker / -dot / -tooltip / -tooltip-label / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-raw / -tooltip-deviation / -tooltip-cusum-pos / -tooltip-cusum-neg / -tooltip-trigger / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -legend-total-points / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-target / -badge-slack / -badge-threshold / -badge-triggers); root mirrors `data-series-count`, `data-visible-series-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-total-triggers`, `data-target`, `data-slack`, `data-threshold`, `data-animate`; raw path `data-kind="raw"`; CUSUM paths `data-kind="cusum-pos"` / `"cusum-neg"`; threshold lines `data-side="upper"` / `"lower"`; trigger markers + lines `data-side` + `data-point-index`; dots `data-deviation`, `data-cusum-pos`, `data-cusum-neg`, `data-triggered`, `data-trigger-side`; series groups `data-series-target`, `data-series-sigma`, `data-series-slack`, `data-series-threshold`, `data-series-allowance`, `data-series-decision-interval`, `data-series-trigger-count`, `data-series-upper-trigger-count`, `data-series-lower-trigger-count`, `data-series-max-cusum-pos`, `data-series-max-cusum-neg`, `data-series-finite-count`. Tooltip on dots shows the series label, x, raw value, deviation from target (with +/- prefix), bold `CUSUM+`, `CUSUM-`, and -- when the sample triggered -- a coloured `drift trigger (<side>)` row. Config badge in top-left calls out `CUSUM T=<target> k=<slack> h=<threshold> drift=<count>`. Legend shows per-series `(T=<target>; drift <count>)`. 80 vitest cases (defaults + helpers, computeLineCusumStats incl. empty -> ok=false + **mean + population sigma** + default target = mean + target/sigma overrides + **allowance = slack * sigma / decisionInterval = threshold * sigma** + constant series -> sigma 0 + ok=false, runLineCusum incl. empty + **no drift -> zero CUSUM + no triggers** + **upward drift accumulates C+ (3,6,9,12) and triggers** + **downward drift accumulates C- and triggers** + **resets to 0 after a trigger** + records deviation + tracks maxCusumPos/maxCusumNeg + sorts ascending + drops non-finite + **threshold 0 disables triggering** + constant-at-target -> no triggers + sums always non-negative, computeLineCusumLayout incl. empty/degenerate/hidden + stacked main + sub panels + raw + CUSUM paths + stats + trigger list exposed + symmetric sub y range + per-series target override + chart-level slack + threshold reach the run + hidden excluded + bounds overrides + totalPoints, describeLineCusumChart incl. No data + summary mentions CUSUM + target + drift triggers, component render incl. empty / raw path kind=raw / cusum-pos + cusum-neg paths / zero line / target line + hide via prop / threshold lines upper + lower + hide via prop / **trigger marker for the detected upward drift** / trigger line + hide via prop / hide triggers / dots via showDots / config badge with target + slack + threshold + drift count + hide via prop / ARIA region + img + sr-only desc / root data-* / series group data-* / tooltip with raw + deviation + CUSUM rows + a trigger row on a triggered dot + hide on leave + omit via prop / onPointClick + onTriggerClick payloads / legend with target + drift + toggle + omit / animate flag + class / ref / displayName / custom ariaLabel / xLabel + yLabel + subLabel / no-drift series renders zero triggers) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for the new files. Exported via components/ui barrel. Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/c4-mgr-auto. Worker auto-w536. Bump 1.11.572. |
| 11.555 | UI: chart-line-ewmsd primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.573 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-ewmsd.tsx` ships `<ChartLineEwmsd>` -- pure-SVG line chart with an **EWMA mean** line and an **exponentially-weighted standard deviation band** overlay. Raw observations render as a dimmed reference line; the EWMA mean renders as a solid coloured line; a translucent `mean +/- k * EWM-std` band fills the volatility envelope around it; points that fall outside the band are flagged as exceedances. The chart uses Finch's **incremental exponentially-weighted mean and variance** recursion: for each new value y_t, `delta_t = y_t - mean_{t-1}`, `mean_t = mean_{t-1} + alpha * delta_t`, `var_t = (1 - alpha) * (var_{t-1} + alpha * delta_t^2)`. The mean recursion is exactly the standard EWMA `mean_t = alpha * y_t + (1 - alpha) * mean_{t-1}`; var_t is the exponentially-weighted variance where every past squared deviation contributes with a geometrically decaying weight, so the EWM standard deviation sqrt(var_t) tracks RECENT volatility. Seed: mean_0 = y_0, var_0 = 0 (a single point has no spread). The band at index t is `[mean_t - k*std_t, mean_t + k*std_t]` where k is the band-width multiplier (default 2, a roughly 95% envelope for Gaussian data). A point y_t is an exceedance when it falls strictly outside the band ('above' when y_t > upper, 'below' when y_t < lower). **Verified properties: a constant series has var = std = 0 throughout (band collapses onto the mean); a step series [0,0,0,10] with alpha 0.5 yields mean = 5, var = 0.5 * 0.5 * 100 = 25, std = 5 at the jump; the calm-then-spike fixture [10,10,10,10,10,100] with alpha 0.1 yields exactly mean = 19, var = 729, std = 27 at the spike; the variance is always non-negative; index 0 is always within the band (std = 0); a spike exceeds the band only when alpha < 1/(1+k^2) -- with k=2 that is alpha < 0.2.** Distinct from `<ChartLineEwma>` (11.539; plots only the EWMA mean line -- a single exponential smoother with NO variance and NO band -- EWMSD adds the second moment: the exponentially-weighted standard deviation and the volatility envelope around the mean), `<ChartLineBollinger>` (11.537; Bollinger Bands use a ROLLING fixed-window simple moving average plus a ROLLING sample standard deviation -- the window has a hard edge, a value N steps old contributes fully and N+1 steps old not at all, whereas EWMSD has INFINITE MEMORY with geometric decay, every past point contributes weighted by alpha*(1-alpha)^age, so the band reacts faster to a volatility regime change and has no abrupt window-drop artifacts), `<ChartLineControl>` (11.534; a Shewhart SPC chart with GLOBAL constant control limits at mean +- k*sigma computed once over the whole series -- EWMSD's band is ADAPTIVE and breathes with local volatility, a calm stretch has a tight band and a turbulent stretch a wide one), `<ChartLineConfidence>` (11.502; renders externally supplied per-point bounds -- EWMSD derives its band from the data via the EWM recursion), and `<ChartLineCusum>` (11.554; a cumulative-sum drift detector for the mean -- EWMSD models the spread not the cumulative mean shift). The volatility-adaptive cousin of Bollinger Bands -- the RiskMetrics-style exponentially-weighted view used when recent volatility should dominate the envelope. API: `series: readonly ChartLineEwmsdSeries[]` (`{id, label, data, color?, alpha?, bandMultiplier?}`; per-series alpha / bandMultiplier overrides beat chart-level); `alpha` (default 0.3; EWMA smoothing factor; clamped to (0, 1], values below 0.01 raised to 0.01); `bandMultiplier` (default 2; band width k; clamped to >= 0); `rawColor` / `exceedanceColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; `bandOpacity` (default 0.15); toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots`/`showLegend`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showRaw`/`showMean`/`showBand`/`showExceedances`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility; standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX` / `formatCoefficient`; `onPointClick({series, point})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_EWMSD_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/RAW_STROKE_WIDTH/MEAN_STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/ALPHA/BAND_MULTIPLIER/RAW_OPACITY/BAND_OPACITY/PALETTE/RAW_COLOR/EXCEEDANCE_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineEwmsdDefaultColor` (10-color cycle); `getLineEwmsdFinitePoints`; `normaliseLineEwmsdAlpha` (clamps to (0, 1]; non-finite -> 0.3; below 0.01 -> 0.01; above 1 -> 1); `normaliseLineEwmsdBandMultiplier` (>= 0; non-finite -> 2); `classifyLineEwmsdExceedance(value, lower, upper)` -- 'above' \| 'below' \| 'within'; strict comparison so boundary values are 'within'; non-finite -> 'within'; `computeLineEwmsd(values, alpha)` -- the incremental EWM mean/variance recursion; returns per-index `{mean, variance, std}`; drops non-finite values so the output matches the finite-only length; `runLineEwmsd(points, options?)` -- canonical pipeline; sorts ascending; drops non-finite; runs the recursion; builds the band and the exceedance flags; returns `{samples, alpha, bandMultiplier, exceedanceCount, aboveCount, belowCount, finalMean, finalStd, maxStd, totalSamples}`; per-sample `{index, x, raw, mean, variance, std, upper, lower, exceedance}`; `computeLineEwmsdLayout({series, ...})` -- projects raw + mean paths, the band fill + upper/lower edge paths, and the exceedance list; per-series carries `{id, label, color, alpha, bandMultiplier, points, rawPath, meanPath, bandFillPath, upperPath, lowerPath, exceedances, finiteCount, totalCount, exceedanceCount, aboveCount, belowCount, finalMean, finalStd, maxStd}`; per-point projected `{...sample, px, rawPy, meanPy, upperPy, lowerPy}`; degenerate canvas / empty / all-hidden -> ok=false; `describeLineEwmsdChart(series, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions EWMA + the standard deviation band + exceedances (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-ewmsd-aria-desc"` span; raw path + mean path + exceedance markers + every dot `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; mean path aria-label includes alpha; dot aria-label includes raw + EWMA mean + EWM std. data-section on every node (chart-line-ewmsd / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -x-label / -y-label / -series / -series-group / -band / -band-fill / -band-upper-path / -band-lower-path / -raw-path / -mean-path / -exceedance-marker / -dot / -tooltip / -tooltip-label / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-raw / -tooltip-mean / -tooltip-std / -tooltip-band / -tooltip-exceedance / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -legend-total-points / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-alpha / -badge-multiplier / -badge-std / -badge-exceedances); root mirrors `data-series-count`, `data-visible-series-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-total-exceedances`, `data-alpha`, `data-band-multiplier`, `data-animate`; raw path `data-kind="raw"`; mean path `data-kind="mean"`; band edge paths `data-kind="upper"` / `"lower"`; dots `data-mean`, `data-std`, `data-upper`, `data-lower`, `data-exceedance`; exceedance markers `data-exceedance`, `data-point-index`; series groups `data-series-alpha`, `data-series-band-multiplier`, `data-series-final-mean`, `data-series-final-std`, `data-series-max-std`, `data-series-exceedance-count`, `data-series-above-count`, `data-series-below-count`, `data-series-finite-count`. | 11.556 | UI: chart-line-zscore primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.574 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-zscore.tsx` ships `<ChartLineZscore>` -- pure-SVG two-panel line chart with a **rolling z-score panel**. Main panel (top) renders the raw series; the sub-panel (bottom) renders the rolling z-score as a **CONTINUOUS line** -- the standardized deviation of every point, not a set of threshold flags. For index t the trailing window is the slice `values[max(0, t - W + 1) .. t]` -- it expands from a single point until it reaches the full window length W. Over that window: `mean = average`, `std = population standard deviation` (sum of squared deviations / n), `z_t = (values[t] - mean) / std`. When std = 0 (a constant window, including the single-point window at index 0) the z-score is defined as 0 -- the value sits exactly on the window mean, zero standard deviations away -- so the z-score is a CONTINUOUS signal with no gaps from the very first point. The z-score answers "how many standard deviations is the current value from its recent rolling mean?" -- a scale-free, locally-standardized view of the series. **Verified properties: a constant series has std = 0 and z = 0 throughout; a linear ramp produces, once the window is full, a CONSTANT z-score of sqrt(3/2) for W = 3 (window [a, a+d, a+2d] has mean a+d, std d*sqrt(2/3), so the last point's z is d / (d*sqrt(2/3)) = sqrt(3/2)); a two-point window gives z = +/- 1; a single spike at the end of a W-length window of zeros has z = sqrt(W-1), so the SPIKE_BEYOND fixture ([0]x9 + [50], W = 10) yields exactly mean = 5, std = 15, z = 3; the trailing window size grows 1, 2, ..., W, W, W.** This primitive is deliberately distinct from `<ChartLineAnomaly>` (11.524): anomaly detection FLAGS the points whose rolling z-score exceeds a threshold, drawing discrete markers only on the extremes. ChartLineZscore instead shows the ENTIRE z-score curve as a continuous line in its own panel -- every point's standardized deviation, plotted over time. Optional dashed reference guide lines at +/- referenceLevel (default 2) give visual context, but no point is marked or flagged; the continuous curve is the whole point. A `band` field ('within' | 'beyond') is computed per sample purely for the tooltip text, not for any on-chart marker. Also distinct from `<ChartLineControl>` (11.534; a Shewhart SPC chart with GLOBAL constant mean +- k*sigma limits computed once over the whole series -- the z-score uses a ROLLING window so the standardization is local and adapts to a changing baseline), `<ChartLineCusum>` (11.554; accumulates deviations into a cumulative sum to detect drift -- the z-score is the PER-POINT standardized deviation not a cumulative quantity), and `<ChartLineEwmsd>` (11.555; plots an EWMA mean and an exponentially-weighted standard-deviation band around the raw series -- ChartLineZscore plots the standardized deviation itself (y - mean) / std as a separate signal, using a flat trailing window rather than exponential weighting). The canonical rolling-standardization view -- the building block under z-score anomaly detection, shown here in full rather than reduced to flags. API: `series: readonly ChartLineZscoreSeries[]` (`{id, label, data, color?, window?}`; the per-series window override beats chart-level); `window` (default 20; rolling window length; clamped to an integer >= 2); `referenceLevel` (default 2; magnitude of the +/- guide lines; clamped to >= 0); `subHeightRatio` (default 0.4; clamped to [0.1, 0.9]); `referenceColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots`/`showLegend`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showRaw`/`showZscore`/`showZeroLine`/`showReferenceLines`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility; standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX` / `formatZscore`; `onPointClick({series, point})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_ZSCORE_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/TICK_COUNT/SUB_HEIGHT_RATIO/RAW_STROKE_WIDTH/ZSCORE_STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/WINDOW/REFERENCE_LEVEL/PALETTE/REFERENCE_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineZscoreDefaultColor` (10-color cycle); `getLineZscoreFinitePoints`; `normaliseLineZscoreWindow` (integer >= 2; non-finite -> 20; floors fractional); `normaliseLineZscoreReferenceLevel` (>= 0; non-finite -> 2); `normaliseLineZscoreSubHeightRatio` (clamps to [0.1, 0.9]); `classifyLineZscoreBand(z, referenceLevel)` -- 'within' \| 'beyond'; strict comparison `|z| > referenceLevel` so boundary values are 'within'; non-finite -> 'within'; `computeRollingZScores(values, window)` -- the rolling mean / std / z recursion; returns per-index `{mean, std, z, windowSize}`; drops non-finite values so the output matches the finite-only length; `runLineZscore(points, options?)` -- canonical pipeline; sorts ascending; drops non-finite; computes the rolling z-score; returns `{samples, window, referenceLevel, beyondCount, maxAbsZ, finalZ, totalSamples}`; per-sample `{index, x, raw, mean, std, z, windowSize, band}`; `computeLineZscoreLayout({series, ...})` -- projects the main + sub panels and the raw + z-score paths; per-series carries `{id, label, color, window, referenceLevel, points, rawPath, zscorePath, finiteCount, totalCount, beyondCount, maxAbsZ, finalZ}`; per-point projected `{...sample, px, rawPy, zPy}`; sub-panel y range symmetric around zero; degenerate canvas / empty / all-hidden -> ok=false; `describeLineZscoreChart(series, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions rolling z-score + window + max absolute z + points beyond the reference level (No data when missing). Layout: two stacked SVG panels sharing the x axis -- main panel (top, ~60%) with the raw series line; z-score sub-panel (bottom, ~40%) with the continuous z-score line, a centred zero reference line, and dashed +/- referenceLevel guide lines. ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-zscore-aria-desc"` span; raw path + z-score path + every dot `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; z-score path aria-label includes the window length; dot aria-label includes raw + z-score. data-section on every node (chart-line-zscore / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -x-label / -y-label / -sub-label / -zero-line / -reference-lines / -reference-line / -series / -series-group / -raw-path / -zscore-path / -dot / -tooltip / -tooltip-label / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-raw / -tooltip-mean / -tooltip-std / -tooltip-zscore / -tooltip-band / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -legend-total-points / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-window / -badge-reference / -badge-max-z); root mirrors `data-series-count`, `data-visible-series-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-window`, `data-reference-level`, `data-max-abs-z`, `data-animate`; raw path `data-kind="raw"`; z-score path `data-kind="zscore"`; reference lines `data-side="upper"` / `"lower"`; dots `data-mean`, `data-std`, `data-zscore`, `data-window-size`, `data-band`; series groups `data-series-window`, `data-series-reference-level`, `data-series-max-abs-z`, `data-series-final-z`, `data-series-beyond-count`, `data-series-finite-count`. Tooltip on dots shows the series label, x, raw value, the window mean, the window std, the bold `z-score`, and a coloured `within`/`beyond <referenceLevel> sigma` row. Config badge in top-left calls out `ZS W=<window> ref=<referenceLevel> maxZ=<maxAbsZ>`. Legend shows per-series `(W=<window>; maxZ <value>)`. 77 vitest cases (defaults + helpers, computeRollingZScores incl. empty + single point + **constant -> std + z 0** + **window expands 1,2,3,3,3** + **linear ramp full-window z = sqrt(3/2)** + **two-point window z = +/- 1** + **SPIKE_BEYOND mean 5 / std 15 / z 3 verified** + drops non-finite, runLineZscore incl. empty + per-sample z matches the rolling computation + **classifies points beyond the reference level** + **z exactly at the reference stays within** + constant series stays within + sorts ascending + drops non-finite + tracks maxAbsZ + finalZ + exposes window + referenceLevel + records the trailing window size, computeLineZscoreLayout incl. empty/degenerate/hidden + stacked main + sub panels + raw + z-score paths + window + reference + maxAbsZ + beyond count exposed + symmetric sub y range + per-series window override + hidden excluded + bounds overrides + totalPoints, describeLineZscoreChart incl. No data + summary mentions rolling z-score + window + beyond, component render incl. empty / raw path kind=raw / **continuous z-score path kind=zscore** / zero line + hide via prop / reference guide lines upper + lower + hide via prop / hide raw / hide zscore / dots via showDots / config badge with window + reference + max z + hide via prop / ARIA region + img + sr-only desc / root data-* / series group data-* / tooltip with raw + mean + std + z-score + band rows + hide on leave + omit via prop / onPointClick payload / legend with window + max z + toggle + omit / animate flag + class / ref / displayName / custom ariaLabel / xLabel + yLabel + subLabel / dot exposes z-score + band attrs) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. | 11.557 | UI: chart-line-mad primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.575 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-mad.tsx` ships `<ChartLineMad>` -- pure-SVG line chart with a **rolling Median Absolute Deviation (MAD) band overlay**. Raw observations render as a dimmed reference line; the rolling median renders as a solid coloured centre line; a translucent `median +/- k * scaledMAD` band fills the robust spread envelope; points outside the band are flagged as robust outliers. For index t the trailing window is the slice `values[max(0, t - W + 1) .. t]` -- it expands from a single point until it reaches the full window length W. Over that window: `median = median of the window`, `MAD = median(|v - median| for v in the window)`, `scaledMAD = MAD * madScale`, `upper = median + k * scaledMAD`, `lower = median - k * scaledMAD`. MAD is the **Median Absolute Deviation** -- the median of the absolute deviations from the window median. madScale (default 1.4826) is the Gaussian-consistency constant that makes scale * MAD an unbiased estimator of the standard deviation for normal data. k (default 3) is the band-width multiplier (the canonical Hampel-style robust threshold). A point is a robust outlier when it falls strictly outside the band ('above' when raw > upper, 'below' when raw < lower). MAD has a **50% breakdown point**: up to half the window can be arbitrarily corrupted without changing it. A lone outlier inside the window does NOT inflate the MAD, so the band does not widen to swallow the outlier -- the outlier stays visibly outside the band. This is the decisive difference from mean/standard-deviation bands. **Verified properties: MAD([1,2,3,4,5]) = 1, MAD([1,2,3,4,5,6]) = 1.5; MAD([1,2,3,4,1000]) = 1 -- unchanged from [1,2,3,4,5] despite the 1000, the lone outlier does not inflate it; a constant window has MAD = 0 (the band collapses onto the median); for the OUTLIER_FIXTURE ([1,2,3,4,5,50], window 5) the final window [2,3,4,5,50] has median = 4, MAD = 1, scaledMAD = 1.4826, band [~-0.45, ~8.45], and the 50 lands far above it, flagged; a lone spike inside an otherwise-constant window ([10,10,10,10,100]) has MAD = 0 so the band collapses and the 100 is caught -- a mean/stddev band would have been inflated by the 100 itself and missed it.** Distinct from `<ChartLineHampel>` (11.548; the Hampel FILTER also uses the rolling median + MAD, but it REPLACES flagged outliers with the window median and draws an original-vs-filtered overlay -- it cleans the data -- whereas ChartLineMad draws the MAD BAND as an overlay, does not modify any value, and shows the robust spread envelope and which points fall outside it), `<ChartLineBollinger>` (11.537; Bollinger Bands use a rolling MEAN +/- k * rolling sample STANDARD DEVIATION -- both NON-robust, a single outlier shifts the mean and inflates the stddev, widening the band so the outlier hides inside it -- ChartLineMad uses the rolling MEDIAN and MAD, both robust, so the band stays tight and the outlier stands out), `<ChartLineEwmsd>` (11.555; plots an EWMA mean and an exponentially-weighted standard-deviation band -- still a non-robust mean/variance pair with exponential weighting -- ChartLineMad uses robust order statistics over a flat trailing window), `<ChartLineControl>` (11.534; a Shewhart SPC chart with GLOBAL constant non-robust mean +- k*sigma limits -- ChartLineMad's band is rolling and robust), and `<ChartLineZscore>` (11.556; a rolling z-score panel using mean / standard deviation -- ChartLineMad uses the robust median / MAD pair and shows the band on the main panel rather than a separate z-score panel). The robust counterpart of Bollinger Bands -- the order-statistic spread envelope that does not let outliers hide themselves. API: `series: readonly ChartLineMadSeries[]` (`{id, label, data, color?, window?, multiplier?}`; per-series window / multiplier overrides beat chart-level); `window` (default 20; rolling window length; clamped to an integer >= 2); `multiplier` (default 3; band-width k; clamped to >= 0); `madScale` (default 1.4826; MAD-to-sigma scale; clamped to > 0); `rawColor` / `outlierColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; `bandOpacity` (default 0.15); toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots`/`showLegend`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showRaw`/`showMedian`/`showBand`/`showOutliers`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility; standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX` / `formatCoefficient`; `onPointClick({series, point})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_MAD_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/RAW_STROKE_WIDTH/MEDIAN_STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/WINDOW/MULTIPLIER/SCALE/RAW_OPACITY/BAND_OPACITY/PALETTE/RAW_COLOR/OUTLIER_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineMadDefaultColor` (10-color cycle); `getLineMadFinitePoints`; `normaliseLineMadWindow` (integer >= 2; non-finite -> 20; floors fractional); `normaliseLineMadMultiplier` (>= 0; non-finite -> 3); `normaliseLineMadScale` (> 0; non-finite or non-positive -> 1.4826); `computeLineMadMedian(values)` -- median; drops non-finite; NaN for an empty input; `computeLineMadDeviation(values, centerMedian?)` -- the MAD (median of absolute deviations from the median); accepts a pre-computed centre median; NaN for empty; `classifyLineMadOutlier(value, lower, upper)` -- 'above' \| 'below' \| 'within'; strict comparison so boundary values are 'within'; non-finite -> 'within'; `computeRollingMad(values, window)` -- rolling median + MAD per index; returns `{median, mad, windowSize}`; drops non-finite values so the output matches the finite-only length; `runLineMad(points, options?)` -- canonical pipeline; sorts ascending; drops non-finite; builds the band and the outlier flags; returns `{samples, window, multiplier, madScale, outlierCount, aboveCount, belowCount, finalMedian, finalScaledMad, maxScaledMad, totalSamples}`; per-sample `{index, x, raw, median, mad, scaledMad, upper, lower, outlier, windowSize}`; `computeLineMadLayout({series, ...})` -- projects raw + median paths, the band fill + upper/lower edge paths, and the outlier list; per-series carries `{id, label, color, window, multiplier, madScale, points, rawPath, medianPath, bandFillPath, upperPath, lowerPath, outliers, finiteCount, totalCount, outlierCount, aboveCount, belowCount, finalMedian, finalScaledMad, maxScaledMad}`; per-point projected `{...sample, px, rawPy, medianPy, upperPy, lowerPy}`; degenerate canvas / empty / all-hidden -> ok=false; `describeLineMadChart(series, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions the Median Absolute Deviation band + window + robust outliers (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-mad-aria-desc"` span; raw path + median path + outlier markers + every dot `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; median path aria-label includes the window length; dot aria-label includes raw + rolling median + scaled MAD. data-section on every node (chart-line-mad / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -x-label / -y-label / -series / -series-group / -band / -band-fill / -band-upper-path / -band-lower-path / -raw-path / -median-path / -outlier-marker / -dot / -tooltip / -tooltip-label / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-raw / -tooltip-median / -tooltip-mad / -tooltip-band / -tooltip-outlier / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -legend-total-points / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-window / -badge-multiplier / -badge-scale / -badge-outliers); root mirrors `data-series-count`, `data-visible-series-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-total-outliers`, `data-window`, `data-multiplier`, `data-mad-scale`, `data-animate`; raw path `data-kind="raw"`; median path `data-kind="median"`; band edge paths `data-kind="upper"` / `"lower"`; dots `data-median`, `data-mad`, `data-scaled-mad`, `data-upper`, `data-lower`, `data-outlier`; outlier markers `data-outlier`, `data-point-index`; series groups `data-series-window`, `data-series-multiplier`, `data-series-mad-scale`, `data-series-final-median`, `data-series-final-scaled-mad`, `data-series-max-scaled-mad`, `data-series-outlier-count`, `data-series-above-count`, `data-series-below-count`, `data-series-finite-count`. Tooltip on dots shows the series label, x, raw value, bold `rolling median`, `scaled MAD`, the `band: [lower, upper]` range, and -- when the point is outside the band -- a coloured `robust outlier (<above\|below>)` row. Config badge in top-left calls out `MAD W=<window> k=<multiplier> c=<madScale> out=<count>`. Legend shows per-series `(W=<window>; out <count>)`. 86 vitest cases (defaults + helpers, computeLineMadMedian incl. empty -> NaN + odd/even length + sorts internally + drops non-finite, computeLineMadDeviation incl. empty -> NaN + **MAD([1..5]) = 1** + **MAD([1..6]) = 1.5** + **robust MAD([1,2,3,4,1000]) = 1** + constant -> 0 + pre-computed centre median, computeRollingMad incl. empty + **window expands 1,2,3,3,3** + rolling median + MAD verified on a ramp + single-point window -> MAD 0 + constant -> MAD 0 + drops non-finite, classifyLineMadOutlier incl. above/below/within/strict-boundary/non-finite, runLineMad incl. empty + **band = median +/- k * scaledMAD** + **flags a robust outlier outside the band** + **robustness: a lone spike inside the window is still flagged (MAD 0)** + constant series no outliers + **index 0 always within** + sorts ascending + drops non-finite + tracks finalMedian/finalScaledMad/maxScaledMad + exposes window+multiplier+madScale, computeLineMadLayout incl. empty/degenerate/hidden + raw + median + band fill + edge paths + config + outliers exposed + per-series window + multiplier overrides + y range covers the band + hidden excluded + bounds overrides + totalPoints, describeLineMadChart incl. No data + summary mentions Median Absolute Deviation + window + robust outliers, component render incl. empty / raw path kind=raw / median path kind=median / band fill + upper/lower edge paths / hide band via prop / hide raw / hide median / **outlier marker for the detected robust outlier** / hide outliers / dots via showDots / config badge with window + multiplier + scale + outlier count + hide via prop / ARIA region + img + sr-only desc / root data-* / series group data-* / tooltip with raw + median + MAD + band rows + an outlier row on the flagged dot + hide on leave + omit via prop / onPointClick payload / legend with window + outlier + toggle + omit / animate flag + class / ref / displayName / custom ariaLabel / xLabel + yLabel / constant series renders zero outliers) all pass under vitest 4.1.5.
