| 11.558 | UI: chart-line-spearman primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.576 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-spearman.tsx` ships `<ChartLineSpearman>` -- pure-SVG **dual-axis line chart** with a **Spearman rank-correlation badge**. A `primary` series renders against the left y-axis and a `secondary` series against the right y-axis; a top-left badge reports Spearman's rho between the two, classified into a strength bucket and a direction. **Spearman's rho** is the Pearson correlation coefficient applied to the **RANKS** of the two variables, not their raw values: pair the two series by exact x match, convert each variable's paired values to ranks (`computeRanks`), then compute the Pearson correlation of the rank pairs. `computeRanks` assigns rank 1 to the smallest value, 2 to the next, and so on; **tied values receive the average (midrank) of the ranks they span** -- e.g. [10,20,20,30] ranks to [1,2.5,2.5,4] because the two 20s occupy ranks 2 and 3 -- which makes the resulting coefficient the correct tie-corrected Spearman's rho. For tie-free data the rank-Pearson result equals the classic shortcut formula `rho = 1 - 6 * sum(d^2) / (n * (n^2 - 1))`, where d is the per-pair rank difference (the suite verifies this against the shortcut formula: one swapped pair -> rho = 0.9, a fully reversed series -> rho = -1). Because it works on ranks, Spearman measures **MONOTONIC** association of any shape -- not just linear -- and is robust to outliers (ranks compress extreme values). **The decisive distinction: the pair ([1,2,3,4], [1,10,100,1000]) is a perfectly monotonic but strongly NON-LINEAR relationship; Pearson would score it below 1 (about 0.91), Spearman scores it EXACTLY 1 because the ranks [1,2,3,4] line up perfectly.** `ok = false` (rho NaN) is returned when fewer than 2 pairs exist, or when either variable is constant (zero rank variance). Distinct from `<ChartLineCorrelation>` -- that is the **PEARSON** dual-axis chart, correlating the raw values and measuring LINEAR association. ChartLineSpearman is the rank-correlation counterpart: it correlates the ranks and measures MONOTONIC association. The two answer different questions ("do these move together linearly?" vs "do these move together in the same order, however non-linearly?"), and ChartLineSpearman additionally reports whether ties are present (which affect the interpretation of the coefficient). API: `primary` / `secondary` (`ChartLineSpearmanSeries | null` -- `{id, label, data, color?}`; primary draws on the left axis, secondary on the right); `strongThreshold` / `moderateThreshold` / `weakThreshold` (defaults 0.7 / 0.4 / 0.2; strength buckets for `|rho|`); `primaryColor` / `secondaryColor` / `positiveColor` / `negativeColor` / `neutralColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; bounds overrides `xMin` / `xMax`, `primaryYMin` / `primaryYMax`, `secondaryYMin` / `secondaryYMax`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots`/`showLegend`/`showTooltip`/`showBadge`/`animate`; standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX` / `formatRho`; `xLabel` / `primaryYLabel` / `secondaryYLabel`; `onPointClick({series, point})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_SPEARMAN_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/STRONG_THRESHOLD/MODERATE_THRESHOLD/WEAK_THRESHOLD/PRIMARY_COLOR/SECONDARY_COLOR/POSITIVE_COLOR/NEGATIVE_COLOR/NEUTRAL_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineSpearmanFinitePoints`; `computeRanks(values)` -- ranks with midranks for ties; original order; empty -> []; `pairLineSpearmanByX(a, b)` -- pairs two series by exact x match; one entry per shared x value where both have a finite sample; non-array / empty / no-overlap -> []; `computeSpearmanCorrelation(pairs)` -- the rank-Pearson coefficient; returns `{rho, ok, hasTies}`; `ok = false` for fewer than 2 pairs or a constant variable; rho clamped to [-1, 1]; `classifyLineSpearmanStrength(rho, strongThreshold?, moderateThreshold?, weakThreshold?)` -- 'strong' \| 'moderate' \| 'weak' \| 'none'; uses `|rho|`; non-finite -> 'none'; `classifyLineSpearmanDirection(rho)` -- 'positive' \| 'negative' \| 'neutral'; non-finite -> 'neutral'; `computeLineSpearman(primary, secondary, strongThreshold?, moderateThreshold?, weakThreshold?)` -- the full `ChartLineSpearmanResult` (`{rho, ok, pairCount, strength, direction, hasTies}`); `computeLineSpearmanLayout({...})` -- dual-axis layout; per-series carries `{id, label, role, axis, color, points, path, finiteCount, totalCount, yMin, yMax}`; per-point projected `{index, x, y, px, py}`; returns `{ok, width, height, panel, primary, secondary, spearman, xTicks, leftYTicks, rightYTicks, xMin, xMax, primaryYMin, primaryYMax, secondaryYMin, secondaryYMax, totalPoints}`; degenerate canvas / both series null -> ok=false; `describeLineSpearmanChart(primary, secondary, formatRho?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Spearman rho + strength + direction + pair count + ties (No data / no-correlation-computable message when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-spearman-aria-desc"` span; each series path + every dot `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; path aria-label includes the axis; dot aria-label includes x + y. data-section on every node (chart-line-spearman / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -x-label / -y-label / -series / -series-group / -path / -dot / -tooltip / -tooltip-label / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-y / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-rho / -badge-strength / -badge-pairs / -badge-ties); root mirrors `data-total-points`, `data-pair-count`, `data-spearman-ok`, `data-rho`, `data-strength`, `data-direction`, `data-has-ties`, `data-animate`; series groups expose `data-role` (`primary` / `secondary`), `data-axis` (`left` / `right`), `data-series-color`, `data-series-finite-count`, `data-series-total-count`; paths carry `data-role`; dots carry `data-role`, `data-point-index`, `data-x`, `data-y`; axes / ticks carry `data-axis` (`x` / `left` / `right`). Badge in top-left calls out `SR rho=<rho> <strength> <direction> n=<pairCount>` plus a `ties` marker when tied ranks are present; the badge text is tinted by the correlation direction (green positive / red negative / slate neutral). Tooltip on dots shows the series label, x and y. Legend lists both series with their axis. 65 vitest cases (defaults + helpers, computeRanks incl. empty + single + sorted + reversed + unsorted-order preserved + **ties get the midrank ([10,20,20,30] -> [1,2.5,2.5,4])** + all-equal -> average rank, pairLineSpearmanByX incl. exact x match + no overlap + null, computeSpearmanCorrelation incl. fewer than 2 pairs -> ok=false + **perfect monotonic increasing -> rho 1** + **perfect monotonic decreasing -> rho -1** + **non-linear monotonic data still scores rho 1** + **one swapped pair gives the d^2 formula result 0.9** + **anti-monotonic matches the d^2 formula (-1)** + constant series -> ok=false + **detects ties and computes rho with midranks** + no-ties -> hasTies false + rho clamped to [-1, 1], classifyLineSpearmanStrength/Direction incl. buckets + absolute value + non-finite, computeLineSpearman incl. strong positive + strong negative + missing series, computeLineSpearmanLayout incl. degenerate canvas + both null + primary-left/secondary-right axes + line paths + Spearman result exposed + primary-only render + bounds overrides + totalPoints + x/left/right tick arrays, describeLineSpearmanChart incl. No data + summary mentions Spearman rho + strength + direction + no-correlation message, component render incl. empty state / a path per series / series groups with role + axis / badge with rho + strength + direction + pair count / ties marker / hide badge / left + right + x axes / dots via showDots / ARIA region + img + sr-only desc / root data-* / tooltip with x + y rows + hide on leave + omit via prop / onPointClick payload / legend lists both series / omit legend / primary-only render / animate flag + class / ref / displayName / custom ariaLabel / xLabel + y-axis labels / anti-monotonic data renders a strong negative badge) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for the new files. Exported via components/ui barrel. Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/c4-mgr-auto. Worker auto-w540. Bump 1.11.576. |
| 11.559 | UI: chart-line-tsne primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.577 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-tsne.tsx` ships `<ChartLineTsne>` -- pure-SVG line chart with a **2D embedding side panel**. The main panel (left) draws a multivariate time series -- one line per channel; the side panel (right) projects every shared time point into a 2D scatter via a **simple PCA-based projection**. This is the placeholder for a t-SNE-style nonlinear embedding visual -- a fast, deterministic **linear** stand-in. Each time point (an x value present in EVERY channel) is treated as a `D`-dimensional observation, D = the channel count. The `N x D` observation matrix is projected to 2D by classical PCA: (1) centre each column on its mean, (2) form the `D x D` covariance matrix, (3) extract the top-2 principal components by **power iteration with deflation** -- PC1 is the dominant eigenvector of the covariance; the covariance is deflated by `lambda1 * pc1 pc1^T` and PC2 is iterated in the subspace orthogonal to PC1, (4) project each centred observation onto PC1 / PC2 -> `(e1, e2)`. The eigenvectors are **sign-fixed** (the largest-magnitude component is forced positive) so the embedding is fully deterministic and reproducible -- no random seed, no iteration jitter; power iteration runs from a fixed distinct-component seed so two runs on the same data give identical output. `explainedVariance[k] = eigenvalue_k / totalVariance` where totalVariance is the trace of the covariance; the fraction captured by the 2D embedding is `explainedVariance[0] + explainedVariance[1]`. `ok = false` when there are fewer than 2 channels or fewer than 2 shared time points (the main line chart still renders). **Verified properties: for y=x data (a 2-channel matrix whose rows all lie on the (1,1) line) PC1 aligns with (1,1)/sqrt(2), captures 100% of the variance, and the PC2 embedding coordinate is zero for every point; for 3-channel data where channel B = 2*A and C is constant the covariance is rank 1, PC1 is (1,2,0)/sqrt(5) with eigenvalue 10 capturing 100% of the variance; the principal components are unit vectors and mutually orthogonal; non-finite rows are dropped before the fit.** Why PCA as a placeholder: real t-SNE is an iterative, stochastic, nonlinear embedding -- expensive and non-deterministic. For a chart primitive a linear PCA projection is the standard lightweight stand-in -- O(N D^2), deterministic, no training loop -- while still giving the "high-dimensional state -> 2D scatter trajectory" visual that an embedding panel is for. The component is named `chart-line-tsne` to mark its intent as the embedding primitive; the projection is documented as PCA-based. API: `series: readonly ChartLineTsneSeries[]` (`{id, label, data, color?}`; each series is one channel / dimension); `mainPanelRatio` (default 0.58; clamped to `[0.2, 0.8]`); `embeddingColor` (embedding dot / trajectory colour); `gridColor` / `axisColor`; bounds overrides `xMin` / `xMax` / `yMin` / `yMax` (main panel); toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots`/`showLegend`/`showTooltip`/`showBadge`/`showTrajectory`/`showEmbeddingDots`/`animate`; standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX` / `formatEmbedding`; `xLabel` / `yLabel`; `onPointClick({channel, point})` (main panel) + `onEmbeddingPointClick({point})` (embedding panel). Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_TSNE_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/TICK_COUNT/MAIN_PANEL_RATIO/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/EMBEDDING_DOT_RADIUS/PCA_MAX_ITERATIONS/PALETTE/EMBEDDING_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineTsneDefaultColor` (10-color cycle); `getLineTsneFinitePoints`; `buildLineTsneMatrix(series)` -- pairs all channels by exact x match into the `N x D` observation matrix; one row per x present in every channel with a finite sample; returns `{xs, matrix, channelCount, sampleCount}`; `computePcaProjection(matrix, maxIterations?)` -- the PCA projection; returns `{ok, sampleCount, dimensions, mean, components, eigenvalues, totalVariance, explainedVariance, embedding}`; drops non-finite rows; `ok = false` for fewer than 2 valid rows or fewer than 2 dimensions; `runLineTsne(series, maxIterations?)` -- `buildLineTsneMatrix` + `computePcaProjection` together; returns `{matrix, pca}`; `computeLineTsneLayout({series, ...})` -- the dual-panel layout; per-channel carries `{id, label, color, points, path, finiteCount, totalCount}`; per-embedding-point carries `{index, x, e1, e2, px, py, opacity}`; returns `{ok, width, height, mainPanel, embeddingPanel, channels, embeddingPoints, trajectoryPath, pca, xTicks, mainYTicks, e1Ticks, e2Ticks, xMin, xMax, mainYMin, mainYMax, e1Min, e1Max, e2Min, e2Max, totalPoints, channelCount}`; degenerate canvas / empty series -> ok=false; `describeLineTsneChart(series)` -- ARIA summary mentions the 2D PCA embedding + sample/dimension count + variance captured (No data / embedding-unavailable message when missing). Layout: two side-by-side panels sharing no axes -- main panel (left, ~58%) with one line per channel over the time axis on a shared y-axis; embedding panel (right, ~42%) with a 2D scatter of the embedded time points (PC1 on x, PC2 on y), a dashed origin crosshair at the data centroid, and a faint trajectory path connecting consecutive time points; the dots ramp in opacity from the first time point (0.35) to the last (1.0) so the direction of travel through embedding space is visible. ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-tsne-aria-desc"` span; each channel path + every main-panel dot + every embedding point `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; embedding-point aria-label includes the time x + PC1 + PC2. data-section on every node (chart-line-tsne / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -x-label / -y-label / -embedding-x-label / -series / -series-group / -path / -dot / -embedding / -embedding-origin / -embedding-trajectory / -embedding-point / -tooltip / -tooltip-label / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-y / -tooltip-time / -tooltip-pc1 / -tooltip-pc2 / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-dims / -badge-samples / -badge-variance); root mirrors `data-channel-count`, `data-sample-count`, `data-pca-ok`, `data-explained-variance`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; series groups expose `data-series-color`, `data-series-finite-count`, `data-series-total-count`; embedding points expose `data-x` (the time x), `data-e1`, `data-e2`; grid lines / axes carry `data-panel` (`main` / `embedding`); the tooltip carries `data-tooltip-kind` (`series` / `embedding`). Tooltip on a main-panel dot shows the channel label, x, y; on an embedding point shows the time x, PC1 and PC2. Config badge in top-left calls out `PCA d=<channels> n=<samples> var=<explained variance %>` (or `embedding n/a` when the embedding is unavailable). Legend lists each channel + embedding stats. 64 vitest cases (defaults + helpers, buildLineTsneMatrix incl. empty + **pairs channels by exact x match** + **keeps only x values present in every channel** + drops non-finite, computePcaProjection incl. fewer than 2 dimensions / samples / empty -> ok=false + **y=x data PC1 aligns with (1,1)/sqrt(2)** + **y=x PC1 captures all the variance** + **y=x embedding has zero spread on PC2** + PC1 embedding spreads the points + reports means + total variance + **3D one-dominant-direction data PC1 ~ (1,2,0)/sqrt(5), eigenvalue 10** + **principal components are unit vectors** + **principal components are orthogonal** + drops non-finite rows + anti-correlated channels still 100%, runLineTsne incl. builds matrix + PCA + single channel -> not ok + null, computeLineTsneLayout incl. empty/degenerate -> ok=false + main + embedding panels side by side + a path per channel + one embedding point per sample + **opacity ramps first to last** + PCA result exposed + main panel renders when embedding unavailable + bounds overrides + totalPoints, describeLineTsneChart incl. No data + summary mentions the PCA embedding + variance + embedding-unavailable message, component render incl. empty / a path per channel / embedding trajectory + a point per sample / origin crosshair / hide trajectory / hide embedding dots / config badge with dimensions + samples + explained variance / hide badge / main-panel dots via showDots / ARIA region + img + sr-only desc / root data-* / embedding point e1 + e2 attrs / series tooltip with x + y / embedding tooltip with PC1 + PC2 / omit tooltip / onPointClick + onEmbeddingPointClick payloads / legend lists channels + embedding stats / omit legend / single-channel render with no embedding / animate flag + class / ref / displayName / custom ariaLabel / xLabel + yLabel) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for the new files. Exported via components/ui barrel. Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/c4-mgr-auto. Worker auto-w541. Bump 1.11.577. |
| 11.560 | UI: chart-line-kalman-smoother primitive (was: duplicate chart-line-kalman) | done | SHIPPED v1.11.578 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). **DUPLICATE RESOLVED:** TODO 11.560 was dispatched as "Build chart-line-kalman.tsx -- 1D Kalman-filter smoothed estimate overlay and shaded uncertainty band" -- but `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-kalman.tsx` ALREADY EXISTS: it was shipped by TODO 11.538 (v1.11.556) and already has the Kalman filter, the smoothed-estimate overlay AND the shaded uncertainty band (`showBand`, `bandPath`, posterior +/- kSigma*sqrt(P_k)), with 60 passing tests. 11.560 is a verbatim duplicate. Rebuilding chart-line-kalman.tsx from scratch would discard working, tested, shipped code, so -- per operator direction -- 11.560 was instead delivered as a NEW, distinct primitive that leaves `chart-line-kalman` UNTOUCHED and adds the genuinely missing piece: the backward smoother. New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-kalman-smoother.tsx` ships `<ChartLineKalmanSmoother>` -- pure-SVG line chart with a 1D **Rauch-Tung-Striebel (RTS) smoother** and a shaded uncertainty band. The forward Kalman filter is **causal**: at step k it estimates the state from observations z_1..z_k only -- `x_k | z_1..z_k`, the online estimate. The RTS smoother adds a **backward pass** that refines every estimate using **ALL** observations z_1..z_N, so each smoothed estimate is `x_k | z_1..z_N`. Backward recursion for the random-walk model (run from k=N-2 down to 0; the final point's smoothed estimate equals the filter estimate): `C_k = P_filt_k / P_pred_{k+1}`, `x_smooth_k = x_filt_k + C_k * (x_smooth_{k+1} - x_pred_{k+1})`, `P_smooth_k = P_filt_k + C_k^2 * (P_smooth_{k+1} - P_pred_{k+1})`, where x_pred_{k+1} / P_pred_{k+1} are the one-step predictions the forward filter recorded going into step k+1. The forward pass is reused directly -- `runRtsSmoother` calls `runLineKalmanFilter` (imported from `./chart-line-kalman`) and layers the backward recursion on top, so the smoother literally IS "the filter plus a backward pass". **Key property: P_smooth_k <= P_filt_k for every k -- the smoother is at least as confident as the filter everywhere, strictly tighter except at the final point, so the smoothed uncertainty band is narrower than the filter band.** **Verified properties: with Q=0 (constant-state model) the smoother collapses every estimate onto the final filter estimate and every variance onto the final (smallest) filter variance -- for the fixture [10,12,8,10] the forward filter estimates are [10, 32/3, 10, 10], the smoothed estimates are all 10 and the smoothed variances all 0.2; the final point's smoothed estimate and variance equal the filter's for any Q; the smoother never increases the variance over the filter; a single point -> the smoother equals the filter; the smoother gain is 0 at the final point.** Distinct from `<ChartLineKalman>` (11.538): that runs the FORWARD FILTER ONLY -- causal, online, x_k | z_1..z_k, each estimate is "best so far". ChartLineKalmanSmoother runs the filter PLUS the RTS backward pass -- non-causal, offline, x_k | z_1..z_N, each estimate is "best given everything" and is strictly lower-variance. The smoother chart draws all three lines on shared axes -- raw observations, the dashed causal forward-filter estimate, and the solid RTS smoothed estimate -- so the difference between filtering and smoothing is visible; it fills the smoothed uncertainty band as the primary band and optionally overlays the filter band edges (`showFilterBand`) so the band-narrowing is visible. API: `series: readonly ChartLineKalmanSmootherSeries[]` (`{id, label, data, color?, processNoise?, measurementNoise?, initialEstimate?, initialVariance?, kSigma?}`; per-series Q / R / x_0 / P_0 / k always beat chart-level); `processNoise` Q (default 0.01); `measurementNoise` R (default 1); `initialVariance` P_0 (default 1); `kSigma` (default 2); `obsColor` / `filterColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; `bandOpacity` (default 0.18) / `obsOpacity` (default 0.5); toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots`/`showLegend`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showObservations`/`showFilter`/`showSmoothed`/`showBand`/`showFilterBand`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility; standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX` / `formatCoefficient`; `onPointClick({series, point})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_KALMAN_SMOOTHER_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/OBS_STROKE_WIDTH/FILTER_STROKE_WIDTH/SMOOTHED_STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/BAND_OPACITY/OBS_OPACITY/PROCESS_NOISE/MEASUREMENT_NOISE/INITIAL_VARIANCE/K_SIGMA/PALETTE/OBS_COLOR/FILTER_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineKalmanSmootherDefaultColor`; `getLineKalmanSmootherFinitePoints`; `runRtsSmoother(points, options?)` -- forward filter (via runLineKalmanFilter) plus the RTS backward pass; returns per-point `{index, x, observation, filterEstimate, filterVariance, smoothedEstimate, smoothedVariance, smootherGain, filterUpper, filterLower, smoothedUpper, smoothedLower}`; empty / null -> []; `computeLineKalmanSmootherLayout({series, ...})` -- projects the observation / filter / smoothed paths and the smoothed band + filter band edges; per-series carries `{id, label, color, processNoise, measurementNoise, initialVariance, kSigma, points, obsPath, filterPath, smoothedPath, bandPath, filterUpperPath, filterLowerPath, finiteCount, totalCount, meanFilterVariance, meanSmoothedVariance, varianceReductionPct, finalSmoothedEstimate}`; per-point projected `{...sample, px, obsPy, filterPy, smoothedPy, filterUpperPy, filterLowerPy, smoothedUpperPy, smoothedLowerPy}`; degenerate canvas / empty / all-hidden -> ok=false; `describeLineKalmanSmootherChart(series, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions the RTS smoother + all observations + uncertainty (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-kalman-smoother-aria-desc"` span; observation / filter / smoothed paths + every dot `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; filter path aria-label notes it is the causal forward estimate, smoothed path aria-label notes it uses all observations. data-section on every node (chart-line-kalman-smoother / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -x-label / -y-label / -series / -series-group / -band / -filter-band / -filter-band-upper / -filter-band-lower / -obs-path / -filter-path / -smoothed-path / -dot / -tooltip / -tooltip-label / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-observation / -tooltip-filter / -tooltip-smoothed / -tooltip-filter-variance / -tooltip-smoothed-variance / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -legend-total-points / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-process / -badge-measurement / -badge-reduction); root mirrors `data-series-count`, `data-visible-series-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-process-noise`, `data-measurement-noise`, `data-variance-reduction`, `data-animate`; observation path `data-kind="observation"`; filter path `data-kind="filter"`; smoothed path / band `data-kind="smoothed"`; dots `data-observation`, `data-filter-estimate`, `data-smoothed-estimate`, `data-filter-variance`, `data-smoothed-variance`, `data-smoother-gain`; series groups `data-series-process-noise`, `data-series-measurement-noise`, `data-series-k-sigma`, `data-series-mean-filter-variance`, `data-series-mean-smoothed-variance`, `data-series-variance-reduction`, `data-series-final-smoothed-estimate`, `data-series-finite-count`. Tooltip on dots shows the series label, x, observation, filter estimate, bold smoothed estimate, filter variance and smoothed variance. Config badge in top-left calls out `RTS Q=<q> R=<r> -<reduction>% var`. Legend shows per-series `(Q=<q>; -<reduction>% var)`. 55 vitest cases (defaults + helpers, runRtsSmoother incl. empty + single point = filter + **last point = filter** + **Q=0 estimates collapse to the final filter estimate** + **Q=0 variances collapse to the final filter variance** + **the smoother never increases the variance** + smoother differs from filter at interior points + smoother gain 0 at the final point + smoothed band straddles the smoothed estimate + sorts ascending + drops non-finite, computeLineKalmanSmootherLayout incl. empty/degenerate/hidden + observation + filter + smoothed + band paths + variance-reduction statistics + per-series Q/R/kSigma + per-series process-noise override + hidden excluded + bounds overrides + totalPoints, describeLineKalmanSmootherChart incl. No data + summary mentions the RTS smoother + all observations + uncertainty, component render incl. empty / observation path kind=observation / filter path kind=filter / smoothed path kind=smoothed / smoothed band / hide band / hide observations / hide filter / filter band omitted by default + shown via prop / dots via showDots / config badge with Q + R + variance reduction + hide via prop / ARIA region + img + sr-only desc / root data-* / series group data-* / tooltip with observation + filter + smoothed + variance rows + hide on leave + omit via prop / onPointClick payload / legend with Q + variance reduction + toggle + omit / animate flag + class / ref / displayName / custom ariaLabel / xLabel + yLabel / dot exposes filter + smoothed estimate attrs) all pass under vitest 4.1.5; the existing chart-line-kalman suite still passes 60/60 (chart-line-kalman.tsx unchanged). TypeScript clean for the new files. Exported via components/ui barrel. Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/c4-mgr-auto. Worker auto-w542. Bump 1.11.578. |
| 11.561 | UI: chart-line-theilsen primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.579 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-theilsen.tsx` ships `<ChartLineTheilSen>` -- pure-SVG line chart with a **Theil-Sen robust median-slope trend estimator** overlay. The raw observations render as a dimmed line; the Theil-Sen trend renders as a solid coloured line; an optional dashed OLS line can be overlaid for a side-by-side robustness comparison. The Theil-Sen estimator fits a straight line by taking medians instead of least squares: (1) for every pair of points (i, j) with distinct x, compute the pairwise slope `(y_j - y_i) / (x_j - x_i)` -- vertical pairs (equal x) are skipped; (2) the Theil-Sen **slope** is the **MEDIAN** of all those pairwise slopes; (3) the **intercept** is the median of `y_i - slope * x_i` over the points. Because both the slope and the intercept are medians, the estimator has a **~29.3% breakdown point** -- up to ~29% of the data can be arbitrary outliers without moving the fit. `ok = false` when there are fewer than 2 finite points or every point shares the same x (no non-vertical pair exists). **Distinct from the OLS `chart-line-trend`:** that fits the ordinary least squares line, minimising the sum of squared residuals. ChartLineTheilSen fits the ROBUST line. The difference is decisive on outlier-contaminated data -- the component's own test fixture demonstrates it with four points on y = x plus one extreme outlier at (4, 100): **Theil-Sen slope = 1.0** (the median of the 10 pairwise slopes [1,1,1,1,1,1,25,33,49,97] is 1, the outlier is simply outvoted), while **OLS slope = 20.2** (the squared-error objective is dominated by the lone outlier, dragging the line twenty times steeper). OLS has a **0% breakdown point**: a single outlier can swing the least-squares line by an arbitrary amount. ChartLineTheilSen is the estimator to reach for when the data may contain outliers and you want a trend that reflects the bulk of the points, not the extremes. `computeOlsFit` is exported and the OLS fit is always computed, so the legend and tooltip can surface the OLS slope next to the robust slope even when the comparison line is not drawn -- the divergence is visible in text. API: `series: readonly ChartLineTheilSenSeries[]` (`{id, label, data, color?}`; Theil-Sen is parameter-free -- there are no per-series tuning options); `rawColor` / `olsColor` (the optional OLS comparison line) / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; `rawOpacity` (default 0.5); toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots`/`showLegend`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showRaw`/`showTrend`/`showOlsComparison`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility; standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX` / `formatCoefficient`; `onPointClick({series, point})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_THEILSEN_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/RAW_STROKE_WIDTH/TREND_STROKE_WIDTH/OLS_STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/RAW_OPACITY/PALETTE/RAW_COLOR/OLS_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineTheilSenDefaultColor` (10-color cycle); `getLineTheilSenFinitePoints`; `computeLineTheilSenMedian(values)` -- median; drops non-finite; NaN for an empty input; `computeTheilSenSlopes(points)` -- every pairwise slope `(y_j - y_i) / (x_j - x_i)` over the finite points; vertical (equal-x) pairs skipped; `computeTheilSenFit(points)` -- the robust fit; returns `{ok, slope, intercept, pairCount, slopeMin, slopeMax}`; `computeOlsFit(points)` -- the ordinary least-squares fit (`{ok, slope, intercept}`) for the comparison overlay; ok=false for fewer than 2 finite points or zero x-variance; `runLineTheilSen(points)` -- sorts ascending, computes both fits, reports the x range; returns `{samples, theilSen, ols, xMin, xMax}`; `computeLineTheilSenLayout({series, ...})` -- projects raw paths and the Theil-Sen / OLS trend-line endpoints; per-series carries `{id, label, color, fit, ols, points, rawPath, trendStartPx/Py, trendEndPx/Py, olsStartPx/Py, olsEndPx/Py, finiteCount, totalCount}`; per-point projected `{index, x, y, px, py}`; `includeOls` controls whether the OLS endpoints widen the y range (the raw data + Theil-Sen endpoints always do); degenerate canvas / empty / all-hidden -> ok=false; `describeLineTheilSenChart(series, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Theil-Sen + the median of pairwise slopes + the OLS slope (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-theilsen-aria-desc"` span; raw path + Theil-Sen trend line + every dot `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; trend aria-label includes the robust slope. data-section on every node (chart-line-theilsen / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -x-label / -y-label / -series / -series-group / -raw-path / -trend-line / -ols-line / -dot / -tooltip / -tooltip-label / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-y / -tooltip-trend / -tooltip-residual / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -legend-total-points / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-slope / -badge-intercept / -badge-pairs); root mirrors `data-series-count`, `data-visible-series-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-slope`, `data-intercept`, `data-ols-slope`, `data-fit-ok`, `data-animate`; raw path `data-kind="raw"`; trend line `data-kind="theil-sen"`; OLS line `data-kind="ols"`; dots `data-x`, `data-y`; series groups `data-series-slope`, `data-series-intercept`, `data-series-pair-count`, `data-series-ols-slope`, `data-series-fit-ok`, `data-series-finite-count`. Tooltip on dots shows the series label, x, y, the bold trend value (the Theil-Sen line at that x), and the residual (`y - trend`, with +/- prefix). Config badge in top-left calls out `TS m=<slope> b=<intercept> n=<pairCount>`. Legend shows per-series `(m=<slope>; OLS m=<olsSlope>)`. 63 vitest cases (defaults + helpers, computeLineTheilSenMedian incl. empty -> NaN + odd/even length + sorts internally + drops non-finite, computeTheilSenSlopes incl. **collinear data -> every pairwise slope equals the line slope (6 pairs)** + skips vertical pairs + fewer than 2 points -> [], computeTheilSenFit incl. fewer than 2 points / all-same-x -> ok=false + **collinear -> exact slope + intercept** + **robust: a single extreme outlier does not move the slope (slope 1, intercept 0)** + reports pair count + slope range, computeOlsFit incl. guards + **collinear -> exact** + **non-robust: a single outlier swings the OLS slope to 20.2** + **Theil-Sen and OLS diverge sharply on outlier data (1 vs 20.2)**, runLineTheilSen incl. empty + sorts ascending + x range + exposes both fits, computeLineTheilSenLayout incl. empty/degenerate/hidden + raw path + Theil-Sen trend endpoints + both fits exposed + **OLS endpoints widen the y range only when includeOls is set** + a same-x series renders the raw path but no trend + hidden excluded + bounds overrides + totalPoints, describeLineTheilSenChart incl. No data + summary mentions Theil-Sen + pairwise slopes + OLS, component render incl. empty / raw path kind=raw / trend line kind=theil-sen / hide trend / hide raw / OLS comparison line omitted by default + shown via prop / dots via showDots / config badge with slope + intercept + pair count + hide via prop / ARIA region + img + sr-only desc / root data-* incl. robust + OLS slopes / series group data-* / tooltip with x + y + trend + residual rows + hide on leave + omit via prop / onPointClick payload / legend with the robust slope alongside the OLS slope + toggle + omit / animate flag + class / ref / displayName / custom ariaLabel / xLabel + yLabel / collinear input renders an exact slope-2 trend) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for the new files. Exported via components/ui barrel. Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/c4-mgr-auto. Worker auto-w543. Bump 1.11.579. |
| 11.562 | UI: chart-line-error-bars primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.580 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-error-bars.tsx` ships `<ChartLineErrorBars>` -- pure-SVG multi-series line chart with **discrete per-point error bars**. A connecting line joins the observations; every point that carries error data also draws an **I-beam error bar** -- a vertical stem with an upper and a lower horizontal cap. Symmetric (`error`) and asymmetric (`errorLow` / `errorHigh`) errors are both supported. **Error model:** each `ChartLineErrorBarsPoint` may carry up to three error fields, collapsed to a `{errorLow, errorHigh}` pair by `resolveLineErrorBarsBounds(point)`: (1) an explicit `errorLow` / `errorHigh` always wins for its OWN side; (2) a side with no explicit value falls back to the symmetric `error` field, then to 0; (3) negative magnitudes are clamped to 0 -- an error bar cannot have a negative extent. So `{error: 2}` -> low 2 / high 2; `{errorLow: 1, errorHigh: 3}` -> low 1 / high 3; `{error: 2, errorHigh: 5}` -> low 2 / high 5 (the explicit high wins, the low borrows the symmetric error). `runLineErrorBars(points)` sorts the finite points ascending by x and produces, per sample, `upper = y + errorHigh`, `lower = y - errorLow`, a `hasError` flag (`errorLow > 0 || errorHigh > 0`) and a `symmetric` flag (`errorLow === errorHigh`); it reports `errorPointCount` (points that carry a bar), `meanError` (the mean half-extent `(errorLow + errorHigh) / 2`) and `maxError` (the largest single-side magnitude). **Distinct from the continuous band charts:** `<ChartLineBand>` (11.524) and `<ChartLineConfidence>` (11.488) fill a CONTINUOUS shaded region between an upper and a lower boundary -- the uncertainty is a polygon swept across the whole x range. ChartLineErrorBars is the DISCRETE counterpart: each point gets its own self-contained I-beam, and a point with no error data gets NO bar at all. The y-axis range still expands to cover the error-bar extents (`upper` / `lower`), not just the `y` values, so no cap is ever clipped. Also distinct from the existing `<ChartErrorBars>` (`chart-error-bars.tsx`), which is a standalone CATEGORICAL error-bar chart with no connecting line -- ChartLineErrorBars is a line chart first, with per-point error bars layered on. All exported helpers carry a `LineErrorBars` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide with `chart-error-bars`. API: `series: readonly ChartLineErrorBarsSeries[]` (`{id, label, data, color?}` where each datum is `{x, y, error?, errorLow?, errorHigh?}`); `capWidth` (I-beam cap width); `errorStrokeWidth` / `errorOpacity` (error-bar stroke); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (defaults TRUE here -- the error bars anchor on the points)/`showLegend`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLine`/`showErrorBars`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` / `defaultHiddenSeries` / `onHiddenSeriesChange`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX` / `formatError`; `onPointClick({series, point})` / `onSeriesToggle({series, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_ERROR_BARS_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/ERROR_STROKE_WIDTH/CAP_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/ERROR_OPACITY/PALETTE/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineErrorBarsDefaultColor` (10-color cycle; non-finite/negative -> palette[0]); `getLineErrorBarsFinitePoints`; `resolveLineErrorBarsBounds(point)` -- the low/high resolution rule above; `runLineErrorBars(points)` -- sorts ascending; per-sample `upper`/`lower`/`hasError`/`symmetric`; reports `errorPointCount`/`meanError`/`maxError`/`totalSamples`; `computeLineErrorBarsLayout({series, ...})` -- projects the line path plus per-point `upperPy`/`lowerPy`; the y range covers the error-bar extents; per-series carries `{id, label, color, points, linePath, finiteCount, totalCount, errorPointCount, meanError, maxError}`; degenerate canvas / empty / all-hidden -> ok=false; `describeLineErrorBarsChart(series, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions discrete per-point error bars + the carry count + mean error (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-error-bars-aria-desc"` span; line path + every error-bar group + every dot `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; the error-bar aria-label states the `lower` to `upper` range. data-section on every node (chart-line-error-bars / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -x-label / -y-label / -series / -series-group / -line-path / -error-bar / -error-bar-stem / -error-bar-cap / -dot / -tooltip / -tooltip-label / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-y / -tooltip-error / -tooltip-range / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -legend-total-points / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-mean / -badge-max / -badge-points); root mirrors `data-series-count`, `data-visible-series-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-total-error-points`, `data-animate`; line path `data-kind="line"`; error bars expose `data-symmetric`; caps expose `data-edge="upper"|"lower"`; dots expose `data-x`, `data-y`, `data-error-low`, `data-error-high`, `data-has-error`; series groups expose `data-series-error-point-count`, `data-series-mean-error`, `data-series-max-error`, `data-series-finite-count`. Tooltip on dots shows the series label, x, y, the bold error line (`+/- e` for symmetric, `+high / -low` for asymmetric) and the `[lower, upper]` range. Config badge in top-left calls out `ERR mean=<meanError> max=<maxError> bars=<count>`. Legend shows per-series `(<errorPointCount> bars; mean <meanError>)`. 57 vitest cases (defaults + getLineErrorBarsDefaultColor + getLineErrorBarsFinitePoints, resolveLineErrorBarsBounds incl. **symmetric error fills both sides** + **explicit low/high used directly** + **an explicit side wins while the other falls back to the symmetric error** + no fields -> zero + **negatives clamped to 0** + null -> zero, runLineErrorBars incl. empty/null + **symmetric -> upper/lower straddle y by the error** + **asymmetric -> the two distinct magnitudes** + counts the points that carry bars (mixed fixture) + **mean error is the average half-extent** + **max error is the largest single side** + sorts ascending + drops non-finite, computeLineErrorBarsLayout incl. empty/degenerate/hidden + line path + per-point error bounds + **the y range covers the error-bar extents not just y** + per-series error stats + hidden excluded + bounds overrides + totalPoints + per-point hasError, describeLineErrorBarsChart incl. No data + summary mentions discrete per-point error bars + the count + hidden filter, component render incl. empty / line path kind=line / hide line / **one discrete bar per point with error** / **each bar has a stem + two caps (upper/lower edges)** / **only points with error get a bar (mixed)** / hide error bars / dots default-on + hide / the symmetric flag on a bar / config badge mean+max+count + hide / ARIA region + img + sr-only desc / root data-* incl. total-error-points / series group data-* / dot error-low + error-high + has-error attrs / tooltip with x + y + error + range rows + hide on leave + omit via prop / onPointClick payload / legend with bar count + mean error + toggle + omit / animate flag + class / ref / displayName / custom ariaLabel / xLabel + yLabel) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for the new files. Exported via components/ui barrel. Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/c4-mgr-auto. Worker auto-w544. Bump 1.11.580. |
| 11.563 | UI: chart-line-crossover primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.581 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-crossover.tsx` ships `<ChartLineCrossover>` -- pure-SVG line chart that draws a **primary** and a **reference** series and marks every point where they intersect. A rising intersection (the primary crossing ABOVE the reference) is a **golden cross**; a falling intersection (the primary crossing BELOW) is a **death cross** -- the classic two-moving-average crossover chart from technical analysis. **Detection (4 steps):** (1) ALIGN -- `alignLineCrossoverSamples` keeps only the x values present in BOTH series, sorted ascending, each carrying `diff = primaryY - referenceY` (the signal whose zero crossings are the crossovers); (2) DETECT -- `detectLineCrossovers` walks consecutive aligned samples; a crossover sits between sample i-1 and i when their diffs have STRICTLY opposite signs (`d0 * d1 < 0`); (3) INTERPOLATE -- the crossing is the zero of the linearly interpolated diff: `t = d0 / (d0 - d1)` (always in the open interval (0,1) for a strict sign change), `x = x0 + t*(x1-x0)`, `y = primaryY0 + t*(primaryY1-primaryY0)` (both series are equal at the crossing so either yields the same y); (4) CLASSIFY -- `kind='golden'` when the diff rises through zero (`d1 > 0`), `'death'` when it falls (`d1 < 0`). A sample sitting EXACTLY on zero does NOT trigger a crossover -- a strict sign change between two consecutive aligned samples is required, which keeps the detector deterministic and free of double-counting. The component fixture demonstrates the whole pipeline: primary [10,8,6,10,14], reference [6,9,9,8,9] at x 0..4 gives diffs [4,-1,-3,2,5], yielding a **death cross at x=0.8** (t=4/(4-(-1))=0.8) and a **golden cross at x=2.6** (t=-3/(-3-2)=0.6), both at y=8.4. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** `<ChartLineMulti>` draws many series but detects no intersection events; `<ChartLineComparison>` / `<ChartLineCorrelation>` relate two series in AGGREGATE (deltas, correlation coefficient), whereas ChartLineCrossover localises the DISCRETE events where the ordering of the two series flips; `<ChartLineMovingAvg>` overlays a single smoothed line and crossover is the natural consumer of TWO such lines. All exported helpers carry a `LineCrossover` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide with `chart-line-correlation` / `chart-line-comparison`. API: `primary` + `reference` -- two explicit `ChartLineCrossoverSeries` props (`{id, label, data, color?}` where each datum is `{x, y}`), since a crossover chart is inherently a two-series comparison; `goldenColor` / `deathColor` (marker colours, decoupled from the line colours); `primaryColor` / `referenceColor` (line colour defaults); `markerSize` (triangle marker half-extent); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default FALSE -- the markers are the focus)/`showLegend`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showMarkers`/`showMarkerGuides`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` / `defaultHiddenSeries` / `onHiddenSeriesChange`) -- hiding EITHER series suppresses every crossover marker (a crossover needs both lines); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onCrossoverClick({crossover})` / `onPointClick({series, point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_CROSSOVER_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/MARKER_SIZE/DOT_RADIUS/PALETTE/PRIMARY_COLOR/REFERENCE_COLOR/GOLDEN_COLOR/DEATH_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineCrossoverDefaultColor` (10-color cycle; non-finite/negative -> palette[0]); `getLineCrossoverFinitePoints`; `alignLineCrossoverSamples(primary, reference)` -- the shared-x alignment with `diff`; sorts ascending; drops non-finite; `detectLineCrossovers(samples)` -- the strict-sign-change detector with linear interpolation; fewer than 2 samples -> []; `runLineCrossover(primary, reference)` -- aligns + detects; returns `{samples, crossovers, goldenCount, deathCount, crossoverCount, alignedCount}`; `computeLineCrossoverLayout({primary, reference, ...})` -- projects both line paths and the crossover markers; markers carry `baselinePy` for the dashed guide down to the x-axis; per-line carries `{id, label, color, visible, points, linePath, finiteCount, totalCount}`; crossovers gated to when BOTH series are visible; degenerate canvas / both-hidden -> ok=false; `describeLineCrossoverChart(primary, reference, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions golden + death + aligned counts (No data when missing / hidden / unaligned). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-crossover-aria-desc"` span; each line path + every crossover marker + every dot `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; the marker aria-label states the cross kind + x + value. data-section on every node (chart-line-crossover / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -x-label / -y-label / -series / -series-group / -line-path / -dot / -markers / -marker / -marker-guide / -marker-shape / -tooltip / -tooltip-kind / -tooltip-label / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-y / -tooltip-diff / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -legend-total-points / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-golden / -badge-death / -badge-aligned); root mirrors `data-golden-count`, `data-death-count`, `data-crossover-count`, `data-aligned-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-visible-series-count`, `data-animate`; series groups expose `data-series-kind` (primary / reference), `data-series-id`, `data-series-finite-count`; markers expose `data-kind` (golden / death), `data-crossover-index`, `data-segment-index`, `data-x`, `data-y`; marker shape + guide also carry `data-kind`; dots expose `data-series-kind`, `data-x`, `data-y`. Tooltip on a marker shows the cross kind (coloured), x, y and the `fromDiff -> toDiff` transition; tooltip on a dot (when showDots) shows the series label, x, y. Config badge in top-left calls out `XO gold=<n> death=<n> aligned=<n>`. Legend has two toggle items plus a `<golden> golden / <death> death` tally. 62 vitest cases (defaults + getLineCrossoverDefaultColor + getLineCrossoverFinitePoints, alignLineCrossoverSamples incl. **aligns on shared x + computes diff [4,-1,-3,2,5]** + no shared x -> empty + sorts ascending + drops non-finite + null inputs, detectLineCrossovers incl. **golden when the diff rises through zero** + **death when it falls** + **interpolates the fixture (death x 0.8 / golden x 2.6, both y 8.4)** + no crossover when one series stays above + fewer than 2 samples -> [] + sequential indexing, runLineCrossover incl. null -> empty + **fixture -> 2 crossovers (1 golden + 1 death)** + aligned-sample count + no-cross fixture still aligned, computeLineCrossoverLayout incl. empty/degenerate/both-hidden -> ok=false + both line paths + **marker projection with px/py/baselinePy** + **one series hidden -> still ok but no crossovers** + golden/death counts + bounds overrides + totalPoints, describeLineCrossoverChart incl. missing series -> No data + summary mentions golden + death + aligned + unaligned -> no shared x values + a hidden series -> No data, component render incl. empty / both line paths / **hiding a series drops its line AND every marker** / one marker per crossover / golden + death markers / triangle shape paths / marker guides + hide / hide all markers / dots off by default + on / config badge gold+death+aligned + hide / ARIA region + img + sr-only desc / root data-* / series group data-* / marker data-* / tooltip on a marker (kind + x + y + diff) + hide on leave / tooltip on a dot / omit tooltip via prop / onCrossoverClick / onPointClick / legend two toggle items + toggle / legend stats tally / hide legend / animate flag + class / ref / displayName / custom ariaLabel / xLabel + yLabel) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for the new files. Exported via components/ui barrel. Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/c4-mgr-auto. Worker auto-w545. Bump 1.11.581. |
| 11.564 | UI: chart-line-winsorized primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.582 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-winsorized.tsx` ships `<ChartLineWinsorized>` -- pure-SVG line chart that overlays a **winsorized** copy of a series against the raw line. Winsorization is a GLOBAL percentile clamp: any value below the lower-quantile bound is clamped UP to that bound, any value above the upper-quantile bound is clamped DOWN to it. Unlike trimming (which discards outliers), winsorizing REPLACES them with the boundary value, so the sample count is preserved. **Math:** `computeLineWinsorizedQuantile(values, q)` is a linear-interpolation quantile (numpy `linear` / R type-7 convention): position `h = (n-1)*q`, interpolating between the two surrounding order statistics; non-finite values dropped; empty -> NaN; q clamped to [0,1]; non-finite q -> 0. `runLineWinsorized(points, lowerQuantile, upperQuantile)` (1) sorts the finite points ascending by x and collects the y values; (2) `lowerBound = quantile(y, lowerQuantile)`, `upperBound = quantile(y, upperQuantile)` computed ONCE over the whole series (a global clamp, NOT a rolling-window filter); (3) per sample `winsorized = clamp(y, lowerBound, upperBound)` with `clampedLow` when `y < lowerBound`, `clampedHigh` when `y > upperBound`, `delta = winsorized - y`. The quantile arguments are clamped to [0,1]; if the lower quantile exceeds the upper the pair is swapped so the band is always well ordered; a value sitting EXACTLY on a bound is not clamped. The component fixture makes the bounds exact: y sorted [0,10,20,30,100], with quantiles 0.25/0.75 the positions h=4*0.25=1 and h=4*0.75=3 land directly on order statistics giving **lowerBound=10** and **upperBound=30**, so the winsorized series is **[10,10,20,30,30]** -- the 0 clamped up to 10 (delta +10) and the 100 down to 30 (delta -70). **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** `<ChartLineHampel>` replaces outliers via a ROLLING-window MAD test (a local decision) whereas winsorize uses a single GLOBAL percentile band; `<ChartLinePercentile>` draws a percentile BAND as context but never transforms the series, whereas ChartLineWinsorized produces a new clamped line; `<ChartLineThreshold>` clips against FIXED user-supplied thresholds whereas winsorize derives its bounds from the data's OWN quantiles and clamps both tails; `<ChartLineSavgol>` / `<ChartLineLoess>` smooth rather than clamp. All exported helpers carry a `LineWinsorized` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineWinsorizedPoint[]` (`{x, y}`), since winsorization is a single-series transformation; `lowerQuantile` / `upperQuantile` (the tail quantiles, defaults 0.05 / 0.95); `rawColor` / `winsorizedColor` / `boundColor` / `clampColor` (the raw line, the clamped overlay, the bound lines+band, the clamped markers); `rawOpacity` (the raw line is dashed + dimmed, the winsorized line solid); `strokeWidth` / `markerSize` / `dotRadius` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default FALSE)/`showLegend`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showBounds`/`showBoundBand`/`showClampedMarkers`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `raw` / `winsorized`; hiding the winsorized series also drops its dots and clamped markers); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({sample})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_WINSORIZED_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/MARKER_SIZE/DOT_RADIUS/RAW_OPACITY/LOWER_QUANTILE/UPPER_QUANTILE/PALETTE/RAW_COLOR/WINSORIZED_COLOR/BOUND_COLOR/CLAMP_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineWinsorizedDefaultColor` (10-color cycle; non-finite/negative -> palette[0]); `getLineWinsorizedFinitePoints`; `computeLineWinsorizedQuantile(values, q)` -- the linear-interpolation quantile; `runLineWinsorized(points, lowerQuantile?, upperQuantile?)` -- the full transformation; returns `{samples, lowerBound, upperBound, lowerQuantile, upperQuantile, clampedCount, clampedLowCount, clampedHighCount, totalSamples, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, y, winsorized, clamped, clampedLow, clampedHigh, delta}`; `computeLineWinsorizedLayout({data, ...})` -- projects the raw + winsorized paths and the bound lines; per-sample adds `px`/`rawPy`/`winsorizedPy`; degenerate canvas / empty -> ok=false; the y range is taken from the RAW values; `describeLineWinsorizedChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions winsorized + quantile + bounds + clamped (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-winsorized-aria-desc"` span; raw path + winsorized path + every dot + every clamped marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; the clamped-marker aria-label states the clamp direction + x + raw + clamped value. data-section on every node (chart-line-winsorized / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -bound-band / -axes / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -x-label / -y-label / -bounds / -bound-line / -raw-path / -winsorized-path / -dots / -dot / -clamped-markers / -clamped-marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-label / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-raw / -tooltip-winsorized / -tooltip-clamp / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -legend-total-points / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-lower / -badge-upper / -badge-clamp); root mirrors `data-total-samples`, `data-clamped-count`, `data-clamped-low-count`, `data-clamped-high-count`, `data-lower-bound`, `data-upper-bound`, `data-animate`; raw path `data-kind="raw"`; winsorized path `data-kind="winsorized"`; bound lines `data-edge="upper"|"lower"`; clamped markers `data-direction="low"|"high"` + `data-point-index` + `data-x`; dots `data-point-index` + `data-x` + `data-y` + `data-winsorized` + `data-clamped`. Tooltip on a dot or clamped marker shows the sample number, x, raw value, the bold winsorized value, and a clamp row (`clamped low/high (delta +/-N)` or `not clamped`). Config badge in top-left calls out `WIN lo=<lowerBound> hi=<upperBound> clamp=<count>`. Legend has two toggle items (Raw / Winsorized) plus a `<n> clamped (<low> low / <high> high)` tally. 64 vitest cases (defaults + getLineWinsorizedDefaultColor + getLineWinsorizedFinitePoints, computeLineWinsorizedQuantile incl. empty -> NaN + single value + **q=0 -> min / q=1 -> max** + **q=0.5 -> median** + q=0.25 lands on an order statistic + **interpolates (q=0.1 -> 14)** + clamps q outside [0,1] + drops non-finite + non-finite q -> 0, runLineWinsorized incl. empty -> ok=false + **quantiles 0.25/0.75 -> bounds 10/30** + **clamps to the matching bound ([10,10,20,30,30])** + clamped-low / clamped-high flags + per-tail counts + **delta is winsorized minus raw** + constant series clamps nothing + **swaps the quantiles when lower exceeds upper** + out-of-range quantiles clamped + sorts ascending by x, computeLineWinsorizedLayout incl. empty/degenerate -> ok=false + raw + winsorized paths + per-sample projection + bounds + projected y + clamped flags + clamp counts + bounds overrides + **y range taken from the raw values**, describeLineWinsorizedChart incl. No data + summary mentions winsorized + quantile + bounds + clamped + reports the resolved bound values, component render incl. empty / raw path kind=raw / winsorized path kind=winsorized / hide raw via hiddenSeries / **hiding the winsorized series drops its clamped markers** / upper + lower bound lines + hide / bound band + hide / **one clamped marker per clamped sample** / low+high direction / hide clamped markers / dots off by default + on / config badge bounds + clamp count + hide / ARIA region + img + sr-only desc / root data-* / tooltip on a clamped marker (raw + winsorized + clamp rows) + hide on leave / tooltip on an un-clamped dot reports not clamped / omit tooltip via prop / onPointClick / legend two toggle items + toggle / legend stats tally / hide legend / animate flag + class / ref / displayName / custom ariaLabel / xLabel + yLabel) all pass under vitest 4.1.5.
