| 11.565 | UI: chart-line-bump primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.583 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-bump.tsx` ships `<ChartLineBump>` -- pure-SVG **bump chart** (a.k.a. rank chart). Where an ordinary multi-series line chart plots raw VALUES, a bump chart plots **RANK**: at every x value the series are sorted by value and assigned positions 1..N, and each series is drawn as a line connecting its rank position from column to column. Lines "bump" up and down as series overtake one another -- the y-axis IS rank, inverted so rank 1 sits at the top. **Ranking:** `computeLineBumpColumns(series, rankOrder)` (1) the union of x values across all series defines the COLUMNS (sorted ascending); (2) within each column the series present there are sorted by value -- `desc` (default) puts the highest value at rank 1, `asc` puts the lowest at rank 1; (3) each series in the column gets an ORDINAL rank 1..k so every series gets a DISTINCT rank even on a tie, ensuring a distinct y position per series; ties are broken deterministically by series declaration order. A series missing at some x is simply absent from that column; its trajectory connects the rank nodes it does have. `runLineBump(series, rankOrder)` walks the columns and collects each series' rank trajectory (`nodes` of `{x, value, rank}`) plus `bestRank` / `worstRank` / `meanRank` per series and the chart-wide `maxRank` (the largest column size). The component fixture is a symmetric carousel: three series, three columns, every series visiting ranks 1, 2 and 3 in turn -- A's rank trajectory is **[3,1,2]**, B's is **[2,3,1]**, C's is **[1,2,3]**, all with mean rank 2. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** `<ChartLineMulti>` plots raw VALUES on a value axis whereas the bump chart plots RANK on a rank axis (the same data produces a completely different shape -- the bump chart's y position depends only on the ORDERING, not the magnitudes); `<ChartSlope>` / `<ChartLineSlope>` connect exactly TWO columns whereas the bump chart tracks rank across an arbitrary number of columns; `<ChartLineComparison>` / `<ChartLinePeriodCompare>` compare VALUES between series or periods and neither transforms the data into ordinal ranks. All exported helpers carry a `LineBump` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `series: readonly ChartLineBumpSeries[]` (`{id, label, data, color?}` where each datum is `{x, value}`); `rankOrder` (`'desc'` default = highest value rank 1, or `'asc'`); `curved` (default TRUE -- the connectors are smooth cubic-bezier S-curves, the classic bump shape; `false` draws straight segments); `strokeWidth` / `nodeRadius` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showNodes` (default true)/`showLegend`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showRankLabels` (default false -- the rank number drawn inside each node)/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` / `defaultHiddenSeries` / `onHiddenSeriesChange`) -- RANKING IS GLOBAL: hiding a series only hides its line, it does NOT re-rank the others; standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onNodeClick({series, node})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_BUMP_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/STROKE_WIDTH/NODE_RADIUS/PALETTE/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineBumpDefaultColor` (10-color cycle; non-finite/negative -> palette[0]); `getLineBumpFinitePoints`; `computeLineBumpColumns(series, rankOrder?)` -- the per-column ranking; returns `{xValues, columns}` where each column `{x, entries: {seriesId, value, rank}[]}`; `runLineBump(series, rankOrder?)` -- per-series rank trajectories; returns `{xValues, columns, series, seriesCount, columnCount, maxRank}`; `computeLineBumpLayout({series, ...})` -- projects each series' rank trajectory; rank 1 at the TOP, `maxRank` at the bottom; builds the curved or straight path; per-series adds `{color, visible, nodes (px/py), path, bestRank, worstRank, meanRank, nodeCount}`; degenerate canvas / empty -> ok=false; `describeLineBumpChart(series, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions bump + rank (No data when missing / all hidden). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-bump-aria-desc"` span; each series path + every rank node `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; the node aria-label states the rank + chart-wide max rank + value. data-section on every node (chart-line-bump / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -x-label / -y-label / -series / -series-group / -path / -node / -rank-label / -tooltip / -tooltip-label / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-rank / -tooltip-value / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -legend-columns / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-series / -badge-columns); root mirrors `data-series-count`, `data-visible-series-count`, `data-column-count`, `data-max-rank`, `data-animate`; series groups expose `data-series-best-rank`, `data-series-worst-rank`, `data-series-node-count`; rank nodes expose `data-rank`, `data-x`, `data-value`; axis ticks carry `data-axis="x"|"rank"`. Tooltip on a node shows the series label, x, `rank: <r> of <maxRank>`, and the value. Config badge in top-left calls out `BUMP series=<n> cols=<m>`. Legend has one toggle item per series (with the series' best rank) plus a `<m> columns` note. 60 vitest cases (defaults + getLineBumpDefaultColor + getLineBumpFinitePoints, computeLineBumpColumns incl. sorted union of x + **desc -> the highest value gets rank 1** + **asc -> the lowest gets rank 1** + **ties broken by declaration order** + a series missing at an x is absent from that column + drops non-finite + empty -> no columns + single series -> every column rank 1, runLineBump incl. empty + **per-series rank trajectories ([3,1,2] / [1,2,3])** + best/worst/mean rank + **maxRank is the largest column size** + series + column counts + asc order + a partial series only has nodes where it appears + single series -> maxRank 1 + nodes preserve x + value, computeLineBumpLayout incl. empty/degenerate -> ok=false + per-series px/py projection + **curved path by default (C) / straight when curved=false (L)** + **rank 1 projects above rank maxRank** + maxRank + column count + hidden series marked + **ranking is global -- hiding a series does not re-rank the others** + x bounds overrides, describeLineBumpChart incl. No data + summary mentions bump + rank + all hidden -> No data, component render incl. empty / one path per series / a node per rank position / hide a series / hide nodes / rank labels off by default + on / curved+straight paths / rank + x ticks / config badge counts + hide / ARIA region + img + sr-only desc / root data-* / series group data-* / node data-* / tooltip with label + x + rank + value + hide on leave / omit tooltip via prop / onNodeClick / legend one item per series + toggle / legend column count / hide legend / animate flag + class / ref / displayName / custom ariaLabel / xLabel + yLabel) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for the new files. Exported via components/ui barrel. Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/c4-mgr-auto. Worker auto-w547. Bump 1.11.583. |
| 11.566 | UI: chart-line-slopegraph primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.584 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-slopegraph.tsx` ships `<ChartLineSlopegraph>` -- pure-SVG **slopegraph** (Tufte's slope graph) for the `chart-line` family. Each series is drawn as a single straight line connecting its value at a **start** time endpoint (left column) to its value at an **end** time endpoint (right column); the slope of the line shows whether the series rose or fell, and how steeply, between the two points. **Time-series input + endpoint extraction:** where the existing `chart-slope` (`<ChartSlope>`) takes pre-computed `{before, after}` SCALAR pairs, ChartLineSlopegraph is the time-series-native slopegraph -- it takes full `{x, value}[]` series and EXTRACTS the two time endpoints itself. `resolveLineSlopegraphEndpoints(series, startX?, endX?)` uses explicit `startX` / `endX` when both are supplied, otherwise defaults to the minimum and maximum x present across every series. `runLineSlopegraph(series, startX?, endX?)` looks up each series' value at `startX` and at `endX` by EXACT x match -- a series present at both endpoints is `complete` and gets a slope, one missing an endpoint is flagged incomplete and gets no slope line; per series it reports `delta = end - start`, `direction` (up / down / flat) and `pctChange = delta / |start|` (NaN when the start value is 0, an undefined percentage). Because the input is a full series, the component also draws the optional **intermediate trajectory** -- a faint ghost polyline through every point of the series, behind the bold slope line -- so the reader sees both the net change AND the path taken; this is the `chart-line`-family touch a pure before/after slopegraph cannot offer. **Distinct from `chart-slope`:** (1) INPUT SHAPE -- `chart-slope` consumes `ChartSlopeItem {before, after}` (the caller must already have reduced each series to two numbers) whereas ChartLineSlopegraph consumes time series and does the endpoint reduction internally with a configurable `startX` / `endX` window; (2) TRAJECTORY OVERLAY -- only ChartLineSlopegraph can draw the intermediate path because it has the full series; (3) DEFAULT COLOURING -- `chart-slope` colours by direction whereas ChartLineSlopegraph defaults to per-series palette colours (`colorMode='series'`, the `chart-line`-family convention) with `colorMode='direction'` available to recover the up/down/flat colouring. All exported helpers carry a `LineSlopegraph` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `series: readonly ChartLineSlopegraphSeries[]` (`{id, label, data, color?}` where each datum is `{x, value}`); `startX` / `endX` (explicit endpoint x-values; default global min / max x); `colorMode` (`'series'` default or `'direction'`); `strokeWidth` / `trajectoryStrokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `upColor` / `downColor` / `flatColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showEndpointDots`/`showEndpointValues`/`showTrajectory` (default true)/`showColumnGuides`/`showLegend`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` / `defaultHiddenSeries` / `onHiddenSeriesChange`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onSeriesClick({series})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_SLOPEGRAPH_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/TRAJECTORY_STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/TRAJECTORY_OPACITY/PALETTE/UP_COLOR/DOWN_COLOR/FLAT_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineSlopegraphDefaultColor` (10-color cycle; non-finite/negative -> palette[0]); `getLineSlopegraphFinitePoints`; `getLineSlopegraphDirection(startValue, endValue)` (up/down/flat; non-finite -> flat); `resolveLineSlopegraphEndpoints(series, startX?, endX?)`; `runLineSlopegraph(series, startX?, endX?)` -- the two-endpoint reduction; returns `{startX, endX, series, risingCount, fallingCount, flatCount, completeCount, ok}`; each series `{id, label, startValue, endValue, delta, pctChange, direction, complete, pointCount}`; ok=false when fewer than 1 series or startX===endX (no window); `computeLineSlopegraphLayout({series, ...})` -- projects each series' slope line, endpoint nodes (`{x, value, px, py}`) and the trajectory ghost path; per-series adds `{color, visible, complete, startNode, endNode, slopePath, trajectoryPath}`; degenerate canvas / no window / all-hidden -> ok=false; `describeLineSlopegraphChart(series, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions slopegraph + rising + falling (No data when missing / all hidden). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-slopegraph-aria-desc"` span; each slope line + every endpoint dot `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-slopegraph / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -column-guides / -column-guide / -axes / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -column-label / -y-label / -series / -series-group / -trajectory / -slope-line / -endpoints / -endpoint / -endpoint-values / -endpoint-value / -tooltip / -tooltip-label / -tooltip-start / -tooltip-end / -tooltip-delta / -tooltip-pct / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-up / -badge-down / -badge-flat); root mirrors `data-series-count`, `data-visible-series-count`, `data-complete-count`, `data-rising-count`, `data-falling-count`, `data-flat-count`, `data-start-x`, `data-end-x`, `data-animate`; series groups expose `data-direction`, `data-complete`, `data-delta`; endpoint dots / column guides / column labels carry `data-edge="start"|"end"`; slope lines carry `data-direction`. Tooltip on a slope line or endpoint dot shows the series label, start value, end value, the bold delta + direction, and the percentage change. Config badge in top-left calls out `SLOPE up=<n> down=<m> flat=<k>`. Legend has one toggle item per series plus a `<n> up / <m> down / <k> flat` tally. 63 vitest cases (defaults + getLineSlopegraphDefaultColor + getLineSlopegraphFinitePoints + getLineSlopegraphDirection, resolveLineSlopegraphEndpoints incl. defaults to the global min/max x + uses explicit endpoints verbatim, runLineSlopegraph incl. empty -> ok=false + **single shared x -> ok=false (no window)** + **extracts start/end values** + **delta + direction per series** + **pctChange = delta over |start|** + **pctChange NaN when start is 0** + rising/falling/flat counts + **a series missing an endpoint is incomplete** + honours explicit endpoints, computeLineSlopegraphLayout incl. empty/degenerate/single-shared-x -> ok=false + slope + trajectory paths + **endpoint nodes on the two columns** + resolved endpoints + **y range covers every finite value** + direction counts + **an incomplete series has no slope path** + hidden series excluded from counts + all hidden -> ok=false + y bounds overrides, describeLineSlopegraphChart incl. No data + summary mentions slopegraph + rising + falling + all hidden -> No data, component render incl. empty / one slope line per complete series / a trajectory per series + hide / endpoint dots + hide / endpoint value labels + hide / **an incomplete series renders no slope line** / hide a series / column guides + labels / config badge up/down/flat + hide / **colorMode=series uses the palette / colorMode=direction colours by up/down** / ARIA region + img + sr-only desc / root data-* / series group data-* / tooltip on a slope line (start + end + delta) + hide on leave / tooltip from an endpoint dot / omit tooltip via prop / onSeriesClick / legend one item per series + toggle / legend stats tally / hide legend / animate flag + class / ref / displayName / custom ariaLabel / yLabel) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for the new files. Exported via components/ui barrel. Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/c4-mgr-auto. Worker auto-w548. Bump 1.11.584. |
| 11.567 | UI: chart-line-heatline primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.585 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-heatline.tsx` ships `<ChartLineHeatline>` -- pure-SVG **heatline**: a single-series line chart whose stroke is colour-graded by value along its length. Instead of one solid stroke colour, the line is split into per-pair segments and each segment is painted with a colour sampled from a value->colour scale, so the line's hue encodes magnitude alongside its y-position -- peaks read hot, troughs read cool. **Colour grading:** the line is rendered as N solid-colour segments (one per consecutive pair of points), NOT a single SVG gradient -- this keeps the rendering deterministic and works for any line shape. `parseHexColor(hex)` parses a 6-digit `#rrggbb` string into RGB channels (null for any other shape). `interpolateLineHeatlineColor(scale, t)` samples a multi-stop colour scale at normalized position `t`: the stops are spread evenly across [0,1], `t` selects a pair of surrounding stops and the RGB channels are linearly interpolated; `t` is clamped to [0,1] and a non-finite `t` falls back to 0. `normalizeLineHeatlineValue(value, min, max)` maps a value to `t`; a zero-width domain collapses every point to the scale midpoint (0.5). `runLineHeatline(points, options?)` grades every point by its value and every SEGMENT by the midpoint value of its two endpoints, so the colour of a segment reflects the values it spans. The colour `domain` defaults to the data's value range but can be pinned via `domainMin` / `domainMax` so the same scale is comparable across charts; an inverted domain is swapped. The component fixture makes the grading exact: values **[0,25,50,75,100]** over the default 5-stop scale give point `t` values **[0,.25,.5,.75,1]** (each landing directly on a scale stop) and segment midpoint `t` values **[.125,.375,.625,.875]**. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** `<ChartLineAreaGradient>` applies a gradient FILL to the area BENEATH the line (a vertical area gradient) whereas the heatline grades the STROKE itself, segment by segment, along the line's length; `<ChartLine>` / `<ChartLineMarker>` draw a single solid stroke colour; `<ChartHeatmap>` / `<ChartCalendarHeatmap>` are 2D grid heatmaps (a different geometry entirely). All exported helpers carry a `LineHeatline` infix (and the shared `parseHexColor`) so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineHeatlinePoint[]` (`{x, value}`), since the heatline is a single-series transformation; `colorScale` (a >=1-stop array of `#rrggbb` strings; default a 5-stop deep-blue -> teal -> green -> amber -> red ramp); `domainMin` / `domainMax` (pin the colour domain; default the data's value range); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default true)/`showScale` (the colour legend)/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`animate`; standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_HEATLINE_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/SCALE/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineHeatlineFinitePoints`; `parseHexColor(hex)`; `interpolateLineHeatlineColor(scale, t)`; `normalizeLineHeatlineValue(value, min, max)`; `runLineHeatline(points, options?)` -- per-point + per-segment colour grading; returns `{points, segments, valueMin, valueMax, domainMin, domainMax, totalSamples, ok}`; each point `{index, x, value, t, color}`; each segment `{index, startIndex, endIndex, value, t, color}`; `computeLineHeatlineLayout({data, ...})` -- projects the points (`px`/`py`) and builds the per-segment paths (`px0/py0/px1/py1` + `path`); degenerate canvas / empty -> ok=false; `describeLineHeatlineChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions heatline + colour domain (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-heatline-aria-desc"` span; every dot `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-heatline / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -x-label / -y-label / -segments / -segment / -dots / -dot / -tooltip / -tooltip-swatch / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-level / -scale / -scale-min / -scale-bar / -scale-swatch / -scale-max / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-lo / -badge-hi); root mirrors `data-total-samples`, `data-segment-count`, `data-value-min`, `data-value-max`, `data-domain-min`, `data-domain-max`, `data-animate`; segments expose `data-segment-index`, `data-value`, `data-t`; dots expose `data-point-index`, `data-x`, `data-value`, `data-t`; scale swatches expose `data-stop-index`. Tooltip on a dot shows a colour swatch, x, the bold value, and the level (`t` as a percentage). Config badge in top-left calls out `HEAT lo=<domainMin> hi=<domainMax>`. The colour scale legend below the chart shows one swatch per scale stop flanked by the domain min / max labels. 61 vitest cases (defaults + parseHexColor valid/malformed/non-string, interpolateLineHeatlineColor incl. **endpoints at t=0/t=1** + **midpoint RGB interpolation (#808080)** + exact stop in a 3-stop scale + **interpolation between adjacent stops (#800000)** + clamps t outside [0,1] + non-finite t -> 0 + empty + single-stop scales, normalizeLineHeatlineValue incl. maps into [0,1] + clamps outside the domain + **a zero-width domain -> midpoint**, getLineHeatlineFinitePoints, runLineHeatline incl. empty -> ok=false + **grades every point by its normalized value** + **one segment per consecutive pair** + **a segment is graded by its midpoint value** + value range + colour domain + **a domain override rescales the mapping** + swaps an inverted domain + sorts ascending by x + a single point yields no segments + point colours come from the scale, computeLineHeatlineLayout incl. empty/degenerate -> ok=false + point projection + segment paths + finite coordinates + x/y ranges + value range + domain + bounds overrides + totalSamples, describeLineHeatlineChart incl. | 11.568 | UI: chart-line-qqplot primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.586 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-qqplot.tsx` ships `<ChartLineQQPlot>` -- pure-SVG line chart with a **quantile-quantile (Q-Q) plot** side panel. The left panel shows the raw time series; the right panel is a normal probability plot -- the sample quantiles charted against the quantiles of a standard normal distribution. If the sample is normally distributed the Q-Q points fall on a straight line; departures from that line reveal skew, heavy tails and other non-normality. **The Q-Q analysis:** `normalInverseCDF(p)` is the probit / quantile function -- the inverse of the standard normal CDF -- via Peter Acklam's rational approximation (relative error below ~1.15e-9 across the open interval); `p` outside [0,1] yields NaN, `p` of exactly 0/1 yields -Infinity/+Infinity, and it is EXACTLY 0 at `p=0.5`. `computeLineQQPlotStats(values)` reports the mean, the variance and the SAMPLE standard deviation (the unbiased `n-1` denominator). `runLineQQPlot(points)` sorts the sample ascending and, for each order statistic at rank `i` of `n`, computes the plotting position `p = (i - 0.5) / n`, the theoretical normal quantile `z = normalInverseCDF(p)`, and pairs it with the observed value; the **reference line** is `sample = mean + stddev * z` (what a perfectly normal sample would follow); it also reports the Pearson **correlation** between the theoretical and sample quantiles -- a value near 1.0 means the sample is close to normal (the statistic behind the Filliben normality test). The component fixture is the symmetric sample **[-2,-1,0,1,2]** (mean 0, sample variance 10/4 = 2.5): its five plotting positions are **[.1,.3,.5,.7,.9]**, the median quantile maps to theoretical **z=0 exactly**, and the Q-Q correlation lands above 0.99. **Distinct from `chart-line-distribution`:** that summarises a series with a HISTOGRAM / DENSITY side panel (it shows the SHAPE of the distribution), whereas ChartLineQQPlot answers a different question -- IS this sample normal, and HOW does it deviate -- by plotting QUANTILE against THEORETICAL QUANTILE with a straight-line reference so the eye reads non-normality directly off the curvature; the defining transform is the probit (`normalInverseCDF`), which a histogram never needs. All exported helpers carry a `LineQQPlot` infix (or the clearly-namespaced `normalInverseCDF`) so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineQQPlotPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `linePanelRatio` (the fraction of the width given to the series panel; default 0.55, clamped to [0.2, 0.8]); `gap` (the pixel gap between the two panels); `tickCount` / `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `qqDotRadius` / `seriesColor` / `qqColor` / `referenceColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showSeriesDots`/`showReferenceLine`/`showPanelLabels`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showFooter`/`animate`; standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onSampleClick({point})` (a series dot) / `onQuantileClick({quantile})` (a Q-Q dot). Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_QQPLOT_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/LINE_PANEL_RATIO/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/QQ_DOT_RADIUS/SERIES_COLOR/QQ_COLOR/REFERENCE_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineQQPlotFinitePoints`; `normalInverseCDF(p)` -- the probit function; `computeLineQQPlotStats(values)` -- `{mean, variance, stddev, count}`; `runLineQQPlot(points)` -- the full Q-Q analysis; returns `{series, sorted, quantiles, mean, variance, stddev, count, referenceSlope, referenceIntercept, correlation, ok}`; each quantile `{rank, p, theoretical, sample}`; `ok` requires at least 2 finite samples; `computeLineQQPlotLayout({data, ...})` -- splits the canvas into the series panel and the Q-Q panel and projects both (`linePath`, `seriesDots`, `qqDots` with `residual`, `referencePath`); degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 samples -> ok=false; `describeLineQQPlotChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Q-Q + normal + correlation (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-qqplot-aria-desc"` span; the series line + every series dot + every Q-Q dot `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-qqplot / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis-group / -axis / -tick / -tick-label / -panel-labels / -panel-label / -line-path / -series-dots / -series-dot / -reference-line / -qq-dots / -qq-dot / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-rank / -tooltip-theoretical / -tooltip-sample / -tooltip-residual / -footer / -footer-stats / -footer-reference / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-correlation / -badge-count); root mirrors `data-count`, `data-mean`, `data-stddev`, `data-correlation`, `data-animate`; axis / tick / grid / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="series"|"qq"`; Q-Q dots expose `data-rank`, `data-theoretical`, `data-sample`; series dots expose `data-x`, `data-value`. Tooltip on a series dot shows x + value; tooltip on a Q-Q dot shows the quantile rank, the theoretical z, the sample value and the residual from the reference line. Config badge in top-left calls out `QQ r=<correlation> n=<count>`. The footer reports `n / mean / sd` and the reference-line equation `sample = <intercept> + <slope> z`. 54 vitest cases (defaults + getLineQQPlotFinitePoints, normalInverseCDF incl. **exactly 0 at p=0.5** + **matches known quantiles (1.959964 at .975, -1.281552 at .1)** + **antisymmetric about p=0.5** + infinities at the boundary + out-of-range / non-finite p -> NaN, computeLineQQPlotStats incl. mean + variance + **sample stddev (n-1)** + empty -> count 0 + a single value has a zero stddev + drops non-finite, runLineQQPlot incl. empty -> ok=false + **a single point -> ok=false** + **(rank - 0.5) / n plotting positions** + **the median quantile maps to theoretical 0** + **sample quantiles are the sorted values** + mean + sample stddev + **reference line is mean + stddev*z** + **a near-linear sample yields a high Q-Q correlation** + sorts the series by x, computeLineQQPlotLayout incl. empty/degenerate -> ok=false + **splits into a series panel + a qq panel** + series line path + series + qq dots + **the normal reference line path** + **the median quantile has a zero residual** + **clamps an extreme line-panel ratio** + distribution stats, describeLineQQPlotChart incl. | 11.569 | UI: chart-line-lag-plot primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.587 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-lag-plot.tsx` ships `<ChartLineLagPlot>` -- pure-SVG line chart with a **lag-k scatter** side panel. The left panel shows the raw time series; the right panel is a lag plot -- each value `y[t]` scattered against the value `k` steps earlier, `y[t-k]`. A tight cluster along the diagonal signals strong lag-k serial dependence; a structureless cloud signals randomness. **The lag analysis:** `runLineLagPlot(points, lag)` (1) sorts the series by x; (2) `normalizeLineLagPlotLag` coerces the lag to a positive integer -- a non-finite or sub-1 lag falls back to 1, a fractional lag is floored; (3) for every `t` from `k` to `n-1` forms a pair `laggedValue = y[t-k]` (the scatter x) and `currentValue = y[t]` (the scatter y) -- there are `n-k` pairs; (4) the **lag-k autocorrelation** is the Pearson correlation between the lagged column and the current column. Each pair also carries an `aboveDiagonal` flag (`currentValue > laggedValue`). The component fixtures make the correlation exact: the perfectly linear series **[0,2,4,6,8,10]** has every lag-1 pair on the line `current = lagged + 2` so the lag-1 correlation is **1**; the strictly alternating series **[0,10,0,10,...]** flips every step so its lag-1 correlation is **-1**. **Distinct from `chart-line-autocorrelation`:** that draws the CORRELOGRAM -- a bar chart of the autocorrelation `r_k` across MANY lags `k=0,1,2,...` whereas ChartLineLagPlot draws the ACTUAL SCATTER CLOUD at ONE chosen lag `k`; the correlogram reduces each lag to a single number while the lag plot keeps every `(y[t-k], y[t])` pair so the SHAPE of the dependence (linear, curved, clustered, outlier-driven) is visible -- they are complementary, the correlogram picks the interesting lag and the lag plot inspects it. All exported helpers carry a `LineLagPlot` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineLagPlotPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `lag` (the lag k; default 1, coerced to a positive integer); `linePanelRatio` (the fraction of the width given to the series panel; default 0.55, clamped to [0.2, 0.8]); `gap` (the pixel gap between the two panels); `tickCount` / `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `scatterDotRadius` / `seriesColor` / `scatterColor` / `identityColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showSeriesDots`/`showIdentityLine`/`showPanelLabels`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showFooter`/`animate`; standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onSampleClick({point})` (a series dot) / `onPairClick({pair})` (a scatter dot). Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_LAG_PLOT_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/LINE_PANEL_RATIO/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/SCATTER_DOT_RADIUS/LAG/SERIES_COLOR/SCATTER_COLOR/IDENTITY_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineLagPlotFinitePoints`; `normalizeLineLagPlotLag(lag)`; `runLineLagPlot(points, lag?)` -- the lag pairing + the lag-k autocorrelation; returns `{series, lag, pairs, pairCount, correlation, valueMin, valueMax, ok}`; each pair `{index, t, laggedValue, currentValue, aboveDiagonal}`; `ok` requires at least 2 finite samples and at least one pair; `computeLineLagPlotLayout({data, ...})` -- splits the canvas into the series panel and the scatter panel and projects both; the scatter panel shares ONE value domain across both axes so the identity line is a true `y = x` diagonal; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 samples / lag at-or-above the length -> ok=false; `describeLineLagPlotChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions lag + scatter + autocorrelation (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-lag-plot-aria-desc"` span; the series line + every series dot + every scatter dot `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-lag-plot / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis-group / -axis / -tick / -tick-label / -panel-labels / -panel-label / -line-path / -series-dots / -series-dot / -identity-line / -scatter-dots / -scatter-dot / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-pair / -tooltip-lagged / -tooltip-current / -tooltip-delta / -footer / -footer-stats / -footer-identity / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-lag / -badge-correlation / -badge-count); root mirrors `data-lag`, `data-pair-count`, `data-correlation`, `data-animate`; axis / tick / grid / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="series"|"scatter"`; scatter dots expose `data-t`, `data-lagged-value`, `data-current-value`, `data-above-diagonal`; series dots expose `data-x`, `data-value`. Tooltip on a series dot shows x + value; tooltip on a scatter dot shows the pair index `t`, the lagged value, the current value and the delta. Config badge in top-left calls out `LAG k=<lag> r=<correlation> n=<pairCount>`. | 11.570 | UI: chart-line-zigzag primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.588 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-zigzag.tsx` ships `<ChartLineZigzag>` -- pure-SVG line chart with a **ZigZag** overlay. The ZigZag indicator filters out every reversal smaller than a percent threshold and connects only the **significant pivots** -- the swing highs and swing lows that survive the filter. The raw series renders as a dimmed line beneath the bold ZigZag, so the major trend structure stands out from the noise. **The ZigZag filter:** `runLineZigzag(points, thresholdPercent)` (1) sorts the series by x -- the first point is always the starting pivot; (2) scanning forward, a running high and a running low since the last confirmed pivot are tracked; (3) when price retraces from the running extreme by at least the percent threshold, that extreme is confirmed as a pivot -- a `peak` off a running high, a `trough` off a running low -- and the trend reverses, with the scan REWINDING to the confirmed pivot so the next swing's running extreme is built correctly; (4) the final point is appended as an `end` pivot unless it is already the last confirmed pivot. A pivot takes the EXTREME value of its swing, not the value at the confirmation point. Each pivot also carries `moveFromPrev` -- the percent move from the previous pivot. The component fixture **[100,102,110,105,95,98,120,115,90]** makes the filtering visible: at a **5%** threshold all five pivots survive -- `start, peak(110), trough(95), peak(120), end` -- but at **20%** the 110 peak is filtered out because the retracement from 110 to 95 is only 13.6%, below the threshold (4 pivots); at a threshold no swing can clear, only `start` and `end` remain. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** `<ChartLineChangepoint>` detects statistical changepoints (shifts in the MEAN) whereas the ZigZag detects SWING PIVOTS by a percent-reversal rule (a geometric filter, not a statistical test); `<ChartLineMinMax>` draws a rolling min/max envelope and never connects discrete pivots; `<ChartLineTrend>` / `<ChartLineSegment>` fit or split a line and neither filters reversals by magnitude. The ZigZag's defining transform is the percent-reversal pivot rule. All exported helpers carry a `LineZigzag` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineZigzagPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `thresholdPercent` (the reversal threshold as a percent; default 5, coerced to a non-negative number); `strokeWidth` / `rawStrokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `pivotRadius` / `rawOpacity` / `seriesColor` / `zigzagColor` / `peakColor` / `troughColor` / `endpointColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showRawLine`/`showRawDots` (default false)/`showZigzag`/`showPivots`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showFooter`/`animate`; standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPivotClick({pivot})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_ZIGZAG_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/RAW_STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PIVOT_RADIUS/RAW_OPACITY/THRESHOLD_PERCENT/SERIES_COLOR/ZIGZAG_COLOR/PEAK_COLOR/TROUGH_COLOR/ENDPOINT_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineZigzagFinitePoints`; `normalizeLineZigzagThreshold(thresholdPercent)` (a non-finite or negative value -> 5; 0 allowed); `runLineZigzag(points, thresholdPercent?)` -- the ZigZag filter; returns `{series, pivots, pivotCount, thresholdPercent, ok}`; each pivot `{index, x, value, kind, moveFromPrev}` where kind is `start`|`peak`|`trough`|`end`; `ok` requires at least 2 finite samples; `computeLineZigzagLayout({data, ...})` -- projects the raw line, the ZigZag overlay path and the pivot markers (`rawPath`, `zigzagPath`, `rawDots`, projected `pivots`); degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 samples -> ok=false; `describeLineZigzagChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions ZigZag + pivots + threshold (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-zigzag-aria-desc"` span; the ZigZag overlay path + every pivot marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-zigzag / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -x-label / -y-label / -raw-path / -raw-dots / -raw-dot / -zigzag-path / -pivots / -pivot / -tooltip / -tooltip-kind / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-move / -footer / -footer-stats / -footer-points / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-threshold / -badge-pivots); root mirrors `data-pivot-count`, `data-threshold-percent`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; the raw path carries `data-kind="raw"`, the overlay `data-kind="zigzag"`; pivot markers expose `data-kind` (start / peak / trough / end), `data-x`, `data-value`, `data-move`. Tooltip on a pivot shows the pivot kind, x, value and the percent move from the previous pivot. Config badge in top-left calls out `ZZ thr=<percent>% pivots=<count>`. The footer reports the threshold, the pivot count and the raw point count. 49 vitest cases (defaults + getLineZigzagFinitePoints, normalizeLineZigzagThreshold incl. keeps a non-negative percent (incl. 0) + **a negative percent falls back to the default** + a non-finite percent falls back, runLineZigzag incl. empty / single point -> ok=false + **confirms the significant pivots at 5% (indices 0,2,4,6,8)** + **classifies each pivot kind** + **the pivots take the extreme values of each swing** + **reports the percent move from the previous pivot** + **a higher threshold filters out smaller swings (5 -> 4)** + **a threshold no swing can clear leaves just start + end** + normalizes the threshold + sorts by x + a two-point series yields start + end, computeLineZigzagLayout incl. empty/degenerate -> ok=false + **the raw path + the zigzag path** + a raw dot per point + a pivot per significant pivot + finite coordinates + x/y ranges + pivot count + threshold + bounds overrides, describeLineZigzagChart incl. | 11.571 | UI: chart-line-fibonacci primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.589 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-fibonacci.tsx` ships `<ChartLineFibonacci>` -- pure-SVG line chart that overlays **Fibonacci retracement levels**. The chart detects the series' swing high and swing low, then draws the canonical Fibonacci ratio lines between them -- horizontal support/resistance levels at 0%, 23.6%, 38.2%, 50%, 61.8%, 78.6% and 100% of the swing range. **The retracement grid:** `runLineFibonacci(points, ratios)` (1) sorts the series by x; (2) detects the **swing high** (max value) and **swing low** (min value); (3) classifies the **trend** -- `up` when the high occurs at or after the low, `down` otherwise; (4) computes the level at ratio `r` measured from the swing END (the later extreme) back toward the swing START (the older one): `value = end - r*(end - start)` -- so `r=0` sits on the most recent extreme and `r=1` on the older one (the standard retracement grid: for an up swing 0% at the high and 100% at the low, a down swing mirrors it). A series whose values are all equal has no range and is not `ok`. `normalizeLineFibonacciRatios` coerces a ratio set to an ascending, de-duplicated list of finite values clamped to [0,1], falling back to the standard seven ratios. The component fixture is an up swing -- low **0** at x=1, high **100** at x=4 -- so the levels land on round values: **100, 76.4, 61.8, 50, 38.2, 21.4, 0**; the down-swing fixture mirrors the grid: **0, 23.6, ..., 100**. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** `<ChartLineThreshold>` draws horizontal lines at USER-SUPPLIED FIXED thresholds whereas the Fibonacci levels are DERIVED from the detected swing high/low at the fixed Fibonacci ratios (the operator supplies no values); `<ChartLineZigzag>` filters swing pivots by a percent reversal and never draws ratio levels; `<ChartLineMinMax>` draws a rolling min/max envelope, not discrete horizontal levels; `<ChartLineTarget>` / `<ChartLineBaseline>` mark a single reference value. The defining transform is the swing detection plus the Fibonacci ratio grid. All exported helpers carry a `LineFibonacci` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineFibonacciPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `ratios` (a custom ratio set; default the standard seven, normalized to ascending unique [0,1] values); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `swingRadius` / `seriesColor` / `levelColor` / `anchorColor` / `highColor` / `lowColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showLevels`/`showLevelLabels`/`showAnchor` (the swing-low-to-swing-high diagonal)/`showSwings`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showFooter`/`animate`; standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onLevelClick({level})` / `onSampleClick({point})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_FIBONACCI_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/SWING_RADIUS/RATIOS/SERIES_COLOR/LEVEL_COLOR/ANCHOR_COLOR/HIGH_COLOR/LOW_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineFibonacciFinitePoints`; `normalizeLineFibonacciRatios(ratios)`; `runLineFibonacci(points, ratios?)` -- swing detection + the retracement levels; returns `{series, swingHigh, swingLow, trend, range, levels, valueMin, valueMax, ok}`; each level `{ratio, value}`; `ok` requires at least 2 finite samples with a non-zero range; `computeLineFibonacciLayout({data, ...})` -- projects the line, the level lines, the swing markers and the anchor (swing-low-to-swing-high diagonal); degenerate canvas / flat series / fewer than 2 samples -> ok=false; `describeLineFibonacciChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Fibonacci + retracement + swing (No data when missing / flat). Tooltip on a level shows the ratio percent and the level value; tooltip on a dot shows x + value. Config badge in top-left calls out `FIB <trend> H=<high> L=<low>`. | 11.572 | UI: chart-line-ichimoku primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.590 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-ichimoku.tsx` ships `<ChartLineIchimoku>` -- pure-SVG line chart with an **Ichimoku Kinko Hyo** overlay (the "one glance equilibrium chart"). On top of the raw price line it draws the conversion line (Tenkan-sen), the base line (Kijun-sen), the lagging span (Chikou) and the **kumo cloud** formed between the two leading spans (Senkou Span A / B). **The five Ichimoku lines:** every line is built from the donchian-midpoint formula -- `computeLineIchimokuMidline(values, period)` computes `(highest + lowest) / 2` over a trailing window (null until the window is full). **Tenkan-sen** (conversion): midline over the conversion period (default 9). **Kijun-sen** (base): midline over the base period (default 26). **Senkou Span A**: `(Tenkan + Kijun) / 2`, plotted `displacement` periods AHEAD (default 26). **Senkou Span B**: midline over the leading period (default 52), plotted ahead. **Chikou** (lagging span): the price itself, plotted `displacement` periods BEHIND. The forward/backward shift extends the x-axis -- x positions beyond the data are extrapolated from the average x-step, so the cloud reaches into the "future" and the Chikou into the "past". The **kumo** is the band between Span A and Span B: a sample is `bullish` when Span A is at or above Span B, `bearish` otherwise, and the cloud is drawn as PER-SEGMENT polygons tinted green (bullish) or orange (bearish) -- the canonical two-tone cloud. The component fixture (periods 2/3/4, displacement 2 on an 8-point series) produces a mixed cloud: five cloud samples with bullish flags **[true,false,false,true,true]** -- 3 bullish, 2 bearish -- which exercises both tints. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** Ichimoku bundles FIVE derived lines plus the two-tone cloud into one overlay -- no existing primitive computes the donchian-midpoint family, the forward-displaced leading spans or the lagging span; `<ChartLineBollinger>` / `<ChartLineBand>` draw a single statistical band, `<ChartLineMovingAvg>` overlays one smoothed line, whereas the Ichimoku cloud is a forward-projected, sign-tinted band between two DISTINCT leading spans. All exported helpers carry a `LineIchimoku` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineIchimokuPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `conversionPeriod` / `basePeriod` / `leadingPeriod` / `displacement` (the four Ichimoku periods; defaults 9/26/52/26); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `priceColor` / `tenkanColor` / `kijunColor` / `chikouColor` / `spanAColor` / `spanBColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showCloud`/`showTenkan`/`showKijun`/`showChikou`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `tenkan` / `kijun` / `chikou` / `cloud`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_ICHIMOKU_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/CONVERSION_PERIOD/BASE_PERIOD/LEADING_PERIOD/DISPLACEMENT/PRICE_COLOR/TENKAN_COLOR/KIJUN_COLOR/CHIKOU_COLOR/SPAN_A_COLOR/SPAN_B_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineIchimokuFinitePoints`; `normalizeLineIchimokuPeriod(period, fallback)` (non-finite / sub-1 -> fallback, fractional floored); `computeLineIchimokuMidline(values, period)` -- the donchian-midpoint window; `runLineIchimoku(points, options?)` -- the full Ichimoku computation; returns `{series, conversionPeriod, basePeriod, leadingPeriod, displacement, tenkan, kijun, spanA, spanB, chikou, cloud, bullishCount, bearishCount, ok}`; `tenkan`/`kijun` are `(number|null)[]`; `spanA`/`spanB`/`chikou` are displaced `{index, x, value}[]`; each cloud sample `{index, x, spanA, spanB, bullish}`; `computeLineIchimokuLayout({data, ...})` -- projects every line and builds the per-segment cloud polygons (`pricePath`, `priceDots` carrying tenkan/kijun, `tenkanPath`, `kijunPath`, `chikouPath`, `spanAPath`, `spanBPath`, `cloudSegments`); degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 samples -> ok=false; the x range extends to the displaced spans; `describeLineIchimokuChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Ichimoku + cloud + kumo + conversion (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-ichimoku-aria-desc"` span; the price line + every price dot `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-ichimoku / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -x-label / -y-label / -cloud / -cloud-segment / -span-a / -span-b / -chikou / -tenkan / -kijun / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-price / -tooltip-tenkan / -tooltip-kijun / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-periods / -badge-cloud); root mirrors `data-bullish-count`, `data-bearish-count`, `data-conversion-period`, `data-base-period`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; cloud segments expose `data-segment-index` and `data-bullish`. Tooltip on a price dot shows x, price, and the Tenkan and Kijun values at that index (the "one glance" read). Config badge in top-left calls out `ICHI <conv>-<base>-<leading> cloud=<bull/bear/flat>`. The legend has five toggle items (Price / Tenkan / Kijun / Chikou / Kumo) plus a bullish-vs-bearish tally. 58 vitest cases (defaults + getLineIchimokuFinitePoints, normalizeLineIchimokuPeriod incl. keeps a positive integer + **a sub-1 period falls back** + **floors a fractional period** + non-finite falls back, computeLineIchimokuMidline incl. **null until the window is full** + **(highest + lowest) / 2 over the window** + a period of 1 returns each value + empty -> [] + a window longer than the data -> all null, runLineIchimoku incl. empty -> ok=false + resolves the default periods + **computes the tenkan line** + **computes the kijun line** + **shifts the leading spans forward** + **shifts the chikou span back** + **builds the kumo cloud where both spans are defined** + **flags each cloud sample bullish/bearish** + counts the bullish + bearish samples + sorts by x + honours custom periods, computeLineIchimokuLayout incl. empty/degenerate -> ok=false + price line path + tenkan + kijun + chikou paths + leading-span paths + **one cloud segment per consecutive pair** + **cloud segments carry the bullish flag** + price dots carry the tenkan + kijun values + cloud counts + **the x range extends to the displaced spans**, describeLineIchimokuChart incl. | 11.573 | UI: chart-line-keltner primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.591 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-keltner.tsx` ships `<ChartLineKeltner>` -- pure-SVG line chart with **Keltner channels** (a volatility envelope built from an EMA centre line and an ATR-scaled band). The middle line is an exponential moving average of the price; the upper and lower bands sit a multiple of the Average True Range above and below it. **EMA centre + ATR band:** `computeLineKeltnerEMA(values, period)` is the exponential moving average with smoothing factor `alpha = 2/(period+1)`, seeded with the first value so the EMA is defined at every index. `computeLineKeltnerATR(values, period)` is the Average True Range, Wilder-smoothed -- for a single-value series the true range at index i is the absolute period-over-period change `|v[i]-v[i-1]|`, the first ATR (at index `period`) is the simple mean of the first `period` true ranges, and subsequent values use Wilder smoothing `(prev*(period-1)+tr)/period`; indices before the window is full are `null`. `runLineKeltner` combines them: the **middle** line is the EMA, the **upper** band is `middle + multiplier*ATR` and the **lower** band is `middle - multiplier*ATR` (defined only where the ATR is). The component fixture (EMA 3, ATR 2, multiplier 2 on a 6-point series) makes every value exact: the EMA is **[10,15,22.5,21.25,30.625,30.3125]**, the ATR is **[null,null,10,10,15,12.5]**, so the band at index 2 is **2.5..42.5** (`22.5 +/- 2*10`). **Distinct from `chart-line-bollinger`:** both are volatility envelopes but they differ in BOTH components -- (1) CENTRE: Bollinger centres on a SIMPLE moving average (SMA) whereas Keltner centres on an EXPONENTIAL moving average (EMA), which reacts faster to recent moves; (2) BAND WIDTH: Bollinger scales the band by the STANDARD DEVIATION of price (a dispersion measure) whereas Keltner scales it by the Average True Range (a range-based volatility measure, Wilder-smoothed, that reacts to the size of each step, not the spread of values around a mean). It also differs from `<ChartLineEwmsd>` (EWMA centre + exponentially-weighted STANDARD DEVIATION band): again the band is deviation-based there and range-based (ATR) here. All exported helpers carry a `LineKeltner` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineKeltnerPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `emaPeriod` / `atrPeriod` / `multiplier` (the three channel parameters; defaults 20/10/2); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `priceColor` / `middleColor` / `upperColor` / `lowerColor` / `channelColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showMiddle`/`showChannel`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `middle` / `channel`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_KELTNER_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/EMA_PERIOD/ATR_PERIOD/MULTIPLIER/PRICE_COLOR/MIDDLE_COLOR/UPPER_COLOR/LOWER_COLOR/CHANNEL_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineKeltnerFinitePoints`; `normalizeLineKeltnerPeriod(period, fallback)`; `normalizeLineKeltnerMultiplier(multiplier, fallback)`; `computeLineKeltnerEMA(values, period)`; `computeLineKeltnerATR(values, period)`; `runLineKeltner(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, emaPeriod, atrPeriod, multiplier, middle, atr, upper, lower, bandPointCount, ok}`; `middle` is `number[]`, `atr`/`upper`/`lower` are `(number|null)[]`; `computeLineKeltnerLayout({data, ...})` -- projects the price, middle, upper, lower lines and the closed channel fill (`pricePath`, `middlePath`, `upperPath`, `lowerPath`, `channelPath`, `priceDots` carrying middle/upper/lower/atr); degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 samples -> ok=false; `describeLineKeltnerChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Keltner + EMA + ATR (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-keltner-aria-desc"` span; the price line + every price dot `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-keltner / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -x-label / -y-label / -channel / -upper / -lower / -middle / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-price / -tooltip-middle / -tooltip-band / -tooltip-atr / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-ema / -badge-atr / -badge-mult); root mirrors `data-band-point-count`, `data-ema-period`, `data-atr-period`, `data-multiplier`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`. Tooltip on a price dot shows x, price, the EMA value, the band range and the ATR at that index. Config badge in top-left calls out `KC ema=<emaPeriod> atr=<atrPeriod> x<multiplier>`. The legend has three toggle items (Price / EMA / Channel) plus a banded-point count. 58 vitest cases (defaults + getLineKeltnerFinitePoints, normalizeLineKeltnerPeriod incl. keeps a positive integer + **a sub-1 period falls back** + floors a fractional period + non-finite falls back, normalizeLineKeltnerMultiplier incl. keeps a positive multiplier + **a non-positive multiplier falls back** + non-finite falls back, computeLineKeltnerEMA incl. **seeds with the first value** + a constant series stays constant + **a period of 1 is the identity** + **smooths with alpha = 2/(period+1)** + empty -> [], computeLineKeltnerATR incl. **null until the window is full** + **Wilder-smooths the true ranges** + a series too short -> all null + empty -> [], runLineKeltner incl. empty -> ok=false + resolves the default periods + **the middle line is the EMA** + **computes the ATR** + **the bands are middle +/- multiplier*ATR** + counts the banded points + **the multiplier scales the band width** + sorts by x + honours custom periods, computeLineKeltnerLayout incl. empty/degenerate -> ok=false + price + middle + upper + lower paths + **a closed channel fill path** + price dots carry the middle / band / atr values + **the y range covers the band extremes** + band point count + periods + bounds overrides, describeLineKeltnerChart incl. No data + summary mentions Keltner + EMA + ATR, component render incl. empty / price line path / middle EMA line + hide / **the channel fill + upper/lower bands** / hide channel / price dots off by default + on / config badge ema + atr + multiplier + hide / ARIA region + img + sr-only desc / root data-* / tooltip on a price dot (price + ema + band + atr) + hide on leave / omit tooltip via prop / onPointClick / legend three toggle items + toggle / the legend toggle hides the channel / hide legend / animate flag + class / ref / displayName / custom ariaLabel / xLabel + yLabel) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. | 11.574 | UI: chart-line-parabolic-sar primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.592 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-parabolic-sar.tsx` ships `<ChartLineParabolicSar>` -- pure-SVG line chart with a **Parabolic SAR** (Stop And Reverse) overlay, Welles Wilder's trend-following trailing-stop indicator. A dot is plotted each period: when the dots sit BELOW the price the trend is up, when they sit ABOVE it the trend is down, and when price pierces the dot the trend "stops and reverses". **The algorithm:** `runLineParabolicSar(points, options)` adapts Wilder's algorithm to a single-value series: (1) the initial trend is taken from the first move (up if the second value is at or above the first, else down); (2) each period the SAR advances toward the **extreme point** (`ep`) by an acceleration factor (`af`): `sar += af * (ep - sar)`, the factor starting at `step` (default 0.02) and growing by `step` -- capped at `maxStep` (default 0.2) -- every time the trend posts a new extreme; (3) the SAR is clamped so it cannot cross into the prior one or two values (it stays on its side of the price); (4) when price pierces the SAR the trend STOPS AND REVERSES -- the SAR jumps to the prior extreme point, the new extreme point becomes the current value, and the acceleration factor resets to `step`. `maxStep` is clamped up to at least `step` so the band is always well ordered. The component fixture rises from 10 to 16 then falls to 8: the SAR accelerates from 10 (`af` growing 0.02, 0.04, ...) to **10.24** and **10.7008**, then at index 5 price drops to 9 (below the clamped SAR of 11) so the trend flips to down, the SAR jumps to the prior extreme point **16**, and the next period settles at **15.86** -- one reversal, five up periods, two down. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** the Parabolic SAR is a trend-following trailing stop with an ACCELERATING recursion and a hard stop-and-reverse flip -- no existing primitive computes it; `<ChartLineZigzag>` filters swing pivots by a percent reversal (a static geometric filter, NOT an accelerating stop), `<ChartLineKeltner>` / `<ChartLineBollinger>` draw volatility bands, `<ChartLineTrend>` fits a regression line. All exported helpers carry a `LineParabolicSar` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineParabolicSarPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `step` / `maxStep` (the acceleration-factor increment and cap; defaults 0.02 / 0.2); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `sarRadius` / `reversalRadius` / `priceColor` / `upColor` / `downColor` / `reversalColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false -- the price dots)/`showSar`/`showReversals`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showFooter`/`animate`; standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onSarClick({sample})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_PARABOLIC_SAR_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/SAR_RADIUS/REVERSAL_RADIUS/STEP/MAX_STEP/PRICE_COLOR/UP_COLOR/DOWN_COLOR/REVERSAL_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineParabolicSarFinitePoints`; `normalizeLineParabolicSarStep(value, fallback)` (non-finite / non-positive -> fallback); `runLineParabolicSar(points, options?)` -- the SAR recursion; returns `{series, step, maxStep, samples, reversalCount, upCount, downCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, sar, trend, reversed}`; `computeLineParabolicSarLayout({data, ...})` -- projects the price line, the price dots and the SAR dots (`pricePath`, `priceDots`, `sarDots` with px/sarPy/pricePy); degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 samples -> ok=false; `describeLineParabolicSarChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Parabolic SAR + stop-and-reverse + trend (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-parabolic-sar-aria-desc"` span; the price line + every SAR dot `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-parabolic-sar / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -x-label / -y-label / -price-path / -price-dots / -price-dot / -reversals / -reversal / -dots / -dot / -tooltip / -tooltip-trend / -tooltip-sar / -tooltip-price / -tooltip-reversal / -footer / -footer-stats / -footer-trend / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-step / -badge-max / -badge-flips); root mirrors `data-reversal-count`, `data-up-count`, `data-down-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; SAR dots expose `data-trend` (up / down), `data-reversed`, `data-sar`, `data-value`; reversal markers expose `data-point-index` and `data-trend`. Tooltip on a SAR dot shows the trend, the SAR (stop) value, the price, and -- on a reversal period -- a "stop and reverse" note. Config badge in top-left calls out `SAR af=<step> max=<maxStep> flips=<count>`. The footer reports the step parameters, the flip count and the up/down period tally. 49 vitest cases (defaults + getLineParabolicSarFinitePoints, normalizeLineParabolicSarStep incl. keeps a positive value + **a non-positive value falls back** + non-finite falls back, runLineParabolicSar incl. empty / single point -> ok=false + **the initial trend is up when the first move rises** + **down when it falls** + **accelerates the SAR toward the extreme point** + **stops and reverses when price pierces the SAR** + continues after a reversal + counts the reversals + **reports the per-period trend sequence** + counts the up/down periods + sorts by x + **clamps maxStep up to at least step**, computeLineParabolicSarLayout incl. empty/degenerate -> ok=false + price line path + a price dot + a SAR dot per period + SAR dots carry the trend + reversed flag + **the y range covers both the price and the SAR** + reversal / trend counts + bounds overrides, describeLineParabolicSarChart incl. No data + summary mentions Parabolic SAR + stop-and-reverse + trend, component render incl. empty / price line path / **one SAR dot per period + hide** / **colours SAR dots by trend** / **a reversal marker per flip + hide** / price dots off by default + on / config badge step + max + flip count + hide / ARIA region + img + sr-only desc / root data-* / SAR dot data-* / tooltip on a SAR dot (trend + SAR + price) + hide on leave / omit tooltip via prop / onSarClick / footer stats + hide / axis ticks / animate flag + class / ref / displayName / custom ariaLabel / xLabel + yLabel) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. | 11.575 | UI: chart-line-vwap primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.593 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-vwap.tsx` ships `<ChartLineVwap>` -- pure-SVG line chart with a **volume-weighted average price (VWAP)** overlay. Unlike every other line primitive, the data carries TWO paired fields per period -- `price` and `volume` -- and the chart plots the cumulative VWAP line on top of the price line, with the volume drawn as faint bars along the bottom. **The VWAP computation:** `runLineVwap(points)` walks the series sorted by x and, at each period, computes the cumulative VWAP = `sum(price[i]*volume[i]) / sum(volume[i])` for `i = 0..t` -- the running average price weighted by traded volume, so a high-volume period pulls the line harder than a low-volume one. Two edge cases are handled deterministically: a NEGATIVE volume is clamped to 0 (a trade cannot have negative size); a period whose CUMULATIVE volume is still 0 has a `null` VWAP (the ratio is undefined), so the VWAP line begins only once volume has accumulated. Each sample also carries `deviation = price - vwap` -- how far the price trades from its volume-weighted benchmark. The component fixture makes the math exact: prices **[10,20,30,20]** with volumes **[100,100,200,100]** give a cumulative VWAP of **[10,15,22.5,22]**, deviations of **[0,5,7.5,-2]**, a total volume of **500** and a final VWAP of **22**. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** VWAP is the ONLY line primitive that consumes a paired `{price, volume}` series -- the volume weighting cannot be reproduced from a plain `{x, value}` series; `<ChartLineMovingAvg>` averages by COUNT (an unweighted SMA), `<ChartLineEwma>` weights by RECENCY, `<ChartLineCumulative>` sums values, and none weight by a second quantity. The volume bars at the bottom surface the weights that drive the line. All exported helpers carry a `LineVwap` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineVwapPoint[]` (`{x, price, volume}`); `volumeBandRatio` (the fraction of the panel height the volume bars occupy; default 0.22, clamped to [0.05, 0.5]); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `priceColor` / `vwapColor` / `volumeColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showVwap`/`showVolume`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `vwap` / `volume`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_VWAP_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/VOLUME_BAND_RATIO/PRICE_COLOR/VWAP_COLOR/VOLUME_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineVwapFinitePoints` (keeps points whose x, price and volume are all finite); `runLineVwap(points)` -- the cumulative VWAP; returns `{series, samples, totalVolume, vwapFinal, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, price, volume, vwap, deviation}` (`vwap` is `number|null`); `computeLineVwapLayout({data, ...})` -- projects the price line, the VWAP overlay path and the volume bars (`pricePath`, `vwapPath`, `dots` carrying price/volume/vwap/deviation, `volumeBars`, `totalVolume`, `vwapFinal`, `maxVolume`); degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 samples -> ok=false; `describeLineVwapChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions VWAP + volume-weighted (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-vwap-aria-desc"` span; the price line + every price dot `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-vwap / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -volume / -volume-bar / -axes / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -x-label / -y-label / -vwap-path / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-price / -tooltip-volume / -tooltip-vwap / -tooltip-deviation / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-final / -badge-volume); root mirrors `data-total-volume`, `data-vwap-final`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; volume bars expose `data-volume`; price dots expose `data-price`, `data-volume`, `data-vwap`, `data-deviation`. Tooltip on a price dot shows x, price, volume, the VWAP and the deviation from it. Config badge in top-left calls out `VWAP final=<vwapFinal> vol=<totalVolume>`. The legend has three toggle items (Price / VWAP / Volume) plus the final VWAP. 46 vitest cases (defaults + getLineVwapFinitePoints incl. drops a non-finite x / price / volume + null, runLineVwap incl. empty / single point -> ok=false + **computes the cumulative volume-weighted average price** + **the deviation is price minus VWAP** + reports the total volume + reports the final VWAP + **a leading zero-volume period has a null VWAP** + **clamps a negative volume to zero** + sorts by x + a constant price yields a flat VWAP, computeLineVwapLayout incl. empty/degenerate -> ok=false + price line path + **the VWAP overlay path** + a volume bar per period + dots carry the price / volume / VWAP / deviation + the y range covers price + VWAP + total volume + final VWAP + max volume + bounds overrides, describeLineVwapChart incl. No data + summary mentions VWAP + volume-weighted, component render incl. empty / price line path / **the VWAP overlay + hide** / **one volume bar per period + hide** / volume bars carry the volume value / price dots off by default + on / config badge final + total volume + hide / ARIA region + img + sr-only desc / root data-* / price dot data-* / tooltip on a price dot (price + volume + vwap + deviation) + hide on leave / omit tooltip via prop / onPointClick / legend three toggle items + toggle / the legend toggle hides the VWAP overlay / hide legend / animate flag + class / ref / displayName / custom ariaLabel / xLabel + yLabel) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for the new files. Exported via components/ui barrel. Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/c4-mgr-auto. Worker auto-w557. Bump 1.11.593. |
| 11.576 | UI: chart-line-macd primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.594 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-macd.tsx` ships `<ChartLineMacd>` -- pure-SVG line chart with a **MACD** (Moving Average Convergence Divergence) sub-panel. The price line sits in the TOP panel; the BOTTOM panel renders the MACD momentum study -- the MACD line, the signal line and the convergence histogram. **The computation:** `computeLineMacdEMA(values, period)` is an exponential moving average (`alpha = 2/(period+1)`, seeded with the first value so it is defined at every index). The **MACD line** is the fast EMA minus the slow EMA of the price (default periods 12 and 26); the **signal line** is an EMA of the MACD line (default period 9); the **histogram** is `macd - signal` -- the convergence/divergence of the two lines -- with positive bars (MACD above signal) reading green and negative bars reading red. All three series are defined at every index because the EMAs are seeded. The component fixture (periods 1/3/3 on a 4-point series) makes every value exact: the MACD line is **[0,5,7.5,-1.25]**, the signal line **[0,2.5,5,1.875]**, and the histogram **[0,2.5,2.5,-3.125]**. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** MACD layers THREE EMAs into one momentum study with a sign-tinted histogram -- `<ChartLineEwma>` overlays a SINGLE EMA, `<ChartLineKeltner>` builds an EMA centre with an ATR band, `<ChartLineMomentum>` is a sub-panel oscillator driven by a DERIVATIVE rather than a difference-of-EMAs; none compute the fast/slow EMA spread, the signal smoothing or the convergence histogram. All exported helpers carry a `LineMacd` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineMacdPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `fastPeriod` / `slowPeriod` / `signalPeriod` (the three EMA periods; defaults 12/26/9); `pricePanelRatio` (the fraction of the height given to the price panel; default 0.58, clamped to [0.3, 0.8]); `gap` (the pixel gap between the stacked panels); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `priceColor` / `macdColor` / `signalColor` / `histUpColor` / `histDownColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showMacd`/`showSignal`/`showHistogram`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `macd` / `signal` / `histogram`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_MACD_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/FAST_PERIOD/SLOW_PERIOD/SIGNAL_PERIOD/PRICE_COLOR/MACD_COLOR/SIGNAL_COLOR/HIST_UP_COLOR/HIST_DOWN_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineMacdFinitePoints`; `normalizeLineMacdPeriod(period, fallback)`; `computeLineMacdEMA(values, period)`; `runLineMacd(points, options?)` -- the MACD / signal / histogram computation; returns `{series, fastPeriod, slowPeriod, signalPeriod, macd, signal, histogram, samples, ok}`; `macd`/`signal`/`histogram` are `number[]`, each sample `{index, x, price, macd, signal, histogram}`; `computeLineMacdLayout({data, ...})` -- stacks the price panel above the MACD panel and projects both (`pricePath`, `priceDots`, `macdPath`, `signalPath`, `histogramBars`, `zeroLineY`); the MACD panel y range is symmetric about zero; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 samples -> ok=false; `describeLineMacdChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions MACD + signal + histogram (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-macd-aria-desc"` span; the price line + every price dot `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-macd / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis-group / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -panel-labels / -panel-label / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -zero-line / -histogram / -histogram-bar / -macd-line / -signal-line / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-price / -tooltip-macd / -tooltip-signal / -tooltip-histogram / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-periods); root mirrors `data-fast-period`, `data-slow-period`, `data-signal-period`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; axis / tick / grid / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"macd"`; histogram bars expose `data-histogram` and `data-positive`. Tooltip on a price dot or a histogram bar shows x, price, and the MACD / signal / histogram values at that index. Config badge in top-left calls out `MACD <fast>/<slow>/<signal>`. The legend has four toggle items (Price / MACD / Signal / Histogram). 53 vitest cases (defaults + getLineMacdFinitePoints, normalizeLineMacdPeriod incl. keeps a positive integer + **a sub-1 period falls back** + floors a fractional period + non-finite falls back, computeLineMacdEMA incl. **a period of 1 is the identity** + **smooths with alpha = 2/(period+1)** + a constant series stays constant + empty -> [], runLineMacd incl. empty / single point -> ok=false + resolves the default periods + **the MACD line is the fast EMA minus the slow EMA** + **the signal line is an EMA of the MACD line** + **the histogram is the MACD minus the signal** + the samples carry every per-period value + sorts by x + honours custom periods, computeLineMacdLayout incl. empty/degenerate -> ok=false + **stacks a price panel above a MACD panel** + price + MACD + signal paths + **one histogram bar per period** + histogram bars carry the value + positive flag + **the MACD panel y range is symmetric about zero** + price dots carry the macd / signal / histogram values + periods + point count, describeLineMacdChart incl.
