| 11.577 | UI: chart-line-rsi primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.595 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-rsi.tsx` ships `<ChartLineRsi>` -- pure-SVG line chart with a **Relative Strength Index (RSI)** oscillator sub-panel. The price line sits in the TOP panel; the BOTTOM panel renders the RSI line bounded to 0-100, with shaded **overbought** and **oversold** zones. **The computation:** `computeLineRsi(values, period)` is Welles Wilder's RSI -- (1) period-over-period changes are split into GAINS (`max(delta, 0)`) and LOSSES (`max(-delta, 0)`); (2) the average gain and average loss are WILDER-SMOOTHED (the first average is the simple mean of the first `period` changes, then `avg = (avg*(period-1) + change)/period`); (3) `RSI = 100 - 100/(1 + avgGain/avgLoss)`, bounded to [0,100]. Three edge cases are handled deterministically: an all-gain window reads **100**, an all-loss window reads **0**, a flat window reads **50**; indices before the window is full are `null`. `runLineRsi` classifies each reading into a ZONE: `overbought` at or above the overbought threshold (default 70), `oversold` at or below the oversold threshold (default 30), `neutral` between. The component fixture (period 2) rises 10 -> 30 then falls hard to 5: the RSI is **[null,null,100,33.333,25]**, which visits the overbought, neutral and oversold zones in turn -- one overbought reading, one oversold. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** RSI is a BOUNDED 0-100 momentum oscillator built from the RATIO of Wilder-smoothed gains to losses, with shaded overbought/oversold zones -- `<ChartLineMacd>` is an UNBOUNDED momentum study (a difference of EMAs), `<ChartLineMomentum>` is a derivative oscillator, `<ChartLineZscore>` is a rolling standardised score; none compute the gain/loss ratio, the 0-100 bound or the overbought/oversold zoning. All exported helpers carry a `LineRsi` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineRsiPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `period` (the RSI period; default 14); `overbought` / `oversold` (the zone thresholds; defaults 70 / 30, clamped to [0,100]); `pricePanelRatio` (the fraction of the height given to the price panel; default 0.58, clamped to [0.3, 0.8]); `gap` (the pixel gap between the stacked panels); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `priceColor` / `rsiColor` / `overboughtColor` / `oversoldColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showRsi`/`showZones`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `rsi` / `zones`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_RSI_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/OVERBOUGHT/OVERSOLD/PRICE_COLOR/RSI_COLOR/OVERBOUGHT_COLOR/OVERSOLD_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineRsiFinitePoints`; `normalizeLineRsiPeriod(period, fallback)`; `computeLineRsi(values, period)` -- the Wilder RSI series (`(number|null)[]`); `runLineRsi(points, options?)` -- the RSI plus the per-sample zone classification; returns `{series, period, overbought, oversold, rsi, samples, overboughtCount, oversoldCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, price, rsi, zone}`; `computeLineRsiLayout({data, ...})` -- stacks the price panel above the RSI panel (the RSI panel y axis fixed 0-100); projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `rsiPath`, `rsiMarkers`, the `overboughtRect` / `oversoldRect` zone rects and the threshold lines; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 samples -> ok=false; `describeLineRsiChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions RSI + overbought + oversold (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-rsi-aria-desc"` span; the price line + every price dot + every RSI marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-rsi / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -zones / -overbought-zone / -oversold-zone / -threshold-line / -axes / -axis-group / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -panel-labels / -panel-label / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -rsi-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-price / -tooltip-rsi / -tooltip-zone / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-period / -badge-zones); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-overbought-count`, `data-oversold-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; axis / tick / grid / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"rsi"`; RSI markers expose `data-rsi` and `data-zone`; threshold lines carry `data-kind` (overbought / oversold). Tooltip on a price dot or an RSI marker shows x, price, the RSI value and the zone. Config badge in top-left calls out `RSI p=<period> ob=<overbought> os=<oversold>`. The legend has three toggle items (Price / RSI / Zones). 55 vitest cases (defaults + getLineRsiFinitePoints, normalizeLineRsiPeriod incl. keeps a positive integer + **a sub-1 period falls back** + floors a fractional period + non-finite falls back, computeLineRsi incl. **null until the window is full** + empty -> [] + **an all-gain window reads 100** + **an all-loss window reads 0** + **a flat window reads 50** + **Wilder-smooths the gains and losses**, runLineRsi incl. empty / single point -> ok=false + resolves the default period + thresholds + **computes the RSI series** + **classifies each sample into a zone** + counts the overbought + oversold readings + honours custom thresholds + sorts by x, computeLineRsiLayout incl. empty/degenerate -> ok=false + **stacks a price panel above an RSI panel** + price + RSI paths + **an RSI marker per defined reading** + **the RSI y axis spans 0 to 100** + the overbought + oversold zone rects + zone counts + period, describeLineRsiChart incl. No data + summary mentions RSI + overbought + oversold, component render incl. empty / price line path / RSI line + hide / **an RSI marker per defined reading** / **colours RSI markers by zone** / the overbought + oversold zones + hide / the threshold lines / panel labels / price dots off by default + on / config badge period + thresholds + hide / ARIA region + img + sr-only desc / root data-* / RSI marker data-* / tooltip on an RSI marker (price + rsi + zone) + hide on leave / omit tooltip via prop / onPointClick from a marker / legend three toggle items + toggle / the legend toggle hides the zones / hide legend / animate flag + class / ref / displayName / custom ariaLabel) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. | 11.578 | UI: chart-line-stochastic primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.596 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-stochastic.tsx` ships `<ChartLineStochastic>` -- pure-SVG line chart with a **stochastic oscillator** sub-panel. The price line sits in the TOP panel; the BOTTOM panel plots the **%K** and **%D** lines, bounded to 0-100, with shaded overbought and oversold zones. **The computation:** `computeLineStochasticK(values, period)` is George Lane's %K -- for each period it measures where the current value sits within its trailing high-low range, `%K = 100 * (value - lowestLow) / (highestHigh - lowestLow)` over a window of `period` values; a flat window (zero range) reads 50, the middle of a range reads 50, the top reads 100 and the bottom 0; indices before the window is full are `null`. `computeLineStochasticD(kValues, dPeriod)` is the **%D** signal line -- a simple moving average of %K over `dPeriod` values, defined only where the full %K window is itself defined. `runLineStochastic` classifies each reading into a ZONE from %K: `overbought` at or above the overbought threshold (default 80), `oversold` at or below the oversold threshold (default 20), `neutral` between. The component fixture (period 3, %D period 2) makes the math exact: %K is **[null,null,100,0,0,100]**, %D is **[null,null,null,50,0,50]** -- two overbought readings, two oversold. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** both the stochastic and `<ChartLineRsi>` are 0-100 oscillators with overbought/oversold zones, but the COMPUTATION is different -- RSI is a gain/loss RATIO whereas the stochastic is a RANGE-POSITION measure (where the close sits between its recent low and high) -- and the stochastic plots TWO lines (%K and its %D signal SMA) where RSI plots one; `<ChartLineMacd>` is an unbounded EMA-difference study and `<ChartLineMomentum>` is a derivative oscillator, neither computing the trailing range position. All exported helpers carry a `LineStochastic` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineStochasticPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `period` / `dPeriod` (the %K lookback and the %D smoothing period; defaults 14 / 3); `overbought` / `oversold` (the zone thresholds; defaults 80 / 20, clamped to [0,100]); `pricePanelRatio` (the fraction of the height given to the price panel; default 0.58, clamped to [0.3, 0.8]); `gap` (the pixel gap between the stacked panels); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `priceColor` / `kColor` / `dColor` / `overboughtColor` / `oversoldColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showK`/`showD`/`showZones`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `k` / `d` / `zones`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_STOCHASTIC_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/K_PERIOD/D_PERIOD/OVERBOUGHT/OVERSOLD/PRICE_COLOR/K_COLOR/D_COLOR/OVERBOUGHT_COLOR/OVERSOLD_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineStochasticFinitePoints`; `normalizeLineStochasticPeriod(period, fallback)`; `computeLineStochasticK(values, period)` -- the %K series; `computeLineStochasticD(kValues, dPeriod)` -- the %D SMA; `runLineStochastic(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, period, dPeriod, overbought, oversold, k, d, samples, overboughtCount, oversoldCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, price, k, d, zone}`; `computeLineStochasticLayout({data, ...})` -- stacks the price panel above the stochastic panel (the stochastic panel y axis fixed 0-100); projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `kPath`, `dPath`, `kMarkers`, the `overboughtRect` / `oversoldRect` zone rects and the threshold lines; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 samples -> ok=false; `describeLineStochasticChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions stochastic + percent-K + percent-D (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-stochastic-aria-desc"` span; the price line + every price dot + every %K marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-stochastic / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -zones / -overbought-zone / -oversold-zone / -threshold-line / -axes / -axis-group / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -panel-labels / -panel-label / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -d-line / -k-line / -k-markers / -k-marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-price / -tooltip-k / -tooltip-d / -tooltip-zone / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-period / -badge-zones); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-d-period`, `data-overbought-count`, `data-oversold-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; axis / tick / grid / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"stoch"`; %K markers expose `data-k` and `data-zone`; threshold lines carry `data-kind` (overbought / oversold). Tooltip on a price dot or a %K marker shows x, price, %K, %D and the zone. Config badge in top-left calls out `STOCH k=<period> d=<dPeriod> ob=<overbought> os=<oversold>`. The legend has four toggle items (Price / %K / %D / Zones). 58 vitest cases (defaults + getLineStochasticFinitePoints, normalizeLineStochasticPeriod incl. keeps a positive integer + **a sub-1 period falls back** + floors a fractional period + non-finite falls back, computeLineStochasticK incl. **null until the window is full** + empty -> [] + **measures the value position in the trailing range** + **a flat window reads 50** + the middle of the range reads 50, computeLineStochasticD incl. **the SMA of %K over the d-period** + a d-period of 1 echoes %K + null where the %K window has a gap + empty -> [], runLineStochastic incl. empty / single point -> ok=false + resolves the default periods + thresholds + **computes the %K and %D series** + **classifies each sample into a zone from %K** + counts the overbought + oversold readings + honours custom thresholds + sorts by x, computeLineStochasticLayout incl. empty/degenerate -> ok=false + **stacks a price panel above a stochastic panel** + price + %K + %D paths + **a %K marker per defined reading** + **the stochastic y axis spans 0 to 100** + the overbought + oversold zone rects + zone counts + periods, describeLineStochasticChart incl. | 11.579 | UI: chart-line-atr primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.597 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-atr.tsx` ships `<ChartLineAtr>` -- pure-SVG line chart with an **Average True Range (ATR) volatility sub-panel**. The price line occupies the top panel; the bottom panel plots the ATR as a standalone volatility study, with the raw per-period **true range** drawn behind it as faint bottom-anchored bars. Both panels share one x-axis. **Indicator math:** `computeLineAtrTrueRanges(values)` is the per-period true range -- for a single-value series the true range is the absolute period-over-period change `trueRange[i] = |values[i] - values[i-1]|` for i >= 1; index 0 has no prior value and reads `null`. `computeLineAtr(values, period)` is **Welles Wilder's Average True Range**: the first ATR, at index `period`, seeds with the simple mean of the first `period` true ranges (`atr[period] = mean(trueRange[1..period])`); every later value uses **Wilder smoothing** `atr[i] = (atr[i-1]*(period-1) + trueRange[i]) / period`. Indices before the window fills read `null`, and the ATR is non-negative throughout since every true range is an absolute value. **Worked fixture:** `values = [10,20,30,20,40,30]`, `period 2` -> true range `[null,10,10,10,20,10]`, then `atr[2] = mean(10,10) = 10`, `atr[3] = (10*1+10)/2 = 10`, `atr[4] = (10*1+20)/2 = 15`, `atr[5] = (15*1+10)/2 = 12.5` -> atr `[null,null,10,10,15,12.5]`, atrFinal 12.5, atrMax 15. **Distinct from chart-line-keltner:** Keltner uses the ATR only to **scale a band envelope** around an EMA centre overlaid on the price; chart-line-atr displays the ATR **itself** as a volatility line in a dedicated sub-panel -- the volatility study, not a derived band. **Distinct from chart-line-bollinger:** Bollinger bands measure dispersion via the rolling standard deviation; ATR measures the typical absolute move (different statistic, band overlay vs sub-panel line). **Distinct from chart-line-macd/-rsi/-stochastic:** those sub-panels plot momentum / range-position oscillators; chart-line-atr plots a non-negative volatility magnitude. API: `data: readonly ChartLineAtrPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `period` (default 14, normalized to a positive integer); sizing `width`/`height`/`padding`/`gap`/`pricePanelRatio`/`tickCount`; styling `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`priceColor`/`atrColor`/`trueRangeColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots`/`showAtr`/`showTrueRange`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled series visibility (`hiddenSeries`/`defaultHiddenSeries`/`onHiddenSeriesChange`); `formatValue`/`formatX`; `onPointClick({point})`; `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`; standard a11y props. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_ATR_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/PRICE_COLOR/ATR_COLOR/TRUE_RANGE_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineAtrFinitePoints` (drops non-finite x/value); `normalizeLineAtrPeriod(period, fallback)` (non-finite or sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineAtrTrueRanges(values)`; `computeLineAtr(values, period)`; `runLineAtr(points, options?)` -- sorts finite points by x, computes true range + ATR, returns `{series, period, trueRange, atr, samples, atrFinal, atrMax, ok}`; ok=false for fewer than 2 points; `computeLineAtrLayout(options)` -- stacks a price panel above an ATR panel sharing one x-axis; the ATR panel y-range floors at 0 and spans the larger of the peak ATR and peak true range so the faint true-range bars and the ATR line share one scale; returns panels, ticks, the price path, ATR path, ATR markers (only where ATR is defined), and bottom-anchored true-range bars (only where true range is defined); `describeLineAtrChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentioning Average True Range + volatility + period + peak ATR (No data when fewer than 2 points). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-atr-aria-desc"` span; price path + price dots `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; ATR markers `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}` with the ATR value in the aria-label. data-section on every node (chart-line-atr / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -true-range / -true-range-bar / -axes / -axis-group / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -panel-labels / -panel-label / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -atr-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-price / -tooltip-true-range / -tooltip-atr / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-period / -badge-max); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-atr-final`, `data-atr-max`, `data-total-points`, `data-empty`, `data-animate`. Tooltip on price dots and ATR markers shows x, the bold price, the true range, and the ATR for the hovered period. Config badge in top-left calls out `ATR p=<period> max=<peakATR>`. Three-series legend (Price / ATR / True Range) with per-series toggle plus a `final ATR <value>` stat. 81 vitest cases (getLineAtrFinitePoints incl. null/undefined + non-finite x/value drops, normalizeLineAtrPeriod incl. floor + sub-1/NaN/negative fallback, computeLineAtrTrueRanges incl. **absolute period-over-period change** + index 0 null + direction-independent + non-array + single-element, computeLineAtr incl. **the Wilder fixture [null,null,10,10,15,12.5]** + simple-mean seed + Wilder smoothing after the seed + shorter-than-period+1 -> all null + non-array + pre-window nulls + sub-1 period clamp + **non-negative ATR**, runLineAtr incl. ok flags + fewer-than-2 / empty -> not ok + true range series + ATR series + atrFinal + atrMax + one sample per point + true range / ATR carried onto samples + **sorts unsorted input by x** + default period, computeLineAtrLayout incl. ok / not-ok guards + **price panel stacked above the ATR panel** + period + atrFinal/atrMax + total points + **atrYMax = larger of peak ATR and peak true range (20)** + one price dot per point + ATR marker only where ATR defined (4) + true-range bar only where true range defined (5) + **bottom-anchored bars to the ATR panel baseline** + non-empty price + ATR paths + markers inside the ATR panel + degenerate canvas -> not ok, describeLineAtrChart incl. mentions Average True Range + volatility + period + peak ATR + No data, component render incl. root region / data-empty false + true / data-period / ARIA desc / price path / ATR line / one ATR marker per defined value (4) / one true-range bar per defined value (5) / both panel labels / config badge period + peak / hide badge / three legend items / legend toggle hides a series + drops the ATR line / onSeriesToggle payload / hide ATR via showAtr / hide true-range bars via showTrueRange / price dots via showDots / **tooltip on ATR marker hover shows atr 15 + true range 20** / clears tooltip on leave / onPointClick / fade-in class + omit via animate / ref / custom class / region role / final ATR in the legend stats) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. | 11.580 | UI: chart-line-obv primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.598 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-obv.tsx` ships `<ChartLineObv>` -- pure-SVG line chart with an **On-Balance Volume (OBV) cumulative sub-panel** computed from paired price and volume series. The price line occupies the top panel; the bottom panel plots the OBV cumulative line with a dashed **zero baseline** and **direction-coloured markers** (up green / down red / flat grey). Both panels share one x-axis. **Indicator math:** `computeLineObvDirections(prices)` is the per-period close direction -- index 0 has no prior close and reads `flat`; later indices read `up` / `down` / `flat` from the sign of `price[i] - price[i-1]`. `computeLineObv(prices, volumes)` is **Joe Granville's On-Balance Volume**, a running cumulative total of volume: `obv[0] = 0`; each later period adds that period's volume on an up close (`obv[i] = obv[i-1] + volume[i]`), subtracts it on a down close (`obv[i] = obv[i-1] - volume[i]`), and leaves the running total unchanged on a flat close (`obv[i] = obv[i-1]`). The OBV is defined at every index -- there is no warm-up window. **Worked fixture:** `prices = [10,13,11,14,12,9]`, `volumes = [100,200,150,400,250,300]` -> `obv[0]=0`, `obv[1]=0+200=200` (13>10 up), `obv[2]=200-150=50` (11<13 down), `obv[3]=50+400=450` (14>11 up), `obv[4]=450-250=200` (12<14 down), `obv[5]=200-300=-100` (9<12 down) -> obv `[0,200,50,450,200,-100]`, directions `[flat,up,down,up,down,down]`, obvFinal -100, obvMin -100, obvMax 450 (the line crosses zero). **Distinct from chart-line-vwap:** VWAP is a volume-weighted average **price** -- a price-domain line (`cumulative price*volume / cumulative volume`) overlaid on the price. chart-line-obv is a **volume-domain momentum line**: it signs each period's volume by the price-change direction and accumulates, producing a running total in volume units in its own sub-panel. **Distinct from chart-line-cumulative:** the generic cumulative chart is a running sum of a **single** series; OBV is a **coupled two-series** accumulation (the volume series is signed by the price series' direction before being summed). **Distinct from chart-line-macd/-rsi/-stochastic/-atr:** those sub-panels plot momentum / range-position / volatility statistics; chart-line-obv plots a cumulative signed-volume total. API: `data: readonly ChartLineObvPoint[]` (`{x, price, volume}`); sizing `width`/`height`/`padding`/`gap`/`pricePanelRatio`/`tickCount`; styling `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`priceColor`/`obvColor`/`upColor`/`downColor`/`flatColor`/`zeroColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots`/`showObv`/`showZeroLine`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled series visibility (`hiddenSeries`/`defaultHiddenSeries`/`onHiddenSeriesChange`); `formatValue`/`formatX`; `onPointClick({point})`; `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`; standard a11y props. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_OBV_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PRICE_COLOR/OBV_COLOR/UP_COLOR/DOWN_COLOR/FLAT_COLOR/ZERO_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineObvDirection` type (`up`|`down`|`flat`); `getLineObvFinitePoints` (drops non-finite x/price/volume); `computeLineObvDirections(prices)`; `computeLineObv(prices, volumes)`; `runLineObv(points)` -- sorts finite points by x, computes OBV + directions, returns `{series, obv, directions, samples, obvFinal, obvMin, obvMax, ok}`; ok=false for fewer than 2 points; `computeLineObvLayout(options)` -- stacks a price panel above an OBV panel sharing one x-axis; the OBV panel y-range spans 0 through the OBV extent so the dashed zero baseline is always placed; each OBV marker carries its close direction for colour coding; returns panels, ticks, the price path, OBV path, OBV markers, and the projected zero-baseline y; `describeLineObvChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentioning On-Balance Volume + cumulative + final OBV + range (No data when fewer than 2 points). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-obv-aria-desc"` span; price path + price dots `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; OBV markers `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}` with the OBV value + direction in the aria-label. data-section on every node (chart-line-obv / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -zero-line / -axes / -axis-group / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -panel-labels / -panel-label / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -obv-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-price / -tooltip-volume / -tooltip-obv / -tooltip-direction / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-final / -badge-peak); root mirrors `data-obv-final`, `data-obv-min`, `data-obv-max`, `data-total-points`, `data-empty`, `data-animate`; OBV markers expose `data-obv` + `data-direction`. Tooltip on price dots and OBV markers shows x, the bold price, volume, OBV, and the close direction for the hovered period. Config badge in top-left calls out `OBV final=<obvFinal> peak=<obvMax>`. Two-series legend (Price / OBV) with per-series toggle plus a `final OBV <value>` stat. 83 vitest cases (getLineObvFinitePoints incl. null/undefined + non-finite x/price/volume drops, computeLineObvDirections incl. index 0 flat + up + down + unchanged-as-flat + the fixture directions + non-array, computeLineObv incl. **the cumulative fixture [0,200,50,450,200,-100]** + starts at zero + **adds volume on an up close** + **subtracts volume on a down close** + **unchanged on a flat close** + non-array price / volume guards + empty + single-element + **crosses zero from positive to negative**, runLineObv incl. ok flags + fewer-than-2 / empty -> not ok + OBV series + direction series + obvFinal + obvMin/obvMax + one sample per point + obv/volume/direction carried onto samples + first sample at zero + **sorts unsorted input by x before accumulating** + drops non-finite points, computeLineObvLayout incl. ok / not-ok guards + **price panel stacked above the OBV panel** + obvFinal + obvMin/obvMax + total points + one price dot per point + one OBV marker per point + **direction carried onto each marker** + **zero baseline placed inside the OBV panel** + non-empty price + OBV paths + markers inside the OBV panel + degenerate canvas -> not ok, describeLineObvChart incl. mentions On-Balance Volume + cumulative + final OBV + range + period count + No data, component render incl. root region / data-empty false + true / data-obv-final / ARIA desc / price path / OBV line / one OBV marker per point (6) / **each marker tagged with its direction** / zero baseline + hide via showZeroLine / both panel labels / config badge final + peak + hide / two legend items / legend toggle hides the OBV series + drops the line / onSeriesToggle payload / hide OBV line + markers via showObv / price dots via showDots / **tooltip on OBV marker hover shows obv 450 + volume 400 + direction up** / clears tooltip on leave / onPointClick / fade-in class + omit via animate / ref / custom class / region role / final OBV in the legend stats / obv-min + obv-max data attributes) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. | 11.581 | UI: chart-line-adx primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.599 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-adx.tsx` ships `<ChartLineAdx>` -- pure-SVG line chart with an **Average Directional Index (ADX) panel** rendering trend-strength alongside the plus and minus directional indicators. The value line occupies the top panel; the bottom panel renders the **ADX** trend-strength line alongside the **+DI** and **-DI** directional indicators, with a dashed reference line at the conventional trend threshold (default 25). Both panels share one x-axis. **Indicator math:** `computeLineAdxDirectionalMovement(values)` is **Wilder's directional movement** for a single-value series -- for each period the change `value[i] - value[i-1]` splits into a plus directional movement `plusDM = change > 0 ? change : 0`, a minus directional movement `minusDM = change < 0 ? -change : 0`, and a true range `trueRange = |change|`; index 0 has no prior value and reads null for all three; plus and minus movement are mutually exclusive and together partition the true range. `computeLineAdx(values, period)` runs **Welles Wilder's full ADX system**: the three directional-movement series are Wilder-smoothed over `period` (simple-mean seed at index `period`, then `(prev*(period-1) + x)/period`); then `+DI = 100 * smoothedPlusDM / smoothedTR`, `-DI = 100 * smoothedMinusDM / smoothedTR`, `DX = 100 * |+DI - -DI| / (+DI + -DI)`, and `ADX = Wilder-smoothed DX`. +DI / -DI / DX are defined from index `period`; the ADX, after a second smoothing window over DX, from index `2*period - 1`. For a single-value series `+DI + -DI = 100` exactly, since each period's plus and minus movement partition the true range. **Worked fixture:** `values = [10,12,11,14,13,16]`, `period 2` -> change/+DM/-DM/TR `i1 +2/2/0/2, i2 -1/0/1/1, i3 +3/3/0/3, i4 -1/0/1/1, i5 +3/3/0/3`; Wilder-smoothed (seed at index 2) `sPlusDM [.,.,1,2,1,2]`, `sMinusDM [.,.,0.5,0.25,0.625,0.3125]`, `sTR [.,.,1.5,2.25,1.625,2.3125]`; `+DI [.,.,66.6667,88.8889,61.5385,86.4865]`, `-DI [.,.,33.3333,11.1111,38.4615,13.5135]`, `DX [.,.,33.3333,77.7778,23.0769,72.9730]`, `ADX [.,.,.,55.5556,39.3162,56.1446]`; adxFinal 56.1446, plusDiFinal 86.4865, minusDiFinal 13.5135. **Distinct from chart-line-atr:** chart-line-atr plots the Wilder ATR alone as a volatility study; chart-line-adx uses the true range only as the **denominator** that normalizes the directional movements into a 0-100 trend-strength reading, and plots three derived lines (ADX, +DI, -DI), none of which is the ATR itself. **Distinct from chart-line-macd/-rsi/-stochastic:** those oscillators measure price **direction** and momentum; the ADX is **direction-agnostic** -- it measures the strength of a trend regardless of whether the trend is up or down, with the +DI/-DI lines carrying the direction separately. API: `data: readonly ChartLineAdxPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `period` (default 14, normalized to a positive integer); `threshold` (default 25, clamped 0-100); sizing `width`/`height`/`padding`/`gap`/`pricePanelRatio`/`tickCount`; styling `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`valueColor`/`adxColor`/`plusDiColor`/`minusDiColor`/`thresholdColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots`/`showAdx`/`showPlusDi`/`showMinusDi`/`showThreshold`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled series visibility (`hiddenSeries`/`defaultHiddenSeries`/`onHiddenSeriesChange`); `formatValue`/`formatX`; `onPointClick({point})`; `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`; standard a11y props. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_ADX_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/THRESHOLD/VALUE_COLOR/ADX_COLOR/PLUS_DI_COLOR/MINUS_DI_COLOR/THRESHOLD_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineAdxFinitePoints` (drops non-finite x/value); `normalizeLineAdxPeriod(period, fallback)` (non-finite or sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineAdxDirectionalMovement(values)` -> `{plusDM, minusDM, trueRange}`; `computeLineAdx(values, period)` -> `{plusDI, minusDI, dx, adx}`; `runLineAdx(points, options?)` -- sorts finite points by x, computes the four series, returns `{series, period, plusDI, minusDI, dx, adx, samples, adxFinal, plusDiFinal, minusDiFinal, ok}`; ok=false for fewer than 2 points; `computeLineAdxLayout(options)` -- stacks a value panel above a fixed 0-100 ADX panel sharing one x-axis, projects the three indicator lines, places the threshold reference line; returns panels, ticks, the value path, ADX/+DI/-DI paths, ADX markers (only where ADX is defined), and the projected threshold y; `describeLineAdxChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentioning Average Directional Index + trend strength + +DI + -DI + period + final ADX (No data when fewer than 2 points). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-adx-aria-desc"` span; value path + value dots `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; ADX markers `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}` with the ADX value in the aria-label. data-section on every node (chart-line-adx / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -threshold / -threshold-line / -threshold-label / -axes / -axis-group / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -panel-labels / -panel-label / -value-path / -dots / -dot / -plus-di-line / -minus-di-line / -adx-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-plus-di / -tooltip-minus-di / -tooltip-dx / -tooltip-adx / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-period / -badge-final); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-adx-final`, `data-plus-di-final`, `data-minus-di-final`, `data-total-points`, `data-empty`, `data-animate`. Tooltip on value dots and ADX markers shows x, the bold value, +DI, -DI, DX, and ADX for the hovered period (n/a where a series is not yet defined). Config badge in top-left calls out `ADX p=<period> ADX=<finalADX>`. Four-series legend (Value / ADX / +DI / -DI) with per-series toggle plus a `final ADX <value>` stat. 93 vitest cases (getLineAdxFinitePoints incl. null/undefined + non-finite x/value drops, normalizeLineAdxPeriod incl. floor + sub-1/NaN/negative fallback, computeLineAdxDirectionalMovement incl. **plus / minus movement split** + absolute true range + index 0 null + flat-series zero movement + non-array + **plus and minus mutually exclusive**, computeLineAdx incl. **the +DI / -DI / DX / ADX fixture series** + null before the smoothing window + ADX null until the second window + **the +DI + -DI = 100 invariant** + shorter-than-period+1 -> all null + non-array + **flat series produces no NaN** + ADX bounded 0-100 + sub-1 period clamp, runLineAdx incl. ok flags + fewer-than-2 / empty -> not ok + ADX series + adxFinal + plusDiFinal/minusDiFinal + one sample per point + metrics carried onto samples + **sorts unsorted input by x** + default period + **ok with NaN ADX when too short for ADX**, computeLineAdxLayout incl. ok / not-ok guards + **value panel stacked above the ADX panel** + period + final ADX/+DI/-DI + total points + one value dot per point + ADX marker only where ADX defined (3) + non-empty ADX/+DI/-DI paths + **threshold line inside the ADX panel** + default threshold 25 + custom threshold + **fixed 0-100 ADX y-axis** + markers inside the ADX panel + degenerate canvas -> not ok, describeLineAdxChart incl. mentions Average Directional Index + trend strength + +DI/-DI + period + final ADX + No data, component render incl. root region / data-empty | 11.582 | UI: chart-line-supertrend primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.600 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-supertrend.tsx` ships `<ChartLineSupertrend>` -- pure-SVG line chart with a **SuperTrend ATR-based trailing-stop band that flips with trend direction**. The price line and the SuperTrend line share one panel; the SuperTrend renders as **colour-coded segments** -- green when it trails below price as a support line in an uptrend, red when it trails above price as a resistance line in a downtrend -- with amber **flip markers** at the bars where the trend reverses. **Indicator math:** `computeLineSupertrendTrueRanges(values)` is the absolute period-over-period change `|v[i] - v[i-1]|` (index 0 null); `computeLineSupertrendATR(values, period)` is Welles Wilder's ATR (simple-mean seed at index `period`, then Wilder smoothing `(prev*(period-1) + tr)/period`). `computeLineSupertrend(values, period, multiplier)` builds the indicator: the ATR-scaled basic bands `basicUpper = value + multiplier*ATR`, `basicLower = value - multiplier*ATR` feed the **final trailing bands** -- `finalUpper[i] = (basicUpper[i] < finalUpper[i-1] || value[i-1] > finalUpper[i-1]) ? basicUpper[i] : finalUpper[i-1]` (the upper band only ratchets down) and `finalLower[i] = (basicLower[i] > finalLower[i-1] || value[i-1] < finalLower[i-1]) ? basicLower[i] : finalLower[i-1]` (the lower band only ratchets up) unless price breaks through. The **SuperTrend line** is the final lower band in an uptrend (support below price) and the final upper band in a downtrend (resistance above price); the trend **flips side** whenever price crosses the active band. **Worked fixture:** `values = [10,11,13,12,9,8]`, `period 2`, `multiplier 1` -> true range `[.,1,2,1,3,1]`, ATR `[.,.,1.5,1.25,2.125,1.5625]`, basicUpper `[.,.,14.5,13.25,11.125,9.5625]`, basicLower `[.,.,11.5,10.75,6.875,6.4375]`, finalUpper `[.,.,14.5,13.25,11.125,9.5625]`, finalLower `[.,.,11.5,11.5,11.5,6.4375]`, supertrend `[.,.,11.5,11.5,11.125,9.5625]`, direction `[.,.,up,up,down,down]`, flip `[F,F,F,F,T,F]`; the trend opens up (value rises 11->13) and the line trails the lower band, then at index 4 price 9 breaks below the lower band 11.5, the trend flips to down and the line jumps to the upper band; supertrendFinal 9.5625, directionFinal down, flipCount 1. **Distinct from chart-line-keltner:** Keltner draws **two persistent** ATR bands around an EMA centre as a static channel; the SuperTrend is a **single line** that is only ever one band at a time and jumps sides at a flip. **Distinct from chart-line-parabolic-sar:** the Parabolic SAR trailing stop follows a **parabolic acceleration formula**; the SuperTrend stop is set by **ATR-scaled bands**. **Distinct from chart-line-atr:** chart-line-atr plots the **raw ATR** in a sub-panel; chart-line-supertrend uses the ATR only to size the trailing band and overlays the resulting line on the price. API: `data: readonly ChartLineSupertrendPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `period` (default 10, normalized to a positive integer); `multiplier` (default 3, normalized to a positive number -- fractional multipliers kept); sizing `width`/`height`/`padding`/`tickCount`; styling `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`priceColor`/`uptrendColor`/`downtrendColor`/`flipColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots`/`showSupertrend`/`showMarkers`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled series visibility (`hiddenSeries`/`defaultHiddenSeries`/`onHiddenSeriesChange`); `formatValue`/`formatX`; `onPointClick({point})`; `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`; standard a11y props. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_SUPERTREND_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/MULTIPLIER/PRICE_COLOR/UPTREND_COLOR/DOWNTREND_COLOR/FLIP_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineSupertrendDirection` type (`up`|`down`); `getLineSupertrendFinitePoints` (drops non-finite x/value); `normalizeLineSupertrendPeriod(period, fallback)`; `normalizeLineSupertrendMultiplier(multiplier, fallback)` (non-finite or non-positive falls back, fractional kept); `computeLineSupertrendTrueRanges(values)`; `computeLineSupertrendATR(values, period)`; `computeLineSupertrend(values, period, multiplier)` -> `{atr, basicUpper, basicLower, finalUpper, finalLower, supertrend, direction, flip}`; `runLineSupertrend(points, options?)` -- sorts finite points by x, computes the core, returns `{series, period, multiplier, samples, supertrendFinal, directionFinal, flipCount, ok}`; ok=false for fewer than 2 points; `computeLineSupertrendLayout(options)` -- projects the price line and splits the SuperTrend into one segment per contiguous trend run so each segment can be coloured by direction; emits per-point markers (flip points flagged); returns the panel, ticks, the price path, segments, and markers; `describeLineSupertrendChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentioning SuperTrend + ATR trailing-stop + period + multiplier + final trend + flip count (No data when fewer than 2 points). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-supertrend-aria-desc"` span; price path + price dots `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; SuperTrend markers `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}` with the value + direction + flip in the aria-label. data-section on every node (chart-line-supertrend / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick / -tick-label / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -segments / -segment / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-atr / -tooltip-supertrend / -tooltip-direction / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-period / -badge-mult); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-multiplier`, `data-supertrend-final`, `data-direction-final`, `data-flip-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-empty`, `data-animate`; segments expose `data-direction`; markers expose `data-supertrend`, `data-direction`, `data-flip`. Tooltip on price dots and SuperTrend markers shows x, the bold value, ATR, SuperTrend, and the trend direction for the hovered period. Config badge in top-left calls out `ST p=<period> m=<multiplier>`. Three-series legend (Price / Uptrend / Downtrend) with per-series toggle (uptrend/downtrend toggle the respective segments + markers) plus a `trend <dir>, <n> flip(s)` stat. 98 vitest cases | 11.583 | UI: chart-line-aroon primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.601 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-aroon.tsx` ships `<ChartLineAroon>` -- pure-SVG line chart with an **Aroon panel** plotting the **Aroon-up** and **Aroon-down** trend-age lines. The value line occupies the top panel; the bottom panel renders the two 0-100 Aroon lines with dashed reference levels at the conventional weak/strong trend bands (default 70 / 30). Both panels share one x-axis. **Indicator math:** `computeLineAroon(values, period)` is **Tushar Chande's Aroon** -- for each index from `period` onward the window of `period + 1` values ending at that index is scanned: `Aroon-up = 100 * (period - periodsSinceHigh) / period` and `Aroon-down = 100 * (period - periodsSinceLow) / period`, where `periodsSinceHigh` is the offset (0..period) of the window's maximum from the current bar and `periodsSinceLow` likewise from the minimum. A reading of 100 means the extreme is the current bar (a fresh high or low); 0 means the extreme is the oldest bar in the window. **On ties the most recent occurrence wins** (scan uses `>=` for the high and `<=` for the low), so a flat series reads both lines at 100. Indices before the window fills read null. The **Aroon oscillator** is `Aroon-up - Aroon-down`, ranging -100 to +100. **Worked fixture:** `values = [10,14,11,12,18,9,20,8]`, `period 4` -> `i=4` window `[10,14,11,12,18]` high@4 off 0 -> up 100, low@0 off 4 -> down 0; `i=5` window `[14,11,12,18,9]` high@4 off 1 -> up 75, low@5 off 0 -> down 100; `i=6` window `[11,12,18,9,20]` high@6 off 0 -> up 100, low@5 off 1 -> down 75; `i=7` window `[12,18,9,20,8]` high@6 off 1 -> up 75, low@7 off 0 -> down 100 -> aroonUp `[.,.,.,.,100,75,100,75]`, aroonDown `[.,.,.,.,0,100,75,100]`, oscillator `[.,.,.,.,100,-25,25,-25]`; aroonUpFinal 75, aroonDownFinal 100, oscillatorFinal -25. **Distinct from chart-line-adx:** the ADX measures trend **strength** from the magnitude of smoothed directional movement; Aroon measures trend **age** -- the count of periods since the window's extreme, a purely time-based reading that does not look at how far price moved. **Distinct from chart-line-stochastic:** the stochastic %K measures **where** the value sits within the high-low range (a position); Aroon measures **how long ago** the high or low occurred (an age) -- both are 0-100 and both involve window extremes, but one is a magnitude position and the other is an elapsed-time count. API: `data: readonly ChartLineAroonPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `period` (default 25, normalized to a positive integer); `upperLevel`/`lowerLevel` (default 70 / 30, clamped 0-100); sizing `width`/`height`/`padding`/`gap`/`pricePanelRatio`/`tickCount`; styling `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`valueColor`/`aroonUpColor`/`aroonDownColor`/`levelColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots`/`showAroonUp`/`showAroonDown`/`showLevels`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled series visibility (`hiddenSeries`/`defaultHiddenSeries`/`onHiddenSeriesChange`); `formatValue`/`formatX`; `onPointClick({point})`; `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`; standard a11y props. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_AROON_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/UPPER_LEVEL/LOWER_LEVEL/VALUE_COLOR/UP_COLOR/DOWN_COLOR/LEVEL_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineAroonFinitePoints` (drops non-finite x/value); `normalizeLineAroonPeriod(period, fallback)` (non-finite or sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineAroon(values, period)` -> `{aroonUp, aroonDown}`; `runLineAroon(points, options?)` -- sorts finite points by x, computes the Aroon series plus the oscillator, returns `{series, period, aroonUp, aroonDown, oscillator, samples, aroonUpFinal, aroonDownFinal, oscillatorFinal, ok}`; ok=false for fewer than 2 points; `computeLineAroonLayout(options)` -- stacks a value panel above a fixed 0-100 Aroon panel sharing one x-axis; returns panels, ticks, the value path, Aroon-up / Aroon-down paths, markers (only where Aroon is defined), and the two projected reference level lines; `describeLineAroonChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentioning Aroon + Aroon-up + Aroon-down + period + final readings (No data when fewer than 2 points). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-aroon-aria-desc"` span; value path + value dots `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; Aroon markers `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}` with the Aroon-up value in the aria-label. data-section on every node (chart-line-aroon / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -levels / -level / -level-line / -level-label / -axes / -axis-group / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -panel-labels / -panel-label / -value-path / -dots / -dot / -up-line / -down-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-aroon-up / -tooltip-aroon-down / -tooltip-oscillator / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-period / -badge-osc); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-aroon-up-final`, `data-aroon-down-final`, `data-oscillator-final`, `data-total-points`, `data-empty`, `data-animate`. Tooltip on value dots and Aroon markers shows x, the bold value, Aroon-up, Aroon-down, and the oscillator for the hovered period. Config badge in top-left calls out `AROON p=<period> osc=<finalOscillator>`. Three-series legend (Value / Aroon-up / Aroon-down) with per-series toggle plus an `up <up> / down <down>` stat. 86 vitest cases (getLineAroonFinitePoints incl. null/undefined + non-finite x/value drops, normalizeLineAroonPeriod incl. floor + sub-1/NaN/negative fallback, computeLineAroon incl. **the Aroon-up / Aroon-down fixture series** + reads 100 when the high is the current bar + reads 0 when the extreme is the oldest bar + **strictly rising -> up 100 / down 0** + **strictly falling -> up 0 / down 100** + **flat -> both 100 via most-recent tie** + null before the window + shorter-than-period+1 -> all null + non-array + 0-100 bound + sub-1 period clamp, runLineAroon incl. ok flags + fewer-than-2 / empty -> not ok + Aroon-up/down series + **oscillator as up minus down** | 11.584 | UI: chart-line-cci primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.602 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-cci.tsx` ships `<ChartLineCci>` -- pure-SVG line chart with a **Commodity Channel Index oscillator panel and shaded extreme zones**. The value line occupies the top panel; the bottom panel renders the CCI oscillator on a **symmetric y-axis** with a dashed zero centerline, dashed +/-100 level lines, and **shaded overbought/oversold zones** beyond those levels. Both panels share one x-axis. **Indicator math:** `computeLineCci(values, period)` is **Donald Lambert's Commodity Channel Index** -- for each index from `period - 1` onward the window of `period` values is reduced to `CCI = (value - SMA) / (0.015 * meanDeviation)`, where `SMA` is the window mean and `meanDeviation` is the mean of the absolute deviations from that mean. The 0.015 constant scales the result so roughly 70-80% of readings fall within +/-100. A flat window (zero mean deviation) reads 0. Indices before the window fills read null. **Worked fixture:** `values = [10,12,14,13,20,8,11]`, `period 4` -> `i=3` window `[10,12,14,13]` SMA 12.25 meanDev 1.25 -> `(13-12.25)/(0.015*1.25) = 0.75/0.01875 = 40`; `i=4` window `[12,14,13,20]` SMA 14.75 meanDev 2.625 -> `5.25/0.039375 = 133.3333`; `i=5` window `[14,13,20,8]` SMA 13.75 meanDev 3.25 -> `-5.75/0.04875 = -117.9487`; `i=6` window `[13,20,8,11]` SMA 13 meanDev 3.5 -> `-2/0.0525 = -38.0952` -> cci `[.,.,.,40,133.3333,-117.9487,-38.0952]`; cci[4] > +100 overbought, cci[5] < -100 oversold; overboughtCount 1, oversoldCount 1, cciFinal ~ -38.0952. **Distinct from chart-line-zscore:** the z-score standardizes a value against the rolling **standard deviation**; CCI normalizes against the **mean absolute deviation** and applies Lambert's 0.015 constant -- a different dispersion measure that yields the conventional +/-100 banding rather than a unit-variance score. **Distinct from chart-line-rsi / chart-line-stochastic:** those oscillators are bounded to 0-100; CCI is **unbounded** -- it can run well past +/-100, which is why the panel uses a dynamic symmetric y-bound and the extreme zones are shaded regions beyond the +/-100 level lines rather than fixed bands. API: `data: readonly ChartLineCciPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `period` (default 20, normalized to a positive integer); `overbought`/`oversold` (default 100 / -100); sizing `width`/`height`/`padding`/`gap`/`pricePanelRatio`/`tickCount`; styling `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`valueColor`/`cciColor`/`overboughtColor`/`oversoldColor`/`zeroColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots`/`showCci`/`showZones`/`showZeroLine`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled series visibility (`hiddenSeries`/`defaultHiddenSeries`/`onHiddenSeriesChange`); `formatValue`/`formatX`; `onPointClick({point})`; `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`; standard a11y props. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_CCI_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/CONSTANT/OVERBOUGHT/OVERSOLD/VALUE_COLOR/CCI_COLOR/OVERBOUGHT_COLOR/OVERSOLD_COLOR/ZERO_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineCciZone` type (`overbought`|`oversold`|`neutral`); `getLineCciFinitePoints` (drops non-finite x/value); `normalizeLineCciPeriod(period, fallback)` (non-finite or sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineCci(values, period, constant?)` -> CCI series; `runLineCci(points, options?)` -- sorts finite points by x, computes the CCI series, classifies each reading as overbought (above +100) / oversold (below -100) / neutral, returns `{series, period, overbought, oversold, cci, samples, cciFinal, cciMin, cciMax, overboughtCount, oversoldCount, ok}`; ok=false for fewer than 2 points; `computeLineCciLayout(options)` -- stacks a value panel above a CCI panel with a symmetric y-bound (the larger of the +/-100 band and the data extent) so the unbounded oscillator and its zero centerline always fit; returns panels, ticks, the value path, CCI path, zone-tagged markers, the zero / level positions, and the two shaded zone rectangles; `describeLineCciChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentioning Commodity Channel Index + oscillator + overbought + oversold + period + extreme counts (No data when fewer than 2 points). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-cci-aria-desc"` span; value path + value dots `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; CCI markers `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}` with the CCI value + zone in the aria-label. data-section on every node (chart-line-cci / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -zones / -zone / -grid / -grid-line / -levels / -level / -level-line / -level-label / -zero-line / -axes / -axis-group / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -panel-labels / -panel-label / -value-path / -dots / -dot / -cci-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-cci / -tooltip-zone / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-period / -badge-extremes); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-cci-final`, `data-overbought-count`, `data-oversold-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-empty`, `data-animate`; markers expose `data-cci` + `data-zone`; zone rects expose `data-zone`. Tooltip on value dots and CCI markers shows x, the bold value, the CCI, and the zone for the hovered period. Config badge in top-left calls out `CCI p=<period> ext=<overboughtCount+oversoldCount>`. Two-series legend (Value / CCI) with per-series toggle plus an `<n> overbought, <m> oversold` stat. 87 vitest cases (getLineCciFinitePoints incl. null/undefined + non-finite x/value drops, normalizeLineCciPeriod incl. floor + sub-1/NaN/negative fallback, computeLineCci incl. **the first CCI from the simple mean and mean deviation (40)** + **overbought CCI above +100 (133.3333)** + **oversold CCI below -100 (-117.9487)** + final CCI + null before the window + **flat window -> 0 via the zero-deviation guard** + shorter-than-period -> all null + non-array + defined from the period-th index + sub-1 period clamp + **can exceed the +/-100 band (unbounded)**, runLineCci incl. ok flags + fewer-than-2 / empty -> not ok + CCI series + final CCI + **overbought / oversold / neutral zone classification** + extreme counts + one sample per point + **sorts unsorted input by x** + default period + **custom overbought/oversold thresholds**, computeLineCciLayout incl. ok / not-ok guards + **value panel stacked above the CCI panel** + period + final CCI + extreme counts + total points + one value dot per point + marker only where CCI defined (4) + non-empty CCI path + **symmetric y-bound = largest CCI magnitude** + **default thresholds +100 / -100** + **overbought level placed above the oversold level** + **extreme zones with positive height** + zero line inside the CCI panel + degenerate canvas -> not ok, describeLineCciChart incl. mentions Commodity Channel Index + oscillator + overbought/oversold + period + extreme counts + No data, component render incl. | 11.585 | UI: chart-line-williams-r primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.603 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-williams-r.tsx` ships `<ChartLineWilliamsR>` -- pure-SVG line chart with a **Williams %R momentum oscillator panel**. The value line occupies the top panel; the bottom panel renders the %R oscillator on a **0 to -100 scale** (0 at the top, -100 at the bottom) with dashed -20/-80 level lines and **shaded overbought/oversold zones**. Both panels share one x-axis. **Indicator math:** `computeLineWilliamsR(values, period)` is **Larry Williams' Percent Range** -- for each index from `period - 1` onward the window of `period` values yields `%R = -100 * (highestHigh - value) / (highestHigh - lowestLow)`. The result runs 0 when the value sits at the window high down to -100 when it sits at the window low. A flat window (zero range) reads -50, the neutral middle. Indices before the window fills read null. **Worked fixture:** `values = [10,14,12,18,8,16]`, `period 3` -> `i=2` window `[10,14,12]` hh 14 ll 10 -> `-100*(14-12)/4 = -50`; `i=3` window `[14,12,18]` hh 18 ll 12 -> `-100*(18-18)/6 = 0`; `i=4` window `[12,18,8]` hh 18 ll 8 -> `-100*(18-8)/10 = -100`; `i=5` window `[18,8,16]` hh 18 ll 8 -> `-100*(18-16)/10 = -20` -> williamsR `[.,.,-50,0,-100,-20]`; %R[3]=0 (value at the window high) overbought, %R[4]=-100 (value at the window low) oversold; williamsRFinal -20, overboughtCount 1, oversoldCount 1. **Distinct from chart-line-stochastic:** although %R is algebraically `%K - 100`, the stochastic plots **two** bounded 0-100 lines (%K and a smoothed %D signal) and reads a high %K as overbought; Williams %R is a **single** line on a **negative 0 to -100 scale** with the orientation **inverted** -- a %R near 0 is overbought, near -100 is oversold -- and carries no signal line; the %R numerator is measured from the window **high** (`highestHigh - value`) rather than the window low, and the panel y-axis runs 0 at the top down to -100. API: `data: readonly ChartLineWilliamsRPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `period` (default 14, normalized to a positive integer); `overbought`/`oversold` (default -20 / -80); sizing `width`/`height`/`padding`/`gap`/`pricePanelRatio`/`tickCount`; styling `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`valueColor`/`wrColor`/`overboughtColor`/`oversoldColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots`/`showWilliamsR`/`showZones`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled series visibility (`hiddenSeries`/`defaultHiddenSeries`/`onHiddenSeriesChange`); `formatValue`/`formatX`; `onPointClick({point})`; `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`; standard a11y props. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_WILLIAMS_R_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/OVERBOUGHT/OVERSOLD/VALUE_COLOR/WR_COLOR/OVERBOUGHT_COLOR/OVERSOLD_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineWilliamsRZone` type (`overbought`|`oversold`|`neutral`); `getLineWilliamsRFinitePoints` (drops non-finite x/value); `normalizeLineWilliamsRPeriod(period, fallback)` (non-finite or sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineWilliamsR(values, period)` -> %R series (negative-zero normalized to +0); `runLineWilliamsR(points, options?)` -- sorts finite points by x, computes the %R series, classifies each reading as overbought (above -20) / oversold (below -80) / neutral, returns `{series, period, overbought, oversold, williamsR, samples, williamsRFinal, overboughtCount, oversoldCount, ok}`; ok=false for fewer than 2 points; `computeLineWilliamsRLayout(options)` -- stacks a value panel above a fixed 0 to -100 %R panel; returns panels, ticks, the value path, %R path, zone-tagged markers, the level positions, and the two shaded zone rectangles; `describeLineWilliamsRChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentioning Williams %R + oscillator + overbought + oversold + period + extreme counts (No data when fewer than 2 points). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-williams-r-aria-desc"` span; value path + value dots `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; %R markers `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}` with the %R value + zone in the aria-label. data-section on every node (chart-line-williams-r / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -zones / -zone / -grid / -grid-line / -levels / -level / -level-line / -level-label / -axes / -axis-group / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -panel-labels / -panel-label / -value-path / -dots / -dot / -wr-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-wr / -tooltip-zone / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-period / -badge-extremes); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-williams-r-final`, `data-overbought-count`, `data-oversold-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-empty`, `data-animate`; markers expose `data-williams-r` + `data-zone`; zone rects expose `data-zone`. Tooltip on value dots and %R markers shows x, the bold value, the %R, and the zone for the hovered period. Config badge in top-left calls out `WR p=<period> ext=<overboughtCount+oversoldCount>`. Two-series legend (Value / %R) with per-series toggle plus an `<n> overbought, <m> oversold` stat. 85 vitest cases (getLineWilliamsRFinitePoints incl. null/undefined + non-finite x/value drops, normalizeLineWilliamsRPeriod incl. floor + sub-1/NaN/negative fallback, computeLineWilliamsR incl. **the %R fixture series** + **reads 0 when the value is at the window high** + **reads -100 when the value is at the window low** + null before the window + **flat window -> -50** + shorter-than-period -> all null + non-array + -100..0 bound + defined from the period-th index + sub-1 period clamp + mid-range proportional reading, runLineWilliamsR incl. ok flags + fewer-than-2 / empty -> not ok + %R series + final %R + **overbought / oversold / neutral zone classification** + extreme counts + one panel** + period + final %R + extreme counts + total points + one value dot per point + marker only where %R defined (4) + non-empty %R path + **0 to -100 %R y-axis** + **default thresholds -20 / -80** + **overbought level placed above the oversold level** + **extreme zones with positive height** + markers inside the %R panel + degenerate canvas -> not ok, describeLineWilliamsRChart incl. | 11.586 | UI: chart-line-trix primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.604 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-trix.tsx` ships `<ChartLineTrix>` -- pure-SVG line chart with a **TRIX triple-smoothed exponential moving average oscillator panel**. The value line occupies the top panel; the bottom panel renders the **TRIX line** and its **signal line** on a symmetric y-axis with a dashed zero centerline. Both panels share one x-axis. **Indicator math:** `computeLineTrixEma(values, period)` is an exponential moving average seeded with the first value, rolling forward with the smoothing factor `k = 2/(period+1)` -- `ema[i] = value[i]*k + ema[i-1]*(1-k)`. `computeLineTrix(values, period)` is **Jack Hutson's TRIX**: the series is smoothed by **three successive EMAs of the same period** (`ema1 = EMA(values)`, `ema2 = EMA(ema1)`, `ema3 = EMA(ema2)`), and TRIX is the 1-period **percentage rate of change** of that triple-smoothed line -- `TRIX[i] = 100 * (ema3[i] - ema3[i-1]) / ema3[i-1]`. Index 0 has no prior value and reads null; an all-zero series reads TRIX 0 without dividing by zero. `computeLineTrixSignal(trix, signalPeriod)` is an EMA of the TRIX series, seeded at the first defined TRIX value. **Worked fixture:** `values = [10,20,30,40,50,30,10]`, `period 3` (so `k = 0.5`) -> `ema1 = [10,15,22.5,31.25,40.625,35.3125,22.65625]`, `ema2 = [10,12.5,17.5,24.375,32.5,33.90625,28.28125]`, `ema3 = [10,11.25,14.375,19.375,25.9375,29.921875,29.1015625]`, `trix = [.,12.5,27.7778,34.7826,33.8710,15.3614,-2.7416]`; the triple-smoothed line peaks then turns down, so TRIX crosses from positive into negative at the final bar (trix[6] ~ -2.7416). **Distinct from chart-line-macd:** MACD is the difference of two EMAs of **different** periods; TRIX applies three EMAs of the **same** period and then takes a rate of change, so it filters short-term noise far more aggressively and reads as a smoothed momentum oscillator centred on zero. **Distinct from chart-line-ewma:** chart-line-ewma plots a single smoothing EMA overlaid on the data; chart-line-trix plots the percentage rate of change of a **triple**-smoothed EMA in a dedicated sub-panel -- a derived oscillator, not a smoothing overlay. API: `data: readonly ChartLineTrixPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `period` (default 15, normalized to a positive integer); `signalPeriod` (default 9); sizing `width`/`height`/`padding`/`gap`/`pricePanelRatio`/`tickCount`; styling `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`valueColor`/`trixColor`/`signalColor`/`zeroColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots`/`showTrix`/`showSignal`/`showZeroLine`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled series visibility (`hiddenSeries`/`defaultHiddenSeries`/`onHiddenSeriesChange`); `formatValue`/`formatX`; `onPointClick({point})`; `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`; standard a11y props. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_TRIX_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/SIGNAL_PERIOD/VALUE_COLOR/TRIX_COLOR/SIGNAL_COLOR/ZERO_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineTrixFinitePoints` (drops non-finite x/value); `normalizeLineTrixPeriod(period, fallback)` (non-finite or sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineTrixEma(values, period)` -> seeded EMA; `computeLineTrix(values, period)` -> `{ema1, ema2, ema3, trix}`; `computeLineTrixSignal(trix, signalPeriod)` -> signal EMA; `runLineTrix(points, options?)` -- sorts finite points by x, computes the TRIX and signal series, returns `{series, period, signalPeriod, ema3, trix, signal, samples, trixFinal, signalFinal, trixMin, trixMax, ok}`; ok=false for fewer than 2 points; `computeLineTrixLayout(options)` -- stacks a value panel above a TRIX panel with a symmetric y-bound covering the TRIX and signal extent so the zero centerline sits in the middle; returns panels, ticks, the value path, TRIX path, signal path, markers, and the zero centerline position; `describeLineTrixChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentioning TRIX + triple-smoothed + EMA + oscillator + period (No data when fewer than 2 points). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-trix-aria-desc"` span; value path + value dots `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; TRIX markers `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}` with the TRIX value in the aria-label. data-section on every node (chart-line-trix / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -zero-line / -axes / -axis-group / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -panel-labels / -panel-label / -value-path / -dots / -dot / -signal-line / -trix-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-ema3 / -tooltip-trix / -tooltip-signal / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-period / -badge-signal); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-signal-period`, `data-trix-final`, `data-signal-final`, `data-total-points`, `data-empty`, `data-animate`; markers expose `data-trix`. Tooltip on value dots and TRIX markers shows x, the bold value, ema3, TRIX, and the signal for the hovered period. Config badge in top-left calls out `TRIX p=<period> s=<signalPeriod>`. Three-series legend (Value / TRIX / Signal) with per-series toggle plus a `final TRIX <value>` stat. 92 vitest cases (getLineTrixFinitePoints incl. null/undefined + non-finite x/value drops, | 11.587 | UI: chart-line-mfi primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.605 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-mfi.tsx` ships `<ChartLineMfi>` -- pure-SVG line chart with a **Money Flow Index panel computed from paired price and volume series**. The price line occupies the top panel; the bottom panel renders the MFI oscillator on a **0-100 scale** with dashed 80/20 level lines and **shaded overbought/oversold zones**. Both panels share one x-axis. **Indicator math:** `computeLineMfi(prices, volumes, period)` is the **Money Flow Index**, a volume-weighted momentum oscillator. Each period's **raw money flow** is `price * volume`; the flow counts as **positive** when the price rose from the prior period and **negative** when it fell. Over a trailing window of `period` flows, `MFI = 100 * positiveFlow / (positiveFlow + negativeFlow)`. A window with no directional flow reads 50. MFI is defined from index `period` onward; earlier indices read null. **Worked fixture:** `prices = [10,20,10,20,10,20]`, `volumes = [100,400,100,50,800,50]`, `period 3` -> rawFlow `price*volume = [1000,8000,1000,1000,8000,1000]`, posMF `[0,8000,0,1000,0,1000]`, negMF `[0,0,1000,0,8000,0]`; `i=3` window 1..3 pos 9000 neg 1000 -> `100*9000/10000 = 90`; `i=4` window 2..4 pos 1000 neg 9000 -> `100*1000/10000 = 10`; `i=5` window 3..5 pos 2000 neg 8000 -> `100*2000/10000 = 20` -> mfi `[.,.,.,90,10,20]`; mfi[3]=90 overbought, mfi[4]=10 oversold; overboughtCount 1, oversoldCount 1, mfiFinal 20. **Distinct from chart-line-rsi:** RSI measures momentum from price changes **alone**; MFI **weights each move by the money flow (price times volume)** behind it, so a move on heavy volume shifts the oscillator far more than the same move on thin volume -- the MFI conventional zones are 80/20 rather than RSI's 70/30. **Distinct from chart-line-obv:** OBV is an **unbounded cumulative** signed-volume line with no window; MFI is a **bounded 0-100 windowed** oscillator. **Distinct from chart-line-vwap:** VWAP is a price-domain volume-weighted average overlaid on the price; MFI is a momentum oscillator in a dedicated sub-panel, not a price overlay. API: `data: readonly ChartLineMfiPoint[]` (`{x, price, volume}`); `period` (default 14, normalized to a positive integer); `overbought`/`oversold` (default 80 / 20); sizing `width`/`height`/`padding`/`gap`/`pricePanelRatio`/`tickCount`; styling `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`valueColor`/`mfiColor`/`overboughtColor`/`oversoldColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots`/`showMfi`/`showZones`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled series visibility (`hiddenSeries`/`defaultHiddenSeries`/`onHiddenSeriesChange`); `formatValue`/`formatX`; `onPointClick({point})`; `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`; standard a11y props. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_MFI_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/OVERBOUGHT/OVERSOLD/VALUE_COLOR/MFI_COLOR/OVERBOUGHT_COLOR/OVERSOLD_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineMfiZone` type (`overbought`|`oversold`|`neutral`); `getLineMfiFinitePoints` (drops non-finite x/price/volume); `normalizeLineMfiPeriod(period, fallback)` (non-finite or sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineMfi(prices, volumes, period)` -> MFI series; `runLineMfi(points, options?)` -- sorts finite points by x, computes the MFI series, classifies each reading as overbought (above 80) / oversold (below 20) / neutral, returns `{series, period, overbought, oversold, mfi, samples, mfiFinal, overboughtCount, oversoldCount, ok}`; ok=false for fewer than 2 points; `computeLineMfiLayout(options)` -- stacks a price panel above a fixed 0-100 MFI panel; returns panels, ticks, the price path, MFI path, zone-tagged markers, the level positions, and the two shaded zone rectangles; `describeLineMfiChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentioning Money Flow Index + volume + overbought + oversold + period + extreme counts (No data when fewer than 2 points). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-mfi-aria-desc"` span; price path + price dots `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; MFI markers `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}` with the MFI value + zone in the aria-label. data-section on every node (chart-line-mfi / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -zones / -zone / -grid / -grid-line / -levels / -level / -level-line / -level-label / -axes / -axis-group / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -panel-labels / -panel-label / -value-path / -dots / -dot / -mfi-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-price / -tooltip-volume / -tooltip-mfi / -tooltip-zone / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-period / -badge-extremes); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-mfi-final`, `data-overbought-count`, `data-oversold-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-empty`, `data-animate`; markers expose `data-mfi` + `data-zone`; zone rects expose `data-zone`. Tooltip on price dots and MFI markers shows x, the bold price, volume, the MFI, and the zone for the hovered period. Config badge in top-left calls out `MFI p=<period> ext=<overboughtCount+oversoldCount>`. Two-series legend (Price / MFI) with per-series toggle plus an `<n> overbought, <m> oversold` stat. 86 vitest cases (getLineMfiFinitePoints incl. null/undefined + non-finite x/price/volume drops, classification** + extreme counts + one sample per point + price/volume carried onto samples + **sorts unsorted input by x** + default period, computeLineMfiLayout incl. ok / not-ok guards + **price + **default thresholds 80 / 20** + **overbought level placed above the oversold level** + **extreme zones with positive height** + markers inside the MFI panel + degenerate canvas -> not ok, | 11.588 | UI: chart-line-vortex primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.606 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-vortex.tsx` ships `<ChartLineVortex>` -- pure-SVG line chart with a **Vortex Indicator panel plotting the positive and negative vortex movement lines**. The value line occupies the top panel; the bottom panel renders the **VI+** and **VI-** lines on a 0 to 1 scale with a dashed **0.5 crossover midline** and amber crossover markers. Both panels share one x-axis. **Indicator math:** `computeLineVortexMovement(values)` is the per-period **vortex movement** for a single-value series -- the positive vortex movement is the up-part of the period's change `vmPlus = max(value[i]-value[i-1], 0)`, the negative is the down-part `vmMinus = max(value[i-1]-value[i], 0)`, and the true range is their sum `trueRange = |change| = vmPlus + vmMinus`; index 0 reads null. `computeLineVortex(values, period)` is the **Vortex Indicator** -- over a trailing window of `period` movements, via **simple window sums**, `VI+ = sum(VM+) / sum(TR)` and `VI- = sum(VM-) / sum(TR)`. With the single-value adaptation `TR = VM+ + VM-` the two lines **sum to 1** and **cross at 0.5** as the trend turns; a flat window (zero true range) reads 0.5 for both. VI is defined from index `period` onward; earlier indices read null. **Worked fixture:** `values = [10,14,10,12,8,12]`, `period 3` -> vmPlus `[.,4,0,2,0,4]`, vmMinus `[.,0,4,0,4,0]`, trueRange `[.,4,4,2,4,4]`; `i=3` window 1..3 VI+ `6/10=0.6` VI- `4/10=0.4` (up); `i=4` window 2..4 VI+ `2/10=0.2` VI- `8/10=0.8` (down); `i=5` window 3..5 VI+ `6/10=0.6` VI- `4/10=0.4` (up) -> viPlus `[.,.,.,0.6,0.2,0.6]`, viMinus `[.,.,.,0.4,0.8,0.4]`; the trend runs up -> down -> up so VI+/VI- cross twice (crossUpCount 1, crossDownCount 1), trendFinal up. **Distinct from chart-line-adx:** ADX **Wilder-smooths** the directional movement and then derives a separate ADX trend-**strength** line; the Vortex Indicator uses plain window **sums** (no Wilder smoothing) and is just the two VI lines, read by their **crossover** rather than by a strength magnitude -- there is no third derived line. API: `data: readonly ChartLineVortexPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `period` (default 14, normalized to a positive integer); sizing `width`/`height`/`padding`/`gap`/`pricePanelRatio`/`tickCount`; styling `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`valueColor`/`viPlusColor`/`viMinusColor`/`midColor`/`crossColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots`/`showViPlus`/`showViMinus`/`showMidline`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled series visibility (`hiddenSeries`/`defaultHiddenSeries`/`onHiddenSeriesChange`); `formatValue`/`formatX`; `onPointClick({point})`; `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`; standard a11y props. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_VORTEX_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/VALUE_COLOR/VI_PLUS_COLOR/VI_MINUS_COLOR/MID_COLOR/CROSS_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineVortexTrend` type (`up`|`down`|`neutral`); `getLineVortexFinitePoints` (drops non-finite x/value); `normalizeLineVortexPeriod(period, fallback)` (non-finite or sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineVortexMovement(values)` -> `{vmPlus, vmMinus, trueRange}`; `computeLineVortex(values, period)` -> `{viPlus, viMinus}`; `runLineVortex(points, options?)` -- sorts finite points by x, computes the VI series, classifies each sample's trend (VI+ above VI- is an uptrend), detects bullish/bearish crossings, returns `{series, period, viPlus, viMinus, samples, viPlusFinal, viMinusFinal, trendFinal, crossUpCount, crossDownCount, ok}`; ok=false for fewer than 2 points; `computeLineVortexLayout(options)` -- stacks a value panel above a fixed 0 to 1 Vortex panel; returns panels, ticks, the value path, VI+ / VI- paths, markers (crossings flagged), and the midline y; `describeLineVortexChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentioning Vortex Indicator + VI+ + VI- + vortex movement + period + final trend + crossing counts (No data when fewer than 2 points).
