| 11.589 | UI: chart-line-dpo primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.607 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-dpo.tsx` ships `<ChartLineDpo>` -- pure-SVG line chart with a **Detrended Price Oscillator panel removing the long-term trend to expose cycles**. The value line occupies the top panel; the bottom panel renders the DPO on a **symmetric y-axis** with a dashed zero centerline and **sign-coloured markers**. Both panels share one x-axis. **Indicator math:** `computeLineDpoShift(period)` is the **DPO displacement**, `floor(period/2) + 1`. `computeLineDpo(values, period)` is the **Detrended Price Oscillator** -- for each index the simple moving average over `period` values is subtracted from the price `shift` bars earlier: `shift = floor(period/2)+1`, `SMA[i] = mean(values[i-period+1..i])`, `DPO[i] = value[i-shift] - SMA[i]`. The displaced subtraction removes the longer trend and leaves the shorter cycle oscillating around zero. Indices before both windows are available read null; a flat series reads 0 throughout. **Worked fixture:** `values = [20,12,16,16,8,8]`, `period 4` (so `shift 3`) -> `i=3` SMA mean(20,12,16,16)=16 -> `value[0]=20 - 16 = 4`; `i=4` SMA mean(12,16,16,8)=13 -> `value[1]=12 - 13 = -1`; `i=5` SMA mean(16,16,8,8)=12 -> `value[2]=16 - 12 = 4` -> dpo `[.,.,.,4,-1,4]`; positiveCount 2, negativeCount 1, dpoFinal 4. **Distinct from chart-line-trix / chart-line-macd:** those oscillators build on **exponential** moving averages (a triple-smoothed EMA rate of change; the difference of two EMAs); DPO subtracts a single **simple** moving average and, critically, **displaces** it backward by half the period -- that displacement is the defining trait, aligning the average with the cycle's natural lag so the trend cancels cleanly, which a non-displaced residual or moving-average difference does not do. DPO is explicitly **not** a momentum indicator -- it is a cycle-isolation tool. API: `data: readonly ChartLineDpoPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `period` (default 20, normalized to a positive integer); sizing `width`/`height`/`padding`/`gap`/`pricePanelRatio`/`tickCount`; styling `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`valueColor`/`dpoColor`/`positiveColor`/`negativeColor`/`zeroColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots`/`showDpo`/`showZeroLine`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled series visibility (`hiddenSeries`/`defaultHiddenSeries`/`onHiddenSeriesChange`); `formatValue`/`formatX`; `onPointClick({point})`; `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`; standard a11y props. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_DPO_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/VALUE_COLOR/DPO_COLOR/POSITIVE_COLOR/NEGATIVE_COLOR/ZERO_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineDpoSign` type (`positive`|`negative`|`zero`); `getLineDpoFinitePoints` (drops non-finite x/value); `normalizeLineDpoPeriod(period, fallback)` (non-finite or sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineDpoShift(period)` -> `floor(p/2)+1` with sub-1 clamp / fractional floor; `computeLineDpo(values, period)` -> DPO series; `runLineDpo(points, options?)` -- sorts finite points by x, computes the DPO series, classifies each reading's sign, returns `{series, period, shift, dpo, samples, dpoFinal, dpoMin, dpoMax, positiveCount, negativeCount, ok}`; ok=false for fewer than 2 points; `computeLineDpoLayout(options)` -- stacks a value panel above a DPO panel with a symmetric y-bound so the zero centerline sits in the middle; returns panels, ticks, the value path, DPO path, sign-tagged markers, and the zero centerline y; `describeLineDpoChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentioning Detrended Price Oscillator + displaced + moving average + cycles + period + zero-line counts (No data when fewer than 2 points). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-dpo-aria-desc"` span; value path + value dots `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; DPO markers `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}` with the DPO value + sign in the aria-label. data-section on every node (chart-line-dpo / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -zero-line / -axes / -axis-group / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -panel-labels / -panel-label / -value-path / -dots / -dot / -dpo-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-dpo / -tooltip-sign / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-period / -badge-shift); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-shift`, `data-dpo-final`, `data-positive-count`, `data-negative-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-empty`, `data-animate`; markers expose `data-dpo` + `data-sign`. Tooltip on value dots and DPO markers shows x, the bold value, the DPO, and the sign for the hovered period. Config badge in top-left calls out `DPO p=<period> d=<shift>`. Two-series legend (Value / DPO) with per-series toggle plus an `<n> above, <m> below` stat. 89 vitest cases (getLineDpoFinitePoints incl. null/undefined + non-finite x/value drops, normalizeLineDpoPeriod incl. floor + sub-1/NaN/negative fallback, computeLineDpoShift incl. **even period -> floor(p/2)+1** + default period 20 -> 11 + odd period + sub-1 clamp + fractional floor, x** + default period, computeLineDpoLayout incl. ok / not-ok guards + **value panel stacked above the DPO panel** + period + shift + final DPO + counts + total points + one value dot per point + | 11.590 | UI: chart-line-survival primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.608 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-survival.tsx` ships `<ChartLineSurvival>` -- pure-SVG **Kaplan-Meier survival curve with a descending step line and optional censoring ticks**. The curve plots the estimated survival probability against time on a single 0 to 1 panel, with a dashed median-survival reference line. **Indicator math:** `computeKaplanMeier(observations)` is the **Kaplan-Meier survival estimate** -- each observation is a `time` plus an `event` flag (true = the event occurred, false = censored); observations are grouped by distinct time, and at each time with `d` events out of `n` subjects at risk the survival estimate is multiplied by `(1 - d/n)`. **Censored** observations do not lower S but reduce the at-risk count `n` for later times, and are marked with ticks. The result is a descending step function. `computeLineSurvivalMedian(steps)` reports the earliest time the estimate falls to 0.5 or below (null if it never does). **Worked fixture:** 4 subjects `t=1 event`, `t=2 event`, `t=3 censored`, `t=4 event` -> `t=1` d=1 n=4 `S = 1*(1-1/4) = 0.75`; `t=2` d=1 n=3 `S = 0.75*(1-1/3) = 0.5`; `t=3` d=0 n=2 `S` unchanged 0.5 (one censoring tick); `t=4` d=1 n=1 `S = 0.5*(1-1/1) = 0` -> steps survival `[0.75,0.5,0.5,0]`; survivalFinal 0, eventCount 3, censorCount 1, medianSurvival 2 (the first step with S <= 0.5). **Distinct from every chart-line-* oscillator in the library:** this is a **biostatistics** chart, not a financial indicator -- its input is **time-to-event data carrying a censoring flag**, and its curve is a **right-continuous step function that only ever descends, never recovering**; the Kaplan-Meier estimator (the multiplicative survival estimate with explicit censoring handling) has no counterpart among the moving-average / momentum / volatility charts. API: `data: readonly ChartLineSurvivalObservation[]` (`{time, event}`); sizing `width`/`height`/`padding`/`tickCount`; styling `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`censorTickHalf`/`survivalColor`/`censorColor`/`medianColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showMarkers`/`showCensorTicks`/`showMedianLine`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled series visibility (`hiddenSeries`/`defaultHiddenSeries`/`onHiddenSeriesChange`); `formatValue`/`formatTime`; `onStepClick({step})`; `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`; standard a11y props. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_SURVIVAL_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/CENSOR_TICK_HALF/SURVIVAL_COLOR/CENSOR_COLOR/MEDIAN_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineSurvivalFiniteObservations` (drops non-finite time, coerces `event` to a strict boolean -- only `event === true` is an event); `computeLineSurvivalMedian(steps)` -> median survival time or null; `computeKaplanMeier(observations)` -> `{steps, censorTicks, total, survivalFinal, eventCount, censorCount, medianSurvival}` (sorts internally); `runLineSurvival(observations)` -- drops non-finite observations, sorts by time, computes the estimate, returns `{observations, steps, censorTicks, total, survivalFinal, eventCount, censorCount, medianSurvival, ok}`; ok=false for fewer than 2 observations; `computeLineSurvivalLayout(options)` -- builds the descending staircase path (flat between event times, dropping at each), the step markers, the censoring tick positions, and the projected median reference line; `describeLineSurvivalChart(data)` -- ARIA summary mentioning Kaplan-Meier + survival + step line + censored + event counts (No data when fewer than 2 observations). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-survival-aria-desc"` span; step path + step markers `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-survival / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -median-line / -axes / -axis / -tick / -tick-label / -step-path / -censor-ticks / -censor-tick / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-time / -tooltip-survival / -tooltip-at-risk / -tooltip-events / -tooltip-censored / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-subjects / -badge-median); root mirrors `data-total`, `data-event-count`, `data-censor-count`, `data-survival-final`, `data-median`, `data-empty`, `data-animate`; markers expose `data-time`, `data-survival`, `data-deaths`; censor ticks expose `data-time`, `data-count`. Tooltip on step markers shows the time, the bold survival, the at-risk count, the events, and the censored count for the hovered step. Config badge in top-left calls out `KM n=<total> median=<medianSurvival>`. Two-series legend (Survival / Censored) with per-series toggle plus an `<n> events, <m> censored` stat. 82 vitest cases (getLineSurvivalFiniteObservations incl. null/undefined + non-finite time drops + **strict-true event coercion** + non-true treated as censored, computeLineSurvivalMedian incl. **earliest time S drops to <= 0.5** + null when never reached + exact-0.5 time + empty + non-array, computeKaplanMeier incl. **the survival step values fixture [0.75,0.5,0.5,0]** + **at-risk counts before each time** + deaths per time + final survival + event/censored counts + total + **a censoring tick per censored time** + median survival + **no survival drop at a purely censored time** + **tied times grouped into one step dropping by the combined deaths** + **all-censored curve holds at 1** + **sorts unsorted observations** + non-array, runLineSurvival incl. ok flags + fewer-than-2 / empty -> not ok + survival steps + final survival + event/censored counts + median + total + **returns the sorted observations** + one censoring tick + drops non-finite observations, computeLineSurvivalLayout incl. ok / not-ok guards + total/event/censored counts + final survival + median + step count + **x-axis spans first to last event time** + non-empty step path + one step marker per step + one censor mark per censored time + **projected median x position** + **0 to 1 survival y-axis** + **descending markers in order down the panel** + markers inside the panel + **null median x when the curve never reaches 0.5** + degenerate canvas -> not ok, describeLineSurvivalChart incl. mentions Kaplan-Meier + survival step line + censored + event/censored counts + No data, component render incl. root region / data-empty false + true / data-total + data-event-count + data-censor-count / ARIA desc / step path / one step marker per step (4) / **one censoring tick per censored time (1)** + hide via showCensorTicks / **median line** + hide via showMedianLine / config badge subject count + median + hide / two legend items / legend toggle hides the survival step path / legend toggle hides the censoring ticks / onSeriesToggle payload / hide step markers via showMarkers / **tooltip on step-marker hover shows survival 0.75 + at risk 4** / clears tooltip on leave / onStepClick / fade-in class + omit via animate / ref / custom class / region role / event + censored counts in the legend stats) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. | 11.591 | UI: chart-line-cdf primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.609 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-cdf.tsx` ships `<ChartLineCdf>` -- pure-SVG **empirical cumulative distribution function (ECDF) curve rising from zero to one across the sorted series**. The x-axis is the sample value, the y-axis the cumulative probability 0 to 1, with a dashed median reference line. **Statistics:** `computeLineCdf(values)` is the **empirical cumulative distribution function** -- the finite sample values are sorted ascending and grouped by distinct value; each distinct value carries a `count` (its multiplicity), a `cumulative` count of sample values at or below it, and a `probability = cumulative / n`. The result is a **non-decreasing step function** whose last step always reaches 1. `computeLineCdfMedian(steps)` reports the smallest value at which the ECDF reaches 0.5 or more (null only for an empty step list, since a non-empty ECDF always reaches 1). **Worked fixture:** `values = [30,10,20,10,40]` -> sorted `[10,10,20,30,40]`, `n 5` -> value 10 count 2 cumulative 2 -> `probability 2/5 = 0.4`; value 20 count 1 cumulative 3 -> `0.6`; value 30 count 1 cumulative 4 -> `0.8`; value 40 count 1 cumulative 5 -> `1.0`; 4 distinct values, median 20 (the first probability >= 0.5). **Distinct from chart-line-survival and chart-line-distribution:** the survival curve **descends** from one to zero and handles **censored** observations; the ECDF **ascends** from zero to one and every observation contributes a plain `1/n` jump with no censoring. A distribution chart shows the **density**; the ECDF shows the **cumulative fraction** -- the proportion of the sample at or below any value, read straight off the curve. API: `data: readonly number[]` (a plain list of sample values -- the value itself is the x-axis); sizing `width`/`height`/`padding`/`tickCount`; styling `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`curveColor`/`medianColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showMarkers`/`showMedianLine`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled series visibility (`hiddenSeries`/`defaultHiddenSeries`/`onHiddenSeriesChange`); `formatValue`/`formatX`; `onStepClick({step})`; `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`; standard a11y props. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_CDF_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/CURVE_COLOR/MEDIAN_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineCdfFiniteValues` (keeps finite sample values, order preserved); `computeLineCdf(values)` -> ECDF step array (filters non-finite, sorts internally); `computeLineCdfMedian(steps)` -> empirical median value or null; `runLineCdf(values)` -- drops non-finite values, sorts ascending, computes the ECDF, returns `{values, n, steps, distinctCount, minValue, maxValue, median, ok}`; ok=false for fewer than 2 finite values; `computeLineCdfLayout(options)` -- builds the rising staircase path (flat between distinct values, jumping up at each), the step markers, and the projected median reference line; `describeLineCdfChart(data)` -- ARIA summary mentioning Empirical cumulative distribution function + step curve + rises from zero to one + distinct / observation counts (No data when fewer than 2 values). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-cdf-aria-desc"` span; step path + step markers `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-cdf / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -median-line / -axes / -axis / -tick / -tick-label / -step-path / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-probability / -tooltip-count / -tooltip-cumulative / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-n / -badge-median); root mirrors `data-n`, `data-distinct-count`, `data-median`, `data-min-value`, `data-max-value`, `data-empty`, `data-animate`; markers expose `data-value`, `data-probability`. Tooltip on step markers shows the value, the bold cumulative probability, the count at that value, and the cumulative count for the hovered step. Config badge in top-left calls out `ECDF n=<n> median=<median>`. | 11.592 | UI: chart-line-tsi primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.610 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-tsi.tsx` ships `<ChartLineTsi>` -- pure-SVG line chart with a **True Strength Index double-smoothed momentum oscillator panel**. The value line occupies the top panel; the bottom panel renders the **TSI** line and its **signal** line on a fixed **-100 to +100** scale with a dashed zero centerline and +25/-25 reference lines. **Indicator math:** `computeLineTsiMomentum(values)` is the per-period price momentum `value[i] - value[i-1]` and its absolute value (index 0 null). `computeLineTsiEma(src, period)` is an exponential moving average that tolerates leading nulls -- it seeds at the first defined value and rolls forward with `k = 2/(period+1)`. `computeLineTsi(values, longPeriod, shortPeriod)` is **William Blau's True Strength Index**: the price momentum is **double-smoothed** (an EMA of `longPeriod` then an EMA of `shortPeriod`) and the absolute momentum is double-smoothed the same way, then `TSI = 100 * doubleSmoothedMomentum / doubleSmoothedAbsMomentum`. Because the absolute momentum dominates the signed momentum the ratio is **bounded**, so TSI stays within -100 to +100; a flat series reads 0 without dividing by zero. **Worked fixture:** `values = [10,14,12,18,16,22]`, `long = short = 3` (so every EMA factor is `k = 0.5`) -> momentum `[.,4,-2,6,-2,6]`, ema1 `[.,4,1,3.5,0.75,3.375]`, ema2 `[.,4,2.5,3,1.875,2.625]` (double-smoothed momentum), ema2abs `[.,4,3.5,4,3.625,4.125]` (double-smoothed absolute momentum) -> tsi `[.,100,71.4286,75,51.7241,63.6364]`; a strictly rising series reads +100, a strictly falling series -100. **Distinct from chart-line-trix:** TRIX triple-smooths the **price** itself and then takes a **rate of change**, producing an **unbounded** oscillator; TSI double-smooths the **momentum** and **normalizes by the double-smoothed absolute momentum** -- that normalization step is what bounds TSI to -100..+100, a property TRIX does not have (TRIX is a smoothed ROC; TSI is a smoothed, normalized momentum ratio). API: `data: readonly ChartLineTsiPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `longPeriod` (default 25), `shortPeriod` (default 13), `signalPeriod` (default 13) -- all normalized to positive integers; `overbought`/`oversold` (default 25 / -25); sizing `width`/`height`/`padding`/`gap`/`pricePanelRatio`/`tickCount`; styling `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`valueColor`/`tsiColor`/`signalColor`/`zeroColor`/`levelColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots`/`showTsi`/`showSignal`/`showZeroLine`/`showLevels`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled series visibility (`hiddenSeries`/`defaultHiddenSeries`/`onHiddenSeriesChange`); `formatValue`/`formatX`; `onPointClick({point})`; `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`; standard a11y props. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_TSI_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/LONG_PERIOD/SHORT_PERIOD/SIGNAL_PERIOD/OVERBOUGHT/OVERSOLD/VALUE_COLOR/TSI_COLOR/SIGNAL_COLOR/ZERO_COLOR/LEVEL_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineTsiFinitePoints` (drops non-finite x/value); `normalizeLineTsiPeriod(period, fallback)`; `computeLineTsiMomentum(values)` -> `{momentum, absMomentum}`; `computeLineTsiEma(src, period)` -> leading-null-tolerant EMA; `computeLineTsi(values, longPeriod, shortPeriod)` -> TSI series; `runLineTsi(points, options?)` -- sorts finite points by x, computes the TSI and a signal EMA, returns `{series, longPeriod, shortPeriod, signalPeriod, tsi, signal, samples, tsiFinal, signalFinal, tsiMin, tsiMax, ok}`; ok=false for fewer than 2 points; `computeLineTsiLayout(options)` -- stacks a value panel above a fixed -100 to +100 TSI panel; returns panels, ticks, the value path, TSI path, signal path, markers, the zero centerline, and the two level positions; `describeLineTsiChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentioning True Strength Index + double-smoothed + momentum + oscillator + long period (No data when fewer than 2 points). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-tsi-aria-desc"` span; value path + value dots `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; TSI markers `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}` with the TSI value in the aria-label. data-section on every node (chart-line-tsi / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -levels / -level / -level-line / -level-label / -zero-line / -axes / -axis-group / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -panel-labels / -panel-label / -value-path / -dots / -dot / -signal-line / -tsi-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-tsi / -tooltip-signal / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-long / -badge-short); root mirrors `data-long-period`, `data-short-period`, `data-signal-period`, `data-tsi-final`, `data-signal-final`, `data-total-points`, `data-empty`, `data-animate`; markers expose `data-tsi`. Tooltip on value dots and TSI markers shows x, the bold value, the TSI, and the signal for the hovered period. Config badge in top-left calls out `TSI L=<longPeriod> S=<shortPeriod>`. | 11.593 | UI: chart-line-cmo primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.611 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-cmo.tsx` ships `<ChartLineCmo>` -- pure-SVG line chart with a **Chande Momentum Oscillator panel on a +/-100 scale**. The value line occupies the top panel; the bottom panel renders the CMO on a fixed **-100 to +100** scale with a dashed zero centerline, +50/-50 level lines, and shaded overbought/oversold zones. **Indicator math:** `computeLineCmoChanges(values)` splits each period's change into a **gain** (the positive part) and a **loss** (the magnitude of the negative part); index 0 reads null. `computeLineCmo(values, period)` is **Tushar Chande's Momentum Oscillator** -- over a trailing window of `period` changes the gains and losses are summed with a **plain sum (no Wilder smoothing)** and `CMO = 100 * (sumUp - sumDown) / (sumUp + sumDown)`. The result runs -100 (all losses) to +100 (all gains), centred on zero; a window with no movement reads 0. CMO is defined from index `period` onward. **Worked fixture:** `values = [10,18,17,18,10,21]`, `period 3` -> changes up/down `+8/0, -1/1, +1/0, -8/8, +11/0`; `i=3` window 1..3 up 9 down 1 -> `100*(9-1)/10 = 80`; `i=4` window 2..4 up 1 down 9 -> `100*(1-9)/10 = -80`; `i=5` window 3..5 up 12 down 8 -> `100*(12-8)/20 = 20` -> cmo `[.,.,.,80,-80,20]`; cmo[3]=80 overbought, cmo[4]=-80 oversold; overboughtCount 1, oversoldCount 1, cmoFinal 20. **Distinct from chart-line-rsi:** RSI uses **Wilder-smoothed** average gains and losses on a **0 to 100** scale centred on 50; CMO sums the **raw** gains and losses over the window with **no smoothing** and reports their net on a **-100 to +100** scale centred on zero. **Distinct from chart-line-tsi:** TSI double-smooths the momentum (two successive EMAs); CMO is the un-smoothed, single-window-sum momentum ratio `(sumUp - sumDown) / (sumUp + sumDown)`. API: `data: readonly ChartLineCmoPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `period` (default 14, normalized to a positive integer); `overbought`/`oversold` (default 50 / -50); sizing `width`/`height`/`padding`/`gap`/`pricePanelRatio`/`tickCount`; styling `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`valueColor`/`cmoColor`/`overboughtColor`/`oversoldColor`/`zeroColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots`/`showCmo`/`showZones`/`showZeroLine`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled series visibility (`hiddenSeries`/`defaultHiddenSeries`/`onHiddenSeriesChange`); `formatValue`/`formatX`; `onPointClick({point})`; `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`; standard a11y props. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_CMO_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/OVERBOUGHT/OVERSOLD/VALUE_COLOR/CMO_COLOR/OVERBOUGHT_COLOR/OVERSOLD_COLOR/ZERO_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineCmoZone` type (`overbought`|`oversold`|`neutral`); `getLineCmoFinitePoints` (drops non-finite x/value); `normalizeLineCmoPeriod(period, fallback)` (non-finite or sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineCmoChanges(values)` -> `{up, down}`; `computeLineCmo(values, period)` -> CMO series; `runLineCmo(points, options?)` -- sorts finite points by x, computes the CMO series, classifies each reading as overbought (above +50) / oversold (below -50) / neutral, returns `{series, period, overbought, oversold, cmo, samples, cmoFinal, overboughtCount, oversoldCount, ok}`; ok=false for fewer than 2 points; `computeLineCmoLayout(options)` -- stacks a value panel above a fixed -100 to +100 CMO panel; returns panels, ticks, the value path, CMO path, zone-tagged markers, the zero centerline, the level positions, and the two shaded zone rectangles; `describeLineCmoChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentioning Chande Momentum Oscillator + gains + losses + overbought + oversold + period + extreme counts (No data when fewer than 2 points). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-cmo-aria-desc"` span; value path + value dots `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; CMO markers `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}` with the CMO value + zone in the aria-label. data-section on every node (chart-line-cmo / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -zones / -zone / -grid / -grid-line / -levels / -level / -level-line / -level-label / -zero-line / -axes / -axis-group / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -panel-labels / -panel-label / -value-path / -dots / -dot / -cmo-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-cmo / -tooltip-zone / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-period / -badge-extremes); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-cmo-final`, `data-overbought-count`, `data-oversold-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-empty`, `data-animate`; markers expose `data-cmo` + `data-zone`; zone rects expose `data-zone`. | 11.594 | UI: chart-line-fisher primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.612 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-fisher.tsx` ships `<ChartLineFisher>` -- pure-SVG line chart with a **Fisher Transform panel sharpening turning points alongside its trigger line**. The value line occupies the top panel; the bottom panel renders the **Fisher** line and its **one-bar-lagged trigger** line on a symmetric y-axis with a dashed zero centerline. **Indicator math:** `computeLineFisher(values, period)` is **John Ehlers' Fisher Transform** -- for each index from `period - 1` onward the value is normalized to a -1..+1 position within the window's high-low range (`rawNorm = 2*(value-low)/(high-low) - 1`), recursively smoothed (`smoothed = 0.33*rawNorm + 0.67*prevSmoothed`, seeded at the first index), and clamped just inside +/-1; the Fisher line applies the **inverse-hyperbolic-tangent transform** `fisher = 0.5*ln((1+smoothed)/(1-smoothed)) + 0.5*prevFisher`. That arctanh term **steepens sharply as the smoothed value approaches +/-1**, so price turning points become pronounced peaks rather than gentle curves -- the "sharpening" the Fisher Transform is known for. The clamp keeps the log finite. The **trigger line is the Fisher lagged by one bar**, and its crossings flag the turns. **Worked fixture:** `values = [10,30,20,40,30,50]`, `period 3` -> `rawNorm [.,.,0,1,0,1]`, `smoothed [.,.,0,0.33,0.2211,0.478137]`, `fisher [.,.,0,0.342828,0.396226,0.718681]`, `trigger [.,.,.,0,0.342828,0.396226]` (Fisher lagged 1). **Distinct from chart-line-stochastic / chart-line-williams-r:** those oscillators report the **raw position** of the value within its high-low range; the Fisher Transform begins with that same range normalization but then applies the **arctanh transform** -- that non-linear step, absent from the plain range oscillators, is what sharpens the extremes (a small move near the range boundary produces a large Fisher move) and makes turning points stand out as spikes; the Fisher output is also unbounded and centred on zero, unlike the bounded range oscillators. API: `data: readonly ChartLineFisherPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `period` (default 9, normalized to a positive integer); sizing `width`/`height`/`padding`/`gap`/`pricePanelRatio`/`tickCount`; styling `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`valueColor`/`fisherColor`/`triggerColor`/`zeroColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots`/`showFisher`/`showTrigger`/`showZeroLine`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled series visibility (`hiddenSeries`/`defaultHiddenSeries`/`onHiddenSeriesChange`); `formatValue`/`formatX`; `onPointClick({point})`; `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`; standard a11y props. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_FISHER_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/VALUE_COLOR/FISHER_COLOR/TRIGGER_COLOR/ZERO_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineFisherFinitePoints` (drops non-finite x/value); `normalizeLineFisherPeriod(period, fallback)` (non-finite or sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineFisher(values, period)` -> `{fisher, trigger}`; `runLineFisher(points, options?)` -- sorts finite points by x, computes the Fisher and trigger series, returns `{series, period, fisher, trigger, samples, fisherFinal, triggerFinal, fisherMin, fisherMax, ok}`; ok=false for fewer than 2 points; `computeLineFisherLayout(options)` -- stacks a value panel above a Fisher panel with a symmetric y-bound so the zero centerline sits in the middle; returns panels, ticks, the value path, Fisher path, trigger path, markers, and the zero centerline y; `describeLineFisherChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentioning Fisher Transform + sharpens + turning points + trigger line + period (No data when fewer than 2 points). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-fisher-aria-desc"` span; value path + value dots `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; Fisher markers `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}` with the Fisher value in the aria-label. data-section on every node (chart-line-fisher / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -zero-line / -axes / -axis-group / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -panel-labels / -panel-label / -value-path / -dots / -dot / -trigger-line / -fisher-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-fisher / -tooltip-trigger / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-period); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-fisher-final`, `data-trigger-final`, `data-total-points`, `data-empty`, `data-animate`; markers expose `data-fisher`. Tooltip on value dots and Fisher markers shows x, the bold value, the Fisher, and the trigger for the hovered period. Config badge in top-left calls out `FISHER p=<period>`. Three-series legend (Value / Fisher / Trigger) with per-series toggle plus a `final Fisher <value>` stat. 84 vitest cases (getLineFisherFinitePoints incl. null/undefined + non-finite x/value drops, normalizeLineFisherPeriod incl. floor + sub-1/NaN/negative fallback, computeLineFisher incl. **Fisher 0 at the first index when the value is mid-range** + **the Fisher Transform fixture series** + | 11.595 | UI: chart-line-coppock primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.613 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-coppock.tsx` ships `<ChartLineCoppock>` -- pure-SVG line chart with a **Coppock Curve** long-term momentum panel. The value line sits in the TOP panel; the BOTTOM panel renders the single Coppock Curve line on a symmetric y-axis with a dashed **zero centerline** and sign-coloured markers (green above zero, red below). **The computation:** the Coppock Curve, devised by economist Edwin Coppock, is `WMA(wmaPeriod, ROC(roc1Period) + ROC(roc2Period))` with defaults roc1Period 14, roc2Period 11, wmaPeriod 10. `computeLineCoppockRoc(values, period)` is the percentage rate of change `ROC[i] = 100*(values[i]-base)/base` with `base = values[i-period]` -- indices before the lookback read `null`, a zero base reads `0`, and a negative-zero result (zero numerator over a negative base) is normalised to positive zero. `computeLineCoppockWma(src, period)` is a LINEARLY weighted moving average: the oldest window value carries weight 1 and the newest weight `period`, divided by the triangular weight sum `period*(period+1)/2`; any window touching a `null` reads `null`. `computeLineCoppock` sums the two ROC legs bar by bar (`null` where either leg is undefined) and smooths the running total with the WMA -- so the curve only begins once both ROC legs and a full WMA window are defined. The curve oscillates around zero; a cross UP through the zero line is the classic long-term momentum buy signal. **Worked fixture** (values [100,100,100,100,100,130,160], roc1Period 3, roc2Period 2, wmaPeriod 2): roc1 = [.,.,.,0,0,30,60], roc2 = [.,.,0,0,0,30,60], rocSum = [.,.,.,0,0,60,120], coppock = [.,.,.,.,0,40,100] -- coppockFinal 100, coppockMin 0, coppockMax 100, positiveCount 2, negativeCount 0. `runLineCoppock` sorts the finite points by x, computes the Coppock series, and classifies each sample with a `positive`/`negative`/`zero` sign. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** `<ChartLineRate>` plots a SINGLE raw ROC line straight off one lookback -- the Coppock Curve sums TWO ROC legs of different lookbacks and then WMA-smooths that total. `<ChartLineTrix>` is the rate of change of a TRIPLE exponentially smoothed average -- the Coppock Curve never triple-smooths and uses a WEIGHTED (linear-weight) MA, not an EMA, applied to a SUM of two ROCs; the two share no computation step. `<ChartLineMomentum>`/`<ChartLineCmo>`/`<ChartLineTsi>` are single-stage momentum measures; the Coppock Curve is uniquely the WMA-smoothed sum of two ROC legs. All exported helpers carry a `LineCoppock`/`Coppock` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineCoppockPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `roc1Period` / `roc2Period` / `wmaPeriod` (the indicator periods; defaults 14 / 11 / 10); `pricePanelRatio` (the fraction of the height given to the value panel; default 0.56, clamped to [0.3, 0.8]); `gap` (the pixel gap between the stacked panels); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `valueColor` / `coppockColor` / `positiveColor` / `negativeColor` / `zeroColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showCoppock`/`showZeroLine`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `value` / `coppock`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_COPPOCK_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/ROC1_PERIOD/ROC2_PERIOD/WMA_PERIOD/VALUE_COLOR/COPPOCK_COLOR/POSITIVE_COLOR/NEGATIVE_COLOR/ZERO_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineCoppockFinitePoints`; `normalizeLineCoppockPeriod(period, fallback)`; `computeLineCoppockRoc(values, period)` -- the rate-of-change series (`(number|null)[]`); `computeLineCoppockWma(src, period)` -- the linearly weighted MA series; `computeLineCoppock(values, roc1Period, roc2Period, wmaPeriod)` -- the full Coppock series; `runLineCoppock(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, roc1Period, roc2Period, wmaPeriod, coppock, samples, coppockFinal, coppockMin, coppockMax, positiveCount, negativeCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, coppock, sign}`; `computeLineCoppockLayout({data, ...})` -- stacks the value panel above the Coppock panel, derives a symmetric y-bound around zero; projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `coppockPath`, `markers`, the `zeroY` centerline; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 samples -> ok=false; `describeLineCoppockChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Coppock Curve + momentum + rate-of-change + weighted moving average + zero (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-coppock-aria-desc"` span; the value line + every value dot + every Coppock marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-coppock / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -zero-line / -axes / -axis-group / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -panel-labels / -panel-label / -value-path / -dots / -dot / -coppock-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-coppock / -tooltip-sign / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-roc / -badge-wma); root mirrors `data-roc1-period`, `data-roc2-period`, `data-wma-period`, `data-coppock-final`, `data-positive-count`, `data-negative-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; axis / tick / grid / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"coppock"`; Coppock markers expose `data-coppock` and `data-sign`. Tooltip on a value dot or a Coppock marker shows x, value, the Coppock reading and the sign. Config badge in top-left calls out `COPPOCK r=<roc1>/<roc2> w=<wma>`. The legend has two toggle items (Value / Coppock). 58 vitest cases (computeLineCoppockRoc incl. **the percentage rate of change** + **leaves indices before the lookback null** + **reads zero when the base is zero** + **normalises a negative-zero ROC to positive zero** + ROC of zero when unchanged + empty -> [], computeLineCoppockWma incl. **weights the most recent value most heavily** + **divides by the triangular weight total** + a flat average when window values are equal + **leaves a window containing a null undefined** + empty -> [], computeLineCoppock incl. **matches the hand-verified pipeline** + array as long as the input + empty -> [], normalizeLineCoppockPeriod incl. positive integer + floors a fractional + **sub-1 falls back** + non-finite falls back, getLineCoppockFinitePoints incl. keeps only finite x + value + non-array -> [], runLineCoppock incl. ok + **the hand-verified Coppock series** + **final/min/max readings** + **counts readings above + below zero** + **classifies each sample sign** + **flags a negative reading on a falling series** + **sorts the series by x** + single point / empty -> ok=false + resolves the default periods + one sample per point, computeLineCoppockLayout incl. ok layout + **stacks the value panel above the Coppock panel** + value + Coppock paths + **places the zero line inside the Coppock panel** + **one marker per defined reading** + **a symmetric y-bound around zero** + carries the run stats + collapsed dims -> ok=false + too little data -> ok=false, describeLineCoppockChart incl. **the indicator vocabulary** + the positive + negative counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / root data-* / svg + value + Coppock paths / **one marker per defined reading** / config badge ROC + WMA periods / both panel labels / legend two items / hide the value path / hide the Coppock line / empty state / omit config badge / ref / displayName / animate flag) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for the new files (conditional spreads honour `exactOptionalPropertyTypes`). Exported via components/ui barrel. Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/c4-mgr-auto. | 11.596 | UI: chart-line-ppo primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.614 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-ppo.tsx` ships `<ChartLinePpo>` -- pure-SVG line chart with a **Percentage Price Oscillator (PPO)** panel rendering the PPO line, the signal line, and the histogram. The value line sits in the TOP panel; the BOTTOM panel renders the PPO line, a dashed **signal** line, and sign-coloured **histogram** bars anchored on a dashed **zero centerline**. **The computation:** the PPO is the percentage-normalised analog of MACD. `computeLinePpoEma(src, period)` is an EMA that tolerates leading `null` placeholders (the PPO line has them) -- it collects the defined values in order, seeds with the SIMPLE MEAN of the first `period` of them placed at that value's index, then folds each later defined value in at weight `2/(period+1)`; indices before the seed read `null`. `computeLinePpo(values, fastPeriod, slowPeriod, signalPeriod)` runs that EMA at a fast and a slow period (defaults 12 / 26), takes the PPO line as `100*(fastEMA - slowEMA)/slowEMA`, runs the EMA again over the PPO line for the signal line (default 9), and takes the histogram as `PPO - signal`. A zero slow EMA reads PPO 0; a negative-zero PPO is normalised to positive zero. **Worked fixture** (values [4,4,4,12,12,12,12,12], fastPeriod 1, slowPeriod 3, signalPeriod 3): fastEMA = the series itself = [4,4,4,12,12,12,12,12]; slowEMA (mult 0.5, SMA seed at i=2) = [.,.,4,8,10,11,11.5,11.75]; PPO = [.,.,0,50,20,100/11,50/11.5,25/11.75]; signal seeds at the 3rd defined PPO = mean(0,50,20) = 70/3; histogram = PPO - signal, defined from index 4 (all negative here), histogram[4] = 20 - 70/3 = -10/3. ppoMin 0, ppoMax 50, positiveCount 0, negativeCount 4. `runLinePpo` sorts the finite points by x, computes the three series, and classifies each sample with a `positive`/`negative`/`zero` HISTOGRAM sign. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** `<ChartLineMacd>` plots the RAW difference of two EMAs (`fastEMA - slowEMA`) in ABSOLUTE price units -- the PPO divides that same difference by the slow EMA and scales by 100, so the reading is a PERCENTAGE comparable across instruments at different price levels and across time as one instrument's price drifts; the signal line and histogram share MACD's shape but are built on the percentage PPO line, not the absolute MACD line. `<ChartLineCoppock>`/`<ChartLineTrix>` smooth ROC legs; the PPO is a ratio of two EMAs of the price itself. All exported helpers carry a `LinePpo`/`Ppo` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLinePpoPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `fastPeriod` / `slowPeriod` / `signalPeriod` (the EMA periods; defaults 12 / 26 / 9); `pricePanelRatio` (the fraction of the height given to the value panel; default 0.56, clamped to [0.3, 0.8]); `gap` (the pixel gap between the stacked panels); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `valueColor` / `ppoColor` / `signalColor` / `positiveColor` / `negativeColor` / `zeroColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showPpo`/`showSignal`/`showHistogram`/`showZeroLine`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `value` / `ppo` / `signal` / `histogram`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_PPO_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/FAST_PERIOD/SLOW_PERIOD/SIGNAL_PERIOD/VALUE_COLOR/PPO_COLOR/SIGNAL_COLOR/POSITIVE_COLOR/NEGATIVE_COLOR/ZERO_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLinePpoFinitePoints`; `normalizeLinePpoPeriod(period, fallback)`; `computeLinePpoEma(src, period)` -- the null-tolerant EMA series (`(number|null)[]`); `computeLinePpo(values, fastPeriod, slowPeriod, signalPeriod)` -- returns `{ppo, signal, histogram}`; `runLinePpo(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, fastPeriod, slowPeriod, signalPeriod, ppo, signal, histogram, samples, ppoFinal, signalFinal, histogramFinal, ppoMin, ppoMax, positiveCount, negativeCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, ppo, signal, histogram, sign}`; `computeLinePpoLayout({data, ...})` -- stacks the value panel above the PPO panel, derives a symmetric y-bound covering the PPO + signal + histogram readings; projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `ppoPath`, `signalPath`, `histogramBars` (zero-anchored), `markers`, the `zeroY` centerline; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 samples -> ok=false; `describeLinePpoChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Percentage Price Oscillator + PPO + signal + histogram + percentage + exponential moving average + zero (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-ppo-aria-desc"` span; the value line + the PPO line + every value dot + every PPO marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-ppo / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -zero-line / -axes / -axis-group / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -panel-labels / -panel-label / -value-path / -dots / -dot / -histogram / -histogram-bar / -ppo-line / -signal-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-ppo / -tooltip-signal / -tooltip-histogram / -tooltip-sign / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-ema / -badge-signal); root mirrors `data-fast-period`, `data-slow-period`, `data-signal-period`, `data-ppo-final`, `data-histogram-final`, `data-positive-count`, `data-negative-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; axis / tick / grid / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"ppo"`; histogram bars expose `data-histogram` and `data-sign`; PPO markers expose `data-ppo` and `data-sign`. Tooltip on a value dot or a PPO marker shows x, value, ppo, signal, histogram and the sign. Config badge in top-left calls out `PPO <fast>/<slow> s=<signal>`. The legend has four toggle items (Value / PPO / Signal / Histogram). 59 vitest cases (computeLinePpoEma incl. **seeds with the simple mean then folds in later values** + **the period-length mean as the seed** + **reproduces the series for a period of one** + **skips leading null placeholders before seeding** + **all-null when fewer defined values than the period** + non-array -> [], computeLinePpo incl. **matches the hand-verified pipeline** + **seeds the signal line from the simple mean** + **derives the histogram as PPO minus signal** + **all-zero oscillator for a flat series** + non-array -> empty arrays, normalizeLinePpoPeriod incl. positive integer + floors a fractional + **sub-1 falls back** + non-finite falls back, getLinePpoFinitePoints incl. keeps only finite x + value + non-array -> [], runLinePpo incl. ok + **the three series** + **final readings + PPO range** + **counts histogram readings above + below zero** + **classifies each sample by histogram sign** + **positive PPO for a rising series** + **negative PPO for a falling series** + **sorts by x** + single point / empty -> ok=false + resolves the default periods + one sample per point, computeLinePpoLayout incl. ok + **stacks the value panel above the PPO panel** + value + PPO + signal paths + **one histogram bar per defined reading** + **one marker per defined PPO reading** + **places the zero line inside the PPO panel** + **a symmetric y-bound covering the widest reading** + **anchors each histogram bar on the zero line** + collapsed dims -> ok=false + too little data -> ok=false, describeLinePpoChart incl. **the indicator vocabulary** + the histogram counts + the EMA periods + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / root data-* / svg + value + PPO + signal lines / **one histogram bar per defined reading** / **one marker per defined PPO reading** / config badge EMA + signal periods / both panel labels / four-item legend / hide | 11.597 | UI: chart-line-mass-index primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.615 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-mass-index.tsx` ships `<ChartLineMassIndex>` -- pure-SVG line chart with a **Mass Index** panel detecting reversals from range-expansion bulges. The TOP panel renders the **high** and **low** lines with a shaded **band** between them; the BOTTOM panel renders the Mass Index line with dashed **setup** and **trigger** threshold lines and markers coloured by reversal **phase**. **Unlike every other chart-line-* primitive, this one consumes `{x, high, low}` points** -- the Mass Index is intrinsically a high-low RANGE study. **The computation:** Donald Dorsey's Mass Index. `computeLineMassIndexEma(src, period)` is an EMA that tolerates leading `null` placeholders (the single-EMA series has them) -- it collects the defined values, seeds with the SIMPLE MEAN of the first `period` of them placed at that value's index, then folds each later value in at weight `2/(period+1)`. `computeLineMassIndex(highs, lows, emaPeriod, sumPeriod)` takes `range[i] = high[i] - low[i]`, smooths it by a SINGLE EMA (default period 9), smooths that single EMA again into a DOUBLE EMA, takes `ratio[i] = single[i]/double[i]`, and sums the ratio over the SUM PERIOD (default 25) for the Mass Index. A widening range pushes the single EMA above its own smoothed double EMA, so the ratio climbs above one and the running sum bulges. `runLineMassIndex` walks the reversal-bulge state machine: a reading at or above the **setup** threshold (default 27) arms a bulge (`bulge` phase), a later reading below the **trigger** threshold (default 26.5) while armed fires a reversal (`signal` phase, `bulgeCount`++), every other reading is `calm`. **Worked fixture** (low 0 throughout, high = range [6,6,6,6,6,18,18,18,18,18], emaPeriod 2, sumPeriod 3): single (EMA mult 2/3) = [.,6,6,6,6,14,50/3,158/9,482/27,1454/81]; double = [.,.,6,6,6,34/3,134/9,50/3,1414/81,4322/243]; ratio = [.,.,1,1,1,21/17,75/67,79/75,723/707,2181/2161]; massIndex = [.,.,.,.,3,55/17,3.354697,3.408030,3.195367,3.085219]. With setup 3.4 / trigger 3.3 the Mass Index crosses above 3.4 at index 7 (bulge) then drops below 3.3 at index 8 (signal) -- one reversal bulge; massIndexMin 3, massIndexMax 3.40803. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** the Mass Index is the ONLY chart-line primitive computed from a high-low range rather than a single value series. `<ChartLineBollinger>`/`<ChartLineKeltner>`/`<ChartLineAtr>` measure volatility as a band width or an average true range -- the Mass Index instead takes the RATIO of a single to a DOUBLE EMA of the range and SUMS it over a window; it is a reversal detector built on the curvature of range smoothing, and its setup/trigger bulge state machine is unique to it. All exported helpers carry a `LineMassIndex`/`MassIndex` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineMassIndexPoint[]` (`{x, high, low}`); `emaPeriod` / `sumPeriod` (the smoothing and sum periods; defaults 9 / 25); `setupThreshold` / `triggerThreshold` (the bulge thresholds; defaults 27 / 26.5); `rangePanelRatio` (the fraction of the height given to the range panel; default 0.5, clamped to [0.3, 0.8]); `gap` (the pixel gap between the stacked panels); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `highColor` / `lowColor` / `bandColor` / `massColor` / `setupColor` / `triggerColor` / `bulgeColor` / `signalColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showBand`/`showMass`/`showThresholds`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `high` / `low` / `mass`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_MASS_INDEX_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/RANGE_PANEL_RATIO/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/EMA_PERIOD/SUM_PERIOD/SETUP_THRESHOLD/TRIGGER_THRESHOLD/HIGH_COLOR/LOW_COLOR/BAND_COLOR/MASS_COLOR/SETUP_COLOR/TRIGGER_COLOR/BULGE_COLOR/SIGNAL_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineMassIndexFinitePoints`; `normalizeLineMassIndexPeriod(period, fallback)`; `computeLineMassIndexEma(src, period)` -- the null-tolerant EMA series (`(number|null)[]`); `computeLineMassIndex(highs, lows, emaPeriod, sumPeriod)` -- returns `{range, single, double, ratio, massIndex}`; `runLineMassIndex(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, emaPeriod, sumPeriod, setupThreshold, triggerThreshold, range, single, double, ratio, massIndex, samples, massIndexFinal, massIndexMin, massIndexMax, bulgeCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, high, low, range, massIndex, phase}`; `computeLineMassIndexLayout({data, ...})` -- stacks the range panel above the Mass Index panel, fits the Mass Index y-domain around both threshold lines; projects `highPath`, `lowPath`, `bandPath` (closed), `massPath`, `rangeDots`, `markers`, the `setupY` / `triggerY` threshold-line positions; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 samples -> ok=false; `describeLineMassIndexChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Mass Index + reversal + bulge + range + exponential moving average (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-mass-index-aria-desc"` span; the high line + the low line + the Mass Index line + every dot + every marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-mass-index / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -thresholds / -threshold-line / -axes / -axis-group / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -panel-labels / -panel-label / -band / -high-path / -low-path / -dots / -dot / -mass-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-high / -tooltip-low / -tooltip-range / -tooltip-mass / -tooltip-phase / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-ema / -badge-sum); root mirrors `data-ema-period`, `data-sum-period`, `data-setup-threshold`, `data-trigger-threshold`, `data-mass-index-final`, `data-bulge-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; axis / tick / grid / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="range"|"mass"`; threshold lines carry `data-kind` (setup / trigger); dots carry `data-kind` (high / low); markers expose `data-mass-index` and `data-phase`. Tooltip on a dot or a marker shows x, high, low, range, mass and the phase. Config badge in top-left calls out `MASS e=<ema> s=<sum>`. The legend has three toggle items (High / Low / Mass Index). 60 vitest cases (getLineMassIndexFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + high + low** + non-array -> [], normalizeLineMassIndexPeriod incl. positive integer + floors a fractional + **sub-1 falls back** + non-finite falls back, computeLineMassIndexEma incl. **seeds with the simple mean then folds in later values** + **the period-length mean as the seed** + **the period-one identity** + **skips leading null placeholders** + **all-null when fewer defined values than the period** + non-array -> [], computeLineMassIndex incl. **takes the range as high minus low** + **smooths into a single then a double EMA** + **takes the ratio of the single to the double EMA** + **sums the ratio over the sum period** + **a flat Mass Index for a constant range** + non-array -> empty arrays, runLineMassIndex incl. ok + **the Mass Index series** + **final/min/max readings** + **detects the reversal bulge** + **classifies the bulge + signal bars by phase** + **no bulge when the setup threshold is never reached** + **sorts by x** + single point / empty -> ok=false + resolves the default periods + thresholds + one sample per point, computeLineMassIndexLayout incl. ok + **stacks the range panel above the Mass Index panel** + high + low + band + Mass paths + **one marker per defined reading** + **both threshold lines inside the | 11.598 | UI: chart-line-force-index primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.616 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-force-index.tsx` ships `<ChartLineForceIndex>` -- pure-SVG line chart with an **Elder Force Index** panel combining price change and volume. The TOP panel renders the price line; the BOTTOM panel renders the smoothed Force Index line and a faint **raw** Force Index line on a symmetric y-axis with a dashed **zero centerline** and sign-coloured markers (green above zero, red below). **Like chart-line-mass-index, this primitive consumes a multi-field point -- here `{x, price, volume}`** -- since the Force Index is intrinsically a price-and-volume study. **The computation:** Alexander Elder's Force Index. `computeLineForceIndexRaw(prices, volumes)` is the raw Force Index `(price[i] - price[i-1]) * volume[i]` -- index 0 has no prior price so it reads `null`; a negative-zero raw force (a zero price change times a negative volume) is normalised to positive zero. `computeLineForceIndexEma(src, period)` is an EMA that tolerates the leading `null` of the raw series -- it seeds with the SIMPLE MEAN of the first `period` defined values placed at that value's index, then folds each later value in at weight `2/(period+1)`. `computeLineForceIndex(prices, volumes, emaPeriod)` smooths the raw series with that EMA (default 13-period); the smoothed Force Index measures the power behind a move -- it swings around zero, positive when buyers dominate and negative when sellers do. **Worked fixture** (prices [10,12,16,16,14,14], volumes all 100, emaPeriod 2): rawForce = (priceChange)*volume = [null,200,400,0,-200,0]; forceIndex = EMA(rawForce, 2) (mult 2/3, SMA seed at index 2) = [null,null,300,100,-100,-100/3]. forceFinal -100/3, rawFinal 0, forceMin -100, forceMax 300, positiveCount 2, negativeCount 2. `runLineForceIndex` sorts the finite points by x, computes the raw and smoothed series, and classifies each sample with a `positive`/`negative`/`zero` sign from the smoothed Force Index. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** `<ChartLineObv>` (On-Balance Volume) is a CUMULATIVE running total -- it adds the whole volume on up bars and subtracts it on down bars, so its magnitude grows without bound and it weights only the DIRECTION of each move; the Force Index is NOT cumulative and weights by the EXTENT of each price change, then EMA-smooths the result. `<ChartLineMfi>` (Money Flow Index) is a BOUNDED 0-100 volume-weighted oscillator (a ratio of positive to negative money flow); the Force Index is UNBOUNDED and centred on zero. `<ChartLinePpo>`/`<ChartLineCoppock>` are price-only momentum studies; the Force Index is the volume-weighted member of the family -- only it multiplies the price change by volume. All exported helpers carry a `LineForceIndex`/`ForceIndex` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineForceIndexPoint[]` (`{x, price, volume}`); `emaPeriod` (the smoothing period; default 13); `pricePanelRatio` (the fraction of the height given to the price panel; default 0.54, clamped to [0.3, 0.8]); `gap` (the pixel gap between the stacked panels); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `priceColor` / `forceColor` / `rawColor` / `positiveColor` / `negativeColor` / `zeroColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showForce`/`showRaw`/`showZeroLine`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `force` / `raw`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_FORCE_INDEX_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/EMA_PERIOD/PRICE_COLOR/FORCE_COLOR/RAW_COLOR/POSITIVE_COLOR/NEGATIVE_COLOR/ZERO_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineForceIndexFinitePoints`; `normalizeLineForceIndexPeriod(period, fallback)`; `computeLineForceIndexEma(src, period)` -- the null-tolerant EMA series (`(number|null)[]`); `computeLineForceIndexRaw(prices, volumes)` -- the raw Force Index series; `computeLineForceIndex(prices, volumes, emaPeriod)` -- returns `{rawForce, forceIndex}`; `runLineForceIndex(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, emaPeriod, rawForce, forceIndex, samples, forceFinal, rawFinal, forceMin, forceMax, positiveCount, negativeCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, price, volume, rawForce, forceIndex, sign}`; `computeLineForceIndexLayout({data, ...})` -- stacks the price panel above the Force Index panel, derives a symmetric y-bound covering the raw + smoothed readings; projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `rawPath`, `forcePath`, `markers`, the `zeroY` centerline; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 samples -> ok=false; `describeLineForceIndexChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Elder Force Index + Force Index + volume + price change + exponential moving average + power + zero (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-force-index-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the Force Index line + every price dot + every marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-force-index / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -zero-line / -axes / -axis-group / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -panel-labels / -panel-label / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -raw-line / -force-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-price / -tooltip-volume / -tooltip-raw / -tooltip-force / -tooltip-sign / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-ema); root mirrors `data-ema-period`, `data-force-final`, `data-raw-final`, `data-positive-count`, `data-negative-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; axis / tick / grid / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"force"`; markers expose `data-force` and `data-sign`. Tooltip on a price dot or a marker shows x, price, volume, raw, force and the sign. Config badge in top-left calls out `FORCE ema=<emaPeriod>`. The legend has three toggle items (Price / Force Index / Raw Force). 55 vitest cases (getLineForceIndexFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + price + volume** + non-array -> [], normalizeLineForceIndexPeriod incl. positive integer + floors a fractional + **sub-1 falls back** + non-finite falls back, computeLineForceIndexEma incl. **seeds with the simple mean then folds in later values** + **the period-one identity** + **skips leading null placeholders** + **all-null when fewer defined values than the period** + non-array -> [], computeLineForceIndexRaw incl. **multiplies the price change by the volume** + **negative raw force on a price drop** + **normalises a negative-zero raw force to positive zero** + non-array -> [], computeLineForceIndex incl. **matches the hand-verified raw Force Index** + **smooths the raw series into the Force Index line**, runLineForceIndex incl. ok + **the raw + smoothed series** + **final/min/max readings** + **counts readings above + below zero** + **classifies each sample by sign** + **sorts by x** + single point / empty -> ok=false + resolves the default EMA period + one sample per point, computeLineForceIndexLayout incl. ok + **stacks the price panel above the Force Index panel** + price + raw + Force paths + **one marker per defined reading** + **places the zero line inside the Force Index panel** + **a symmetric y-bound covering the widest raw + smoothed reading** + carries the run stats + collapsed dims -> ok=false + too little data -> ok=false, describeLineForceIndexChart incl. **the indicator vocabulary** + the EMA period + the reading counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / root data-* / svg + price + raw + Force lines / **one marker per defined reading** / config badge EMA period / both panel labels / three-item legend / hide the price path / hide the Force line / hide the raw Force line / empty state / omit config badge / ref / displayName / animate flag) all pass under vitest 4.1.5; the recursive EMA readings are asserted with toBeCloseTo (only the SMA seed is bit-exact). TypeScript clean for the new files (conditional spreads honour `exactOptionalPropertyTypes`). Exported via components/ui barrel. Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/c4-mgr-auto. | 11.599 | UI: chart-line-emv primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.617 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-emv.tsx` ships `<ChartLineEmv>` -- pure-SVG line chart with an **Ease of Movement (EMV)** panel relating price change to volume. The TOP panel renders the **midpoint** line with a shaded **high-low band** behind it; the BOTTOM panel renders the Ease of Movement line on a symmetric y-axis with a dashed **zero centerline** and sign-coloured markers (green above zero, red below). **This primitive consumes the richest point shape of the chart-line family -- `{x, high, low, volume}`** -- since the Ease of Movement combines the bar range, the midpoint move, and volume. **The computation:** Richard Arms' Ease of Movement. `computeLineEmvRaw(highs, lows, volumes, volumeDivisor)` is the raw one-period EMV -- each bar's midpoint move (`(high+low)/2` minus the prior bar's midpoint) divided by its BOX RATIO (volume scaled by the divisor, over the high-low range), which works out to `distanceMoved * range * divisor / volume`; index 0 has no prior midpoint so it reads `null`, a zero volume reads `0` (no division by zero), a negative-zero result is normalised to positive zero. `computeLineEmvSma(src, period)` is a simple moving average that tolerates the leading `null` of the raw series. `computeLineEmv(highs, lows, volumes, smaPeriod, volumeDivisor)` smooths the raw EMV with that SMA (default 14-period); the Ease of Movement rises when price advances on light volume and falls when price drops on light volume, swinging around zero. **Worked fixture** (highs [12,16,22,20,14], lows [8,12,18,16,10], volumes all 8, smaPeriod 2, volumeDivisor 1): midpoint = [10,14,20,18,12]; range = [4,4,4,4,4]; emv1 = distanceMoved*range/volume = [null,2,3,-1,-3]; emv = SMA(emv1, 2) = [null,null,2.5,1,-2]. emvFinal -2, emvMin -2, emvMax 2.5, positiveCount 2, negativeCount 1. `runLineEmv` sorts the finite points by x, computes the midpoint/range/raw/smoothed series, and classifies each sample with a `positive`/`negative`/`zero` sign from the smoothed EMV. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** `<ChartLineForceIndex>` MULTIPLIES the price change by volume (measuring the power behind a move) -- the Ease of Movement DIVIDES the midpoint move by volume (via the box ratio), measuring how EASILY price moves: high readings mean a large move on light volume. `<ChartLineObv>` is a cumulative running total of signed volume; the EMV is not cumulative and the volume appears in the DENOMINATOR. `<ChartLineMassIndex>` also consumes high-low data but never uses volume and sums an EMA ratio; the EMV is the only chart-line primitive consuming `{x, high, low, volume}`. All exported helpers carry a `LineEmv`/`Emv` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineEmvPoint[]` (`{x, high, low, volume}`); `smaPeriod` (the smoothing period; default 14); `volumeDivisor` (scales the box ratio; default 1, a non-positive value falls back); `pricePanelRatio` (the fraction of the height given to the price panel; default 0.52, clamped to [0.3, 0.8]); `gap` (the pixel gap between the stacked panels); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `midpointColor` / `bandColor` / `emvColor` / `positiveColor` / `negativeColor` / `zeroColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showBand`/`showEmv`/`showZeroLine`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `midpoint` / `emv`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_EMV_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/SMA_PERIOD/VOLUME_DIVISOR/MIDPOINT_COLOR/BAND_COLOR/EMV_COLOR/POSITIVE_COLOR/NEGATIVE_COLOR/ZERO_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineEmvFinitePoints`; `normalizeLineEmvPeriod(period, fallback)`; `computeLineEmvSma(src, period)` -- the null-tolerant SMA series (`(number|null)[]`); `computeLineEmvRaw(highs, lows, volumes, volumeDivisor)` -- the raw one-period EMV series; `computeLineEmv(highs, lows, volumes, smaPeriod, volumeDivisor)` -- returns `{midpoint, range, emv1, emv}`; `runLineEmv(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, smaPeriod, volumeDivisor, midpoint, range, emv1, emv, samples, emvFinal, emvMin, emvMax, positiveCount, negativeCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, high, low, midpoint, volume, emv1, emv, sign}`; `computeLineEmvLayout({data, ...})` -- stacks the price panel above the EMV panel, derives a symmetric y-bound around zero; projects `midpointPath`, `bandPath` (closed), `priceDots`, `emvPath`, `markers`, the `zeroY` centerline; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 samples -> ok=false; `describeLineEmvChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Ease of Movement + EMV + volume + price change + simple moving average + range + zero (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-emv-aria-desc"` span; the midpoint line + the EMV line + every midpoint dot + every marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-emv / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -zero-line / -axes / -axis-group / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -panel-labels / -panel-label / -band / -midpoint-path / -dots / -dot / -emv-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-high / -tooltip-low / -tooltip-midpoint / -tooltip-volume / -tooltip-raw / -tooltip-emv / -tooltip-sign / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-sma); root mirrors `data-sma-period`, `data-volume-divisor`, `data-emv-final`, `data-positive-count`, `data-negative-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; axis / tick / grid / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"emv"`; markers expose `data-emv` and `data-sign`. Tooltip on a midpoint dot or a marker shows x, high, low, midpoint, volume, raw, emv and the sign. Config badge in top-left calls out `EMV n=<smaPeriod>`. The legend has two toggle items (Midpoint / EMV). 63 vitest cases (getLineEmvFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + high + low + volume** + non-array -> [], normalizeLineEmvPeriod incl. positive integer + floors a fractional + **sub-1 falls back** + non-finite falls back, computeLineEmvSma incl. **averages each window of the period** + **leaves a window containing a null undefined** + **the period-one identity** + **leaves indices before the first full window null** + non-array -> [], computeLineEmvRaw incl. **divides the midpoint move by the box ratio** + **negative reading on a downward move** + **scales by the volume divisor** + **reads zero when the volume is zero** + **reads zero when the bar range is zero** + **normalises a negative-zero reading to positive zero** + non-array -> [], computeLineEmv incl. **takes the midpoint as the high-low average** + **takes the range as high minus low** + **matches the hand-verified raw EMV** + **smooths the raw EMV into the Ease of Movement** + non-array -> empty arrays, runLineEmv incl. ok + **the raw + smoothed series** + **final/min/max readings** + **counts readings above + below zero** + **classifies each sample by sign** + **non-positive divisor falls back** + **sorts by x** + single point / empty -> ok=false + resolves the default period + divisor + one sample per point, computeLineEmvLayout incl. ok + **stacks the price panel above the EMV panel** + midpoint + band + EMV paths + **one marker per defined reading** + **places the zero line inside the EMV panel** + **a symmetric y-bound covering the widest reading** + carries the run stats + collapsed dims -> ok=false + too little data -> ok=false, describeLineEmvChart incl. **the indicator vocabulary** + the SMA period + the reading counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / root data-* / svg + midpoint + EMV lines / **the high-low band by default** / **one marker per defined reading** / config badge SMA period / both panel labels / two-item legend / hide the midpoint path + band / hide the EMV line / hide the band / empty state / omit config badge / ref / displayName / animate flag) all pass
