| 11.600 | UI: chart-line-kst primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.618 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-kst.tsx` ships `<ChartLineKst>` -- pure-SVG line chart with a **Know Sure Thing (KST)** summed rate-of-change momentum oscillator panel and signal line. The TOP panel renders the price line; the BOTTOM panel renders the KST line and a dashed **signal** line on a symmetric y-axis with a dashed **zero centerline** and sign-coloured markers (green above zero, red below). **The computation:** Martin Pring's Know Sure Thing. Four percentage rate-of-change series of increasing lookback (default ROC 10/15/20/30) are EACH smoothed by a SEPARATE simple moving average (default SMA 10/10/10/15) to give the four RCMA legs, then summed at the FIXED ascending weights 1/2/3/4 -- the slower, more reliable legs carry the most weight. `computeLineKstRoc(values, period)` is the percentage ROC `100*(value[i]-value[i-period])/value[i-period]` (leading nulls before the lookback, zero on a zero base, negative-zero normalised). `computeLineKstSma(src, period)` is a simple moving average that tolerates leading `null` placeholders. `computeLineKst(values, config)` takes a `LineKstConfig` (the nine resolved periods), computes the four RCMA legs and `KST = RCMA1*1 + RCMA2*2 + RCMA3*3 + RCMA4*4`, and the signal line as `SMA(KST, signalPeriod)` (default 9); a cross of the KST and its signal marks a momentum shift. **Worked fixture** (values [100,100,100,100,200,200,200,200], ROC 1/2/3/4, SMA 1/1/1/1 identity, signal 2): rcma1 = [null,0,0,0,100,0,0,0]; rcma2 = [null,null,0,0,100,100,0,0]; rcma3 = [null,null,null,0,100,100,100,0]; rcma4 = [null,null,null,null,100,100,100,100]; kst = sum(rcma_k*weight_k) = [null,null,null,null,1000,900,700,400]; signal = SMA(kst,2) = [null,null,null,null,null,950,800,550]. At index 7 only the slowest leg (rcma4, weight 4) is non-zero so kst[7] = 100*4 = 400 -- the weighting is visible in the descending 1000->900->700->400. kstFinal 400, signalFinal 550, kstMin 400, kstMax 1000, positiveCount 4, negativeCount 0. `runLineKst` sorts the finite points by x, computes the four RCMA legs / KST / signal, and classifies each sample with a `positive`/`negative`/`zero` sign from the KST. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** `<ChartLineCoppock>` sums only TWO ROC legs and smooths the TOTAL with ONE weighted moving average -- the KST smooths EACH of FOUR ROC legs SEPARATELY with its own SMA, then sums them at distinct ascending weights 1/2/3/4, and adds a signal line, none of which the Coppock Curve does. `<ChartLineTrix>` is the rate of change of a TRIPLE EMA; the KST never triple-smooths. `<ChartLinePpo>` is a percentage of two EMAs of the price; the KST has no EMA and no percentage-of-EMA step. All exported helpers carry a `LineKst`/`Kst` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineKstPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `roc1Period` / `roc2Period` / `roc3Period` / `roc4Period` (the four ROC lookbacks; defaults 10/15/20/30); `sma1Period` / `sma2Period` / `sma3Period` / `sma4Period` (the four RCMA smoothing periods; defaults 10/10/10/15); `signalPeriod` (the signal SMA period; default 9); `pricePanelRatio` (the fraction of the height given to the price panel; default 0.54, clamped to [0.3, 0.8]); `gap` (the pixel gap between the stacked panels); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `priceColor` / `kstColor` / `signalColor` / `positiveColor` / `negativeColor` / `zeroColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showKst`/`showSignal`/`showZeroLine`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `kst` / `signal`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_KST_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/ROC1_PERIOD/ROC2_PERIOD/ROC3_PERIOD/ROC4_PERIOD/SMA1_PERIOD/SMA2_PERIOD/SMA3_PERIOD/SMA4_PERIOD/SIGNAL_PERIOD/PRICE_COLOR/KST_COLOR/SIGNAL_COLOR/POSITIVE_COLOR/NEGATIVE_COLOR/ZERO_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineKstFinitePoints`; `normalizeLineKstPeriod(period, fallback)`; `computeLineKstRoc(values, period)` -- the percentage ROC series (`(number|null)[]`); `computeLineKstSma(src, period)` -- the null-tolerant SMA series; `computeLineKst(values, config)` -- returns `{rcma1, rcma2, rcma3, rcma4, kst, signal}`; `runLineKst(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, config, rcma1..rcma4, kst, signal, samples, kstFinal, signalFinal, kstMin, kstMax, positiveCount, negativeCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, kst, signal, sign}`; `LineKstConfig` / `LineKstOptions` interfaces; `computeLineKstLayout({data, ...})` -- stacks the price panel above the KST panel, derives a symmetric y-bound covering the KST + signal; projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `kstPath`, `signalPath`, `markers`, the `zeroY` centerline; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 samples -> ok=false; `describeLineKstChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Know Sure Thing + KST + rate-of-change + momentum + signal + simple moving average + zero (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-kst-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the KST line + every price dot + every marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-kst / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -zero-line / -axes / -axis-group / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -panel-labels / -panel-label / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -signal-line / -kst-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-kst / -tooltip-signal / -tooltip-sign / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-roc / -badge-signal); root mirrors `data-roc1-period`, `data-roc2-period`, `data-roc3-period`, `data-roc4-period`, `data-signal-period`, `data-kst-final`, `data-positive-count`, `data-negative-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; axis / tick / grid / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"kst"`; markers expose `data-kst` and `data-sign`. Tooltip on a price dot or a marker shows x, value, kst, signal and the sign. Config badge in top-left calls out `KST <roc1>/<roc2>/<roc3>/<roc4> s=<signal>`. The legend has three toggle items (Price / KST / Signal). 58 vitest cases (getLineKstFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + value** + non-array -> [], normalizeLineKstPeriod incl. positive integer + floors a fractional + **sub-1 falls back** + non-finite falls back, computeLineKstRoc incl. **computes the percentage rate of change** + **leaves indices before the lookback null** + **reads zero when the base is zero** + non-array -> [], computeLineKstSma incl. **averages each window of the period** + **leaves a window containing a null undefined** + **the period-one identity** + non-array -> [], computeLineKst incl. **smooths each ROC leg into an RCMA** + **sums the four weighted RCMA legs into the KST** + **weights the slower legs more heavily** + **smooths the KST into the signal line** + non-array -> empty arrays, runLineKst incl. ok + **the KST + signal series** + **the four RCMA legs** + **final/min/max readings** + **counts readings above + below zero** + **classifies each sample by sign** + **sorts by x** + single point / empty -> ok=false + resolves the default config + one sample per point, computeLineKstLayout incl. ok + **stacks the price panel above the KST panel** + price + KST + signal paths + **one marker per defined reading** + **places the zero line inside the KST panel** + **a symmetric y-bound covering the widest reading** + carries the run stats + collapsed dims -> ok=false + too little data -> ok=false, describeLineKstChart incl. **the indicator vocabulary** + the reading counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / root data-* / svg + price + KST + signal lines / **one marker per defined reading** / config badge ROC + signal periods / both panel labels / three-item legend / hide the price path / hide the KST line / hide the signal line / empty state / omit config badge / ref / displayName / animate flag) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. | 11.601 | UI: chart-line-rvi primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.619 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-rvi.tsx` ships `<ChartLineRvi>` -- pure-SVG line chart with a **Relative Vigor Index (RVI)** oscillator panel and its signal line. The TOP panel renders the **close** line with a shaded **high-low band** behind it; the BOTTOM panel renders the RVI line and a dashed **signal** line on a symmetric y-axis with a dashed **zero centerline** and sign-coloured markers (green above zero, red below). **This primitive consumes full OHLC points -- `{x, open, high, low, close}`** -- since the RVI relates the close-open move to the high-low range. **The computation:** John Ehlers' Relative Vigor Index. `computeLineRviSwma(src)` is a FIXED 4-bar symmetric weighted average with weights 1/2/2/1 over 6 -- `(src[i] + 2*src[i-1] + 2*src[i-2] + src[i-3])/6`; indices before the fourth bar or any window touching a null read null. `computeLineRviSma(src, period)` is a simple moving average tolerating leading nulls. `computeLineRvi(opens, highs, lows, closes, period)` takes `co = close - open` and `hl = high - low`, runs each through the SWMA to get the numerator and denominator, smooths both by an SMA (default period 10), and divides: `RVI = SMA(SWMA(co), period) / SMA(SWMA(hl), period)` (a zero denominator reads RVI 0). The signal line is `SWMA(RVI)` -- a cross of the RVI and its signal marks a vigor shift; conviction is high when price closes far from where it opened relative to the bar's range. **Worked fixture** (open 10, high 30, low 6 throughout; close 16 bars 0-5 / 22 bars 6-9; period 2): co = close-open = [6,6,6,6,6,6,12,12,12,12]; hl = high-low = [24x10]; numerator = SWMA(co) = [.,.,.,6,6,6,7,9,11,12]; denominator = SWMA(hl) = [.,.,.,24,24,24,24,24,24,24]; rvi = SMA(num,2)/SMA(den,2) = [.,.,.,.,0.25,0.25,6.5/24,8/24,10/24,11.5/24]; signal = SWMA(rvi) = [.,.,.,.,.,.,.,39/144,0.3125,0.375]. rviMin 0.25, positiveCount 6, negativeCount 0. `runLineRvi` sorts the finite points by x, computes the spans / numerator / denominator / RVI / signal, and classifies each sample with a `positive`/`negative`/`zero` sign from the RVI. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** `<ChartLineStochastic>` and `<ChartLineWilliamsR>` measure where the CLOSE SITS within the high-low range -- the RVI instead compares the close-open MOVE to the range (how far price travelled open-to-close, not where the close landed), runs everything through the 1/2/2/1 symmetric weighted average, and carries a signal line. `<ChartLineMassIndex>` sums an EMA ratio of the range and `<ChartLineEmv>` divides the midpoint move by volume; the RVI is the only chart-line primitive built on the close-open span and the only one consuming full OHLC `{x, open, high, low, close}`. All exported helpers carry a `LineRvi`/`Rvi` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineRviPoint[]` (`{x, open, high, low, close}`); `period` (the SMA smoothing period; default 10); `pricePanelRatio` (the fraction of the height given to the price panel; default 0.52, clamped to [0.3, 0.8]); `gap` (the pixel gap between the stacked panels); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `closeColor` / `bandColor` / `rviColor` / `signalColor` / `positiveColor` / `negativeColor` / `zeroColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showBand`/`showRvi`/`showSignal`/`showZeroLine`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `close` / `rvi` / `signal`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_RVI_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/CLOSE_COLOR/BAND_COLOR/RVI_COLOR/SIGNAL_COLOR/POSITIVE_COLOR/NEGATIVE_COLOR/ZERO_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineRviFinitePoints`; `normalizeLineRviPeriod(period, fallback)`; `computeLineRviSwma(src)` -- the fixed 1/2/2/1 symmetric weighted average (`(number|null)[]`); `computeLineRviSma(src, period)` -- the null-tolerant SMA series; `computeLineRvi(opens, highs, lows, closes, period)` -- returns `{co, hl, numerator, denominator, rvi, signal}`; `runLineRvi(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, period, co, hl, numerator, denominator, rvi, signal, samples, rviFinal, signalFinal, rviMin, rviMax, positiveCount, negativeCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, open, high, low, close, rvi, signal, sign}`; `computeLineRviLayout({data, ...})` -- stacks the price panel above the RVI panel, derives a symmetric y-bound around zero; projects `closePath`, `bandPath` (closed), `priceDots`, `rviPath`, `signalPath`, `markers`, the `zeroY` centerline; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 samples -> ok=false; `describeLineRviChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Relative Vigor Index + RVI + vigor + close-open + high-low range + signal + zero (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-rvi-aria-desc"` span; the close line + the RVI line + every close dot + every marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-rvi / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -zero-line / -axes / -axis-group / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -panel-labels / -panel-label / -band / -close-path / -dots / -dot / -signal-line / -rvi-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-open / -tooltip-high / -tooltip-low / -tooltip-close / -tooltip-rvi / -tooltip-signal / -tooltip-sign / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-period); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-rvi-final`, `data-signal-final`, `data-positive-count`, `data-negative-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; axis / tick / grid / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"rvi"`; markers expose `data-rvi` and `data-sign`. Tooltip on a close dot or a marker shows x, open, high, low, close, rvi, signal and the sign. Config badge in top-left calls out `RVI n=<period>`. The legend has three toggle items (Close / RVI / Signal). 60 vitest cases (getLineRviFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + open + high + low + close** + non-array -> [], normalizeLineRviPeriod incl. positive integer + floors a fractional + **sub-1 falls back** + non-finite falls back, computeLineRviSwma incl. **applies the fixed 1/2/2/1 symmetric weighted average** + **weights the two middle bars double** + **leaves a window containing a null undefined** + **all indices null when fewer than four bars** + non-array -> [], computeLineRviSma incl. **averages each window of the period** + **leaves a window containing a null undefined** + **the period-one identity** + non-array -> [], computeLineRvi incl. **takes the close-open + high-low spans** + **runs the spans through the symmetric weighted average** + **divides the smoothed numerator by the smoothed denominator** + **takes the signal line as the SWMA of the RVI** + non-array -> empty arrays, runLineRvi incl. ok + **the RVI + signal series** + **the close-open + high-low spans** + **final/min/max readings** + **counts readings above + below zero** + **classifies each sample by sign** + **sorts by x** + single point / empty -> ok=false + resolves the default period + one sample per point, computeLineRviLayout incl. ok + **stacks the price panel above the RVI panel** + close + band + RVI + signal paths + **one marker per defined reading** + **places the zero line inside the RVI panel** + **a symmetric y-bound covering the widest reading** + carries the run stats + collapsed dims -> ok=false + too little data -> ok=false, describeLineRviChart incl. **the indicator vocabulary** + the period + the reading counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / root data-* / svg + close + RVI + signal lines / **the high-low band by default** / **one marker per defined reading** / config badge period / both panel labels / three-item legend / hide the close path + band / hide the RVI line / hide the signal line / empty state / omit config badge / ref / displayName / animate flag) all pass under vitest 4.1.5; the SMA-ratio RVI / signal readings are asserted with toBeCloseTo (only the SWMA-of-integers and exact dyadic intermediates are bit-exact). | 11.602 | UI: chart-line-stc primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.620 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-stc.tsx` ships `<ChartLineStc>` -- pure-SVG line chart with a **Schaff Trend Cycle (STC)** oscillator panel on a 0 to 100 scale. The TOP panel renders the price line; the BOTTOM panel renders the STC line on a FIXED 0-100 y-axis with shaded **overbought** and **oversold** zones, dashed threshold lines, and markers coloured by zone. **The computation:** Doug Schaff's Trend Cycle. `computeLineStcMacd(values, fastPeriod, slowPeriod)` is the MACD line -- a fast EMA minus a slow EMA (defaults 23/50). `computeLineStcStochK(src, cyclePeriod)` scales each value to its position within a cycle-length high-low window 0-100 (default cycle 10; a flat window with zero range reads the NEUTRAL 50; results clamped to [0,100]). `computeLineStcSmooth(src, factor)` is the Schaff modified-EMA `out[i] = out[i-1] + factor*(src[i]-out[i-1])` seeded with the first defined value (default factor 0.5). `computeLineStc(values, fastPeriod, slowPeriod, cyclePeriod, factor)` runs the MACD line through TWO cycles of stochastic-then-smooth: `k1 = StochK(MACD)`, `d1 = Smooth(k1)`, `k2 = StochK(d1)`, `STC = Smooth(k2)` -- yielding a fast oscillator BOUNDED to 0-100. **Worked fixture** (values [4,4,4,12,12,12,4,4,4,12], fast 1, slow 3, cycle 2, factor 0.5): macd = fastEMA-slowEMA = [.,.,0,4,2,1,-3.5,-1.75,-0.875,3.5625]; k1 = StochK(macd,2) = [.,.,.,100,0,0,0,100,100,100]; d1 = Smooth(k1,0.5) = [.,.,.,100,50,25,12.5,56.25,78.125,89.0625]; k2 = StochK(d1,2) = [.,.,.,.,0,0,0,100,100,100]; stc = Smooth(k2,0.5) = [.,.,.,.,0,0,0,50,75,87.5]. With thresholds 75/25: stcFinal 87.5, stcMin 0, stcMax 87.5, overboughtCount 2 (75, 87.5), oversoldCount 3 (0,0,0). `runLineStc` sorts the finite points by x, computes the MACD and STC, and classifies each sample into an `overbought`/`oversold`/`neutral` zone (>=upper / <=lower / between). **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** `<ChartLineMacd>` plots the RAW, UNBOUNDED EMA difference -- the STC takes that MACD line as its INPUT and runs it through a double stochastic, producing a BOUNDED 0-100 oscillator that responds far faster than MACD itself. `<ChartLineStochastic>` runs a SINGLE stochastic transform on the PRICE -- the STC runs TWO stochastic transforms, each followed by a modified-EMA smoothing, on the MACD line rather than price. `<ChartLinePpo>`/`<ChartLineTrix>` stay in the EMA/percentage family; the STC is the only chart-line primitive that composes MACD with a double stochastic cycle. All exported helpers carry a `LineStc`/`Stc` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineStcPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `fastPeriod` / `slowPeriod` (the MACD EMA periods; defaults 23 / 50); `cyclePeriod` (the stochastic window; default 10); `factor` (the modified-EMA smoothing factor; default 0.5, clamped to (0,1]); `upperThreshold` / `lowerThreshold` (the zone thresholds; defaults 75 / 25); `pricePanelRatio` (the fraction of the height given to the price panel; default 0.55, clamped to [0.3, 0.8]); `gap` (the pixel gap between the stacked panels); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `priceColor` / `stcColor` / `overboughtColor` / `oversoldColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showStc`/`showZones`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `stc`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_STC_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/FAST_PERIOD/SLOW_PERIOD/CYCLE_PERIOD/FACTOR/UPPER_THRESHOLD/LOWER_THRESHOLD/PRICE_COLOR/STC_COLOR/OVERBOUGHT_COLOR/OVERSOLD_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineStcFinitePoints`; `normalizeLineStcPeriod(period, fallback)`; `computeLineStcEma(src, period)` -- the null-tolerant EMA series (`(number|null)[]`); `computeLineStcMacd(values, fastPeriod, slowPeriod)` -- the MACD line; `computeLineStcStochK(src, cyclePeriod)` -- the raw stochastic %K (0-100, flat window 50); `computeLineStcSmooth(src, factor)` -- the Schaff modified-EMA smoother; `computeLineStc(values, fastPeriod, slowPeriod, cyclePeriod, factor)` -- returns `{macd, k1, d1, k2, stc}`; `runLineStc(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, fastPeriod, slowPeriod, cyclePeriod, factor, upperThreshold, lowerThreshold, macd, stc, samples, stcFinal, stcMin, stcMax, overboughtCount, oversoldCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, macd, stc, zone}`; `computeLineStcLayout({data, ...})` -- stacks the price panel above the STC panel whose y-axis is fixed 0-100; projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `stcPath`, `markers`, `overboughtRect` / `oversoldRect` zone rects, `upperY` / `lowerY` threshold lines; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 samples -> ok=false; `describeLineStcChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Schaff Trend Cycle + STC + MACD + stochastic + 0-100 + overbought + oversold (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-stc-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the STC line + every price dot + every marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-stc / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -zones / -overbought-zone / -oversold-zone / -threshold-line / -axes / -axis-group / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -panel-labels / -panel-label / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -stc-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-macd / -tooltip-stc / -tooltip-zone / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-macd / -badge-cycle); root mirrors `data-fast-period`, `data-slow-period`, `data-cycle-period`, `data-upper-threshold`, `data-lower-threshold`, `data-stc-final`, `data-overbought-count`, `data-oversold-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; axis / tick / grid / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"stc"`; threshold lines carry `data-kind` (upper / lower); markers expose `data-stc` and `data-zone`. Tooltip on a price dot or a marker shows x, value, macd, stc and the zone. Config badge in top-left calls out `STC <fast>/<slow> c=<cycle>`. The legend has two toggle items (Price / STC). 69 vitest cases (getLineStcFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + value** + non-array -> [], normalizeLineStcPeriod incl. positive integer + floors a fractional + **sub-1 falls back** + non-finite falls back, computeLineStcEma incl. **seeds with the simple mean** + **the period-length mean as the seed** + **the period-one identity** + non-array -> [], computeLineStcMacd incl. **matches the hand-verified fast-minus-slow EMA difference** + non-array -> [], computeLineStcStochK incl. **scales each value to its position in the window range** + **reads zero when the value sits at the window low** + **reads the neutral 50 for a flat window** + **leaves a window containing a null undefined** + non-array -> [], computeLineStcSmooth incl. **applies the modified-EMA smoothing seeded with the first value** + **skips leading nulls before seeding** + **honours the smoothing factor** + non-array -> [], computeLineStc incl. **matches the hand-verified MACD line** + **the first smoothed stochastic** + **the Schaff Trend Cycle** + **keeps every defined STC reading within 0 and 100** + non-array -> empty arrays, runLineStc incl. ok + **the STC series** + **final/min/max readings** + **counts overbought + oversold readings** + **classifies each sample into a zone** + **honours custom thresholds** + **sorts by x** + single point / empty -> ok=false + resolves the default config + one sample per point, computeLineStcLayout incl. ok + **stacks the price panel above the STC panel** + price + STC paths + **one marker per defined reading** + **spans the STC y axis ticks from 0 to 100** + **places the upper threshold above the lower threshold** + **builds overbought + oversold zone rects with positive height** + carries the run stats + collapsed dims -> ok=false + too little data -> ok=false, describeLineStcChart incl. **the indicator vocabulary** + the zone counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / root data-* / svg + price + STC lines / **the overbought + oversold zones** / **both threshold lines** / **one marker per defined reading** / **marks the overbought + oversold markers with their zone** / config badge MACD + cycle periods / both panel labels / two-item legend / hide the price path / hide the STC line / hide the zones / empty state / omit config badge / ref / | 11.603 | UI: chart-line-elder-ray primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.621 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-elder-ray.tsx` ships `<ChartLineElderRay>` -- pure-SVG line chart with an **Elder Ray** panel rendering **bull power** and **bear power** around an EMA baseline. The TOP panel renders the close line with a dashed **EMA baseline**; the BOTTOM panel renders the bull power and bear power lines on a symmetric y-axis with a dashed **zero centerline** and per-series markers. **The computation:** Alexander Elder's Elder Ray. `computeLineElderRayEma(src, period)` is a null-tolerant, SMA-seeded EMA. `computeLineElderRay(highs, lows, closes, emaPeriod)` takes the EMA of the close (default period 13) as the trend BASELINE, then `bullPower = high - baseline` (how far buyers can lift the bar's high above the trend) and `bearPower = low - baseline` (how far sellers can press the bar's low below it); both swing around zero -- positive bull power means buyers can reach above the trend, negative bear power means sellers can reach below it. **Worked fixture** (close [4,4,4,12,12,12], high [10,10,10,14,18,15], low [2,2,1,5,4,9], emaPeriod 3): ema = EMA(close,3) (mult 0.5) = [null,null,4,8,10,11]; bullPower = high-ema = [null,null,6,6,8,4]; bearPower = low-ema = [null,null,-3,-3,-6,-2]. bullFinal 4, bearFinal -2, bullMax 8, bearMin -6, bullPositiveCount 4, bearNegativeCount 4. `runLineElderRay` sorts the finite points by x, computes the EMA baseline / bull power / bear power, and counts positive bull-power bars and negative bear-power bars. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** `<ChartLineForceIndex>` multiplies the close-to-close price change by VOLUME -- the Elder Ray uses no volume and no price change; it measures the bar's HIGH and LOW against an EMA baseline. `<ChartLineMacd>` is the difference of TWO EMAs of one price series -- the Elder Ray uses a SINGLE EMA and measures TWO different prices (the high and the low) against it, producing two distinct power series rather than one difference line. `<ChartLineBollinger>`/`<ChartLineKeltner>` wrap a band around a moving average; the Elder Ray draws no band, plotting the signed gap from the high and from the low to the EMA as two independent oscillators around zero. All exported helpers carry a `LineElderRay`/`ElderRay` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineElderRayPoint[]` (`{x, high, low, close}`); `emaPeriod` (the baseline EMA period; default 13); `pricePanelRatio` (the fraction of the height given to the price panel; default 0.54, clamped to [0.3, 0.8]); `gap` (the pixel gap between the stacked panels); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `closeColor` / `emaColor` / `bullColor` / `bearColor` / `zeroColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showEma`/`showBull`/`showBear`/`showZeroLine`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `close` / `ema` / `bull` / `bear`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_ELDER_RAY_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/EMA_PERIOD/CLOSE_COLOR/EMA_COLOR/BULL_COLOR/BEAR_COLOR/ZERO_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineElderRayFinitePoints`; `normalizeLineElderRayPeriod(period, fallback)`; `computeLineElderRayEma(src, period)` -- the null-tolerant EMA series (`(number|null)[]`); `computeLineElderRay(highs, lows, closes, emaPeriod)` -- returns `{ema, bullPower, bearPower}`; `runLineElderRay(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, emaPeriod, ema, bullPower, bearPower, samples, bullFinal, bearFinal, bullMax, bearMin, bullPositiveCount, bearNegativeCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, high, low, close, ema, bullPower, bearPower}`; `computeLineElderRayLayout({data, ...})` -- stacks the price panel above the Elder Ray panel, derives a symmetric y-bound around zero covering both power series; projects `closePath`, `emaPath`, `priceDots`, `bullPath`, `bearPath`, `bullMarkers`, `bearMarkers`, the `zeroY` centerline; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 samples -> ok=false; `describeLineElderRayChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Elder Ray + bull power + bear power + EMA + baseline + zero (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-elder-ray-aria-desc"` span; the close line + the bull power line + the bear power line + every close dot + every marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-elder-ray / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -zero-line / -axes / -axis-group / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -panel-labels / -panel-label / -ema-path / -close-path / -dots / -dot / -bull-line / -bear-line / -bull-markers / -bear-markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-high / -tooltip-low / -tooltip-close / -tooltip-ema / -tooltip-bull / -tooltip-bear / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-ema); root mirrors `data-ema-period`, `data-bull-final`, `data-bear-final`, `data-bull-positive-count`, `data-bear-negative-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; axis / tick / grid / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"ray"`; markers carry `data-kind` (bull / bear) and expose `data-power`. Tooltip on a close dot or a marker shows x, high, low, close, ema, bull and bear. Config badge in top-left calls out `ELDER RAY ema=<emaPeriod>`. The legend has four toggle items (Close / EMA / Bull Power / Bear Power). 54 vitest cases (getLineElderRayFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + high + low + close** + non-array -> [], normalizeLineElderRayPeriod incl. positive integer + floors a fractional + **sub-1 falls back** + non-finite falls back, computeLineElderRayEma incl. **seeds with the simple mean** + **the period-length mean as the seed** + **the period-one identity** + non-array -> [], computeLineElderRay incl. **takes the EMA of the close as the trend baseline** + **bull power as the high minus the baseline** + **bear power as the low minus the baseline** + **leaves power undefined before the baseline is defined** + non-array -> empty arrays, runLineElderRay incl. ok + **the bull + bear power series** + **final/max/min readings** + **counts positive bull + negative bear bars** + **per-sample bull + bear power** + **sorts by x** + single point / empty -> ok=false + resolves the default EMA period + one sample per point, computeLineElderRayLayout incl. ok + **stacks the price panel above the Elder Ray panel** + close + EMA + bull + bear paths + **one bull + one bear marker per defined reading** + **places the zero line inside the Elder Ray panel** + **a symmetric y-bound covering the widest power reading** + carries the run stats + collapsed dims -> ok=false + too little data -> ok=false, describeLineElderRayChart incl. **the indicator vocabulary** + the power-bar counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / root data-* / svg + close + EMA + bull + bear lines / **one bull + one bear marker per defined reading** / config badge EMA period / both panel labels / four-item legend / hide the close path / hide the EMA path / hide the bull line + bull markers / hide the bear line / empty state / omit config badge / ref / displayName / animate flag) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. | 11.604 | UI: chart-line-ultimate primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.622 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-ultimate.tsx` ships `<ChartLineUltimate>` -- pure-SVG line chart with an **Ultimate Oscillator** panel blending three lookback windows on a 0-100 scale. The TOP panel renders the close price line; the BOTTOM panel renders the oscillator line on a FIXED 0-100 y-axis with shaded **overbought** and **oversold** zones, dashed threshold lines, and markers coloured by zone. **The computation:** Larry Williams' Ultimate Oscillator. `computeLineUltimateBpTr(highs, lows, closes)` derives each bar's **buying pressure** `BP = close - min(low, prevClose)` and **true range** `TR = max(high, prevClose) - min(low, prevClose)`; index 0 has no prior close so both read `null`. `computeLineUltimateAvg(bp, tr, period)` sums buying pressure and true range over a lookback window and divides them (a zero total true range reads the neutral 0.5). `computeLineUltimate(highs, lows, closes, shortPeriod, mediumPeriod, longPeriod)` blends three such ratios over SHORT/MEDIUM/LONG windows (defaults 7/14/28) at the FIXED descending weights 4/2/1 and scales to 0-100: `UO = 100*(4*Avg_short + 2*Avg_medium + Avg_long)/7`. Because `0 <= BP <= TR` for every bar, each Avg lies in [0,1] and the weighted blend stays BOUNDED to [0,100]. **Worked fixture** (high [12,16,16,20,20,24,22], low [8,8,10,10,12,14,12], close [10,14,12,18,16,20,14], lookbacks 1/2/3): bp = close-min(low,prevClose) = [null,6,2,8,4,6,2]; tr = max(high,prevClose)-min(low,prevClose) = [null,8,6,10,8,10,10]; uo = 100*(4*Avg1+2*Avg2+Avg3)/7 = [.,.,.,73.10,55.95,59.34,28.98]. With thresholds 70/30: overboughtCount 1 (73.10), oversoldCount 1 (28.98). `runLineUltimate` sorts the finite points by x, computes the oscillator, and classifies each sample into an `overbought`/`oversold`/`neutral` zone. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** `<ChartLineRsi>` and `<ChartLineStochastic>` are SINGLE-WINDOW oscillators (one gain/loss ratio or one range position) -- the Ultimate Oscillator blends THREE windows at the fixed weights 4/2/1, and is built from BUYING PRESSURE and TRUE RANGE rather than gains/losses or range position. `<ChartLineStc>` is a double stochastic of the MACD line; the Ultimate Oscillator never touches MACD or a stochastic. `<ChartLineWilliamsR>` is also a Larry Williams indicator but is a single-window range-position reading; the Ultimate Oscillator is the three-window buying-pressure blend. All exported helpers carry a `LineUltimate`/`Ultimate` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineUltimatePoint[]` (`{x, high, low, close}`); `shortPeriod` / `mediumPeriod` / `longPeriod` (the three lookback windows; defaults 7 / 14 / 28); `upperThreshold` / `lowerThreshold` (the zone thresholds; defaults 70 / 30); `pricePanelRatio` (the fraction of the height given to the price panel; default 0.55, clamped to [0.3, 0.8]); `gap` (the pixel gap between the stacked panels); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `priceColor` / `uoColor` / `overboughtColor` / `oversoldColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showUo`/`showZones`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `uo`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_ULTIMATE_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/SHORT_PERIOD/MEDIUM_PERIOD/LONG_PERIOD/UPPER_THRESHOLD/LOWER_THRESHOLD/PRICE_COLOR/UO_COLOR/OVERBOUGHT_COLOR/OVERSOLD_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineUltimateFinitePoints`; `normalizeLineUltimatePeriod(period, fallback)`; `computeLineUltimateBpTr(highs, lows, closes)` -- returns `{bp, tr}`; `computeLineUltimateAvg(bp, tr, period)` -- the BP/TR ratio series (`(number|null)[]`); `computeLineUltimate(highs, lows, closes, shortPeriod, mediumPeriod, longPeriod)` -- returns `{bp, tr, avg1, avg2, avg3, uo}`; `runLineUltimate(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, shortPeriod, mediumPeriod, longPeriod, upperThreshold, lowerThreshold, bp, tr, uo, samples, uoFinal, uoMin, uoMax, overboughtCount, oversoldCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, high, low, close, uo, zone}`; `computeLineUltimateLayout({data, ...})` -- stacks the price panel above the oscillator panel whose y-axis is fixed 0-100; projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `uoPath`, `markers`, `overboughtRect` / `oversoldRect` zone rects, `upperY` / `lowerY` threshold lines; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 samples -> ok=false; `describeLineUltimateChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Ultimate Oscillator + lookback + buying pressure + true range + 0-100 + overbought + oversold (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-ultimate-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the oscillator line + every price dot + every marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-ultimate / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -zones / -overbought-zone / -oversold-zone / -threshold-line / -axes / -axis-group / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -panel-labels / -panel-label / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -uo-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-high / -tooltip-low / -tooltip-close / -tooltip-uo / -tooltip-zone / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-periods); root mirrors `data-short-period`, `data-medium-period`, `data-long-period`, `data-upper-threshold`, `data-lower-threshold`, `data-uo-final`, `data-overbought-count`, `data-oversold-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; axis / tick / grid / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"uo"`; threshold lines carry `data-kind` (upper / lower); markers expose `data-uo` and `data-zone`. Tooltip on a price dot or a marker shows x, high, low, close, uo and the zone. Config badge in top-left calls out `ULT <short>/<medium>/<long>`. The legend has two toggle items (Price / Ultimate). 61 vitest cases (getLineUltimateFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + high + low + close** + non-array -> [], normalizeLineUltimatePeriod incl. positive integer + floors a fractional + **sub-1 falls back** + non-finite falls back, computeLineUltimateBpTr incl. **takes buying pressure + true range against the prior close** + **leaves the first bar null with no prior close** + non-array -> empty arrays, computeLineUltimateAvg incl. **divides the buying-pressure sum by the true-range sum** + **reads the neutral 0.5 for a zero total true range** + **sums BP + TR over the period window** + **leaves a window containing a null undefined** + non-array -> [], computeLineUltimate incl. **matches the hand-verified buying pressure + true range** + **the hand-verified Ultimate Oscillator** + **keeps every defined reading within 0 and 100** + non-array -> empty arrays, runLineUltimate incl. ok + **the BP/TR/oscillator series** + **final/min/max readings** + **counts overbought + oversold readings** + **classifies each sample into a zone** + **honours custom thresholds** + **sorts by x** + single point / empty -> ok=false + resolves the default config + one sample per point, computeLineUltimateLayout incl. ok + **stacks the price panel above the oscillator panel** + price + oscillator paths + **one marker per defined reading** + **spans the oscillator y axis ticks from 0 to 100** + **places the upper threshold above the lower threshold** + **builds overbought + oversold zone rects with positive height** + carries | 11.605 | UI: chart-line-hma primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.623 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-hma.tsx` ships `<ChartLineHma>` -- pure-SVG line chart with a **Hull Moving Average (HMA)** overlay that reduces lag while staying smooth. **Unlike the recent two-panel indicator primitives, this is a SINGLE-PANEL chart** -- the HMA line is drawn directly OVER the price line on shared axes. **The computation:** Alan Hull's Hull Moving Average. `computeLineHmaWma(src, period)` is a LINEARLY weighted moving average -- the oldest window value carries weight 1, the newest weight `period`, divided by the triangular weight sum; it tolerates leading `null` placeholders so the WMA of the (null-prefixed) raw line is well-defined. `computeLineHma(values, period)` derives `halfPeriod = floor(period/2)` and `sqrtPeriod = floor(sqrt(period))`, builds the lag-reduced RAW line `raw = 2*WMA(values, halfPeriod) - WMA(values, period)` (the over-shoot cancels a plain MA's lag), and smooths it: `HMA = WMA(raw, sqrtPeriod)`. The result cuts lag while staying smooth -- on a perfectly linear trend the HMA tracks the price with ZERO lag. **Worked fixture** (values [0,3,6,9,12,15,18] a step-3 linear ramp, period 4 -> halfPeriod 2, sqrtPeriod 2): wmaHalf = WMA(values,2) = [null,2,5,8,11,14,17]; wmaFull = WMA(values,4) = [null,null,null,6,9,12,15]; raw = 2*wmaHalf-wmaFull = [null,null,null,10,13,16,19]; hma = WMA(raw,2) = [null,null,null,null,12,15,18]. The HMA reproduces the value exactly (12/15/18) -- the zero-lag property on a linear trend. hmaFinal 18, hmaMin 12, hmaMax 18, aboveCount 0, belowCount 0 (price sits ON the HMA on a linear trend). `runLineHma` sorts the finite points by x, computes the HMA, and tags each sample with whether the price sits `above`/`below`/`on` the HMA. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** a single EMA (`<ChartLineEwma>`) or SMA (`<ChartLineMovingAvg>`) always LAGS the trend -- the HMA nests THREE weighted moving averages (a half, a full, and a square-root length) in the `2*WMA(half) - WMA(full)` arrangement specifically to CANCEL that lag, which no single moving average does. `<ChartLineCoppock>`/`<ChartLineKst>` apply a WMA to RATE-OF-CHANGE legs in a sub-panel oscillator; the HMA applies the WMA to the PRICE itself and is a single-panel price OVERLAY, not an oscillator. All exported helpers carry a `LineHma`/`Hma` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineHmaPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `period` (the HMA period; default 16, halfPeriod and sqrtPeriod derived); `padding` / `tickCount` / `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `priceColor` / `hmaColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showHma`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `hma`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_HMA_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/PRICE_COLOR/HMA_COLOR/ABOVE_COLOR/BELOW_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineHmaFinitePoints`; `normalizeLineHmaPeriod(period, fallback)`; `computeLineHmaWma(src, period)` -- the null-tolerant linearly weighted MA (`(number|null)[]`); `computeLineHma(values, period)` -- returns `{wmaHalf, wmaFull, raw, hma}`; `runLineHma(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, period, halfPeriod, sqrtPeriod, wmaHalf, wmaFull, raw, hma, samples, hmaFinal, hmaMin, hmaMax, aboveCount, belowCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, hma, position}`; `computeLineHmaLayout({data, ...})` -- fits ONE panel whose y-domain spans both the price and the HMA so the overlay never clips; projects `pricePath`, `hmaPath`, `priceDots`, `hmaMarkers`; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 samples -> ok=false; `describeLineHmaChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Hull Moving Average + HMA + overlay + weighted moving average + lag + smooth (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-hma-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the HMA line + every price dot + every marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-hma / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick / -tick-label / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -hma-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-hma / -tooltip-position / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-period); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-half-period`, `data-sqrt-period`, `data-hma-final`, `data-above-count`, `data-below-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; markers expose `data-hma`. Tooltip on a price dot or a marker shows x, value, hma and the position. Config badge in top-left calls out `HMA p=<period>`. The legend has two toggle items (Price / HMA). 53 vitest cases (getLineHmaFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + value** + non-array -> [], normalizeLineHmaPeriod incl. positive integer + floors a fractional + **sub-1 falls back** + non-finite falls back, computeLineHmaWma incl. **applies the linearly weighted moving average** + **weights the most recent value most heavily** + **leaves a window containing a null undefined** + **leaves indices before the first full window null** + non-array -> [], computeLineHma incl. **takes the half-length + full-length weighted moving averages** + **the raw line as twice the half WMA minus the full WMA** + **smooths the raw line into the HMA** + **tracks a linear trend with zero lag** + non-array -> empty arrays, runLineHma incl. ok + derived lengths + **the HMA series** + **final/min/max readings** + **classifies a linear trend as price sitting on the HMA** + **classifies price above the HMA on a late upward spike** + **sorts by x** + single point / empty -> ok=false + resolves the default period + one sample per point, computeLineHmaLayout incl. ok + price + HMA paths + **one marker per defined reading** + **spans the y domain over both the price + the HMA** + carries the derived lengths + collapsed dims -> ok=false + too little data -> ok=false, describeLineHmaChart incl. **the indicator vocabulary** + the price-versus-HMA counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / root data-* / svg + price + HMA lines / **one marker per defined reading** / config badge period / two-item legend / hide the price path / hide the HMA line + markers / **price dots when showDots** + omit by default / empty state / omit config badge / omit legend / ref / displayName / animate flag) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. | 11.606 | UI: chart-line-zlema primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.624 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-zlema.tsx` ships `<ChartLineZlema>` -- pure-SVG line chart with a **Zero-Lag Exponential Moving Average (ZLEMA)** overlay. Like chart-line-hma it is a SINGLE-PANEL chart -- the ZLEMA line is drawn directly OVER the price line on shared axes. **The computation:** John Ehlers and Ric Way's Zero-Lag Exponential Moving Average. The LAG is `floor((period-1)/2)`; the DE-LAGGED series adds each bar's own momentum over that lag back into the value: `deLagged[i] = 2*value[i] - value[i-lag]`, shifting the price forward to anticipate the trend. `computeLineZlemaEma(src, period)` is a null-tolerant, SMA-seeded EMA (the de-lagged series has a `null` prefix for indices below lag). `computeLineZlema(values, period)` runs that EMA over the de-lagged series -- the EMA's OWN lag cancels the forward shift, so the average keeps pace with the price instead of trailing it. On a linear trend the ZLEMA tracks the price with ZERO lag. **Worked fixture** (values [0,3,6,9,12,15,18] a step-3 linear ramp, period 3 -> lag 1): deLagged = 2*value[i]-value[i-1] = [null,6,9,12,15,18,21]; zlema = EMA(deLagged, 3) = [null,null,null,9,12,15,18]. The ZLEMA reproduces the value exactly (9/12/15/18) -- the zero-lag property on a linear trend. zlemaFinal 18, zlemaMin 9, zlemaMax 18, aboveCount 0, belowCount 0 (price sits ON the ZLEMA on a linear trend). `runLineZlema` sorts the finite points by x, computes the ZLEMA, and tags each sample with whether the price sits `above`/`below`/`on` the ZLEMA. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** `<ChartLineHma>` is also a zero-lag price overlay but nests THREE weighted moving averages (`2*WMA(half) - WMA(full)` then a sqrt-length WMA) -- the ZLEMA cancels lag DIFFERENTLY: it shifts the price forward by its own momentum (`2*value - value[lag]`) and then applies ONE EMA, a lag-shift plus one EMA rather than nested WMAs. `<ChartLineEwma>` is a plain EMA that always TRAILS the trend; the ZLEMA feeds the EMA a momentum-anticipated series specifically so the EMA's lag is cancelled. Like the HMA and unlike the recent two-panel indicator primitives, the ZLEMA shares one set of axes with the price. All exported helpers carry a `LineZlema`/`Zlema` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineZlemaPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `period` (the ZLEMA period; default 14, lag derived as `floor((period-1)/2)`); `padding` / `tickCount` / `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `priceColor` / `zlemaColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showZlema`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `zlema`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_ZLEMA_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/PRICE_COLOR/ZLEMA_COLOR/ABOVE_COLOR/BELOW_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineZlemaFinitePoints`; `normalizeLineZlemaPeriod(period, fallback)`; `computeLineZlemaEma(src, period)` -- the null-tolerant SMA-seeded EMA (`(number|null)[]`); `computeLineZlema(values, period)` -- returns `{lag, deLagged, zlema}`; `runLineZlema(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, period, lag, deLagged, zlema, samples, zlemaFinal, zlemaMin, zlemaMax, aboveCount, belowCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, zlema, position}`; `computeLineZlemaLayout({data, ...})` -- fits ONE panel whose y-domain spans both the price and the ZLEMA so the overlay never clips; projects `pricePath`, `zlemaPath`, `priceDots`, `zlemaMarkers`; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 samples -> ok=false; `describeLineZlemaChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Zero-Lag Exponential Moving Average + ZLEMA + overlay + lag + exponential moving average + momentum (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-zlema-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the ZLEMA line + every price dot + every marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-zlema / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick / -tick-label / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -zlema-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-zlema / -tooltip-position / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-period / -badge-lag); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-lag`, `data-zlema-final`, `data-above-count`, `data-below-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; markers expose `data-zlema`. Tooltip on a price dot or a marker shows x, value, zlema and the position. Config badge in top-left calls out `ZLEMA p=<period> lag=<lag>`. The legend has two toggle items (Price / ZLEMA). 53 vitest cases (getLineZlemaFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + value** + non-array -> [], normalizeLineZlemaPeriod incl. positive integer + floors a fractional + **sub-1 falls back** + non-finite falls back, computeLineZlemaEma incl. **seeds with the simple mean** + **the period-length mean as the seed** + **skips leading null placeholders** + non-array -> [], computeLineZlema incl. **derives the lag as floor of half the period minus one** + **the de-lagged series as twice the value minus the lagged value** + **smooths the de-lagged series into the ZLEMA** + **tracks a linear trend with zero lag** + **reproduces the series for a period of one** + non-array -> empty arrays, runLineZlema incl. ok + period + lag + **the ZLEMA series** + **final/min/max readings** + **classifies a linear trend as price sitting on the ZLEMA** + **classifies price above the ZLEMA on an upward step** + **sorts by x** + single point / empty -> ok=false + resolves the default period + one sample per point, computeLineZlemaLayout incl. ok + price + ZLEMA paths + **one marker per defined reading** + **spans the y domain over both the price + the ZLEMA** + carries the period + lag + collapsed dims -> ok=false + too little data -> ok=false, describeLineZlemaChart incl. **the indicator vocabulary** + the price-versus-ZLEMA counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / root data-* / svg + price + ZLEMA lines / **one marker per defined reading** / config badge period + lag / two-item legend / hide the price path / hide the ZLEMA line + markers / **price dots when showDots** + omit by default / empty state / omit config badge / omit legend / ref / displayName / animate flag) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. | 11.607 | UI: chart-line-alma primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.625 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-alma.tsx` ships `<ChartLineAlma>` -- pure-SVG line chart with an **Arnaud Legoux Moving Average (ALMA)** overlay using a GAUSSIAN-weighted window. Like chart-line-hma and chart-line-zlema it is a SINGLE-PANEL chart -- the ALMA line is drawn directly OVER the price line on shared axes. **The computation:** Arnaud Legoux's Moving Average. `computeLineAlmaWeights(period, offset, sigma)` builds the window weights as a Gaussian curve over `period` slots: `m = offset*(period-1)`, `s = period/sigma`, `w[k] = exp(-(k-m)^2 / (2*s^2))`. An offset near 1 (default 0.85) pushes the peak toward the recent end of the window (cutting lag); a larger sigma (default 6) narrows the curve (stronger noise filtering). `computeLineAlma(values, period, offset, sigma)` averages each `period`-length window with those weights, normalised by their sum: `ALMA[i] = sum(w[k]*value[i-(period-1)+k]) / sum(w)`. Because every weight is positive, each ALMA reading stays inside its window's value range. **Worked fixture** (values [0,6,12,18,24] a step-6 linear ramp, period 3, offset 0.5, sigma 2): with a CENTRED offset the weights are symmetric [w,1,w] ~= [0.8007,1,0.8007], so the ALMA of a linear ramp is the window CENTRE exactly -- alma ~= [null,null,6,12,18]. The price (12/18/24) runs above the lagging ALMA (6/12/18) -- aboveCount 3, belowCount 0 in the uptrend. almaFinal ~=18, almaMin ~=6, almaMax ~=18. `runLineAlma` sorts the finite points by x, computes the ALMA, and tags each sample with whether the price sits `above`/`below`/`on` the ALMA. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** `<ChartLineHma>` cancels lag by NESTING three weighted moving averages (`2*WMA(half) - WMA(full)` then a sqrt-length WMA); the ALMA uses ONE window and instead reshapes the weight CURVE -- a Gaussian whose peak is slid toward the recent end by the offset. `<ChartLineZlema>` SHIFTS the price forward by its momentum then EMA-smooths it; the ALMA never shifts the price, tuning responsiveness purely through the offset of the Gaussian weights. `<ChartLineEwma>`/`<ChartLineMovingAvg>` weight a window exponentially or uniformly with the peak FIXED at the most recent bar; the ALMA's peak is freely positioned, and the curve is Gaussian rather than exponential or flat. All exported helpers carry a `LineAlma`/`Alma` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineAlmaPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `period` (the window length; default 9); `offset` (the Gaussian peak position 0-1; default 0.85, an out-of-range value falls back); `sigma` (the Gaussian width divisor; default 6, a non-positive value falls back); `padding` / `tickCount` / `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `priceColor` / `almaColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showAlma`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `alma`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_ALMA_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/OFFSET/SIGMA/PRICE_COLOR/ALMA_COLOR/ABOVE_COLOR/BELOW_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineAlmaFinitePoints`; `normalizeLineAlmaPeriod(period, fallback)`; `computeLineAlmaWeights(period, offset, sigma)` -- the Gaussian window weights (`number[]`); `computeLineAlma(values, period, offset, sigma)` -- returns `{weights, alma}`; `runLineAlma(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, period, offset, sigma, weights, alma, samples, almaFinal, almaMin, almaMax, aboveCount, belowCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, alma, position}`; `computeLineAlmaLayout({data, ...})` -- fits ONE panel whose y-domain spans both the price and the ALMA so the overlay never clips; projects `pricePath`, `almaPath`, `priceDots`, `almaMarkers`; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 samples -> ok=false; `describeLineAlmaChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Arnaud Legoux Moving Average + ALMA + overlay + Gaussian + offset + lag (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-alma-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the ALMA line + every price dot + every marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-alma / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick / -tick-label / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -alma-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-alma / -tooltip-position / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-period / -badge-shape); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-offset`, `data-sigma`, `data-alma-final`, `data-above-count`, `data-below-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; markers expose `data-alma`. Tooltip on a price dot or a marker shows x, value, alma and the position. Config badge in top-left calls out `ALMA p=<period> o=<offset>/s=<sigma>`. The legend has two toggle items (Price / ALMA). 55 vitest cases (getLineAlmaFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + value** + non-array -> [], normalizeLineAlmaPeriod incl. positive integer + floors a fractional + **sub-1 falls back** + non-finite falls back, computeLineAlmaWeights incl. **one weight per slot of the period** + **peaks at the offset position with a unit weight** + **is symmetric for a centred offset** + **keeps every weight positive** + **shifts the weight toward recent bars for a high offset** + **makes the offset slot the heaviest**, computeLineAlma incl. **reproduces a constant series** + **averages the window to the centre on a symmetric ramp** + **keeps every reading inside its window range** + **exposes the window weights** + non-array -> empty arrays, runLineAlma incl. ok + period + offset + sigma + **the ALMA series + window weights** + **final/min/max readings** + **counts the price running above the lagging ALMA** + **classifies each sample by position** + **out-of-range offset falls back** + **sorts by x** + single point / empty -> ok=false + resolves the default config + one sample per point, computeLineAlmaLayout incl. ok + price + ALMA paths + **one marker per defined reading** + **spans the y domain over both the price + the ALMA** + carries the period + offset + sigma + collapsed dims -> ok=false + too little data -> ok=false, describeLineAlmaChart incl. **the indicator vocabulary** + the price-versus-ALMA counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / root data-* / svg + price + ALMA lines / **one marker per defined reading** / config badge period + offset + sigma / two-item legend / hide the price path / hide the ALMA line + markers / **price dots when showDots** + omit by default / empty state / omit config badge / omit legend / ref / displayName / animate flag) all pass under vitest 4.1.5; the Gaussian-weighted ALMA readings are asserted with toBeCloseTo (the exp weights are transcendental). TypeScript clean for the new files (conditional spreads honour `exactOptionalPropertyTypes`). Exported via components/ui barrel. Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/c4-mgr-auto. | 11.608 | UI: chart-line-adl primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.626 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-adl.tsx` ships `<ChartLineAdl>` -- pure-SVG line chart with an **Accumulation Distribution Line (ADL)** cumulative volume-flow panel. The TOP panel renders the close price line; the BOTTOM panel renders the ADL line on its own DATA-DRIVEN y-axis with a dashed **zero reference line** and markers coloured by money flow direction. (The ADL's cumulative scale is unrelated to price, so it gets its own sub-panel rather than overlaying the price axes.) **The computation:** Marc Chaikin's Accumulation/Distribution Line. `computeLineAdlMfm(highs, lows, closes)` is the Money Flow Multiplier `MFM = ((close-low)-(high-close))/(high-low)` -- where the close sits within the bar's high-low range, scaled to [-1,+1]: a close at the HIGH reads +1, at the LOW -1, at the MIDPOINT 0; a zero-range bar reads 0. `computeLineAdl(highs, lows, closes, volumes)` scales each multiplier by volume into Money Flow Volume `MFV = MFM*volume` and accumulates it into a RUNNING CUMULATIVE total `ADL[i] = ADL[i-1] + MFV[i]` with `ADL[0] = MFV[0]`. Unlike the moving-average primitives the ADL has NO WARM-UP -- it is defined from the first bar. **Worked fixture** (high 10, low 0 throughout; close [10,0,5,10,0] at high/low/mid; volume [100,100,100,200,50]): mfm = [1,-1,0,1,-1]; mfv = mfm*volume = [100,-100,0,200,-50]; adl = cumulative = [100,0,0,200,150]. adlFinal 150, adlMin 0, adlMax 200, accumulationCount 2, distributionCount 2. `runLineAdl` sorts the finite points by x, computes the mfm/mfv/adl series, and tags each sample with an `accumulation`/`distribution`/`neutral` flow. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** `<ChartLineObv>` adds or subtracts the WHOLE volume by the close's up/down DIRECTION -- the ADL weights the volume by the Money Flow Multiplier, so a close near the high accumulates more than a close near the low even on identical volume, and a bar that closes mid-range contributes near zero regardless of how large its volume is. `<ChartLineMfi>` is a BOUNDED 0-100 oscillator and `<ChartLineEmv>` DIVIDES by volume; the ADL is an UNBOUNDED CUMULATIVE line that MULTIPLIES the multiplier by volume. The ADL takes no period parameter. All exported helpers carry a `LineAdl`/`Adl` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineAdlPoint[]` (`{x, high, low, close, volume}`); `pricePanelRatio` (the fraction of the height given to the price panel; default 0.5, clamped to [0.3, 0.8]); `gap` (the pixel gap between the stacked panels); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `priceColor` / `adlColor` / `accumulationColor` / `distributionColor` / `zeroColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showAdl`/`showZeroLine`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `adl`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_ADL_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PRICE_COLOR/ADL_COLOR/ACCUMULATION_COLOR/DISTRIBUTION_COLOR/ZERO_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineAdlFinitePoints`; `computeLineAdlMfm(highs, lows, closes)` -- the Money Flow Multiplier series (`number[]`); `computeLineAdl(highs, lows, closes, volumes)` -- returns `{mfm, mfv, adl}`; `runLineAdl(points)` -- returns `{series, mfm, mfv, adl, samples, adlFinal, adlMin, adlMax, accumulationCount, distributionCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, high, low, close, volume, mfm, mfv, adl, flow}`; `computeLineAdlLayout({data, ...})` -- stacks the price panel above the ADL panel, gives the ADL panel a data-driven y-domain; projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `adlPath`, `markers`, the `zeroY` reference line + a `zeroInRange` flag; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 samples -> ok=false; `describeLineAdlChart(data)` -- ARIA summary mentions Accumulation Distribution Line + ADL + money flow + volume + cumulative + accumulation + distribution (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-adl-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the ADL line + every price dot + every marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-adl / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -zero-line / -axes / -axis-group / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -panel-labels / -panel-label / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -adl-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-high / -tooltip-low / -tooltip-close / -tooltip-volume / -tooltip-mfv / -tooltip-adl / -tooltip-flow / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-flow); root mirrors `data-adl-final`, `data-adl-min`, `data-adl-max`, `data-accumulation-count`, `data-distribution-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; axis / tick / grid / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"adl"`; markers expose `data-adl` and `data-flow`. Tooltip on a price dot or a marker shows x, high, low, close, volume, money flow, adl and the flow. Config badge in top-left calls out `ADL cumulative`. The legend has two toggle items (Price / ADL). 53 vitest cases (getLineAdlFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + high + low + close + volume** + non-array -> [], computeLineAdlMfm incl. **plus one when the close sits at the high** + **minus one when the close sits at the low** + **zero when the close sits at the midpoint** + **zero for a zero-range bar** + **across a multi-bar series** + non-array -> [], computeLineAdl incl. **takes the money flow multiplier per bar** + **scales the multiplier by volume into money flow volume** + **accumulates the money flow volume into the ADL** + **seeds the ADL with the first bar money flow volume** + non-array -> empty arrays, runLineAdl incl. ok + **the money flow + ADL series** + **final/min/max readings** + **counts accumulation + distribution bars** + **classifies each sample by money flow direction** + **defines the ADL for every sample with no warm-up** + **sorts by x** + single point / empty -> ok=false + one sample per point, computeLineAdlLayout incl. ok + **stacks the price panel above the ADL panel** + price + ADL paths + **one marker per sample since the ADL has no warm-up** + **a data-driven ADL y domain** + **places the zero line inside the ADL panel when zero is in range** + carries the run stats + collapsed dims -> ok=false + too little data -> ok=false, describeLineAdlChart incl. **the indicator vocabulary** + the accumulation + distribution counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / root data-* / svg + price + ADL lines / **one marker per sample** / **marks the accumulation + distribution markers with their flow** / **the zero line** / both panel labels / two-item legend / config badge / hide the price path / hide the ADL line + markers / hide the zero line / empty state / omit config badge / ref / displayName / animate flag) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for the new files (conditional spreads honour `exactOptionalPropertyTypes`). Exported via components/ui barrel. Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/c4-mgr-auto. | 11.609 | UI: chart-line-chaikin-osc primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.627 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-chaikin-osc.tsx` ships `<ChartLineChaikinOsc>` -- pure-SVG line chart with a **Chaikin Oscillator** panel measuring accumulation-distribution momentum. The TOP panel renders the close price line; the BOTTOM panel renders the oscillator line on a symmetric y-axis with a dashed **zero centerline** and sign-coloured markers (green above zero, red below). **The computation:** Marc Chaikin's Oscillator is the MACD of the Accumulation/Distribution Line. `computeLineChaikinOscMfm(highs, lows, closes)` is the Money Flow Multiplier `((close-low)-(high-close))/(high-low)` (where the close sits in the high-low range, scaled to [-1,+1]; a zero-range bar reads 0). `computeLineChaikinOscAdl(highs, lows, closes, volumes)` scales the multiplier by volume into Money Flow Volume and accumulates the RUNNING cumulative ADL. `computeLineChaikinOscEma(src, period)` is an SMA-seeded EMA. `computeLineChaikinOsc(highs, lows, closes, volumes, fastPeriod, slowPeriod)` takes the oscillator as `EMA(ADL, fastPeriod) - EMA(ADL, slowPeriod)` (defaults fast 3 / slow 10): it swings around zero, positive when accumulation momentum is building, negative when distribution momentum dominates. **Worked fixture** (high 10, low 0 throughout; close at high bars 0-3 then low bars 4-5; volume [4,4,4,4,8,8]; fast 1, slow 3): adl = [4,8,12,16,8,0]; fastEma = EMA(adl,1) = the ADL itself; slowEma = EMA(adl,3) = [.,.,8,12,10,5]; oscillator = fastEma-slowEma = [.,.,4,4,-2,-5]. oscFinal -5, oscMin -5, oscMax 4, positiveCount 2, negativeCount 2. `runLineChaikinOsc` sorts the finite points by x, computes the ADL / fast EMA / slow EMA / oscillator, and classifies each sample with a `positive`/`negative`/`zero` sign. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** `<ChartLineAdl>` plots the CUMULATIVE Accumulation/Distribution Line itself -- the Chaikin Oscillator takes that ADL as its INPUT and plots the DIFFERENCE of two EMAs of it, so it measures the MOMENTUM of money flow rather than its running total. `<ChartLineMacd>` is the difference of two EMAs of the PRICE; the Chaikin Oscillator is the difference of two EMAs of the ADL -- the same EMA-difference shape applied to money flow rather than price. `<ChartLineMfi>`/`<ChartLineObv>` are bounded or cumulative volume measures; the Chaikin Oscillator is an UNBOUNDED momentum oscillator centred on zero. All exported helpers carry a `LineChaikinOsc`/`ChaikinOsc` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineChaikinOscPoint[]` (`{x, high, low, close, volume}`); `fastPeriod` / `slowPeriod` (the EMA periods; defaults 3 / 10); `pricePanelRatio` (the fraction of the height given to the price panel; default 0.54, clamped to [0.3, 0.8]); `gap` (the pixel gap between the stacked panels); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `priceColor` / `oscColor` / `positiveColor` / `negativeColor` / `zeroColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showOscillator`/`showZeroLine`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `osc`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_CHAIKIN_OSC_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/FAST_PERIOD/SLOW_PERIOD/PRICE_COLOR/OSC_COLOR/POSITIVE_COLOR/NEGATIVE_COLOR/ZERO_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineChaikinOscFinitePoints`; `normalizeLineChaikinOscPeriod(period, fallback)`; `computeLineChaikinOscMfm(highs, lows, closes)` -- the Money Flow Multiplier series (`number[]`); `computeLineChaikinOscAdl(highs, lows, closes, volumes)` -- returns `{mfm, mfv, adl}`; `computeLineChaikinOscEma(src, period)` -- the SMA-seeded EMA series (`(number|null)[]`); `computeLineChaikinOsc(highs, lows, closes, volumes, fastPeriod, slowPeriod)` -- returns `{adl, fastEma, slowEma, oscillator}`; `runLineChaikinOsc(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, fastPeriod, slowPeriod, adl, fastEma, slowEma, oscillator, samples, oscFinal, oscMin, oscMax, positiveCount, negativeCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, high, low, close, volume, adl, oscillator, sign}`; `computeLineChaikinOscLayout({data, ...})` -- stacks the price panel above the oscillator panel, derives a symmetric y-bound around zero; projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `oscPath`, `markers`, the `zeroY` centerline; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 samples -> ok=false; `describeLineChaikinOscChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Chaikin Oscillator + Accumulation Distribution + momentum + exponential moving average + difference + zero (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-chaikin-osc-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the oscillator line + every price dot + every marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-chaikin-osc / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -zero-line / -axes / -axis-group / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -panel-labels / -panel-label / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -osc-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-close / -tooltip-adl / -tooltip-osc / -tooltip-sign / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-ema); root mirrors `data-fast-period`, `data-slow-period`, `data-osc-final`, `data-positive-count`, `data-negative-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; axis / tick / grid / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"osc"`; markers expose `data-oscillator` and `data-sign`. Tooltip on a price dot or a marker shows x, close, adl, chaikin and the sign. Config badge in top-left calls out `CHAIKIN <fast>/<slow>`. The legend has two toggle items (Price / Chaikin). 61 vitest cases (getLineChaikinOscFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + high + low + close + volume** + non-array -> [], normalizeLineChaikinOscPeriod incl. positive integer + floors a fractional + **sub-1 falls back** + non-finite falls back, computeLineChaikinOscMfm incl. **plus one when the close sits at the high** + **minus one when the close sits at the low** + **zero when the close sits at the midpoint** + non-array -> [], computeLineChaikinOscAdl incl. **scales the multiplier by volume into money flow volume** + **accumulates the money flow volume into the ADL** + non-array -> empty arrays, computeLineChaikinOscEma incl. **seeds with the simple mean** + **the period-one identity** + **the period-length mean as the seed** + non-array -> [], computeLineChaikinOsc incl. **exposes the ADL** + **takes the fast + slow EMA of the ADL** + **the oscillator as the fast minus the slow EMA** + non-array -> empty arrays, runLineChaikinOsc incl. ok + **the ADL + oscillator series** + **final/min/max readings** + **counts readings above + below zero** + **classifies each sample by sign** + **sorts by x** + single point / empty -> ok=false + resolves the default periods + one sample per point + **exposes the ADL on every sample**, computeLineChaikinOscLayout incl. ok + **stacks the price panel above the oscillator panel** + price + oscillator paths + **one marker per defined reading** + **places the zero line inside the oscillator panel** + **a symmetric y-bound covering the widest reading** + carries the run stats + collapsed dims -> ok=false + too little data -> ok=false, describeLineChaikinOscChart incl. **the indicator vocabulary** + the EMA periods + the reading counts + No data,
