| 11.610 | UI: chart-line-klinger primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.628 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-klinger.tsx` ships `<ChartLineKlinger>` -- pure-SVG line chart with a **Klinger Volume Oscillator (KVO)** panel and signal line. The TOP panel renders the close price line; the BOTTOM panel renders the oscillator line and a dashed **signal** line on a symmetric y-axis with a dashed **zero centerline** and sign-coloured markers (green above zero, red below). **The computation:** Stephen Klinger's Volume Force. `computeLineKlingerVf(highs, lows, closes, volumes)` -- each bar's typical price `hlc = (high+low+close)/3` sets a +1/-1 TREND (`trend[i] = hlc[i] > hlc[i-1] ? +1 : -1`, trend[0] seeded +1); the DAILY MEASUREMENT `dm = high-low` accumulates into a CUMULATIVE MEASUREMENT that RESETS to `dm[i-1]+dm[i]` when the trend flips (else `cm[i-1]+dm[i]`); the VOLUME FORCE `vf = volume * |2*(dm/cm - 1)| * trend * 100` (vf[0]=0; a zero cm reads vf 0). `computeLineKlingerEma(src, period)` is an SMA-seeded EMA. `computeLineKlinger(highs, lows, closes, volumes, fastPeriod, slowPeriod, signalPeriod)` takes the oscillator `KVO = EMA(vf, fastPeriod) - EMA(vf, slowPeriod)` (defaults fast 34 / slow 55) and the SIGNAL line `EMA(KVO, signalPeriod)` (default 13). **Worked fixture** (typical price alternates so the trend flips every bar and the daily/cumulative ratio settles at 0.5, dm constant 6; fast 1, slow 3, signal 3): hlc = [13,7,19,5,23,3]; trend = [1,-1,1,-1,1,-1]; dm = [6,6,6,6,6,6]; cm = [6,12,12,12,12,12]; vf = volume*trend*100 = [0,-100,400,-100,200,-100]; kvo = EMA(vf,1)-EMA(vf,3) = [.,.,300,-100,100,-100]; signal = EMA(kvo,3) = [.,.,.,.,100,0]. kvoFinal -100, signalFinal 0, kvoMin -100, kvoMax 300, positiveCount 2, negativeCount 2. `runLineKlinger` sorts the finite points by x, computes the vf/kvo/signal series, and classifies each sample with a `positive`/`negative`/`zero` sign. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** `<ChartLineChaikinOsc>` is the MACD of the CUMULATIVE Accumulation/Distribution Line -- the Klinger builds a DIFFERENT base series first: a VOLUME FORCE that combines a TREND-RESETTING cumulative measurement with the daily range and volume, and only then takes the EMA difference. `<ChartLineMacd>`/`<ChartLinePpo>` take the EMA difference of the PRICE; the Klinger takes it of the volume force, tracking volume conviction rather than price momentum. `<ChartLineObv>`/`<ChartLineAdl>` are cumulative volume totals; the Klinger is an unbounded EMA-difference oscillator with a signal line. All exported helpers carry a `LineKlinger`/`Klinger` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineKlingerPoint[]` (`{x, high, low, close, volume}`); `fastPeriod` / `slowPeriod` (the volume-force EMA periods; defaults 34 / 55); `signalPeriod` (the signal EMA period; default 13); `pricePanelRatio` (the fraction of the height given to the price panel; default 0.54, clamped to [0.3, 0.8]); `gap` (the pixel gap between the stacked panels); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `priceColor` / `kvoColor` / `signalColor` / `positiveColor` / `negativeColor` / `zeroColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showKvo`/`showSignal`/`showZeroLine`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `kvo` / `signal`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_KLINGER_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/FAST_PERIOD/SLOW_PERIOD/SIGNAL_PERIOD/PRICE_COLOR/KVO_COLOR/SIGNAL_COLOR/POSITIVE_COLOR/NEGATIVE_COLOR/ZERO_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineKlingerFinitePoints`; `normalizeLineKlingerPeriod(period, fallback)`; `computeLineKlingerVf(highs, lows, closes, volumes)` -- returns `{hlc, trend, dm, cm, vf}`; `computeLineKlingerEma(src, period)` -- the SMA-seeded EMA series (`(number|null)[]`); `computeLineKlinger(highs, lows, closes, volumes, fastPeriod, slowPeriod, signalPeriod)` -- returns `{vf, fastEma, slowEma, kvo, signal}`; `runLineKlinger(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, fastPeriod, slowPeriod, signalPeriod, vf, kvo, signal, samples, kvoFinal, signalFinal, kvoMin, kvoMax, positiveCount, negativeCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, high, low, close, volume, vf, kvo, signal, sign}`; `computeLineKlingerLayout({data, ...})` -- stacks the price panel above the oscillator panel, derives a symmetric y-bound around zero; projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `kvoPath`, `signalPath`, `markers`, the `zeroY` centerline; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 samples -> ok=false; `describeLineKlingerChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Klinger Volume Oscillator + volume + trend + signal + exponential moving average + zero (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-klinger-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the oscillator line + every price dot + every marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-klinger / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -zero-line / -axes / -axis-group / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -panel-labels / -panel-label / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -signal-line / -kvo-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-close / -tooltip-volume / -tooltip-vf / -tooltip-kvo / -tooltip-signal / -tooltip-sign / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-ema / -badge-signal); root mirrors `data-fast-period`, `data-slow-period`, `data-signal-period`, `data-kvo-final`, `data-positive-count`, `data-negative-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; axis / tick / grid / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"kvo"`; markers expose `data-kvo` and `data-sign`. Tooltip on a price dot or a marker shows x, close, volume, volume force, kvo, signal and the sign. Config badge in top-left calls out `KLINGER <fast>/<slow> s=<signal>`. The legend has three toggle items (Price / KVO / Signal). 60 vitest cases (getLineKlingerFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + high + low + close + volume** + non-array -> [], average** + **takes the trend as the typical-price direction** + **takes the daily measurement as the high-low range** + **resets the cumulative measurement when the trend flips** + **takes the volume force from volume + trend + the daily ratio** + non-array -> empty arrays, computeLineKlingerEma incl. **seeds with the simple mean** + **the period-one identity** + **skips leading null placeholders** + non-array -> [], computeLineKlinger incl. **exposes the volume force** + **the oscillator as the fast minus slow EMA** + **the signal line as an EMA of the oscillator** + non-array -> empty arrays, runLineKlinger incl. ok + **the volume force/oscillator/signal series** + **final/min/max readings** + **counts readings above + below zero** + **classifies each sample by sign** + **sorts by x** + single point / empty -> ok=false + resolves the default periods + one sample per point + **exposes the volume force on every sample**, computeLineKlingerLayout incl. ok + **stacks the covering the widest reading** + carries the run stats + collapsed dims -> ok=false + too little data -> ok=false, describeLineKlingerChart incl. **the indicator vocabulary** + the EMA periods + the reading counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / root data-* / svg + price + oscillator + signal lines / **one marker per defined reading** / config badge | 11.611 | UI: chart-line-pvt primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.629 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-pvt.tsx` ships `<ChartLinePvt>` -- pure-SVG line chart with a **Price Volume Trend cumulative line**. The close price line occupies the top panel; the bottom panel renders the PVT on its own **data-driven y-axis** with a dashed zero reference line and markers coloured by money-flow direction. **Indicator math:** `computeLinePvt(closes, volumes)` returns `{roc, pvc, pvt}` -- `roc[i] = (close[i] - close[i-1]) / close[i-1]` (index 0 has no prior close so `roc[0]=0`; a zero prior close reads `0`), `pvc[i] = volume[i] * roc[i]` (the bar's price-volume contribution), and `pvt` is the running cumulative total of `pvc` with `pvt[0]=0`. Negative-zero is normalized everywhere `0/negative` or `0*negative` could appear. Like the ADL the PVT has **no warm-up** -- it is defined from the very first bar. **Worked fixture:** `closes = [8,12,6,6,12]`, `volumes = [100,20,40,100,30]` -> `roc [0,0.5,-0.5,0,1]` -> `pvc [0,10,-20,0,30]` -> `pvt [0,10,-10,-10,20]`; `pvtFinal 20`, `pvtMin -10`, `pvtMax 20`, risingCount 2, fallingCount 1. **Distinct from chart-line-obv:** On-Balance Volume adds or subtracts the **whole** bar volume by the close's up/down direction; the PVT adds only the **fraction** of volume proportional to the **size** of the percentage price move, so a 1% move shifts the line a tenth as far as a 10% move on the same volume. **Distinct from chart-line-adl:** the ADL weights volume by where the close sits within the bar's high-low **range**; the PVT weights it by the close-to-close **percentage change** and needs no high or low. **Distinct from chart-line-force-index:** the Force Index multiplies the **absolute** price change by volume and EMA-smooths it; the PVT uses the **percentage** change and is a raw, un-smoothed cumulative total. API: `data: readonly ChartLinePvtPoint[]` (`{x, close, volume}`); **no period parameter** (PVT is parameterless); sizing `width`/`height`/`padding`/`gap`/`pricePanelRatio`/`tickCount`; styling `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`priceColor`/`pvtColor`/`risingColor`/`fallingColor`/`zeroColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots`/`showPvt`/`showZeroLine`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled series visibility (`hiddenSeries`/`defaultHiddenSeries`/`onHiddenSeriesChange`); `formatValue`/`formatX`; `onPointClick({point})`; `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`; standard a11y props. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_PVT_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PRICE_COLOR/PVT_COLOR/RISING_COLOR/FALLING_COLOR/ZERO_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLinePvtFlow` type (`rising`|`falling`|`flat`); `getLinePvtFinitePoints` (drops non-finite x/close/volume); `computeLinePvt(closes, volumes)` -> `{roc, pvc, pvt}` (non-array -> empty arrays); `runLinePvt(points)` -- sorts finite points by x, computes the series, classifies each bar's flow from the `pvc` sign, returns `{series, roc, pvc, pvt, samples, pvtFinal, pvtMin, pvtMax, risingCount, fallingCount, ok}`; ok=false for fewer than 2 points; `computeLinePvtLayout(options)` -- stacks a price panel above a data-driven PVT panel; returns panels, ticks, the price path, the PVT path, flow-coloured markers, and the zero reference line `y` plus a `zeroInRange` flag; `describeLinePvtChart(data)` -- ARIA summary mentioning Price Volume Trend + cumulative + the final PVT + rising/falling counts (No data when fewer than 2 points). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-pvt-aria-desc"` span; price path + price dots `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; PVT line + markers `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}` with the PVT value + flow in the aria-label. data-section on every node; root mirrors `data-pvt-final`, `data-rising-count`, `data-falling-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-empty`, `data-animate`; markers expose `data-pvt` + `data-flow`. Tooltip on price dots and PVT markers shows x, the bold close, the volume, the contribution, the PVT, and the flow for the hovered bar. Config badge in top-left calls out `PVT n=<samples>`. Two-series legend (Price / PVT) with per-series toggle plus a `<n> rising, <m> falling` stat. 50 vitest cases (getLinePvtFinitePoints incl. non-finite x/close/volume drops, computeLinePvt incl. the roc/pvc/pvt fixture series + first-bar zero seed + zero-prior-close -> 0 + non-array -> empty, runLinePvt incl. ok flags + fewer-than-2 -> not ok + series + final/min/max readings + rising/falling counts + flow classification + no-warm-up + sorts unsorted input by x + one sample per point + per-sample contribution, computeLinePvtLayout incl. ok/not-ok guards + price panel stacked above the PVT panel + price/PVT paths + one marker per sample + data-driven y-domain + zero line placement + zeroInRange flag, describeLinePvtChart incl. mentions Price Volume Trend + cumulative + final PVT + rising/falling + No data, component render incl. root region / data-empty / price + PVT lines / flow-tagged markers / zero line + hide via showZeroLine / both panel labels / config badge / two legend items / legend toggle hides the PVT line / hidden series / price dots via showDots / empty state / fade-in class + omit via animate / ref) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for the new files (exactOptionalPropertyTypes-safe conditional spreads). Exported via components/ui barrel. Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/c4-mgr-auto. | 11.612 | UI: chart-line-cmf primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.630 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-cmf.tsx` ships `<ChartLineCmf>` -- pure-SVG line chart with a **Chaikin Money Flow panel summing money-flow volume over a window**. The close price line occupies the top panel; the bottom panel renders the CMF line on a **fixed -1 to +1 y-axis** with a dashed zero centerline and markers coloured by money-flow pressure. **Indicator math:** `computeLineCmfMfm(highs, lows, closes)` is Marc Chaikin's Money Flow Multiplier -- `MFM = ((close - low) - (high - close)) / (high - low)`, where the close sits within the bar's high-low range scaled to [-1, +1] (close at high -> +1, at low -> -1, at midpoint -> 0; a zero-range bar reads 0). `computeLineCmf(highs, lows, closes, volumes, period)` returns `{mfm, mfv, cmf}` -- `mfv = MFM * volume` is the Money Flow Volume, and `CMF[i] = sum(mfv over the trailing period-bar window) / sum(volume over the same window)`, algebraically a **volume-weighted average of the multiplier** so the result is itself **bounded -1 to +1**. CMF is defined from index `period - 1` onward (a `period - 1` bar warm-up); a window with no volume reads 0 rather than dividing by zero; negative-zero is normalized. **Worked fixture:** every bar `high 12, low 4` (range 8) so the multiplier is exact, volumes chosen so any three consecutive sum to 400: `closes [12,4,10,6,12]`, `volumes [100,100,200,100,100]` -> `mfm [1,-1,0.5,-0.5,1]` -> `mfv [100,-100,100,-50,100]`; with `period 3` -> `cmf[2]=(100-100+100)/400=0.25`, `cmf[3]=(-100+100-50)/400=-0.125`, `cmf[4]=(100-50+100)/400=0.375` -> `cmf [null,null,0.25,-0.125,0.375]`; `cmfFinal 0.375`, buyingCount 2, sellingCount 1. **Distinct from chart-line-adl:** the ADL is the **unbounded running cumulative total** of money flow volume from the first bar (no warm-up); the CMF **normalizes a windowed sum** of money flow volume by the windowed sum of volume, producing a bounded -1 to +1 oscillator with a `period - 1` bar warm-up -- same multiplier formula, different accumulation (cumulative-total vs windowed-ratio). **Distinct from chart-line-mfi:** the Money Flow Index works from the **typical price** `(high+low+close)/3` and a RSI-style positive/negative raw-money-flow ratio on a 0-100 scale; the CMF weights volume by the **close's position in the range** and divides by raw volume on a -1..+1 scale. **Distinct from chart-line-chaikin-osc:** the Chaikin Oscillator is the difference of two EMAs of the ADL (a MACD of the cumulative line); the CMF is a single windowed ratio, not an EMA difference. API: `data: readonly ChartLineCmfPoint[]` (`{x, high, low, close, volume}`); `period` (default 20, normalized to a positive integer); sizing `width`/`height`/`padding`/`gap`/`pricePanelRatio`/`tickCount`; styling `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`priceColor`/`cmfColor`/`buyingColor`/`sellingColor`/`zeroColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots`/`showCmf`/`showZeroLine`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled series visibility (`hiddenSeries`/`defaultHiddenSeries`/`onHiddenSeriesChange`); `formatValue`/`formatX`; `onPointClick({point})`; `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`; standard a11y props. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_CMF_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/PRICE_COLOR/CMF_COLOR/BUYING_COLOR/SELLING_COLOR/ZERO_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineCmfPressure` type (`buying`|`selling`|`flat`); `getLineCmfFinitePoints` (drops non-finite x/high/low/close/volume); `normalizeLineCmfPeriod(period, fallback)` (non-finite or sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineCmfMfm(highs, lows, closes)` -> multiplier series; `computeLineCmf(highs, lows, closes, volumes, period)` -> `{mfm, mfv, cmf}` (cmf null through warm-up; non-array -> empty); `runLineCmf(points, options?)` -- sorts finite points by x, computes the series, classifies each bar's pressure from the CMF sign, returns `{series, period, mfm, mfv, cmf, samples, cmfFinal, buyingCount, sellingCount, ok}`; ok=false for fewer than 2 points; `computeLineCmfLayout(options)` -- stacks a price panel above a CMF panel on a fixed -1 to +1 scale; returns panels, ticks, the price path, the CMF path, pressure-coloured markers (only where the CMF is defined), and the centred zero line `y`; `describeLineCmfChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentioning Chaikin Money Flow + money flow multiplier + volume-weighted average + buying/selling pressure + period + buying/selling counts (No data when fewer than 2 points). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-cmf-aria-desc"` span; price path + price dots `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; CMF line + markers `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}` with the CMF value + pressure in the aria-label. data-section on every node; root mirrors `data-period`, `data-cmf-final`, `data-buying-count`, `data-selling-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-empty`, `data-animate`; markers expose `data-cmf` + `data-pressure`. Tooltip on price dots and CMF markers shows x, the high, low, bold close, volume, multiplier, CMF, and pressure for the hovered bar. Config badge in top-left calls out `CMF p=<period> buy=<buyingCount> sell=<sellingCount>`. Two-series legend (Price / CMF) with per-series toggle plus a `<n> buying, <m> selling` stat. 63 vitest cases (getLineCmfFinitePoints incl. non-finite x/high/low/close/volume drops + non-array, normalizeLineCmfPeriod incl. floor + sub-1/NaN/negative fallback, computeLineCmfMfm incl. close at high -> +1 + at low -> -1 + at midpoint -> 0 + fractional multiplier + zero-range bar -> 0 + non-array, computeLineCmf incl. the multiplier / money flow volume / CMF fixture series + null warm-up of period-1 bars + -1..+1 bound + +1 when all bars close at the high + -1 when all close at the low + zero-volume window -> 0 not NaN + no-warm-up when period 1 + non-array -> empty, runLineCmf incl. ok flags + fewer-than-2 / empty / null -> not ok + the mfm/mfv/cmf series + final CMF + buying/selling counts + pressure classification + warm-up null cmf + sorts unsorted input by x + one sample per point + default period 20 -> no CMF for a short series + per-sample multiplier, computeLineCmfLayout incl. ok / not-ok guards + price panel stacked above the CMF panel + price/CMF paths + marker only where CMF defined (3) + priceDots per point (5) + fixed -1..+1 CMF y-axis + zero line centred in the CMF panel + run statistics + degenerate canvas -> not ok + too-little-data -> not ok, describeLineCmfChart incl. mentions Chaikin Money Flow + money flow multiplier + volume-weighted average + buying/selling pressure + buying/selling counts + No data, component render incl. root region / data-empty false + true / data attributes / ARIA desc / price + CMF lines / one marker per defined CMF value (3) / markers tagged with buying/selling pressure / zero line + hide via showZeroLine / both panel labels / config badge period + hide / two legend items / hide price path via hiddenSeries / hide CMF line + markers via showCmf / price dots via showDots / ref / displayName / fade-in toggle via animate) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for the new files (exactOptionalPropertyTypes-safe conditional spreads). Exported via components/ui barrel. Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/c4-mgr-auto. | 11.613 | UI: chart-line-kama primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.631 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-kama.tsx` ships `<ChartLineKama>` -- pure-SVG line chart with a **Kaufman Adaptive Moving Average overlay that speeds up in trends and slows in noise**, drawn on the same axes as the price (single-panel overlay). **Indicator math:** `computeLineKamaEfficiencyRatio(values, erPeriod)` is Perry Kaufman's Efficiency Ratio -- `ER[i] = |value[i] - value[i-erPeriod]| / sum(|value[j]-value[j-1]|)` over the trailing window, which runs **0 for pure noise** (the bar moves cancel out so the net change is small relative to the path travelled) to **1 for a clean one-directional trend** (net change equals the path); a flat window reads 0 rather than dividing by zero; ER is naturally bounded [0,1] by the triangle inequality and defined from index `erPeriod` onward. `computeLineKama(values, erPeriod, fastPeriod, slowPeriod)` returns `{er, sc, kama}` -- `fastSC = 2/(fastPeriod+1)`, `slowSC = 2/(slowPeriod+1)`, the per-bar smoothing constant `SC[i] = (ER[i]*(fastSC-slowSC)+slowSC)^2` (squaring makes it linger near the slow bound unless the trend is genuinely efficient), and the recursive `KAMA[i] = KAMA[i-1] + SC[i]*(value[i]-KAMA[i-1])`. The KAMA is **seeded at index `erPeriod-1` with that bar's price** and is recursive from index `erPeriod` onward. **Worked fixture:** `values [10,14,10,22,30]`, `erPeriod 2`, `fastPeriod 2`, `slowPeriod 30` -> `ER[2]=|10-10|/(|10-14|+|14-10|)=0/8=0` (noise), `ER[3]=|22-14|/(|22-10|+|10-14|)=8/16=0.5`, `ER[4]=|30-10|/(|30-22|+|22-10|)=20/20=1` (trend) -> `er [null,null,0,0.5,1]`; `fastSC 2/3`, `slowSC 2/31`; KAMA seed `KAMA[1]=14`, `KAMA[2]=14+(2/31)^2*(10-14)~=13.983351`, `KAMA[3]=KAMA[2]+(34/93)^2*(22-KAMA[2])~=15.054833`, `KAMA[4]=KAMA[3]+(2/3)^2*(30-KAMA[3])~=21.697129`. ER values are exact; recursive KAMA values accumulate floating-point error (2/3, 2/31 inexact) so tests assert them with `toBeCloseTo` and keep the seed (14) as exact `toBe`. **Distinct from chart-line-zlema / chart-line-hma / chart-line-alma:** those moving-average overlays reduce **lag** with a **fixed** weighting scheme (a de-lagged EMA, a weighted Hull blend, a gaussian-weighted window); the KAMA instead **varies its own responsiveness every bar** -- the smoothing constant is recomputed each bar from the efficiency ratio, fast in a trend and slow in noise. **Distinct from a plain EMA:** an EMA folds each bar in at one fixed weight `2/(period+1)`; the KAMA's weight is the squared, ratio-scaled smoothing constant that collapses toward the slow bound whenever the market is choppy -- no other primitive computes a signal-to-noise efficiency ratio. API: `data: readonly ChartLineKamaPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `erPeriod` (default 10), `fastPeriod` (default 2), `slowPeriod` (default 30), each normalized to a positive integer; sizing `width`/`height`/`padding`/`tickCount`; styling `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`priceColor`/`kamaColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots`/`showKama`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled series visibility (`hiddenSeries`/`defaultHiddenSeries`/`onHiddenSeriesChange`); `formatValue`/`formatX`; `onPointClick({point})`; `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`; standard a11y props. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_KAMA_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/ER_PERIOD/FAST_PERIOD/SLOW_PERIOD/PRICE_COLOR/KAMA_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineKamaPosition` type (`above`|`below`|`on`); `getLineKamaFinitePoints` (drops non-finite x/value); `normalizeLineKamaPeriod(period, fallback)` (non-finite or sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineKamaEfficiencyRatio(values, erPeriod)` -> efficiency ratio series (null through warm-up); `computeLineKama(values, erPeriod, fastPeriod, slowPeriod)` -> `{er, sc, kama}` (non-array -> empty); `runLineKama(points, options?)` -- sorts finite points by x, computes the series, classifies each sample by the price's position vs the KAMA, returns `{series, erPeriod, fastPeriod, slowPeriod, er, sc, kama, samples, kamaFinal, kamaMin, kamaMax, aboveCount, belowCount, ok}`; ok=false for fewer than 2 points; `computeLineKamaLayout(options)` -- projects the price and KAMA onto one shared panel with a y-domain spanning both; returns the panel, ticks, the price path, the KAMA path, and KAMA markers (only where the KAMA is defined); `describeLineKamaChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentioning Kaufman Adaptive Moving Average + efficiency + smoothing constant + trend + noise + above/below counts (No data when fewer than 2 points). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-kama-aria-desc"` span; price path + price dots `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; KAMA line + markers `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}` with the KAMA value in the aria-label. data-section on every node; root mirrors `data-er-period`, `data-fast-period`, `data-slow-period`, `data-kama-final`, `data-above-count`, `data-below-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-empty`, `data-animate`; markers expose `data-kama`. Tooltip on price dots and KAMA markers shows x, the bold value, the KAMA, the efficiency ratio, and the price position for the hovered bar. Config badge in top-left calls out `KAMA er=<erPeriod> sc=<fastPeriod>/<slowPeriod>`. | 11.614 | UI: chart-line-choppiness primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.632 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-choppiness.tsx` ships `<ChartLineChoppiness>` -- pure-SVG line chart with a **Choppiness Index panel rating whether the market trends or ranges**. The close price line occupies the top panel; the bottom panel renders the Choppiness Index on a **fixed 0 to 100 scale** with dashed choppy / trending threshold lines and shaded zones. **Indicator math:** `computeLineChoppinessTrueRange(highs, lows, closes)` is Welles Wilder's True Range -- per bar the greatest of the high-low span, `|high-prevClose|` (gap up) and `|low-prevClose|` (gap down); the first bar has no prior close so it uses the bare high-low span. `computeLineChoppiness(highs, lows, closes, period)` is E.W. Dreiss's Choppiness Index -- over a trailing window of `period` bars `CHOP = 100 * log10( sum(TR over window) / (maxHigh - minLow) ) / log10(period)`: the summed True Range is the total path travelled, the high-low extent is the net ground covered, their ratio runs 1 (the path never shorter than the extent) to `period`, and `log10(ratio)/log10(period)` rescales onto 0..1. A **strong trend** travels little more than its net extent so the ratio is near 1, `log10(1)=0`, and the index sits near **0**; a **choppy range** retraces constantly so the summed range dwarfs the extent and the index climbs toward **100**. The index is defined from index `period-1` onward; a zero-extent window reads 0; the result is clamped to 0..100; a period below 2 leaves it undefined. **Worked fixture:** every bar has a high-low range of 10 and no gaps so each True Range is exactly 10; bars 0-3 climb a clean staircase and bars 4-6 range inside [30,40] -- `highs/lows` give `TR [10,10,10,10,10,10,10]`; with `period 4`, `CHOP[3]` window 0..3 sumTR 40 extent 40 ratio 1 -> 0 (trend), `CHOP[4]` window 1..4 sumTR 40 extent 30 ratio 4/3 -> ~20.752, `CHOP[5]` window 2..5 sumTR 40 extent 20 ratio 2 -> 50, `CHOP[6]` window 3..6 sumTR 40 extent 10 ratio 4 -> 100 (chop) -> `chop [null,null,null,0,~20.752,50,100]`; choppyCount 1, trendingCount 2. True Ranges and ratios are exact; the log10 step makes intermediate index values inexact so tests assert them with `toBeCloseTo` and keep the exact `ratio 1 -> 0` case as `toBe`. **Distinct from chart-line-adx:** ADX rates trend **strength** from directional movement (the +DI/-DI spread); the Choppiness Index makes **no directional judgement at all** -- it only measures how much the price **retraced** relative to the ground it covered, so a strong uptrend and a strong downtrend both read near 0 and only sideways chop reads high. **Distinct from chart-line-atr:** the ATR is the **smoothed True Range**, an unbounded absolute volatility measure; the Choppiness Index uses True Range only as an input, dividing its windowed **sum** by the window extent to produce a **bounded 0..100 ratio** about trend-versus-range, not volatility. **Distinct from chart-line-rsi / chart-line-cmo:** those are momentum oscillators built from up/down price changes; the Choppiness Index is built from True Range and the high-low extent and says nothing about momentum direction. API: `data: readonly ChartLineChoppinessPoint[]` (`{x, high, low, close}`); `period` (default 14, normalized to a positive integer); `choppyThreshold` / `trendingThreshold` (default 61.8 / 38.2, the Fibonacci-derived levels); sizing `width`/`height`/`padding`/`gap`/`pricePanelRatio`/`tickCount`; styling `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`priceColor`/`chopColor`/`choppyColor`/`trendingColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots`/`showChop`/`showZones`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled series visibility (`hiddenSeries`/`defaultHiddenSeries`/`onHiddenSeriesChange`); `formatValue`/`formatX`; `onPointClick({point})`; `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`; standard a11y props. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_CHOPPINESS_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/CHOPPY_THRESHOLD/TRENDING_THRESHOLD/PRICE_COLOR/CHOP_COLOR/CHOPPY_COLOR/TRENDING_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineChoppinessState` type (`choppy`|`trending`|`neutral`); `getLineChoppinessFinitePoints` (drops non-finite x/high/low/close); `normalizeLineChoppinessPeriod(period, fallback)` (non-finite or sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineChoppinessTrueRange(highs, lows, closes)` -> True Range series; `computeLineChoppiness(highs, lows, closes, period)` -> `{trueRange, chop}` (chop null through warm-up; non-array -> empty); `runLineChoppiness(points, options?)` -- sorts finite points by x, computes the series, classifies each sample choppy/trending/neutral against the thresholds, returns `{series, period, choppyThreshold, trendingThreshold, trueRange, chop, samples, chopFinal, choppyCount, trendingCount, ok}`; ok=false for fewer than 2 points; `computeLineChoppinessLayout(options)` -- stacks a price panel above a Choppiness panel on a fixed 0..100 scale; returns panels, ticks, the price path, the Choppiness path, state-coloured markers (only where the index is defined), the two threshold lines, and the two shaded zones; `describeLineChoppinessChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentioning Choppiness Index + true range + trending + ranging + the 0 to 100 scale + choppy/trending counts (No data when fewer than 2 points). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-choppiness-aria-desc"` span; price path + price dots `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; Choppiness line + markers `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}` with the index value + state in the aria-label. data-section on every node; root mirrors `data-period`, `data-chop-final`, `data-choppy-count`, `data-trending-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-empty`, `data-animate`; markers expose `data-chop` + `data-state`; zone rects expose `data-zone`. Tooltip on price dots and Choppiness markers shows x, the high, low, bold close, true range, Choppiness Index, and state for the hovered bar. Config badge in top-left calls out `CHOP p=<period> chop=<choppyCount> trend=<trendingCount>`. Two-series legend (Price / CHOP) with per-series toggle plus a `<n> choppy, <m> trending` stat. 62 vitest cases (getLineChoppinessFinitePoints incl. non-finite x/high/low/close drops + non-array, normalizeLineChoppinessPeriod incl. floor + sub-1/NaN/negative fallback, computeLineChoppinessTrueRange incl. the first-bar high-low span + the no-gap case + a gap up + a gap down + non-array, computeLineChoppiness incl. the true-range series + 0 for a trending staircase window + 100 for a fully retracing window + the intermediate windows + the null period-1 warm-up + the 0..100 bound + the zero-extent window -> 0 not NaN + the undefined period-below-2 case + non-array, runLineChoppiness incl. ok flags + the period and thresholds + the true-range/chop series + the final reading + state classification + choppy/trending counts + the warm-up null chop + custom thresholds + sorts unsorted input by x + the not-ok short series + empty/null -> not ok + default period 14 -> no index for a short series, computeLineChoppinessLayout incl. ok layout + price panel stacked above the Choppiness panel + price/Choppiness paths + marker only where the index defined (4) + priceDots per point (7) + fixed 0..100 Choppiness y-axis + choppy threshold line above the trending line + both zone heights positive + run statistics + collapsed canvas -> not ok + too-little-data -> not ok, showZones / both panel labels / two legend items / config badge period / hide price path via hiddenSeries / hide Choppiness line + markers via showChop / price dots via showDots / empty state / omit | 11.615 | UI: chart-line-rwi primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.633 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-rwi.tsx` ships `<ChartLineRwi>` -- pure-SVG line chart with a **Random Walk Index panel plotting the RWI-high and RWI-low trend-strength lines**. The close price line occupies the top panel; the bottom panel renders the two RWI lines on a **data-driven y-axis** with a dashed threshold reference line. **Indicator math:** `computeLineRwiTrueRange(highs, lows, closes)` is Welles Wilder's True Range (the greatest of the high-low span, `|high-prevClose|`, `|low-prevClose|`; first bar uses the bare span). `computeLineRwi(highs, lows, closes, period)` is E. Michael Poulos's Random Walk Index -- for each bar `i` and each lookback `k` from 2 to `period`, `atr(k)` is the mean True Range over the `k` bars ending at `i`, and the index compares the actual high-low displacement against what a random walk of the same volatility would cover (the ATR scaled by `sqrt(k)`, since a random walk's expected travel grows as the square root of time): `RWI-high = max over k of (high[i]-low[i-k])/(atr(k)*sqrt(k))`, `RWI-low = max over k of (high[i-k]-low[i])/(atr(k)*sqrt(k))`. A reading **above 1** means the price travelled further than random noise would explain -- a real trend. Both lines are clamped non-negative (a negative displacement reads 0) and are defined from index `period` onward (all lookbacks `2..period` need `period` bars of history); a zero-volatility window reads 0 rather than dividing by zero, and a period below 2 leaves the index undefined. **Worked fixture:** seven bars, each with a high-low range of 4 and no gaps so every True Range is exactly 4 and the ATR of any window is 4; the price climbs a clean staircase to a peak at bar 3 then descends -- with `period 3` (lookbacks k=2,3), `RWI-high [.,.,.,2.5/sqrt3,1.5/sqrt3,0.5/sqrt3,0] ~= [.,.,.,1.443376,0.866025,0.288675,0]` and `RWI-low [.,.,.,0,1/sqrt2,sqrt2,2.5/sqrt3] ~= [.,.,.,0,0.707107,1.414214,1.443376]`; uptrendCount 1, downtrendCount 2. True Ranges, displacements and ATRs are exact integers; the sqrt(k) factor makes RWI values irrational so tests assert them with `toBeCloseTo` and keep the exact zeros as `toBe`. **Distinct from chart-line-adx:** ADX rates trend **strength** from smoothed directional movement (the +DI/-DI spread); the RWI benchmarks the price's **displacement** against a random walk of equal volatility -- it asks whether the move is statistically larger than noise, not how persistent the direction has been, and splits the answer into an explicit up line and down line. **Distinct from chart-line-choppiness:** the Choppiness Index divides a single windowed sum of True Range by the window extent for a bounded 0..100 trend-vs-range rating; the RWI takes the **maximum over many lookbacks** of a displacement-to-`sqrt(k)`-ATR ratio, producing an unbounded pair of lines whose dominant member also signals trend **direction**. **Distinct from chart-line-kama:** the KAMA uses an efficiency ratio to **adapt a moving average**; the RWI produces no average at all -- it is a direct statistical-significance score for the move itself. API: `data: readonly ChartLineRwiPoint[]` (`{x, high, low, close}`); `period` (default 8, normalized to a positive integer); `trendThreshold` (default 1, the random-walk boundary); sizing `width`/`height`/`padding`/`gap`/`pricePanelRatio`/`tickCount`; styling `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`priceColor`/`rwiHighColor`/`rwiLowColor`/`thresholdColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots`/`showRwiHigh`/`showRwiLow`/`showThreshold`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled series visibility (`hiddenSeries`/`defaultHiddenSeries`/`onHiddenSeriesChange`; keys `price`/`rwiHigh`/`rwiLow`); `formatValue`/`formatX`; `onPointClick({point})`; `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`; standard a11y props. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_RWI_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/TREND_THRESHOLD/PRICE_COLOR/HIGH_COLOR/LOW_COLOR/THRESHOLD_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineRwiState` type (`uptrend`|`downtrend`|`ranging`); `getLineRwiFinitePoints` (drops non-finite x/high/low/close); `normalizeLineRwiPeriod(period, fallback)` (non-finite or sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineRwiTrueRange(highs, lows, closes)` -> True Range series; `computeLineRwi(highs, lows, closes, period)` -> `{trueRange, rwiHigh, rwiLow}` (RWI lines null through warm-up; non-array -> empty); `runLineRwi(points, options?)` -- sorts finite points by x, computes the series, classifies each sample uptrend/downtrend/ranging from which line leads above the threshold, returns `{series, period, trendThreshold, trueRange, rwiHigh, rwiLow, samples, rwiHighFinal, rwiLowFinal, uptrendCount, downtrendCount, ok}`; ok=false for fewer than 2 points; `computeLineRwiLayout(options)` -- stacks a price panel above an RWI panel with a zero-anchored y-domain; returns panels, ticks, the price path, the two RWI paths, per-line markers (only where defined), and the threshold line; `describeLineRwiChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentioning Random Walk Index + random walk + true range + the square root scaling + uptrend/downtrend counts (No data when fewer than 2 points). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-rwi-aria-desc"` span; price path + price dots `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; both RWI lines + markers `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}` with the RWI value in the aria-label. data-section on every node; root mirrors `data-period`, `data-rwi-high-final`, `data-rwi-low-final`, `data-uptrend-count`, `data-downtrend-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-empty`, `data-animate`; markers expose `data-value` + `data-line` (high/low). Tooltip on price dots and RWI markers shows x, the close, both RWI readings, and the state for the hovered bar. Config badge in top-left calls out `RWI p=<period> up=<uptrendCount> down=<downtrendCount>`. Three-series legend (Price / RWI-H / RWI-L) with per-series toggle plus a `<n> uptrend, <m> downtrend` stat. 62 vitest cases (getLineRwiFinitePoints incl. non-finite x/high/low/close drops + non-array, normalizeLineRwiPeriod incl. floor + sub-1/NaN/negative fallback, computeLineRwiTrueRange incl. the first-bar span + the no-gap case + a gap + non-array, computeLineRwi incl. the true-range series + the RWI-high and RWI-low lines against the hand-verified fixture + the max-over-lookbacks selection + the null warm-up + the non-negative clamp + the zero-volatility window -> 0 not NaN + the undefined period-below-2 case + non-array, runLineRwi incl. ok flags + the period and threshold + the series + final readings + state classification + uptrend/downtrend counts + the warm-up null readings + a custom threshold + sorts unsorted input by x + the not-ok short series + empty/null -> not ok + one sample per point + default period 8 -> no index for a short series, computeLineRwiLayout incl. ok layout + price panel stacked above the RWI panel + price/RWI-high/RWI-low paths + per-line markers only where defined (4 each) + priceDots per point (7) + zero-anchored RWI y-domain + threshold line inside the panel + run statistics + collapsed canvas -> not ok + too-little-data -> not ok, describeLineRwiChart incl. mentions Random Walk Index + random walk + true range + square root + uptrend/downtrend counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / data attributes / svg + price + both RWI lines / per-line markers (4 high + 4 low) / the threshold line + hide via showThreshold / both panel labels / three legend items / config badge period / hide price path via hiddenSeries / hide RWI-high line + markers via showRwiHigh / hide RWI-low line via hiddenSeries / onSeriesToggle payload / price dots via showDots / empty state / omit config badge / ref / displayName / animate flag) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. | 11.616 | UI: chart-line-dema primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.634 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-dema.tsx` ships `<ChartLineDema>` -- pure-SVG line chart with a **Double Exponential Moving Average overlay that cuts lag versus a plain EMA**, drawn on the same axes as the price (single-panel overlay). **Indicator math:** `computeLineDemaEma(src, period)` is the project's null-tolerant SMA-seeded EMA -- it collects the defined values, seeds with the simple mean of the first `period` of them at that value's index, then folds each later value in at weight `2/(period+1)`. `computeLineDema(values, period)` is Patrick Mulloy's Double Exponential Moving Average: `ema1 = EMA(value, period)`, `ema2 = EMA(ema1, period)`, `DEMA = 2*ema1 - ema2`. A plain EMA lags the price; the EMA of that EMA lags twice as much, so the gap `ema1 - ema2` is itself a measure of the lag, and adding it back (`2*ema1 - ema2`) cancels most of the lag while keeping the line smooth. The first EMA is defined from index `period-1`; the EMA-of-EMA needs another `period` defined values, so the **DEMA is defined from index `2*period-2` onward**. Because the DEMA is a second-order lag corrector it reproduces a linear price ramp with **zero lag** once warmed up. **Worked fixture:** `values [3,6,9,12,15,18,21]` (a perfect linear ramp), `period 3` -- the EMA multiplier is exactly `2/(3+1)=0.5` and every window sum divides cleanly so the whole pipeline is bit-exact: `ema1 [.,.,6,9,12,15,18]`, `ema2 [.,.,.,.,9,12,15]`, `dema = 2*ema1-ema2 [.,.,.,.,15,18,21]`; the DEMA reproduces the ramp value exactly from index 4 onward (`dema[4]=15=value[4]`, `dema[6]=21=value[6]`), so the series is asserted with `toEqual` rather than `toBeCloseTo`. **Distinct from chart-line-zlema:** the ZLEMA removes lag by feeding a **de-lagged input** (the price plus its own recent momentum) through a single EMA; the DEMA leaves the input untouched and removes lag through an **algebraic combination of two EMAs** `2*EMA - EMA(EMA)` -- no momentum term, just the EMA-of-EMA correction. **Distinct from chart-line-kama:** the KAMA **adapts** its smoothing every bar from an efficiency ratio; the DEMA applies a **fixed** two-EMA formula regardless of market conditions. **Distinct from a plain EMA / a triple EMA (TEMA):** a plain EMA has no lag correction; the DEMA adds one `EMA - EMA(EMA)` correction term; a TEMA would add a further `EMA(EMA(EMA))` stage -- the DEMA stops at the double stage. API: `data: readonly ChartLineDemaPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `period` (default 20, normalized to a positive integer); sizing `width`/`height`/`padding`/`tickCount`; styling `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`priceColor`/`demaColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots`/`showDema`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled series visibility (`hiddenSeries`/`defaultHiddenSeries`/`onHiddenSeriesChange`; keys `price`/`dema`); `formatValue`/`formatX`; `onPointClick({point})`; `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`; standard a11y props. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_DEMA_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/PRICE_COLOR/DEMA_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineDemaPosition` type (`above`|`below`|`on`); `getLineDemaFinitePoints` (drops non-finite x/value); `normalizeLineDemaPeriod(period, fallback)` (non-finite or sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineDemaEma(src, period)` -> the null-tolerant EMA series; `computeLineDema(values, period)` -> `{ema1, ema2, dema}` (non-array -> empty); `runLineDema(points, options?)` -- sorts finite points by x, computes the series, classifies each sample by the price's position vs the DEMA, returns `{series, period, ema1, ema2, dema, samples, demaFinal, demaMin, demaMax, aboveCount, belowCount, ok}`; ok=false for fewer than 2 points; `computeLineDemaLayout(options)` -- projects the price and DEMA onto one shared panel with a y-domain spanning both; returns the panel, ticks, the price path, the DEMA path, and DEMA markers (only where the DEMA is defined); `describeLineDemaChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentioning Double Exponential Moving Average + lag + exponential moving average + the `2*EMA - EMA(EMA)` formula + above/below counts (No data when fewer than 2 points). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-dema-aria-desc"` span; price path + price dots `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; DEMA line + markers `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}` with the DEMA value in the aria-label. data-section on every node; root mirrors `data-period`, `data-dema-final`, `data-above-count`, `data-below-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-empty`, `data-animate`; markers expose `data-dema`. Tooltip on price dots and DEMA markers shows x, the bold value, the DEMA, and the price position for the hovered bar. Config badge in top-left calls out `DEMA p=<period>`. Two-series legend (Price / DEMA) with per-series toggle plus an `<n> above, <m> below` stat. 57 vitest cases (getLineDemaFinitePoints incl. non-finite x/value drops + non-array, normalizeLineDemaPeriod incl. floor + sub-1/NaN/negative fallback, computeLineDemaEma incl. the SMA seed then fold + the period-length mean + the period-one identity + skipping leading null placeholders + the all-null short series + non-array, computeLineDema incl. the ema1/ema2/dema series against the hand-verified fixture + the zero-lag reproduction of a linear ramp + the null warm-up + the flat-series constant + non-array -> empty, runLineDema incl. ok flags + the period + the series + final/min/max readings + the on-the-DEMA position for a linear ramp + above/below counts for an oscillating series + the warm-up null DEMA + sorts unsorted input by x + the not-ok short series + empty/null -> not ok + one sample per point + default period 20 -> no DEMA for a short series, computeLineDemaLayout incl. ok layout + price/DEMA paths + marker only where DEMA defined (3) + priceDots per point (7) + shared y-domain covering price + DEMA + run statistics + marker containment + collapsed canvas -> not ok + too-little-data -> not ok, describeLineDemaChart incl. mentions Double Exponential Moving Average + lag + exponential moving average + EMA(EMA) + above/below counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / data attributes / svg + price + DEMA lines / one marker per defined DEMA value (3) / two legend items / config badge period / hide price path via hiddenSeries / hide DEMA line + markers via showDema / hide DEMA via hiddenSeries / onSeriesToggle payload / price dots via showDots / empty state / omit config badge / ref / displayName / animate flag) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for the new files (exactOptionalPropertyTypes-safe conditional spreads). Exported via components/ui barrel. Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/c4-mgr-auto. | 11.617 | UI: chart-line-tema primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.635 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-tema.tsx` ships `<ChartLineTema>` -- pure-SVG line chart with a **Triple Exponential Moving Average overlay for very low lag smoothing**, drawn on the same axes as the price (single-panel overlay). **Indicator math:** `computeLineTemaEma(src, period)` is the project's null-tolerant SMA-seeded EMA -- it collects the defined values, seeds with the simple mean of the first `period` of them at that value's index, then folds each later value in at weight `2/(period+1)`. `computeLineTema(values, period)` is Patrick Mulloy's Triple Exponential Moving Average: `ema1 = EMA(value, period)`, `ema2 = EMA(ema1, period)`, `ema3 = EMA(ema2, period)`, `TEMA = 3*ema1 - 3*ema2 + ema3`. A plain EMA lags the price; nesting three EMAs and combining them with the `3/-3/1` coefficients cancels the lag to third order, driving it lower than a double EMA can while keeping the line smooth. EMA1 is defined from index `period-1`; each later nesting needs another `period` defined values, so EMA2 begins at `2*period-2`, EMA3 at `3*period-3`, and the **TEMA is defined from index `3*period-3` onward**. Because the TEMA is a third-order lag corrector it reproduces a linear price ramp with **zero lag** once warmed up. **Worked fixture:** `values [3,6,9,12,15,18,21,24,27,30]` (a perfect linear ramp), `period 3` -- the EMA multiplier is exactly `2/(3+1)=0.5` and every window sum divides cleanly so the whole three-stage pipeline is bit-exact: `ema1 [.,.,6,9,12,15,18,21,24,27]`, `ema2 [.,.,.,.,9,12,15,18,21,24]`, `ema3 [.,.,.,.,.,.,12,15,18,21]`, `tema = 3*ema1-3*ema2+ema3 [.,.,.,.,.,.,21,24,27,30]`; the TEMA reproduces the ramp value exactly from index 6 onward (`tema[6]=21=value[6]`, `tema[9]=30=value[9]`), so the series is asserted with `toEqual` rather than `toBeCloseTo`. **Distinct from chart-line-dema:** the DEMA stops at two EMA stages (`2*EMA - EMA(EMA)`, a second-order lag corrector); the TEMA adds a third nested EMA and uses the `3/-3/1` coefficient combination for a third-order correction -- lower lag, but a longer `3*period-3` warm-up against the DEMA's `2*period-2`. **Distinct from chart-line-zlema:** the ZLEMA removes lag by feeding a **de-lagged input** (the price plus its own recent momentum) through a single EMA; the TEMA leaves the input untouched and removes lag through an **algebraic combination of three nested EMAs**. **Distinct from chart-line-kama:** the KAMA **adapts** its smoothing every bar from an efficiency ratio; the TEMA applies a **fixed** three-EMA formula regardless of market conditions. API: `data: readonly ChartLineTemaPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `period` (default 14, normalized to a positive integer); sizing `width`/`height`/`padding`/`tickCount`; styling `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`priceColor`/`temaColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots`/`showTema`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled series visibility (`hiddenSeries`/`defaultHiddenSeries`/`onHiddenSeriesChange`; keys `price`/`tema`); `formatValue`/`formatX`; `onPointClick({point})`; `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`; standard a11y props. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_TEMA_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/PRICE_COLOR/TEMA_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineTemaPosition` type (`above`|`below`|`on`); `getLineTemaFinitePoints` (drops non-finite x/value); `normalizeLineTemaPeriod(period, fallback)` (non-finite or sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineTemaEma(src, period)` -> the null-tolerant EMA series; `computeLineTema(values, period)` -> `{ema1, ema2, ema3, tema}` (non-array -> empty); `runLineTema(points, options?)` -- sorts finite points by x, computes the series, classifies each sample by the price's position vs the TEMA, returns `{series, period, ema1, ema2, ema3, tema, samples, temaFinal, temaMin, temaMax, aboveCount, belowCount, ok}`; ok=false for fewer than 2 points; `computeLineTemaLayout(options)` -- projects the price and TEMA onto one shared panel with a y-domain spanning both; returns the panel, ticks, the price path, the TEMA path, and TEMA markers (only where the TEMA is defined); `describeLineTemaChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentioning Triple Exponential Moving Average + lag + exponential moving average + the `3*EMA - 3*EMA(EMA) + EMA(EMA(EMA))` formula + above/below counts (No data when fewer than 2 points). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-tema-aria-desc"` span; price path + price dots `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; TEMA line + markers `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}` with the TEMA value in the aria-label. data-section on every node; root mirrors `data-period`, `data-tema-final`, `data-above-count`, `data-below-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-empty`, `data-animate`; markers expose `data-tema`. Tooltip on price dots and TEMA markers shows x, the bold value, the TEMA, and the price position for the hovered bar. Config badge in top-left calls out `TEMA p=<period>`. Two-series legend (Price / TEMA) with per-series toggle plus an `<n> above, <m> below` stat. 58 vitest cases (getLineTemaFinitePoints incl. non-finite x/value drops + non-array, normalizeLineTemaPeriod incl. floor + sub-1/NaN/negative fallback, computeLineTemaEma incl. the SMA seed then fold + the period-length mean + the period-one identity + skipping leading null placeholders + the all-null short series + non-array, computeLineTema incl. the ema1/ema2/ema3/tema series against the hand-verified fixture + the zero-lag reproduction of a linear ramp + the null warm-up + the flat-series constant + non-array -> empty, runLineTema incl. ok flags + the period + the series + final/min/max readings + the on-the-TEMA position for a linear ramp + above/below counts for an oscillating series + the warm-up null TEMA + sorts unsorted input by x + the not-ok short series + empty/null -> not ok + one sample per point + default period 14 -> no TEMA for a short series, computeLineTemaLayout incl. ok layout + price/TEMA paths + marker only where TEMA defined (4) + priceDots per point (10) + shared y-domain covering price + TEMA + run statistics + marker containment + collapsed canvas -> not ok + too-little-data -> not ok, describeLineTemaChart incl. mentions Triple Exponential Moving Average + lag + exponential moving average + 3 * EMA + above/below counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / data attributes / svg + price + TEMA lines / one marker per defined TEMA value (4) / two legend items / config badge period / hide price path via hiddenSeries / hide TEMA line + markers via showTema / hide TEMA via hiddenSeries / onSeriesToggle payload / price dots via showDots / empty state / omit config badge / ref / displayName / animate flag) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. | 11.618 | UI: chart-line-wma primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.636 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-wma.tsx` ships `<ChartLineWma>` -- pure-SVG line chart with a **linearly Weighted Moving Average overlay**, drawn on the same axes as the price (single-panel overlay). **Indicator math:** `computeLineWma(values, period)` is the linearly Weighted Moving Average -- over a trailing window of `period` values each value is weighted by its recency (the oldest in the window carries weight 1, the next 2, ..., the newest weight `period`), and the weighted sum is divided by the triangular weight total `period*(period+1)/2`. Because the newest value carries the heaviest weight the WMA reacts to the latest price faster than a simple equal-weight moving average. It is a **non-recursive fixed-window** average (carries no running state) and is defined from index `period-1` onward; a period of 1 reproduces the series. **Worked fixture:** `values [6,6,12,12,6,6]`, `period 3` -- the weight total is `3*(3+1)/2 = 6` and the window values divide cleanly so the WMA is bit-exact: `WMA[2]=(1*6+2*6+3*12)/6=54/6=9`, `WMA[3]=(1*6+2*12+3*12)/6=66/6=11`, `WMA[4]=(1*12+2*12+3*6)/6=54/6=9`, `WMA[5]=(1*12+2*6+3*6)/6=42/6=7` -> `wma [null,null,9,11,9,7]`; the price climbs above the WMA on the way up and drops below it on the way down (aboveCount 2, belowCount 2). Every value is exact so the series is asserted with `toEqual`; a separate case shows the recency weighting -- the window `[1,1,4]` yields `(1+2+12)/6=2.5`, pulled above the simple mean `(1+1+4)/3=2` because the newest value 4 carries the heaviest weight. **Distinct from a simple moving average:** the SMA weights every value in the window equally; the WMA weights them by a **linear ramp** so the newest bar moves the average `period` times as much as the oldest. **Distinct from chart-line-alma:** the ALMA weights the window with a **gaussian** curve centred by an offset parameter; the WMA uses a plain **linear** ramp peaking at the newest bar. **Distinct from chart-line-dema / chart-line-tema:** those combine recursive **exponential** moving averages to cancel lag; the WMA is a single **non-recursive** fixed-window average carrying no running state (the Hull moving average is itself composed from several WMAs -- the WMA is the building block, not a competitor). API: `data: readonly ChartLineWmaPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `period` (default 20, normalized to a positive integer); sizing `width`/`height`/`padding`/`tickCount`; styling `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`priceColor`/`wmaColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots`/`showWma`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled series visibility (`hiddenSeries`/`defaultHiddenSeries`/`onHiddenSeriesChange`; keys `price`/`wma`); `formatValue`/`formatX`; `onPointClick({point})`; `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`; standard a11y props. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_WMA_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/PRICE_COLOR/WMA_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineWmaPosition` type (`above`|`below`|`on`); `getLineWmaFinitePoints` (drops non-finite x/value); `normalizeLineWmaPeriod(period, fallback)` (non-finite or sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineWma(values, period)` -> the WMA series (null through warm-up; non-array -> empty); `runLineWma(points, options?)` -- sorts finite points by x, computes the series, classifies each sample by the price's position vs the WMA, returns `{series, period, wma, samples, wmaFinal, wmaMin, wmaMax, aboveCount, belowCount, ok}`; ok=false for fewer than 2 points; `computeLineWmaLayout(options)` -- projects the price and WMA onto one shared panel with a y-domain spanning both; returns the panel, ticks, the price path, the WMA path, and WMA markers (only where the WMA is defined); `describeLineWmaChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentioning Weighted Moving Average + weight + recency + the simple equal-weight moving average comparison + above/below counts (No data when fewer than 2 points). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-wma-aria-desc"` span; price path + price dots `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; WMA line + markers `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}` with the WMA value in the aria-label. data-section on every node; root mirrors `data-period`, `data-wma-final`, `data-above-count`, `data-below-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-empty`, `data-animate`; markers expose `data-wma`. Tooltip on price dots and WMA markers shows x, the bold value, the WMA, and the price position for the hovered bar. Config badge in top-left calls out `WMA p=<period>`. Two-series legend (Price / WMA) with per-series toggle plus an `<n> above, <m> below` stat. 52 vitest cases (getLineWmaFinitePoints incl. non-finite x/value drops + non-array, normalizeLineWmaPeriod incl. floor + sub-1/NaN/negative fallback, computeLineWma incl. the weighted-average series against the hand-verified fixture + the null warm-up + the recency weighting (`[1,1,4]` -> 2.5) + the flat-series constant + the period-one identity + non-array + the shorter-than-period series, runLineWma incl. ok flags + the period + the series + final/min/max readings + position classification + above/below counts + the warm-up null WMA + sorts unsorted input by x + the not-ok short series + empty/null -> not ok + one sample per point + default period 20 -> no WMA for a short series, computeLineWmaLayout incl. ok layout + price/WMA paths + marker only where WMA defined (4) + priceDots per point (6) + shared y-domain covering price + WMA + run statistics + marker containment + collapsed canvas -> not ok + too-little-data -> not ok, describeLineWmaChart incl. mentions Weighted Moving Average + weight + recency + the simple equal-weight comparison + above/below counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / data attributes / svg + price + WMA lines / one marker per defined WMA value (4) / two legend items with distinct series ids / config badge period / hide price path via hiddenSeries / hide WMA line + markers via showWma / hide WMA via hiddenSeries / onSeriesToggle payload / price dots via showDots / empty state / omit config badge / ref / displayName / animate flag) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. | 11.619 | UI: chart-line-trima primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.637 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-trima.tsx` ships `<ChartLineTrima>` -- pure-SVG line chart with a **Triangular Moving Average overlay (a doubly-smoothed average)**, drawn on the same axes as the price (single-panel overlay). **Indicator math:** `lineTrimaSubPeriods(period)` resolves the two simple-moving-average sub-periods -- for an **odd** period both span `(period+1)/2`; for an **even** period the first spans `period/2+1` and the second `period/2`; either way they sum to `period+1` so the combined warm-up is `period-1` (the same as a plain SMA). `computeLineTrimaSma(src, period)` is a null-tolerant windowed simple moving average (a window containing any null reads null). `computeLineTrima(values, period)` is the Triangular Moving Average: `inner = SMA(values, p1)`, `TRIMA = SMA(inner, p2)` -- the price run through a simple moving average, and that average run through a second simple moving average. Composing the two box-car averages makes the effective weights form a **triangle peaking in the middle of the window**, so the TRIMA emphasises the centre of the window and produces an exceptionally smooth line. **Worked fixture:** `values [4,8,16,8,4,8,16]`, `period 3` -- an odd period splits into two sub-averages of 2 so the TRIMA is `SMA(SMA(values,2),2)` and with these window values dividing cleanly stays bit-exact: `inner = SMA(values,2) [null,6,12,12,6,6,12]`, `trima = SMA(inner,2) [null,null,9,12,9,6,9]`; the price runs above the TRIMA on the spikes and below it in the troughs (aboveCount 3, belowCount 2); every value is exact so the series is asserted with `toEqual`. **Distinct from chart-line-wma:** the WMA weights the window with a linear ramp peaking at the **newest** bar; the TRIMA's triangular weights peak in the **middle** of the window, so it lags more but smooths more. **Distinct from a simple moving average:** the SMA weights every value in the window equally; the TRIMA is the SMA **of an SMA**, giving the triangular centre-weighted profile -- it smooths twice. **Distinct from chart-line-dema / chart-line-tema:** those combine recursive exponential averages to **cut** lag; the TRIMA deliberately **adds** smoothing -- its goal is a maximally smooth line, the opposite trade-off. API: `data: readonly ChartLineTrimaPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `period` (default 20, normalized to a positive integer); sizing `width`/`height`/`padding`/`tickCount`; styling `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`priceColor`/`trimaColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots`/`showTrima`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled series visibility (`hiddenSeries`/`defaultHiddenSeries`/`onHiddenSeriesChange`; keys `price`/`trima`); `formatValue`/`formatX`; `onPointClick({point})`; `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`; standard a11y props. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_TRIMA_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/PRICE_COLOR/TRIMA_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineTrimaPosition` type (`above`|`below`|`on`); `getLineTrimaFinitePoints` (drops non-finite x/value); `normalizeLineTrimaPeriod(period, fallback)` (non-finite or sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `lineTrimaSubPeriods(period)` -> `{first, second}` (the two SMA sub-periods, summing to period+1); `computeLineTrimaSma(src, period)` -> null-tolerant windowed SMA; `computeLineTrima(values, period)` -> `{inner, trima}` (non-array -> empty); `runLineTrima(points, options?)` -- sorts finite points by x, computes the series, classifies each sample by the price's position vs the TRIMA, returns `{series, period, firstPeriod, secondPeriod, inner, trima, samples, trimaFinal, trimaMin, trimaMax, aboveCount, belowCount, ok}`; ok=false for fewer than 2 points; `computeLineTrimaLayout(options)` -- projects the price and TRIMA onto one shared panel with a y-domain spanning both; returns the panel, ticks, the price path, the TRIMA path, and TRIMA markers (only where the TRIMA is defined); `describeLineTrimaChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentioning Triangular Moving Average + doubly-smoothed + simple moving average + the triangle weighting + above/below counts (No data when fewer than 2 points). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-trima-aria-desc"` span; price path + price dots `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; TRIMA line + markers `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}` with the TRIMA value in the aria-label. data-section on every node; root mirrors `data-period`, `data-first-period`, `data-second-period`, `data-trima-final`, `data-above-count`, `data-below-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-empty`, `data-animate`; markers expose `data-trima`. Tooltip on price dots and TRIMA markers shows x, the bold value, the TRIMA, and the price position for the hovered bar. Config badge in top-left calls out `TRIMA p=<period> sma=<p1>/<p2>`. Two-series legend (Price / TRIMA) with per-series toggle plus an `<n> above, <m> below` stat. 57 vitest cases (getLineTrimaFinitePoints incl. non-finite x/value drops + non-array, normalizeLineTrimaPeriod incl. floor + sub-1/NaN/negative fallback, lineTrimaSubPeriods incl. the odd equal split + the even larger-half-first split + the sum-to-period+1 invariant, computeLineTrimaSma incl. the windowed average + the null-in-window -> null case + the period-one identity + non-array, computeLineTrima incl. the inner and TRIMA series against the hand-verified fixture + the null warm-up + the flat-series constant + the even-period warm-up boundary + non-array -> empty, runLineTrima incl. ok flags + the period and sub-periods + the series + final/min/max readings + position classification + above/below counts + the warm-up null TRIMA + sorts unsorted input by x + the not-ok short series + empty/null -> not ok + one sample per point + default period 20 -> no TRIMA for a short series, computeLineTrimaLayout incl. ok layout + price/TRIMA paths + marker only where TRIMA defined (5) + priceDots per point (7) + shared y-domain covering price + TRIMA + run statistics + marker containment + collapsed canvas -> not ok + too-little-data -> not ok, describeLineTrimaChart incl. mentions Triangular Moving Average + doubly-smoothed + simple moving average + triangle + above/below counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / data attributes / svg + price + TRIMA lines / one marker per defined TRIMA value (5) / two legend items / config badge period + sub-periods / hide price path via hiddenSeries / hide TRIMA line + markers via showTrima / hide TRIMA via hiddenSeries / onSeriesToggle payload / price dots via showDots / empty state / omit config badge / ref / displayName / animate flag) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. | 11.620 | UI: chart-line-vidya primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.638 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-vidya.tsx` ships `<ChartLineVidya>` -- pure-SVG line chart with a **Variable Index Dynamic Average overlay whose smoothing adapts to volatility**, drawn on the same axes as the price (single-panel overlay). **Indicator math:** `computeLineVidyaCmo(values, cmoPeriod)` is Tushar Chande's Momentum Oscillator -- over a window of `cmoPeriod` price changes, `CMO = 100*(sumUp-sumDown)/(sumUp+sumDown)`, running -100 (all losses) to +100 (all gains); defined from index `cmoPeriod`. `computeLineVidya(values, period, cmoPeriod)` is Chande's Variable Index Dynamic Average -- an exponential moving average whose smoothing constant is scaled bar by bar by a **volatility index** `k = |CMO|/100` (runs 0..1): `VIDYA[i] = alpha*k*value[i] + (1-alpha*k)*VIDYA[i-1]` where `alpha = 2/(period+1)` is the base smoothing constant. A strong trend pushes the CMO toward +/-100 so `k` approaches 1 and the average tracks the price closely; a directionless market pushes the CMO toward 0 so `k` approaches 0 and the average **freezes**, holding its prior value. The VIDYA is seeded at index `cmoPeriod-1` with that bar's price and is recursive from index `cmoPeriod` onward. **Worked fixture:** `values [10,14,18,14,18,14,10]`, `period 3`, `cmoPeriod 2` -- the base smoothing constant is exactly `2/(3+1)=0.5` and the 2-change CMO windows come out to clean 100/0/-100, so the volatility index is 1/0/1 and the whole pipeline is bit-exact: `changes [_,+4,+4,-4,+4,-4,-4]` -> `cmo [_,_,100,0,0,0,-100]` -> `k [_,_,1,0,0,0,1]`; VIDYA seeded at index 1 with `value[1]=14`, then `VIDYA[2]=0.5*1*18+0.5*14=16`, `VIDYA[3..5]` frozen at 16 (k=0, the price oscillating so CMO=0), `VIDYA[6]=0.5*1*10+0.5*16=13` -> `vidya [null,14,16,16,16,16,13]`; the fixture exercises the defining behaviour -- at indices 3-5 the price oscillates so the VIDYA freezes at 16, and at the trending bars it tracks; every value is exact so the series is asserted with `toEqual`. **Distinct from chart-line-kama:** both are adaptive recursive averages, but the KAMA scales its smoothing by an **efficiency ratio** and its squared smoothing constant never reaches zero (a slow bound); the VIDYA scales by the **absolute Chande Momentum Oscillator** so a directionless market drives the index to 0 and the line fully **freezes** -- something the KAMA cannot do. **Distinct from chart-line-ema / dema / tema / wma / trima:** those use a **fixed** smoothing scheme; the VIDYA's smoothing varies bar to bar with the volatility index. **Distinct from chart-line-cmo:** that primitive **plots** the CMO as an oscillator panel; the VIDYA **consumes** the CMO internally as a volatility gauge to scale an exponential average -- it never plots the oscillator itself. API: `data: readonly ChartLineVidyaPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `period` (default 14), `cmoPeriod` (default 9), each normalized to a positive integer; sizing `width`/`height`/`padding`/`tickCount`; styling `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`priceColor`/`vidyaColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots`/`showVidya`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled series visibility (`hiddenSeries`/`defaultHiddenSeries`/`onHiddenSeriesChange`; keys `price`/`vidya`); `formatValue`/`formatX`; `onPointClick({point})`; `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`; standard a11y props. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_VIDYA_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/CMO_PERIOD/PRICE_COLOR/VIDYA_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineVidyaPosition` type (`above`|`below`|`on`); `getLineVidyaFinitePoints` (drops non-finite x/value); `normalizeLineVidyaPeriod(period, fallback)` (non-finite or sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineVidyaCmo(values, cmoPeriod)` -> the Chande Momentum Oscillator series; `computeLineVidya(values, period, cmoPeriod)` -> `{cmo, k, vidya}` (non-array -> empty); `runLineVidya(points, options?)` -- sorts finite points by x, computes the series, classifies each sample by the price's position vs the VIDYA, returns `{series, period, cmoPeriod, cmo, k, vidya, samples, vidyaFinal, vidyaMin, vidyaMax, aboveCount, belowCount, ok}`; ok=false for fewer than 2 points; `computeLineVidyaLayout(options)` -- projects the price and VIDYA onto one shared panel with a y-domain spanning both; returns the panel, ticks, the price path, the VIDYA path, and VIDYA markers (only where the VIDYA is defined); `describeLineVidyaChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentioning Variable Index Dynamic Average + adaptive + Chande Momentum Oscillator + volatility + above/below counts (No data when fewer than 2 points). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-vidya-aria-desc"` span; price path + price dots `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; VIDYA line + markers `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}` with the VIDYA value in the aria-label. data-section on every node; root mirrors `data-period`, `data-cmo-period`, `data-vidya-final`, `data-above-count`, `data-below-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-empty`, `data-animate`; markers expose `data-vidya`. Tooltip on price dots and VIDYA markers shows x, the bold value, the VIDYA, the volatility index, and the price position for the hovered bar. Config badge in top-left calls out `VIDYA p=<period> cmo=<cmoPeriod>`.
