| 11.621 | UI: chart-line-t3 primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.639 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-t3.tsx` ships `<ChartLineT3>` -- pure-SVG line chart with a **Tillson T3 Moving Average overlay using a cascaded smoothing factor**, drawn on the same axes as the price (single-panel overlay). **Indicator math:** `computeLineT3Ema(src, period)` is the project's null-tolerant SMA-seeded EMA. `lineT3Coefficients(vfactor)` returns Tim Tillson's four cubic coefficients in the volume factor `v` -- `c1 = -v^3`, `c2 = 3v^2+3v^3`, `c3 = -6v^2-3v-3v^3`, `c4 = 1+3v+3v^2+v^3` (always summing to 1, negative-zero normalized). `computeLineT3(values, period, vfactor)` is Tillson's T3 Moving Average -- the price is run through a **cascade of six exponential moving averages** and four of them are blended: `T3 = c1*EMA6 + c2*EMA5 + c3*EMA4 + c4*EMA3`. The volume factor (the cascaded smoothing factor) dials how responsive the average is: at 0 the blend collapses to a plain triple-nested EMA (`c1=c2=c3=0`, `c4=1`), a higher factor sharpens the response. EMA1 is defined from index `period-1`; each later nesting needs another `period` defined values, so EMA6 -- and therefore the **T3 is defined from index `6*period-6` onward**. **Worked fixture:** `values` a linear ramp of 15 points (`value[i]=3+3i`), `period 3`, `vfactor 0.5` -- with period 3 the EMA multiplier is exactly 0.5 and with `v=0.5` the four coefficients are exact dyadic numbers (-0.125, 1.125, -3.375, 3.375), so the six-EMA cascade and the blend stay bit-exact: each EMA stage lags the ramp by 3 (`ema-k = value - 3k`) and the T3 settles to `value - 4.5` from index `6*3-6=12` onward -> `t3 [.,.,.,.,.,.,.,.,.,.,.,.,34.5,37.5,40.5]`; every value is exact so the series is asserted with `toBe`. **Distinct from chart-line-dema / chart-line-tema:** the DEMA combines two EMAs (`2*EMA - EMA(EMA)`) and the TEMA three (`3*EMA - 3*EMA(EMA) + EMA^3`) with **fixed integer** coefficients; the T3 uses a **six-EMA cascade** and **four volume-factor** coefficients -- a generalised, tunable smoother that collapses to a plain triple-nested EMA when the volume factor is 0. **Distinct from chart-line-zlema:** the ZLEMA removes lag by feeding a de-lagged input through one EMA; the T3 leaves the input untouched and tunes lag with the volume-factor blend of a deeper EMA cascade. **Distinct from chart-line-kama / chart-line-vidya:** those **adapt** their smoothing bar to bar from market conditions; the T3 applies a **fixed** cascade and a fixed volume factor. API: `data: readonly ChartLineT3Point[]` (`{x, value}`); `period` (default 5, normalized to a positive integer), `vfactor` (default 0.7, clamped to [0,1]); sizing `width`/`height`/`padding`/`tickCount`; styling `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`priceColor`/`t3Color`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots`/`showT3`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled series visibility (`hiddenSeries`/`defaultHiddenSeries`/`onHiddenSeriesChange`; keys `price`/`t3`); `formatValue`/`formatX`; `onPointClick({point})`; `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`; standard a11y props. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_T3_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/VFACTOR/PRICE_COLOR/T3_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineT3Position` type (`above`|`below`|`on`); `getLineT3FinitePoints` (drops non-finite x/value); `normalizeLineT3Period(period, fallback)` (non-finite or sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `normalizeLineT3Vfactor(vfactor, fallback)` (non-finite falls back, out-of-[0,1] clamps); `lineT3Coefficients(vfactor)` -> `{c1,c2,c3,c4}` (sum to 1); `computeLineT3Ema(src, period)` -> the null-tolerant EMA series; `computeLineT3(values, period, vfactor)` -> `{ema1,ema2,ema3,ema4,ema5,ema6,t3}` (non-array -> empty); `runLineT3(points, options?)` -- sorts finite points by x, computes the series, classifies each sample by the price's position vs the T3, returns `{series, period, vfactor, ema1..ema6, t3, samples, t3Final, t3Min, t3Max, aboveCount, belowCount, ok}`; ok=false for fewer than 2 points; `computeLineT3Layout(options)` -- projects the price and T3 onto one shared panel with a y-domain spanning both; returns the panel, ticks, the price path, the T3 path, and T3 markers (only where the T3 is defined); `describeLineT3Chart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentioning Tillson T3 Moving Average + cascade + exponential moving average + volume factor + above/below counts (No data when fewer than 2 points). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-t3-aria-desc"` span; price path + price dots `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; T3 line + markers `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}` with the T3 value in the aria-label. data-section on every node; root mirrors `data-period`, `data-vfactor`, `data-t3-final`, `data-above-count`, `data-below-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-empty`, `data-animate`; markers expose `data-t3`. Tooltip on price dots and T3 markers shows x, the bold value, the T3, and the price position for the hovered bar. Config badge in top-left calls out `T3 p=<period> v=<vfactor>`. | 11.622 | UI: chart-line-gmma primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.640 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-gmma.tsx` ships `<ChartLineGmma>` -- pure-SVG line chart with a **Guppy Multiple Moving Average ribbon of short and long EMA groups**, drawn on the same axes as the price (single-panel overlay). **Indicator math:** `computeLineGmmaEma(src, period)` is the project's SMA-seeded EMA. `computeLineGmma(values, shortPeriods, longPeriods)` is Daryl Guppy's Multiple Moving Average -- the price is run through a fast **trader group** of short-period EMAs (Guppy default 3/5/8/10/12/15) and a slow **investor group** of long-period EMAs (default 30/35/40/45/50/60), producing a ribbon of EMA lines. The signal is the **relationship between the two ribbons**, not any single line: per bar, with `shortMin/shortMax` and `longMin/longMax` the envelopes of the two groups, `shortMin > longMax` -> **bullish** (short ribbon entirely above), `shortMax < longMin` -> **bearish** (entirely below), otherwise **crossing** (ribbons overlap, a trend change), and **forming** while any EMA in either group is still in warm-up. **Worked fixture:** `values` an 18-point linear ramp (`value[i]=10+2i`), short periods `[1,3]`, long periods `[7,15]` -- the EMA of a ramp with step `d` settles exactly to `value - d*(period-1)/2`, so with step 2 each `EMA-k = value - (k-1)`: short `EMA-1 = value`, `EMA-3 = value-2`; long `EMA-7 = value-6`, `EMA-15 = value-14`. The short ribbon sits entirely above the long ribbon, so once all four EMAs are defined (from index 14) the state is `bullish` -- bullishCount 4, the warm-up bars `forming`; every EMA value is exact so the bands and EMA values are asserted with `toBe`/`toEqual`. A descending ramp yields `bearish` and a flat series yields `crossing`. **Distinct from every single-line moving-average primitive** (chart-line-ema, chart-line-dema, chart-line-tema, chart-line-wma, chart-line-trima, chart-line-t3, chart-line-vidya, ...): those plot **one** average line and read the trend from where the price sits relative to it; the GMMA plots a **ribbon of twelve EMAs in two groups** and reads the trend from the **separation between the groups** -- it is the only moving-average primitive whose signal is a group-versus-group relationship rather than a single line. API: `data: readonly ChartLineGmmaPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `shortPeriods` / `longPeriods` (the two EMA-period arrays; defaults the six-and-six Guppy periods, each normalized to ascending deduped positive integers); sizing `width`/`height`/`padding`/`tickCount`; styling `strokeWidth`/`emaStrokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`priceColor`/`shortColor`/`longColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots`/`showShort`/`showLong`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled series visibility (`hiddenSeries`/`defaultHiddenSeries`/`onHiddenSeriesChange`; keys `price`/`short`/`long`); `formatValue`/`formatX`; `onPointClick({point})`; `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`; standard a11y props. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_GMMA_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/EMA_STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/SHORT_PERIODS/LONG_PERIODS/PRICE_COLOR/SHORT_COLOR/LONG_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineGmmaState` type (`bullish`|`bearish`|`crossing`|`forming`); `getLineGmmaFinitePoints` (drops non-finite x/value); `normalizeLineGmmaPeriods(periods, fallback)` (ascending deduped positive integers, empty/non-array -> fallback); `computeLineGmmaEma(src, period)` -> a single SMA-seeded EMA series; `computeLineGmma(values, shortPeriods, longPeriods)` -> `{short, long}` (one EMA series per period in each group; non-array -> empty); `runLineGmma(points, options?)` -- sorts finite points by x, computes every EMA, classifies each sample, returns `{series, shortPeriods, longPeriods, short, long, samples, bullishCount, bearishCount, crossingCount, ok}`; ok=false for fewer than 2 points; `computeLineGmmaLayout(options)` -- projects the price and every ribbon line onto one shared panel with a y-domain spanning the price and every EMA; returns the panel, ticks, the price path, price dots, and `shortLines`/`longLines` (each a `{period, path}`); `describeLineGmmaChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentioning Guppy Multiple Moving Average + ribbon + exponential moving average + bullish/bearish + state counts (No data when fewer than 2 points). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-gmma-aria-desc"` span; price path + price dots `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node; each ribbon line carries `data-period`; root mirrors `data-short-count`, `data-long-count`, `data-bullish-count`, `data-bearish-count`, `data-crossing-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-empty`, `data-animate`; price dots expose `data-state`. Tooltip on price dots shows x, the bold value, the short ribbon band, the long ribbon band, and the state for the hovered bar. Config badge in top-left calls out `GMMA s=<shortCount> l=<longCount>`. Three-series legend (Price / Short / Long) with per-series toggle plus a `<n> bullish, <m> bearish, <k> crossing` stat. 55 vitest cases (getLineGmmaFinitePoints incl. non-finite x/value drops + non-array, normalizeLineGmmaPeriods incl. the sort/dedupe/floor + dropping non-finite/sub-1/negative entries + the empty fallback + the non-array fallback, computeLineGmmaEma incl. the period-one identity + the SMA seed then fold + the null warm-up + non-array, computeLineGmma incl. one EMA series per short and long period + matching the single-EMA helper + the exact ramp settle + non-array -> empty, runLineGmma incl. ok flags + the normalized period groups + the EMA series + the bullish state for an uptrend + the bearish state for a downtrend + the crossing state for a flat series + the forming warm-up + the ribbon bands per sample + the state counts + sorts unsorted input by x + the not-ok short series + empty/null -> not ok + one sample per point + the default six-and-six Guppy periods, computeLineGmmaLayout incl. ok layout + the price path + one ribbon line per period in each group + the period tags + priceDots per point (18) + run statistics + collapsed canvas -> not ok + too-little-data -> not ok, describeLineGmmaChart incl. mentions Guppy Multiple Moving Average + ribbon + exponential moving average + bullish/bearish + state counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / data attributes / svg + price line / one ribbon line per short and long period (2+2) / three legend items / config badge group sizes / hide price path via hiddenSeries / hide the short ribbon via showShort / hide the long ribbon via hiddenSeries / onSeriesToggle payload / price dots via showDots / empty state / omit config badge / ref / displayName / animate flag) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. | 11.623 | UI: chart-line-disparity primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.641 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-disparity.tsx` ships `<ChartLineDisparity>` -- pure-SVG line chart with a **Disparity Index panel rating price distance from its moving average as a percent**. The close price line occupies the top panel; the bottom panel renders the Disparity Index on a **symmetric data-driven y-axis** around zero with a centred dashed zero line and sign-coloured markers. **Indicator math:** `computeLineDisparitySma(values, period)` is the simple moving average (the mean of each trailing window of `period` values, defined from index `period-1`). `computeLineDisparity(values, period)` is Steve Nison's Disparity Index -- `disparity = 100 * (price - SMA) / SMA`, which rates how far the price sits from its own moving average as a percentage. The index swings around zero: a positive reading means the price is above its moving average, a negative reading means below, and the size of the swing shows how stretched the price has become. It is defined from index `period-1` onward; a zero moving average reads 0 rather than dividing by zero; negative-zero is normalized. **Worked fixture:** `values [6,9,15,6,9,15]`, `period 3` -- every three-bar window sums to 30 so the period-3 SMA is exactly 10 throughout, and the Disparity Index is `100*(price-10)/10 = 10*price-100`: `sma [null,null,10,10,10,10]`, `disparity [null,null,50,-40,-10,50]`; `disparityFinal 50`, `disparityMin -40`, `disparityMax 50`, positiveCount 2, negativeCount 2; every value is exact so the series is asserted with `toEqual`. **Distinct from chart-line-roc:** the Rate of Change measures the percentage change from a **past price** N bars ago; the Disparity Index measures the percentage gap from the **moving average** -- a different reference point. **Distinct from the moving-average overlays** (chart-line-ema, chart-line-sma, chart-line-dema, chart-line-tema, ...): those plot the average itself as a line drawn over the price; the Disparity Index plots the price's percentage **deviation** from that average as a separate, zero-centred oscillator panel. **Distinct from chart-line-ppo:** the PPO is the percentage difference of **two EMAs of the price**; the Disparity Index is the percentage difference of the **price itself** from one moving average. API: `data: readonly ChartLineDisparityPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `period` (default 14, normalized to a positive integer); sizing `width`/`height`/`padding`/`gap`/`pricePanelRatio`/`tickCount`; styling `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`priceColor`/`disparityColor`/`positiveColor`/`negativeColor`/`zeroColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots`/`showDisparity`/`showZeroLine`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled series visibility (`hiddenSeries`/`defaultHiddenSeries`/`onHiddenSeriesChange`; keys `price`/`disparity`); `formatValue`/`formatX`; `onPointClick({point})`; `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`; standard a11y props. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_DISPARITY_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/PRICE_COLOR/DISPARITY_COLOR/POSITIVE_COLOR/NEGATIVE_COLOR/ZERO_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineDisparitySign` type (`positive`|`negative`|`zero`); `getLineDisparityFinitePoints` (drops non-finite x/value); `normalizeLineDisparityPeriod(period, fallback)` (non-finite or sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineDisparitySma(values, period)` -> the simple moving average series; `computeLineDisparity(values, period)` -> `{sma, disparity}` (both null through warm-up; non-array -> empty); `runLineDisparity(points, options?)` -- sorts finite points by x, computes the series, classifies each sample by the disparity sign, returns `{series, period, sma, disparity, samples, disparityFinal, disparityMin, disparityMax, positiveCount, negativeCount, ok}`; ok=false for fewer than 2 points; `computeLineDisparityLayout(options)` -- stacks a price panel above a Disparity panel on a symmetric data-driven y-domain around zero; returns panels, ticks, the price path, the Disparity path, sign-coloured markers (only where the disparity is defined), and the centred zero line `y`; `describeLineDisparityChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentioning Disparity Index + moving average + percentage + positive/negative + counts (No data when fewer than 2 points). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-disparity-aria-desc"` span; price path + price dots `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; Disparity line + markers `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}` with the disparity value + sign in the aria-label. data-section on every node; root mirrors `data-period`, `data-disparity-final`, `data-positive-count`, `data-negative-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-empty`, `data-animate`; markers expose `data-disparity` + `data-sign`. Tooltip on price dots and Disparity markers shows x, the value, the moving average, the bold Disparity Index, and the sign for the hovered bar. Config badge in top-left calls out `DISP p=<period>`. Two-series legend (Price / Disparity) with per-series toggle plus a `<n> positive, <m> negative` stat. 57 vitest cases (getLineDisparityFinitePoints incl. non-finite x/value drops + non-array, normalizeLineDisparityPeriod incl. floor + sub-1/NaN/negative fallback, computeLineDisparitySma incl. the windowed mean + the null warm-up + the period-one identity + non-array, computeLineDisparity incl. the SMA and disparity series against the hand-verified fixture + the null warm-up + the 0 reading when price equals its average + the zero-MA guard -> 0 not NaN + non-array -> empty, runLineDisparity incl. ok flags + the period + the series + final/min/max readings + sign classification + positive/negative counts + the warm-up null disparity + sorts unsorted input by x + the not-ok short series + empty/null -> not ok + one sample per point + default period 14 -> no disparity for a short series, computeLineDisparityLayout incl. ok layout + price panel stacked above the Disparity panel + price/Disparity paths + marker only where disparity defined (4) + priceDots per point (6) + symmetric Disparity y-domain around zero (-50/50) + zero line centred in the Disparity panel + run statistics + collapsed canvas -> not ok + too-little-data -> not ok, describeLineDisparityChart incl. mentions Disparity Index + moving average + percentage + positive/negative + counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / data attributes / svg + price + Disparity lines / one marker per defined disparity value (4) / markers tagged with positive/negative sign / zero line + hide via showZeroLine / both panel labels / two legend items / config badge period / hide price path via hiddenSeries / hide Disparity line + markers via showDisparity / price dots via showDots / empty state / omit config badge / ref / displayName / animate flag) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. | 11.624 | UI: chart-line-frama primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.642 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-frama.tsx` ships `<ChartLineFrama>` -- pure-SVG line chart with a **Fractal Adaptive Moving Average overlay whose smoothing tracks the fractal dimension of price**, drawn on the same axes as the price (single-panel overlay). **Indicator math:** `computeLineFramaDimension(values, period)` reads the **fractal dimension** of the price over each trailing window -- the window (period must be even) splits into two halves; with N1/N2/N3 the per-bar normalized ranges of the first half, second half and whole window, `D = (log(N1+N2)-log(N3))/log(2)` clamped to [1,2]. `D` runs near 1 for a smooth trend (the two halves stack into the whole) and near 2 for a jagged choppy market (each half fills the whole range); a flat window reads 1. `computeLineFrama(values, period)` is John Ehlers' Fractal Adaptive Moving Average -- the dimension drives an adaptive smoothing constant `alpha = exp(-4.6*(D-1))` clamped to [0.01,1] (a low dimension makes alpha near 1 so the average tracks the price closely, a high dimension near 0.01 so it slows almost to a halt), and `FRAMA[i] = alpha*value[i] + (1-alpha)*FRAMA[i-1]`. The FRAMA is seeded at index `period-1` with that bar's price and is recursive from index `period` onward. **Worked fixture:** `values` a linear ramp of 8 points (`value[i]=10+2i`), `period 4` -- a ramp is maximally smooth so its fractal dimension clamps to exactly 1, the adaptive alpha is `exp(0)=1`, and the FRAMA tracks the price exactly: `dimension [.,.,.,1,1,1,1,1]`, `alpha [.,.,.,1,1,1,1,1]`, `frama = value [.,.,.,16,18,20,22,24]` -- every value exact, asserted with `toEqual`. A square-wave fixture fills each half-window with the full range, giving a fractal dimension of 2 (a `log(8)/log(2)` rounding artifact makes it `1.9999999999999998` so tests assert it with `toBeCloseTo`) and an adaptive alpha of `exp(-4.6)`, which barely moves the average. **Distinct from chart-line-kama:** both are adaptive recursive averages, but the KAMA scales its smoothing by an **efficiency ratio** (net change over the path travelled); the FRAMA scales by the **fractal dimension** -- a roughness measure read from comparing the range of two half-windows to the whole. **Distinct from chart-line-vidya:** the VIDYA scales its smoothing by the **absolute Chande Momentum Oscillator**; the FRAMA scales by the fractal dimension -- three adaptive averages, three different market-state drivers. **Distinct from chart-line-ema / dema / tema / wma / trima / t3:** those use a **fixed** smoothing scheme; the FRAMA's smoothing varies bar to bar with the measured fractal dimension. API: `data: readonly ChartLineFramaPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `period` (default 16, normalized to an even positive integer of at least 2); sizing `width`/`height`/`padding`/`tickCount`; styling `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`priceColor`/`framaColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots`/`showFrama`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled series visibility (`hiddenSeries`/`defaultHiddenSeries`/`onHiddenSeriesChange`; keys `price`/`frama`); `formatValue`/`formatX`; `onPointClick({point})`; `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`; standard a11y props. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_FRAMA_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/PRICE_COLOR/FRAMA_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineFramaPosition` type (`above`|`below`|`on`); `getLineFramaFinitePoints` (drops non-finite x/value); `normalizeLineFramaPeriod(period, fallback)` (non-finite or sub-2 falls back, fractional floors, odd rounds down to even); `computeLineFramaDimension(values, period)` -> the fractal dimension series clamped to [1,2]; `computeLineFrama(values, period)` -> `{dimension, alpha, frama}` (non-array -> empty); `runLineFrama(points, options?)` -- sorts finite points by x, computes the series, classifies each sample by the price's position vs the FRAMA, returns `{series, period, dimension, alpha, frama, samples, framaFinal, framaMin, framaMax, aboveCount, belowCount, ok}`; ok=false for fewer than 2 points; `computeLineFramaLayout(options)` -- projects the price and FRAMA onto one shared panel with a y-domain spanning both; returns the panel, ticks, the price path, the FRAMA path, and FRAMA markers (only where the FRAMA is defined); `describeLineFramaChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentioning Fractal Adaptive Moving Average + fractal dimension + adaptive + choppy + above/below counts (No data when fewer than 2 points). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-frama-aria-desc"` span; price path + price dots `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; FRAMA line + markers `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}` with the FRAMA value in the aria-label. data-section on every node; root mirrors `data-period`, `data-frama-final`, `data-above-count`, `data-below-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-empty`, `data-animate`; markers expose `data-frama`. Tooltip on price dots and FRAMA markers shows x, the bold value, the FRAMA, the fractal dimension, and the price position for the hovered bar. Config badge in top-left calls out `FRAMA p=<period>`. Two-series legend (Price / FRAMA) with per-series toggle plus an `<n> above, <m> below` stat. 58 vitest cases (getLineFramaFinitePoints incl. non-finite x/value drops + non-array, normalizeLineFramaPeriod incl. even kept + fractional floored + odd rounded down to even + sub-2 fallback + non-finite fallback, computeLineFramaDimension incl. dimension 1 for a smooth ramp window + ~2 for a square-wave window + 1 for a flat window + the null warm-up + non-array, computeLineFrama incl. the dimension/alpha/frama series against the hand-verified ramp fixture + the exact tracking of a clean trend + the seed at period-1 + the square-wave exp(-4.6) alpha + the barely-moving choppy average + non-array -> empty, runLineFrama incl. ok flags + the normalized even period + the series + final/min/max readings + the on-the-FRAMA position for a clean trend + above/below counts for a choppy market + the warm-up null FRAMA + sorts unsorted input by x + the not-ok short series + empty/null -> not ok + one sample per point + default period 16 -> no FRAMA for a short series, computeLineFramaLayout incl. ok layout + price/FRAMA paths + marker only where FRAMA defined (5) + priceDots per point (8) + shared y-domain covering price + FRAMA + run statistics + marker containment + collapsed canvas -> not ok + too-little-data -> not ok, describeLineFramaChart incl. mentions Fractal Adaptive Moving Average + fractal dimension + adaptive + choppy + above/below counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / data attributes / svg + price + FRAMA lines / one marker per defined FRAMA value (5) / two legend items / config badge period / hide price path via hiddenSeries / hide FRAMA line + markers via showFrama / hide FRAMA via hiddenSeries / onSeriesToggle payload / price dots via showDots / empty state / omit config badge / ref / displayName / animate flag) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. | 11.625 | UI: chart-line-jma primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.643 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-jma.tsx` ships `<ChartLineJma>` -- pure-SVG line chart with a **Jurik-style low-lag low-noise moving average overlay**, drawn on the same axes as the price (single-panel overlay). **Indicator math:** `computeLineJma(values, length, phase)` runs the price through a three-stage adaptive filter -- `beta = 0.45*(length-1)/(0.45*(length-1)+2)` sets the smoothing, `alpha = beta^2`, and the phase (clamped to [-100,100], folded into `phaseRatio = phase/100 + 1.5`) trades a touch of overshoot for even less lag. The three filter stages are `e0 = (1-alpha)*price + alpha*e0'`, `e1 = (price-e0)*(1-beta) + beta*e1'` and `e2 = (e0 + phaseRatio*e1 - jma')*(1-alpha)^2 + alpha^2*e2'`, and the JMA is `e2 + jma'`. It is seeded at the first bar with that bar's price (`e0=price[0]`, `e1=e2=0`, `jma=price[0]`) -- so a flat series stays flat and there is **no warm-up period**, the JMA producing a value from the very first bar -- and is recursive from the second bar onward. **Worked verification:** the recursive `beta^2` filter is not exactly representable, so tests anchor on exact cases -- a **flat series** holds exactly at its constant (the seed keeps `e0=c`, `e1=e2=0`); a **length of 1** makes `beta=0`/`alpha=0` so the filter degenerates to the identity (JMA = price exactly); and for input `[0,1]` with `length 2`, `phase 0` the recursion gives `jma[1] = 638029696000/678223072849 = 0.9407372...` (asserted with `toBeCloseTo`). For the non-degenerate adaptive behaviour the tests assert robust properties: the seed (`jma[0]=price[0]`), that the JMA lags just below a rising ramp, that a different phase produces a different result, and that a value is produced for every bar. **Distinct from chart-line-dema / chart-line-tema:** those are **fixed linear combinations** of nested EMAs (`2*EMA - EMA(EMA)` and `3*EMA - 3*EMA(EMA) + EMA^3`); the JMA is a **three-stage adaptive filter** with a `phase` parameter that explicitly tunes the lag-versus-overshoot tradeoff, and -- unlike the EMA cascades, which have a multi-period warm-up -- it produces a value from the very first bar. **Distinct from chart-line-kama / chart-line-vidya / chart-line-frama:** those derive their smoothing from a single market-state index (efficiency ratio, the CMO, the fractal dimension); the JMA's low-lag, low-noise behaviour is built into its multi-stage filter structure and the phase parameter rather than an external gauge. API: `data: readonly ChartLineJmaPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `length` (default 7, normalized to a positive integer), `phase` (default 0, clamped to [-100,100]); sizing `width`/`height`/`padding`/`tickCount`; styling `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`priceColor`/`jmaColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots`/`showJma`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled series visibility (`hiddenSeries`/`defaultHiddenSeries`/`onHiddenSeriesChange`; keys `price`/`jma`); `formatValue`/`formatX`; `onPointClick({point})`; `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`; standard a11y props. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_JMA_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/LENGTH/PHASE/PRICE_COLOR/JMA_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineJmaPosition` type (`above`|`below`|`on`); `getLineJmaFinitePoints` (drops non-finite x/value); `normalizeLineJmaLength(length, fallback)` (non-finite or sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `normalizeLineJmaPhase(phase, fallback)` (non-finite falls back, out-of-[-100,100] clamps); `computeLineJma(values, length, phase)` -> the JMA series, one value per input bar (no warm-up nulls; non-array/empty -> empty); `runLineJma(points, options?)` -- sorts finite points by x, computes the series, classifies each sample by the price's position vs the JMA, returns `{series, length, phase, jma, samples, jmaFinal, jmaMin, jmaMax, aboveCount, belowCount, ok}`; ok=false for fewer than 2 points; `computeLineJmaLayout(options)` -- projects the price and JMA onto one shared panel with a y-domain spanning both; returns the panel, ticks, the price path, the JMA path, and a JMA marker for every bar; `describeLineJmaChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentioning Jurik-style Moving Average + low-lag + low-noise + phase + above/below counts (No data when fewer than 2 points). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-jma-aria-desc"` span; price path + price dots `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; JMA line + markers `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}` with the JMA value in the aria-label. data-section on every node; root mirrors `data-length`, `data-phase`, `data-jma-final`, `data-above-count`, `data-below-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-empty`, `data-animate`; markers expose `data-jma`. Tooltip on price dots and JMA markers shows x, the bold value, the JMA, and the price position for the hovered bar. Config badge in top-left calls out `JMA L=<length> ph=<phase>`. Two-series legend (Price / JMA) with per-series toggle plus an `<n> above, <m> below` stat. 55 vitest cases (getLineJmaFinitePoints incl. non-finite x/value drops + non-array, normalizeLineJmaLength incl. floor + sub-1/NaN/negative fallback, normalizeLineJmaPhase incl. in-range unchanged + out-of-[-100,100] clamp + non-finite fallback, computeLineJma incl. the first-bar seed + the flat-series exact hold + the length-1 identity + the fraction-exact step-input value + the no-warm-up property + lagging below a rising ramp + the phase sensitivity + non-array/empty -> empty, runLineJma incl. ok flags + the length and phase + the series + final/min/max readings + position classification + above/below counts + sorts unsorted input by x + the not-ok short series + empty/null -> not ok + one sample per point + default length 7/phase 0 + a flat series sitting on the JMA, computeLineJmaLayout incl. ok layout + price/JMA paths + a marker for every bar (8, no warm-up) + priceDots per point (8) + shared y-domain covering price + JMA + run statistics + marker containment + collapsed canvas -> not ok + too-little-data -> not ok, describeLineJmaChart incl. mentions Jurik-style Moving Average + low-lag + low-noise + phase + above/below counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / data attributes / svg + price + JMA lines / a marker for every bar (8) / two legend items / config badge length + phase / hide price path via hiddenSeries / hide JMA line + markers via showJma / hide JMA via hiddenSeries / onSeriesToggle payload / price dots via showDots / empty state / omit config badge / ref / displayName / animate flag) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. | 11.626 | UI: chart-line-smma primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.644 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-smma.tsx` ships `<ChartLineSmma>` -- a pure-SVG line chart with a **Smoothed Moving Average (SMMA)** overlay using Welles Wilder's running-mean smoothing, drawn on ONE shared price panel (the SMMA shares the price's value axis, so it overlays rather than getting its own sub-panel). **The computation:** `computeLineSmma(values, period)` is Wilder's Smoothed Moving Average. The series is undefined through the warm-up and seeded at index `period-1` with the SIMPLE MEAN of the first `period` values; every later bar folds in by the RUNNING-MEAN recursion `SMMA[i] = (SMMA[i-1]*(period-1) + value[i]) / period`. That recursion is an exponential average with the SLOW weight `1/period` -- every past value still contributes with an exponentially decaying weight, so no value is ever fully dropped and the line stays exceptionally smooth. Period 1 reproduces the input series exactly (the identity case); a series shorter than `period` is all-null; a non-array yields `[]`. **Worked fixture** (`values = [3,6,9,12,11,9,6]`, period 3 -- prices chosen so every running-mean step lands on an integer): `SMMA[2]=(3+6+9)/3=6`; `SMMA[3]=(6*2+12)/3=8`; `SMMA[4]=(8*2+11)/3=9`; `SMMA[5]=(9*2+9)/3=9`; `SMMA[6]=(9*2+6)/3=8`; so `smma=[null,null,6,8,9,9,8]` -- every value exact, asserted with `toEqual`. smmaFinal 8, smmaMin 6, smmaMax 9, aboveCount 3, belowCount 1. `runLineSmma` sorts the finite points by x, computes the series, and tags each sample with the price's `above`/`below`/`on` position versus the SMMA. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs a plain EMA -- an EMA uses the FASTER weight `2/(period+1)`; the SMMA's slower `1/period` weight makes it equivalent to an EMA of period `2*period-1`, so it lags more and stays smoother. vs a simple moving average (`<ChartLineWma>`'s linear-ramp window, or a plain SMA) -- those are FINITE windowed means that fully drop the oldest value each bar; the SMMA is an INFINITE running mean where every past value still contributes with a decaying weight. vs `<ChartLineDema>` / `<ChartLineTema>` -- those REDUCE lag; the SMMA deliberately lags MORE in exchange for heavy smoothing (it is the smoothing Wilder built RSI / ATR / ADX on). All exported helpers carry a `LineSmma`/`Smma` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineSmmaPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `period` (default 14); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `priceColor` / `smmaColor` / `aboveColor` / `belowColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showSmma`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `smma`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_SMMA_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/PERIOD/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PRICE_COLOR/SMMA_COLOR/ABOVE_COLOR/BELOW_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineSmmaFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value); `normalizeLineSmmaPeriod(period, fallback)` (coerce to a positive integer; non-finite or sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineSmma(values, period)`; `runLineSmma(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, period, smma, samples, smmaFinal, smmaMin, smmaMax, aboveCount, belowCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, smma, position}` (`position` is `above`/`below`/`on`); `computeLineSmmaLayout({data, ...})` -- projects the price and SMMA onto one shared panel, returns the panel, ticks, the price path, the SMMA path, price dots and SMMA markers (markers only where the SMMA is defined); degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLineSmmaChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary. ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-smma-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the SMMA line + every price dot + every SMMA marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node; root mirrors run statistics as `data-*`; `motion-safe:animate-fade-in` entrance; config badge calls out the SMMA period; a two-item legend (Price / SMMA) with toggle; hover/focus tooltip exposes the value, the SMMA and the position. 51 vitest cases covering finite-point filtering, period normalization, `computeLineSmma` (the seeded running-mean series against the hand-verified fixture, the `(SMMA_prev*(period-1)+price)/period` recursion, the null warm-up, the period-one identity, the flat-series hold, the shorter-than-period series, non-array), `runLineSmma` (the period, the series, final / min / max readings, position classification, above/below counts, the warm-up nulls, x-sorting, the not-ok short series, the default period 14), layout geometry (the price/SMMA paths, markers only where the SMMA is defined, the shared y-domain, the run statistics, marker containment), the description string, and component rendering (price/SMMA lines, markers, legend, badge, hidden series, series toggle, price dots, empty state, ref, animation) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for the new files (conditional spreads honour `exactOptionalPropertyTypes`). Exported via components/ui barrel. Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/c4-mgr-auto. | 11.627 | UI: chart-line-lsma primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.645 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-lsma.tsx` ships `<ChartLineLsma>` -- a pure-SVG line chart with a **Least Squares Moving Average (LSMA)** overlay tracing the endpoint of a rolling linear regression, drawn on ONE shared price panel (the LSMA shares the price's value axis, so it overlays rather than getting its own sub-panel). **The computation:** `computeLineLsma(values, period)` fits an ordinary least-squares straight line `y = a + b*x` across each window of the last `period` prices over the relative x-indices `0..period-1`, then reads the line at its NEWEST end `x = period-1`. With `meanX = (period-1)/2`, `Sxx = period*(period^2-1)/12`, `Sxy = sum(x*y) - meanX*sum(y)`, `slope = Sxy/Sxx` the endpoint `a + b*(period-1)` simplifies to `meanY + slope*meanX` because `(period-1) - meanX === meanX`. Because the regression line is read at its ENDPOINT rather than averaged across the whole window, the LSMA tracks price more closely and turns sooner than a same-length simple moving average, while the linear fit filters single-bar noise. The LSMA is defined from index `period-1` onward; earlier indices are null. `computeLineLsmaSlope(values, period)` exposes the regression `slope` per window -- the local linear trend per bar. A perfectly linear series reproduces itself exactly from index `period-1` (the regression line IS the data); a flat series holds at its constant with slope 0; `period` 1 is the identity (`Sxx` is 0, slope 0); `period` 2 reads the latest value (two points fix an exact line). **Worked fixture** (`values = [2,4,6,14,10,12]`, period 3 -- for a window `[y0,y1,y2]` the endpoint is `mean + slope` with `slope = (y2-y0)/2`): `W@2=[2,4,6]` mean 4 slope 2 -> lsma 6 (linear, on); `W@3=[4,6,14]` mean 8 slope 5 -> lsma 13 (concave up, above); `W@4=[6,14,10]` mean 10 slope 2 -> lsma 12 (concave down, below); `W@5=[14,10,12]` mean 12 slope -1 -> lsma 11 (concave up, above); so `lsma=[null,null,6,13,12,11]`, `slope=[null,null,2,5,2,-1]` -- every value exact, asserted with `toEqual`. The window `W@2` is perfectly linear so its regression endpoint reproduces the price exactly (`lsma 6 == price 6`). Positions `[on,on,on,above,below,above]` -- aboveCount 2, belowCount 1, lsmaFinal 11, lsmaMin 6, lsmaMax 13, slopeFinal -1. A second fixture `RAMP=[1,3,5,7,9,11]` (`y=1+2x`) verifies `computeLineLsma(RAMP,4)` reproduces the series from index 3 with a constant slope 2. `runLineLsma` sorts the finite points by x, computes the LSMA and slope series, and tags each sample with the price's `above`/`below`/`on` position versus the LSMA plus its window `slope`. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs the moving averages that weight a window into an AVERAGE (a plain SMA, the just-shipped `<ChartLineSmma>`, `<ChartLineWma>`, an EMA) -- the LSMA is the ENDPOINT of a regression line that extrapolates the fitted linear trend to the current bar, which removes the half-window centering lag a plain average carries; on a steady trend the LSMA sits exactly on price (the regression IS the trend) whereas an EMA still lags. vs `<ChartLineDema>` / `<ChartLineTema>` -- those cascade EMAs to cancel lag; the LSMA removes lag a different way, by projecting a least-squares regression to its endpoint. The LSMA is the only member of the moving-average family here that also yields a per-bar regression SLOPE, surfaced in the tooltip and as `data-slope-final`. All exported helpers carry a `LineLsma`/`Lsma` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineLsmaPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `period` (default 25); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `priceColor` / `lsmaColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showLsma`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `lsma`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_LSMA_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/PERIOD/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PRICE_COLOR/LSMA_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineLsmaFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value); `normalizeLineLsmaPeriod(period, fallback)` (coerce to a positive integer; non-finite or sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineLsma(values, period)`; `computeLineLsmaSlope(values, period)`; `runLineLsma(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, period, lsma, slope, samples, lsmaFinal, lsmaMin, lsmaMax, slopeFinal, aboveCount, belowCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, lsma, slope, position}` (`position` is `above`/`below`/`on`); `computeLineLsmaLayout({data, ...})` -- projects the price and LSMA onto one shared panel, returns the panel, ticks, the price path, the LSMA path, price dots and LSMA markers (markers only where the LSMA is defined); degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLineLsmaChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Least Squares Moving Average + LSMA + regression + endpoint + slope (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-lsma-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the LSMA line + every price dot + every LSMA marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-lsma / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick / -tick-label / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -lsma-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-lsma / -tooltip-slope / -tooltip-position / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-period); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-lsma-final`, `data-slope-final`, `data-above-count`, `data-below-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; markers expose `data-lsma`. Tooltip on a price dot or a marker shows x, value, lsma, slope and the position. Config badge in top-left calls out `LSMA p=<period>`. The legend has two toggle items (Price / LSMA). 60 vitest cases (getLineLsmaFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + value** + non-array -> [], normalizeLineLsmaPeriod incl. floors a fractional + **sub-1 / NaN / negative falls back**, computeLineLsma incl. **traces the endpoint of the rolling least-squares regression** + the null warm-up + **the period-one identity** + **reads the latest value for a period of two** + **reproduces a perfectly linear series from index period-1** + the flat-series hold + the shorter-than-period series + non-array -> [], computeLineLsmaSlope incl. **exposes the regression slope per window** + **a flat slope of zero for a constant series** + **a constant slope for a perfectly linear series** + the null warm-up + **a zero slope for a period of one** + non-array -> [], runLineLsma incl. ok + the period + **the LSMA series** + **the regression slope series** + **final/min/max readings** + **the final slope reading** + position classification + above/below counts + **warm-up samples with a null LSMA and slope** + x-sorting + not-ok short series + one sample per point + **the default period 25**, computeLineLsmaLayout incl. ok + the price/LSMA paths + **a marker only where the LSMA is defined** + the shared y-domain + the run statistics + marker containment + collapsed dims -> ok=false + too little data -> ok=false, describeLineLsmaChart incl. **the indicator vocabulary** + the above/below counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / root data-* / svg + price + LSMA lines / **one marker per defined LSMA value** / two-item legend / config badge period / hide the price path / hide the LSMA line + markers via showLsma / hide the LSMA line via the hidden set / series toggle / price dots / empty state / omit config badge / ref / displayName / animate flag) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. | 11.628 | UI: chart-line-fractal primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.646 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-fractal.tsx` ships `<ChartLineFractal>` -- a pure-SVG line chart with **Williams Fractal** up and down reversal markers overlaid directly on a single price line. **The computation:** `computeLineFractals(values, wing)` detects Williams Fractal swing pivots. For a `wing` of `w` (the classic Williams fractal uses `w=2`, a 5-bar window) a bar at index `i` is an `up` fractal when `value[i] > value[i-k]` AND `value[i] > value[i+k]` for every `k` in `1..w` (a local swing high marking a potential reversal down), and a `down` fractal when `value[i] < value[i-k]` AND `value[i] < value[i+k]` for every such `k` (a swing low marking a potential reversal up). Comparisons are STRICT, so a tie with any neighbour disqualifies the bar -- a flat or strictly monotonic series carries no fractals. Because a fractal needs `w` bars of confirmation on each side, the first `w` and last `w` bars can never be fractals. A bar can never be both up and down (it cannot be strictly above and strictly below the same neighbour). The function returns an array parallel to the values carrying `'up'`/`'down'`/`null` per bar; non-array yields `[]`; a series shorter than `2*w+1` is all-null. **Worked fixture** (`values = [5,8,20,7,3,10,22,6,9]`, wing 2): `i=2 (20) > {5,8,7,3}` -> up; `i=4 (3) < {20,7,10,22}` -> down; `i=6 (22) > {3,10,6,9}` -> up; so `fractals=[null,null,'up',null,'down',null,'up',null,null]` -- indices 0,1,7,8 always null (the wing-2 warm-up edges), asserted with `toEqual`. upCount 2, downCount 1, lastUp 22, lastDown 3. A `wing` of 1 (a 3-bar window) on `[1,5,2,4,1]` yields `[null,'up','down','up',null]`. `runLineFractals` sorts the finite points by x, computes the fractal array, and returns the detected fractals as `signals` (`{index,x,value,type}` per fractal), per-bar `samples` (each tagged with its fractal type), the up/down counts, and `lastUp`/`lastDown` (the value of the most recent up/down fractal). **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs the moving-average overlays (`<ChartLineSmma>`, `<ChartLineLsma>`, `<ChartLineWma>`, ...) -- those plot a continuous SMOOTHED line derived by averaging or regressing a window of prices; the fractal primitive plots DISCRETE swing-pivot markers, detecting local extrema rather than averaging, so it takes a `wing` (bars per side) rather than a smoothing `period`. vs an oscillator panel (`<ChartLineCmf>`, `<ChartLineDisparity>`, ...) -- those derive a continuous second series on a sub-panel; the fractal output is a sparse set of typed events overlaid directly on the price line. All exported helpers carry a `LineFractal`/`Fractal` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineFractalPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `wing` (default 2); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `markerSize` / `priceColor` / `upColor` / `downColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showFractals`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `up` / `down`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_FRACTAL_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/WING/MARKER_SIZE/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PRICE_COLOR/UP_COLOR/DOWN_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineFractalFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value); `normalizeLineFractalWing(wing, fallback)` (coerce to a positive integer; non-finite or sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineFractals(values, wing)`; `runLineFractals(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, wing, fractals, signals, samples, upCount, downCount, lastUp, lastDown, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, fractal}` and each signal `{index, x, value, type}` (`type` is `up`/`down`); `computeLineFractalLayout({data, ...})` -- projects the price path plus one marker per detected fractal; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLineFractalChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Williams Fractal + swing + reversal + up fractal + down fractal (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-fractal-aria-desc"` span; the price line + every price dot + every fractal marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-fractal / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick / -tick-label / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-fractal / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-wing); root mirrors `data-wing`, `data-up-count`, `data-down-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; markers expose `data-fractal-type` (`up`/`down`) and `data-value`. Up fractals draw as an up-pointing SVG triangle above the price line, down fractals as a down-pointing triangle below it. Tooltip on a price dot or a marker shows x, value and the fractal type. Config badge in top-left calls out `FRACTAL w=<wing>`. The legend has three toggle items (Price / Up fractal / Down fractal); `showFractals` is a master toggle for all markers. 54 vitest cases (getLineFractalFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + value** + non-array -> [], normalizeLineFractalWing incl. floors a fractional + **sub-1 / NaN / negative falls back**, computeLineFractals incl. **detects up and down swing pivots** + **never marks the first wing or last wing bars** + **no fractals in a strictly monotonic series** + **a strict comparison so a flat series has no fractals** + **honours a custom wing of one** + the shorter-than-window series + non-array -> [], runLineFractals incl. ok + the wing + **the per-bar fractal array** + **collects the detected fractals as signals** + the up/down counts + **the most recent up + down fractal value** + per-sample tags + x-sorting + not-ok short series + one sample per point + the default wing 2, computeLineFractalLayout incl. ok + the price path + **one marker per detected fractal** + the y-domain + the run statistics + **marker typing** + marker containment + collapsed dims -> ok=false + too little data -> ok=false, describeLineFractalChart incl. **the indicator vocabulary** + the up/down counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / root data-* / svg + price line / **one marker per detected fractal** / **marker typing** / three-item legend / config badge wing / hide the price path / **hide the up markers via the hidden set** / **hide the down markers via the hidden set** / **hide every marker when showFractals is false** / series toggle / price dots / empty state / omit config badge / ref / displayName / animate flag) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for the new files (conditional spreads honour `exactOptionalPropertyTypes`). Exported via components/ui barrel. Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/c4-mgr-auto. | 11.629 | UI: chart-line-pivot-points primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.647 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-pivot-points.tsx` ships `<ChartLinePivotPoints>` -- a pure-SVG line chart with classic **pivot point** levels rendering the pivot plus three support and three resistance lines overlaid on a single price line. **The computation:** `computeLinePivotPoints({high, low, close})` derives the seven classic ("floor-trader" / standard) pivot levels from the PRIOR period's high/low/close. The pivot `P = (H+L+C)/3` anchors three resistance levels above and three support levels below: `R1 = 2P-L`, `S1 = 2P-H`, `R2 = P+(H-L)`, `S2 = P-(H-L)`, `R3 = H+2(P-L)`, `S3 = L-2(H-P)`. The pivot is the mean of the prior HLC; R1/S1 reflect the low/high across the pivot; R2/S2 offset by the prior range; R3/S3 extend furthest. The function returns `null` when any of high/low/close is not finite. The seven levels are CONSTANTS for the whole chart -- they are not a per-bar transform of the plotted price series. `getLinePivotPointsLevelList(levels)` orders the seven top to bottom (R3, R2, R1, pivot, S1, S2, S3), each tagged with a stable `id`, a short `label`, the `value`, and a `kind` (`resistance`/`pivot`/`support`). **Worked fixture** (`prior = {high:20, low:10, close:18}`): `P=(20+10+18)/3=16`, range `H-L=10`; `R1=2*16-10=22`, `S1=2*16-20=12`; `R2=16+10=26`, `S2=16-10=6`; `R3=20+2(16-10)=32`, `S3=10-2(20-16)=2` -- all seven land on integers, ordered `S3 2 < S2 6 < S1 12 < P 16 < R1 22 < R2 26 < R3 32`, asserted with `toEqual`. The price fixture `[12,14,16,19,22,17,13]` straddles the pivot 16 -- 3 bars above, 3 below, 1 on. `runLinePivotPoints` sorts the finite price points by x, computes the levels from `options.prior`, and returns per-bar `samples` (each classified `above`/`below`/`on` the pivot), the above/below counts, and the level list; `ok` is false for fewer than two price points or a missing/invalid prior period. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs the moving-average overlays (`<ChartLineSmma>`, `<ChartLineLsma>`, ...) -- those derive a continuous line by averaging or regressing a sliding window of the price series; the pivot levels are seven STATIC horizontal support/resistance lines computed once from a SEPARATE prior-period HLC, not a per-bar transform of the plotted series. vs `<ChartLineFractal>` -- the fractal primitive detects swing pivots WITHIN the price series and marks discrete points; pivot points are precomputed support/resistance price levels that do not depend on the in-period price action. The component takes a `prior` `{high,low,close}` input rather than a smoothing `period` or a `wing`. All exported helpers carry a `LinePivotPoints`/`PivotPoints` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLinePivotPointsPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `prior` -- a `ChartLinePivotPointsInput` (`{high, low, close}`); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `priceColor` / `pivotColor` / `resistanceColor` / `supportColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showLevels`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `pivot` / `resistance` / `support`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_PIVOT_POINTS_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PRICE_COLOR/PIVOT_COLOR/RESISTANCE_COLOR/SUPPORT_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLinePivotPointsFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value); `computeLinePivotPoints(input)`; `getLinePivotPointsLevelList(levels)`; `runLinePivotPoints(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, levels, levelList, samples, aboveCount, belowCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, position}` (`position` is `above`/`below`/`on`) and each level `{id, label, value, kind}`; `computeLinePivotPointsLayout({data, prior, ...})` -- projects the price path plus one horizontal line per level, with a y-domain spanning both the price series and all seven levels; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points / invalid prior -> ok=false; `describeLinePivotPointsChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions pivot point + classic + floor-trader + resistance + support (No data when the prior is missing/invalid). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-pivot-points-aria-desc"` span; the price line + every price dot + every level line `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}` (level lines carry a Resistance/Pivot/Support aria-label with the value). data-section on every node (chart-line-pivot-points / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick / -tick-label / -levels / -level / -level-line / -level-label / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-position / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-pivot); root mirrors `data-pivot`, `data-above-count`, `data-below-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; each level group exposes `data-level-id`, `data-level-kind` and `data-level-value`. The pivot line is solid; resistance and support lines are dashed and each carries its label and value at the right edge. Tooltip on a price dot shows x, value and the position versus the pivot. Config badge in top-left calls out `PIVOT P=<pivot>`. The legend has four toggle items (Price / Pivot / Resistance / Support); `showLevels` is a master toggle for all seven lines. 56 vitest cases (getLinePivotPointsFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + value** + non-array -> [], computeLinePivotPoints incl. **derives the seven classic pivot levels** + **the pivot as the mean of high/low/close** + **R1 above + S1 below** + **R2/S2 offset by the range** + **R3/S3 extend furthest** + **null when a field is not finite** + null for null/undefined, getLinePivotPointsLevelList incl. **lists the seven levels top to bottom** + **tags each level with its kind** + the values + the labels, runLinePivotPoints incl. ok + **the computed levels** + the seven-entry level list + one sample per point + position classification + the above/below counts + **not-ok for fewer than two points** + **not-ok when the prior is invalid** + **not-ok when no prior is supplied** + x-sorting, computeLinePivotPointsLayout incl. ok + the price path + **one line per pivot level** + **a y-domain covering both the price and every level** + the run statistics + level-line containment + collapsed dims -> ok=false + too little data -> ok=false + **invalid prior -> ok=false**, describeLinePivotPointsChart incl. **the indicator vocabulary** + **the pivot value + position counts** + No data when the prior is missing + No data for an insufficient series, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / root data-* / svg + price line / **one line per pivot level** / **level-line kind tagging** / four-item legend / config badge pivot value / hide the price path / **hide the pivot line via the hidden set** / **hide the resistance lines** / **hide the support lines** / **hide every level line when showLevels is false** / series toggle / price dots / empty state on invalid prior / omit config badge / ref / displayName / animate flag) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. | 11.630 | UI: chart-line-gann primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.648 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-gann.tsx` ships `<ChartLineGann>` -- a pure-SVG line chart with a **Gann fan** of nine angled rays projected from a chosen pivot. **The computation:** `computeLineGannRays(options)` builds the nine W.D. Gann fan rays for a given price-per-time scale and fan direction. The classic price-time ratios are `1x8, 1x4, 1x3, 1x2, 1x1, 2x1, 3x1, 4x1, 8x1` (the exported `GANN_RATIOS` constant lists them in fan order). The notation `AxB` is A price units per B time units; `ratio = priceUnits/timeUnits`. The central `1x1` ray is the 45-degree reference of the fan (flagged `isPrimary`). Each ray's `slope` is `ratio * pricePerUnit` price per one x-unit, where `pricePerUnit` is the price rise of the 1x1 ray over one time unit; the slope is positive for an up fan (from a swing low) and negative for a down fan (from a swing high). A ray's value at coordinate `x` is `pivotValue + slope*(x - pivotX)`. **Worked fixture** (pivot at index 0 -- `x 0, value 10` -- `pricePerUnit 3`, up fan): slopes `ratio*3` = `1x8 0.375, 1x4 0.75, 1x3 1, 1x2 1.5, 1x1 3, 2x1 6, 3x1 9, 4x1 12, 8x1 24`; projected to the last bar `x 8` each ray endpoint value is `10 + slope*8` = `13, 16, 18, 22, 34, 58, 82, 106, 202` (asserted with `toEqual`). The 1x1 ray's value at `x` is `10 + 3x`; the price fixture `[10,14,13,18,25,24,31,30,40]` straddles it -- 4 bars above, 4 below, 1 on (the pivot itself always sits on the 1x1). `runLineGann` sorts the finite price points by x, resolves the pivot from a clamped `pivotIndex`, builds the rays, and returns per-bar `samples` classified `above`/`below`/`on` the 1x1 ray plus the above/below counts; `ok` is false for fewer than two points. `computeLineGannLayout` projects the price path plus one line per ray from the pivot to the chart's right edge -- the y-domain covers only the price range and the rays are clipped to the panel with an SVG `clipPath`, so a steep ray that leaves the panel does NOT stretch the price axis. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs `<ChartLinePivotPoints>` -- pivot points are seven HORIZONTAL support/resistance levels; the Gann fan is a set of SLOPED rays radiating from one anchor point at fixed angular ratios. vs the moving-average overlays (`<ChartLineSmma>`, `<ChartLineLsma>`, ...) -- those derive a continuous line from a sliding window of the price series; the Gann rays are static straight lines determined entirely by the pivot, the price-per-unit scale and the fixed ratio set. vs `<ChartLineFractal>` -- the fractal markers are discrete per-bar swing detections; the Gann fan is a fixed geometric construction projected forward from a single chosen pivot. All exported helpers carry a `LineGann`/`Gann` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineGannPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `pivotIndex` (which data point anchors the fan; default 0); `pricePerUnit` (the 1x1 price rise per x-unit; default 1); `direction` (`up`/`down`; default `up`); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `pivotRadius` / `priceColor` / `fanColor` / `primaryColor` / `pivotColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showFan`/`showPivot`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `fan` / `pivot`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_GANN_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PIVOT_RADIUS/PRICE_PER_UNIT/PRICE_COLOR/FAN_COLOR/PRIMARY_COLOR/PIVOT_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `GANN_RATIOS`; `getLineGannFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value); `normalizeLineGannPivotIndex(index, count)` (clamp to [0, count-1]; non-finite/negative -> 0, past-end -> last, fractional floors); `computeLineGannRays(options?)`; `runLineGann(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, pivotIndex, pivot, pricePerUnit, direction, rays, samples, aboveCount, belowCount, ok}`; each ray `{id, label, priceUnits, timeUnits, ratio, slope, isPrimary}` and each sample `{index, x, value, position}` (`position` is `above`/`below`/`on`); `computeLineGannLayout({data, pivotIndex, pricePerUnit, direction, ...})` -- projects the price path + nine ray lines (each `{id, label, ratio, slope, isPrimary, pivotPx, pivotPy, endPx, endPy, endValue}`); degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLineGannChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Gann fan + 1x1 + 45-degree + ray + pivot (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-gann-aria-desc"` span; the price line + every price dot + every fan ray + the pivot marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-gann / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick / -tick-label / -rays / -ray / -pivot / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-position / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-direction); root mirrors `data-pivot-index`, `data-pivot-value`, `data-price-per-unit`, `data-direction`, `data-above-count`, `data-below-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; each ray exposes `data-ray-id`, `data-ratio` and `data-primary`. The 1x1 ray is drawn solid and emphasized; the other eight rays are dashed; the pivot is a diamond marker; the rays group is clipped to the panel with an SVG clipPath. Tooltip on a price dot shows x, value and the position versus the 1x1. Config badge in top-left calls out `GANN <direction>`. The legend has three toggle items (Price / Gann fan / Pivot); `showFan` and `showPivot` are master toggles. 57 vitest cases (getLineGannFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + value** + non-array -> [], normalizeLineGannPivotIndex incl. floors a fractional + **clamps past the end** + **falls back for negative/NaN**, GANN_RATIOS incl. **the nine classic ratios in fan order**, computeLineGannRays incl. **nine rays** + **ratio as price units over time units** + **slope scaled by price-per-unit for an up fan** + **slope negated for a down fan** + **defaults the price-per-unit to one** + **flags only the 1x1 as primary**, runLineGann incl. ok + **the pivot resolved from the pivot index** + the echoed scale/direction + nine rays + one sample per point + position classification + the above/below counts + **not-ok for fewer than two points** + **clamps an out-of-range pivot index** + x-sorting + the default options, computeLineGannLayout incl. ok + the price path + **one line per fan ray** + **the pivot projected to the panel corner** + the y-domain + **each ray projected to its endpoint value** + the run statistics + collapsed dims -> ok=false + too little data -> ok=false, describeLineGannChart incl. **the indicator vocabulary** + the above/below counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / root data-* / **svg with a clip path** / **nine fan rays** / **exactly one primary 1x1 ray** / **the pivot marker** / the price line / three-item legend / config badge direction / hide the price path / **hide the fan rays via the hidden set** / **hide the pivot marker via the hidden set** / **hide every ray when showFan is false** / **hide the pivot when showPivot is false** / series toggle / price dots / empty state / omit config badge / ref / displayName / animate flag) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for the new files (conditional spreads honour `exactOptionalPropertyTypes`). Exported via components/ui barrel. Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/c4-mgr-auto. | 11.631 | UI: chart-line-andrews primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.649 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-andrews.tsx` ships `<ChartLineAndrews>` -- a pure-SVG line chart with an **Andrews Pitchfork** drawn from three pivot points. **The computation:** `computeLineAndrewsFork(pivots)` builds the pitchfork from a `{p1, p2, p3}` object. The midpoint `M = ((p2.x+p3.x)/2, (p2.value+p3.value)/2)`; the MEDIAN line runs from `p1` through `M`, so its slope is `(M.value - p1.value)/(M.x - p1.x)`. The UPPER and LOWER tines are two lines parallel to the median (same slope) passing through `p2` and `p3`. A line through `(ax,av)` with slope `s` has intercept `av - s*ax`; the tine with the LARGER intercept is the upper line. Because `M` is the average of `p2` and `p3`, the median's intercept is EXACTLY the average of the two tine intercepts -- the median sits midway between the tines. The function returns `null` when a pivot coordinate is not finite, or when `M.x === p1.x` (a vertical, slope-less median). **Worked fixture** (`p1 (0,10)`, `p2 (3,42)`, `p3 (7,18)`): `M = ((3+7)/2,(42+18)/2) = (5,30)`; median slope `(30-10)/(5-0) = 4`; intercepts -> p2 tine `42-4*3 = 30` (upper), median `10-4*0 = 10`, p3 tine `18-4*7 = -10` (lower) -- the median intercept 10 is exactly `(30 + -10)/2`. The median line is `value = 10 + 4x`; the price fixture `[12,13,20,21,26,33,32,40,41]` straddles it -- 4 bars above, 4 below, 1 on. Projected across `x 0..8` the three lines run from start values `[30,10,-10]` to end values `[62,42,22]` (asserted with `toEqual`). `runLineAndrews` sorts the finite price points by x, builds the fork from `options.pivots`, and returns per-bar `samples` classified `above`/`below`/`on` the median line plus the above/below counts; `ok` is false for fewer than two price points or invalid pivots. `computeLineAndrewsLayout` projects the price path, the three fork lines across the full x range, and a marker per pivot -- the fork lines are clipped to the panel with an SVG `clipPath` so a tine that leaves the panel does NOT stretch the price axis. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs `<ChartLineGann>` -- the Gann fan radiates nine rays from a SINGLE anchor at fixed angular ratios; the pitchfork's three lines are mutually PARALLEL and their slope and offsets are derived entirely from the geometry of the three chosen pivots. vs `<ChartLinePivotPoints>` -- pivot points are HORIZONTAL levels from a prior-period HLC; the pitchfork lines are sloped and anchored to three explicit chart points. vs the moving-average overlays -- those average a sliding window of the price series; the pitchfork is a static geometric construction independent of the in-period price. All exported helpers carry a `LineAndrews`/`Andrews` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineAndrewsPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `pivots` -- a `ChartLineAndrewsPivots` (`{p1, p2, p3}`, each `{x, value}`); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `pivotRadius` / `priceColor` / `medianColor` / `tineColor` / `pivotColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showFork`/`showPivots`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `fork` / `pivots`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_ANDREWS_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PIVOT_RADIUS/PRICE_COLOR/MEDIAN_COLOR/TINE_COLOR/PIVOT_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineAndrewsFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value); `computeLineAndrewsFork(pivots)`; `runLineAndrews(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, pivots, fork, samples, aboveCount, belowCount, ok}`; the fork `{slope, midpoint, lines}` where `lines` is `[upper, median, lower]` each `{id, label, slope, anchorX, anchorValue, intercept}`, and each sample `{index, x, value, position}` (`position` is `above`/`below`/`on`); `computeLineAndrewsLayout({data, pivots, ...})` -- projects the price path + three fork lines + three pivot markers; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points / invalid pivots -> ok=false; `describeLineAndrewsChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Andrews Pitchfork + median line + pivot + parallel + tine (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-andrews-aria-desc"` span; the price line + every price dot + every fork line + every pivot marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-andrews / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick / -tick-label / -fork / -fork-line / -pivots / -pivot / -pivot-marker / -pivot-label / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-position / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-slope); root mirrors `data-slope`, `data-above-count`, `data-below-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; each fork line exposes `data-line-id` and `data-median`; each pivot exposes `data-pivot-id`. The median line is drawn solid and emphasized; the two tines are dashed; each pivot is a labelled diamond marker; the fork group is clipped to the panel with an SVG clipPath. Tooltip on a price dot shows x, value and the position versus the median. Config badge in top-left calls out `ANDREWS m=<slope>`. The legend has three toggle items (Price / Pitchfork / Pivots); `showFork` and `showPivots` are master toggles. 55 vitest cases (getLineAndrewsFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + value** + non-array -> [], computeLineAndrewsFork incl. **the median slope through the midpoint of p2 and p3** + **orders the lines upper/median/lower** + **anchors the median on the first pivot** + **derives the three line intercepts** + **keeps the median intercept midway between the two tines** + **null when a pivot coordinate is not finite** + null for null/undefined + **null when the median would be vertical**, runLineAndrews incl. ok + **the computed fork** + **echoes the pivots** + one sample per point + position classification + the above/below counts + **not-ok for fewer than two points** + **not-ok when the pivots are invalid** + **not-ok when no pivots are supplied** + x-sorting, computeLineAndrewsLayout incl. ok + the price path + **three fork lines and three pivot markers** + **a y-domain covering the price range and the pivots** + **each fork line projected across the full x range** + the run statistics + collapsed dims -> ok=false + too little data -> ok=false + invalid pivots -> ok=false, describeLineAndrewsChart incl. **the indicator vocabulary** + the slope + the above/below counts + No data when pivots are missing + No data for an insufficient series, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / root data-* / **svg with a clip path** / **three fork lines** / **exactly one median line** / **three pivot markers** / the price line / three-item legend / config badge slope / hide the price path / **hide the fork lines via the hidden set** / **hide the pivot markers via the hidden set** / **hide every fork line when showFork is false** / **hide the pivot markers when showPivots is false** / series toggle / price dots / empty state / omit config badge / ref / displayName / animate flag) all pass under vitest 4.1.5.
