| 11.632 | UI: chart-line-heikin-ashi primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.650 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-heikin-ashi.tsx` ships `<ChartLineHeikinAshi>` -- a pure-SVG line chart overlaying a **Heikin-Ashi** smoothed close series that filters noise from the raw close line. **The computation:** `computeLineHeikinAshi(bars)` applies the Heikin-Ashi ("average bar") transform to raw OHLC bars. `haClose = (open+high+low+close)/4`; `haOpen = (open+close)/2` for the first bar, else `(haOpen[prev]+haClose[prev])/2`; `haHigh = max(high, haOpen, haClose)`; `haLow = min(low, haOpen, haClose)`. `haClose` blends all four OHLC fields so a single-bar spike is damped to a quarter of its weight; `haOpen` is RECURSIVE -- it carries the previous bar forward, so a noisy close is averaged down rather than swinging the line, and that recursion is what filters noise out of the raw close. A bar is `bullish` when its `haClose` sits above its `haOpen`, `bearish` when below, a `doji` when equal. **Worked fixture** (five OHLC bars chosen so the recursive `haOpen` lands on integers): `bar 0` haClose `(10+20+10+12)/4=13` haOpen `(10+12)/2=11` bullish; `bar 1` haClose `(14+22+10+18)/4=16` haOpen `(11+13)/2=12` bullish; `bar 2` haClose `(18+26+14+22)/4=20` haOpen `(12+16)/2=14` bullish; `bar 3` haClose `(16+18+4+6)/4=11` haOpen `(14+20)/2=17` bearish; `bar 4` haClose `(14+16+12+14)/4=14` haOpen `(17+11)/2=14` doji -- the raw close drops to 6 at bar 3 but the HA close only falls to 11 (the spike is filtered), asserted with `toEqual`. 3 bullish, 1 bearish, 1 doji; final HA close 14. `runLineHeikinAshi` sorts the finite OHLC bars by x, runs the transform, and returns per-bar `samples` classified `bullish`/`bearish`/`doji` plus the bullish/bearish/doji counts and the final HA close; `ok` is false for fewer than two bars. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs the moving-average overlays (`<ChartLineSmma>`, `<ChartLineLsma>`, ...) -- those weight a sliding WINDOW of a single value series; Heikin-Ashi blends all four OHLC fields of EACH bar and carries a recursive open forward, so it is a per-bar transform, not a windowed average. vs `<ChartLineFractal>` -- the fractal detects discrete swing pivots; Heikin-Ashi produces a continuous smoothed series and a per-bar bullish/bearish/doji trend. The component takes full OHLC bars rather than a single `{x, value}` series, and exposes the trend as the canonical Heikin-Ashi signal. All exported helpers carry a `LineHeikinAshi`/`HeikinAshi` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineHeikinAshiPoint[]` (`{x, open, high, low, close}`); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `priceColor` / `haColor` / `bullishColor` / `bearishColor` / `dojiColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showHa`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `close` / `ha`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_HEIKIN_ASHI_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PRICE_COLOR/HA_COLOR/BULLISH_COLOR/BEARISH_COLOR/DOJI_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineHeikinAshiFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + open + high + low + close); `computeLineHeikinAshi(bars)` -- returns `{haOpen, haHigh, haLow, haClose}` per bar; `runLineHeikinAshi(points)` -- returns `{series, bars, samples, haCloseSeries, bullishCount, bearishCount, dojiCount, haCloseFinal, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, open, high, low, close, haOpen, haHigh, haLow, haClose, trend}` (`trend` is `bullish`/`bearish`/`doji`); `computeLineHeikinAshiLayout({data, ...})` -- projects the raw close path and the HA close path onto one shared panel with markers per bar (markers coloured by trend); degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 bars -> ok=false; `describeLineHeikinAshiChart(data)` -- ARIA summary mentions Heikin-Ashi + smoothed + noise + bullish + bearish (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-heikin-ashi-aria-desc"` span; the raw close line + the HA close line + every price dot + every HA marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-heikin-ashi / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick / -tick-label / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -ha-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-close / -tooltip-ha-close / -tooltip-trend / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-trend); root mirrors `data-bullish-count`, `data-bearish-count`, `data-doji-count`, `data-ha-close-final`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; markers expose `data-ha-close` and `data-trend`. The raw close is a plain line; the HA close line's markers are coloured by trend (bullish/bearish/doji). Tooltip on a price dot or a marker shows x, the raw close, the HA close and the trend. Config badge in top-left calls out `HEIKIN-ASHI <latest trend>`. The legend has two toggle items (Close / HA Close); `showHa` is a master toggle for the HA line + markers. 48 vitest cases (getLineHeikinAshiFinitePoints incl. **keeps only bars with finite x + open + high + low + close** + non-array -> [], computeLineHeikinAshi incl. **applies the Heikin-Ashi transform** + **seeds the first HA open with the raw open-close mean** + **takes each HA close as the OHLC mean** + **carries the HA open forward recursively** + **haHigh + haLow as the max + min** + non-array -> [], runLineHeikinAshi incl. ok + **the HA bars** + **the HA close series** + **classifies each sample bullish/bearish/doji** + **counts the bullish/bearish/doji bars** + **the final HA close** + not-ok short series + empty/null -> ok=false + x-sorting + one sample per bar, computeLineHeikinAshiLayout incl. ok + the price + HA paths + **one dot + marker per bar** + **a y-domain covering the raw close + the HA close** + the run statistics + **marker trend tagging** + marker containment + collapsed dims -> ok=false + too little data -> ok=false, describeLineHeikinAshiChart incl. **the indicator vocabulary** + the bullish/bearish/doji counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / root data-* / svg + raw close + HA lines / **one marker per bar** / **marker trend tagging** / two-item legend / **config badge latest trend** / hide the raw close path / **hide the HA line + markers when showHa is false** / hide the HA line via the hidden set / series toggle / price dots / empty state / omit config badge / ref / displayName / animate flag) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. | 11.633 | UI: chart-line-renko primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.651 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-renko.tsx` ships `<ChartLineRenko>` -- a pure-SVG chart rendering a **Renko brick** transform that advances only on fixed price moves. **The computation:** `computeLineRenkoBricks(values, brickSize)` discards time and sub-brick noise: it walks the price series from the first value tracking the last brick boundary `level`, and `while price >= level + brickSize` emits an UP brick `[level, level+brickSize]` then advances `level`; `while price <= level - brickSize` emits a DOWN brick `[level, level-brickSize]`. A move smaller than one brick emits NOTHING; a single large move emits several bricks in one step. Each brick records its ordinal `index`, the `sourceIndex` of the price that triggered it, a `direction`, and its `open`/`close` levels (up brick `open < close`, down brick `open > close`). **Worked fixture** (`brickSize 2`, price series `[10,11,14,13,9,10,16]`): `->14` emits two up bricks `(10-12, 12-14)`; `->9` emits two down bricks `(14-12, 12-10)`; `->16` emits three up bricks `(10-12, 12-14, 14-16)` -- the 11, 13 and 10 wiggles never move a full brick so they emit nothing; seven bricks total (5 up, 2 down) spanning the price range 10 to 16, last brick close 16, asserted with `toEqual`. `runLineRenko` sorts the finite price points by x, runs the transform, and returns the brick series, the up/down counts, the price range and the last brick close; `ok` is false when no brick forms (a low-volatility series). **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs the time-based line charts (`<ChartLineSmma>`, `<ChartLineHeikinAshi>`, ...) -- those plot one mark per time bar; the Renko x-axis is a BRICK ORDINAL, not time, and a brick is added only on a full price move, so a flat stretch of the series produces no bricks at all. vs `<ChartLineHeikinAshi>` -- Heikin-Ashi smooths every bar into a bar of the same cadence; Renko re-samples the series onto a fixed price grid and drops the time axis entirely. vs `<ChartLineFractal>` -- fractals mark discrete pivots on the original series; Renko rebuilds the whole series as a row of equal-size bricks. All exported helpers carry a `LineRenko`/`Renko` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineRenkoPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `brickSize` (the fixed price move; default 1); `strokeWidth` (trend line) / `brickGap` / `upColor` / `downColor` / `trendColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showTrend`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `up` / `down` / `trend`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onBrickClick({brick})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_RENKO_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/BRICK_SIZE/BRICK_GAP/UP_COLOR/DOWN_COLOR/TREND_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineRenkoFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value); `normalizeLineRenkoBrickSize(size, fallback)` (coerce to a positive finite number; zero/negative/non-finite falls back); `computeLineRenkoBricks(values, brickSize)`; `runLineRenko(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, brickSize, bricks, upCount, downCount, priceMin, priceMax, lastClose, ok}`; each brick `{index, sourceIndex, direction, open, close}`; `computeLineRenkoLayout({data, brickSize, ...})` -- lays the bricks out one per column and projects each to a rect `{index, sourceIndex, direction, open, close, x, y, width, height, cx, closeY}`, plus a trend path through the brick closes; degenerate canvas / no bricks -> ok=false; `describeLineRenkoChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Renko + brick + fixed + noise (No data when no brick forms). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-renko-aria-desc"` span; every brick rect + the trend line `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-renko / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick / -tick-label / -bricks / -brick / -trend-line / -tooltip / -tooltip-index / -tooltip-direction / -tooltip-open / -tooltip-close / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-brick); root mirrors `data-brick-size`, `data-brick-count`, `data-up-count`, `data-down-count`, `data-last-close`, `data-animate`; each brick exposes `data-brick-index`, `data-direction`, `data-open` and `data-close`. Each brick is an SVG rect -- up bricks green, down bricks red -- laid out one per column, with a trend line connecting the brick closes. Tooltip on a brick shows the brick index, direction, open and close. Config badge in top-left calls out `RENKO b=<brick size>`. The legend has three toggle items (Up brick / Down brick / Trend). 49 vitest cases (getLineRenkoFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + value** + non-array -> [], normalizeLineRenkoBrickSize incl. **keeps a positive finite size** + **zero/negative/non-finite falls back**, computeLineRenkoBricks incl. **transforms the price series into fixed-size bricks** + **an up brick for a brick-size rise** + **a down brick for a brick-size fall** + **several bricks for a single large move** + **nothing while the price stays inside one brick** + **non-positive brick size -> []** + non-array / empty -> [], runLineRenko incl. ok + the brick size + **the brick series** + the up/down counts + **the price range + last close** + **not-ok when no brick forms** + x-sorting + the default brick size, computeLineRenkoLayout incl. ok + **one rect per brick** + the trend path + the y-domain + the run statistics + **rect direction tagging** + **positive rect width + equal height** + collapsed dims -> ok=false + **no bricks -> ok=false**, describeLineRenkoChart incl. **the indicator vocabulary** + the brick counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / root data-* / **svg with one rect per brick** / **brick direction tagging** / the trend line / three-item legend / config badge brick size / **hide the up bricks via the hidden set** / **hide the down bricks via the hidden set** / hide the trend line via the hidden set / **hide the trend line when showTrend is false** / series toggle / empty state when no brick forms / omit config badge / ref / displayName / animate flag) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. | 11.634 | UI: chart-line-kagi primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.652 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-kagi.tsx` ships `<ChartLineKagi>` -- a pure-SVG **Kagi line** transform that reverses direction only on a reversal threshold and thickens on trend confirmation. **The computation:** a Kagi line is a continuous zigzag of vertical segments that ignores time and reacts only to price. `computeLineKagiTurns(values, reversalAmount)` walks the price from the first value: the line EXTENDS with the price while it advances (`p > extreme` updates the extreme), and REVERSES direction only when the price moves back by at least `reversalAmount` from the current segment extreme (`extreme - p >= rev` for an up line, `p - extreme >= rev` for a down line), pushing the reversal price as a new vertex. It returns the vertex sequence; if the price never moves a full reversal amount it returns a single starting vertex. `computeLineKagiSegments(turns)` derives the segments between consecutive vertices -- each `up` or `down` -- and the line's THICKNESS, a persistent state: it starts `thin` (yin), flips to `thick` (yang) when an up segment pushes past the vertex two turns back (the prior shoulder), and back to `thin` when a down segment breaks below the vertex two turns back (the prior waist); a segment that confirms nothing carries the previous thickness. **Worked fixture** (`reversalAmount 3`, price series `[10,14,13,11,16,12,20]`): `10->14` start an up line; `->13->11` the 11 is a 3-unit pullback -> reverse down; `->16` a 5-unit rebound -> reverse up; `->12` a 4-unit pullback -> reverse down; `->20` an 8-unit rebound -> reverse up; `turns = [10,14,11,16,12,20]`. Segments: `up thin` (10-14), `down thin` (14-11), `up thick` (11-16, breaks the prior shoulder 14), `down thick` (16-12, the pullback does NOT break the prior waist 11 so the thickness CARRIES), `up thick` (12-20) -- 3 up, 2 down; 2 thin, 3 thick, asserted with `toEqual`. `runLineKagi` sorts the finite price points by x, runs the transform, and returns the turns, segments, and up/down/thin/thick counts; `ok` is false when the price never reverses. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs `<ChartLineRenko>` -- Renko fixes the brick size and steps once per full price move; a Kagi segment has a VARIABLE length and the line reverses only on a counter-move of the reversal amount, so a long trend is one long segment rather than many bricks. vs the time-based line charts -- the Kagi x-axis is a segment ordinal, not time; flat price stretches add nothing. vs `<ChartLineHeikinAshi>` -- Heikin-Ashi smooths every bar; Kagi re-samples the series onto reversal-driven vertices and carries a thin/thick trend state. All exported helpers carry a `LineKagi`/`Kagi` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineKagiPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `reversalAmount` (the counter-move threshold; default 1); `thinWidth` / `thickWidth` / `thinColor` / `thickColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `thin` / `thick`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onSegmentClick({segment})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_KAGI_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/REVERSAL/THIN_WIDTH/THICK_WIDTH/THIN_COLOR/THICK_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineKagiFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value); `normalizeLineKagiReversal(amount, fallback)` (coerce to a positive finite number; zero/negative/non-finite falls back); `computeLineKagiTurns(values, reversalAmount)`; `computeLineKagiSegments(turns)`; `runLineKagi(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, reversalAmount, turns, segments, upCount, downCount, thinCount, thickCount, ok}`; each segment `{index, fromValue, toValue, direction, thickness}`; `computeLineKagiLayout({data, reversalAmount, ...})` -- lays the segments out one per column and projects each to a right-angled vertical-plus-connector path `{index, direction, thickness, fromValue, toValue, cx, fromY, toY, path}`; degenerate canvas / no segment -> ok=false; `describeLineKagiChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Kagi + reversal + thin + thick + yang (No data when the price never reverses). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-kagi-aria-desc"` span; every segment path `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-kagi / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick / -tick-label / -segments / -segment / -tooltip / -tooltip-index / -tooltip-direction / -tooltip-thickness / -tooltip-range / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-reversal); root mirrors `data-reversal-amount`, `data-segment-count`, `data-up-count`, `data-down-count`, `data-thin-count`, `data-thick-count`, `data-animate`; each segment exposes `data-segment-index`, `data-direction` and `data-thickness`. Each segment is an SVG path -- yin segments thin and red, yang segments thick and green -- laid out one per column with a right-angled connector. Tooltip on a segment shows the segment index, direction, thickness and price range. Config badge in top-left calls out `KAGI r=<reversal amount>`. The legend has two toggle items (Yin / Yang). 49 vitest cases (getLineKagiFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + value** + non-array -> [], normalizeLineKagiReversal incl. **keeps a positive finite amount** + **zero/negative/non-finite falls back**, computeLineKagiTurns incl. **walks the price into Kagi turning points** + **extends the line without a turn while the price advances** + **collapses a monotonic run into a single segment** + **a single vertex when the price never reverses** + **non-positive reversal amount -> []** + non-array / empty -> [], computeLineKagiSegments incl. **derives the segments with direction + thickness** + **starts the line thin** + **thickens when an up segment pushes past the prior shoulder** + **carries the thickness when a segment confirms nothing** + fewer than two turns -> [], runLineKagi incl. ok + the reversal amount + **the turning points** + **the segment series** + **the up/down/thin/thick counts** + **not-ok when the price never reverses** + x-sorting + the default reversal, computeLineKagiLayout incl. ok + **one segment line per Kagi segment** + **a path string for each segment** + the y-domain + the run statistics + **segment direction + thickness tagging** + collapsed dims -> ok=false + **not-ok when the price never reverses**, describeLineKagiChart incl. **the indicator vocabulary** + the segment counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / root data-* / **svg with one path per segment** / **segment thickness tagging** / two-item legend / config badge reversal amount / **hide the thin segments via the hidden set** / **hide the thick segments via the hidden set** / series toggle / empty state when the price never reverses / omit config badge / ref / displayName / animate flag) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. | 11.635 | UI: chart-line-mcginley primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.653 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-mcginley.tsx` ships `<ChartLineMcGinley>` -- a pure-SVG line chart with a **McGinley Dynamic** adaptive moving average overlay that self-adjusts to speed, drawn on ONE shared price panel. **The computation:** `computeLineMcGinley(values, period)` is John R. McGinley's adaptive moving average. It seeds with the first price (`MD[0] = price[0]`) and folds each later bar in as `MD[i] = MD[i-1] + (price[i] - MD[i-1]) / (N * (price[i] / MD[i-1])^4)`. The `(price / MD_prev)^4` divisor is the ADAPTIVE term: in an up market `price/MD_prev > 1` so the divisor grows and the adjustment shrinks -- the average SLOWS; in a down market the ratio is below one, the divisor shrinks and the adjustment grows -- the average SPEEDS UP to track the fall. The fourth power makes the response strongly non-linear. The line is defined from index 0 with NO warm-up; a flat series holds exactly at its constant; a non-positive previous value re-seeds on the current price to avoid a divide-by-zero. **Worked fixture** (`period N = 10`, price series `[10,16,12,9,13]`): the first move `10->16` has divisor `10 * (16/10)^4 = 10 * 6.5536 = 65.536`, so `MD[1] = 10 + 6/65.536 = 10.091552734375` exactly (the dyadic anchor; later recursive values are genuinely inexact and asserted with `toBeCloseTo` plus the invariant that each McGinley value is a fractional step strictly between the previous value and the current price). The series runs up then drops sharply to 9 -- the down step adapts faster (a smaller divisor) than the up steps -- and the price ends above the McGinley line on 3 bars, below on 1. `runLineMcGinley` sorts the finite price points by x, computes the series, and returns per-bar `samples` classified `above`/`below`/`on` the McGinley line plus the final/min/max readings and the above/below counts; `ok` is false for fewer than two points. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs the fixed-weight moving averages (`<ChartLineSmma>`, `<ChartLineWma>`, an EMA) -- those fold price in at a CONSTANT weight; the McGinley divisor changes every bar with the price-to-average ratio raised to the fourth power, so the line accelerates into declines and decelerates into rallies. vs `<ChartLineKama>` / `<ChartLineVidya>` -- those adapt their smoothing from an efficiency ratio or volatility band; McGinley adapts purely from the instantaneous price/average ratio with no separate look-back window. vs `<ChartLineLsma>` -- the LSMA is a regression endpoint; the McGinley Dynamic is a single recursive step with an adaptive divisor and no warm-up. All exported helpers carry a `LineMcGinley`/`McGinley` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineMcGinleyPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `period` (the base smoothing constant N; default 14); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `priceColor` / `mdColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showMd`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `md`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_MCGINLEY_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/PRICE_COLOR/MD_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineMcGinleyFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value); `normalizeLineMcGinleyPeriod(period, fallback)` (coerce to a positive integer; non-finite or sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineMcGinley(values, period)` -- returns a plain `number[]` (no warm-up); `runLineMcGinley(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, period, md, samples, mdFinal, mdMin, mdMax, aboveCount, belowCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, md, position}` (`position` is `above`/`below`/`on`); `computeLineMcGinleyLayout({data, ...})` -- projects the price path and the McGinley path onto one shared panel with a marker per bar; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLineMcGinleyChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions McGinley Dynamic + adaptive + self-adjusts + speed (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-mcginley-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the McGinley line + every price dot + every McGinley marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-mcginley / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick / -tick-label / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -md-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-md / -tooltip-position / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-period); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-md-final`, `data-above-count`, `data-below-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; markers expose `data-md`. Tooltip on a price dot or a marker shows x, value, the McGinley reading and the position. Config badge in top-left calls out `MCGINLEY p=<period>`. The legend has two toggle items (Price / McGinley); `showMd` is a master toggle for the McGinley line + markers. 49 vitest cases (getLineMcGinleyFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + value** + non-array -> [], normalizeLineMcGinleyPeriod incl. floors a fractional + **sub-1 / NaN / negative falls back**, computeLineMcGinley incl. **seeds the line with the first price** + **folds the first bar in by the adaptive divisor** + **holds a flat series exactly at its constant** + **keeps each value a fractional step between the previous and the price** + the single-point series + non-array / empty -> [], runLineMcGinley incl. ok + the period + **the McGinley series with no warm-up** + position classification + the above/below counts + **the final/min/max readings** + not-ok short series + empty/null -> ok=false + x-sorting + one sample per point + the default period 14, computeLineMcGinleyLayout incl. ok + the price + McGinley paths + **one dot + marker per bar** + **a y-domain covering both the price + the McGinley line** + the run statistics + marker containment + collapsed dims -> ok=false + too little data -> ok=false, describeLineMcGinleyChart incl. **the indicator vocabulary** + the above/below counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / root data-* / svg + price + McGinley lines / **one marker per bar** / two-item legend / config badge period / hide the price path / **hide the McGinley line + markers when showMd is false** / hide the McGinley line via the hidden set / series toggle / price dots / empty state / omit config badge / ref / displayName / animate flag) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. | 11.636 | UI: chart-line-laguerre primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.654 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-laguerre.tsx` ships `<ChartLineLaguerre>` -- a pure-SVG line chart with an **Ehlers Laguerre filter** overlay using a four-stage cascade, drawn on ONE shared price panel. **The computation:** `computeLineLaguerreStages(values, gamma)` runs John Ehlers' four-stage Laguerre cascade. Seeded with the first price, each bar advances the stages: `L0 = (1-gamma)*price + gamma*L0_prev`, then each later stage damps the one before it as `L_k = -gamma*L_{k-1} + L_{k-1}_prev + gamma*L_k_prev`. `computeLineLaguerre(values, gamma)` blends the four stages into the filter, `(L0 + 2*L1 + 2*L2 + L3)/6`. Each stage damps the one before it; chaining four gives the filter its smooth, low-lag shape from a single parameter. The `gamma` damping factor lives in `[0, 1)` -- a higher gamma damps more (smoother, more lag). The filter is defined from index 0 with no warm-up (the seed makes `Filter[0] = price[0]`); a flat series holds at its constant. **Worked fixture** (`gamma 0.5`, price series `[10,18,8,16,6]` -- with gamma a half every cascade step is an exact half): the stages `L0=[10,14,11,13.5,9.75]`, `L1=[10,8,12.5,10.5,13.875]`, `L2=[10,11,7.25,10.875,9]`, `L3=[10,9.5,12.125,7.875,10.3125]` blend exactly into the filter `[10,10.25,10.4375,10.6875,10.96875]` (asserted with `toEqual`). The price oscillates wildly (10,18,8,16,6) but the filter rises smoothly -- the cascade is the noise filter. 2 bars above, 2 below, 1 on. `runLineLaguerre` sorts the finite price points by x, computes the filter, and returns per-bar `samples` classified `above`/`below`/`on` the filter plus the final/min/max readings and the above/below counts; `ok` is false for fewer than two points. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs the windowed moving averages (`<ChartLineSmma>`, `<ChartLineWma>`) -- those average a fixed look-back window; the Laguerre filter has NO window, it chains four damped recursive stages governed by one gamma. vs `<ChartLineMcGinley>` -- the McGinley Dynamic is a single recursive step with an adaptive divisor; the Laguerre filter is a fixed four-stage cascade, its smoothness coming from the depth of the cascade rather than from adapting the weight. vs `<ChartLineT3>` -- the T3 cascades EMAs with a volume factor; the Laguerre cascade uses Laguerre-polynomial stages and blends them by the fixed `(1,2,2,1)/6` weights. All exported helpers carry a `LineLaguerre`/`Laguerre` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineLaguerrePoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `gamma` (the damping factor in `[0,1)`; default 0.5); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `priceColor` / `filterColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showFilter`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `filter`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_LAGUERRE_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/GAMMA/PRICE_COLOR/FILTER_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineLaguerreFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value); `normalizeLineLaguerreGamma(gamma, fallback)` (coerce to the valid damping range [0,1); anything outside, or non-finite, falls back); `computeLineLaguerreStages(values, gamma)` -- returns `{l0, l1, l2, l3}` cascade stage series; `computeLineLaguerre(values, gamma)` -- the filter line, a plain `number[]` (no warm-up); `runLineLaguerre(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, gamma, filter, samples, filterFinal, filterMin, filterMax, aboveCount, belowCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, filter, position}` (`position` is `above`/`below`/`on`); `computeLineLaguerreLayout({data, ...})` -- projects the price path and the filter path onto one shared panel with a marker per bar; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLineLaguerreChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Ehlers + Laguerre + four-stage cascade + gamma + filter (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-laguerre-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the Laguerre filter line + every price dot + every filter marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-laguerre / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick / -tick-label / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -filter-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-filter / -tooltip-position / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-gamma); root mirrors `data-gamma`, `data-filter-final`, `data-above-count`, `data-below-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; markers expose `data-filter`. Tooltip on a price dot or a marker shows x, value, the filter reading and the position. Config badge in top-left calls out `LAGUERRE g=<gamma>`. The legend has two toggle items (Price / Laguerre); `showFilter` is a master toggle for the filter line + markers. 54 vitest cases (getLineLaguerreFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + value** + non-array -> [], normalizeLineLaguerreGamma incl. **keeps a gamma inside the damping range** + **falls back for a gamma of one or above** + **falls back for a negative or non-finite gamma**, computeLineLaguerreStages incl. **runs the four-stage Laguerre cascade** + **seeds every stage with the first price** + **holds a flat series flat in every stage** + non-array -> empty stages, computeLineLaguerre incl. **blends the cascade into the Laguerre filter** + **starts the filter at the first price** + **is the (L0+2L1+2L2+L3)/6 blend of the stages** + **holds a flat series exactly at its constant** + the single-point series + non-array / empty -> [], runLineLaguerre incl. ok + the gamma + **the Laguerre filter series with no warm-up** + position classification + the above/below counts + **the final/min/max readings** + not-ok short series + empty/null -> ok=false + x-sorting + one sample per point + the default gamma 0.5, computeLineLaguerreLayout incl. ok + the price + filter paths + **one dot + marker per bar** + **a y-domain covering both the price + the filter** + the run statistics + marker containment + collapsed dims -> ok=false + too little data -> ok=false, describeLineLaguerreChart incl. **the indicator vocabulary** + the above/below counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / root data-* / svg + price + Laguerre lines / **one marker per bar** / two-item legend / config badge gamma / hide the price path / **hide the Laguerre line + markers when showFilter is false** / hide the Laguerre line via the hidden set / series toggle / price dots / empty state / omit config badge / ref / displayName / animate flag) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. | 11.637 | UI: chart-line-supersmoother primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.655 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-supersmoother.tsx` ships `<ChartLineSuperSmoother>` -- a pure-SVG line chart with an **Ehlers Super Smoother** two-pole filter overlay, drawn on ONE shared price panel. **The computation:** `computeLineSuperSmootherCoefficients(period)` derives John Ehlers' two-pole low-pass filter coefficients from `arg = 1.414*PI/period`: `a1 = exp(-arg)`, `b1 = 2*a1*cos(arg)`, `c2 = b1`, `c3 = -(a1*a1)`, `c1 = 1 - c2 - c3`. The coefficients sum to one, so the filter has unit gain at zero frequency and passes a flat series straight through. `computeLineSuperSmoother(values, period)` runs the filter: it seeds the first two bars straight from the price (`SS[0]=price[0]`, `SS[1]=price[1]`) and from the third bar applies the recursion `SS[i] = c1*(price[i]+price[i-1])/2 + c2*SS[i-1] + c3*SS[i-2]`. The two-pole feedback gives a steep frequency roll-off -- it rejects bar-to-bar noise sharply while adding very little lag. The line is defined from index 0 with NO warm-up. **Worked fixture** (`period 10`, price series `[10,10,30,8,32,6]`): the coefficients come from `exp` and `cos` so they are irrational -- the filter values are asserted against the recursion itself (using the exported coefficients) rather than hard-coded. The price oscillates wildly while the filter stays in a smooth 10..19 band, so bars 2-5 sit clearly above or below it with a wide margin -- positions `on, on, above, below, above, below`, 2 above and 2 below. `runLineSuperSmoother` sorts the finite price points by x, computes the filter, and returns per-bar `samples` classified `above`/`below`/`on` the smoother plus the final/min/max readings and the above/below counts; `ok` is false for fewer than two points. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs the single-pole exponential averages (an EMA, `<ChartLineSmma>`) -- those are one-pole filters with a gentle 6 dB/octave roll-off; the Super Smoother is a true TWO-POLE filter, so it rejects high-frequency noise far more sharply for the same lag. vs `<ChartLineLaguerre>` -- the Laguerre filter chains four Laguerre-polynomial stages governed by gamma; the Super Smoother is a fixed second-order recursion whose coefficients come directly from a cutoff period via `exp` and `cos`. vs `<ChartLineMcGinley>` -- the McGinley Dynamic adapts its divisor every bar; the Super Smoother is a fixed linear filter, its response set entirely by the period. All exported helpers carry a `LineSuperSmoother`/`SuperSmoother` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineSuperSmootherPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `period` (the cutoff period; default 10); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `priceColor` / `smootherColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showSmoother`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `smoother`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_SUPERSMOOTHER_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/PRICE_COLOR/SMOOTHER_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineSuperSmootherFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value); `normalizeLineSuperSmootherPeriod(period, fallback)` (coerce to a positive integer; non-finite or sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineSuperSmootherCoefficients(period)` -- returns `{a1, b1, c1, c2, c3}`; `computeLineSuperSmoother(values, period)` -- the filter line, a plain `number[]` (no warm-up); `runLineSuperSmoother(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, period, smoother, samples, smootherFinal, smootherMin, smootherMax, aboveCount, belowCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, smoother, position}` (`position` is `above`/`below`/`on`); `computeLineSuperSmootherLayout({data, ...})` -- projects the price path and the smoother path onto one shared panel with a marker per bar; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLineSuperSmootherChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Ehlers + Super Smoother + two-pole + filter (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-supersmoother-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the Super Smoother line + every price dot + every smoother marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-supersmoother / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick / -tick-label / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -smoother-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-smoother / -tooltip-position / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-period); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-smoother-final`, `data-above-count`, `data-below-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; markers expose `data-smoother`. Tooltip on a price dot or a marker shows x, value, the smoother reading and the position. Config badge in top-left calls out `SMOOTHER p=<period>`. The legend has two toggle items (Price / Super Smoother); `showSmoother` is a master toggle for the smoother line + markers. 54 vitest cases (getLineSuperSmootherFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + value** + non-array -> [], normalizeLineSuperSmootherPeriod incl. floors a fractional + **sub-1 / NaN / negative falls back**, computeLineSuperSmootherCoefficients incl. **derives c2 directly from b1** + **derives c3 as the negative a1 squared** + **derives c1 so the coefficients sum to one** + **keeps a1 inside the unit interval** + **falls back to the default period for a non-positive period**, computeLineSuperSmoother incl. **seeds the first two bars straight from the price** + **follows the two-pole recursion from the third bar** + **passes a flat series through at its constant** + **the two-point series** + the single-point series + non-array / empty -> [], runLineSuperSmoother incl. ok + the period + **the Super Smoother series with no warm-up** + position classification + the above/below counts + **the final/min/max readings** + not-ok short series + empty/null -> ok=false + x-sorting + one sample per point + the default period 10, computeLineSuperSmootherLayout incl. ok + the price + smoother paths + **one dot + marker per bar** + **a y-domain covering both the price + the smoother** + the run statistics + marker containment + collapsed dims -> ok=false + too little data -> ok=false, describeLineSuperSmootherChart incl. **the indicator vocabulary** + the above/below counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / root data-* / svg + price + Super Smoother lines / **one marker per bar** / two-item legend / config badge period / hide the price path / **hide the smoother line + markers when showSmoother is false** / hide the smoother line via the hidden set / series toggle / price dots / empty state / omit config badge / ref / displayName / animate flag) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. | 11.638 | UI: chart-line-roofing primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.656 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-roofing.tsx` ships `<ChartLineRoofing>` -- a pure-SVG TWO-PANEL chart with an **Ehlers Roofing filter** combining a high-pass and a smoother (the price panel on top, the Roofing filter oscillator sub-panel below; the Roofing output is detrended/zero-centred so it cannot share the price axis). **The computation:** the Roofing filter chains two stages. STAGE ONE -- `computeLineRoofingHighpass(values, hpPeriod)` is a two-pole high-pass that strips the low-frequency trend ("wander"). With `angle = 0.707*2*PI/hpPeriod` the alpha is `(cos(angle)+sin(angle)-1)/cos(angle)` (exported as `computeLineRoofingHighpassAlpha`), and `HP[i] = (1-alpha1/2)^2*(price[i]-2*price[i-1]+price[i-2]) + 2*(1-alpha1)*HP[i-1] - (1-alpha1)^2*HP[i-2]`. STAGE TWO -- `computeLineRoofingSmoother(values, ssPeriod)` is an Ehlers Super Smoother (two-pole low-pass) that strips the high-frequency noise; with `arg = 1.414*PI/ssPeriod` the coefficients are `a1 = exp(-arg)`, `b1 = 2*a1*cos(arg)`, `c2 = b1`, `c3 = -(a1*a1)`, `c1 = 1-c2-c3` (exported as `computeLineRoofingSmootherCoefficients`), and `Filt[i] = c1*(in[i]+in[i-1])/2 + c2*Filt[i-1] + c3*Filt[i-2]`. `computeLineRoofing(values, hpPeriod, ssPeriod)` chains them: the Roofing filter is the Super Smoother of the high-pass of the price -- combining the two leaves only a band of cycle frequencies, an oscillator centred on zero. The first two bars seed at zero; a flat price series stays at zero through BOTH stages. **Worked fixture** (`hpPeriod 48`, `ssPeriod 10`, price series `[10,12,11,15,13,18,14,20]`): the coefficients come from `cos`/`sin`/`exp` so they are irrational -- the filter values are asserted against the recursions themselves (using the exported alpha/coefficients) rather than hard-coded; a flat series stays at zero throughout (asserted with `toEqual` `[0,0,...]`). `runLineRoofing` sorts the finite price points by x, runs both stages, and returns the high-pass and roofing series, per-bar `samples` classified `positive`/`negative`/`zero`, and the positive/negative counts; `ok` is false for fewer than two points. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs `<ChartLineSuperSmoother>` -- the Super Smoother is a single low-pass stage that KEEPS the trend; the Roofing filter prepends a high-pass so its output is DETRENDED -- a band-pass oscillator, not a smoothed price. vs the moving-average overlays -- those produce a smoothed price that shares the price axis; the Roofing filter is zero-centred and lives on its own sub-panel. vs `<ChartLineLaguerre>` -- the Laguerre filter is one low-pass cascade; the Roofing filter explicitly combines a high-pass AND a low-pass to pass only a band. All exported helpers carry a `LineRoofing`/`Roofing` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineRoofingPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `hpPeriod` (the high-pass cutoff; default 48) / `ssPeriod` (the smoother cutoff; default 10); `pricePanelRatio` (the fraction of the height given to the price panel; default 0.55, clamped to [0.3, 0.8]); `gap` (the pixel gap between the stacked panels); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `priceColor` / `roofingColor` / `positiveColor` / `negativeColor` / `zeroColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showRoofing`/`showZeroLine`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `roofing`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_ROOFING_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/HP_PERIOD/SS_PERIOD/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/PRICE_COLOR/ROOFING_COLOR/POSITIVE_COLOR/NEGATIVE_COLOR/ZERO_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineRoofingFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value); `normalizeLineRoofingPeriod(period, fallback)` (coerce to a positive integer; non-finite or sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineRoofingHighpassAlpha(hpPeriod)`; `computeLineRoofingHighpass(values, hpPeriod)`; `computeLineRoofingSmootherCoefficients(ssPeriod)` -- returns `{a1, b1, c1, c2, c3}`; `computeLineRoofingSmoother(values, ssPeriod)`; `computeLineRoofing(values, hpPeriod, ssPeriod)`; `runLineRoofing(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, hpPeriod, ssPeriod, highpass, roofing, samples, roofingFinal, roofingMin, roofingMax, positiveCount, negativeCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, highpass, roofing, sign}` (`sign` is `positive`/`negative`/`zero`); `computeLineRoofingLayout({data, hpPeriod, ssPeriod, ...})` -- stacks the price panel above the roofing panel, gives the roofing panel a symmetric y-bound around zero, projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `roofingPath`, `roofingMarkers`, the `zeroY` centerline; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLineRoofingChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Roofing filter + high-pass + Super Smoother + band-pass (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-roofing-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the roofing line + every price dot + every roofing marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-roofing / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -panel-label / -zero-line / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -roofing-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-highpass / -tooltip-roofing / -tooltip-sign / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-periods); root mirrors `data-hp-period`, `data-ss-period`, `data-roofing-final`, `data-positive-count`, `data-negative-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; grid / axis / tick / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"roofing"`; markers expose `data-roofing` and `data-sign`. The roofing panel carries a dashed zero centerline and sign-coloured markers (green positive, red negative, grey zero). Tooltip on a price dot or a marker shows x, value, the high-pass reading, the roofing reading and the sign. Config badge in top-left calls out `ROOFING <hp>/<ss>`. The legend has two toggle items (Price / Roofing filter). 65 vitest cases (getLineRoofingFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + value** + non-array -> [], normalizeLineRoofingPeriod incl. floors a fractional + **sub-1 / NaN / negative falls back**, computeLineRoofingHighpassAlpha incl. **alpha inside the unit interval** + **non-positive period falls back**, computeLineRoofingHighpass incl. **seeds the first two bars at zero** + **follows the two-pole high-pass recursion** + **holds a flat series at zero** + single-point + non-array -> [], computeLineRoofingSmootherCoefficients incl. **c2 from b1 + c3 from a1 squared** + **c1 so the coefficients sum to one** + **a1 inside the unit interval** + **non-positive period falls back**, computeLineRoofingSmoother incl. **seeds the first two bars from the input** + **follows the two-pole smoother recursion** + **holds an all-zero input at zero** + non-array -> [], computeLineRoofing incl. **the smoother of the high-pass composition** + **seeds the first two bars at zero** + **holds a flat series at zero** + non-array -> [], runLineRoofing incl. ok + the periods + **the high-pass + roofing series** + **the zero-seeded signs** + **self-consistent positive/negative counts** + **the final/min/max readings** + not-ok short series + empty/null -> ok=false + x-sorting + one sample per point + the default periods, computeLineRoofingLayout incl. ok + **stacks the price panel above the roofing panel** + both paths + **one dot + marker per bar** + **the zero line inside the roofing panel** + the run statistics + | 11.639 | UI: chart-line-hilbert primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.657 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-hilbert.tsx` ships `<ChartLineHilbert>` -- a pure-SVG TWO-PANEL chart with a **Hilbert Transform** dominant-cycle period panel (the price panel on top, the dominant-cycle-period sub-panel below; the period is a count of bars, not a price, so it gets its own panel). **The computation:** three stages measure how many bars the current price cycle takes. STAGE ONE -- `computeLineHilbertSmooth(values)` applies Ehlers' 4-3-2-1 weighted pre-smoother `(4*p[i]+3*p[i-1]+2*p[i-2]+p[i-3])/10` (the first three bars pass the raw price through). STAGE TWO -- `computeLineHilbertTransform(values)` is Ehlers' Hilbert transform filter, a seven-tap quadrature FIR `0.0962*(v[i]-v[i-6]) + 0.5769*(v[i-2]-v[i-4])`; it shifts the signal 90 degrees and has no DC response, so it both detrends and produces the quadrature component (the first six bars are zero; a CONSTANT series transforms to zero EXACTLY since both differences vanish). STAGE THREE -- `computeLineHilbertCycle(values, minPeriod, maxPeriod)` smooths the price, detrends it through the transform, and takes a SECOND transform for the quadrature; the in-phase signal is the detrender delayed three bars (the filter's group delay). The instantaneous phase `atan2(quadrature, in-phase)` rotates once per cycle, so the per-bar phase change `deltaPhase` gives the dominant cycle period `2*PI / deltaPhase`. The period is CLAMPED to the `[minPeriod, maxPeriod]` band and exponentially smoothed -- both operations keep every reading provably inside the band; a flat series reports the maximum period for every bar. **Worked fixture:** the phase uses `atan2` so the period values are irrational -- they are asserted via the `[min, max]` clamp band (every value provably inside it, the rock-solid property) plus the exact-testable smoother and transform recursions; a flat series reports `maxPeriod` for every bar (asserted with `toEqual`), the smoother holds a flat series at its constant, and the transform takes a constant to zero exactly. `runLineHilbert` sorts the finite price points by x, runs the pipeline, and returns the smooth and period series, per-bar `samples` classified `fast`/`slow`/`mid` relative to the band midpoint, and the fast/slow counts; `ok` is false for fewer than two points; an inverted band (`maxPeriod <= minPeriod`) is repaired to `minPeriod+1`. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs the smoothing filters (`<ChartLineSuperSmoother>`, `<ChartLineRoofing>`, `<ChartLineLaguerre>`) -- those produce a smoothed or filtered PRICE; this primitive measures the PERIOD of the price cycle, its output being a count of bars. vs `<ChartLineRoofing>` -- the Roofing filter is a band-pass on the price; the Hilbert dominant cycle uses the transform's quadrature output to read the cycle LENGTH via the phase rate. vs the oscillator panels -- the cycle panel is not zero-centred; it spans the fixed `[minPeriod, maxPeriod]` band with a midline at the fast/slow boundary. All exported helpers carry a `LineHilbert`/`Hilbert` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineHilbertPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `minPeriod` (the lower cycle bound; default 6) / `maxPeriod` (the upper bound; default 50); `pricePanelRatio` (the fraction of the height given to the price panel; default 0.55, clamped to [0.3, 0.8]); `gap` (the pixel gap between the stacked panels); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `priceColor` / `cycleColor` / `fastColor` / `slowColor` / `midColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showCycle`/`showMidline`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `cycle`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_HILBERT_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/MIN_PERIOD/MAX_PERIOD/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/PRICE_COLOR/CYCLE_COLOR/FAST_COLOR/SLOW_COLOR/MID_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineHilbertFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value); `normalizeLineHilbertPeriod(period, fallback)` (coerce to a positive integer; non-finite or sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineHilbertSmooth(values)`; `computeLineHilbertTransform(values)`; `computeLineHilbertCycle(values, minPeriod, maxPeriod)`; `runLineHilbert(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, minPeriod, maxPeriod, smooth, period, samples, periodFinal, periodMin, periodMax, fastCount, slowCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, smooth, period, cycleClass}` (`cycleClass` is `fast`/`slow`/`mid`); `computeLineHilbertLayout({data, minPeriod, maxPeriod, ...})` -- stacks the price panel above the cycle panel (the cycle panel y-domain fixed to the `[minPeriod, maxPeriod]` band so the fast/slow midline always sits at the panel centre), projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `cyclePath`, `cycleMarkers`, the `midY` line; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLineHilbertChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Hilbert Transform + dominant cycle + quadrature + phase + period (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-hilbert-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the cycle line + every price dot + every cycle marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-hilbert / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -panel-label / -midline / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -cycle-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-smooth / -tooltip-period / -tooltip-class / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-range); root mirrors `data-min-period`, `data-max-period`, `data-period-final`, `data-fast-count`, `data-slow-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; grid / axis / tick / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"cycle"`; markers expose `data-period` and `data-cycle-class`. The cycle panel carries a dashed midline at the fast/slow boundary and class-coloured markers (green fast, red slow, grey mid). Tooltip on a price dot or a marker shows x, value, the smoothed price, the period and the cycle class. Config badge in top-left calls out `HILBERT <min>-<max>`. The legend has two toggle items (Price / Cycle period). 62 vitest cases (getLineHilbertFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + value** + non-array -> [], normalizeLineHilbertPeriod incl. floors a fractional + **sub-1 / NaN / negative falls back**, computeLineHilbertSmooth incl. **passes the first three bars through raw** + **the 4-3-2-1 weighted average from the fourth bar** + **holds a flat series at its constant** + non-array -> [], computeLineHilbertTransform incl. **seeds the first six bars at zero** + **applies the seven-tap quadrature filter** + **transforms a constant series to zero** + non-array -> [], computeLineHilbertCycle incl. **the maximum period for a flat series** + **keeps every period inside the clamp band** + **seeds the first bar at the maximum period** + one period per input + **honours custom period bounds** + non-array -> [], runLineHilbert incl. ok + the period bounds + **the smooth + period series** + **the slow first bar** + **self-consistent fast/slow counts** + **the final/min/max readings** + not-ok short series + empty/null -> ok=false + x-sorting + one sample per point + the default band + **repairs an inverted band**, computeLineHilbertLayout incl. ok + **stacks the price panel above the cycle panel** + both paths + **one dot + marker per bar** + **the fixed cycle y-domain** + **the midline placement** + the run statistics + collapsed dims -> ok=false + too little data -> ok=false, describeLineHilbertChart incl. **the indicator vocabulary** + the fast/slow counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / root data-* / svg + price + cycle lines / **both panel labels** / **one marker per bar** / **the midline** / two-item legend / config badge period range / hide the price path / **hide the cycle line + markers when showCycle is false** / hide the cycle line via the hidden set / **hide the midline when showMidline is false** / series toggle / price dots / empty state / omit config badge / ref / displayName / animate flag) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. | 11.640 | UI: chart-line-stddev primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.658 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-stddev.tsx` ships `<ChartLineStdDev>` -- a pure-SVG line chart with a **rolling standard deviation band** overlay around a moving average, drawn on ONE shared price panel. **The computation:** the band has three lines around the price. `computeLineStdDevBasis(values, period)` is the BASIS -- the `period`-bar simple moving average. `computeLineStdDev(values, period)` is the rolling POPULATION standard deviation over the same window: `mean = sum(window)/period`, `variance = sum((value-mean)^2)/period`, `stddev = sqrt(variance)`; a flat window reports zero. `runLineStdDev` derives the band edges `upper = basis + multiplier*stddev` and `lower = basis - multiplier*stddev` -- so the band WIDENS when the price turns volatile and TIGHTENS when it settles (a Bollinger-style volatility channel). All three series are null through the `period-1` warm-up. **Worked fixture** (`period 2`, `multiplier 2`, price series `[10,14,11,17,12]` -- with a two-bar window the basis is the midpoint and the standard deviation is exactly half the absolute bar-to-bar move, so every value lands on a clean number): `[10,14] -> basis 12, stddev 2, upper 16, lower 8`; `[14,11] -> basis 12.5, stddev 1.5, upper 15.5, lower 9.5`; `[11,17] -> basis 14, stddev 3, upper 20, lower 8`; `[17,12] -> basis 14.5, stddev 2.5, upper 19.5, lower 9.5` -- asserted with `toEqual`. The price runs above the basis on 2 bars and below on 2. `runLineStdDev` sorts the finite price points by x, computes the basis, standard deviation and band series, and returns per-bar `samples` classified `above`/`below`/`on` the basis plus the final basis/band readings and the above/below counts; `ok` is false for fewer than two points. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs the smoothing overlays (`<ChartLineSmma>`, `<ChartLineSuperSmoother>`) -- those draw a SINGLE smoothed line; this primitive draws a volatility CHANNEL: a basis line bracketed by an upper and a lower band edge. vs `<ChartLineRoofing>` -- the Roofing filter is a band-pass in the FREQUENCY sense; this band is a PRICE channel whose width is the rolling dispersion of the price. vs `<ChartLineDisparity>` -- disparity measures the percent gap from a moving average; this primitive measures the absolute dispersion and draws it as a band. All exported helpers carry a `LineStdDev`/`StdDev` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineStdDevPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `period` (the moving-average / window length; default 20) / `multiplier` (the deviations to the band edge; default 2); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `priceColor` / `basisColor` / `bandColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showBasis`/`showBands`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `basis` / `band`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_STDDEV_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/MULTIPLIER/PRICE_COLOR/BASIS_COLOR/BAND_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineStdDevFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value); `normalizeLineStdDevPeriod(period, fallback)` (coerce to a positive integer; non-finite or sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `normalizeLineStdDevMultiplier(multiplier, fallback)` (coerce to a positive finite number); `computeLineStdDevBasis(values, period)`; `computeLineStdDev(values, period)`; `runLineStdDev(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, period, multiplier, basis, stddev, upper, lower, samples, basisFinal, upperFinal, lowerFinal, aboveCount, belowCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, basis, stddev, upper, lower, position}` (`position` is `above`/`below`/`on`); `computeLineStdDevLayout({data, period, multiplier, ...})` -- projects the price / basis / upper / lower paths plus a closed filled band-area path on one shared panel, with price dots and basis markers; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLineStdDevChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions standard deviation + band + moving average + basis + volatile (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-stddev-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the basis line + the upper line + the lower line + every price dot + every basis marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-stddev / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick / -tick-label / -band-area / -upper-line / -lower-line / -basis-line / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-basis / -tooltip-upper / -tooltip-lower / -tooltip-position / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-multiplier`, `data-basis-final`, `data-above-count`, `data-below-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; markers expose `data-basis`. The band edges are drawn as upper + lower lines with a translucent filled area between them; the basis is a separate line with per-bar markers. Tooltip on a price dot or a basis marker shows x, value, basis, upper, lower and the position. Config badge in top-left calls out `STDDEV <period>/<multiplier>`. The legend has three toggle items (Price / Basis / Std dev band); `showBasis` and `showBands` are master toggles. 60 vitest cases (getLineStdDevFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + value** + non-array -> [], normalizeLineStdDevPeriod incl. floors a fractional + **sub-1 / NaN / negative falls back**, normalizeLineStdDevMultiplier incl. **keeps a positive finite multiplier** + **zero/negative/non-finite falls back**, computeLineStdDevBasis incl. **the period-bar simple moving average** + the null warm-up + **the flat-series hold** + the shorter-than-period series + non-array -> [], computeLineStdDev incl. **the rolling population standard deviation** + the null warm-up + **zero for a flat series** + the shorter-than-period series + non-array -> [], runLineStdDev incl. ok + the period + multiplier + **the basis + stddev series** + **the upper + lower band series** + **the multiplier-of-deviations band placement** + position classification + the above/below counts + the final readings + not-ok short series + empty/null -> ok=false + x-sorting + one sample per point + the default period 20 + multiplier 2, computeLineStdDevLayout incl. ok + the price/basis/upper/lower paths + **the closed band area path** + **one dot per bar + one marker per defined basis** + **a y-domain covering the price + the bands** + the run statistics + marker containment + collapsed dims -> ok=false + too little data -> ok=false, describeLineStdDevChart incl. **the indicator vocabulary** + the above/below counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / root data-* / svg + price + basis + upper + lower lines / **the band area** / **one marker per defined basis** / three-item legend / config badge period + multiplier / hide the price path / **hide the basis line + markers when showBasis is false** / **hide the bands when showBands is false** / hide the bands via the hidden set / series toggle / price dots / empty state / omit config badge / ref / displayName / animate flag) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for the new files (conditional spreads honour `exactOptionalPropertyTypes`). Exported via components/ui barrel. Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/c4-mgr-auto. | 11.641 | UI: chart-line-bandwidth primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.659 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-bandwidth.tsx` ships `<ChartLineBandwidth>` -- a pure-SVG TWO-PANEL chart with a **Bollinger Bandwidth** indicator panel measuring band width relative to the middle line (the price panel on top, the bandwidth oscillator sub-panel below). **The computation:** the Bollinger Bandwidth reduces a Bollinger band to one number. `computeLineBandwidthBasis(values, period)` is the `period`-bar simple moving average (the middle line); `computeLineBandwidthStdDev(values, period)` is the rolling POPULATION standard deviation over the same window; `computeLineBandwidth(values, period, multiplier)` derives the bandwidth: `upper = basis + multiplier*stddev`, `lower = basis - multiplier*stddev`, `bandwidth = (upper - lower)/basis`. The bandwidth is the band width expressed as a FRACTION of its middle line -- a LOW reading marks a volatility squeeze (the bands are tight, often the prelude to a breakout); a HIGH reading marks an expansion. All three series are null through the warm-up; a flat series reports zero bandwidth; a zero basis reports zero. **Worked fixture** (`period 2`, `multiplier 2`, price series `[5,15,9,27,45]` -- with a two-bar window the basis is the midpoint and the standard deviation is exactly half the bar-to-bar move, so every value lands on a clean number): `[5,15] -> basis 10, stddev 5, band [0,20], bandwidth 2`; `[15,9] -> basis 12, stddev 3, band [6,18], bandwidth 1`; `[9,27] -> basis 18, stddev 9, band [0,36], bandwidth 2`; `[27,45] -> basis 36, stddev 9, band [18,54], bandwidth 1` -- asserted with `toEqual` `[null,2,1,2,1]`. The mean bandwidth is 1.5; 2 bars read wide, 2 narrow. `runLineBandwidth` sorts the finite price points by x, runs the pipeline, and returns the basis/stddev/bandwidth series, per-bar `samples` classified `wide`/`narrow`/`mid` relative to the mean bandwidth, the mean reading, and the wide/narrow counts; `ok` is false for fewer than two points. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs `<ChartLineStdDev>` -- the standard deviation band primitive DRAWS the band (basis, upper, lower) on the price panel; this primitive does NOT draw the band, it reduces it to a single scalar (the band width over the basis) in its own sub-panel. vs `<ChartLineDisparity>` -- disparity measures the percent gap between price and a moving average; bandwidth measures the WIDTH of the volatility band, not the price's distance from it. vs the oscillator panels -- the bandwidth panel is ANCHORED at zero (the bandwidth is non-negative) rather than centred on zero, with a mean line marking the wide/narrow boundary. All exported helpers carry a `LineBandwidth`/`Bandwidth` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineBandwidthPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `period` (the moving-average / window length; default 20) / `multiplier` (the band deviations; default 2); `pricePanelRatio` (the fraction of the height given to the price panel; default 0.55, clamped to [0.3, 0.8]); `gap` (the pixel gap between the stacked panels); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `priceColor` / `bandwidthColor` / `wideColor` / `narrowColor` / `midColor` / `meanColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showBandwidth`/`showMeanLine`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `bandwidth`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_BANDWIDTH_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/MULTIPLIER/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/PRICE_COLOR/BANDWIDTH_COLOR/WIDE_COLOR/NARROW_COLOR/MID_COLOR/MEAN_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineBandwidthFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value); `normalizeLineBandwidthPeriod(period, fallback)` (coerce to a positive integer; non-finite or sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `normalizeLineBandwidthMultiplier(multiplier, fallback)` (coerce to a positive finite number); `computeLineBandwidthBasis(values, period)`; `computeLineBandwidthStdDev(values, period)`; `computeLineBandwidth(values, period, multiplier)`; `runLineBandwidth(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, period, multiplier, basis, stddev, bandwidth, samples, bandwidthMean, bandwidthFinal, bandwidthMin, bandwidthMax, wideCount, narrowCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, basis, stddev, bandwidth, widthClass}` (`widthClass` is `wide`/`narrow`/`mid`); `computeLineBandwidthLayout({data, period, multiplier, ...})` -- stacks the price panel above the bandwidth panel (the bandwidth panel y-domain anchored at zero), projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `bandwidthPath`, `bandwidthMarkers`, the `meanY` line; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLineBandwidthChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Bollinger Bandwidth + middle line + squeeze + expansion (No data when missing). The bandwidth panel carries a dashed mean line at the wide/narrow boundary and class-coloured markers (red wide, green narrow, grey mid). | 11.642 | UI: chart-line-envelope primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.660 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-envelope.tsx` ships `<ChartLineEnvelope>` -- a pure-SVG line chart with a **moving average envelope of fixed percentage bands above and below**, drawn on ONE shared price panel (single-panel overlay). **The computation:** the envelope brackets a moving average with two bands at a fixed percentage offset. `computeLineEnvelopeBasis(values, period)` is the BASIS -- the `period`-bar simple moving average (the middle line). `computeLineEnvelopeBands(values, period, percent)` derives the band edges: with `factor = percent/100`, `upper = basis*(1+factor)` and `lower = basis*(1-factor)`. Because the offset is a fixed fraction OF THE BASIS ITSELF, the envelope keeps a CONSTANT RELATIVE WIDTH regardless of volatility -- and even a perfectly flat price series still carries a non-zero band. All three series are null through the `period-1` warm-up. **Worked fixture** (`period 2`, `percent 25`, price series `[6,18,10,30,14]` -- with a two-bar window the basis is the exact midpoint and a 25% envelope multiplies it by 1.25 / 0.75, so every value lands on a clean number): `[6,18] -> basis 12, upper 15, lower 9`; `[18,10] -> basis 14, upper 17.5, lower 10.5`; `[10,30] -> basis 20, upper 25, lower 15`; `[30,14] -> basis 22, upper 27.5, lower 16.5` -- so `basis [null,12,14,20,22]`, `upper [null,15,17.5,25,27.5]`, `lower [null,9,10.5,15,16.5]`, asserted with `toEqual`. The price runs above the basis on 2 bars and below on 2. A flat fixture `[8,8,8,8]` `percent 25` keeps `upper [null,10,10,10]` / `lower [null,6,6,6]` -- the band never collapses. `runLineEnvelope` sorts the finite price points by x, computes the basis and envelope series, and returns per-bar `samples` classified `above`/`below`/`on` the basis plus the final basis/band readings and the above/below counts; `ok` is false for fewer than two points. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs `<ChartLineStdDev>` -- the standard deviation band sizes its offset from the ROLLING DISPERSION of the price, so it widens and narrows with volatility and collapses to zero on a flat series; the envelope offset is a CONSTANT PERCENTAGE of the basis -- fixed relative width, never collapsing. vs `<ChartLineBandwidth>` -- bandwidth reduces a band to a single scalar in a sub-panel; the envelope draws the full upper/lower channel on the price panel. vs the smoothing overlays (`<ChartLineSmma>`, `<ChartLineSuperSmoother>`, `<ChartLineLaguerre>`) -- those draw a SINGLE smoothed line; the envelope draws a CHANNEL: a basis line bracketed by an upper and a lower band edge with a filled band area between them. All exported helpers carry a `LineEnvelope`/`Envelope` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineEnvelopePoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `period` (the moving-average window length; default 20) / `percent` (the band offset percentage; default 5); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `priceColor` / `basisColor` / `envelopeColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showBasis`/`showEnvelope`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `basis` / `envelope`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_ENVELOPE_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/PERCENT/PRICE_COLOR/BASIS_COLOR/ENVELOPE_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineEnvelopeFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value); `normalizeLineEnvelopePeriod(period, fallback)` (coerce to a positive integer; non-finite or sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `normalizeLineEnvelopePercent(percent, fallback)` (coerce to a positive finite number; non-positive or non-finite falls back); `computeLineEnvelopeBasis(values, period)`; `computeLineEnvelopeBands(values, period, percent)` -- returns `{upper, lower}`; `runLineEnvelope(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, period, percent, basis, upper, lower, samples, basisFinal, upperFinal, lowerFinal, aboveCount, belowCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, basis, upper, lower, position}` (`position` is `above`/`below`/`on`); `computeLineEnvelopeLayout({data, period, percent, ...})` -- projects the price / basis / upper / lower paths plus a closed filled band-area path (`M...L...Z`) on one shared panel, with price dots and basis markers; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLineEnvelopeChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions moving average envelope + fixed percentage bands + basis + above/below counts (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-envelope-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the basis line + the upper line + the lower line + every price dot + every basis marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-envelope / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick / -tick-label / -band-area / -upper-line / -lower-line / -basis-line / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-basis / -tooltip-upper / -tooltip-lower / -tooltip-position / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-percent`, `data-basis-final`, `data-above-count`, `data-below-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; markers expose `data-basis`/`data-upper`/`data-lower`. Tooltip on a price dot or a basis marker shows x, value, basis, upper, lower and the position. Config badge in top-left calls out `ENVELOPE <period>/<percent>%`.
