| 11.643 | UI: chart-line-pmo primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.661 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-pmo.tsx` ships `<ChartLinePmo>` -- a pure-SVG TWO-PANEL chart with a **DecisionPoint Price Momentum Oscillator** using a double-smoothed rate of change (the price panel on top, the PMO oscillator sub-panel below; the PMO is normalized and zero-centred so it cannot share the price axis). **The computation:** the PMO double-smooths a rate of change. `computeLinePmoRoc(values)` is the 1-period rate of change as a percentage, `roc = 100*(price[i]/price[i-1]-1)` -- null for the first bar (no prior price) and for a zero prior price (division by zero). `computeLinePmoCustomEma(values, period)` is the DecisionPoint custom EMA with smoothing constant `2/period` (exported as `computeLinePmoSmoothingConstant`); it accepts a nullable input, carries leading nulls, seeds at the first defined value, and runs `ema[i] = k*input[i] + (1-k)*ema[i-1]`. `computeLinePmo(values, smooth1Period, smooth2Period, signalPeriod)` chains them: `smoothedRoc = customEMA(roc, smooth1Period)*10`, `pmo = customEMA(smoothedRoc, smooth2Period)`, `signal = customEMA(pmo, signalPeriod)`. The ROC carries the raw momentum, the two custom EMAs strip its noise, the `*10` rescales it into a readable oscillator centred on zero, and the signal line smooths the PMO once more. Defaults are the classic 35/20/10. A flat price series stays at zero through the whole pipeline. **Worked fixture** (`smooth1Period 4`, `smooth2Period 4`, `signalPeriod 4`, price series `[64,32,48,72,108,162]` -- the price ratios are exactly 0.5 and 1.5 so the ROC is -50/+50, and every custom EMA constant is `2/4 = 0.5`, keeping the arithmetic dyadic and exact): `roc [.,-50,50,50,50,50]`, `ema1 [.,-50,0,25,37.5,43.75]`, `smoothedRoc [.,-500,0,250,375,437.5]`, `pmo [.,-500,-250,0,187.5,312.5]`, `signal [.,-500,-375,-187.5,0,156.25]` -- asserted with `toEqual`. The PMO runs negative on 2 bars, positive on 2, and crosses zero once. `runLinePmo` sorts the finite price points by x, runs the pipeline, and returns the roc/smoothedRoc/pmo/signal series, per-bar `samples` classified `positive`/`negative`/`zero` by the sign of the PMO, the final PMO and signal readings, and the positive/negative counts; `ok` is false for fewer than two points. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs `<ChartLineStdDev>` / `<ChartLineEnvelope>` -- those draw a BAND around the price on the price panel; the PMO is a momentum oscillator on its own zero-centred sub-panel, not a band. vs `<ChartLineRoofing>` -- the Roofing filter is a band-pass in the FREQUENCY sense; the PMO is a double-smoothed rate of change, a momentum measure, not a frequency filter. vs `<ChartLineBandwidth>` -- bandwidth is a single non-negative scalar anchored at zero; the PMO swings positive and negative around zero and carries a second (signal) line. All exported helpers carry a `LinePmo`/`Pmo` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLinePmoPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `smooth1Period` (the first custom-EMA period; default 35) / `smooth2Period` (the second; default 20) / `signalPeriod` (the signal-line period; default 10); `pricePanelRatio` (the fraction of the height given to the price panel; default 0.55, clamped to [0.3, 0.8]); `gap` (the pixel gap between the stacked panels); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `priceColor` / `pmoColor` / `signalColor` / `positiveColor` / `negativeColor` / `zeroColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showPmo`/`showSignal`/`showZeroLine`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `pmo` / `signal`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_PMO_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/SMOOTH1_PERIOD/SMOOTH2_PERIOD/SIGNAL_PERIOD/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/PRICE_COLOR/PMO_COLOR/SIGNAL_COLOR/POSITIVE_COLOR/NEGATIVE_COLOR/ZERO_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLinePmoFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value); `normalizeLinePmoPeriod(period, fallback)` (coerce to an integer of at least 2 so the `2/period` constant stays in (0,1]; non-finite or sub-2 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLinePmoSmoothingConstant(period, fallback?)`; `computeLinePmoRoc(values)`; `computeLinePmoCustomEma(values, period)`; `computeLinePmo(values, s1, s2, sig)` -- returns `{roc, smoothedRoc, pmo, signal}`; `runLinePmo(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, smooth1Period, smooth2Period, signalPeriod, roc, smoothedRoc, pmo, signal, samples, pmoFinal, signalFinal, pmoMin, pmoMax, positiveCount, negativeCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, roc, smoothedRoc, pmo, signal, sign}` (`sign` is `positive`/`negative`/`zero`); `computeLinePmoLayout({data, smooth1Period, smooth2Period, signalPeriod, ...})` -- stacks the price panel above the PMO panel (the PMO panel y-domain symmetric around zero, covering both the PMO and the signal line), projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `pmoPath`, `pmoMarkers`, `signalPath`, the `zeroY` centerline; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLinePmoChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Price Momentum Oscillator + double-smoothed + rate of change + signal line (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-pmo-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the PMO line + the signal line + every price dot + every PMO marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-pmo / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -panel-label / -zero-line / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -pmo-line / -signal-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-roc / -tooltip-pmo / -tooltip-signal / -tooltip-sign / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-periods); root mirrors `data-smooth1-period`, `data-smooth2-period`, `data-signal-period`, `data-pmo-final`, `data-positive-count`, `data-negative-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; grid / axis / tick / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"pmo"`; markers expose `data-pmo` and `data-sign`. The PMO panel carries a dashed zero centerline, the PMO line with sign-coloured markers (green positive, red negative, grey zero), and the signal line as a dashed overlay. Tooltip on a price dot or a marker shows x, value, the roc, the pmo, the signal and the sign. Config badge in top-left calls out `PMO <s1>/<s2>/<sig>`. The legend has three toggle items (Price / PMO / Signal). 67 vitest cases (getLinePmoFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + value** + non-array -> [], normalizeLinePmoPeriod incl. floors a fractional + **sub-2 / NaN / negative falls back**, computeLinePmoSmoothingConstant incl. **two over the period** + **sub-2 period falls back**, computeLinePmoRoc incl. **the 1-period percentage rate of change** + **the null first bar** + **the null zero-prior-price reading** + **the flat-series zero hold** + non-array -> [], computeLinePmoCustomEma incl. **seeds with the first value** + **carries leading nulls and seeds at the first defined value** + **follows the custom-EMA recursion** + **holds a flat input** + non-array -> [], computeLinePmo incl. **the roc/smoothedRoc/pmo/signal series against the hand-verified fixture** + **the pmo as the custom EMA of the smoothed roc** + **the signal as the custom EMA of the pmo** + **the smoothed roc as ten times the first custom EMA** + **the flat-series zero hold through the whole pipeline** + non-array -> empty series, runLinePmo incl. ok + the periods + **the four series** + **the null-pmo first bar classified zero** + **sign classification by the pmo sign** + **self-consistent positive/negative counts** + **the final pmo + signal readings** + **the min/max pmo readings** + not-ok short series + empty/null -> ok=false + | 11.644 | UI: chart-line-trendflex primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.662 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-trendflex.tsx` ships `<ChartLineTrendflex>` -- a pure-SVG TWO-PANEL chart with an **Ehlers Trendflex indicator** measuring trend strength from a Super Smoother (the price panel on top, the Trendflex oscillator sub-panel below; the Trendflex is RMS-normalized and zero-centred so it cannot share the price axis). **The computation:** the Trendflex reads trend strength off a Super Smoother in three stages. STAGE ONE -- `computeLineTrendflexSmoother(values, period)` is the Ehlers Super Smoother of the price, a two-pole low-pass filter run at HALF the period; with `arg = 1.414*PI/(0.5*period)` the coefficients are `a1 = exp(-arg)`, `b1 = 2*a1*cos(arg)`, `c2 = b1`, `c3 = -(a1*a1)`, `c1 = 1-c2-c3` (exported as `computeLineTrendflexSmootherCoefficients`), and `filt[i] = c1*(price[i]+price[i-1])/2 + c2*filt[i-1] + c3*filt[i-2]`. STAGE TWO -- `computeLineTrendflexSum(filt, period)` is the trend sum, `sum[i] = (1/period)*SUM[count=1..period](filt[i]-filt[i-count])` -- equivalently the filtered value minus the mean of the previous `period` filtered values: positive while the smoother is rising, negative while it is falling; null until the lookback window is full. STAGE THREE -- `computeLineTrendflex(values, period)` normalizes the sum by the square root of an adaptively accumulated mean square, `ms[i] = 0.04*sum[i]^2 + 0.96*ms[i-1]` and `trendflex[i] = ms[i]!=0 ? sum[i]/sqrt(ms[i]) : 0`. The result is a zero-centred oscillator -- a large positive reading marks a strong uptrend, a large negative reading a strong downtrend, a reading near zero no trend; an all-zero price series stays at zero through the whole pipeline. **Worked fixture:** the Super Smoother coefficients come from `exp`/`cos` so the pipeline values are irrational -- they are pinned by the recursions themselves (recomputed with the exported coefficients/recursion) plus exact anchors. The trend sum is exact-testable on its own: fed the linear ramp `[0,1,2,3,4,5,6,7,8]` with `period 4`, every filtered value sits a constant 2.5 above the mean of its four predecessors, so `computeLineTrendflexSum` returns `[null,null,null,null,2.5,2.5,2.5,2.5,2.5]` (asserted with `toEqual`; a falling ramp returns a constant -2.5, a flat filter 0); an all-zero price series produces `filt/sum/ms/trendflex` all zero (with the warm-up nulls), asserted with `toEqual`. `runLineTrendflex` sorts the finite price points by x, runs the pipeline, and returns the filt/sum/ms/trendflex series, per-bar `samples` classified `up`/`down`/`flat` by the sign of the Trendflex, the final reading, and the up/down counts; `ok` is false for fewer than two points. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs `<ChartLineRoofing>` -- the Roofing filter is a band-pass in the FREQUENCY sense (a high-pass plus a smoother); the Trendflex measures trend STRENGTH, the average displacement of a single Super Smoother, RMS-normalized. vs `<ChartLinePmo>` -- the PMO is a double-smoothed rate of CHANGE; the Trendflex sums the smoother's displacement directly and normalizes by an adaptive mean square. vs the band primitives `<ChartLineStdDev>` / `<ChartLineEnvelope>` -- those draw a band around the price; the Trendflex is a zero-centred oscillator on its own sub-panel. All exported helpers carry a `LineTrendflex`/`Trendflex` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineTrendflexPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `period` (drives both the Super Smoother, at half the period, and the trend-sum lookback length; default 20); `pricePanelRatio` (the fraction of the height given to the price panel; default 0.55, clamped to [0.3, 0.8]); `gap` (the pixel gap between the stacked panels); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `priceColor` / `trendflexColor` / `upColor` / `downColor` / `zeroColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showTrendflex`/`showZeroLine`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `trendflex`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_TRENDFLEX_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/PRICE_COLOR/TRENDFLEX_COLOR/UP_COLOR/DOWN_COLOR/ZERO_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineTrendflexFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value); `normalizeLineTrendflexPeriod(period, fallback)` (coerce to an integer of at least 2 so half the period is at least 1; non-finite or sub-2 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineTrendflexSmootherCoefficients(period)` -- returns `{a1, b1, c1, c2, c3}`; `computeLineTrendflexSmoother(values, period)`; `computeLineTrendflexSum(filt, period)`; `computeLineTrendflex(values, period)` -- returns `{filt, sum, ms, trendflex}`; `runLineTrendflex(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, period, filt, sum, ms, trendflex, samples, trendflexFinal, trendflexMin, trendflexMax, upCount, downCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, filt, sum, ms, trendflex, trend}` (`trend` is `up`/`down`/`flat`); `computeLineTrendflexLayout({data, period, ...})` -- stacks the price panel above the Trendflex panel (the Trendflex panel y-domain symmetric around zero), projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `trendflexPath`, `trendflexMarkers`, the `zeroY` centerline; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLineTrendflexChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Trendflex + Super Smoother + trend strength + root mean square (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-trendflex-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the Trendflex line + every price dot + every Trendflex marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-trendflex / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -panel-label / -zero-line / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -trendflex-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-filt / -tooltip-sum / -tooltip-trendflex / -tooltip-trend / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-period); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-trendflex-final`, `data-up-count`, `data-down-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; grid / axis / tick / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"trendflex"`; markers expose `data-trendflex` and `data-trend`. The Trendflex panel carries a dashed zero centerline and trend-coloured markers (green up, red down, grey flat). Tooltip on a price dot or a marker shows x, value, the filter, the sum, the trendflex and the trend. Config badge in top-left calls out `TRENDFLEX <period>`. The legend has two toggle items (Price / Trendflex). 68 vitest cases (getLineTrendflexFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + value** + non-array -> [], normalizeLineTrendflexPeriod incl. floors a fractional + **sub-2 / NaN / negative falls back**, computeLineTrendflexSmootherCoefficients incl. **c2 from b1 + c3 from a1 squared** + **c1 so the coefficients sum to one** + **a1 inside the unit interval** + **sub-2 period falls back**, computeLineTrendflexSmoother incl. **seeds the first two bars from the input** + **follows the two-pole recursion** + **holds an all-zero series at zero** + non-array -> [], computeLineTrendflexSum incl. **the average bar-to-bar displacement for a rising ramp** + **negative for a falling filter** + **a flat filter held at zero** + **null until the lookback window is full** + non-array -> [], computeLineTrendflex incl. **the all-zero pipeline anchor** + **the smoother stage wired to the price** + **the sum stage wired to the smoothed series** + x-sorting + one sample per point + **the period-20 default**, computeLineTrendflexLayout incl. ok + **stacks the price panel above the trendflex panel** + both paths + **one dot per bar + one marker | 11.645 | UI: chart-line-reflex primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.663 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-reflex.tsx` ships `<ChartLineReflex>` -- a pure-SVG TWO-PANEL chart with an **Ehlers Reflex indicator** detecting cycle turning points from a Super Smoother (the price panel on top, the Reflex oscillator sub-panel below; the Reflex is RMS-normalized and zero-centred so it cannot share the price axis). **The computation:** the Reflex reads cycle turning points off a Super Smoother in three stages. STAGE ONE -- `computeLineReflexSmoother(values, period)` is the Ehlers Super Smoother of the price, a two-pole low-pass filter run at HALF the period; with `arg = 1.414*PI/(0.5*period)` the coefficients are `a1 = exp(-arg)`, `b1 = 2*a1*cos(arg)`, `c2 = b1`, `c3 = -(a1*a1)`, `c1 = 1-c2-c3` (exported as `computeLineReflexSmootherCoefficients`), and `filt[i] = c1*(price[i]+price[i-1])/2 + c2*filt[i-1] + c3*filt[i-2]`. STAGE TWO -- `computeLineReflexSum(filt, period)` is the Reflex sum: a trendline runs from `filt[i]` back to `filt[i-period]` with `slope = (filt[i-period]-filt[i])/period`, and `sum[i] = (1/period)*SUM[count=1..period](filt[i] + count*slope - filt[i-count])` -- each term is the trendline value `count` bars back minus the actual filtered value there. Because the trendline is SUBTRACTED, the Reflex has NO TREND RESPONSE -- a perfectly linear filter returns exactly zero; it reacts only to the cyclic swings away from the line; null until the lookback window is full. STAGE THREE -- `computeLineReflex(values, period)` normalizes the sum by the square root of an adaptively accumulated mean square, `ms[i] = 0.04*sum[i]^2 + 0.96*ms[i-1]` and `reflex[i] = ms[i]!=0 ? sum[i]/sqrt(ms[i]) : 0`. The result is a zero-centred oscillator with no trend lag -- it crosses zero at cycle turning points, spiking positive and negative as the price swings; an all-zero price series stays at zero through the whole pipeline. **Worked fixture:** the Super Smoother coefficients come from `exp`/`cos` so the pipeline values are irrational -- they are pinned by the recursions themselves (recomputed with the exported coefficients/recursion) plus exact anchors. The Reflex sum is exact-testable on its own: fed the linear ramp `[0,1,2,3,4,5,6,7,8]` with `period 4` the trendline equals the ramp so every deviation term is exactly zero and `computeLineReflexSum` returns `[null,null,null,null,0,0,0,0,0]` -- the defining no-trend-response property (vs the Trendflex, whose sum on the same ramp is a constant 2.5); fed a curved filter `[0,2,4,2,0]` (`period 4`) the deviation is the exact non-zero value -2; an all-zero price series produces `filt/sum/ms/reflex` all zero (with the warm-up nulls), asserted with `toEqual`. `runLineReflex` sorts the finite price points by x, runs the pipeline, and returns the filt/sum/ms/reflex series, per-bar `samples` classified `positive`/`negative`/`zero` by the sign of the Reflex, the final reading, and the positive/negative counts; `ok` is false for fewer than two points. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs `<ChartLineTrendflex>` -- the Trendflex sums the smoother's displacement DIRECTLY (full trend response, a linear ramp gives a constant non-zero sum); the Reflex subtracts a TRENDLINE first, so it carries no trend lag (a linear ramp gives zero) and crosses zero at cycle TURNING POINTS. vs `<ChartLineRoofing>` -- the Roofing filter is a band-pass built from a high-pass and a smoother; the Reflex is a single Super Smoother whose deviation from a trendline is measured and RMS-normalized. vs the band primitives `<ChartLineStdDev>` / `<ChartLineEnvelope>` -- those draw a band around the price; the Reflex is a zero-centred oscillator on its own sub-panel. All exported helpers carry a `LineReflex`/`Reflex` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineReflexPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `period` (drives both the Super Smoother, at half the period, and the deviation lookback length; default 20); `pricePanelRatio` (the fraction of the height given to the price panel; default 0.55, clamped to [0.3, 0.8]); `gap` (the pixel gap between the stacked panels); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `priceColor` / `reflexColor` / `positiveColor` / `negativeColor` / `zeroColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showReflex`/`showZeroLine`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `reflex`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_REFLEX_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/PRICE_COLOR/REFLEX_COLOR/POSITIVE_COLOR/NEGATIVE_COLOR/ZERO_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineReflexFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value); `normalizeLineReflexPeriod(period, fallback)` (coerce to an integer of at least 2 so half the period is at least 1; non-finite or sub-2 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineReflexSmootherCoefficients(period)` -- returns `{a1, b1, c1, c2, c3}`; `computeLineReflexSmoother(values, period)`; `computeLineReflexSum(filt, period)`; `computeLineReflex(values, period)` -- returns `{filt, sum, ms, reflex}`; `runLineReflex(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, period, filt, sum, ms, reflex, samples, reflexFinal, reflexMin, reflexMax, positiveCount, negativeCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, filt, sum, ms, reflex, sign}` (`sign` is `positive`/`negative`/`zero`); `computeLineReflexLayout({data, period, ...})` -- stacks the price panel above the Reflex panel (the Reflex panel y-domain symmetric around zero), projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `reflexPath`, `reflexMarkers`, the `zeroY` centerline; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLineReflexChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Reflex + Super Smoother + cycle turning points + trendline + root mean square (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-reflex-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the Reflex line + every price dot + every Reflex marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-reflex / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -panel-label / -zero-line / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -reflex-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-filt / -tooltip-sum / -tooltip-reflex / -tooltip-sign / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-period); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-reflex-final`, `data-positive-count`, `data-negative-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; grid / axis / tick / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"reflex"`; markers expose `data-reflex` and `data-sign`. The Reflex panel carries a dashed zero centerline and sign-coloured markers (green positive, red negative, grey zero). Tooltip on a price dot or a marker shows x, value, the filter, the sum, the reflex and the sign. Config badge in top-left calls out `REFLEX <period>`. The legend has two toggle items (Price / Reflex). 68 vitest cases (getLineReflexFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + value** + non-array -> [], normalizeLineReflexPeriod incl. floors a | 11.646 | UI: chart-line-hurst primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.664 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-hurst.tsx` ships `<ChartLineHurst>` -- a pure-SVG TWO-PANEL chart with a **rolling Hurst exponent** panel classifying the series as trending or mean reverting (the price panel on top, the Hurst panel below; the Hurst exponent is bounded to [0,1] so the panel uses a FIXED unit-interval y-domain, not a data-driven oscillator scale). **The computation:** the Hurst exponent measures the long-term memory of a series via rescaled range (R/S) analysis. `computeLineHurstRescaledRange(window)` analyzes a single window: the window is mean-adjusted, the cumulative deviation `Z` is tracked bar by bar, the range `R = max(Z) - min(Z)` is its spread, `S` is the population standard deviation of the window, and the rescaled range is `R/S` (a flat window has `S = 0` and reports a NaN R/S). `computeLineHurst(values, period)` is the rolling estimate: for each bar the trailing window of `period` values gives the single-window R/S estimate `H = log(R/S)/log(period)`, CLAMPED into the unit interval [0,1]. `H > 0.5` marks a persistent (trending) series, `H < 0.5` an anti-persistent (mean reverting) series, `H` near 0.5 a random walk. Bars before the window is full -- and flat windows, whose R/S is undefined -- are null. **Worked fixture:** `H` involves `log` and `sqrt` and is generally irrational, so it is pinned by `toBeCloseTo` plus exact anchors; the rescaled range is exact-testable on its own. The step window `[8,8,12,12]` has mean 10, cumulative deviation `[-2,-4,-2,0]`, `R = 4`, `S = 2`, `R/S = 2`. The alternating window `[12,8,12,8]` has cumulative deviation `[2,0,2,0]`, `R = 2`, `S = 2`, `R/S = 1` -- so `H = log(1)/log(4) = 0` EXACTLY (an alternating series is maximally mean reverting; `computeLineHurst` of an alternating series is exactly 0 on every defined bar, asserted with `toEqual`). The trending step `[8,8,8,8,12,12,12,12]` has `R = 8`, `S = 2`, `R/S = 4` -- so `H = log(4)/log(8) ~ 0.667`, trending. A flat series produces a null Hurst everywhere. `runLineHurst` sorts the finite price points by x, runs the pipeline, and returns the hurst/rs series, per-bar `samples` classified `trending`/`reverting`/`random` against the 0.5 random-walk level, the final reading, and the trending/reverting counts; `ok` is false for fewer than two points. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs the oscillator panels `<ChartLineRoofing>` / `<ChartLineTrendflex>` / `<ChartLineReflex>` / `<ChartLinePmo>` -- those draw a zero-centred oscillator with a data-driven y-domain; the Hurst panel is a FIXED unit-interval gauge with a 0.5 midline (the Hurst exponent is bounded to [0,1] by definition). vs `<ChartLineTrendflex>` -- the Trendflex measures trend strength from a Super Smoother's displacement; the Hurst exponent measures the long-term memory of the RAW series via rescaled range analysis -- a statistical, not a filter-based, classification. vs the band primitives `<ChartLineStdDev>` / `<ChartLineEnvelope>` -- those draw a band around the price; the Hurst panel is a classification gauge. All exported helpers carry a `LineHurst`/`Hurst` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineHurstPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `period` (the rolling window length; default 20); `pricePanelRatio` (the fraction of the height given to the price panel; default 0.55, clamped to [0.3, 0.8]); `gap` (the pixel gap between the stacked panels); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `priceColor` / `hurstColor` / `trendingColor` / `revertingColor` / `randomColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showHurst`/`showRefLine`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `hurst`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_HURST_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/PRICE_COLOR/HURST_COLOR/TRENDING_COLOR/REVERTING_COLOR/RANDOM_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineHurstFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value); `normalizeLineHurstPeriod(period, fallback)` (coerce to an integer of at least 2 so `log(period)` stays above zero; non-finite or sub-2 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineHurstRescaledRange(window)` -- returns `{range, stddev, rs}`; `computeLineHurst(values, period)` -- returns `{hurst, rs}`; `runLineHurst(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, period, hurst, rs, samples, hurstFinal, hurstMin, hurstMax, trendingCount, revertingCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, hurst, rs, classification}` (`classification` is `trending`/`reverting`/`random`); `computeLineHurstLayout({data, period, ...})` -- stacks the price panel above the Hurst panel (the Hurst panel y-domain FIXED to [0,1]), projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `hurstPath`, `hurstMarkers`, the `refY` 0.5 reference line; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLineHurstChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Hurst exponent + rescaled range + trending + mean reverting + random walk (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-hurst-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the Hurst line + every price dot + every Hurst marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-hurst / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -panel-label / -ref-line / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -hurst-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-rs / -tooltip-hurst / -tooltip-class / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-period); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-hurst-final`, `data-trending-count`, `data-reverting-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; grid / axis / tick / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"hurst"`; markers expose `data-hurst` and `data-class`. The Hurst panel carries a dashed 0.5 reference centerline (the random-walk level) and class-coloured markers (green trending, red reverting, grey random). Tooltip on a price dot or a marker shows x, value, the rescaled range, the hurst and the class. Config badge in top-left calls out `HURST <period>`. The legend has two toggle items (Price / Hurst). 65 vitest cases (getLineHurstFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + value** + non-array -> [], normalizeLineHurstPeriod incl. floors a fractional + **sub-2 / NaN / negative falls back**, computeLineHurstRescaledRange incl. **the step window range/stddev/rescaled range** + **the unit rescaled range for an alternating window** + **the larger rescaled range for a trending step** + **the NaN rescaled range for a flat window** + **the NaN for a too-short window** + non-array input, computeLineHurst incl. **the zero-Hurst alternating anchor** + **the per-bar rescaled range** + **the trending step above 0.5** + **the flat-series null** + **the null warm-up** + **the unit-interval clamp** + non-array -> empty series, runLineHurst incl. ok + case** + **the trending-step case** + **the final reading** + **the min/max readings** + not-ok short series + empty/null -> ok=false + x-sorting + one sample per point + **the period-20 default**, | 11.647 | UI: chart-line-entropy primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.665 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-entropy.tsx` ships `<ChartLineEntropy>` -- a pure-SVG TWO-PANEL chart with a **rolling Shannon entropy** panel measuring local signal disorder (the price panel on top, the entropy panel below; the normalized entropy is bounded to [0,1] so the panel uses a FIXED unit-interval y-domain). **The computation:** Shannon entropy measures the disorder of a probability distribution. `computeLineEntropyHistogram(window, bins)` bins a window of values into an equal-width histogram -- the window range `[min,max]` is split into `bins` buckets, each value counted into its bucket (the max lands in the last bucket; a flat window collapses into the first), and the `probabilities` are the counts over the window length. `computeLineEntropyValue(probabilities)` is the Shannon entropy of a distribution in bits, `H = -SUM[p>0] p*log2(p)` (zero-probability bins skipped per the `0*log0=0` convention; accumulated as a sum of non-negative terms so a certain outcome returns positive zero). `computeLineEntropy(values, period, bins)` is the rolling estimate: for each bar the trailing window of `period` values is binned into `bins` buckets and the Shannon entropy of that distribution is taken; `entropy` is the raw bits, `normalized` divides it by `log2(bins)` (the entropy of a uniform distribution) so it lands in [0,1]. A normalized entropy near 1 marks a disordered (noisy) window, near 0 an ordered (concentrated) window. Bars before the window is full are null. **Worked fixture:** Shannon entropy is EXACT when every probability is a power of 1/2 (`log2` of an exact power of two is an exact integer). A two-bin even split `[0.5,0.5]` has entropy 1 bit; four equal outcomes `[0.25,0.25,0.25,0.25]` have entropy 2 bits; a certain outcome `[1,0,0]` has entropy 0. A flat series `[5,5,5,5,5,5]` collapses every window into one bin -> entropy 0, normalized 0 everywhere (perfectly ordered). A strict integer ramp `[1,2,3,4,5,6]` with `period 4`/`bins 4` puts one value in each bin every window -> entropy 2 bits, normalized `2/log2(4) = 1` everywhere (maximally disordered). A two-level series `[10,10,20,20,10,10,20,20]` fills two bins evenly -> entropy 1 bit, normalized `1/log2(4) = 0.5` everywhere -- all asserted with `toEqual`. `runLineEntropy` sorts the finite price points by x, runs the pipeline, and returns the entropy/normalized series, per-bar `samples` classified `disordered`/`ordered`/`neutral` against the 0.5 level, the final reading, and the disordered/ordered counts; `ok` is false for fewer than two points. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs `<ChartLineHurst>` -- both are fixed [0,1] gauge panels with a 0.5 reference, but the Hurst exponent measures the long-term MEMORY of the series (rescaled range analysis) while the Shannon entropy measures the DISORDER of the local amplitude distribution (histogram). vs the oscillator panels `<ChartLineRoofing>` / `<ChartLineTrendflex>` / `<ChartLineReflex>` / `<ChartLinePmo>` -- those draw a zero-centred oscillator with a data-driven y-domain; the entropy panel is a fixed unit-interval gauge. vs the band primitives `<ChartLineStdDev>` / `<ChartLineEnvelope>` -- those draw a band around the price; the entropy panel is a disorder gauge. All exported helpers carry a `LineEntropy`/`Entropy` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineEntropyPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `period` (the rolling window length; default 20); `bins` (the histogram bucket count; default 8); `pricePanelRatio` (the fraction of the height given to the price panel; default 0.55, clamped to [0.3, 0.8]); `gap` (the pixel gap between the stacked panels); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `priceColor` / `entropyColor` / `disorderedColor` / `orderedColor` / `neutralColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showEntropy`/`showRefLine`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `entropy`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_ENTROPY_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/BINS/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/PRICE_COLOR/ENTROPY_COLOR/DISORDERED_COLOR/ORDERED_COLOR/NEUTRAL_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineEntropyFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value); `normalizeLineEntropyPeriod(period, fallback)` (coerce to an integer of at least 2; non-finite or sub-2 falls back, fractional floors); `normalizeLineEntropyBins(bins, fallback)` (coerce to an integer of at least 2 so `log2(bins)` stays above zero); `computeLineEntropyHistogram(window, bins)` -- returns `{counts, probabilities}`; `computeLineEntropyValue(probabilities)` -- the Shannon entropy in bits; `computeLineEntropy(values, period, bins)` -- returns `{entropy, normalized}`; `runLineEntropy(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, period, bins, entropy, normalized, samples, normalizedFinal, normalizedMin, normalizedMax, disorderedCount, orderedCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, entropy, normalized, classification}` (`classification` is `disordered`/`ordered`/`neutral`); `computeLineEntropyLayout({data, period, bins, ...})` -- stacks the price panel above the entropy panel (the entropy panel y-domain FIXED to [0,1]), projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `entropyPath`, `entropyMarkers`, the `refY` 0.5 reference line; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLineEntropyChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Shannon entropy + histogram + disorder + normalized (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-entropy-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the entropy line + every price dot + every entropy marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-entropy / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -panel-label / -ref-line / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -entropy-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-entropy / -tooltip-normalized / -tooltip-class / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-bins`, `data-normalized-final`, `data-disordered-count`, `data-ordered-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; grid / axis / tick / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"entropy"`; markers expose `data-normalized` and `data-class`. The entropy panel carries a dashed 0.5 reference centerline and class-coloured markers (red disordered, green ordered, grey neutral). Tooltip on a price dot or a marker shows x, value, the raw entropy, the normalized entropy and the class. Config badge in top-left calls out `ENTROPY <period>/<bins>`. The legend has two toggle items (Price / Entropy). 72 vitest cases (getLineEntropyFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + value** + non-array -> [], normalizeLineEntropyPeriod incl. floors a fractional + **sub-2 / NaN / negative falls back**, normalizeLineEntropyBins incl. floors a fractional + **sub-2 / NaN / negative falls back**, computeLineEntropyHistogram incl. **the bucket counts** + **a ramp spread across every bin** + **a flat window collapsed into one bin** + **the counts normalized into probabilities** + non-array -> empty arrays, computeLineEntropyValue incl. **one bit for an even two-way split** + **two bits for four equal outcomes** + **zero for a certain outcome** + **zero-probability bins skipped** + **zero for an empty/non-array distribution**, computeLineEntropy incl. **zero entropy for a flat series** + **full normalized entropy for a ramp** + **the raw entropy in bits** + **a mid entropy for a two-level series** + **the null warm-up** + **the unit-interval bound** + non-array -> empty series, runLineEntropy incl. ok + the period + bins + **the entropy + normalized series** + **the null warm-up** + **the 0.5-level classification** + **self-consistent disordered/ordered counts** + **the ramp disordered case** + **the two-level neutral case** + **the flat ordered case** + **the final reading** + **the min/max readings** + not-ok short series + empty/null -> ok=false + x-sorting + one sample per point + **the period-20 / bins-8 defaults**, computeLineEntropyLayout incl. ok + **stacks the price panel above the entropy panel** + both paths + **one dot per bar + one marker per defined entropy** + **the fixed [0,1] y-domain** + **the 0.5 reference line inside the entropy panel** + the run statistics + collapsed dims -> ok=false + too little data -> ok=false, describeLineEntropyChart incl. **the indicator vocabulary** + the disordered/ordered counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / root data-* / svg + price + entropy lines / **both panel labels** / **one marker per defined entropy** / **the class attribute on each marker** / **the reference line** / two-item legend / config badge period + bins / hide the price path / **hide the entropy line + markers when showEntropy is false** / hide the entropy line via the hidden set / **hide the reference line when showRefLine is false** / series toggle / price dots / empty state / omit config badge / ref / displayName / animate flag) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. | 11.648 | UI: chart-line-vwma primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.666 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-vwma.tsx` ships `<ChartLineVwma>` -- a pure-SVG SINGLE-PANEL line chart with a **Volume Weighted Moving Average overlay** weighting each price by its bar volume (the price line + the VWMA line on one shared panel). **First chart primitive to consume a per-bar `volume` field** -- `ChartLineVwmaPoint` is `{x, value, volume}` (all prior chart-line primitives took `{x, value}`). **The computation:** `computeLineVwma(values, volumes, period)` -- for each bar the trailing window of `period` bars is averaged with each price weighted by its bar's traded volume: `VWMA[i] = sum(price[j]*volume[j]) / sum(volume[j])` over the window. A high-volume bar pulls the average toward its price more strongly than a low-volume bar, so the VWMA tracks where the trading actually happened; a window whose total volume is zero has an undefined 0/0 average and is null, as are bars before the window is full. **Worked fixture** (`period 2`, hand-tuned so every window divides cleanly): prices `[10,20,40,10,16,4,34,7]`, volumes `[1,3,1,2,2,2,1,2]` -> the first window `[10,20]` with volumes `[1,3]` gives `(10*1 + 20*3)/(1+3) = 70/4 = 17.5` -- pulled toward 20 by its higher volume, NOT the simple average 15; the full series `vwma = [null,17.5,25,20,13,10,14,16]`, asserted with `toEqual`. When all volumes in the window are equal the VWMA reduces EXACTLY to the simple moving average (`computeLineVwma([10,20,30,40],[5,5,5,5],2)` returns `[null,15,25,35]`, the SMA, asserted with `toEqual`). `runLineVwma` sorts the finite price points by x, computes the VWMA, and returns the vwma series, per-bar `samples` classified `above`/`below`/`on` the VWMA, the final reading, and the above/below counts; `ok` is false for fewer than two points. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs a simple moving average -- the SMA weights every bar in the window EQUALLY; the VWMA weights each bar by its traded VOLUME, so it tracks where the volume concentrated (and reduces exactly to the SMA when volumes are equal). vs the EMA / WMA / smoothing overlays -- those weight bars by RECENCY (exponential or linear); the VWMA weights by volume, an independent per-bar input. vs the band primitives `<ChartLineEnvelope>` / `<ChartLineStdDev>` -- those draw a band; the VWMA is a single overlay line. It is the first primitive in the chart-line family to require a `volume` field on each data point. All exported helpers carry a `LineVwma`/`Vwma` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineVwmaPoint[]` (`{x, value, volume}`); `period` (the moving-average window length; default 20); `width`/`height`/`padding`/`tickCount`; styling `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`priceColor`/`vwmaColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showVwma`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `vwma`); standard format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_VWMA_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/PRICE_COLOR/VWMA_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineVwmaPosition` type (`above`|`below`|`on`); `getLineVwmaFinitePoints` (keeps only points with finite x + value + volume AND volume >= 0; a negative volume is rejected, a zero volume kept); `normalizeLineVwmaPeriod(period, fallback)` (coerce to a positive integer; non-finite or sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineVwma(values, volumes, period)` -- the volume-weighted moving average series, null through the warm-up and for zero-volume windows (non-array -> []); `runLineVwma(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, period, vwma, samples, vwmaFinal, vwmaMin, vwmaMax, aboveCount, belowCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, volume, vwma, position}` (`position` is `above`/`below`/`on`); `computeLineVwmaLayout({data, period, ...})` -- projects the price and VWMA paths onto one shared panel with a y-domain spanning both, returns the panel, ticks, `pricePath`, `vwmaPath`, `priceDots`, `vwmaMarkers` (only where the VWMA is defined); degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLineVwmaChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Volume Weighted Moving Average + volume + weighted + above/below counts (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-vwma-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the VWMA line + every price dot + every VWMA marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-vwma / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick / -tick-label / -price-path / -vwma-line / -dots / -dot / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-volume / -tooltip-vwma / -tooltip-position / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-vwma-final`, `data-above-count`, `data-below-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; price dots expose `data-volume`; markers expose `data-vwma`. Tooltip on a price dot or a VWMA marker shows x, value, the bar volume, the VWMA and the price position. Config badge in top-left calls out `VWMA <period>`. The legend has two toggle items (Price / VWMA). 54 vitest cases (getLineVwmaFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + value + non-negative volume** (negative volume rejected, zero volume kept) + non-array -> [], normalizeLineVwmaPeriod incl. floors a fractional + **sub-1 / NaN / negative falls back**, computeLineVwma incl. **the volume-weighted average over each window** + **the reduction to a simple average when volumes are equal** + **null for a zero-total-volume window** + **null before the window is full** + **all-null for a series shorter than the period** + non-array -> [], runLineVwma incl. ok + the period + **the vwma series** + **the exact volume-weighted moving average** + **the null warm-up** + **the bar volume on each sample** + **position classification vs the vwma** + **self-consistent above/below counts** + **the final reading** + **the min/max readings** + not-ok short series + empty/null -> ok=false + x-sorting + one sample per point + **the period-20 default**, computeLineVwmaLayout incl. ok + **a single panel filling the inner area** + the price/vwma paths + **one dot per bar + one marker per defined vwma** + **a y-domain covering the price + the vwma** + marker containment + the run statistics + collapsed dims -> ok=false + too little data -> ok=false, describeLineVwmaChart incl. **the indicator vocabulary** + the above/below counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / root data-* / svg + price + vwma lines / **one marker per defined vwma** / config badge period / two-item legend / hide the price path / **hide the vwma line + markers when showVwma is false** / hide the vwma line via the hidden set / series toggle / price dots / empty state / omit config badge / ref / displayName / animate flag) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for the new files (conditional spreads honour `exactOptionalPropertyTypes`). Exported via components/ui barrel. Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/c4-mgr-auto. | 11.649 | UI: chart-line-qstick primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.667 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-qstick.tsx` ships `<ChartLineQstick>` -- a pure-SVG TWO-PANEL chart with a **Qstick indicator** panel showing a moving average of the close minus open spread (the price panel on top, the Qstick oscillator sub-panel below; the Qstick is centred on zero so it cannot share the price axis). **The computation:** the Qstick indicator (Tushar Chande) measures buying and selling pressure from the position of each bar's close relative to its open. `ChartLineQstickPoint` is `{x, value, open}` -- `value` is the bar CLOSE (the plotted price), `open` the bar OPEN. `computeLineQstickSpread(values, opens)` is the per-bar close-minus-open spread, `value - open` (a positive spread is a bar that closed above its open, a bullish bar; a negative spread one that closed below it). `computeLineQstick(values, opens, period)` is the Qstick: `Qstick[i] = SMA(close[j] - open[j], period)`, the `period`-bar simple moving average of the spread. A positive Qstick marks sustained buying pressure (closes consistently above opens), a negative Qstick selling pressure, and a reading near zero a balanced market; bars before the window is full are null. **Worked fixture:** the Qstick is an SMA of a subtraction -- NO transcendentals, so the whole pipeline is exact (asserted with `toEqual`). The fixture (`period 4`) is hand-tuned so every spread and every moving average divides cleanly: spread `[6,-2,-8,-4,10,-6,2,-12,8,4]`, qstick `[null,null,null,-2,-1,-2,0.5,-1.5,-2,0.5]` (e.g. `qstick[3] = (6-2-8-4)/4 = -2`); a doji series (close==open every bar) gives spread 0 and qstick 0; a constant spread holds the qstick at that value. `runLineQstick` sorts the finite price points by x, runs the pipeline, and returns the spread/qstick series, per-bar `samples` classified `bullish`/`bearish`/`neutral` by the sign of the Qstick, the final reading, and the bullish/bearish counts; `ok` is false for fewer than two points. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs the moving-average overlays `<ChartLineVwma>` / smoothing overlays -- those average the PRICE LEVEL; the Qstick averages the intrabar close-minus-open SPREAD, a sentiment measure that does not track the price level at all, so it gets its own zero-centred panel. vs the oscillator panels `<ChartLineRoofing>` / `<ChartLineTrendflex>` / `<ChartLineReflex>` / `<ChartLinePmo>` -- those derive their oscillator from a filter or rate of change of the CLOSE; the Qstick derives it from the relationship between two distinct per-bar inputs, the OPEN and the CLOSE. It is a primitive that requires an `open` field on each data point, alongside the plotted `value` (the close). All exported helpers carry a `LineQstick`/`Qstick` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineQstickPoint[]` (`{x, value, open}`); `period` (the moving-average window length; default 10); `pricePanelRatio` (the fraction of the height given to the price panel; default 0.55, clamped to [0.3, 0.8]); `gap` (the pixel gap between the stacked panels); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `priceColor` / `qstickColor` / `bullishColor` / `bearishColor` / `neutralColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showQstick`/`showZeroLine`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `qstick`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_QSTICK_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/PRICE_COLOR/QSTICK_COLOR/BULLISH_COLOR/BEARISH_COLOR/NEUTRAL_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineQstickSentiment` type (`bullish`|`bearish`|`neutral`); `getLineQstickFinitePoints` (keeps only points with finite x + value + open); `normalizeLineQstickPeriod(period, fallback)` (coerce to a positive integer; non-finite or sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineQstickSpread(values, opens)` -- the per-bar close-minus-open spread (non-array -> []); `computeLineQstick(values, opens, period)` -- the Qstick series, null through the warm-up (non-array -> []); `runLineQstick(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, period, spread, qstick, samples, qstickFinal, qstickMin, qstickMax, bullishCount, bearishCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, open, spread, qstick, sentiment}` (`sentiment` is `bullish`/`bearish`/`neutral`); `computeLineQstickLayout({data, period, ...})` -- stacks the price panel above the Qstick panel (the Qstick panel y-domain symmetric around zero), projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `qstickPath`, `qstickMarkers`, the `zeroY` centerline; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLineQstickChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Qstick + close minus the open + moving average + buying pressure (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-qstick-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the Qstick line + every price dot + every Qstick marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-qstick / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -panel-label / -zero-line / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -qstick-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-open / -tooltip-spread / -tooltip-qstick / -tooltip-sentiment / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-period); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-qstick-final`, `data-bullish-count`, `data-bearish-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; grid / axis / tick / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"qstick"`; price dots expose `data-open`; markers expose `data-qstick` and `data-sentiment`. The Qstick panel carries a dashed zero centerline and sentiment-coloured markers (green bullish, red bearish, grey neutral). Tooltip on a price dot or a marker shows x, the close, the open, the spread, the qstick and the sentiment. Config badge in top-left calls out `QSTICK <period>`. The legend has two toggle items (Price / Qstick). 62 vitest cases (getLineQstickFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + value + open** + non-array -> [], normalizeLineQstickPeriod incl. floors a fractional + **sub-1 / NaN / negative falls back**, computeLineQstickSpread incl. **the close-minus-open spread** + **the zero doji spread** + **one spread per bar** + non-array -> [], computeLineQstick incl. **the moving average of the spread** + **the null warm-up** + **a constant spread held at that value** + **the doji-series zero** + **all-null for a series shorter than the period** + non-array -> [], runLineQstick incl. ok + the period + **the qstick series** + **the exact qstick series** + **the per-bar spread on each sample** + **the null warm-up** + **sign-based sentiment classification** + **self-consistent bullish/bearish counts** + **the final reading** + **the min/max readings** + not-ok short series + empty/null -> ok=false + x-sorting + one sample per point + **the period-10 default**, computeLineQstickLayout incl. ok + **stacks the price panel above the qstick panel** + both paths + **one dot per bar + one marker per defined qstick** + **the zero line inside the qstick panel** + **the zero-centred y-domain** + the run statistics + collapsed dims -> ok=false + too little data -> ok=false, describeLineQstickChart incl. **the indicator vocabulary** + the bullish/bearish counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / root data-* / svg + price + qstick lines / **both panel labels** / **one marker per defined qstick** / **the sentiment attribute on each marker** / **the zero line** / two-item legend / config badge period / hide the price path / **hide the qstick line + markers when showQstick is false** / hide the qstick line via the hidden set / **hide the zero line when showZeroLine is false** / series toggle / price dots / empty state / omit config badge / ref / displayName / animate flag) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. | 11.650 | UI: chart-line-cog primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.668 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-cog.tsx` ships `<ChartLineCog>` -- a pure-SVG TWO-PANEL chart with an **Ehlers Center of Gravity oscillator** panel from a weighted price sum (the price panel on top, the CoG oscillator sub-panel below; the CoG is centred on zero so it cannot share the price axis). **The computation:** the Ehlers Center of Gravity (CoG) oscillator finds the centroid -- the center of gravity -- of the prices in a rolling window. `computeLineCogWeightedSum(window)` reduces a window (ordered oldest-first) to its weighted price sum: each bar is weighted by its AGE, the oldest bar carrying the heaviest weight `m` and the most recent bar the lightest weight `1` (m = window length): `numerator = SUM[j] (m-j)*window[j]`, `denominator = SUM[j] window[j]`. `computeLineCog(values, period)` is the Center of Gravity: `CoG[i] = -numerator/denominator + (period+1)/2` -- the centroid `numerator/denominator` negated and offset by `(period+1)/2`. The offset is EXACTLY the centroid of a flat window, so a flat series reads zero. A positive CoG marks recent prices outweighing older ones (a rising window), a negative CoG the reverse; a window whose prices sum to zero has an undefined centroid and is null, as are bars before the window is full. **Worked fixture:** the CoG is a ratio of two weighted sums plus a constant offset -- NO transcendentals, so the whole pipeline is exact (asserted with `toEqual`). The fixture is a period-4 periodic series `[4,12,20,28]`: every window sums to 64 (a power of two), so every CoG divides cleanly; the weighted numerators cycle 120/168/184/168, giving `CoG = -120/64+2.5 = 0.625`, `-168/64+2.5 = -0.125`, `-184/64+2.5 = -0.375` (cog series `[null,null,null,0.625,-0.125,-0.375,-0.125,0.625,-0.125,-0.375]`); a flat series reads exactly zero. `runLineCog` sorts the finite price points by x, runs the pipeline, and returns the cog series, per-bar `samples` classified `positive`/`negative`/`zero` by the sign of the CoG, the final reading, and the positive/negative counts; `ok` is false for fewer than two points. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs the moving-average overlays `<ChartLineVwma>` / smoothing overlays -- those average the PRICE LEVEL; the CoG measures the CENTROID of the price window -- where its weight sits in TIME -- so it is a zero-centred oscillator on its own panel, not a price overlay. vs the oscillator panels `<ChartLineRoofing>` / `<ChartLineTrendflex>` / `<ChartLineReflex>` -- those derive their oscillator from a FILTER of the close; the CoG derives it from the time-position of the price mass: a weighted price sum normalized by the plain price sum. vs `<ChartLinePmo>` -- the PMO is a double-smoothed rate of change; the CoG is a single weighted-sum ratio with no smoothing. All exported helpers carry a `LineCog`/`Cog` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineCogPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `period` (the rolling window length; default 10); `pricePanelRatio` (the fraction of the height given to the price panel; default 0.55, clamped to [0.3, 0.8]); `gap` (the pixel gap between the stacked panels); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `priceColor` / `cogColor` / `positiveColor` / `negativeColor` / `zeroColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showCog`/`showZeroLine`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `cog`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_COG_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/PRICE_COLOR/COG_COLOR/POSITIVE_COLOR/NEGATIVE_COLOR/ZERO_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineCogSign` type (`positive`|`negative`|`zero`); `getLineCogFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value); `normalizeLineCogPeriod(period, fallback)` (coerce to an integer of at least 2; non-finite or sub-2 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineCogWeightedSum(window)` -- returns `{numerator, denominator}` (empty/non-array -> zeros); `computeLineCog(values, period)` -- the CoG series, null through the warm-up and for zero-sum windows (non-array -> []); `runLineCog(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, period, cog, samples, cogFinal, cogMin, cogMax, positiveCount, negativeCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, cog, sign}` (`sign` is `positive`/`negative`/`zero`); `computeLineCogLayout({data, period, ...})` -- stacks the price panel above the CoG panel (the CoG panel y-domain symmetric around zero), projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `cogPath`, `cogMarkers`, the `zeroY` centerline; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLineCogChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Center of Gravity + oscillator + weighted + centroid (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-cog-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the CoG line + every price dot + every CoG marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-cog / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -panel-label / -zero-line / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -cog-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-cog / -tooltip-sign / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-period); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-cog-final`, `data-positive-count`, `data-negative-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; grid / axis / tick / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"cog"`; markers expose `data-cog` and `data-sign`. The CoG panel carries a dashed zero centerline and sign-coloured markers (green positive, red negative, grey zero). Tooltip on a price dot or a marker shows x, value, the cog and the sign. Config badge in top-left calls out `COG <period>`. The legend has two toggle items (Price / CoG). 62 vitest cases (getLineCogFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + value** + non-array -> [], normalizeLineCogPeriod incl. floors a fractional + **sub-2 / NaN / negative falls back**, computeLineCogWeightedSum incl. **the weighted numerator + the denominator** + **the oldest bar weighted the heaviest** + **a single-element window** + **zeros for an empty window** + zeros for non-array, computeLineCog incl. **the center of gravity oscillator** + **the null warm-up** + **the flat-series zero** + **null for a zero-sum window** + **all-null for a series shorter than the period** + non-array -> [], runLineCog incl. ok + the period + **the cog series** + **the exact cog series** + **the null warm-up** + **sign classification** + **self-consistent positive/negative counts** + **the final reading** + **the min/max readings** + not-ok short series + empty/null -> ok=false + x-sorting + one sample per point + **the period-10 default**, computeLineCogLayout incl. ok + **stacks the price panel above the cog panel** + both paths + **one dot per bar + one marker per defined cog** + **the zero line inside the cog panel** + **the zero-centred y-domain** + the run statistics + collapsed dims -> ok=false + too little data -> ok=false, describeLineCogChart incl. **the indicator vocabulary** + the positive/negative counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / root data-* / svg + price + cog lines / **both panel labels** / **one marker per defined cog** / **the sign attribute on each marker** / **the zero line** / two-item legend / config badge period / hide the price path / **hide the cog line + markers when showCog is false** / hide the cog line via the hidden set / **hide the zero line when showZeroLine is false** / series toggle / price dots / empty state / omit config badge / ref / displayName / animate flag) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. | 11.651 | UI: chart-line-pgo primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.669 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-pgo.tsx` ships `<ChartLinePgo>` -- a pure-SVG TWO-PANEL chart with a **Pretty Good Oscillator** panel normalizing price distance from a moving average by the average true range (the price panel on top, the PGO oscillator sub-panel below; the PGO is centred on zero so it cannot share the price axis). **The computation:** the Pretty Good Oscillator (Mark Johnson) reads how far the close has moved from its moving average, in units of Average True Range. `ChartLinePgoPoint` is `{x, value, high, low}` -- `value` is the bar CLOSE. `computeLinePgoTrueRange(highs, lows, closes)` is the per-bar true range: the first bar has no prior close so its TR is `high-low`; every later bar is the widest of the bar range and the two gaps from the prior close, `TR[i] = max(high[i]-low[i], abs(high[i]-close[i-1]), abs(low[i]-close[i-1]))`. `computeLinePgoAtr(highs, lows, closes, period)` is the Average True Range, the `period`-bar simple moving average of the true range. `computeLinePgo(highs, lows, closes, period)` is the Pretty Good Oscillator: `PGO[i] = (close[i] - SMA(close, period)) / ATR(period)` -- the displacement of the close from its moving average, divided by the ATR, so it reads the price move in ATR units. A positive PGO marks the close above its moving average, a negative PGO below; a window whose ATR is zero (a perfectly flat market) has an undefined PGO and is null, as are bars before the window is full. **Worked fixture:** the PGO is a ratio of plain sums -- NO transcendentals -- so the whole pipeline is exact (asserted with `toEqual`). The fixture (`period 2`) is hand-tuned so every bar has a true range of EXACTLY 4, hence every ATR is 4 and every PGO divides cleanly by 4: closes `[10,12,16,14,16,20,18,22]` -> pgo `[null,0.25,0.5,-0.25,0.25,0.5,-0.25,0.5]`; a flat close on a non-zero range reads zero (the close sits on its own moving average); a perfectly flat market reads null (the ATR is zero). `runLinePgo` sorts the finite price points by x, runs the pipeline, and returns the pgo series, per-bar `samples` classified `positive`/`negative`/`zero` by the sign of the PGO, the final reading, and the positive/negative counts; `ok` is false for fewer than two points. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs a raw distance-from-MA oscillator -- a plain `close - SMA` is in PRICE units and is not comparable across instruments; the PGO divides by the ATR, so the reading is SCALE-FREE: a PGO of 1 means one ATR above the mean regardless of the instrument's volatility. vs `<ChartLineCog>` -- the CoG measures the centroid of the price window; the PGO measures the displacement of the close from a plain moving average, normalized by volatility. It requires `high` and `low` fields on each data point (alongside the plotted `value`, the close) to build the true range. All exported helpers carry a `LinePgo`/`Pgo` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLinePgoPoint[]` (`{x, value, high, low}`); `period` (the moving-average and ATR window length; default 14); `pricePanelRatio` (the fraction of the height given to the price panel; default 0.55, clamped to [0.3, 0.8]); `gap` (the pixel gap between the stacked panels); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `priceColor` / `pgoColor` / `positiveColor` / `negativeColor` / `zeroColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showPgo`/`showZeroLine`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `pgo`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_PGO_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/PRICE_COLOR/PGO_COLOR/POSITIVE_COLOR/NEGATIVE_COLOR/ZERO_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLinePgoSign` type (`positive`|`negative`|`zero`); `getLinePgoFinitePoints` (keeps only points with finite x + value + high + low); `normalizeLinePgoPeriod(period, fallback)` (coerce to an integer of at least 2; non-finite or sub-2 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLinePgoTrueRange(highs, lows, closes)` -- the per-bar true range (non-array -> []); `computeLinePgoAtr(highs, lows, closes, period)` -- the Average True Range, null through the warm-up (non-array -> []); `computeLinePgo(highs, lows, closes, period)` -- the PGO series, null through the warm-up and for zero-ATR markets (non-array -> []); `runLinePgo(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, period, pgo, samples, pgoFinal, pgoMin, pgoMax, positiveCount, negativeCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, high, low, pgo, sign}` (`sign` is `positive`/`negative`/`zero`); `computeLinePgoLayout({data, period, ...})` -- stacks the price panel above the PGO panel (the PGO panel y-domain symmetric around zero), projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `pgoPath`, `pgoMarkers`, the `zeroY` centerline; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLinePgoChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Pretty Good Oscillator + moving average + average true range (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-pgo-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the PGO line + every price dot + every PGO marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-pgo / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -panel-label / -zero-line / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -pgo-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-high / -tooltip-low / -tooltip-pgo / -tooltip-sign / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-period); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-pgo-final`, `data-positive-count`, `data-negative-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; grid / axis / tick / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"pgo"`; markers expose `data-pgo` and `data-sign`. The PGO panel carries a dashed zero centerline and sign-coloured markers (green positive, red negative, grey zero). Tooltip on a price dot or a marker shows x, the close, the high, the low, the pgo and the sign. Config badge in top-left calls out `PGO <period>`. | 11.652 | UI: chart-line-tii primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.670 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-tii.tsx` ships `<ChartLineTii>` -- a pure-SVG TWO-PANEL chart with a **Trend Intensity Index** panel measuring the share of deviations above a moving average (the price panel on top, the TII panel below; the TII is bounded to [0,100] so the panel uses a FIXED 0-to-100 y-domain). **The computation:** the Trend Intensity Index (M.H. Pee) measures trend strength from how the close deviates from a moving average. `computeLineTiiDeviations(closes, period)` is the per-bar deviation of the close from its `period`-bar moving average, `deviation[i] = close[i] - SMA(close, period)[i]`, null through the moving-average warm-up. `computeLineTii(closes, period)` is the Trend Intensity Index: over the trailing `period` window the deviations are split -- the positive deviations summed into `SDPos`, the negative deviations summed in magnitude into `SDNeg` -- and `TII[i] = 100*SDPos/(SDPos+SDNeg)`, the percentage SHARE of the positive deviation sum. The TII runs 0 to 100: a reading above 50 marks an uptrend (positive deviations dominate), below 50 a downtrend. A window with no deviation at all (a flat market) is null, as are bars before the window is full -- the warm-up is `2*period-2` bars, since the deviation itself warms up before the sum window can fill. **Worked fixture:** the TII is a ratio of summed deviations -- NO transcendentals -- so the whole pipeline is exact (asserted with `toEqual`). The fixture (`period 2`) is hand-tuned so the deviations are `[null,2,6,-2,-6,4,-1,1,3,-5]` and every TII window total divides cleanly: tii `[null,null,100,75,0,40,80,50,100,37.5]` (e.g. `TII = 100*6/(6+2) = 75`); a series whose close runs above its moving average on every bar reads 100, one below on every bar reads 0, a flat market reads null. `runLineTii` sorts the finite price points by x, runs the pipeline, and returns the deviations/tii series, per-bar `samples` classified `up`/`down`/`neutral` against the 50 level, the final reading, and the up/down counts; `ok` is false for fewer than two points. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs the zero-centred deviation oscillators `<ChartLineCog>` / `<ChartLinePgo>` -- those report the SIGNED deviation from a reference; the TII reports the SHARE of the deviation magnitude that is positive -- a 0-to-100 gauge, not a signed oscillator. vs `<ChartLineHurst>` / `<ChartLineEntropy>` -- those are fixed-domain gauges too, but the Hurst exponent measures long-term memory and the entropy measures disorder; the TII measures directional bias: how much of the recent deviation sits above the moving average. vs `<ChartLineQstick>` -- the Qstick averages the close-minus-open spread; the TII averages the share of close-minus-MA deviations that are positive. All exported helpers carry a `LineTii`/`Tii` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineTiiPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `period` (the moving-average and sum-window length; default 20); `pricePanelRatio` (the fraction of the height given to the price panel; default 0.55, clamped to [0.3, 0.8]); `gap` (the pixel gap between the stacked panels); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `priceColor` / `tiiColor` / `upColor` / `downColor` / `neutralColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showTii`/`showRefLine`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `tii`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_TII_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/PRICE_COLOR/TII_COLOR/UP_COLOR/DOWN_COLOR/NEUTRAL_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineTiiTrend` type (`up`|`down`|`neutral`); `getLineTiiFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value); `normalizeLineTiiPeriod(period, fallback)` (coerce to an integer of at least 2; non-finite or sub-2 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineTiiDeviations(closes, period)` -- the per-bar deviation from the moving average (non-array -> []); `computeLineTii(closes, period)` -- the TII series, null through the warm-up and for flat markets (non-array -> []); `runLineTii(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, period, deviations, tii, samples, tiiFinal, tiiMin, tiiMax, upCount, downCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, deviation, tii, trend}` (`trend` is `up`/`down`/`neutral`); `computeLineTiiLayout({data, period, ...})` -- stacks the price panel above the TII panel (the TII panel y-domain FIXED to [0,100]), projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `tiiPath`, `tiiMarkers`, the `refY` 50 reference line; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLineTiiChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Trend Intensity Index + deviation + moving average + share (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-tii-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the TII line + every price dot + every TII marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-tii / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -panel-label / -ref-line / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -tii-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-deviation / -tooltip-tii / -tooltip-trend / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-period); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-tii-final`, `data-up-count`, `data-down-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; grid / axis / tick / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"tii"`; markers expose `data-tii` and `data-trend`. The TII panel carries a dashed 50 reference centerline (the no-trend level) and trend-coloured markers (green up, red down, grey neutral). Tooltip on a price dot or a marker shows x, value, the deviation, the tii and the trend. Config badge in top-left calls out `TII <period>`. The legend has two toggle items (Price / TII). 62 vitest cases (getLineTiiFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + value** + non-array -> [], normalizeLineTiiPeriod incl. floors a fractional + **sub-2 / NaN / negative falls back**, computeLineTiiDeviations incl. **the deviation from the moving average** + **the null warm-up** + **the flat-series zero** + non-array -> [], computeLineTii incl. **the trend intensity index** + **the null warm-up** + **null for a flat series** + **100 when every deviation is positive** + **0 when every deviation is negative** + non-array -> [], runLineTii incl. ok + the period + **the tii series** + **the exact tii series** + **the deviations** + **the null warm-up** + **the 50-level classification** + **self-consistent up/down counts** + **the final reading** + **the min/max readings** + not-ok short series + empty/null -> ok=false + x-sorting + one sample per point + **the period-20 default**, computeLineTiiLayout incl. ok + **stacks the price panel above the tii panel** + both paths + **one dot per bar + one marker per defined tii** + **the fixed [0,100] y-domain** + **the 50 reference line inside the tii panel** + the run statistics + collapsed dims -> ok=false + too little data -> ok=false, describeLineTiiChart incl. **the indicator vocabulary** + the up/down counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / root data-* / svg + price + tii lines / **both panel labels** / **one marker per defined tii** / **the trend attribute on each marker** / **the reference line** / two-item legend / config badge period / hide the price path / **hide the tii line + markers when showTii is false** / hide the tii line via the hidden set / **hide the reference line when showRefLine is false** / series toggle / price dots / empty state / omit config badge / ref / displayName / animate flag) all pass under vitest 4.1.5.
