| 11.653 | UI: chart-line-vhf primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.671 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-vhf.tsx` ships `<ChartLineVhf>` -- a pure-SVG TWO-PANEL chart with a **Vertical Horizontal Filter** panel scoring trend versus range from price travel (the price panel on top, the VHF panel below; the VHF is bounded to [0,1] so the panel uses a FIXED 0-to-1 y-domain). **The computation:** the Vertical Horizontal Filter (Adam White) decides whether a market is trending or ranging from the geometry of the price path. `computeLineVhfWindow(window)` scores a single window: the `range` is the net travel of the close (its highest value minus its lowest), the `travel` is the total distance walked (the sum of the absolute bar-to-bar moves), and the `vhf` is `range/travel` -- which sits in [0,1] because the net displacement can never exceed the total path. `computeLineVhf(values, period)` is the rolling estimate: for each bar the trailing window of `period` closes is scored, `VHF[i] = (highest close - lowest close) / sum(abs bar-to-bar move)`. A VHF near 1 marks a TRENDING market (the price moved in one direction so the range nearly equals the path), near 0 a RANGING market (the price travelled far but went nowhere). A flat window has a zero travel and an undefined VHF and is null, as are bars before the window is full. **Worked fixture:** the VHF is a ratio of a max-minus-min and a sum of absolute moves -- NO transcendentals -- so the whole pipeline is exact (asserted with `toEqual`). The fixture (`period 4`) is hand-tuned so every window's travel is exactly 16, hence every VHF divides cleanly by 16: vhf `[null,null,null,1,1,0.625,0.375,0.375,0.5,0.375,0.375,0.5]` (a monotone window has `range = travel` so it reads exactly 1; a zigzag window travels far but spans little so it reads low; a flat window reads null). `runLineVhf` sorts the finite price points by x, runs the pipeline, and returns the vhf series, per-bar `samples` classified `trending`/`ranging`/`neutral` against the 0.5 level, the final reading, and the trending/ranging counts; `ok` is false for fewer than two points. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs the deviation oscillators `<ChartLineCog>` / `<ChartLinePgo>` / `<ChartLineTii>` -- those measure the price relative to a REFERENCE (a moving average or a centroid); the VHF measures the GEOMETRY of the price path itself -- net displacement over total distance walked -- and needs no moving average. vs `<ChartLineHurst>` -- both are fixed [0,1] gauges judging trend versus chop, but the Hurst exponent uses rescaled range analysis (a statistical scaling law) while the VHF is a direct path-geometry ratio. vs `<ChartLineTii>` -- the TII reports the directional bias of deviations; the VHF reports how efficiently the price travelled, regardless of direction. All exported helpers carry a `LineVhf`/`Vhf` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineVhfPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `period` (the rolling window length; default 28); `pricePanelRatio` (the fraction of the height given to the price panel; default 0.55, clamped to [0.3, 0.8]); `gap` (the pixel gap between the stacked panels); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `priceColor` / `vhfColor` / `trendingColor` / `rangingColor` / `neutralColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showVhf`/`showRefLine`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `vhf`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_VHF_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/PRICE_COLOR/VHF_COLOR/TRENDING_COLOR/RANGING_COLOR/NEUTRAL_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineVhfRegime` type (`trending`|`ranging`|`neutral`); `getLineVhfFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value); `normalizeLineVhfPeriod(period, fallback)` (coerce to an integer of at least 2; non-finite or sub-2 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineVhfWindow(window)` -- returns `{range, travel, vhf}` (flat/too-short/non-array -> `{0,0,NaN}`); `computeLineVhf(values, period)` -- the rolling VHF series, null through the warm-up and for flat windows (non-array -> []); `runLineVhf(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, period, vhf, samples, vhfFinal, vhfMin, vhfMax, trendingCount, rangingCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, vhf, regime}` (`regime` is `trending`/`ranging`/`neutral`); `computeLineVhfLayout({data, period, ...})` -- stacks the price panel above the VHF panel (the VHF panel y-domain FIXED to [0,1]), projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `vhfPath`, `vhfMarkers`, the `refY` 0.5 reference line; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLineVhfChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Vertical Horizontal Filter + trending + ranging + travel (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-vhf-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the VHF line + every price dot + every VHF marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-vhf / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -panel-label / -ref-line / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -vhf-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-vhf / -tooltip-regime / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-period); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-vhf-final`, `data-trending-count`, `data-ranging-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; grid / axis / tick / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"vhf"`; markers expose `data-vhf` and `data-regime`. The VHF panel carries a dashed 0.5 reference centerline and regime-coloured markers (green trending, red ranging, grey neutral). Tooltip on a price dot or a marker shows x, value, the vhf and the regime. Config badge in top-left calls out `VHF <period>`. The legend has two toggle items (Price / VHF). 62 vitest cases (getLineVhfFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + value** + non-array -> [], normalizeLineVhfPeriod incl. floors a fractional + **sub-2 / NaN / negative falls back**, computeLineVhfWindow incl. **the range/travel/vhf of a window** + **the sub-one zigzag score** + **the flat-window NaN** + **the too-short NaN** + non-array -> {0,0,NaN}, computeLineVhf incl. **the rolling vertical horizontal filter** + **the null warm-up** + **1 for a monotone trend** + **null for a flat series** + **all-null for a series shorter than the period** + non-array -> [], runLineVhf incl. ok + the period + **the vhf series** + **the exact vhf series** + **the null warm-up** + **regime classification** + **self-consistent trending/ranging counts** + **the final reading** + **the min/max readings** + not-ok short series + empty/null -> ok=false + x-sorting + one sample per point + **the period-28 default**, computeLineVhfLayout incl. ok + **stacks the price panel above the vhf panel** + both paths + **one dot per bar + one marker per defined vhf** + **the fixed [0,1] y-domain** + **the 0.5 reference line inside the vhf panel** + the run statistics + collapsed dims -> ok=false + too little data -> ok=false, describeLineVhfChart incl. **the indicator vocabulary** + the trending/ranging counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / root data-* / svg + price + vhf lines / **both panel labels** / **one marker per defined vhf** / **the regime attribute on each marker** / **the reference line** / two-item legend / config badge period / hide the price path / **hide the vhf line + markers when showVhf is false** / hide the vhf line via the hidden set / **hide the reference line when showRefLine is false** / series toggle / price dots / empty state / omit config badge / ref / displayName / animate flag) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. | 11.654 | UI: chart-line-ravi primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.672 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-ravi.tsx` ships `<ChartLineRavi>` -- a pure-SVG TWO-PANEL chart with a **Range Action Verification Index** panel comparing a fast and a slow moving average spread (the price panel on top, the RAVI oscillator sub-panel below; the RAVI is centred on zero so it cannot share the price axis). **The computation:** the Range Action Verification Index (Tushar Chande) decides whether a market is trending or ranging by comparing two moving averages of different speed. `computeLineRaviSma(values, period)` is the `period`-bar simple moving average, null through the warm-up. `computeLineRavi(values, fastPeriod, slowPeriod)` is the RAVI: `RAVI[i] = 100*(SMA(close, fast) - SMA(close, slow))/SMA(close, slow)` -- the spread between the fast and the slow moving average, expressed as a percentage of the slow average. A positive RAVI marks the fast average above the slow (an uptrend), a negative RAVI below; the further from zero, the stronger the trend, while a reading near zero marks a ranging market. A zero-valued slow average has an undefined RAVI and is null, as are bars before the slow window is full. **Worked fixture:** the RAVI is a ratio of simple moving averages -- NO transcendentals -- so the whole pipeline is exact (asserted with `toEqual`). The fixture (fast 2, slow 4) is a period-4 periodic series `[70,150,130,50]`: every 4-window sums to 400, so the slow average is a constant 100 and the RAVI reduces to `fast average - 100` -- ravi `[null,null,null,-10,-40,10,40,-10,-40,10]`; a flat series makes the fast and slow averages agree so it reads exactly zero, a zero-priced series makes the slow average zero so it reads null. `runLineRavi` sorts the finite price points by x, runs the pipeline, and returns the ravi series, per-bar `samples` classified `positive`/`negative`/`zero` by the sign of the RAVI, the final reading, and the positive/negative counts; `ok` is false for fewer than two points. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs a single moving-average overlay `<ChartLineVwma>` / smoothing overlays -- those plot a smoothed price LEVEL on the price panel; the RAVI plots the GAP between two averages of different speed, on its own zero-centred panel. vs `<ChartLinePmo>` -- the PMO double-smooths a rate of change; the RAVI is the raw normalized spread of two plain moving averages, with no extra smoothing. vs `<ChartLineVhf>` -- both score trend versus range, but the VHF measures the geometry of the price path while the RAVI measures the divergence of two moving averages. All exported helpers carry a `LineRavi`/`Ravi` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineRaviPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `fastPeriod` (the fast moving-average length; default 7) / `slowPeriod` (the slow moving-average length; default 65); `pricePanelRatio` (the fraction of the height given to the price panel; default 0.55, clamped to [0.3, 0.8]); `gap` (the pixel gap between the stacked panels); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `priceColor` / `raviColor` / `positiveColor` / `negativeColor` / `zeroColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showRavi`/`showZeroLine`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `ravi`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_RAVI_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/FAST_PERIOD/SLOW_PERIOD/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/PRICE_COLOR/RAVI_COLOR/POSITIVE_COLOR/NEGATIVE_COLOR/ZERO_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineRaviSign` type (`positive`|`negative`|`zero`); `getLineRaviFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value); `normalizeLineRaviPeriod(period, fallback)` (coerce to a positive integer; non-finite or sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineRaviSma(values, period)` -- the simple moving average (non-array -> []); `computeLineRavi(values, fastPeriod, slowPeriod)` -- the RAVI series, null through the warm-up and for a zero slow average (non-array -> []); `runLineRavi(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, fastPeriod, slowPeriod, ravi, samples, raviFinal, raviMin, raviMax, positiveCount, negativeCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, ravi, sign}` (`sign` is `positive`/`negative`/`zero`); `computeLineRaviLayout({data, fastPeriod, slowPeriod, ...})` -- stacks the price panel above the RAVI panel (the RAVI panel y-domain symmetric around zero), projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `raviPath`, `raviMarkers`, the `zeroY` centerline; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLineRaviChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Range Action Verification Index + fast + slow + moving average (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-ravi-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the RAVI line + every price dot + every RAVI marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-ravi / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -panel-label / -zero-line / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -ravi-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-ravi / -tooltip-sign / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config); root mirrors `data-fast-period`, `data-slow-period`, `data-ravi-final`, `data-positive-count`, `data-negative-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; grid / axis / tick / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"ravi"`; markers expose `data-ravi` and `data-sign`. The RAVI panel carries a dashed zero centerline and sign-coloured markers (green positive, red negative, grey zero). Tooltip on a price dot or a marker shows x, value, the ravi and the sign. Config badge in top-left calls out `RAVI <fast>/<slow>`. The legend has two toggle items (Price / RAVI). 61 vitest cases (getLineRaviFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + value** + non-array -> [], normalizeLineRaviPeriod incl. floors a fractional + **sub-1 / NaN / negative falls back**, computeLineRaviSma incl. **the simple moving average** + **a shorter moving average** + **the null warm-up** + non-array -> [], computeLineRavi incl. **the range action verification index** + **the null warm-up** + **zero when the fast and slow averages agree** + **null for a zero-priced slow window** + **all-null for a series shorter than the slow period** + non-array -> [], runLineRavi incl. ok + **the fast + slow periods** + **the ravi series** + **the exact ravi series** + **the null warm-up** + **sign classification** + **self-consistent positive/negative counts** + **the final reading** + **the min/max readings** + not-ok short series + empty/null -> ok=false + x-sorting + one sample per point + **the 7/65 defaults**, computeLineRaviLayout incl. ok + **stacks the price panel above the ravi panel** + both paths + **one dot per bar + one marker per defined ravi** + **the zero line inside the ravi panel** + **the zero-centred y-domain** + the run statistics + collapsed dims -> ok=false + too little data -> ok=false, describeLineRaviChart incl. **the indicator vocabulary** + the positive/negative counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / root data-* / svg + price + ravi lines / **both panel labels** / **one marker per defined ravi** / **the sign attribute on each marker** / **the zero line** / two-item legend / config badge fast + slow periods / hide the price path / **hide the ravi line + markers when showRavi is false** / hide the ravi line via the hidden set / **hide the zero line when showZeroLine is false** / series toggle / price dots / empty state / omit config badge / ref / displayName / animate flag) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. | 11.655 | UI: chart-line-gator primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.673 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-gator.tsx` ships `<ChartLineGator>` -- a pure-SVG TWO-PANEL chart with a **Gator Oscillator** panel of two histograms from the Alligator moving average jaws (the price panel on top, the Gator panel below; the Gator panel renders the two histograms as BARS straddling a zero line -- the first chart-line primitive whose sub-panel is a bar histogram rather than a line). **The computation:** the Gator Oscillator (Bill Williams) is built from the Alligator -- three moving averages of different speed: the jaw (slow), the teeth (medium), the lips (fast). `computeLineGatorSma(values, period)` is the simple moving average used for each jaw. `computeLineGator(values, jawPeriod, teethPeriod, lipsPeriod)` computes the three averages and reduces them to two histograms: `upper[i] = abs(jaw[i]-teeth[i])` (the jaw-teeth gap, non-negative) and `lower[i] = -abs(teeth[i]-lips[i])` (the negated teeth-lips gap, non-positive). When the three averages converge the Alligator SLEEPS and the histograms shrink toward zero; when they spread it FEEDS and the histograms grow. Both histograms share the jaw warm-up, so they are null until all three averages are defined. **Worked fixture:** the histograms are absolute gaps between simple moving averages -- NO transcendentals -- so the whole pipeline is exact (asserted with `toEqual`). The fixture (jaw 4, teeth 3, lips 2) uses closes that are all multiples of 12, so every SMA is an integer and every histogram value divides cleanly: jaw `[.,.,.,30,36,36,30,24,24,30,36,36]`, teeth `[.,.,24,36,40,36,24,20,24,36,40,36]`, lips `[.,18,30,42,42,30,18,18,30,42,42,30]`, upper `[.,.,.,6,4,0,6,4,0,6,4,0]`, lower `[.,.,.,-6,-2,-6,-6,-2,-6,-6,-2,-6]`. Each sample's phase compares its total gator spread (`upper + abs(lower)`) to the previous bar's -- a larger spread is `feeding`, a smaller one `sleeping`, an equal one `steady`. `runLineGator` sorts the finite price points by x, runs the pipeline, and returns the upper/lower histogram series, per-bar `samples` classified `feeding`/`sleeping`/`steady`, the final readings, and the feeding/sleeping counts; `ok` is false for fewer than two points. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs the line oscillators `<ChartLineRoofing>` / `<ChartLineTrendflex>` / `<ChartLineCog>` / `<ChartLineRavi>` -- those draw a single oscillator LINE with circle markers; the Gator panel renders two HISTOGRAMS of bars, and it measures the convergence of THREE moving averages rather than one derived signal. vs `<ChartLineRavi>` -- the RAVI compares two moving averages as one signed line; the Gator compares three averages as two separate gap histograms. It is the first chart-line primitive whose sub-panel is a bar histogram rather than a line. All exported helpers carry a `LineGator`/`Gator` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineGatorPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `jawPeriod` (the slow average; default 13) / `teethPeriod` (the medium average; default 8) / `lipsPeriod` (the fast average; default 5); `pricePanelRatio` (the fraction of the height given to the price panel; default 0.55, clamped to [0.3, 0.8]); `gap` (the pixel gap between the stacked panels); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `priceColor` / `upperColor` / `lowerColor` / `zeroColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showUpper`/`showLower`/`showZeroLine`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `upper` / `lower`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_GATOR_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/JAW_PERIOD/TEETH_PERIOD/LIPS_PERIOD/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/PRICE_COLOR/UPPER_COLOR/LOWER_COLOR/ZERO_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineGatorPhase` type (`feeding`|`sleeping`|`steady`); `getLineGatorFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value); `normalizeLineGatorPeriod(period, fallback)` (coerce to a positive integer; non-finite or sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineGatorSma(values, period)` -- the simple moving average (non-array -> []); `computeLineGator(values, jawPeriod, teethPeriod, lipsPeriod)` -- returns `{jaw, teeth, lips, upper, lower}` (non-array -> empty series); `runLineGator(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, jawPeriod, teethPeriod, lipsPeriod, upper, lower, samples, upperFinal, lowerFinal, upperMax, lowerMin, feedingCount, sleepingCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, upper, lower, phase}` (`phase` is `feeding`/`sleeping`/`steady`); `computeLineGatorLayout({data, jawPeriod, teethPeriod, lipsPeriod, ...})` -- stacks the price panel above the Gator panel (the Gator panel y-domain symmetric around zero), projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `gatorBars` (one per defined sample, each with the upper + lower rect geometry), the `barWidth`, the `zeroY` centerline; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLineGatorChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Gator Oscillator + Alligator + jaw + histogram (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-gator-aria-desc"` span; the price line + every price dot + every upper bar + every lower bar `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-gator / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -panel-label / -zero-line / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -upper-histogram / -upper-bar / -lower-histogram / -lower-bar / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-upper / -tooltip-lower / -tooltip-phase / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config); root mirrors `data-jaw-period`, `data-teeth-period`, `data-lips-period`, `data-upper-final`, `data-lower-final`, `data-feeding-count`, `data-sleeping-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; grid / axis / tick / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"gator"`; upper bars expose `data-upper` + `data-phase`, lower bars `data-lower` + `data-phase`. The Gator panel renders the two histograms as `<rect>` bars -- green upper bars above a dashed zero centerline, red lower bars below. Tooltip on a price dot or a bar shows x, value, the upper, the lower and the phase. Config badge in top-left calls out `GATOR <jaw>/<teeth>/<lips>`. The legend has three toggle items (Price / Upper / Lower). 65 vitest cases (getLineGatorFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + value** + non-array -> [], normalizeLineGatorPeriod incl. floors a fractional + **sub-1 / NaN / negative falls back**, computeLineGatorSma incl. **the simple moving average** + **a shorter moving average** + **the null warm-up** + non-array -> [], computeLineGator incl. **the jaw/teeth/lips moving averages** + **the upper histogram as the jaw-teeth gap** + **the lower histogram as the negated teeth-lips gap** + **the non-negative upper / non-positive lower property** + **the null warm-up** + **the shared jaw warm-up** + non-array -> empty series, runLineGator incl. ok + **the three periods** + **the upper + lower series** + **the exact upper histogram** + **the exact lower histogram** + **the null warm-up** + **phase classification by the gator-spread change** + **self-consistent feeding/sleeping counts** + **the final readings** + **the upper max + lower min** + not-ok short series + empty/null -> ok=false + x-sorting + one sample per point + **the 13/8/5 defaults**, computeLineGatorLayout incl. ok + **stacks the price panel above the gator panel** + **a non-empty price path + a positive bar width** + **one dot per bar + one gator bar per defined sample** + **the zero line inside the gator panel** + **the zero-centred y-domain** + the | 11.656 | UI: chart-line-tdi primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.674 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-tdi.tsx` ships `<ChartLineTdi>` -- a pure-SVG TWO-PANEL chart with a **Traders Dynamic Index** panel combining an RSI line, a smoothed signal line and a volatility band (the price panel on top, the TDI panel below). **The computation:** the Traders Dynamic Index (Dean Malone) is a composite indicator layered on the RSI, with three co-plotted parts, all in RSI space. `computeLineTdiRsi(closes, period)` computes Cutler's RSI of the close: for each bar `gain = max(close-prevClose, 0)` and `loss = max(prevClose-close, 0)`, and `RSI[i] = 100 * avgGain / (avgGain + avgLoss)` where avgGain/avgLoss are SIMPLE moving averages of the gains and losses over the trailing `period` bars (Cutler's variant, not Wilder's recursive smoothing); a window with no movement at all reads 50. `computeLineTdiSma(values, period)` is the moving average of a NULLABLE series -- a window containing a null is null -- and is used for both the signal line and the band middle. `computeLineTdiBand(rsi, period, multiplier)` builds the volatility band as a Bollinger band on the RSI: the middle is `computeLineTdiSma(rsi, period)`, and the upper/lower sit `multiplier` POPULATION standard deviations of the RSI above and below the middle. `computeLineTdi(closes, rsiPeriod, signalPeriod, bandPeriod, multiplier)` wires all three together: the RSI of the close, a `signalPeriod` moving average of that RSI, and a `bandPeriod` volatility band around the RSI. **Worked fixture:** the RSI is a ratio of moving averages and the test band period is 2 -- and the 2-bar population standard deviation is exactly `|a-b|/2` -- so with the right closes the whole pipeline lands on exact dyadic numbers (asserted with `toEqual`). The fixture closes `[20,26,36,30,40,34,24,30,40,34]` are chosen so every 2-bar RSI window has gain+loss total 16, hence `RSI = 100*gainSum/16` is always an exact dyadic: rsi `[.,.,100,62.5,62.5,62.5,0,37.5,100,62.5]`, signal `[.,.,.,81.25,62.5,62.5,31.25,18.75,68.75,81.25]`, bandUpper `[.,.,.,118.75,62.5,62.5,93.75,56.25,131.25,118.75]`, bandLower `[.,.,.,43.75,62.5,62.5,-31.25,-18.75,6.25,43.75]`. Each sample is classified `bullish` when the RSI is above the signal, `bearish` when below, `neutral` when equal or either is undefined. `runLineTdi` sorts the finite price points by x, runs the pipeline, and returns the rsi/signal/bandUpper/bandLower series, per-bar `samples` classified bullish/bearish/neutral, the final rsi + signal readings, and the bullish/bearish counts; `ok` is false for fewer than two points. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs `<ChartLineRsi>` -- that primitive plots the bare RSI; the TDI layers a moving-average signal of the RSI AND a Bollinger band on the RSI on top, in one panel. vs the line oscillators `<ChartLineGator>` / `<ChartLineRavi>` / `<ChartLineCog>` / `<ChartLinePmo>` -- those reduce price to a single derived signal; the TDI is a composite of three co-plotted series. vs `<ChartLineBollinger>` -- that band wraps the raw PRICE; the TDI band wraps the RSI, measuring how volatile the oscillator itself has been. All exported helpers carry a `LineTdi`/`Tdi` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineTdiPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `rsiPeriod` (the RSI lookback; default 13) / `signalPeriod` (the RSI moving-average lookback; default 7) / `bandPeriod` (the volatility-band lookback; default 34) / `multiplier` (the band standard-deviation multiple; default 1.6185); `pricePanelRatio` (the fraction of the height given to the price panel; default 0.55, clamped to [0.3, 0.8]); `gap` (the pixel gap between the stacked panels); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `priceColor` / `rsiColor` / `signalColor` / `bandColor` / `refColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showRsi`/`showSignal`/`showBand`/`showRefLine`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `rsi` / `signal` / `band`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_TDI_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/RSI_PERIOD/SIGNAL_PERIOD/BAND_PERIOD/BAND_MULTIPLIER/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/PRICE_COLOR/RSI_COLOR/SIGNAL_COLOR/BAND_COLOR/REF_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineTdiCross` type (`bullish`|`bearish`|`neutral`); `getLineTdiFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value); `normalizeLineTdiPeriod(period, fallback)` (coerce to a positive integer; non-finite or sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineTdiRsi(closes, period)` -- Cutler's RSI (non-array -> []); `computeLineTdiSma(values, period)` -- the moving average of a nullable series (non-array -> []); `computeLineTdiBand(rsi, period, multiplier)` -- returns `{middle, upper, lower}` (non-array -> empty series); `computeLineTdi(closes, rsiPeriod, signalPeriod, bandPeriod, multiplier)` -- returns `{rsi, signal, bandMiddle, bandUpper, bandLower}` (non-array -> empty series); `runLineTdi(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, rsiPeriod, signalPeriod, bandPeriod, multiplier, rsi, signal, bandUpper, bandLower, samples, rsiFinal, signalFinal, bullishCount, bearishCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, rsi, signal, bandUpper, bandLower, cross}` (`cross` is `bullish`/`bearish`/`neutral`); `computeLineTdiLayout({data, rsiPeriod, signalPeriod, bandPeriod, multiplier, ...})` -- stacks the price panel above the TDI panel (the TDI panel y-domain data-driven over rsi/signal/bandUpper/bandLower), projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `rsiPath`, `signalPath`, `bandAreaPath` (the closed upper-to-lower fill), `rsiMarkers` (one per defined rsi sample), the `refY` (the 50 reference line); degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLineTdiChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Traders Dynamic Index + RSI + signal + volatility band (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-tdi-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the rsi line + the signal line + every price dot + every rsi marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-tdi / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -panel-label / -ref-line / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -band-area / -signal-line / -rsi-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-rsi / -tooltip-signal / -tooltip-cross / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config); root mirrors `data-rsi-period`, `data-signal-period`, `data-band-period`, `data-rsi-final`, `data-signal-final`, `data-bullish-count`, `data-bearish-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; grid / axis / tick / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"tdi"`; rsi markers expose `data-rsi` + `data-cross`. The TDI panel renders the volatility band as a filled `<path>` area, the signal and RSI as lines with RSI circle markers, and a dashed 50 reference line. Tooltip on a price dot or an rsi marker shows x, value, the rsi, the signal and the cross. Config badge in top-left calls out `TDI <rsi>/<signal>/<band>`. The legend has four toggle items (Price / RSI / Signal / Band). 76 vitest cases (getLineTdiFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + value** + non-array -> [], normalizeLineTdiPeriod incl. floors a fractional + **sub-1 / NaN / negative falls back**, computeLineTdiRsi incl. **the gains/losses RSI** + **the null warm-up** + **reads 100 when every bar rises** + **reads 0 when every bar falls** + **reads 50 for a flat series** + non-array -> [], computeLineTdiSma incl. **the nullable moving average** + **null where the window contains a null** + non-array -> [], computeLineTdiBand incl. **the band middle as the RSI moving average** + **the upper/lower from the RSI stddev** + **the flat-RSI collapse onto the middle** + non-array -> empty series, computeLineTdi incl. **the rsi/signal/band wired together** + **the signal as the RSI moving average** + **the null warm-up** + non-array -> empty series, runLineTdi incl. ok + **the three periods** + **the band multiplier** + **the rsi series** + **the signal series** + **the band upper + lower series** + one sample per point + **the bullish/bearish classification** + **the rsi/signal/band sample fields** + **the bullish + bearish counts** + **self-consistent counts** + **the final rsi + signal readings** + not-ok single point + empty/null -> ok=false + x-sorting + **the 13/7/34 defaults**, computeLineTdiLayout incl. ok + **stacks the price panel above the tdi panel** + **a non-empty price path** + **the rsi + signal paths** + **a closed band area path** + **one dot per point** + **one rsi marker per defined sample** + **the reference line inside the tdi panel** + the run statistics + collapsed canvas -> ok=false + too little data -> ok=false, describeLineTdiChart incl. **the indicator vocabulary** + the bullish/bearish counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / root data-* / svg + price line / **both panel labels** / **the rsi line** / **the signal line** / | 11.657 | UI: chart-line-squeeze primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.675 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-squeeze.tsx` ships `<ChartLineSqueeze>` -- a pure-SVG TWO-PANEL chart with a **TTM Squeeze** panel flagging when the Bollinger Bands sit inside the Keltner Channels (the price panel on top, the squeeze panel below). **The computation:** the TTM Squeeze (John Carter) detects periods of low volatility -- a "coil" -- that often precede a sharp move, by comparing two volatility bands drawn around the SAME middle. `computeLineSqueezeSma(values, period)` is the moving average of a nullable series (a window with a null is null). `computeLineSqueezeStd(values, period)` is the rolling POPULATION standard deviation. `computeLineSqueezeRange(closes)` is the close-only true range -- the absolute bar-to-bar change `abs(close[i]-close[i-1])`, null on the first bar -- and its moving average is the ATR. `computeLineSqueeze(closes, period, bbMult, kcMult)` assembles the Bollinger Bands `mid +/- bbMult*stddev(close)` and the Keltner Channels `mid +/- kcMult*ATR` around `mid` (the period-bar moving average of the close), and returns the `compression = kcMult*atr - bbMult*stddev` -- because both bands share the same middle, the compression is positive EXACTLY when the Bollinger Bands sit inside the Keltner Channels, so it is the squeeze captured in a single scalar. The squeeze is ON when `bbUpper < kcUpper AND bbLower > kcLower` (the Bollinger Bands fully inside the Keltner Channels) and OFF when they expand outside. **Worked fixture:** with `period` 2 the population standard deviation of a window is exactly `abs(a-b)/2`, so with integer closes the whole pipeline -- both bands and the compression -- lands on exact integers (asserted with `toEqual`). The fixture closes `[50,54,58,78,70,78,82,106,98,102]` give: bbUpper `[.,56,60,88,82,82,84,118,110,104]`, bbLower `[.,48,52,48,66,66,76,70,94,96]`, kcUpper `[.,.,62,86,95,86,89,115,126,109]`, kcLower `[.,.,50,50,53,62,71,73,78,91]`, compression `[.,.,2,-2,13,4,5,-3,16,5]`, state `[none,none,on,off,on,on,on,off,on,on]`. `runLineSqueeze` sorts the finite price points by x, runs the pipeline, and returns the mid/bbUpper/bbLower/kcUpper/kcLower/compression series, per-bar `samples` classified on/off/none by the literal band-containment test, the squeeze-on count (6) and squeeze-off count (2), the longest unbroken squeeze run (3) and the final state; `ok` is false for fewer than two points. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs `<ChartLineBollinger>` / `<ChartLineKeltner>` -- those draw a SINGLE volatility band; the squeeze co-plots BOTH bands and flags their containment as an explicit on/off state. vs `<ChartLineBandwidth>` -- that measures one band's width; the squeeze compares two bands' widths against each other. vs the oscillator panels `<ChartLineTdi>` / `<ChartLineGator>` / `<ChartLineRavi>` -- those reduce price to a derived line; the squeeze panel is a state-flag dot strip plus a compression line. All exported helpers carry a `LineSqueeze`/`Squeeze` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineSqueezePoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `period` (the band lookback; default 20) / `bbMult` (the Bollinger standard-deviation multiple; default 2) / `kcMult` (the Keltner ATR multiple; default 1.5); `pricePanelRatio` (the fraction of the height given to the price panel; default 0.58, clamped to [0.3, 0.8]); `gap` (the pixel gap between the stacked panels); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `priceColor` / `bbColor` / `kcColor` / `compressionColor` / `squeezeOnColor` / `squeezeOffColor` / `zeroColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showBb`/`showKc`/`showSqueeze`/`showZeroLine`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `bb` / `kc` / `squeeze`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_SQUEEZE_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/BB_MULT/KC_MULT/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/PRICE_COLOR/BB_COLOR/KC_COLOR/COMPRESSION_COLOR/ON_COLOR/OFF_COLOR/ZERO_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineSqueezeState` type (`on`|`off`|`none`); `getLineSqueezeFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value); `normalizeLineSqueezePeriod(period, fallback)` (coerce to a positive integer; non-finite or sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineSqueezeSma(values, period)` -- the moving average (non-array -> []); `computeLineSqueezeStd(values, period)` -- the rolling population stddev (non-array -> []); `computeLineSqueezeRange(closes)` -- the close-only true range (non-array -> []); `computeLineSqueeze(closes, period, bbMult, kcMult)` -- returns `{mid, bbUpper, bbLower, kcUpper, kcLower, compression}` (non-array -> empty series); `runLineSqueeze(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, period, bbMult, kcMult, mid, bbUpper, bbLower, kcUpper, kcLower, compression, samples, squeezeOnCount, squeezeOffCount, longestSqueeze, finalState, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, bbUpper, bbLower, kcUpper, kcLower, compression, state}` (`state` is `on`/`off`/`none`); `computeLineSqueezeLayout({data, period, bbMult, kcMult, ...})` -- stacks the price panel above the squeeze panel, projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `bbAreaPath` (the closed Bollinger upper-to-lower fill), `kcUpperPath` + `kcLowerPath` (the Keltner channel lines), `compressionPath`, `squeezeDots` (one per classified sample), the `zeroY` centerline; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLineSqueezeChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions TTM Squeeze + Bollinger Bands + Keltner Channels + squeeze (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-squeeze-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the Keltner upper/lower lines + the compression line + every price dot + every squeeze dot `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-squeeze / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -panel-label / -zero-line / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -bb-area / -kc-upper / -kc-lower / -compression-line / -squeeze-dots / -squeeze-dot / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-compression / -tooltip-state / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-bb-mult`, `data-kc-mult`, `data-squeeze-on-count`, `data-squeeze-off-count`, `data-longest-squeeze`, `data-final-state`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; grid / axis / tick / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"squeeze"`; squeeze dots expose `data-state`. The price panel draws the Bollinger Bands as a shaded `<path>` area and the Keltner Channels as two dashed channel lines (so the squeeze reads directly as the blue area inside the orange lines); the squeeze panel draws the compression line over a dashed zero line plus a row of squeeze dots coloured by state (red on / green off). Tooltip on a price dot or a squeeze dot shows x, value, compression and the squeeze state. Config badge in top-left calls out `SQZ <period>/<bbMult>/<kcMult>`. The legend has four toggle items (Price / Bollinger / Keltner / Squeeze). 75 vitest cases (getLineSqueezeFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + value** + non-array -> [], normalizeLineSqueezePeriod incl. floors a fractional + **sub-1 / NaN / negative falls back**, computeLineSqueezeSma incl. **the moving average** + **null where the window contains a null** + non-array -> [], computeLineSqueezeStd incl. **the rolling population stddev** + **reads zero for a flat window** + non-array -> [], computeLineSqueezeRange incl. **the absolute bar-to-bar change** + **null first bar** + non-array -> [], computeLineSqueeze incl. **the Bollinger Bands** + **the Keltner Channels** + **the compression as the Keltner minus Bollinger width** + **the shared middle** + **compression positive iff BB inside KC** + non-array -> empty series, runLineSqueeze incl. ok + **the period + two multipliers** + **the Bollinger series** + **the Keltner series** + **the | 11.658 | UI: chart-line-rmi primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.676 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-rmi.tsx` ships `<ChartLineRmi>` -- a pure-SVG TWO-PANEL chart with a **Relative Momentum Index** panel applying the RSI formula across a momentum lookback (the price panel on top, the RMI panel below). **The computation:** the Relative Momentum Index (Roger Altman) generalises the RSI -- the ordinary RSI measures each bar's gain or loss against the IMMEDIATELY PRIOR close (a 1-bar momentum); the RMI measures it against the close `momentumPeriod` bars EARLIER (a longer momentum lookback), then applies the same RSI smoothing and formula. `computeLineRmiChange(closes, momentumPeriod)` is the momentum change `change[i] = close[i] - close[i-momentumPeriod]`, null through the lookback warm-up. `computeLineRmi(closes, period, momentumPeriod)` turns that change into gains and losses (`gain = max(change,0)`, `loss = max(-change,0)`) and computes `RMI[i] = 100 * avgGain / (avgGain + avgLoss)` where avgGain/avgLoss are `period`-bar SIMPLE moving averages of the gains and losses; a window with no movement at all reads 50, and with `momentumPeriod` 1 the RMI is exactly the ordinary RSI. **Worked fixture:** the RMI is a ratio of summed gains and losses -- no transcendentals -- so with the right closes it is exact (asserted with `toEqual`). The fixture closes `[40,44,48,52,56,60,48,52,40,44,48,52,40,60]` are chosen so every 2-bar momentum change is exactly `+/-8`; with `period` 4 each RMI window then has gain+loss total `4*8=32`, so `RMI = 100*gainSum/32` lands on the exact dyadics `{0,25,50,75,100}`: change `[.,.,8,8,8,8,-8,-8,-8,-8,8,8,-8,8]`, rmi `[.,.,.,.,.,100,75,50,25,0,25,50,50,75]`. Against an overbought level of 70 and an oversold level of 30 the bars classify as overbought/oversold/neutral 3 each. `runLineRmi` sorts the finite price points by x, runs the pipeline, and returns the rmi series, per-bar `samples` classified overbought/oversold/neutral/none against the two levels, the zone counts and the final reading; `ok` is false for fewer than two points. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs `<ChartLineRsi>` -- that primitive measures each bar against the PRIOR close (a 1-bar momentum); the RMI parameterises the momentum lookback, so it is the RSI generalised across a multi-bar change. vs `<ChartLineTdi>` -- the TDI is a composite that layers a signal line and a Bollinger band on the RSI; the RMI is a single oscillator line with overbought/oversold levels. vs the momentum oscillators `<ChartLineDpo>` / `<ChartLineCmo>` / `<ChartLineTsi>` -- those use their own normalisations; the RMI keeps the EXACT RSI ratio formula and only widens the change window. All exported helpers carry a `LineRmi`/`Rmi` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineRmiPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `period` (the gain/loss averaging length; default 20) / `momentumPeriod` (the momentum lookback distance; default 5) / `overbought` (the overbought level; default 70, clamped to [0,100]) / `oversold` (the oversold level; default 30, clamped to [0,100]); `pricePanelRatio` (the fraction of the height given to the price panel; default 0.55, clamped to [0.3, 0.8]); `gap` (the pixel gap between the stacked panels); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `priceColor` / `rmiColor` / `overboughtColor` / `oversoldColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showRmi`/`showLevels`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `rmi` / `levels`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_RMI_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/MOMENTUM_PERIOD/OVERBOUGHT/OVERSOLD/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/PRICE_COLOR/RMI_COLOR/OVERBOUGHT_COLOR/OVERSOLD_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineRmiZone` type (`overbought`|`oversold`|`neutral`|`none`); `getLineRmiFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value); `normalizeLineRmiPeriod(period, fallback)` (coerce to a positive integer; non-finite or sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineRmiChange(closes, momentumPeriod)` -- the momentum change (non-array -> []); `computeLineRmi(closes, period, momentumPeriod)` -- the RMI series (non-array -> []); `runLineRmi(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, period, momentumPeriod, overbought, oversold, rmi, samples, rmiFinal, overboughtCount, oversoldCount, neutralCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, rmi, zone}` (`zone` is `overbought`/`oversold`/`neutral`/`none`); `computeLineRmiLayout({data, period, momentumPeriod, overbought, oversold, ...})` -- stacks the price panel above the RMI panel (the RMI panel on a FIXED 0-100 scale), projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `rmiPath`, `rmiMarkers` (one per defined bar), the `overboughtY` + `oversoldY` reference-line positions; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLineRmiChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Relative Momentum Index + RSI formula + momentum + overbought + oversold (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-rmi-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the rmi line + every price dot + every rmi marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-rmi / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -panel-label / -levels / -level-line / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -rmi-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-rmi / -tooltip-zone / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-momentum-period`, `data-rmi-final`, `data-overbought-count`, `data-oversold-count`, `data-neutral-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; grid / axis / tick / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"rmi"`; level lines carry `data-level="overbought"|"oversold"`; rmi markers expose `data-rmi` + `data-zone`. The RMI panel is fixed to a 0-100 scale, draws dashed overbought and oversold reference lines, and colours each RMI marker by its zone (red overbought, green oversold, violet neutral). Tooltip on a price dot or an rmi marker shows x, value, the rmi and the zone. Config badge in top-left calls out `RMI <period>/<momentum>`. | 11.659 | UI: chart-line-decycler primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.677 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-decycler.tsx` ships `<ChartLineDecycler>` -- a pure-SVG SINGLE-PANEL line chart with an **Ehlers Decycler** overlay removing the dominant short cycle with a highpass subtraction (the price line and the decycler line overlaid in one panel sharing one y-domain). **The computation:** John Ehlers' Decycler rests on the idea that a price series is the sum of a slow trend and a fast cycle (`price = trend + cycle`); if a highpass filter extracts the cycle, then subtracting it leaves the trend (`decycler = price - highpass`). `computeLineDecyclerAlpha(period)` is the Ehlers one-pole highpass coefficient for a cutoff `period`: `alpha = (cos(w) + sin(w) - 1) / cos(w)` with `w = 2*pi/period`, clamped to the stable range `[0,1]`; the lone degenerate integer period -- 4, where `cos(w)` is zero -- returns 1. `computeLineDecyclerHighpass(values, period)` runs the one-pole highpass recursion `HP[i] = (1-alpha/2)*(v[i]-v[i-1]) + (1-alpha)*HP[i-1]` seeded with `HP[0]=0` -- the fast cycle component. `computeLineDecycler(values, period)` subtracts it: `decycler[i] = v[i] - HP[i]`, leaving a smooth trend line. **Exactness:** the highpass recursion uses `cos`/`sin` of `2*pi/period`, so the decycler is transcendental in general and the varying-series tests assert with `toBeCloseTo`; three properties are exact (`toEqual`/`toBe`): the highpass is seeded at zero so `decycler[0] = price[0]` exactly for any input, a flat price has no bar-to-bar change so the highpass stays zero and the decycler equals the price exactly, and `decycler[i] = price[i] - HP[i]` is an exact identity. Two further checks pin the transcendental filter: the recursion relationship is verified bar by bar against `computeLineDecyclerAlpha`, and a long constant-slope ramp is checked against the closed-form steady state `HP* = (1-alpha/2)*slope/alpha` (for slope 5, period 8 -> HP converges to ~6.0355). `runLineDecycler` sorts the finite price points by x, runs the filter, and returns the highpass + decycler series, per-bar `samples` classified rising/falling/flat by the decycler slope, the trend counts and the final reading; `ok` is false for fewer than two points. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs the moving-average overlays `<ChartLineVwma>` / `<ChartLineKama>` / `<ChartLineHma>` -- those smooth the price with a LAGGING average; the decycler is the price minus a highpass, so it tracks the price closely WITHOUT the lag. vs `<ChartLineRoofing>` / `<ChartLineSupersmoother>` -- those keep a filtered cycle band; the decycler does the OPPOSITE -- it removes the cycle and keeps the trend. vs `<ChartLineDpo>` -- the DPO plots the cycle (price minus trend); the decycler plots the trend (price minus cycle). All exported helpers carry a `LineDecycler`/`Decycler` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineDecyclerPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `period` (the highpass cutoff period; default 30, normalized to an integer >= 2); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `priceColor` / `decyclerColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showDecycler`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `decycler`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_DECYCLER_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/PRICE_COLOR/DECYCLER_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineDecyclerTrend` type (`rising`|`falling`|`flat`); `getLineDecyclerFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value); `normalizeLineDecyclerPeriod(period, fallback)` (coerce to an integer >= 2; non-finite or sub-2 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineDecyclerAlpha(period)` -- the highpass coefficient; `computeLineDecyclerHighpass(values, period)` -- the one-pole highpass series (non-array -> []); `computeLineDecycler(values, period)` -- the decycler series (non-array -> []); `runLineDecycler(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, period, alpha, highpass, decycler, samples, decyclerFinal, risingCount, fallingCount, flatCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, highpass, decycler, trend}` (`trend` is `rising`/`falling`/`flat`); `computeLineDecyclerLayout({data, period, ...})` -- projects the price and decycler as two overlaid lines in a single full-width panel sharing one y-domain, returns `pricePath`, `decyclerPath`, `priceDots`, `decyclerMarkers` (one per bar); degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLineDecyclerChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Ehlers Decycler + highpass + cycle + trend (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-decycler-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the decycler line + every price dot + every decycler marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-decycler / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -decycler-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-decycler / -tooltip-trend / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-alpha`, `data-decycler-final`, `data-rising-count`, `data-falling-count`, `data-flat-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; axis nodes carry `data-axis="x"|"y"`; decycler markers expose `data-decycler` + `data-trend`. The price and decycler are drawn as two overlaid lines; the decycler carries one circle marker per bar. Tooltip on a price dot or a decycler marker shows x, value, the decycler value and the trend. Config badge in top-left calls out `DECYCLER <period>`. The legend has two toggle items (Price / Decycler). 66 vitest cases (getLineDecyclerFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + value** + non-array -> [], normalizeLineDecyclerPeriod incl. floors a fractional + **sub-2 / NaN / negative falls back**, computeLineDecyclerAlpha incl. **the one-pole highpass coefficient** + **stays inside the stable range** + **clamps a short period to 1** + **returns 1 for the degenerate period 4**, computeLineDecyclerHighpass incl. **seeds the first value at zero** + **all zero for a flat series** + **follows the one-pole recursion** + **converges to the steady state for a ramp** + non-array -> [], computeLineDecycler incl. **passes the first bar through unchanged** + **equals the price for a flat series** + **is the price minus the highpass** + **matches the hand-computed second bar** + **finite for every bar** + non-array -> [], runLineDecycler incl. ok + **the period** + **the highpass coefficient** + **the highpass + decycler series** + one sample per point + **the trend classification** + **the rising/falling/flat counts** + **self-consistent counts** + **the final reading** + **the highpass/decycler/trend sample fields** + not-ok path** + **one dot per point** + **one marker per point** + the run statistics + collapsed canvas -> ok=false + too little data -> ok=false, describeLineDecyclerChart incl. **the indicator | 11.660 | UI: chart-line-ift primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.678 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-ift.tsx` ships `<ChartLineIft>` -- a pure-SVG TWO-PANEL chart with an **Inverse Fisher Transform** panel compressing an oscillator into a sharp bounded signal (the price panel on top, the IFT panel below). **The computation:** John Ehlers' Inverse Fisher Transform squashes any real input into the open interval `(-1, 1)` via `IFT(x) = (exp(2x) - 1) / (exp(2x) + 1)` (this is the hyperbolic tangent); large positive inputs saturate near `+1`, large negative inputs near `-1`, with a fast transition through zero -- so a soft oscillator becomes a sharp, bounded signal. `computeLineIftTransform(x)` evaluates the transform in the OVERFLOW-SAFE form -- it divides through by `exp` of the larger-magnitude exponent so a large positive input saturates at `+1` cleanly rather than producing `Infinity/Infinity = NaN`. `computeLineIftOscillator(closes, period, scale)` builds the input oscillator -- the scaled deviation of the close from its `period`-bar moving average, `osc[i] = scale * (close[i] - sma[i])`. `computeLineIft(closes, period, scale)` compresses that oscillator through the transform. **Exactness:** the transform uses `exp`, so the IFT is transcendental in general and the tests assert with `toBeCloseTo` -- in particular `computeLineIftTransform(x)` is checked against `Math.tanh(x)`; two anchors are exact -- `IFT(0) = 0` exactly (`exp(0) = 1`, so `(1-1)/(1+1) = 0`), and therefore the IFT is exactly zero wherever the oscillator is zero (which happens whenever the price sits exactly on its moving average). The fixture closes `[20,24,20,28,20,12,24,20,20,28]` with `period` 2 and `scale` 0.5 make the oscillator an exact dyadic -- `osc[i] = (close[i]-close[i-1])/4` -- so the oscillator array `[.,1,-1,2,-2,-2,3,-1,0,2]` is asserted with `toEqual`, and the bar where it is zero produces an exact `IFT = 0`. `runLineIft` sorts the finite price points by x, runs the pipeline, and returns the oscillator + ift series, per-bar `samples` classified bullish/bearish/neutral/none against the trigger threshold, the zone counts and the final reading; `ok` is false for fewer than two points. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs the oscillator panels `<ChartLineRmi>` / `<ChartLineTdi>` / `<ChartLineCmo>` -- those DERIVE a new oscillator from the price; the IFT does not derive a signal, it is a non-linear COMPRESSOR that sharpens an input oscillator toward the bounds. vs `<ChartLineFisher>` -- the Fisher Transform EXPANDS the tails of a bounded input (`0.5*ln((1+x)/(1-x))`); the Inverse Fisher Transform is its inverse, COMPRESSING an unbounded input back into bounds. vs `<ChartLineZscore>` -- a z-score is an unbounded LINEAR normalisation; the IFT is a bounded NON-LINEAR one. All exported helpers carry a `LineIft`/`Ift` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineIftPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `period` (the oscillator moving-average lookback; default 9) / `scale` (the oscillator scale factor applied before the transform; default 0.5) / `threshold` (the bullish/bearish trigger level; default 0.5, accepted in (0,1)); `pricePanelRatio` (the fraction of the height given to the price panel; default 0.55, clamped to [0.3, 0.8]); `gap` (the pixel gap between the stacked panels); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `priceColor` / `iftColor` / `bullishColor` / `bearishColor` / `zeroColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showIft`/`showLevels`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `ift` / `levels`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_IFT_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/SCALE/THRESHOLD/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/PRICE_COLOR/IFT_COLOR/BULLISH_COLOR/BEARISH_COLOR/ZERO_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineIftZone` type (`bullish`|`bearish`|`neutral`|`none`); `getLineIftFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value); `normalizeLineIftPeriod(period, fallback)` (coerce to a positive integer; non-finite or sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineIftTransform(x)` -- the Inverse Fisher Transform of a scalar (non-finite -> 0); `computeLineIftSma(values, period)` -- the moving average of a nullable series (non-array -> []); `computeLineIftOscillator(closes, period, scale)` -- the scaled deviation from the moving average (non-array -> []); `computeLineIft(closes, period, scale)` -- the oscillator compressed through the transform (non-array -> []); `runLineIft(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, period, scale, threshold, oscillator, ift, samples, iftFinal, bullishCount, bearishCount, neutralCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, oscillator, ift, zone}` (`zone` is `bullish`/`bearish`/`neutral`/`none`); `computeLineIftLayout({data, period, scale, threshold, ...})` -- stacks the price panel above the IFT panel (the IFT panel on a FIXED -1 to +1 scale), projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `iftPath`, `iftMarkers` (one per defined bar), the `zeroY` + `upperY` + `lowerY` reference-line positions; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLineIftChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Inverse Fisher Transform + oscillator + compresses + the -1 to +1 bound (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-ift-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the ift line + every price dot + every ift marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-ift / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -panel-label / -levels / -level-line / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -ift-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-osc / -tooltip-ift / -tooltip-zone / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-scale`, `data-ift-final`, `data-bullish-count`, `data-bearish-count`, `data-neutral-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; grid / axis / tick / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"ift"`; level lines carry `data-level="upper"|"zero"|"lower"`; ift markers expose `data-ift` + `data-zone`. The IFT panel is fixed to a -1 to +1 scale, draws dashed zero and trigger reference lines, and colours each IFT marker by its zone (green bullish, red bearish, cyan neutral). Tooltip on a price dot or an ift marker shows x, value, the oscillator, the ift and the zone. Config badge in top-left calls out `IFT <period>/<scale>`. The legend has three toggle items (Price / IFT / Levels). 74 vitest cases (getLineIftFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + value** + non-array -> [], normalizeLineIftPeriod incl. floors a fractional + **sub-1 / NaN / negative falls back**, computeLineIftTransform incl. **exactly zero at the origin** + **matches the hyperbolic tangent** + **stays inside the open interval -1 to 1** + **an odd function** + **saturates near +1** + **saturates near -1** + **non-finite -> 0**, computeLineIftSma incl. **the moving average** + **null where the window contains a null** + non-array -> [], computeLineIftOscillator incl. **the scaled deviation from the moving average** + **the null warm-up** + **scales with the scale factor** + non-array -> [], computeLineIft incl. **compresses the oscillator through the transform** + **exactly zero where the oscillator is zero** + **the null warm-up** + **the transform of the oscillator** + non-array -> [], runLineIft incl. ok + **the **the final reading** + **the oscillator/ift/zone sample fields** + not-ok single point + empty/null -> ok=false + x-sorting + **the period 9 scale 0.5 default**, computeLineIftLayout incl. ok + | 11.661 | UI: chart-line-pzo primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.679 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-pzo.tsx` ships `<ChartLinePzo>` -- a pure-SVG TWO-PANEL chart with a **Price Zone Oscillator** panel from the ratio of signed price change to total price change (the price panel on top, the PZO panel below). **The computation:** the Price Zone Oscillator measures how DIRECTIONAL a price move has been over a lookback, as the ratio of two quantities -- the **signed price change** (the sum of the signed bar-to-bar changes, which telescopes to the net move `close[i] - close[i-period]`) and the **total price change** (the sum of the ABSOLUTE bar-to-bar changes, the total distance travelled). `PZO[i] = 100 * signedChange[i] / totalChange[i]`. Because `|signedChange| <= totalChange` always (the triangle inequality), the PZO is bounded in `-100..+100`: a clean up-trend where every step is up reads `+100`, a clean down-trend reads `-100`, a choppy range where the up and down steps cancel reads near zero, and a window with no movement reads 0. `computeLinePzoSignedChange`, `computeLinePzoTotalChange` and `computeLinePzo` implement the three quantities. **Exactness:** the PZO is a ratio of integer sums -- no transcendentals -- so it is exact when the closes are integers and the window total divides cleanly. The fixture closes `[50,52,56,58,60,58,54,52,50,52,56,54,56]` with `period` 4 have an absolute-change sequence that repeats every four bars, so every window has a total change of 10, and `PZO = 10*signedChange` lands on exact multiples of 10: signed `[.,.,.,.,10,6,-2,-6,-10,-6,2,2,6]`, pzo `[.,.,.,.,100,60,-20,-60,-100,-60,20,20,60]` -- the whole pipeline is asserted with `toEqual`. `runLinePzo` sorts the finite price points by x, runs the pipeline, and returns the signed/total/pzo series, per-bar `samples` classified bullish/bearish/neutral/none against the threshold, the zone counts and the final reading; `ok` is false for fewer than two points. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs `<ChartLineVhf>` / `<ChartLineChoppiness>` -- those measure directional strength as an UNSIGNED ratio; the PZO keeps the SIGN of the move, so a single bounded scale reads both the direction AND the strength of the trend. vs `<ChartLineRavi>` / `<ChartLineCmo>` -- those derive momentum from moving averages or summed gains; the PZO is the net-move-over-total-move efficiency ratio. vs `<ChartLineRate>` -- a rate of change is the raw signed move; the PZO NORMALISES that move by the total distance travelled, bounding it. All exported helpers carry a `LinePzo`/`Pzo` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLinePzoPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `period` (the lookback window; default 14) / `threshold` (the bullish/bearish trigger level; default 40, accepted in (0,100)); `pricePanelRatio` (the fraction of the height given to the price panel; default 0.55, clamped to [0.3, 0.8]); `gap` (the pixel gap between the stacked panels); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `priceColor` / `pzoColor` / `bullishColor` / `bearishColor` / `zeroColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showPzo`/`showLevels`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `pzo` / `levels`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_PZO_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/THRESHOLD/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/PRICE_COLOR/PZO_COLOR/BULLISH_COLOR/BEARISH_COLOR/ZERO_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLinePzoZone` type (`bullish`|`bearish`|`neutral`|`none`); `getLinePzoFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value); `normalizeLinePzoPeriod(period, fallback)` (coerce to a positive integer; non-finite or sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLinePzoSignedChange(closes, period)` -- the signed change over the window (non-array -> []); `computeLinePzoTotalChange(closes, period)` -- the total absolute change over the window (non-array -> []); `computeLinePzo(closes, period)` -- the PZO series (non-array -> []); `runLinePzo(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, period, threshold, signedChange, totalChange, pzo, samples, pzoFinal, bullishCount, bearishCount, neutralCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, signedChange, totalChange, pzo, zone}` (`zone` is `bullish`/`bearish`/`neutral`/`none`); `computeLinePzoLayout({data, period, threshold, ...})` -- stacks the price panel above the PZO panel (the PZO panel on a FIXED -100 to +100 scale), projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `pzoPath`, `pzoMarkers` (one per defined bar), the `zeroY` + `upperY` + `lowerY` reference-line positions; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLinePzoChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Price Zone Oscillator + ratio + signed price change + total price change (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-pzo-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the pzo line + every price dot + every pzo marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-pzo / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -panel-label / -levels / -level-line / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -pzo-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-signed / -tooltip-pzo / -tooltip-zone / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-threshold`, `data-pzo-final`, `data-bullish-count`, `data-bearish-count`, `data-neutral-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; grid / axis / tick / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"pzo"`; level lines carry `data-level="upper"|"zero"|"lower"`; pzo markers expose `data-pzo` + `data-zone`. The PZO panel is fixed to a -100 to +100 scale, draws dashed zero and trigger reference lines, and colours each PZO marker by its zone (green bullish, red bearish, purple neutral). Tooltip on a price dot or a pzo marker shows x, value, the signed change, the pzo and the zone. Config badge in top-left calls out `PZO <period>`. The legend has three toggle items (Price / PZO / Levels). 70 vitest cases (getLinePzoFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + value** + non-array -> [], normalizeLinePzoPeriod incl. floors a | 11.662 | UI: chart-line-vzo primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.680 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-vzo.tsx` ships `<ChartLineVzo>` -- a pure-SVG TWO-PANEL chart with a **Volume Zone Oscillator** panel from the ratio of signed volume to total volume (the price panel on top, the VZO panel below). **The computation:** the Volume Zone Oscillator is the volume-based counterpart of the Price Zone Oscillator -- it measures whether the volume of a lookback window leaned to the buy side or the sell side, as the ratio of two quantities: the **signed volume** (the sum, over the window, of each bar's volume signed by its price direction -- positive on an up bar where `close >= prior close`, negative on a down bar) and the **total volume** (the sum of the raw bar volumes). `VZO[i] = 100 * signedVolume[i] / totalVolume[i]`. Because each signed contribution is `+/- volume`, the magnitude of the signed volume never exceeds the total volume, so the VZO is bounded in `-100..+100`: every-up-bar reads `+100`, every-down-bar reads `-100`, mixed volume reads in between, and a window with no volume at all reads 0. `computeLineVzoSignedVolume`, `computeLineVzoTotalVolume` and `computeLineVzo` implement the three quantities, each consuming `{x, value, volume}` points where `value` is the close and `volume` is the bar volume. **Exactness:** the VZO is a ratio of integer sums -- no transcendentals -- so it is exact when the volumes are integers and the window total divides cleanly. The fixture volumes repeat the pattern `[10,40,30,20]` every four bars, so with `period` 4 every window has a total volume of 100 and `VZO = signedVolume` lands on exact integers: signed `[.,.,.,.,100,80,0,-60,-100,-80,-80,-20,-20]`, vzo `[.,.,.,.,100,80,0,-60,-100,-80,-80,-20,-20]` -- the whole pipeline is asserted with `toEqual`. `runLineVzo` sorts the finite price points by x, runs the pipeline, and returns the signed/total/vzo series, per-bar `samples` classified bullish/bearish/neutral/none against the threshold, the zone counts and the final reading; `ok` is false for fewer than two points. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs `<ChartLinePzo>` -- the PZO weights each bar by its signed PRICE change; the VZO weights each bar by its VOLUME, signed by the price direction, so it reads buying-versus-selling CONVICTION rather than the efficiency of the price move. vs `<ChartLineObv>` / `<ChartLinePvt>` -- those accumulate a running signed-volume total WITHOUT bound; the VZO NORMALISES the signed volume by the total volume, bounding it in -100..+100. vs `<ChartLineMfi>` -- the Money Flow Index weights volume by the typical price and reads 0..100; the VZO uses raw volume signed by direction and reads -100..+100. All exported helpers carry a `LineVzo`/`Vzo` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineVzoPoint[]` (`{x, value, volume}`); `period` (the lookback window; default 14) / `threshold` (the bullish/bearish trigger level; default 40, accepted in (0,100)); `pricePanelRatio` (the fraction of the height given to the price panel; default 0.55, clamped to [0.3, 0.8]); `gap` (the pixel gap between the stacked panels); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `priceColor` / `vzoColor` / `bullishColor` / `bearishColor` / `zeroColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showVzo`/`showLevels`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `vzo` / `levels`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_VZO_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/THRESHOLD/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/PRICE_COLOR/VZO_COLOR/BULLISH_COLOR/BEARISH_COLOR/ZERO_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineVzoZone` type (`bullish`|`bearish`|`neutral`|`none`); `getLineVzoFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value + volume, and volume >= 0); `normalizeLineVzoPeriod(period, fallback)` (coerce to a positive integer; non-finite or sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineVzoSignedVolume(closes, volumes, period)` -- the signed volume over the window (non-array -> []); `computeLineVzoTotalVolume(volumes, period)` -- the total volume over the window (non-array -> []); `computeLineVzo(closes, volumes, period)` -- the VZO series (non-array -> []); `runLineVzo(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, period, threshold, signedVolume, totalVolume, vzo, samples, vzoFinal, bullishCount, bearishCount, neutralCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, volume, signedVolume, totalVolume, vzo, zone}` (`zone` is `bullish`/`bearish`/`neutral`/`none`); `computeLineVzoLayout({data, period, threshold, ...})` -- stacks the price panel above the VZO panel (the VZO panel on a FIXED -100 to +100 scale), projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `vzoPath`, `vzoMarkers` (one per defined bar), the `zeroY` + `upperY` + `lowerY` reference-line positions; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLineVzoChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Volume Zone Oscillator + ratio + signed volume + total volume (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-vzo-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the vzo line + every price dot + every vzo marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-vzo / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -panel-label / -levels / -level-line / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -vzo-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-volume / -tooltip-vzo / -tooltip-zone / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-threshold`, `data-vzo-final`, `data-bullish-count`, `data-bearish-count`, `data-neutral-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; grid / axis / tick / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"vzo"`; level lines carry `data-level="upper"|"zero"|"lower"`; vzo markers expose `data-vzo` + `data-zone`. The VZO panel is fixed to a -100 to +100 scale, draws dashed zero and trigger reference lines, and colours each VZO marker by its zone (green bullish, red bearish, blue neutral). Tooltip on a price dot or a vzo marker shows x, value, the volume, the vzo and the zone. Config badge in top-left calls out `VZO <period>`.
