| 11.663 | UI: chart-line-cfo primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.681 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-cfo.tsx` ships `<ChartLineCfo>` -- a pure-SVG TWO-PANEL chart with a **Chande Forecast Oscillator** panel of the percent gap between price and its linear regression forecast (the price panel on top with the regression forecast overlaid, the CFO panel below). **The computation:** Tushar Chande's Forecast Oscillator measures how far the price has strayed from the value a linear regression predicts for it. `computeLineCfoForecast(closes, period)` fits a least-squares line `y = a + b*t` through the trailing `period` closes (with `t = 0..period-1`) and returns its endpoint value `a + b*(period-1)` -- the regression's forecast for the current bar. `computeLineCfo(closes, period)` is the percent gap: `CFO[i] = 100 * (close[i] - forecast[i]) / close[i]`. A positive CFO means the price is running above the forecast, a negative CFO below it; the oscillator crosses zero whenever the price meets its regression line. **Exactness:** the regression is rational, but its slope divides by `period*sumT2 - sumT^2` (20 for `period` 4), which is not a power of two, so the forecast is generally not exactly representable and the non-linear-fixture tests assert with `toBeCloseTo`; one case is exact -- a perfectly linear price, where the regression fits with no residual so the forecast equals the price exactly and the CFO is exactly zero (asserted with `toEqual` on the linear fixture `[10,20,30,40,50,60]`). The non-linear fixture uses closes that are all powers of two so each CFO ratio is dyadic, and the hand-computed values `[.,.,.,0,15,-40,10,5,-50,20,-20,10,-20]` are matched with `toBeCloseTo` (the flat-window bar is an exact 0). `runLineCfo` sorts the finite price points by x, runs the pipeline, and returns the forecast + cfo series, per-bar `samples` classified above/below/neutral/none against the threshold, the zone counts and the final reading; `ok` is false for fewer than two points. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs the deviation oscillators `<ChartLineCog>` / `<ChartLineDpo>` -- those measure the price against a trailing MOVING AVERAGE; the CFO measures it against a forward-fitted REGRESSION line, so it reads the residual of a trend forecast. vs `<ChartLineTheilsen>` / `<ChartLineLsma>` -- those plot the regression line itself; the CFO plots the percent GAP between the price and that line as a zero-centred oscillator. vs `<ChartLineResidual>` -- a residual is the raw price minus a fit; the CFO NORMALISES that residual by the price, so it is a percentage comparable across price levels. All exported helpers carry a `LineCfo`/`Cfo` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineCfoPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `period` (the regression lookback; default 14, normalized to an integer >= 2) / `threshold` (the above/below trigger level; default 3); `pricePanelRatio` (the fraction of the height given to the price panel; default 0.55, clamped to [0.3, 0.8]); `gap` (the pixel gap between the stacked panels); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `priceColor` / `forecastColor` / `cfoColor` / `aboveColor` / `belowColor` / `zeroColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showForecast`/`showCfo`/`showLevels`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `forecast` / `cfo` / `levels`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_CFO_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/THRESHOLD/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/PRICE_COLOR/FORECAST_COLOR/CFO_COLOR/ABOVE_COLOR/BELOW_COLOR/ZERO_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineCfoZone` type (`above`|`below`|`neutral`|`none`); `getLineCfoFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value); `normalizeLineCfoPeriod(period, fallback)` (coerce to an integer >= 2; non-finite or sub-2 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineCfoForecast(closes, period)` -- the linear regression endpoint forecast (non-array -> []); `computeLineCfo(closes, period)` -- the percent-gap series (non-array -> []); `runLineCfo(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, period, threshold, forecast, cfo, samples, cfoFinal, aboveCount, belowCount, neutralCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, forecast, cfo, zone}` (`zone` is `above`/`below`/`neutral`/`none`); `computeLineCfoLayout({data, period, threshold, ...})` -- stacks the price panel (price plus the forecast line) above the CFO panel, projects `pricePath`, `forecastPath`, `priceDots`, `cfoPath`, `cfoMarkers` (one per defined bar), the `zeroY` + `upperY` + `lowerY` reference-line positions (the CFO panel y-domain data-driven, extended to include 0 and +/- threshold); degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLineCfoChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Chande Forecast Oscillator + percent gap + linear regression + forecast (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-cfo-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the forecast line + the cfo line + every price dot + every cfo marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-cfo / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -panel-label / -levels / -level-line / -price-path / -forecast-path / -dots / -dot / -cfo-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-forecast / -tooltip-cfo / -tooltip-zone / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-threshold`, `data-cfo-final`, `data-above-count`, `data-below-count`, `data-neutral-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; grid / axis / tick / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"cfo"`; level lines carry `data-level="upper"|"zero"|"lower"`; cfo markers expose `data-cfo` + `data-zone`. The price panel overlays the linear regression forecast as a dashed amber line; the CFO panel draws the oscillator over dashed zero and trigger reference lines, with each marker coloured by its zone (green above, red below, teal neutral). Tooltip on a price dot or a cfo marker shows x, value, the forecast, the cfo and the zone. Config badge in top-left calls out `CFO <period>`. The legend has four toggle items (Price / Forecast / CFO / Levels). 67 vitest cases (getLineCfoFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + value** + non-array -> [], normalizeLineCfoPeriod incl. floors a fractional + **sub-2 / NaN / negative falls back**, computeLineCfoForecast incl. **forecasts the linear regression endpoint** + **equals the price for a perfectly linear series** + **the null warm-up** + **matches a hand-computed non-linear value** + non-array -> [], computeLineCfo incl. **computes zero for a perfectly linear series** + **the null warm-up** + **matches the hand-computed percent gaps** + **positive when the price is above the forecast** + **the percent-gap identity** + non-array -> [], runLineCfo incl. ok + **the period + threshold** + **the forecast series** + **the cfo series** + one sample per point + **the zone classification** + **the above/below/neutral counts** + **self-consistent counts** + **the final reading** + **the forecast/cfo/zone sample fields** + not-ok single point + empty/null -> ok=false + x-sorting + **the period 14 default**, computeLineCfoLayout incl. ok + **stacks the price panel above the cfo panel** + **a non-empty price path** + **a | 11.664 | UI: chart-line-nvi primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.682 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-nvi.tsx` ships `<ChartLineNvi>` -- a pure-SVG TWO-PANEL chart with a **Negative Volume Index** panel tracking cumulative change on lower-volume bars (the price panel on top, the NVI panel below). **The computation:** Paul Dysart's Negative Volume Index is a cumulative index built on a single rule -- it moves only on the QUIET bars; the premise is that informed money trades on low-volume days, so the NVI isolates what the price does on those days. `computeLineNviIndex(closes, volumes, base)` seeds the index at `base` and, for each later bar: `NVI[i] = NVI[i-1] * close[i] / close[i-1]` if `volume[i] < volume[i-1]` (a lower-volume bar), else `NVI[i] = NVI[i-1]` (a higher- or equal-volume bar holds flat). So on a lower-volume bar the index compounds the price's percent change; on a higher-volume bar it holds flat. An NVI above its starting base means the price has gained ground on the quiet bars. **Exactness:** the index is a chain of ratio multiplications, so it is exact when the closes are powers of two -- every ratio `close[i]/close[i-1]` is then dyadic and the index, seeded at 1000, stays a dyadic multiple of 1000. The fixture closes `[128,256,256,128,128,256,64,128,64,256]` paired with the volumes `[50,30,60,20,70,40,30,80,25,50]` give the exact index `[1000,2000,2000,1000,1000,2000,500,500,250,250]`, asserted with `toEqual`. `runLineNvi` sorts the finite price points by x, runs the pipeline, and returns the nvi series, per-bar `samples` classified up/down/flat by the index movement, the movement counts and the final reading; `ok` is false for fewer than two points. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs `<ChartLinePvi>` -- the Positive Volume Index is the mirror, moving on the LOUD (rising-volume) bars; the NVI moves on the QUIET (falling-volume) bars. vs `<ChartLineObv>` / `<ChartLineVzo>` -- those accumulate volume itself; the NVI IGNORES volume size entirely and only uses it as a GATE to decide which bars compound the price change. vs `<ChartLineCumulative>` -- a cumulative return runs on EVERY bar; the NVI runs on a selected subset and freezes on the rest. All exported helpers carry a `LineNvi`/`Nvi` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineNviPoint[]` (`{x, value, volume}`); `base` (the starting index value; default 1000, normalized to a positive number); `pricePanelRatio` (the fraction of the height given to the price panel; default 0.55, clamped to [0.3, 0.8]); `gap` (the pixel gap between the stacked panels); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `priceColor` / `nviColor` / `upColor` / `downColor` / `baseColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showNvi`/`showBaseLine`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `nvi`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_NVI_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/BASE/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/PRICE_COLOR/NVI_COLOR/UP_COLOR/DOWN_COLOR/BASE_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineNviState` type (`up`|`down`|`flat`); `getLineNviFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value + volume, and volume >= 0); `normalizeLineNviBase(base)` (coerce to a positive number; non-finite or non-positive -> 1000); `computeLineNviIndex(closes, volumes, base)` -- the cumulative index (non-array -> []); `runLineNvi(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, base, nvi, samples, nviFinal, upCount, downCount, flatCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, volume, nvi, state}` (`state` is `up`/`down`/`flat`); `computeLineNviLayout({data, base, ...})` -- stacks the price panel above the NVI panel (the NVI panel y-domain data-driven, extended to include the base), projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `nviPath`, `nviMarkers` (one per bar), the `baseY` reference-line position; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLineNviChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Negative Volume Index + cumulative + lower-volume (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-nvi-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the nvi line + every price dot + every nvi marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-nvi / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -panel-label / -base-line / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -nvi-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-volume / -tooltip-nvi / -tooltip-state / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config); root mirrors `data-base`, `data-nvi-final`, `data-up-count`, `data-down-count`, `data-flat-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; grid / axis / tick / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"nvi"`; nvi markers expose `data-nvi` + `data-state`. The NVI panel draws the index line over a dashed base reference line, with each marker coloured by its movement (green up, red down, indigo flat). Tooltip on a price dot or an nvi marker shows x, value, the volume, the nvi and the movement state. Config badge in top-left calls out `NVI <base>`. The legend has two toggle items (Price / NVI). 59 vitest cases (getLineNviFinitePoints incl. **keeps finite x + value + volume** + **drops a negative volume** + non-array -> [], normalizeLineNviBase incl. **uses a positive base** + **non-positive / non-finite -> 1000**, computeLineNviIndex incl. **starts at the base value** + **computes the cumulative index** + **holds flat on a higher-volume bar** + **applies the price change on a lower-volume bar** + **holds when the volume is unchanged** + non-array -> [], runLineNvi incl. ok + **the base** + **the nvi series** + one sample per point + **the up/down/flat classification** + **the up/down/flat counts** + **self-consistent counts** + **the final reading** + **the volume/nvi/state sample fields** + not-ok single point + empty/null -> ok=false + x-sorting + **the base 1000 default**, computeLineNviLayout incl. ok + **stacks the price panel above the nvi panel** + **a non-empty price path** + **a non-empty nvi path** + **one dot per point** + **one marker per point** + **the base line inside the nvi panel** + the run statistics + collapsed canvas -> ok=false + too little data -> ok=false, describeLineNviChart incl. **the indicator vocabulary** + the state counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / root data-* / svg + price line / **both panel labels** / **the nvi line** / **one nvi marker per point** / **the base line** / **the state attribute on each marker** / **the two-item legend** / config badge base / hide the price path / **hide the nvi line + markers when showNvi is false** / hide the nvi via the hidden set / **hide the base line when showBaseLine is false** / series toggle / price dots / empty state / omit config badge / ref / displayName / animate flag) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. | 11.665 | UI: chart-line-pvi primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.683 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-pvi.tsx` ships `<ChartLinePvi>` -- a pure-SVG TWO-PANEL chart with a **Positive Volume Index** panel tracking cumulative change on higher-volume bars (the price panel on top, the PVI panel below). **The computation:** the Positive Volume Index is the MIRROR of the Negative Volume Index -- a cumulative index that moves only on the LOUD bars; the premise is that the crowd trades on high-volume days, so the PVI isolates what the price does on those days. `computeLinePviIndex(closes, volumes, base)` seeds the index at `base` and, for each later bar: `PVI[i] = PVI[i-1] * close[i] / close[i-1]` if `volume[i] > volume[i-1]` (a higher-volume bar), else `PVI[i] = PVI[i-1]` (a lower- or equal-volume bar holds flat). So on a higher-volume bar the index compounds the price's percent change; on a lower-volume bar it holds flat. A PVI above its starting base means the price has gained ground on the busy days. **Exactness:** the index is a chain of ratio multiplications, so it is exact when the closes are powers of two -- every ratio `close[i]/close[i-1]` is then dyadic and the index, seeded at 1000, stays a dyadic multiple of 1000. The fixture closes `[256,512,512,1024,1024,512,512,256,256,512]` paired with the volumes `[20,50,30,70,40,80,30,60,25,90]` give the exact index `[1000,2000,2000,4000,4000,2000,2000,1000,1000,2000]`, asserted with `toEqual`. `runLinePvi` sorts the finite price points by x, runs the pipeline, and returns the pvi series, per-bar `samples` classified up/down/flat by the index movement, the movement counts and the final reading; `ok` is false for fewer than two points. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs `<ChartLineNvi>` -- the Negative Volume Index is the mirror, moving on the QUIET (falling-volume) bars; the PVI moves on the LOUD (rising-volume) bars. vs `<ChartLineObv>` / `<ChartLineVzo>` -- those accumulate volume itself; the PVI IGNORES volume size entirely and only uses it as a GATE to decide which bars compound the price change. vs `<ChartLineCumulative>` -- a cumulative return runs on EVERY bar; the PVI runs on a selected subset and freezes on the rest. All exported helpers carry a `LinePvi`/`Pvi` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLinePviPoint[]` (`{x, value, volume}`); `base` (the starting index value; default 1000, normalized to a positive number); `pricePanelRatio` (the fraction of the height given to the price panel; default 0.55, clamped to [0.3, 0.8]); `gap` (the pixel gap between the stacked panels); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `priceColor` / `pviColor` / `upColor` / `downColor` / `baseColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showPvi`/`showBaseLine`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `pvi`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_PVI_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/BASE/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/PRICE_COLOR/PVI_COLOR/UP_COLOR/DOWN_COLOR/BASE_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLinePviState` type (`up`|`down`|`flat`); `getLinePviFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value + volume, and volume >= 0); `normalizeLinePviBase(base)` (coerce to a positive number; non-finite or non-positive -> 1000); `computeLinePviIndex(closes, volumes, base)` -- the cumulative index (non-array -> []); `runLinePvi(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, base, pvi, samples, pviFinal, upCount, downCount, flatCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, volume, pvi, state}` (`state` is `up`/`down`/`flat`); `computeLinePviLayout({data, base, ...})` -- stacks the price panel above the PVI panel (the PVI panel y-domain data-driven, extended to include the base), projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `pviPath`, `pviMarkers` (one per bar), the `baseY` reference-line position; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLinePviChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Positive Volume Index + cumulative + higher-volume (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-pvi-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the pvi line + every price dot + every pvi marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-pvi / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -panel-label / -base-line / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -pvi-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-volume / -tooltip-pvi / -tooltip-state / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config); root mirrors `data-base`, `data-pvi-final`, `data-up-count`, `data-down-count`, `data-flat-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; grid / axis / tick / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"pvi"`; pvi markers expose `data-pvi` + `data-state`. The PVI panel draws the index line over a dashed base reference line, with each marker coloured by its movement (green up, red down, fuchsia flat). Tooltip on a price dot or a pvi marker shows x, value, the volume, the pvi and the movement state. Config badge in top-left calls out `PVI <base>`. The legend has two toggle items (Price / PVI). 59 vitest cases (getLinePviFinitePoints incl. **keeps finite x + value + volume** + **drops a negative volume** + non-array -> [], normalizeLinePviBase incl. **uses a positive base** + **non-positive / non-finite -> 1000**, computeLinePviIndex incl. **starts at the base value** + **computes the cumulative index** + **holds flat on a lower-volume bar** + **applies the price change on a higher-volume bar** + **holds when the volume is unchanged** + non-array -> [], runLinePvi incl. ok + **the base** + **the pvi series** + one sample per point + **the up/down/flat classification** + **the up/down/flat counts** + **self-consistent counts** + **the final reading** + **the volume/pvi/state sample fields** + not-ok single point + empty/null -> ok=false + x-sorting + **the base 1000 default**, computeLinePviLayout incl. ok + **stacks the price panel above the pvi panel** + **a non-empty price path** + **a non-empty pvi path** + **one dot per point** + **one marker per point** + **the base line inside the pvi panel** + the run statistics + collapsed canvas -> ok=false + too little data -> ok=false, describeLinePviChart incl. **the indicator vocabulary** + the state counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / root data-* / svg + price line / **both panel labels** / **the pvi line** / **one pvi marker per point** / **the base line** / **the state attribute on each marker** / **the two-item legend** / config badge base / hide the price path / **hide the pvi line + markers when showPvi is false** / hide the pvi via the hidden set / **hide the base line when showBaseLine is false** / series toggle / price dots / empty state / omit config badge / ref / displayName / animate flag) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. | 11.666 | UI: chart-line-trin primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.684 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-trin.tsx` ships `<ChartLineTrin>` -- a pure-SVG TWO-PANEL chart with an **Arms Index (TRIN)** panel from the ratio of advancing issues to advancing volume (the market-index panel on top, the TRIN panel below). **The computation:** Richard Arms' Index (TRIN -- the TRading INdex) is a market-breadth indicator; each bar carries four market-wide counts -- advancing issues, declining issues, advancing volume, declining volume -- and the TRIN is a RATIO OF RATIOS: `TRIN = (advIssues/decIssues) / (advVolume/decVolume)`. `computeLineTrinValue(advIssues, decIssues, advVolume, decVolume)` evaluates it in the cross-multiplied form `(advIssues * decVolume) / (decIssues * advVolume)`; a zero in the denominator (no declining issues or no advancing volume) returns null. A TRIN of 1 means volume is spread in proportion to the issue counts; a TRIN below 1 is bullish (volume concentrating in the advancing stocks), above 1 is bearish. The chart classifies each bar against a neutral band of half-width `threshold` around 1: bullish below `1-threshold`, bearish above `1+threshold`, neutral in between. `computeLineTrin(points)` maps the per-bar value over a series. **Exactness:** the TRIN is a ratio of integer products, so it is exact when the breadth counts are integers and the division lands cleanly; the fixture counts give the exact TRIN series `[1,0.5,2,1,0.5,1.5,0.5,2,1,4]`, asserted with `toEqual`. `runLineTrin` sorts the finite points by x, runs the pipeline, and returns the trin series, per-bar `samples` classified bullish/bearish/neutral/none, the zone counts and the final reading; `ok` is false for fewer than two points. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs the single-series oscillators `<ChartLineRmi>` / `<ChartLineCmo>` / `<ChartLinePzo>` -- those derive their value from ONE price series; the TRIN is a market-breadth indicator fed by FOUR advance/decline counts per bar. vs `<ChartLineVzo>` / `<ChartLineObv>` -- those use a single instrument's volume; the TRIN compares MARKET-WIDE advancing volume against declining volume. vs an advance/decline LINE -- that accumulates a running net breadth total; the TRIN is an instantaneous per-bar ratio with no accumulation. All exported helpers carry a `LineTrin`/`Trin` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineTrinPoint[]` (`{x, value, advIssues, decIssues, advVolume, decVolume}` -- `value` is the market index, the rest are the breadth counts); `threshold` (the neutral-band half-width around 1; default 0.2, accepted in (0,1)); `pricePanelRatio` (the fraction of the height given to the price panel; default 0.55, clamped to [0.3, 0.8]); `gap` (the pixel gap between the stacked panels); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `priceColor` / `trinColor` / `bullishColor` / `bearishColor` / `balanceColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showTrin`/`showLevels`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `trin` / `levels`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_TRIN_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/THRESHOLD/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/PRICE_COLOR/TRIN_COLOR/BULLISH_COLOR/BEARISH_COLOR/BALANCE_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineTrinZone` type (`bullish`|`bearish`|`neutral`|`none`); `getLineTrinFinitePoints` (keeps only points with all six fields finite and the four breadth counts >= 0); `normalizeLineTrinThreshold(threshold, fallback)` (coerce into (0,1); outside or non-finite -> fallback); `computeLineTrinValue(advIssues, decIssues, advVolume, decVolume)` -- the TRIN for one bar (zero denominator / non-finite -> null); `computeLineTrin(points)` -- the TRIN for each point (non-array -> []); `runLineTrin(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, threshold, trin, samples, trinFinal, bullishCount, bearishCount, neutralCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, advIssues, decIssues, advVolume, decVolume, trin, zone}` (`zone` is `bullish`/`bearish`/`neutral`/`none`); `computeLineTrinLayout({data, threshold, ...})` -- stacks the market-index panel above the TRIN panel (the TRIN panel y-domain data-driven, extended to include the band around 1), projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `trinPath`, `trinMarkers` (one per defined bar), the `balanceY` + `bullishY` + `bearishY` reference-line positions; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLineTrinChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Arms Index + TRIN + advancing issues + advancing volume (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-trin-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the trin line + every price dot + every trin marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-trin / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -panel-label / -levels / -level-line / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -trin-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-trin / -tooltip-zone / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config); root mirrors `data-threshold`, `data-trin-final`, `data-bullish-count`, `data-bearish-count`, `data-neutral-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; grid / axis / tick / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"trin"`; level lines carry `data-level="bearish"|"balance"|"bullish"`; trin markers expose `data-trin` + `data-zone`. The TRIN panel draws the index line over dashed balance (1.0) and band reference lines, with each marker coloured by its zone (green bullish, red bearish, violet neutral). Tooltip on a price dot or a trin marker shows x, value, the trin and the zone. Config badge in top-left calls out `TRIN <threshold>`. The legend has three toggle items (Price / TRIN / Levels). 62 vitest cases (getLineTrinFinitePoints incl. **keeps all-finite-field points** + **drops a negative breadth value** + non-array -> [], normalizeLineTrinThreshold incl. **uses a threshold in (0,1)** + **outside or non-finite falls back**, computeLineTrinValue incl. **the ratio of ratios** + **reads 1 for balanced breadth** + **null when declining issues is zero** + **null when advancing volume is zero** + **null for a non-finite input**, computeLineTrin incl. **the per-point series** + non-array -> [], runLineTrin incl. ok + **the threshold** + **the trin series** + one sample per point + **the zone classification** + **the bullish/bearish/neutral counts** + **self-consistent counts** + **the final reading** + **the trin/zone sample fields** + not-ok single point + empty/null -> ok=false + x-sorting + **the 0.2 threshold default**, computeLineTrinLayout incl. ok + **stacks the price panel above the trin panel** + **a non-empty price path** + **a non-empty trin path** + **one dot per point** + **one trin marker per defined sample** + **the bearish/balance/bullish level-line ordering** + **the level lines inside the trin panel** + the run statistics + collapsed canvas -> ok=false + too little data -> ok=false, describeLineTrinChart incl. **the indicator vocabulary** + the zone counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / root data-* / svg + price line / **both panel labels** / **the trin line** / **one trin marker per defined sample** / **the bearish/balance/bullish level lines** / **the zone attribute on each marker** / **the three-item legend** / config badge threshold / hide the price path / **hide the trin line + markers when showTrin is false** / **hide the level lines when showLevels is false** / hide the trin via the hidden set / **hide the level lines via the hidden set** / series toggle / price dots / empty state / omit config badge / ref / displayName / animate flag) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. | 11.667 | UI: chart-line-twiggs primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.685 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-twiggs.tsx` ships `<ChartLineTwiggs>` -- a pure-SVG TWO-PANEL chart with a **Twiggs Money Flow** panel from volume weighted by a smoothed range position (the price panel on top, the TMF panel below). **The computation:** Colin Twiggs' Money Flow measures buying and selling pressure by weighting each bar's volume by where the close sits within the bar's range. `computeLineTwiggsClv(highs, lows, closes)` is the close location value -- the range position in `[-1,1]`: `clv = ((close-trl) - (trh-close)) / (trh-trl)` where the TRUE range extends the bar's high and low to take in the prior close (`trh = max(high, prevClose)`, `trl = min(low, prevClose)`); a zero-width range reads 0, a clv of +1 means the close finished at the top of the range, -1 at the bottom. `computeLineTwiggs(highs, lows, closes, volumes, period)` weights each bar's volume by its clv, then smooths both the weighted volume and the raw volume with a moving average and divides: `TMF[i] = SMA(clv*volume, period)[i] / SMA(volume, period)[i]`. A positive TMF signals accumulation (buying pressure), a negative TMF distribution; a window of zero volume is null. **Exactness:** the TMF is a ratio of moving averages -- no transcendentals -- so it is exact when the inputs make the divisions land cleanly; the fixture closes finish at the high or the low (clv +1 or -1) and every window's volume sums to 256, so the whole pipeline lands on exact dyadic numbers -- the TMF series `[.,.,.,1,0.25,-0.25,-0.75,-0.75,0,0,0.5,0.25]` is asserted with `toEqual`. The true-range extension is checked on its own: a bar whose prior close pokes above the high pulls the clv to -0.5 where it would otherwise read 0. `runLineTwiggs` sorts the finite points by x, runs the pipeline, and returns the clv + tmf series, per-bar `samples` classified bullish/bearish/neutral/none against the threshold band, the zone counts and the final reading; `ok` is false for fewer than two points. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs `<ChartLineNvi>` / `<ChartLinePvi>` -- those use volume only as a GATE to decide which bars to count; the TMF weights every bar's volume by where the close lands in the range. vs `<ChartLineCmf>` -- the Chaikin Money Flow uses the raw high/low and a sum-based window; the Twiggs variant extends the range to the PRIOR CLOSE (the true range) and smooths with a moving average. vs `<ChartLineVzo>` -- the Volume Zone Oscillator signs WHOLE-BAR volume by the price direction; the TMF uses a GRADED close location value, not a binary sign. All exported helpers carry a `LineTwiggs`/`Twiggs` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineTwiggsPoint[]` (`{x, value, high, low, volume}` -- `value` is the close); `period` (the smoothing lookback; default 21) / `threshold` (the bullish/bearish trigger level; default 0.1); `pricePanelRatio` (the fraction of the height given to the price panel; default 0.55, clamped to [0.3, 0.8]); `gap` (the pixel gap between the stacked panels); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `priceColor` / `twiggsColor` / `bullishColor` / `bearishColor` / `zeroColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showTwiggs`/`showLevels`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `twiggs` / `levels`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_TWIGGS_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/THRESHOLD/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/PRICE_COLOR/TWIGGS_COLOR/BULLISH_COLOR/BEARISH_COLOR/ZERO_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineTwiggsZone` type (`bullish`|`bearish`|`neutral`|`none`); `getLineTwiggsFinitePoints` (keeps only points with all five fields finite and volume >= 0); `normalizeLineTwiggsPeriod(period, fallback)` (coerce to a positive integer; non-finite or sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineTwiggsClv(highs, lows, closes)` -- the close location value per bar (non-array -> []); `computeLineTwiggsSma(values, period)` -- the moving average of a nullable series (non-array -> []); `computeLineTwiggs(highs, lows, closes, volumes, period)` -- the TMF series (non-array -> []); `runLineTwiggs(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, period, threshold, clv, tmf, samples, twiggsFinal, bullishCount, bearishCount, neutralCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, high, low, volume, clv, tmf, zone}` (`zone` is `bullish`/`bearish`/`neutral`/`none`); `computeLineTwiggsLayout({data, period, threshold, ...})` -- stacks the price panel above the TMF panel (the TMF panel on a FIXED -1 to +1 scale), projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `tmfPath`, `tmfMarkers` (one per defined bar), the `zeroY` + `upperY` + `lowerY` reference-line positions; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLineTwiggsChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Twiggs Money Flow + volume + range + smooth (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-twiggs-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the tmf line + every price dot + every tmf marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-twiggs / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -panel-label / -levels / -level-line / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -tmf-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-clv / -tooltip-tmf / -tooltip-zone / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-threshold`, `data-twiggs-final`, `data-bullish-count`, `data-bearish-count`, `data-neutral-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; grid / axis / tick / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"twiggs"`; level lines carry `data-level="upper"|"zero"|"lower"`; tmf markers expose `data-tmf` + `data-zone`. The TMF panel is fixed to a -1 to +1 scale, draws dashed zero and trigger reference lines, and colours each marker by its zone (green bullish, red bearish, teal neutral). Tooltip on a price dot or a tmf marker shows x, value, the clv, the tmf and the zone. Config badge in top-left calls out `TMF <period>`. The legend has three toggle items (Price / TMF / Levels). 67 vitest cases (getLineTwiggsFinitePoints incl. **keeps all-finite-field points** + **drops a negative volume** + non-array -> [], **reads zero for a zero-width range** + non-array -> [], computeLineTwiggsSma incl. **the moving average** + **null where the window contains a null** + non-array -> [], computeLineTwiggs incl. **the + empty/null -> ok=false + x-sorting + **the period 21 default**, computeLineTwiggsLayout incl. ok + **stacks the price panel above the twiggs panel** + **a non-empty price path** + **a non-empty tmf marker** / **the three-item legend** / config badge period / hide the price path / **hide the tmf line + markers when showTwiggs is false** / **hide the level lines when showLevels is false** / hide | 11.668 | UI: chart-line-mama primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.686 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-mama.tsx` ships `<ChartLineMama>` -- a pure-SVG SINGLE-PANEL line chart with a **MESA Adaptive Moving Average** overlay adapting its alpha to the measured cycle phase (the price, MAMA and FAMA lines overlaid in one panel). **The computation:** John Ehlers' MESA Adaptive Moving Average (MAMA) is an exponential moving average whose smoothing factor `alpha` is NOT fixed -- it adapts to how fast the price cycle is turning. `computeLineMamaPhase(values, period)` measures the phase: the price is detrended (the `period`-bar moving average subtracted) to expose its cycle, the cycle value and its one-bar change are treated as the in-phase and quadrature components of a phasor, so the instantaneous phase is `phase = atan2(cycle - cycle[-1], cycle)`. `computeLineMama(values, period, slowLimit, fastLimit)` turns the phase rate of change into the adaptive alpha and runs two recursions: `alpha = slowLimit + (fastLimit-slowLimit) * |dPhase|/pi`, `MAMA = alpha*price + (1-alpha)*MAMA[-1]`, `FAMA = 0.5*alpha*MAMA + (1-0.5*alpha)*FAMA[-1]`. A fast-advancing phase pushes alpha toward `fastLimit` so the MAMA hugs the price; a slow phase pulls it toward `slowLimit` so it smooths. The FAMA -- the Following Adaptive Moving Average -- trails the MAMA at HALF its alpha, and a MAMA above its FAMA is read as bullish; both are seeded with the first price. **Exactness:** the phase uses `atan2`, so the MAMA is transcendental and the varying-series tests assert with `toBeCloseTo`; three properties are exact or near-exact -- the MAMA and FAMA are seeded at the first price (`toBe`), a flat price detrends to a zero cycle whose phase is exactly 0 so the MAMA and FAMA stay on the price, and `alpha` is always inside `[slowLimit, fastLimit]`; the two recursions are verified bar by bar against the exposed `alpha` series. `runLineMama` sorts the finite price points by x, runs the pipeline, and returns the mama/fama/alpha series, per-bar `samples` classified bullish/bearish/neutral by the MAMA versus its FAMA, the cross counts and the final readings; `ok` is false for fewer than two points. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs the fixed-weight moving averages `<ChartLineEwma>` / `<ChartLineWma>` / `<ChartLineHma>` -- those use a CONSTANT smoothing weight; the MAMA changes its own alpha bar by bar from the measured cycle. vs `<ChartLineKama>` -- Kaufman's adaptive MA scales alpha by an EFFICIENCY RATIO of price travel; the MAMA scales it by the rate of the cycle PHASE. vs `<ChartLineFrama>` -- the fractal adaptive MA uses the fractal dimension of the price; the MAMA pairs its average with a FAMA follower for a crossover signal. All exported helpers carry a `LineMama`/`Mama` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineMamaPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `period` (the detrend lookback; default 10) / `slowLimit` (the minimum alpha; default 0.05, in (0,1)) / `fastLimit` (the maximum alpha; default 0.5, in (0,1)); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `priceColor` / `mamaColor` / `famaColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showMama`/`showFama`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `mama` / `fama`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_MAMA_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/SLOW_LIMIT/FAST_LIMIT/PRICE_COLOR/MAMA_COLOR/FAMA_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineMamaCross` type (`bullish`|`bearish`|`neutral`); `getLineMamaFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value); `normalizeLineMamaPeriod(period, fallback)` (coerce to a positive integer; non-finite or sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `normalizeLineMamaLimit(limit, fallback)` (coerce into (0,1); outside or non-finite -> fallback); `computeLineMamaPhase(values, period)` -- the cycle phase per bar (non-array -> []); `computeLineMama(values, period, slowLimit, fastLimit)` -- returns `{mama, fama, alpha}` (non-array -> empty series); `runLineMama(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, period, slowLimit, fastLimit, mama, fama, alpha, samples, mamaFinal, famaFinal, bullishCount, bearishCount, neutralCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, mama, fama, alpha, cross}` (`cross` is `bullish`/`bearish`/`neutral`); `computeLineMamaLayout({data, period, slowLimit, fastLimit, ...})` -- projects the price, MAMA and FAMA as three overlaid lines in a single full-width panel sharing one y-domain, returns `pricePath`, `mamaPath`, `famaPath`, `priceDots`, `mamaMarkers` (one per bar); degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLineMamaChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions MESA Adaptive Moving Average + alpha + phase + adapt (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-mama-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the MAMA line + the FAMA line + every price dot + every MAMA marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-mama / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -mama-line / -fama-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-mama / -tooltip-fama / -tooltip-cross / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-mama-final`, `data-fama-final`, `data-bullish-count`, `data-bearish-count`, `data-neutral-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; axis nodes carry `data-axis="x"|"y"`; MAMA markers expose `data-mama` + `data-cross`. The price, MAMA and FAMA are drawn as three overlaid lines (the FAMA dashed); the MAMA carries one circle marker per bar. Tooltip on a price dot or a MAMA marker shows x, value, the MAMA, the FAMA and the cross. Config badge in top-left calls out `MAMA <period>`. The legend has three toggle items (Price / MAMA / FAMA). 65 vitest cases (getLineMamaFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + value** + non-array -> [], normalizeLineMamaPeriod incl. floors a fractional + **sub-1 / NaN / negative falls back**, normalizeLineMamaLimit incl. **uses a limit in (0,1)** + **outside or non-finite falls back**, computeLineMamaPhase incl. **null through the detrend warm-up** + **reads zero for a flat series** + **a finite phase for a varying series** + non-array -> [], computeLineMama incl. **seeds the mama and fama at the first value** + **keeps the mama and fama flat for a flat price** + **follows the mama recursion** + **follows the fama recursion** + **keeps alpha within the slow and fast limits** + **finite for every bar** + non-array -> empty series, runLineMama incl. ok + **the period + alpha limits** + **the mama/fama/alpha series** + one sample per point + **the neutral first bar** + **the bullish/bearish/neutral classification** + **self-consistent counts** + **the final readings** + **the mama/fama/alpha/cross sample fields** + not-ok single point + empty/null -> ok=false + x-sorting + **the period 10 default**, computeLineMamaLayout incl. ok + **a single full-width panel** + **a non-empty price path** + **the mama + fama paths** + **one dot per point** + **one marker per point** + the run statistics + collapsed canvas -> ok=false + too little data -> ok=false, describeLineMamaChart incl. **the indicator vocabulary** + the cross counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible legend** / config badge period / hide the price path / **hide the mama line + markers when showMama is false** / **hide the fama line when showFama is false** / hide the mama via the hidden set / **hide the fama via the hidden set** / series toggle / price dots / empty state / omit config badge / ref / displayName / animate flag) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. | 11.669 | UI: chart-line-vix-fix primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.687 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-vix-fix.tsx` ships `<ChartLineVixFix>` -- a pure-SVG TWO-PANEL chart with a **Williams VIX Fix** panel estimating implied volatility from the recent highest close (the price panel on top, the VIX Fix panel below). **The computation:** Larry Williams' VIX Fix is a synthetic volatility gauge that approximates what a fear index would show, computed from PRICE ALONE -- no options data. `computeLineVixFixHighest(closes, period)` is the highest close over the trailing `period` bars. `computeLineVixFix(closes, period)` is the VIX Fix itself: `WVF[i] = 100 * (highestClose - close) / highestClose` -- how far the close has fallen below its recent peak, as a percentage; when the price makes a new high the highest close equals the close and the WVF is zero, when the price drops sharply below its recent peak the WVF spikes up; a non-positive highest close is null. The chart classifies each bar against a `threshold`: a WVF above the threshold is a `spike` (volatility flare-up), at or below is `calm`. **Exactness:** the VIX Fix is a ratio of integers -- no transcendentals -- so it is exact when the closes are integers and the highest close divides cleanly; the fixture places a close of 100 inside every window so the highest close is always 100 and `WVF = 100 - close` lands on exact integers -- the series `[.,.,.,30,0,15,25,0,40,0]` is asserted with `toEqual`. `runLineVixFix` sorts the finite price points by x, runs the pipeline, and returns the highest + vixFix series, per-bar `samples` classified spike/calm/none, the zone counts and the final reading; `ok` is false for fewer than two points. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs the volatility-band indicators `<ChartLineBollinger>` / `<ChartLineKeltner>` / `<ChartLineBandwidth>` -- those wrap a band around the price; the VIX Fix is a ONE-SIDED drawdown gauge, never negative. vs `<ChartLineStddev>` -- a rolling standard deviation measures dispersion BOTH ways; the VIX Fix measures only the drop from the recent peak, so it spikes on fear and rests at zero in a rising market. vs `<ChartLineAtr>` -- the average true range needs the bar's high and low; the VIX Fix needs only the CLOSE. All exported helpers carry a `LineVixFix`/`VixFix` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineVixFixPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `period` (the highest-close lookback; default 22) / `threshold` (the spike trigger level; default 10, accepted as a positive number); `pricePanelRatio` (the fraction of the height given to the price panel; default 0.55, clamped to [0.3, 0.8]); `gap` (the pixel gap between the stacked panels); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `priceColor` / `vixFixColor` / `spikeColor` / `calmColor` / `thresholdColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showVixFix`/`showThreshold`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `vixfix` / `threshold`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_VIX_FIX_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/THRESHOLD/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/PRICE_COLOR/VIX_COLOR/SPIKE_COLOR/CALM_COLOR/THRESHOLD_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineVixFixZone` type (`spike`|`calm`|`none`); `getLineVixFixFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value); `normalizeLineVixFixPeriod(period, fallback)` (coerce to a positive integer; non-finite or sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineVixFixHighest(closes, period)` -- the rolling highest close (non-array -> []); `computeLineVixFix(closes, period)` -- the VIX Fix series (non-array -> []); `runLineVixFix(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, period, threshold, highest, vixFix, samples, vixFixFinal, spikeCount, calmCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, highest, vixFix, zone}` (`zone` is `spike`/`calm`/`none`); `computeLineVixFixLayout({data, period, threshold, ...})` -- stacks the price panel above the VIX Fix panel (the VIX Fix panel y-domain non-negative, from 0, extended to include the threshold), projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `vixPath`, `vixMarkers` (one per defined bar), the `thresholdY` reference-line position; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLineVixFixChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Williams VIX Fix + implied volatility + highest close (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-vix-fix-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the vix fix line + every price dot + every vix marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-vix-fix / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -panel-label / -threshold-line / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-vix / -tooltip-zone / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-threshold`, `data-vix-fix-final`, `data-spike-count`, `data-calm-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; grid / axis / tick / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"vix"`; vix markers expose `data-vix` + `data-zone`. The VIX Fix panel is a non-negative scale, draws a dashed threshold reference line, and colours each marker by its zone (red spike, cyan calm). Tooltip on a price dot or a vix marker shows x, value, the vix fix and the zone. Config badge in top-left calls out `WVF <period>`. The legend has three toggle items (Price / VIX Fix / Threshold). 62 vitest cases (getLineVixFixFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + value** + non-array -> [], normalizeLineVixFixPeriod incl. floors a fractional + **sub-1 / NaN / negative falls back**, computeLineVixFixHighest incl. **the rolling highest close** + **the null warm-up** + non-array -> [], computeLineVixFix incl. **the percent drop from the highest close** + **the null warm-up** + **reads zero when the price makes a new high** + **non-negative** + non-array -> [], runLineVixFix incl. ok + **the period + threshold** + **the highest series** + **the vix fix series** + one sample per point + **the spike/calm/none classification** + **the spike + calm counts** + **self-consistent counts** + **the final reading** + **the highest/vixFix/zone sample fields** + not-ok single point + empty/null -> ok=false + x-sorting + **the period 22 default**, computeLineVixFixLayout incl. ok + **stacks the price panel above the vix panel** + **a non-empty price path** + **a non-empty vix path** + **one dot per point** + **one vix marker per defined sample** + **the threshold line inside the vix panel** + the run statistics + collapsed canvas -> ok=false + too little data -> ok=false, describeLineVixFixChart incl. **the indicator vocabulary** + the zone counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / root data-* / svg + price line / **both panel labels** / **the vix fix line** / **one vix marker per defined sample** / **the threshold line** / **the zone attribute on each marker** / **the three-item legend** / config badge period / hide the price path / **hide the vix line + markers when showVixFix is false** / **hide the threshold line when showThreshold is false** / hide the vix fix via the hidden set / **hide the threshold line via the hidden set** / series toggle / price dots / empty state / omit config badge / ref / displayName / animate flag) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for the new files (conditional spreads honour `exactOptionalPropertyTypes`). Exported via components/ui barrel. Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/c4-mgr-auto. | 11.670 | UI: chart-line-ulcer primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.688 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-ulcer.tsx` ships `<ChartLineUlcer>` -- a pure-SVG TWO-PANEL chart with an **Ulcer Index** panel from the root mean square of drawdowns off the running peak (the price panel on top, the Ulcer panel below). **The computation:** Peter Martin's Ulcer Index measures downside risk -- not just the depth of a fall but how long it drags on. `computeLineUlcerDrawdown(closes)` tracks the RUNNING PEAK (the highest close from the start up to each bar) and the percentage drawdown of the close below it: `drawdown[i] = 100 * (runningPeak - close) / runningPeak` -- zero at a new running high, growing as the close falls away. `computeLineUlcer(closes, period)` is the Ulcer Index -- the ROOT MEAN SQUARE of those drawdowns over the trailing `period` bars: `UI[i] = sqrt( mean of drawdown[k]^2 over the window )`. A single deep drawdown raises the index, but so does a shallow drawdown that persists, because every bar in the window contributes its square. The chart classifies each bar against a `threshold`: an Ulcer Index above the threshold is `stress`, at or below is `calm`. **Exactness:** the Ulcer Index uses `sqrt`, but a window whose mean squared drawdown is a perfect square is exact; the fixture holds the running peak at 100 (so each drawdown is `100 - close`) and is arranged so every window's mean squared drawdown is a perfect square (0, 9, 25, 16) -- the Ulcer series `[.,.,.,0,3,5,5,5,4,0,3,5,5]` is asserted with `toEqual`. `runLineUlcer` sorts the finite price points by x, runs the pipeline, and returns the drawdown + ulcer series, per-bar `samples` classified stress/calm/none, the zone counts and the final reading; `ok` is false for fewer than two points. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs `<ChartLineStddev>` -- a rolling standard deviation measures dispersion BOTH up and down; the Ulcer Index counts only DOWNSIDE, the drawdown from the running peak. vs `<ChartLineVixFix>` -- the VIX Fix is a SINGLE bar's drop from its recent peak; the Ulcer Index is the root mean square over a WINDOW, so a long shallow decline scores higher than one sharp dip. vs a maximum-drawdown reading -- that reports only the worst point; the Ulcer Index INTEGRATES the whole drawdown history in the window. All exported helpers carry a `LineUlcer`/`Ulcer` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineUlcerPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `period` (the RMS lookback; default 14) / `threshold` (the stress trigger level; default 5, accepted as a positive number); `pricePanelRatio` (the fraction of the height given to the price panel; default 0.55, clamped to [0.3, 0.8]); `gap` (the pixel gap between the stacked panels); `strokeWidth` / `dotRadius` / `priceColor` / `ulcerColor` / `stressColor` / `calmColor` / `thresholdColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showUlcer`/`showThreshold`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `ulcer` / `threshold`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_ULCER_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/THRESHOLD/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/PRICE_COLOR/ULCER_COLOR/STRESS_COLOR/CALM_COLOR/THRESHOLD_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineUlcerZone` type (`stress`|`calm`|`none`); `getLineUlcerFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value); `normalizeLineUlcerPeriod(period, fallback)` (coerce to a positive integer; non-finite or sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineUlcerDrawdown(closes)` -- the percentage drawdown off the running peak (non-array -> []); `computeLineUlcer(closes, period)` -- the Ulcer Index series (non-array -> []); `runLineUlcer(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, period, threshold, drawdown, ulcer, samples, ulcerFinal, stressCount, calmCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, drawdown, ulcer, zone}` (`zone` is `stress`/`calm`/`none`); `computeLineUlcerLayout({data, period, threshold, ...})` -- stacks the price panel above the Ulcer panel (the Ulcer panel y-domain non-negative, from 0, extended to include the threshold), projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `ulcerPath`, `ulcerMarkers` (one per defined bar), the `thresholdY` reference-line position; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLineUlcerChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Ulcer Index + root mean square + drawdown + running peak (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-ulcer-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the ulcer line + every price dot + every ulcer marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-ulcer / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -panel-label / -threshold-line / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -ulcer-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-drawdown / -tooltip-ulcer / -tooltip-zone / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-threshold`, `data-ulcer-final`, `data-stress-count`, `data-calm-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; grid / axis / tick / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"ulcer"`; ulcer markers expose `data-ulcer` + `data-zone`. The Ulcer panel is a non-negative scale, draws a dashed threshold reference line, and colours each marker by its zone (red stress, green calm). Tooltip on a price dot or an ulcer marker shows x, value, the drawdown, the ulcer and the zone. Config badge in top-left calls out `UI <period>`. The legend has three toggle items (Price / Ulcer / Threshold). 63 vitest cases (getLineUlcerFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + value** + non-array -> [], normalizeLineUlcerPeriod incl. floors | 11.671 | UI: chart-line-pfe primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.689 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-pfe.tsx` ships `<ChartLinePfe>` -- a pure-SVG TWO-PANEL chart with a **Polarized Fractal Efficiency** panel scoring how directly the price travels its path (the price panel on top, the PFE panel below on a FIXED -100..+100 y-domain). **The computation:** the Polarized Fractal Efficiency (Hans Hannula) judges how efficiently a price moves -- how directly it gets from where it was to where it is. `computeLinePfeRaw(closes, period)` forms, for each bar, the STRAIGHT-LINE distance from the start of the trailing window to its end, `sqrt(priceChange^2 + period^2)`, treating each bar as one unit of horizontal travel; it divides that by the PATH LENGTH, the sum over the window of `sqrt(barChange^2 + 1)` -- the total length of the zig-zag the price actually walked. The ratio is at most 1 (the straight line is the shortest path), POLARIZED by the window's trend direction (+ for an up-window, - for a down-window) and scaled to -100..+100: `raw = sign * 100 * straight / path`. A reading near +100 is an efficient UP-trend, near -100 an efficient DOWN-trend, near zero a CHOPPY, inefficient market. `computeLinePfe(closes, period, smooth)` exponentially smooths the raw efficiency over the smoothing period (a smooth of 1 leaves the raw value untouched; the EMA seeds with the first defined raw value). Both are null through the warm-up (the first `period` bars) and for any window touching a non-finite close. **Worked fixture:** the PFE divides by an IRRATIONAL sum of segment lengths, so it is transcendental in general -- anchored three ways. (1) A FLAT price is exact: the straight line equals the path, the efficiency is exactly 1, the polarized reading is exactly `+100` (`sqrt` of a perfect square `period^2` is exact, each flat segment `sqrt(0+1)` is exactly 1) -- asserted with `toEqual`. (2) A constant-slope RAMP keeps the irrational `sqrt(slope^2 + 1)` factor in BOTH the straight line and the path where it cancels, so the efficiency is 1 to floating-point precision -- `toBeCloseTo`. (3) A THERE-AND-BACK move (up 3 then back down 3 to the start) has a straight distance of just the horizontal span 2 and a path `2*sqrt(10)`, giving the exact closed form `100 / sqrt(10)` for the period-2 case -- `toBeCloseTo`. The EMA smoothing is checked against its recurrence `pfe[i] = alpha*raw[i] + (1-alpha)*pfe[i-1]` (`alpha = 2/(smooth+1)`); zone classification is checked against the threshold. `runLinePfe` sorts the finite price points by x, runs the pipeline, and returns the `raw` + `pfe` series, per-bar `samples` classified `up`/`down`/`choppy` against the threshold, the up/down/choppy counts, and the final reading; `ok` is false for fewer than two points. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs `<ChartLineVhf>` (Vertical Horizontal Filter) -- both score path geometry, but the VHF is a fixed [0,1] ratio of net range over total travel and is UNSIGNED, while the PFE divides a Euclidean straight-line distance (with the time axis built in) by a Euclidean path length and is POLARIZED to -100..+100 by the trend direction. vs `<ChartLineChoppiness>` -- the Choppiness Index uses a log-scaled ATR-sum ratio; the PFE is a direct Euclidean efficiency. vs `<ChartLineHurst>` -- the Hurst exponent is a statistical scaling law from rescaled-range analysis; the PFE is a single-window geometric ratio with a direction sign. All exported helpers carry a `LinePfe`/`Pfe` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLinePfePoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `period` (the fractal lookback; default 10, coerced to an integer >= 2); `smooth` (the EMA smoothing period; default 5, coerced to an integer >= 1); `threshold` (the up/down zone level; default 50, accepted when finite and in (0,100)); `pricePanelRatio` (the fraction of the height given to the price panel; default 0.55, clamped to [0.3, 0.8]); `gap` (the pixel gap between the stacked panels); `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`priceColor`/`pfeColor`/`upColor`/`downColor`/`zeroColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showPfe`/`showLevels`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `pfe` / `levels`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_PFE_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/SMOOTH/THRESHOLD/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/PRICE_COLOR/PFE_COLOR/UP_COLOR/DOWN_COLOR/ZERO_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLinePfeZone` type (`up`|`down`|`choppy`|`none`); `getLinePfeFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value); `normalizeLinePfePeriod(period, fallback)` (coerce to an integer >= 2; non-finite or sub-2 falls back, fractional floors); `normalizeLinePfeSmooth(smooth, fallback)` (coerce to a positive integer, 1 = no smoothing); `computeLinePfeRaw(closes, period)` -- the raw polarized fractal efficiency, clamped to [-100,100], null through the warm-up (non-array -> []); `computeLinePfe(closes, period, smooth)` -- the EMA-smoothed PFE (non-array -> []); `runLinePfe(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, period, smooth, threshold, raw, pfe, samples, pfeFinal, upCount, downCount, choppyCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, raw, pfe, zone}` (`zone` is `up`/`down`/`choppy`/`none`); `computeLinePfeLayout({data, period, ...})` -- stacks the price panel above the PFE panel (the PFE panel y-domain FIXED to [-100,100]), projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `pfePath`, `pfeMarkers`, and `zeroY`/`upperY`/`lowerY` for the reference lines; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLinePfeChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Polarized Fractal Efficiency + straight-line + path + efficient + the zone counts (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-pfe-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the PFE line + every price dot + every PFE marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-pfe / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -panel-label / -levels / -level-line / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -pfe-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-pfe / -tooltip-zone / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-smooth`, `data-threshold`, `data-pfe-final`, `data-up-count`, `data-down-count`, `data-choppy-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; grid / axis / tick / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"pfe"`; level lines carry `data-level="upper"|"zero"|"lower"`; markers expose `data-pfe` and `data-zone`. The PFE panel carries dashed upper / zero / lower reference lines and zone-coloured markers (green up, red down, cyan choppy). Tooltip on a price dot or a marker shows x, value, the pfe and the zone. Config badge in top-left calls out `PFE <period>/<smooth>`. The legend has three toggle items (Price / PFE / Levels). 76 vitest cases (normalizeLinePfePeriod incl. floors a fractional + **sub-2 / NaN / zero falls back** + the minimum of 2, normalizeLinePfeSmooth incl. **1 = no smoothing** + floors a fractional + zero/negative/NaN falls back, getLinePfeFinitePoints incl. keeps only finite x + value + non-array -> [] + preserves order, computeLinePfeRaw incl. non-array/empty -> [] + matches length + **all-null warm-up** + **the exact +100 flat anchor** + **near +100 up-ramp** + **near -100 down-ramp** + **the 100/sqrt(10) there-and-back** + **bounded -100..+100** + sign up/down + **null for windows touching a non-finite close**, computeLinePfe incl. non-array -> [] + matches length + null warm-up + **equals the raw at smooth 1** + **the exact +100 flat anchor** + **the EMA seed** + **the EMA recurrence** + **smooths a sharp swing** + bounded, runLinePfe incl. not-ok short series + ok + **carries period/smooth/threshold** + **the default options** + raw/pfe series length + one sample per point + **the exact flat +100 zone anchor** + **zone consistency with the pfe** + **every zone present** + self-consistent counts + **the final reading** + x-sorting, computeLinePfeLayout incl. not-ok single point + ok + **stacks the price panel above the pfe panel** + both paths + **one marker per defined bar** + **the upper/zero/lower level ordering** + the total point count + carries the final pfe, describeLinePfeChart incl. **the indicator vocabulary** + straight-line/path/efficient + the zone counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / **empty state** / not-empty root / **root data-period/smooth/threshold** / img-role svg / **the pfe line** / **one marker per defined bar** / **the three level lines** / **both panel labels** / config badge `4/1` / three legend items / **legend toggle hides a series** / **controlled hiddenSeries** / ref / displayName) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. | 11.672 | UI: chart-line-chandelier primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.690 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-chandelier.tsx` ships `<ChartLineChandelier>` -- a pure-SVG SINGLE-PANEL overlay chart with a **Chandelier Exit** trailing a stop a multiple of the average true range below the highest high (the price line carries two dashed volatility-based trailing stops on the same panel). **The computation:** the Chandelier Exit (Chuck LeBeau) hangs a trailing stop from the recent price extreme like a chandelier hangs from a ceiling. On a close-only `{x,value}` series the per-bar high/low collapse onto the close, matching the existing `chart-line-atr` convention. `computeLineChandelierTrueRanges(closes)` is the bar-to-bar true range, the absolute close-to-close change `|close[i]-close[i-1]|` (index 0 null). `computeLineChandelierAtr(closes, period)` is Welles Wilder's Average True Range -- the first value at index `period` is the simple mean of the first `period` true ranges, then Wilder smoothing `(prev*(period-1)+tr)/period`. `computeLineChandelierRollingMax`/`...RollingMin` track the highest and lowest close of the trailing `period` bars (inclusive). `computeLineChandelierExit(closes, period, multiplier)` assembles the exit pair `{long, short}`: the LONG exit is `highestClose - ATR*multiplier` (a trailing stop below price; a long trade is stopped when the close falls beneath it), the SHORT exit is `lowestClose + ATR*multiplier` (the mirror stop above price). This is the BASIC non-ratcheting form -- each bar is recomputed from the current rolling extreme and ATR -- which keeps it distinct from the position-state flip of `<ChartLineSupertrend>`. Both exits are null until the ATR window is full (i >= period). **Worked fixture:** the Chandelier Exit chains a recursive Wilder ATR with rolling extremes, so it is not exact in general -- the fixture sidesteps the recursion. Every close steps by EXACTLY +/-10, so every close-to-close true range is a constant 10 and Wilder's ATR (a weighted blend of a constant) settles at exactly 10; with an integer ATR and integer extremes the long and short exits are exact integers, asserted with `toEqual`. The fixture price ramps up to 50, crashes to 0, then ramps to 60, so the rolling max/min move non-trivially and all three zones appear: closes `[0,10,20,30,40,50,40,30,20,10,0,10,20,30,40,50,60]` (period 5, multiplier 3) -> atr `[null x5, 10 x12]`, longExit `[null x5,20,20,20,20,20,10,0,-10,0,10,20,30]`, shortExit `[null x5,40,50,60,50,40,30,30,30,30,30,40,50]`, zones `[none x5,above,inside,inside,inside,below,below,inside,inside,inside,above,above,above]` (4 above, 2 below, 6 inside). A separate dyadic fixture `[0,2,2,4,4]` checks the Wilder recurrence after the seed (`atr [null,null,1,1.5,0.75]`). `runLineChandelier` sorts the finite price points by x, runs the pipeline, and returns the trueRange + atr + highestClose + lowestClose + longExit + shortExit series, per-bar `samples` classified `above`/`below`/`inside` the exit channel (a bar is `below` when the close is under the long stop, `above` when over the short stop, `inside` between), the above/below/inside counts, and the final long/short readings; `ok` is false for fewer than two points. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs `<ChartLineSupertrend>` -- both are ATR-banded trailing stops, but SuperTrend bands HL2 and FLIPS between a single up/down line via a position state machine, while the Chandelier Exit hangs from the rolling highest/lowest CLOSE and shows both stops at once with no flip. vs `<ChartLineKeltner>` -- the Keltner Channel centres ATR bands on a moving average; the Chandelier anchors them to the rolling price EXTREME, not an average. vs `<ChartLineAtr>` -- that primitive plots the ATR itself in a sub-panel; the Chandelier consumes the ATR to position price-level stops as an overlay. vs `<ChartLineParabolicSar>` -- both are trailing stops, but the SAR accelerates a parabola toward price while the Chandelier holds a fixed ATR-multiple offset from the extreme. All exported helpers carry a `LineChandelier`/`Chandelier` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineChandelierPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `period` (the lookback for the ATR and the rolling extremes; default 22, coerced to an integer >= 2); `multiplier` (the ATR multiple for the stop offset; default 3, accepted when finite and positive, fractional allowed); `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`priceColor`/`longColor`/`shortColor`/`insideColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showLongExit`/`showShortExit`/`showMarkers`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `long` / `short`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_CHANDELIER_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/MULTIPLIER/PRICE_COLOR/LONG_COLOR/SHORT_COLOR/INSIDE_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineChandelierZone` type (`above`|`below`|`inside`|`none`); `getLineChandelierFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value); `normalizeLineChandelierPeriod(period, fallback)` (coerce to an integer >= 2; non-finite or sub-2 falls back, fractional floors); `normalizeLineChandelierMultiplier(mult, fallback)` (finite positive, else fallback, no floor); `computeLineChandelierTrueRanges(closes)` (the absolute close-to-close change, index 0 null; non-array -> []); `computeLineChandelierAtr(closes, period)` (Wilder ATR, null through the warm-up; non-array -> []); `computeLineChandelierRollingMax`/`computeLineChandelierRollingMin(closes, period)` (the rolling highest/lowest close; non-array -> []); `computeLineChandelierExit(closes, period, multiplier)` (the `{long, short}` exit pair; non-array -> {long:[],short:[]}); `runLineChandelier(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, period, multiplier, trueRange, atr, highestClose, lowestClose, longExit, shortExit, samples, longExitFinal, shortExitFinal, aboveCount, belowCount, insideCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, atr, longExit, shortExit, zone}`; `computeLineChandelierLayout({data, period, multiplier, ...})` -- overlays the price line and the two dashed exit lines on a single panel, projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `longExitPath`, `shortExitPath`, one `markers` entry per classified bar coloured by zone, and the shared y-domain spanning price + both exits; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLineChandelierChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Chandelier Exit + average true range + trailing + highest close + the zone counts (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-chandelier-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the long exit line + the short exit line + every price dot + every marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-chandelier / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -price-path / -long-exit-path / -short-exit-path / -dots / -dot / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-long / -tooltip-short / -tooltip-zone / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-multiplier`, `data-long-exit-final`, `data-short-exit-final`, `data-above-count`, `data-below-count`, `data-inside-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; markers expose `data-zone`. The two exit lines are dashed; markers on the price are zone-coloured (green above the channel, red below, cyan inside). Tooltip on a price dot or a marker shows x, value, the long exit, the short exit and the zone. Config badge in top-left calls out `CE <period>x<multiplier>`. The legend has three toggle items (Price / Long Exit / Short Exit). 77 vitest cases (normalizeLineChandelierPeriod incl. floors a fractional + **sub-2 / NaN / zero falls back** + the minimum of 2, normalizeLineChandelierMultiplier incl. **keeps a fractional unchanged** + zero/negative/NaN/Infinity falls back, getLineChandelierFinitePoints incl. keeps only finite x + value + non-array -> [] + preserves order, computeLineChandelierTrueRanges incl. non-array -> [] + **index 0 null** + **the absolute close-to-close change** + matches length + **the constant 10 for the equal-step fixture**, computeLineChandelierAtr incl. non-array -> [] + all-null when shorter than the window + **seeds with the simple mean** + **Wilder smoothing after the seed** + **settles at the constant true range** + matches length, computeLineChandelierRollingMax/Min incl. non-array -> [] + null warm-up + **the highest/lowest close of the window** + **the fixture extremes**, computeLineChandelierExit incl. non-array -> {long:[],short:[]} + **the long exit below the highest close** + **the short exit above the lowest close** + **null until the ATR window is full** + **widens the channel with a larger multiplier** + matches length, runLineChandelier incl. not-ok short series + ok + **carries period/multiplier** + **the default options** + **the exact trueRange/atr series** + **the exact highest/lowest close series** + **the exact long/short exit series** + **zone classification against the channel** + one sample per point + **self-consistent above/below/inside counts** + **the final readings** + x-sorting, computeLineChandelierLayout incl. not-ok single point + **collapsed canvas -> ok=false** + ok + **price/long/short paths** + **the y-domain spanning price and both exits** + **one marker per classified bar** + the total point count + carries the final readings, describeLineChandelierChart incl. **the indicator vocabulary** + average true range/trailing/highest close + the zone counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / **empty state** / not-empty root / **root data-period/multiplier** / img-role svg / **the long + short exit
