| 11.673 | UI: chart-line-special-k primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.691 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-special-k.tsx` ships `<ChartLineSpecialK>` -- a pure-SVG TWO-PANEL chart with a **Pring Special K** panel summing weighted smoothed rate of change across many lookbacks (the price panel on top, the Special K panel below on a dynamic zero-centred y-domain). **The computation:** Martin Pring's Special K is the long-form sibling of the KST (Know Sure Thing) -- where the KST blends four momentum components, the Special K blends TWELVE, a far wider span of lookbacks, into one line capturing short, intermediate and long-term momentum at once. `computeLineSpecialKRoc(closes, period)` is the percentage rate of change `100*(close[i]-close[i-period])/close[i-period]`; bars before the lookback is reachable, and any bar whose base close is zero, are null. `computeLineSpecialKComponent(closes, rocPeriod, smaPeriod)` is one Special K term -- the simple moving average over `smaPeriod` of the rate of change over `rocPeriod` (the weight is applied later). `computeLineSpecialK(closes, components)` is the Special K itself: the WEIGHTED SUM of every smoothed component, defined at a bar only once EVERY component has cleared its warm-up. The default `components` table is Pring's canonical twelve daily terms -- roc/sma/weight `(10,10,1) (15,10,2) (20,10,3) (30,15,4) (50,50,1) (65,65,2) (75,75,3) (100,100,4) (195,130,1) (265,130,2) (390,130,3) (530,195,4)` -- spanning lookbacks from 10 to 530 bars. `computeLineSpecialKSignal(closes, components, signalPeriod)` is the moving average of the Special K, used as a signal line (default 100). **Worked fixture:** the Special K chains a percentage rate of change (a DIVISION) with two layers of moving average -- the division is the only non-exact step, so the fixture pins every rate-of-change base close to exactly 100. Ten leading 100s mean that for the small custom test component table `[(roc4,sma2,w1),(roc5,sma2,w2),(roc6,sma3,w3)]` every lookback reaches back into the flat region, making `ROC = 100*(close-100)/100 = close-100`, an exact integer; the price deltas are multiples of 6 so every moving-average window divides cleanly. closes `[100 x10,112,124,106,88]` -> specialK `[null x8,0,0,30,90,87,9]` (asserted with `toEqual`), signal (period 2, halving to an exact dyadic) `[null x9,0,15,60,88.5,48]`, zones `[none x9,flat,bull,bull,bear,bear]` (2 bull, 2 bear, 1 flat). The component-table validation and zone classification are checked separately. `runLineSpecialK` sorts the finite price points by x, runs the pipeline, and returns the specialK + signal series, per-bar `samples` classified `bull` (Special K above its signal) / `bear` (below) / `flat` (equal) -- `none` until both are defined -- the bull/bear/flat counts, and the final readings; `ok` is false for fewer than two points. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs `<ChartLineKst>` -- both sum weighted smoothed ROCs, but the KST uses only FOUR components (short-term momentum) while the Special K uses TWELVE spanning short, intermediate AND long cycles, so it is a single-line long-horizon momentum gauge rather than a fast oscillator. vs `<ChartLineCoppock>` -- the Coppock Curve is a weighted-MA of just two long ROCs aimed at major bottoms; the Special K is a much broader twelve-term blend. vs `<ChartLinePmo>` / `<ChartLineTsi>` -- those double-smooth a single momentum stream; the Special K sums many independent ROC/SMA pairs at different scales. All exported helpers carry a `LineSpecialK`/`SpecialK` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineSpecialKPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `components` -- a `ChartLineSpecialKComponent[]` (`{roc, sma, weight}`, default the twelve-term Pring table, invalid entries dropped, fractional lookbacks floored, an empty/all-invalid table falls back); `signalPeriod` (the signal SMA length; default 100, coerced to an integer >= 1); `pricePanelRatio` (default 0.55, clamped to [0.3, 0.8]); `gap`; `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`priceColor`/`specialKColor`/`signalColor`/`bullColor`/`bearColor`/`zeroColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showSignal`/`showZeroLine`/`showMarkers`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `special-k` / `signal`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_SPECIAL_K_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/SIGNAL/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/PRICE_COLOR/SPECIAL_K_COLOR/SIGNAL_COLOR/BULL_COLOR/BEAR_COLOR/ZERO_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_SPECIAL_K_COMPONENTS` (the twelve-term Pring table); `ChartLineSpecialKComponent` + `ChartLineSpecialKZone` types (`bull`|`bear`|`flat`|`none`); `getLineSpecialKFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value); `normalizeLineSpecialKComponents(components, fallback)` (validate/floor the table, drop invalid entries, fall back when empty or all-invalid); `normalizeLineSpecialKSignal(period, fallback)` (coerce to an integer >= 1); `computeLineSpecialKRoc(closes, period)` (the percentage ROC, null through the warm-up and on a zero base; non-array -> []); `computeLineSpecialKComponent(closes, rocPeriod, smaPeriod)` (the smoothed ROC term; non-array -> []); `computeLineSpecialK(closes, components)` (the weighted sum; non-array -> []); `computeLineSpecialKSignal(closes, components, signalPeriod)` (the signal SMA; non-array -> []); `runLineSpecialK(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, components, signalPeriod, specialK, signal, samples, specialKFinal, signalFinal, bullCount, bearCount, flatCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, specialK, signal, zone}`; `computeLineSpecialKLayout({data, components, signalPeriod, ...})` -- stacks the price panel above the Special K panel (the SK panel y-domain DYNAMIC and seeded with 0 so the zero line is always in view), projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `specialKPath`, `signalPath`, one `markers` entry per classified bar coloured by zone, and `zeroY`; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLineSpecialKChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Special K + weighted + rate-of-change + lookbacks + signal + the zone counts (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-special-k-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the Special K line + the signal line + every price dot + every marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-special-k / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -panel-label / -zero-line / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -special-k-line / -signal-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-sk / -tooltip-signal / -tooltip-zone / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config); root mirrors `data-component-count`, `data-signal-period`, `data-special-k-final`, `data-signal-final`, `data-bull-count`, `data-bear-count`, `data-flat-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; grid / axis / tick / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"special-k"`; markers expose `data-special-k` and `data-zone`. The Special K panel carries a dashed zero reference line, the dashed signal line and zone-coloured markers (green bull, red bear, purple flat). Tooltip on a price dot or a marker shows x, value, the special k, the signal and the zone. Config badge in top-left calls out `SK <componentCount>c`. The legend has three toggle items (Price / Special K / Signal). 70 vitest cases (normalizeLineSpecialKComponents incl. **keeps a valid table** + non-array/empty falls back + **floors fractional lookbacks** + **drops a sub-1 lookback entry** + **falls back when every entry is invalid**, normalizeLineSpecialKSignal incl. valid integer + accepts 1 + floors a fractional + zero/NaN falls back, getLineSpecialKFinitePoints incl. keeps only finite x + value + non-array -> [] + preserves order, computeLineSpecialKRoc incl. non-array -> [] + **all-null warm-up** + **the percentage change** + **clean percentage gain** + **null on a zero base close** + matches length, computeLineSpecialKComponent incl. non-array -> [] + null until both windows full + **smooths the rate of change** + matches length, computeLineSpecialK incl. non-array -> [] + **the exact weighted-sum series** + null until every component clears warm-up + **all-null when shorter than the lookbacks** + matches length, computeLineSpecialKSignal incl. non-array -> [] + **the exact signal SMA** + lags by the signal period + matches length, runLineSpecialK incl. not-ok short series + ok + **carries components/signalPeriod** + **the twelve-component default** + **the exact Special K series** + **the exact signal series** + **zone classification against the signal** + one sample per point + **self-consistent bull/bear/flat counts** + **the final readings** + x-sorting, computeLineSpecialKLayout incl. not-ok single point + ok + **stacks the price panel above the Special K panel** + price/special-k/signal paths + **zero inside the y-domain** + **one marker per classified bar** + the component count + total points + the final readings, describeLineSpecialKChart incl. **the indicator vocabulary** + weighted/rate-of-change/lookback + the zone counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / **empty state** / not-empty root / **root data-component-count/signal-period** / img-role svg / **the Special K + signal lines** / **one marker per classified bar** / **both panel labels** / config badge `3c` / **legend toggle hides the signal** / **controlled hiddenSeries hides the Special K** / ref) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for the new files (conditional spreads honour `exactOptionalPropertyTypes`, `noUncheckedIndexedAccess` guarded). Exported via components/ui barrel. Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/c4-mgr-auto. Worker auto-w655. Bump 1.11.691. |
| 11.674 | UI: chart-line-wad primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.692 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-wad.tsx` ships `<ChartLineWad>` -- a pure-SVG TWO-PANEL chart with a **Williams Accumulation/Distribution** panel from the cumulative true range gain or loss (the price panel on top, the Williams AD panel below on a dynamic zero-centred y-domain). **The computation:** the Williams Accumulation/Distribution (Larry Williams) is a cumulative PRICE-ONLY flow indicator -- distinct from the volume-based Chaikin A/D line -- that measures bar by bar how much the close gained or lost relative to the bar's "true range". Unlike the close-only indicator primitives in this family, the Williams AD genuinely needs the intrabar high and low: a close-only series would collapse the true-range machinery into the trivial close-to-close change, so `ChartLineWadPoint` carries `{x, high, low, close}` OHLC bars. `computeLineWadTrueRangeHigh(bars)` is the higher of the bar's high and the prior close; `computeLineWadTrueRangeLow(bars)` is the lower of the bar's low and the prior close (the opening bar has no prior close and is null). `computeLineWadDelta(bars)` is the per-bar accumulation/distribution amount: when the close finishes ABOVE the prior close the gain is `close - trueRangeLow`, when it finishes BELOW the loss is `close - trueRangeHigh`, an UNCHANGED close contributes zero. `computeLineWad(bars)` is the Williams AD line itself -- the running cumulative sum of the per-bar amount, starting at zero on the opening bar. **Worked fixture:** every step is an integer max / min / subtraction / running sum -- no division, no transcendental -- so an integer OHLC fixture makes the whole pipeline exact, asserted with `toEqual`. The eight bars are arranged so every branch fires -- an up-close with the low dipping below the prior close, a down-close with the high spiking above it, an up-close with the low holding above the prior close, a down-close with the high exactly at the prior close, and two unchanged closes: bars (high/low/close) `(12,8,10) (15,9,14) (16,13,14) (16,10,11) (14,12,13) (13,8,9) (11,7,9) (16,5,15)` -> trueRangeHigh `[null,15,16,16,14,13,11,16]`, trueRangeLow `[null,9,13,10,11,8,7,5]`, delta `[null,5,0,-5,2,-4,0,10]`, wad `[0,5,5,0,2,-2,-2,8]`, zones `[none,accumulation,flat,distribution,accumulation,distribution,flat,accumulation]` (3 accumulation, 2 distribution, 2 flat). `runLineWad` sorts the finite bars by x, runs the pipeline, and returns the trueRangeHigh + trueRangeLow + delta + wad series, per-bar `samples` classified `accumulation` (delta positive) / `distribution` (delta negative) / `flat` (delta zero) -- `none` on the opening bar -- the accumulation/distribution/flat counts, and the final reading; `ok` is false for fewer than two bars. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs `<ChartLineAdl>` / `<ChartLineCmf>` / `<ChartLineChaikinOsc>` -- those are the Chaikin family, weighting each bar's close location by VOLUME; the Williams AD uses NO volume, only the close versus the prior close and the true range. vs `<ChartLineObv>` -- OBV adds or subtracts the whole volume on the sign of the close change; the Williams AD adds a true-range-scaled price amount and ignores volume. vs `<ChartLineAtr>` / `<ChartLineChandelier>` -- those consume the true range as a volatility measure; the Williams AD uses the true-range high/low only to anchor a directional accumulation amount it then sums. All exported helpers carry a `LineWad`/`Wad` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineWadPoint[]` (`{x, high, low, close}` OHLC bars); `pricePanelRatio` (the fraction of the height given to the price panel; default 0.55, clamped to [0.3, 0.8]); `gap` (the pixel gap between the stacked panels); `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`priceColor`/`wadColor`/`accumulationColor`/`distributionColor`/`zeroColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showZeroLine`/`showMarkers`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `wad`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. The Williams AD has no tunable parameters -- it is purely cumulative -- so there is no period or multiplier. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_WAD_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/PRICE_COLOR/WAD_COLOR/ACCUMULATION_COLOR/DISTRIBUTION_COLOR/ZERO_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineWadZone` type (`accumulation`|`distribution`|`flat`|`none`); `getLineWadFinitePoints` (keeps only bars with a finite x + high + low + close); `computeLineWadTrueRangeHigh`/`computeLineWadTrueRangeLow(bars)` (the true-range high/low, opening bar null; non-array -> []); `computeLineWadDelta(bars)` (the per-bar amount, opening bar null; non-array -> []); `computeLineWad(bars)` (the cumulative line, zero on the opening bar; non-array -> [], empty -> []); `runLineWad(points)` -- returns `{series, trueRangeHigh, trueRangeLow, delta, wad, samples, wadFinal, accumulationCount, distributionCount, flatCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, close, trueRangeHigh, trueRangeLow, delta, wad, zone}`; `computeLineWadLayout({data, ...})` -- stacks the price panel above the Williams AD panel (the AD panel y-domain DYNAMIC and seeded with 0 so the zero line is always in view), projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `wadPath`, one `markers` entry per classified bar coloured by zone, and `zeroY`; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 bars -> ok=false; `describeLineWadChart(data)` -- ARIA summary mentions Williams Accumulation/Distribution + true range + cumulative + accumulation/distribution + the zone counts (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-wad-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the Williams AD line + every price dot + every marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-wad / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -panel-label / -zero-line / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -wad-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-close / -tooltip-delta / -tooltip-wad / -tooltip-zone / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config); root mirrors `data-wad-final`, `data-accumulation-count`, `data-distribution-count`, `data-flat-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; grid / axis / tick / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"wad"`; markers expose `data-wad` and `data-zone`. The Williams AD panel carries a dashed zero reference line and zone-coloured markers (green accumulation, red distribution, teal flat). Tooltip on a price dot or a marker shows x, close, the delta, the williams ad and the zone. Config badge in top-left calls out `WAD <barCount> bars`. The legend has two toggle items (Price / Williams AD). 63 vitest cases (getLineWadFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x/high/low/close bars** + non-array -> [] + empty -> [] + preserves order, computeLineWadTrueRangeHigh incl. non-array -> [] + **opening bar null** + **the higher of bar high and prior close** + **uses the prior close when it tops the high** + matches length, computeLineWadTrueRangeLow incl. non-array -> [] + opening bar null + **the lower of bar low and prior close** + **uses the prior close when it undercuts the low** + matches length, computeLineWadDelta incl. non-array -> [] + opening bar null + **measures an up-close from the true range low** + **measures a down-close from the true range high** + **zero on an unchanged close** + **the exact accumulation/distribution series** + matches length, computeLineWad incl. non-array -> [] + empty -> [] + **starts at zero** + **the exact cumulative running total** + **single zero for a one-bar series** + matches length, runLineWad incl. not-ok short series + ok + **the exact true range high/low series** + **the exact delta series** + **the exact cumulative series** + **zone classification by delta sign** + one sample per bar + **self-consistent accumulation/distribution/flat counts** + **the final reading** + x-sorting, computeLineWadLayout incl. not-ok single bar + **collapsed canvas -> ok=false** + ok + **stacks the price panel above the Williams AD panel** + price/wad paths + **zero inside the y-domain** + **one marker per classified bar** + the total bar count + the final reading, describeLineWadChart incl. **the indicator vocabulary** + true range/cumulative + the zone counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / **empty state** / not-empty root / **root data-wad-final/accumulation-count** / img-role svg / **the Williams AD line** / **one marker per classified bar** / **both panel labels** / config badge `8 bars` / two legend items / **legend toggle hides the Williams AD** / **controlled hiddenSeries hides the price** / ref) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for the new files (conditional spreads honour `exactOptionalPropertyTypes`, `noUncheckedIndexedAccess` guarded). Exported via components/ui barrel. | 11.675 | UI: chart-line-derivative-osc primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.693 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-derivative-osc.tsx` ships `<ChartLineDerivativeOsc>` -- a pure-SVG TWO-PANEL chart with a **Derivative Oscillator** panel from a double-smoothed RSI minus its signal average (the price panel on top, the oscillator panel below on a dynamic zero-centred y-domain). **The computation:** the Derivative Oscillator (Constance Brown) refines the RSI into a momentum oscillator that swings around zero. `computeLineDerivativeOscRsi(closes, period)` is Welles Wilder's RSI -- the first value seeds the average gain and loss with their simple mean over the first `period` changes, later values use Wilder smoothing, and a window with no movement reads 50 (avoiding the 0/0). `computeLineDerivativeOscEma(values, period)` is an exponential moving average over a series that may carry a null warm-up prefix -- it seeds with the first finite value and advances `ema += alpha*(value-ema)` (alpha = 2/(period+1)), a form chosen so a CONSTANT input yields a BIT-EXACT constant output. `computeLineDerivativeOscSmoothed(closes, params)` is the double-smoothed RSI -- the RSI run through two successive EMAs (classic 5 then 3). `computeLineDerivativeOscSignal(closes, params)` is the simple moving average of that double-smoothed RSI (classic 9). `computeLineDerivativeOsc(closes, params)` is the Derivative Oscillator itself: the double-smoothed RSI minus its signal, defined only where both exist. **Worked fixture:** the RSI is transcendental for a varying price (it divides the Wilder-smoothed gain by the smoothed gain-plus-loss), so the oscillator is anchored two ways. (1) EXACT anchors: a strictly rising price pins every RSI at exactly 100 (avgLoss is 0 so 100*avgGain/avgGain = 100), a strictly falling price at 0, a flat price at 50; because the EMA advances by `alpha*(value-ema)` a constant RSI passes through BOTH EMAs and the signal SMA bit-exactly unchanged, so the oscillator collapses to an exact `0` -- the end-to-end pipeline anchor, asserted with `toEqual`. The RSI seed and one Wilder step are also pinned with the hand fixture `[10,13,12,14]` period 2 -> `[null,null,75,87.5]` (the period-2 averages have their /2 cancel in the RSI ratio). (2) STRUCTURAL identities verified exactly for the varying fixture: the smoothed RSI equals `EMA(EMA(rsi))`, the signal equals the moving average of the smoothed RSI, and the oscillator equals smoothed minus signal element-wise. The EMA helper itself is pinned with the dyadic-alpha fixture `EMA([2,8,4], 3) = [2,5,4.5]` (period 3 -> alpha exactly 0.5). `runLineDerivativeOsc` sorts the finite price points by x, runs the pipeline, and returns the rsi + smoothed + signal + osc series, per-bar `samples` classified `positive` / `negative` / `zero` by the oscillator sign (`none` through the warm-up), the positive/negative/zero counts, and the final reading; `ok` is false for fewer than two points. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs `<ChartLineRsi>` -- that plots the raw RSI in 0..100; the Derivative Oscillator double-smooths the RSI and subtracts its own signal so it oscillates around zero as a momentum-of-momentum reading. vs `<ChartLineTsi>` / `<ChartLinePmo>` -- those double-smooth the raw price MOMENTUM; the Derivative Oscillator double-smooths the RSI instead, inheriting the RSI's bounded 0..100 base. vs `<ChartLineMacd>` -- the MACD subtracts two EMAs of price; the Derivative Oscillator subtracts an SMA signal from a double-EMA-smoothed RSI. vs `<ChartLineStc>` -- the Schaff Trend Cycle re-applies stochastics to a MACD; the Derivative Oscillator stays in the RSI family. All exported helpers carry a `LineDerivativeOsc`/`DerivativeOsc` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineDerivativeOscPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `rsiPeriod` (default 14), `ema1Period` (default 5), `ema2Period` (default 3), `signalPeriod` (default 9) -- each coerced to an integer >= 1; `pricePanelRatio` (default 0.55, clamped to [0.3, 0.8]); `gap`; `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`priceColor`/`oscColor`/`positiveColor`/`negativeColor`/`zeroColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showZeroLine`/`showMarkers`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `osc`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_DERIVATIVE_OSC_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/RSI_PERIOD/EMA1_PERIOD/EMA2_PERIOD/SIGNAL_PERIOD/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/PRICE_COLOR/OSC_COLOR/POSITIVE_COLOR/NEGATIVE_COLOR/ZERO_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineDerivativeOscZone` type (`positive`|`negative`|`zero`|`none`); `ChartLineDerivativeOscParams` type (`{rsiPeriod?, ema1Period?, ema2Period?, signalPeriod?}`); `getLineDerivativeOscFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value); `normalizeLineDerivativeOscPeriod(period, fallback)` (coerce to an integer >= 1; non-finite or sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineDerivativeOscRsi(closes, period)` (Wilder RSI, null warm-up, no-movement window -> 50; non-array -> []); `computeLineDerivativeOscEma(values, period)` (EMA over a nullable series, constant-stable; non-array -> []); `computeLineDerivativeOscSmoothed(closes, params?)` (the double-smoothed RSI; non-array -> []); `computeLineDerivativeOscSignal(closes, params?)` (the SMA of the smoothed RSI; non-array -> []); `computeLineDerivativeOsc(closes, params?)` (the oscillator = smoothed - signal; non-array -> []); `runLineDerivativeOsc(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, rsiPeriod, ema1Period, ema2Period, signalPeriod, rsi, smoothed, signal, osc, samples, oscFinal, positiveCount, negativeCount, zeroCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, rsi, smoothed, signal, osc, zone}`; `computeLineDerivativeOscLayout({data, rsiPeriod, ..., width, height, padding})` -- stacks the price panel above the oscillator panel (the oscillator y-domain DYNAMIC and seeded with 0 so the zero line is always in view), projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `oscPath`, one `markers` entry per defined oscillator bar coloured by zone, and `zeroY`; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLineDerivativeOscChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Derivative Oscillator + RSI + double-smooth + signal + the zone counts (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-derivative-osc-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the oscillator line + every price dot + every marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-derivative-osc / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -panel-label / -zero-line / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -osc-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-rsi / -tooltip-osc / -tooltip-zone / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config); root mirrors `data-rsi-period`, `data-ema1-period`, `data-ema2-period`, `data-signal-period`, `data-osc-final`, `data-positive-count`, `data-negative-count`, `data-zero-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; grid / axis / tick / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"osc"`; markers expose `data-osc` and `data-zone`. The oscillator panel carries a dashed zero reference line and sign-coloured markers (green positive, red negative). Tooltip on a price dot or a marker shows x, value, the rsi, the osc and the zone. Config badge in top-left calls out `DO <rsi>/<ema1>/<ema2>/<signal>`. The legend has two toggle items (Price / Derivative Osc). 74 vitest cases (normalizeLineDerivativeOscPeriod incl. floors a fractional + **zero/negative/NaN falls back** + the minimum of 1, getLineDerivativeOscFinitePoints incl. keeps only finite x + value + non-array -> [] + preserves order, computeLineDerivativeOscRsi incl. non-array -> [] + null warm-up + **the exact seed RSI 75** + **the exact Wilder-smoothed RSI 87.5** + **100 for a strictly rising price** + **0 for a strictly falling price** + **50 for a flat price** + **bounded 0..100** + matches length, computeLineDerivativeOscEma incl. non-array -> [] + **the alpha-times-gap recurrence** + **skips a null warm-up prefix** + **holds a constant input bit-exactly constant** + matches length, [] + **equals the moving average of the smoothed RSI** + lags by the signal window + matches length, computeLineDerivativeOsc incl. non-array -> [] + **equals smoothed minus signal** + **exact zero for a rising price** + **exact zero for a falling price** + **exact zero for a flat price** + matches length, runLineDerivativeOsc incl. not-ok short series + ok + **carries the resolved periods** + | 11.676 | UI: chart-line-bop primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.694 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-bop.tsx` ships `<ChartLineBop>` -- a pure-SVG TWO-PANEL chart with a **Balance of Power** panel from the close minus open spread over the bar range (the price panel on top, the Balance of Power panel below on a FIXED -1..+1 y-domain). **The computation:** the Balance of Power (Igor Livshin) gauges the tug-of-war between buyers and sellers WITHIN each bar. It needs the open as well as the high, low and close, so `ChartLineBopPoint` carries `{x, open, high, low, close}` OHLC bars. `computeLineBopRaw(bars)` is each bar's Balance of Power -- the close-minus-open spread divided by the high-to-low range, `(close-open)/(high-low)`, clamped to -1..+1; a bar with no range (high == low) reads zero, a bar with a non-finite field is null. A reading of +1 means the bar closed at its HIGH after opening at its LOW (buyers in full control across the whole range), -1 the mirror case (sellers in control), zero a doji that closes where it opened. `computeLineBop(bars, period)` is the signal line -- the simple moving average of the raw Balance of Power over `period` bars (default 14, Livshin's). **Worked fixture:** the Balance of Power is a single division per bar, so pinning every bar's high-to-low range to exactly 8 -- a power of two -- makes each raw reading `(close-open)/8` land on an eighth, an exact binary fraction; a four-bar signal SMA then divides a sum of eighths by four, landing on a thirty-second, still exact in float -- so the whole pipeline is asserted with `toEqual`. The fixture (all bars high 18 / low 10, range 8) runs the close-minus-open spread across the full +8..-8 swing including dojis: raw `[1,0.5,0,-0.5,-1,0.25,-0.25,1,-1,0,0.75,-0.75]`, signal (period 4) `[null,null,null,0.25,-0.25,-0.3125,-0.375,0,0,-0.0625,0.1875,-0.25]`, zones `[buy,buy,balanced,sell,sell,buy,sell,buy,sell,balanced,buy,sell]` (5 buy, 5 sell, 2 balanced). The clamp (a malformed out-of-range bar), the zero-range bar and the non-finite guard are checked separately. `runLineBop` sorts the finite OHLC bars by x, runs the pipeline, and returns the raw + signal series, per-bar `samples` classified `buy` (raw positive) / `sell` (raw negative) / `balanced` (raw zero) by the sign of the raw reading, the buy/sell/balanced counts, and the final readings; `ok` is false for fewer than two bars. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs `<ChartLineWad>` (Williams Accumulation/Distribution) -- the WAD CUMULATES a true-range gain or loss; the Balance of Power is a per-bar bounded -1..+1 ratio with no cumulation. vs `<ChartLineCmf>` / `<ChartLineAdl>` -- the Chaikin family weights the close LOCATION inside the range by VOLUME; the Balance of Power compares the open-to-close spread to the range and uses no volume. vs `<ChartLineRvi>` (Relative Vigor Index) -- the RVI also ratios the close-open spread against the range but smooths a four-bar weighted numerator and denominator separately; the Balance of Power is the raw per-bar ratio with a plain SMA signal. vs `<ChartLineQstick>` -- Qstick averages the raw close-minus-open spread; the Balance of Power normalises that spread by the bar range first. All exported helpers carry a `LineBop`/`Bop` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineBopPoint[]` (`{x, open, high, low, close}` OHLC bars); `period` (the signal SMA length; default 14, coerced to an integer >= 1); `pricePanelRatio` (default 0.55, clamped to [0.3, 0.8]); `gap`; `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`priceColor`/`bopColor`/`signalColor`/`buyColor`/`sellColor`/`zeroColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showSignal`/`showZeroLine`/`showMarkers`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `bop` / `signal`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_BOP_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/PRICE_COLOR/BOP_COLOR/SIGNAL_COLOR/BUY_COLOR/SELL_COLOR/ZERO_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineBopZone` type (`buy`|`sell`|`balanced`|`none`); `getLineBopFinitePoints` (keeps only bars with a finite x + open + high + low + close); `normalizeLineBopPeriod(period, fallback)` (coerce to an integer >= 1; non-finite or sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineBopRaw(bars)` (the raw Balance of Power, clamped, zero-range -> 0, non-finite -> null; non-array -> []); `computeLineBop(bars, period)` (the signal SMA; non-array -> []); `runLineBop(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, period, raw, signal, samples, bopFinal, signalFinal, buyCount, sellCount, balancedCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, open, high, low, close, raw, signal, zone}`; `computeLineBopLayout({data, period, ...})` -- stacks the price panel above the Balance of Power panel (the BOP panel y-domain FIXED to [-1,1]), projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `bopPath`, `signalPath`, one `markers` entry per bar coloured by zone, and `zeroY`; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 bars -> ok=false; `describeLineBopChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Balance of Power + close + open + range + signal + the zone counts (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-bop-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the Balance of Power line + the signal line + every price dot + every marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-bop / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -panel-label / -zero-line / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -bop-line / -signal-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-close / -tooltip-bop / -tooltip-signal / -tooltip-zone / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-bop-final`, `data-signal-final`, `data-buy-count`, `data-sell-count`, `data-balanced-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; grid / axis / tick / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"bop"`; markers expose `data-bop` and `data-zone`. The Balance of Power panel carries a dashed zero reference line, the dashed signal line and zone-coloured markers (green buy, red sell, cyan balanced). Tooltip on a price dot or a marker shows x, close, the bop, the signal and the zone. Config badge in top-left calls out `BOP <period>`. The legend has three toggle items (Price / BOP / Signal). 60 vitest cases (getLineBopFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x/open/high/low/close bars** + non-array -> [] + empty -> [] + preserves order, normalizeLineBopPeriod incl. floors a fractional + **zero/negative/NaN falls back**, computeLineBopRaw incl. non-array -> [] + **the close-minus-open spread over the range** + **+1 when opening at the low and closing at the high** + **-1 in the mirror case** + **zero for a doji** + **zero for a no-range bar** + **clamps a malformed out-of-range bar** + **null for a non-finite field** + matches length, computeLineBop incl. non-array -> [] + **the simple moving average of the raw Balance of Power** + null warm-up + **bounded -1..+1** + matches length, runLineBop incl. not-ok short series + ok + **carries the signal period** + **the default period** + **the exact raw series** + **the exact signal series** + **zone classification by the raw sign** + one sample per bar + **self-consistent buy/sell/balanced counts** + **the final readings** + x-sorting, computeLineBopLayout incl. not-ok single bar + **collapsed canvas -> ok=false** + ok + **stacks the price panel above the Balance of Power panel** + price/bop/signal paths + **the zero line inside the panel** + **one marker per bar** + the total points + the final readings, describeLineBopChart incl. **the indicator vocabulary** + range/signal + the zone counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / **empty state** / not-empty root / **root data-period/buy-count** / img-role svg / **the Balance of Power + signal lines** / **one marker per bar** / **both panel labels** / config badge `4` / three legend items / **legend toggle hides the signal** / **controlled hiddenSeries hides the BOP line** / ref) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. | 11.677 | UI: chart-line-asi primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.695 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-asi.tsx` ships `<ChartLineAsi>` -- a pure-SVG TWO-PANEL chart with an **Accumulative Swing Index** panel summing the Wilder swing index across bars (the price panel on top, the Accumulative Swing Index panel below on a dynamic zero-centred y-domain). **The computation:** Welles Wilder's Accumulative Swing Index (ASI) is the running total of his Swing Index, which scores each bar-to-bar price swing. It needs the full OHLC bar, so `ChartLineAsiPoint` carries `{x, open, high, low, close}`. `computeLineAsiSwingIndex(bars, limitMove)` is the per-bar Swing Index -- given the current bar `(O,H,L,C)` and the prior `(Op,Cp)`: `K = max(|H-Cp|, |L-Cp|)`; `R` is chosen from three candidates `|H-Cp|` / `|L-Cp|` / `|H-L|` by which is LARGEST (a-branch `|H-Cp| - 0.5|L-Cp| + 0.25|Cp-Op|`, b-branch `|L-Cp| - 0.5|H-Cp| + 0.25|Cp-Op|`, c-branch `|H-L| + 0.25|Cp-Op|`); `N = (C-Cp) + 0.5(C-O) + 0.25(Cp-Op)`; `SI = 50*(N/R)*(K/limitMove)`, defined as 0 when R is 0 (avoiding the divide-by-zero on a flat bar). The opening bar has no prior bar and is null. `computeLineAsi(bars, limitMove)` is the Accumulative Swing Index itself -- the running cumulative sum of the Swing Index, starting at zero on the opening bar. **Worked fixture:** the Swing Index divides by R and by the limit move, so the fixture pins BOTH to powers of two -- every bar's high-to-low range is 8 and the limit move is 4 -- and the numerator and R are integer sums with 0.5 and 0.25 weights (all exact binary fractions for integer prices), so every Swing Index lands on a quarter and the cumulative ASI stays exact, asserted with `toEqual`. The main fixture uses doji bars (open == close) whose R picks exercise each of the three branches in turn (c, c, a, b, c): bars (open/high/low/close) `(50,54,46,50) (54,56,48,54) (52,56,48,52) (58,60,52,58) (50,58,50,50) (50,54,46,50)` -> swingIndex `[null,37.5,-18.75,75,-100,0]`, asi `[0,37.5,18.75,93.75,-6.25,-6.25]`, zones `[none,up,down,up,down,flat]` (2 up, 2 down, 1 flat). A second fixture with open differing from close exercises the open-weighted numerator term and the `0.25*|Cp-Op|` term in R (`[null,100,50]`); a flat fixture drives R to zero to check the guard (`[null,0]`). `runLineAsi` sorts the finite OHLC bars by x, runs the pipeline, and returns the swingIndex + asi series, per-bar `samples` classified `up` (Swing Index positive) / `down` (negative) / `flat` (zero) -- `none` on the opening bar -- the up/down/flat counts, and the final reading; `ok` is false for fewer than two bars. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs `<ChartLineWad>` -- both are cumulative price-only sums, but the WAD accumulates a true-range gain/loss while the ASI accumulates Wilder's far more elaborate Swing Index (the three-branch R, the open/prior-close/limit-move weighting). vs `<ChartLineObv>` / `<ChartLineAdl>` -- those cumulate volume-weighted flow; the ASI uses no volume, only OHLC geometry. vs `<ChartLineAtr>` -- the ATR averages the true range as a volatility gauge; the ASI folds a true-range term into a SIGNED swing score and cumulates it. vs `<ChartLineBop>` -- the Balance of Power is a bounded per-bar ratio with no cumulation; the ASI is an unbounded running total. All exported helpers carry a `LineAsi`/`Asi` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineAsiPoint[]` (`{x, open, high, low, close}` OHLC bars); `limitMove` (the Swing Index limit-move divisor; default 3, accepted when finite and positive, fractional allowed); `pricePanelRatio` (default 0.55, clamped to [0.3, 0.8]); `gap`; `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`priceColor`/`asiColor`/`upColor`/`downColor`/`zeroColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showZeroLine`/`showMarkers`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `asi`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_ASI_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/LIMIT_MOVE/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/PRICE_COLOR/ASI_COLOR/UP_COLOR/DOWN_COLOR/ZERO_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineAsiZone` type (`up`|`down`|`flat`|`none`); `getLineAsiFinitePoints` (keeps only bars with a finite x + open + high + low + close); `normalizeLineAsiLimitMove(limitMove, fallback)` (finite positive, else fallback, no floor); `computeLineAsiSwingIndex(bars, limitMove)` (the per-bar Swing Index, opening bar null, R-zero -> 0; non-array -> []); `computeLineAsi(bars, limitMove)` (the cumulative ASI, zero on the opening bar; non-array -> [], empty -> []); `runLineAsi(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, limitMove, swingIndex, asi, samples, asiFinal, upCount, downCount, flatCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, open, high, low, close, swingIndex, asi, zone}`; `computeLineAsiLayout({data, limitMove, ...})` -- stacks the price panel above the Accumulative Swing Index panel (the ASI panel y-domain DYNAMIC and seeded with 0 so the zero line is always in view), projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `asiPath`, one `markers` entry per scored bar coloured by zone, and `zeroY`; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 bars -> ok=false; `describeLineAsiChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Accumulative Swing Index + Wilder + Swing Index + limit move + the zone counts (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-asi-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the Accumulative Swing Index line + every price dot + every marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-asi / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -panel-label / -zero-line / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -asi-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-close / -tooltip-si / -tooltip-asi / -tooltip-zone / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config); root mirrors `data-limit-move`, `data-asi-final`, `data-up-count`, `data-down-count`, `data-flat-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; grid / axis / tick / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"asi"`; markers expose `data-asi` and `data-zone`. The Accumulative Swing Index panel carries a dashed zero reference line and swing-coloured markers (green up, red down, purple flat). Tooltip on a price dot or a marker shows x, close, the swing index, the asi and the zone. Config badge in top-left calls out `ASI <limitMove>`. The legend has two toggle items (Price / ASI). 61 vitest cases (getLineAsiFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x/open/high/low/close bars** + non-array -> [] + empty -> [] + preserves order, normalizeLineAsiLimitMove incl. **keeps a fractional limit move** + zero/negative/NaN falls back, computeLineAsiSwingIndex incl. non-array -> [] + **opening bar null** + **the exact Wilder Swing Index series** + **weighs the open and prior-close terms** + **zero when the range collapses** + positive on an up-swing + negative on a down-swing + matches length, computeLineAsi incl. non-array -> [] + empty -> [] + **starts at zero** + **the exact cumulative running total** + **the open-weighted fixture** + **single zero for a one-bar series** + matches length, runLineAsi incl. not-ok short series + ok + **carries the limit move** + **the default limit move** + **the exact Swing Index series** + **the exact Accumulative Swing Index series** + **zone classification by the swing sign** + one sample per bar + **self-consistent up/down/flat counts** + **the final reading** + x-sorting, computeLineAsiLayout incl. not-ok single bar + **collapsed canvas -> ok=false** + ok + **stacks the price panel above the Swing Index panel** + price/asi paths + **zero inside the y-domain** + **one marker per scored bar** + the total bars + the final reading, describeLineAsiChart incl. **the Accumulative Swing Index name** + **credits Wilder + the Swing Index** + the zone counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / **empty state** / not-empty root / **root data-limit-move/asi-final** / img-role svg / **the Accumulative Swing Index line** / **one marker per scored bar** / **both panel labels** / config badge `4` / two legend items / **legend toggle hides the ASI line** / **controlled hiddenSeries hides the price** / ref) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. | 11.678 | UI: chart-line-gapo primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.696 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-gapo.tsx` ships `<ChartLineGapo>` -- a pure-SVG TWO-PANEL chart with a **Gopalakrishnan Range Index** panel from the log of range over the lookback span (the price panel on top, the Gopalakrishnan Range Index panel below on a dynamic y-domain). **The computation:** the Gopalakrishnan Range Index (GAPO) is a fractal-style gauge of the trading range. It needs the high and low, so `ChartLineGapoPoint` carries `{x, high, low, close}` -- high and low feed the range, the close draws the price line. `computeLineGapoRange(bars, period)` is the high-to-low range over each trailing window of `period` bars -- the highest high minus the lowest low; bars before the window is full are null. `computeLineGapo(bars, period)` is the Gopalakrishnan Range Index itself: `ln(range) / ln(period)`, the natural log of that range divided by the natural log of the lookback span -- equivalently the base-`period` log of the range. It climbs as the trading range widens and falls as it narrows. The period must be at least 2 so `ln(period)` is positive (avoiding the divide-by-zero at period 1); a bar whose range is not positive (no movement at all) is null. **Worked fixture:** the index divides two natural logs, so it is transcendental in general -- but `ln(range)/ln(period)` is the base-`period` log of the range, an exact integer when the range is a power of the period. The fixture uses a lookback of 2 and tunes the two-bar ranges to 1, 2, 4, 8 and 16 so the index reads 0, 1, 2, 3 and 4: bars (high/low/close) `(10,9,10) (10,9,10) (11,9,11) (13,9,12) (17,9,15) (25,9,22) (10,9,11) (10,9,10)` -> range `[null,1,2,4,8,16,16,1]` (asserted exactly with `toEqual`, integer max/min/subtraction), gapo `[null,0,1,~2,~3,~4,~4,0]`. `Math.log(1)` is exactly 0 and `Math.log(2)/Math.log(2)` is exactly 1, so those two anchors are asserted with `toBe`; the higher powers (`~2,~3,~4`) are asserted with `toBeCloseTo` to nine digits. The range holds (16 -> 16) and collapses (16 -> 1) so the expanding / steady / contracting zones all appear; zones `[none,none,expanding,expanding,expanding,expanding,steady,contracting]` (4 expanding, 1 contracting, 1 steady). A base-3 fixture (period 3, range 9) checks `log3(9) ~ 2`; a zero-range fixture checks the null guard. `runLineGapo` sorts the finite OHLC bars by x, runs the pipeline, and returns the range + gapo series, per-bar `samples` classified `expanding` (the index rose from the prior defined bar) / `contracting` (fell) / `steady` (unchanged) -- `none` through the warm-up and on the first defined bar -- the expanding/contracting/steady counts, the mean reading and the final reading; `ok` is false for fewer than two bars. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs `<ChartLineAtr>` / `<ChartLineChoppiness>` -- those gauge range/volatility via the true range; the GAPO is a pure high-low range fed through a log/log ratio, a fractal-dimension flavour rather than an average. vs `<ChartLineHurst>` -- the Hurst exponent uses rescaled-range analysis (a statistical scaling law); the GAPO is a single direct log ratio of the window range to the window length. vs `<ChartLineBandwidth>` -- Bollinger bandwidth normalises the band spread by the middle band; the GAPO normalises the raw range by the LOG of the lookback count, not by price. vs `<ChartLineDpo>` -- the Detrended Price Oscillator removes a moving-average trend; the GAPO never references an average, only the window extremes. All exported helpers carry a `LineGapo`/`Gapo` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineGapoPoint[]` (`{x, high, low, close}`); `period` (the lookback span; default 5, coerced to an integer >= 2); `pricePanelRatio` (default 0.55, clamped to [0.3, 0.8]); `gap`; `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`priceColor`/`gapoColor`/`expandingColor`/`contractingColor`/`meanColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showMeanLine`/`showMarkers`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `gapo`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_GAPO_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/PRICE_COLOR/GAPO_COLOR/EXPANDING_COLOR/CONTRACTING_COLOR/MEAN_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineGapoZone` type (`expanding`|`contracting`|`steady`|`none`); `getLineGapoFinitePoints` (keeps only bars with a finite x + high + low + close); `normalizeLineGapoPeriod(period, fallback)` (coerce to an integer >= 2; non-finite or sub-2 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineGapoRange(bars, period)` (the rolling high-low range, null warm-up; non-array -> []); `computeLineGapo(bars, period)` (the index, null warm-up and null on a non-positive range; non-array -> []); `runLineGapo(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, period, range, gapo, gapoMean, samples, gapoFinal, expandingCount, contractingCount, steadyCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, high, low, close, range, gapo, zone}`; `computeLineGapoLayout({data, period, ...})` -- stacks the price panel above the Gopalakrishnan Range Index panel (the index y-domain DYNAMIC over the defined readings), projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `gapoPath`, one `markers` entry per classified bar coloured by zone, and `meanY` + `hasMean` for the dashed mean reference line; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 bars -> ok=false; `describeLineGapoChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Gopalakrishnan Range Index + log + range + lookback span + the zone counts (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-gapo-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the Gopalakrishnan Range Index line + every price dot + every marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-gapo / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -panel-label / -mean-line / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -gapo-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-close / -tooltip-range / -tooltip-gapo / -tooltip-zone / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-gapo-final`, `data-gapo-mean`, `data-expanding-count`, `data-contracting-count`, `data-steady-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; grid / axis / tick / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"gapo"`; markers expose `data-gapo` and `data-zone`. The Gopalakrishnan Range Index panel carries a dashed mean reference line and zone-coloured markers (green expanding, red contracting, teal steady). Tooltip on a price dot or a marker shows x, close, the range, the gapo and the zone. Config badge in top-left calls out `GAPO <period>`. The legend has two toggle items (Price / GAPO). 61 vitest cases (getLineGapoFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x/high/low/close bars** + non-array -> [] + empty -> [] + preserves order, normalizeLineGapoPeriod incl. floors a fractional + **sub-2 / zero / NaN falls back** + the minimum of 2, computeLineGapoRange incl. non-array -> [] + null warm-up + **the highest high minus the lowest low** + **the exact range series** + matches length, computeLineGapo incl. non-array -> [] + null warm-up + **exactly zero when the range is one** + **exactly one when the range equals the period** + **the base-2 log of a power-of-two range** + **the base-3 log under a period of 3** + **null when the range collapses to zero** + matches length, runLineGapo incl. not-ok short series + ok + **carries the period** + **the default period** + **the exact range series** + **the index with exact integer anchors** + **zone classification by the index change** + **the mean reading** + one sample per bar + **self-consistent expanding/contracting/steady counts** + **the final reading** + x-sorting, computeLineGapoLayout incl. not-ok single bar + **collapsed canvas -> ok=false** + ok + **stacks the price panel above the index panel** + price/gapo paths + **the mean line inside the panel** + **one marker per classified bar** + the total bars + the final reading, describeLineGapoChart incl. **the indicator name** + log/range + the zone counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / **empty state** / not-empty root / **root data-period/expanding-count** / img-role svg / **the index line** / **the mean reference line** / **one marker per classified bar** / config badge `2` / two legend items / **legend toggle hides the index line** / **controlled hiddenSeries hides the price** / ref) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for the new files (conditional spreads honour `exactOptionalPropertyTypes`, `noUncheckedIndexedAccess` guarded). Exported via components/ui barrel. | 11.679 | UI: chart-line-inertia primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.697 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-inertia.tsx` ships `<ChartLineInertia>` -- a pure-SVG TWO-PANEL chart with an **Inertia** panel from a linear regression smoothing of the relative vigor index (the price panel on top, the Inertia panel below on a dynamic zero-centred y-domain). **The computation:** Donald Dorsey's Inertia smooths a vigor index with a linear regression. It needs the open as well as the high/low/close, so `ChartLineInertiaPoint` carries `{x, open, high, low, close}` OHLC bars. `computeLineInertiaRvgi(bars, vigorPeriod)` is the Relative Vigor Index -- the SUMMED close-minus-open spread over the SUMMED high-to-low range across the trailing vigor window (`sum(close-open) / sum(high-low)`); it reads how vigorously the closes finished against their opens relative to the bar ranges, and a window with no total range is null. `computeLineInertiaLinReg(values, period)` is a rolling linear regression smoothing -- for each bar the least-squares trend line is fit to the trailing `period` values (x = 0..period-1) and evaluated at the endpoint via `slope = (m*Sxy - Sx*Sy)/(m*Sxx - Sx^2)`, `intercept = (Sy - slope*Sx)/m`, `endpoint = intercept + slope*(m-1)`. `computeLineInertia(bars, vigorPeriod, regPeriod)` is the Inertia itself: the Relative Vigor Index passed through that regression smoothing. **Worked fixture:** the pipeline is verified piece by piece. The Relative Vigor Index is a ratio of summed integer spreads to summed integer ranges -- the fixture pins every bar's range to 8 and every close-open spread to -8, 0 or +8, so a four-bar index divides by 32 and lands on an exact eighth, asserted with `toEqual`: spreads `[8,0,8,8,0,8,-8,0,-8,-8,0,-8]` -> rvgi `[null,null,null,0.75,0.5,0.75,0.25,0,-0.25,-0.75,-0.5,-0.75]`. The linear regression is pinned with three hand fixtures -- a constant input stays constant (`[5,5,5,5]` -> `[null,null,5,5]`), a linear input is reproduced at its endpoint (`[0,2,4,6,8]` -> `[null,null,4,6,8]`), and a non-linear window resolves to its exact least-squares endpoint (`[0,0,3]` -> `2.5`). The Inertia is then checked as the EXACT COMPOSITION of the two -- `computeLineInertia` equals `computeLineInertiaLinReg(computeLineInertiaRvgi(...))` -- and an all-identical-bars fixture drives the whole chain to an exact constant `0.5`. The fixture's vigor runs up then down so the regression-smoothed Inertia crosses zero and both the bull and bear zones appear. `runLineInertia` sorts the finite OHLC bars by x, runs the pipeline, and returns the rvgi + inertia series, per-bar `samples` classified `bull` (Inertia positive) / `bear` (negative) / `neutral` (zero) -- `none` through the warm-up -- the bull/bear/neutral counts, and the final reading; `ok` is false for fewer than two bars. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs `<ChartLineRvi>` -- the Relative Vigor Index is the INPUT to the Inertia; the Inertia adds the linear-regression smoothing layer on top. vs `<ChartLineLsma>` -- the LSMA applies a linear regression to PRICE; the Inertia applies it to the vigor index. vs `<ChartLineBop>` -- the Balance of Power is a per-bar close-open / range ratio; the Relative Vigor Index here sums spread and range over a window before dividing, then the Inertia regresses it. vs `<ChartLineTheilsen>` -- that fits a robust median-of-slopes line to price; the Inertia uses an ordinary least-squares fit on the vigor index. All exported helpers carry a `LineInertia`/`Inertia` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineInertiaPoint[]` (`{x, open, high, low, close}` OHLC bars); `vigorPeriod` (the Relative Vigor Index window; default 10, coerced to an integer >= 2); `regPeriod` (the linear regression window; default 14, coerced to an integer >= 2); `pricePanelRatio` (default 0.55, clamped to [0.3, 0.8]); `gap`; `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`priceColor`/`inertiaColor`/`rvgiColor`/`bullColor`/`bearColor`/`zeroColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showRvgi`/`showZeroLine`/`showMarkers`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `inertia` / `rvgi`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_INERTIA_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/VIGOR_PERIOD/REG_PERIOD/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/PRICE_COLOR/INERTIA_COLOR/RVGI_COLOR/BULL_COLOR/BEAR_COLOR/ZERO_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineInertiaZone` type (`bull`|`bear`|`neutral`|`none`); `getLineInertiaFinitePoints` (keeps only bars with a finite x + open + high + low + close); `normalizeLineInertiaPeriod(period, fallback)` (coerce to an integer >= 2; non-finite or sub-2 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineInertiaRvgi(bars, vigorPeriod)` (the Relative Vigor Index, null warm-up and null on a zero total range; non-array -> []); `computeLineInertiaLinReg(values, period)` (the rolling regression endpoint over a nullable series; non-array -> []); `computeLineInertia(bars, vigorPeriod, regPeriod)` (the Inertia = regression of the index; non-array -> []); `runLineInertia(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, vigorPeriod, regPeriod, rvgi, inertia, samples, inertiaFinal, bullCount, bearCount, neutralCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, open, high, low, close, rvgi, inertia, zone}`; `computeLineInertiaLayout({data, vigorPeriod, regPeriod, ...})` -- stacks the price panel above the Inertia panel (the Inertia y-domain DYNAMIC over the rvgi + inertia readings and seeded with 0 so the zero line is always in view), projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `inertiaPath`, `rvgiPath`, one `markers` entry per defined Inertia bar coloured by zone, and `zeroY`; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 bars -> ok=false; `describeLineInertiaChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Inertia + Relative Vigor Index + linear regression + the zone counts (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-inertia-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the Inertia line + the Relative Vigor Index line + every price dot + every marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-inertia / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -panel-label / -zero-line / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -inertia-line / -rvgi-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-close / -tooltip-rvgi / -tooltip-inertia / -tooltip-zone / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config); root mirrors `data-vigor-period`, `data-reg-period`, `data-inertia-final`, `data-bull-count`, `data-bear-count`, `data-neutral-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; grid / axis / tick / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"inertia"`; markers expose `data-inertia` and `data-zone`. The Inertia panel carries a dashed zero reference line, the dashed Relative Vigor Index line and zone-coloured markers (green bull, red bear, purple neutral). Tooltip on a price dot or a marker shows x, close, the rvgi, the inertia and the zone. Config badge in top-left calls out `INRT <vigorPeriod>/<regPeriod>`. The legend has three toggle items (Price / Inertia / RVGI). 60 vitest cases (getLineInertiaFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x/open/high/low/close bars** + non-array -> [] + empty -> [] + preserves order, normalizeLineInertiaPeriod incl. floors a fractional + **sub-2 / NaN falls back** + the minimum of 2, computeLineInertiaRvgi incl. non-array -> [] + null warm-up + **the summed spread over the summed range** + **null when the total range collapses** + matches length, computeLineInertiaLinReg incl. non-array -> [] + **holds a constant input** + **reproduces a linear input at its endpoint** + **the least-squares endpoint of a non-linear window** + **null while the window holds a null** + matches length, computeLineInertia incl. non-array -> [] + **the regression smoothing of the Relative Vigor Index** + **an exact constant for identical bars** + matches length, runLineInertia incl. not-ok short series + ok + **carries the vigor/regression periods** + **the default periods** + **the exact Relative Vigor Index series** + **the Inertia equals the regression of the index** + **zone classification by the Inertia sign** + **both bull and bear appear** + one sample per bar + **the final reading** + x-sorting, computeLineInertiaLayout incl. not-ok single bar + **collapsed canvas -> ok=false** + ok + **stacks the price panel above the Inertia panel** + price/inertia/rvgi paths + **zero inside the y-domain** + **one marker per defined Inertia bar** + the periods + total points, describeLineInertiaChart incl. **the indicator name** + Relative Vigor Index/linear regression + the zone counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / **empty state** / not-empty root / **root data-vigor-period/reg-period** / img-role svg / **the Inertia + Relative Vigor Index lines** / **one marker per defined Inertia bar** / **both panel labels** / config badge `4/3` / **legend toggle hides the RVGI line** / **controlled hiddenSeries hides the Inertia line** / ref) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. | 11.680 | UI: chart-line-donchian primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.698 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-donchian.tsx` ships `<ChartLineDonchian>` -- a pure-SVG SINGLE-PANEL overlay chart with a **Donchian Channel** from the rolling highest high and lowest low (the price line and the channel bands share one panel). **The computation:** Richard Donchian's channel frames the price's recent trading range. It needs the high and low, so `ChartLineDonchianPoint` carries `{x, high, low, close}` -- high and low feed the bands, the close draws the price line. `computeLineDonchianUpper(bars, period)` is the upper band -- the highest high over each trailing window of `period` bars (inclusive of the current bar). `computeLineDonchianLower(bars, period)` is the lower band -- the lowest low over the same window. `computeLineDonchianMiddle(bars, period)` is the middle band -- the midpoint `(upper+lower)/2`. Bars before the window is full are null. **Worked fixture:** the Donchian Channel is a rolling MAXIMUM of the high and a rolling MINIMUM of the low, with the middle their midpoint -- all integer max / min / halving, no transcendental and no division beyond a halving -- so an integer OHLC fixture makes the whole pipeline exact, asserted with `toEqual` (an odd band sum simply lands the midpoint on a `.5`, still an exact binary fraction). The fixture (period 3) bars (high/low/close) `(20,10,15) (22,12,20) (24,14,16) (22,12,21) (20,8,10) (18,10,17) (26,16,25) (24,18,20) (20,12,13) (22,14,18)` -> upper `[null,null,24,24,24,22,26,26,26,24]`, lower `[null,null,10,12,8,8,8,10,12,12]`, middle `[null,null,17,18,16,15,17,18,19,18]`, zones `[none,none,lower,upper,lower,upper,upper,upper,lower,mid]` (4 upper, 3 lower, 1 mid). The closes are placed above, below and exactly on the channel midpoint so all three zones appear. `runLineDonchian` sorts the finite OHLC bars by x, runs the pipeline, and returns the upper + lower + middle series, per-bar `samples` classified `upper` (close above the midline) / `lower` (below) / `mid` (on it) -- `none` through the warm-up -- the upper/lower/mid counts, and the final band readings; `ok` is false for fewer than two bars. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs `<ChartLineKeltner>` / `<ChartLineBollinger>` / `<ChartLineEnvelope>` -- those centre their bands on a moving average and offset by ATR / standard deviation / a percent; the Donchian Channel takes the RAW rolling high and low extremes with no average and no volatility measure. vs `<ChartLineChandelier>` -- the Chandelier hangs ATR-offset stops from the rolling extreme; the Donchian plots the extreme itself as the band. vs `<ChartLineMinMax>` -- both track rolling extremes, but the Donchian pairs them into a channel with a midline and a filled band, framing the range as a trading structure. vs `<ChartLineGapo>` -- the GAPO reduces the same high-low range to a single log-ratio scalar; the Donchian keeps the bands as price-level lines. All exported helpers carry a `LineDonchian`/`Donchian` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineDonchianPoint[]` (`{x, high, low, close}`); `period` (the channel lookback; default 20, coerced to an integer >= 2); `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`priceColor`/`channelColor`/`middleColor`/`fillColor`/`upperZoneColor`/`lowerZoneColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showChannel`/`showMiddle`/`showChannelFill`/`showMarkers`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `channel` / `middle`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_DONCHIAN_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/PRICE_COLOR/CHANNEL_COLOR/MIDDLE_COLOR/FILL_COLOR/UPPER_ZONE_COLOR/LOWER_ZONE_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineDonchianZone` type (`upper`|`lower`|`mid`|`none`); `getLineDonchianFinitePoints` (keeps only bars with a finite x + high + low + close); `normalizeLineDonchianPeriod(period, fallback)` (coerce to an integer >= 2; non-finite or sub-2 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineDonchianUpper`/`computeLineDonchianLower(bars, period)` (the rolling highest-high / lowest-low band, null warm-up; non-array -> []); `computeLineDonchianMiddle(bars, period)` (the midpoint band; non-array -> []); `runLineDonchian(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, period, upper, lower, middle, samples, upperFinal, lowerFinal, middleFinal, upperCount, lowerCount, midCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, high, low, close, upper, lower, middle, zone}`; `computeLineDonchianLayout({data, period, ...})` -- overlays the price line, the three band lines and a closed `channelArea` fill polygon on a single panel (the y-domain spanning the price and both bands), projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `upperPath`, `lowerPath`, `middlePath`, `channelArea`, one `markers` entry per classified bar coloured by zone; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 bars -> ok=false; `describeLineDonchianChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Donchian Channel + highest high + lowest low + middle + the zone counts (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-donchian-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the upper / lower / middle band lines + every price dot + every marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-donchian / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -channel-area / -upper-path / -lower-path / -middle-path / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-close / -tooltip-upper / -tooltip-lower / -tooltip-middle / -tooltip-zone / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-upper-final`, `data-lower-final`, `data-middle-final`, `data-upper-count`, `data-lower-count`, `data-mid-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`; markers expose `data-zone`. The channel is drawn as a faint filled area between the solid upper and lower bands plus a dashed middle band; markers on the price are zone-coloured (green upper half, red lower half, amber on the midline). Tooltip on a price dot or a marker shows x, close, the upper / lower / middle bands and the zone. Config badge in top-left calls out `DC <period>`. The legend has three toggle items (Price / Channel / Middle). 61 vitest cases (getLineDonchianFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x/high/low/close bars** + non-array -> [] + empty -> [] + preserves order, normalizeLineDonchianPeriod incl. floors a fractional + **sub-2 / zero / NaN falls back** + the minimum of 2, computeLineDonchianUpper incl. non-array -> [] + null warm-up + **the highest high of the window** + **the exact upper band series** + matches length, computeLineDonchianLower incl. non-array -> [] + null warm-up + **the lowest low of the window** + **the exact lower band series** + matches length, computeLineDonchianMiddle incl. non-array -> [] + **the midpoint of the bands** + **the exact middle band series** + matches length, runLineDonchian incl. not-ok short series + ok + **carries the period** + **the default period** + **the exact upper/lower/middle series** + **zone classification against the midline** + one sample per bar + **self-consistent upper/lower/mid counts** + **the final band readings** + **the close stays within the channel bounds** + x-sorting, computeLineDonchianLayout incl. not-ok single bar + **collapsed canvas -> ok=false** + ok + **price/upper/lower/middle paths** + **a closed channel area path** + **the y-domain spanning price and bands** + **one marker per classified bar** + the total bars + the final readings, describeLineDonchianChart incl. **the indicator name** + highest high/lowest low + the zone counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / **empty state** / not-empty root / **root data-period/upper-final** / img-role svg / **the upper/lower/middle bands** / **the channel fill area** / **one marker per classified bar** / config badge `3` / three legend items / **legend toggle hides the channel** / **controlled hiddenSeries hides the middle band** / ref) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for the new files (conditional spreads honour `exactOptionalPropertyTypes`, `noUncheckedIndexedAccess` guarded). Exported via components/ui barrel.
