| 11.690 | UI: chart-line-median primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.708 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-median.tsx` ships `<ChartLineMedian>` -- a pure-SVG SINGLE-PANEL line chart with a **Median Price** overlay from the midpoint of the bar high and low (the close line and the Median Price line share one panel). **The computation:** the Median Price is the midpoint of each bar's high and low -- `(high + low) / 2`. Unlike the Typical Price or the Weighted Close it IGNORES the close entirely -- it is the pure geometric center of the bar's range. It works on an OHLC-like bar, so `ChartLineMedianPoint` carries `{x, high, low, close}` (the close feeds the overlaid close line, NOT the Median Price). `computeLineMedian(bars)` is the Median Price per bar; it reads ONLY the high and low, never the close; it is purely per-bar with NO lookback, so it is defined for every bar (a bar with a non-finite high or low yields null). Each bar is classified by where the Median Price sits relative to the close: `above` (median exceeds close -- the close finished in the LOWER half of its range), `below` (median under close -- the close finished in the UPPER half), `equal` (median matches close -- the close landed on the bar midpoint). **Worked fixture:** the Median Price is `(high + low) / 2`, an exact integer whenever the high and low share parity. The fixture uses 8 OHLC bars built that way -- bars `(h,l,c)`: `(20,10,15)(24,16,24)(30,14,14)(36,24,30)(40,28,40)(44,20,20)(50,38,50)(54,30,32)` -> median `[15,20,22,30,34,32,44,42]` (asserted with `toEqual`), zones `[equal,below,above,equal,below,above,below,above]`. The close sits level with the Median Price (bars 1,4), below it (bars 3,6,8) and above it (bars 2,5,7), so all three zones are exercised: 3 above, 3 below, 2 equal; final Median Price 42, final close 32. A dedicated test passes two bars with the SAME high and low but a DIFFERENT close and asserts an identical median, proving the Median Price is close-independent. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs `<ChartLineTypical>` (11.688) -- the Typical Price averages high, low and close in equal thirds (`(h+l+c)/3`); the Median Price drops the close term entirely. vs `<ChartLineWeightedClose>` (11.689) -- the Weighted Close gives the close 50% weight (`(h+l+2c)/4`); the Median Price gives it 0%. The three form a weight family: Median 0%, Typical 33%, Weighted Close 50% close-weight -- the Median Price is the only one that is purely a function of the bar range and is unaffected by where the close landed. vs `<ChartLineKeltner>` / `<ChartLineDonchian>` -- those band indicators bracket a range; the Median Price is the single center line of the high-low range. vs the `<ChartLineHeikinAshi>` average-bar -- that recursively blends prior bars; the Median Price is a stateless per-bar midpoint. All exported helpers carry a `LineMedian` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineMedianPoint[]` (`{x, high, low, close}`); the Median Price is parameter-free so there is NO period prop; `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`closeColor`/`medianColor`/`aboveColor`/`belowColor`/`equalColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showMedian`/`showMarkers`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `close` / `median`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_MEDIAN_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/CLOSE_COLOR/MEDIAN_COLOR/ABOVE_COLOR/BELOW_COLOR/EQUAL_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineMedianZone` type (`above`|`below`|`equal`|`none`); `getLineMedianFinitePoints` (keeps only bars with finite x + high + low + close); `computeLineMedian(bars)` -- the Median Price per bar, reading only high + low (non-array -> [], non-finite high/low -> null); `runLineMedian(data)` -- returns `{series, median, samples, medianFinal, closeFinal, aboveCount, belowCount, equalCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, high, low, close, median, zone}`; `computeLineMedianLayout({data, width, height, padding})` -- projects a single panel with the close line, the Median Price line overlaid, one `closeDots` entry per bar, one `markers` entry per bar with a defined Median Price coloured by zone, and the value domain spanning the close and Median Price; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 bars -> ok=false; `describeLineMedianChart(data)` -- ARIA summary mentions Median Price + midpoint + high + low + the zone counts (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-median-aria-desc"` span; the close line + the Median Price line + every close dot + every marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-median / -aria-desc / -svg / -empty / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -close-path / -dots / -dot / -median-path / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-high / -tooltip-low / -tooltip-close / -tooltip-median / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config); root mirrors `data-total-points`, `data-median-final`, `data-close-final`, `data-above-count`, `data-below-count`, `data-equal-count`, `data-animate`, `data-empty`; markers expose `data-median` and `data-zone`. The markers on the Median Price line are coloured by zone (red above the close, green below the close, slate level). Tooltip on a close dot or a marker shows x, high, low, close and the Median Price with its zone. Config badge in top-left calls out `HL/2`. The legend has two toggle items (Close / Median Price). 51 vitest cases (getLineMedianFinitePoints incl. **keeps only bars with finite x + high + low + close** + non-array -> [] + empty -> [] + preserves order, computeLineMedian incl. non-array -> [] + empty -> [] + **the midpoint of each bar high and low** + matches length + **null for a bar with a non-finite high or low** + **ignores the close entirely** + **equals the high and low when high equals low**, runLineMedian incl. not-ok short series + ok + **the exact Median Price series** + **zone classification of every bar** + one sample per bar + **carries high/low/close/median on each sample** + **above when median exceeds close** + **below when median is under close** + **equal when median matches close** + **self-consistent zone counts** + **the final Median Price + close** + x-sorting + empty/null -> ok=false, computeLineMedianLayout incl. not-ok single bar + **collapsed canvas -> ok=false** + ok + close/median paths + **one close dot per bar** + **one marker per bar with a defined Median Price** + **the value domain spans the close + Median Price** + **every marker inside the panel** + carries the run, describeLineMedianChart incl. **the indicator name** + **the midpoint of the high and low** + the zone counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / **empty state** / **root data-total-points/median-final/above-count** / img-role svg / **the close + Median Price lines** / **one marker per bar** / **the zone attribute on each marker** / config badge `HL/2` / **legend toggle hides the Median Price line** / **showMedian=false hides the Median Price line** / **controlled hiddenSeries hides the close line** / onPointClick on a marker / ref) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for the new files (`noUncheckedIndexedAccess` guarded). Exported via components/ui barrel. Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/c4-mgr-auto. Worker auto-w672. | 11.691 | UI: chart-line-elder-impulse primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.709 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-elder-impulse.tsx` ships `<ChartLineElderImpulse>` -- a pure-SVG SINGLE-PANEL line chart with an **Elder Impulse** overlay colouring each bar from the EMA slope and the MACD histogram. **The computation:** Alexander Elder's Impulse System colours each bar from two momentum readings -- the slope of an EMA of the close, and the slope of the MACD histogram. It is a close-only indicator, so `ChartLineElderImpulsePoint` carries `{x, value}`. `computeLineElderImpulseEma(values, period)` is an EMA seeded from the first value (`EMA[0]=value[0]`). `computeLineElderImpulse(values, options)` builds the impulse EMA (default period 13), the MACD line (fast EMA 12 minus slow EMA 26), the signal line (9-EMA of the MACD) and the histogram (MACD minus signal), then classifies each bar. `classifyLineElderImpulse(emaSlope, histSlope)` is the colouring rule: `green` when BOTH slopes rise, `red` when BOTH fall, `blue` when they disagree or either is flat, `none` when a slope is undefined. Because the EMA is seeded from the first value, the EMA and the MACD pipeline are defined from the FIRST bar and the impulse class is defined from the SECOND bar onward (the first bar has no prior slope, so it is `none`). **Worked fixture:** the classification is a pure function of two slope SIGNS and is tested exhaustively (green / red / blue / flat / null cases). For the integrated pipeline a FLAT series gives exact results -- the EMA stays flat, the fast EMA equals the slow EMA so the MACD is exactly zero, the signal is zero and the histogram is exactly zero; every slope is zero, so every bar after the first is `blue`. `[50,50,50,50,50,50]` -> `ema=[50,50,50,50,50,50]`, `macd=[0,0,0,0,0,0]`, `hist=[0,0,0,0,0,0]`, `impulse=[none,blue,blue,blue,blue,blue]` -- all asserted with `toEqual`. The EMA helper is checked exactly with a dyadic alpha: period 3 gives `alpha=2/4=0.5`, so `[4,8,16]` -> `[4,6,11]`. Rising and falling series are checked STRUCTURALLY -- a strictly increasing series has a strictly positive EMA slope on every bar (a weighted average of strictly smaller prior values is always below the current value), so no bar can be red; the test asserts `redCount===0` and `greenCount>=1` for an accelerating doubling series, and the mirror `greenCount===0`/`redCount>=1` for a falling series. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs `<ChartLineMacd>` -- the MACD chart plots the MACD line, signal and histogram in their own panel; the Elder Impulse FEEDS the MACD histogram slope into a per-bar colour and never plots the histogram itself. vs `<ChartLineSupertrend>` / `<ChartLineParabolicSar>` -- those flip a single trailing stop; the Elder Impulse is a three-state permission gauge (green=long allowed, red=short allowed, blue=either) requiring TWO indicators to agree. vs `<ChartLineHeikinAshi>` -- that recolours bars from a recursive average of OHLC; the Elder Impulse recolours from the agreement of an EMA trend and a MACD-histogram momentum. vs `<ChartLineCrossover>` -- a crossover marks a single EMA-cross event; the Elder Impulse is a continuous per-bar classification. All exported helpers carry a `LineElderImpulse` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineElderImpulsePoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `emaPeriod` (the impulse EMA lookback; default 13), `fastPeriod` (default 12), `slowPeriod` (default 26), `signalPeriod` (default 9), each coerced to an integer >= 1; `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`greenColor`/`redColor`/`blueColor`/`noneColor`/`emaColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showEma`/`showMarkers`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `ema`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_ELDER_IMPULSE_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/EMA_PERIOD/FAST_PERIOD/SLOW_PERIOD/SIGNAL_PERIOD/GREEN_COLOR/RED_COLOR/BLUE_COLOR/NONE_COLOR/EMA_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineElderImpulseClass` type (`green`|`red`|`blue`|`none`); `getLineElderImpulseFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value); `normalizeLineElderImpulsePeriod(period, fallback)` (coerce to an integer >= 1; non-finite or sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineElderImpulseEma(values, period)` (EMA seeded from the first value; non-array -> []); `classifyLineElderImpulse(emaSlope, histSlope)` (the colouring rule); `computeLineElderImpulse(values, options?)` -- returns `{ema, macd, signal, hist, impulse}`; `runLineElderImpulse(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, emaPeriod, fastPeriod, slowPeriod, signalPeriod, ema, macd, signal, hist, impulse, samples, impulseFinal, greenCount, redCount, blueCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, ema, hist, emaSlope, histSlope, impulse}`; `computeLineElderImpulseLayout({data, emaPeriod, ...})` -- projects a single panel with the price line drawn as one `segments` entry between each pair of bars (each segment coloured by its ENDING bar's impulse), one `markers` entry per bar coloured by impulse, and the `emaPath`; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLineElderImpulseChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Elder Impulse + EMA + MACD histogram + green/red/blue + the counts (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-elder-impulse-aria-desc"` span; the EMA line + every marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-elder-impulse / -aria-desc / -svg / -empty / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -price-segments / -segment / -ema-path / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-ema / -tooltip-hist / -tooltip-impulse / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -impulse-key / -impulse-key-item / -impulse-key-swatch / -impulse-key-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config); root mirrors `data-ema-period`, `data-fast-period`, `data-slow-period`, `data-signal-period`, `data-green-count`, `data-red-count`, `data-blue-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-impulse-final`, `data-animate`, `data-empty`; segments + markers expose `data-impulse`. The price line renders as impulse-coloured segments (green/red/blue, grey for the warm-up bar) with one coloured marker per bar; the impulse EMA is overlaid as a dashed violet line. Tooltip on a marker shows x, price, EMA, MACD histogram and the impulse class. Config badge in top-left calls out `EI <emaPeriod>`. The legend has two toggle items (Price / EMA) plus a three-swatch impulse colour key (Green / Red / Blue) and a count stats span. 69 vitest cases (getLineElderImpulseFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + value** + non-array -> [] + empty -> [] + preserves order, normalizeLineElderImpulsePeriod incl. floors a fractional + **zero / negative / NaN falls back** + the minimum of 1, computeLineElderImpulseEma incl. non-array -> [] + **seeds from the first value** + **the exact dyadic EMA for a period-3 alpha of one half** + **keeps a constant series constant** + matches length + rises with a rising series, classifyLineElderImpulse incl. **green when both slopes rise** + **red when both slopes fall** + **blue when the slopes disagree** + **blue when either slope is flat** + **none when either slope is null**, computeLineElderImpulse incl. non-array -> empty arrays + **keeps the EMA of a constant series constant** + **zero MACD for a constant series** + **zero MACD histogram for a constant series** + **the first bar none** + **a flat series blue after the first bar** + matches impulse length, runLineElderImpulse incl. not-ok short series + ok + **the default periods** + **custom periods** + **the flat EMA for a constant series** + **the zero histogram** + **the blue classification for a constant series** + **self-consistent counts** + **the final impulse class** + one sample per point with the first none + **a strictly positive EMA slope on a rising series** + **green bars + no red bars on a rising series** + **red bars + no green bars on a falling series** + x-sorting, computeLineElderImpulseLayout incl. not-ok single point + **collapsed canvas -> ok=false** + ok + **one segment between each pair of bars** + **one marker per bar** + **colours each segment by its ending bar impulse** + the EMA path + **every marker inside the panel** + carries the run, describeLineElderImpulseChart incl. **the indicator name** + **the EMA + MACD histogram** + the counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / **empty state** / **root data-ema-period/blue-count/impulse-final** / img-role svg / **one coloured segment between each pair of bars** / **the impulse EMA line** / **one marker per bar** / **the impulse class attribute on each marker** / config badge `EI 13` / **the three-swatch impulse colour key** / **legend toggle hides the price segments** / **showEma=false hides the EMA line** / onPointClick on a marker / ref) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. | 11.692 | UI: chart-line-sine-weighted primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.710 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-sine-weighted.tsx` ships `<ChartLineSineWeighted>` -- a pure-SVG SINGLE-PANEL line chart with a **Sine Weighted Moving Average** (SWMA) overlay weighting the lookback window by a half sine cycle (the price line and the SWMA line share one panel). **The computation:** the SWMA weights its lookback window by a HALF SINE cycle -- the weight for window slot k is `sin((k + 1) * pi / (period + 1))`, k = 0..period-1. The weights climb from a small value at the oldest slot to a PEAK in the MIDDLE of the window and taper back down at the newest slot; the weighting is symmetric (`weight[k] = weight[period-1-k]`, since `sin(x) = sin(pi - x)`), so the SWMA emphasizes the CENTER of the window and de-emphasizes both the oldest AND the newest values. It is a close-only indicator, so `ChartLineSineWeightedPoint` carries `{x, value}`. `computeLineSineWeightedWeights(period)` is the raw half-sine weight set; `computeLineSineWeighted(values, period)` is the SWMA itself -- the weighted average of each full window, `sum(weight[k] * value[k]) / sum(weight)`, defined once a full window is available (a window with a non-finite value yields null). Each defined bar is classified by where the SWMA sits relative to the price: `above` (SWMA exceeds price), `below`, `equal`. **Worked fixture:** the sine weights are transcendental, but two cases collapse to exact arithmetic. PERIOD 1: the single weight is `sin(pi/2) = 1` exactly (IEEE754 `Math.sin(Math.PI/2) === 1`), so the SWMA is the IDENTITY -- `computeLineSineWeighted([5,10,15], 1)` is `[5,10,15]`, asserted with `toEqual`. PERIOD 2: the two weights are `sin(pi/3)` and `sin(2*pi/3)` -- both `sqrt(3)/2`, so they are EQUAL and the SWMA collapses to the simple two-bar average `(v[i-1] + v[i]) / 2`; the fixture `[10,20,14,30,22,40]` gives `[null,15,17,22,26,31]`, checked with `toBeCloseTo`, zones `[none,below,above,below,above,below]` (the values never have an equal consecutive pair so every bar is unambiguous: 2 above, 3 below). The weight shape is checked structurally -- every weight is positive, the set is symmetric about the center, the middle slot carries the largest weight, and the center slot of an odd window has a unit weight (`sin(pi/2)`). The classification is a pure function tested directly (above / below / equal / none). **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs `<ChartLineWma>` -- the linear Weighted Moving Average ramps the weights UP toward the newest value (newest-heavy, lagging less); the SWMA peaks in the MIDDLE, so it is a symmetric, centered, low-lag-asymmetry smoother. vs `<ChartLineMovingAvg>` / a plain SMA -- the SMA gives every slot an EQUAL weight; the SWMA tapers the window edges with a sine bell. vs `<ChartLineHma>` / `<ChartLineGaussian>`-style smoothers -- those compose or convolve to cut lag; the SWMA is a single direct half-sine convolution. vs `<ChartLineTriangular>` / TRIMA -- a triangular MA also peaks in the middle but with LINEAR ramps; the SWMA uses a SINE bell, a smoother taper. All exported helpers carry a `LineSineWeighted` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineSineWeightedPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `period` (the lookback window; default 14, coerced to an integer >= 1); `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`priceColor`/`swmaColor`/`aboveColor`/`belowColor`/`equalColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showSwma`/`showMarkers`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `swma`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_SINE_WEIGHTED_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/PRICE_COLOR/SWMA_COLOR/ABOVE_COLOR/BELOW_COLOR/EQUAL_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineSineWeightedZone` type (`above`|`below`|`equal`|`none`); `getLineSineWeightedFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value); `normalizeLineSineWeightedPeriod(period, fallback)` (coerce to an integer >= 1; non-finite or sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineSineWeightedWeights(period)` (the raw half-sine weights, length = period); `computeLineSineWeighted(values, period)` (the SWMA, null through the warm-up; non-array -> []); `classifyLineSineWeightedZone(swma, value)` (the above/below/equal/none rule); `runLineSineWeighted(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, period, weights, swma, samples, swmaFinal, aboveCount, belowCount, equalCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, swma, zone}`; `computeLineSineWeightedLayout({data, period, ...})` -- projects a single panel with the price line, the SWMA line overlaid, one `priceDots` entry per bar, one `markers` entry per bar with a defined SWMA coloured by zone, and the value domain spanning the price and SWMA; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLineSineWeightedChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Sine Weighted Moving Average + half sine + window + the zone counts (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-sine-weighted-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the SWMA line + every price dot + every marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-sine-weighted / -aria-desc / -svg / -empty / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -swma-path / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-swma / -tooltip-zone / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-swma-final`, `data-above-count`, `data-below-count`, `data-equal-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`, `data-empty`; markers expose `data-swma` and `data-zone`. The markers on the SWMA line are coloured by zone (red SWMA above price, green SWMA below price, slate level). Tooltip on a price dot or a marker shows x, price, the SWMA and the zone. Config badge in top-left calls out `SWMA <period>`. The legend has two toggle items (Price / SWMA). 69 vitest cases (getLineSineWeightedFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + value** + non-array -> [] + empty -> [] + preserves order, normalizeLineSineWeightedPeriod incl. floors a fractional + **zero / negative / NaN falls back** + the minimum of 1, computeLineSineWeightedWeights incl. **one weight per slot** + **a single unit weight for period one** + **every weight positive** + **symmetric about the centre** + **peaks at the middle** + **the centre slot of an odd window a unit weight** + **a period-2 window two equal weights**, computeLineSineWeighted incl. non-array -> [] + **the identity for period one** + matches length + **the warm-up window null** + **collapses to the simple two-bar average for period two** + **all null when the period exceeds the series length** + **keeps a constant series at its constant level** + **null for a window with a non-finite value** + **one value per full window**, classifyLineSineWeightedZone incl. **above / below / equal** + **none for a null SWMA** + **none for a non-finite SWMA**, runLineSineWeighted incl. not-ok short series + ok + **the default period 14** + **a custom period** + **one weight per window slot** + **the warm-up SWMA null** + **collapses to the two-bar average for period two** + **zone classification of every bar** + **self-consistent zone counts** + **the final SWMA** + one sample per point + x-sorting + empty/null -> ok=false, computeLineSineWeightedLayout incl. not-ok single point + **collapsed canvas -> ok=false** + ok + price/swma paths + **one price dot per bar** + **one marker per bar with a defined SWMA** + **every marker inside the panel** + carries the run, describeLineSineWeightedChart incl. **the indicator name** + **the half sine window weighting** + the zone counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / **empty state** / **root data-period/above-count/total-points** / img-role svg / **the price + SWMA lines** / **one marker per bar with a defined SWMA** / **the zone attribute on each marker** / config badge `SWMA 2` / **legend toggle hides the SWMA line** / **showSwma=false hides the SWMA line** / **controlled hiddenSeries hides the price line** / onPointClick on a marker / ref) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. | 11.693 | UI: chart-line-geometric-ma primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.711 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-geometric-ma.tsx` ships `<ChartLineGeometricMa>` -- a pure-SVG SINGLE-PANEL line chart with a **Geometric Moving Average** (GMA) overlay from the rolling geometric mean of prices (the price line and the GMA line share one panel). **The computation:** the Geometric Moving Average is the rolling geometric mean -- for each window of `period` values it is the period-th ROOT of their PRODUCT. It is a close-only indicator, so `ChartLineGeometricMaPoint` carries `{x, value}`. `computeLineGeometricMa(values, period)` computes the GMA in LOG space as `exp(mean(ln(window)))` -- the period-th root of the product, evaluated as the exponential of the average log so a long window cannot overflow a direct product. The geometric mean needs strictly POSITIVE inputs, so a window with a non-finite OR non-positive value yields null; a period of 1 is the identity (the 1st root of a value is the value). Each defined bar is classified by where the GMA sits relative to the price: `above` (GMA exceeds price), `below`, `equal`. The geometric mean has a defining property -- by the AM-GM inequality it always sits AT OR BELOW the arithmetic mean of the same window, with equality only when every value is equal; it is pulled down by low values, so it dampens upside spikes more than a simple moving average. **Worked fixture:** the geometric mean involves roots and is transcendental, but two cases collapse to clean arithmetic. PERIOD 1: the identity -- `computeLineGeometricMa([5,10,15], 1)` is `[5,10,15]`, asserted with `toEqual`. PERIOD 2: the GMA is `sqrt(v[i-1] * v[i])`; the fixture `[4,9,4,25,4,49]` is built so every consecutive product is a PERFECT SQUARE, so the GMA lands on an exact integer -- `[null,6,6,10,10,14]`, checked with `toBeCloseTo`, zones `[none,below,above,below,above,below]` (the values never have an equal consecutive pair so every bar is unambiguous: 2 above, 3 below). Structural checks: the geometric mean of a geometric progression is its MIDDLE term (`[1,2,4]` -> cube root of 8 = 2); a constant series stays at its constant level; the GMA never exceeds the arithmetic mean of its window (AM-GM, asserted with `toBeLessThanOrEqual`); a window with a negative or a zero value yields null. The classification is a pure function tested directly (above / below / equal / none). **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs `<ChartLineMovingAvg>` / a simple SMA -- the SMA is the ARITHMETIC mean; the GMA is the GEOMETRIC mean (Nth root of the product), always at or below the SMA, and it averages RATIOS rather than levels so equal up/down percentage moves cancel. vs `<ChartLineSineWeighted>` (11.692) / `<ChartLineWma>` -- those are still arithmetic means, just re-weighted; the GMA changes the MEAN itself from additive to multiplicative. vs `<ChartLineEwma>` -- the EWMA is an arithmetic exponential mean; the GMA is an unweighted multiplicative mean over a fixed window. The GMA is the natural average for compounding/return data, and uniquely among the recent MA family it REQUIRES positive prices. All exported helpers carry a `LineGeometricMa` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineGeometricMaPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `period` (the lookback window; default 14, coerced to an integer >= 1); `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`priceColor`/`gmaColor`/`aboveColor`/`belowColor`/`equalColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showGma`/`showMarkers`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `gma`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_GEOMETRIC_MA_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/PRICE_COLOR/GMA_COLOR/ABOVE_COLOR/BELOW_COLOR/EQUAL_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineGeometricMaZone` type (`above`|`below`|`equal`|`none`); `getLineGeometricMaFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value); `normalizeLineGeometricMaPeriod(period, fallback)` (coerce to an integer >= 1; non-finite or sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineGeometricMa(values, period)` (the GMA, null through the warm-up and for a non-positive window; period 1 is the identity; non-array -> []); `classifyLineGeometricMaZone(gma, value)` (the above/below/equal/none rule); `runLineGeometricMa(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, period, gma, samples, gmaFinal, aboveCount, belowCount, equalCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, gma, zone}`; `computeLineGeometricMaLayout({data, period, ...})` -- projects a single panel with the price line, the GMA line overlaid, one `priceDots` entry per bar, one `markers` entry per bar with a defined GMA coloured by zone, and the value domain spanning the price and GMA; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLineGeometricMaChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Geometric Moving Average + geometric mean + product/root + positive prices + the zone counts (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-geometric-ma-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the GMA line + every price dot + every marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-geometric-ma / -aria-desc / -svg / -empty / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -gma-path / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-gma / -tooltip-zone / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-gma-final`, `data-above-count`, `data-below-count`, `data-equal-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`, `data-empty`; markers expose `data-gma` and `data-zone`. The markers on the GMA line are coloured by zone (red GMA above price, green GMA below price, slate level). Tooltip on a price dot or a marker shows x, price, the GMA and the zone. Config badge in top-left calls out `GMA <period>`. The legend has two toggle items (Price / GMA). 63 vitest cases (getLineGeometricMaFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + value** + non-array -> [] + empty -> [] + preserves order, normalizeLineGeometricMaPeriod incl. floors a fractional + **zero / negative / NaN falls back** + the minimum of 1, computeLineGeometricMa incl. non-array -> [] + **the identity for period one** + matches length + **the warm-up window null** + **the square root of the product for period two** + **the middle term for a geometric progression** + **keeps a constant series at its constant level** + **never exceeds the arithmetic mean of the window** + **all null when the period exceeds the series length** + **null for a window with a negative value** + **null for a window with a zero value**, classifyLineGeometricMaZone incl. **above / below / equal** + **none for a null GMA** + **none for a non-finite GMA**, runLineGeometricMa incl. not-ok short series + ok + **the default period 14** + **a custom period** + **the warm-up GMA null** + **the square root of the product for period two** + **zone classification of every bar** + **self-consistent zone counts** + **the final GMA** + one sample per point + x-sorting + empty/null -> ok=false, computeLineGeometricMaLayout incl. not-ok single point + **collapsed canvas -> ok=false** + ok + price/gma paths + **one price dot per bar** + **one marker per bar with a defined GMA** + **every marker inside the panel** + carries the run, describeLineGeometricMaChart incl. **the indicator name** + **the geometric mean + positive prices** + the zone counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / **empty state** / **root data-period/above-count/total-points** / img-role svg / **the price + GMA lines** / **one marker per bar with a defined GMA** / **the zone attribute on each marker** / config badge `GMA 2` / **legend toggle hides the GMA line** / **showGma=false hides the GMA line** / **controlled hiddenSeries hides the price line** / onPointClick on a marker / ref) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for the new files (conditional spreads honour `exactOptionalPropertyTypes`, `noUncheckedIndexedAccess` guarded). Exported via components/ui barrel. | 11.694 | UI: chart-line-rainbow primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.712 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-rainbow.tsx` ships `<ChartLineRainbow>` -- a pure-SVG SINGLE-PANEL line chart with a **Rainbow Moving Average** overlay of recursively smoothed average bands (the price line and the rainbow fan share one panel). **The computation:** the Rainbow Moving Average is a FAN of recursively smoothed bands -- the first band is a simple moving average of the price, and every NEXT band is a simple moving average of the band BEFORE it. Each successive band is smoother and lags more, so plotted together they fan out like a rainbow (wide apart in a strong trend, tightly tangled during consolidation). It is a close-only indicator, so `ChartLineRainbowPoint` carries `{x, value}`. `computeLineRainbowSma(arr, period)` is the windowed simple moving average that drives the recursion (a window touching a null slot yields null). `computeLineRainbowBands(values, period, bandCount)` is the recursive fan: band 0 is `SMA(prices)`, band k is `SMA(band k-1)` -- returns one array per band, each successive band warming up one window later. `classifyLineRainbowZone(value, low, high)` is where the price sits relative to the FULL rainbow envelope (the min..max of every band at that bar): `above` (price tops the envelope), `below`, `inside`, `none` (until every band is defined). `getLineRainbowBandColor(index, bandCount)` walks a red-to-violet hue ramp (`hsl(hue, 72%, 52%)`, hue 0..270) so the bands plot as a rainbow. **Worked fixture:** a simple moving average of an arithmetic progression over an ODD window is its CENTRE value, so a 3-period SMA of a linear ramp is the ramp lagged exactly one bar -- and every recursive band lags one more bar than the band before it. The fixture is a 10-bar linear ramp `[10,20,...,100]` read with period 3 and 3 bands, so every band lands on an EXACT integer (each /3 average is of three consecutive multiples of 10): band 0 `[.,.,20,30,40,50,60,70,80,90]`, band 1 `[.,.,.,.,30,40,50,60,70,80]`, band 2 `[.,.,.,.,.,.,40,50,60,70]` -- all three asserted with `toEqual`, and the recursion itself checked (band 1 equals `SMA(band 0)`, band 2 equals `SMA(band 1)`). The ramp rises faster than the bands lag, so once the full rainbow is defined the price runs ABOVE it: zones `[none x6, above x4]` (4 above, 0 below, 0 inside). A constant series collapses every band to the same level, so the price runs INSIDE the rainbow. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs `<ChartLineGmma>` -- the Guppy Multiple Moving Average plots two GROUPS of EMAs each taken directly off the price; the Rainbow takes each band off the PREVIOUS band, a single recursive chain. vs `<ChartLineBollinger>` / `<ChartLineEnvelope>` -- those are a single average plus two offset bands; the Rainbow is many genuine moving averages of progressively deeper smoothing. vs `<ChartLineMovingAvg>` / `<ChartLineEwma>` -- those are one smoother; the Rainbow stacks `bandCount` of them recursively to read trend STRENGTH from the fan spread. The recursive (SMA-of-SMA) construction is what makes it a rainbow rather than a multi-MA bundle. All exported helpers carry a `LineRainbow` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineRainbowPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `period` (the SMA lookback for each band; default 2) and `bandCount` (the number of bands; default 6), both coerced to an integer >= 1; `strokeWidth`/`bandStrokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`priceColor`/`aboveColor`/`belowColor`/`insideColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showRainbow`/`showMarkers`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `rainbow`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_RAINBOW_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/BAND_STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/BAND_COUNT/PRICE_COLOR/ABOVE_COLOR/BELOW_COLOR/INSIDE_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineRainbowZone` type (`above`|`below`|`inside`|`none`); `getLineRainbowFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value); `normalizeLineRainbowInt(value, fallback)` (coerce to an integer >= 1; non-finite or sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineRainbowSma(arr, period)` (the windowed mean; non-array -> []); `computeLineRainbowBands(values, period, bandCount)` (the recursive fan; non-array -> []); `classifyLineRainbowZone(value, low, high)` (the above/below/inside/none rule); `getLineRainbowBandColor(index, bandCount)` (the hue ramp); `runLineRainbow(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, period, bandCount, bands, samples, aboveCount, belowCount, insideCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, envelopeLow, envelopeHigh, zone}`; `computeLineRainbowLayout({data, period, bandCount, ...})` -- projects a single panel with the price line, one `bandPaths` entry per band (each with its rainbow colour), one `markers` entry per classified bar coloured by zone, and the value domain spanning the price and every band; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLineRainbowChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Rainbow Moving Average + recursively smoothed + bands + the zone counts (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-rainbow-aria-desc"` span; the price line + every marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-rainbow / -aria-desc / -svg / -empty / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -bands / -band / -price-path / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-envelope / -tooltip-zone / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-band-count`, `data-above-count`, `data-below-count`, `data-inside-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`, `data-empty`; each band path carries `data-band-index`, markers expose `data-zone`. The bands draw as a red-to-violet rainbow fan behind the dark price line; markers on the price line are coloured by zone (green above the rainbow, red below, slate inside). Tooltip on a marker shows x, price, the rainbow envelope range and the zone. Config badge in top-left calls out `RB <period>/<bandCount>`. The legend has two toggle items (Price / Rainbow). 74 vitest cases (getLineRainbowFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + value** + non-array -> [] + empty -> [] + preserves order, normalizeLineRainbowInt incl. floors a fractional + **zero / negative / NaN falls back** + the minimum of 1, computeLineRainbowSma incl. non-array -> [] + the warm-up null + **the simple moving average of the window** + matches length + **null for a window touching a null slot**, computeLineRainbowBands incl. non-array -> [] + **one array per band** + **the first band the SMA of the prices** + **the second band the SMA of the first** + **the third band the SMA of the second** + **recursively smooths -- band k is the SMA of band k-1** + matches length + **keeps a constant series constant across every band** + **lags each band one more bar than the band before it**, classifyLineRainbowZone incl. **above / below / inside** + **none for a null low / null high** + **none for a non-finite bound**, getLineRainbowBandColor incl. **an hsl colour string** + **the first band at hue zero** + **the last band at hue 270** + **different bands different colours**, runLineRainbow incl. not-ok short series + ok + **the default period 2 + band count 6** + **custom period + band count** + **one array per band** + one sample per point + **zone classification of every bar** + **runs the price above the rising rainbow** + **the envelope null through the warm-up** + **the envelope once the full rainbow exists** + **a constant series inside the rainbow** + x-sorting + empty/null -> ok=false, computeLineRainbowLayout incl. not-ok single point + **collapsed canvas -> ok=false** + ok + the price path + **one band path per band** + **a colour on each band path** + **one marker per classified bar** + **every marker inside the panel** + carries the run, describeLineRainbowChart incl. **the indicator name** + **the recursively smoothed bands** + the zone counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / **empty state** / **root data-period/band-count/above-count** / img-role svg / the price line / **one band path per band** / **one marker per classified bar** / **the zone attribute on each marker** / config badge `RB 3/3` / **legend toggle hides the rainbow bands** / **showRainbow=false hides the bands** / **controlled hiddenSeries hides the price line** / onPointClick on a marker / ref) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. | 11.695 | UI: chart-line-pascal primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.713 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-pascal.tsx` ships `<ChartLinePascal>` -- a pure-SVG SINGLE-PANEL line chart with a **Pascal Triangle Moving Average** (PMA) overlay weighting the lookback window by binomial coefficients (the price line and the PMA line share one panel). **The computation:** the Pascal Triangle Moving Average weights its lookback window by the BINOMIAL COEFFICIENTS of a Pascal's triangle row -- for a window of `period` values the weights are `C(period - 1, k)`, k = 0..period-1. Those coefficients form a symmetric bell that peaks in the MIDDLE of the window and approaches a Gaussian as the window grows. It is a close-only indicator, so `ChartLinePascalPoint` carries `{x, value}`. `computeLinePascalWeights(period)` is row `period - 1` of Pascal's triangle, built by the ADDITIVE Pascal recurrence (`row[k] = prev[k-1] + prev[k]`) so every weight is an exact integer. `computeLinePascal(values, period)` is the PMA -- the binomial-weighted average of each full window, `sum(weight[k] * value[k]) / 2^(period-1)`; the row sums to `2^(period-1)`, a POWER OF TWO, so dividing by it is bit-exact (a window with a non-finite value yields null; a period of 1 is the identity). Each defined bar is classified by where the PMA sits relative to the price: `above` (PMA exceeds price), `below`, `equal`. **Worked fixture:** the Pascal MA is the rare moving average that is FULLY EXACT -- integer binomial weights and a power-of-two normalizer mean dividing the weighted sum by `2^(period-1)` introduces no rounding, so the WHOLE PMA series is asserted with `toEqual` (not `toBeCloseTo`). The fixture is an 8-bar series read with period 3; the period-3 weights are `[1,2,1]` and the normalizer is 4, so the PMA is `(v[i-2] + 2*v[i-1] + v[i]) / 4`. The values `[16,16,16,32,8,24,40,8]` are picked so every weighted sum is a multiple of 4 -- the PMA is `[null,null,16,20,22,18,24,28]`, zones `[none,none,equal,below,above,below,below,above]` (2 above, 3 below, 1 equal). A separate test checks a DYADIC FRACTION (`(10 + 2*10 + 11)/4 = 10.25`) to show the power-of-two division is exact for non-integer results too. The weight rows are checked directly: `[1]`, `[1,1]`, `[1,2,1]`, `[1,3,3,1]`, `[1,4,6,4,1]`; the rows are symmetric and sum to a power of two. A period of 1 is the identity, and a constant series stays at its constant level. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs `<ChartLineSineWeighted>` (11.692) -- both are symmetric center-weighted smoothers, but the sine-weighted uses a half-sine bell (transcendental, `toBeCloseTo`) whereas the Pascal uses binomial integer weights (a Gaussian-approaching bell, fully exact). vs `<ChartLineWma>` -- the linear WMA ramps weights toward the newest value (newest-heavy, asymmetric); the Pascal bell is symmetric. vs `<ChartLineMovingAvg>` / a flat SMA -- the SMA weights every slot equally; the Pascal tapers the window edges with the binomial curve. vs a triangular MA (TRIMA) -- a triangular MA also peaks in the middle but with LINEAR ramps; the Pascal uses the binomial curve, a smoother near-Gaussian taper. The binomial / power-of-two construction is what makes the Pascal MA uniquely exact among the recent MA family. All exported helpers carry a `LinePascal` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLinePascalPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `period` (the lookback window; default 5, coerced to an integer >= 1); `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`priceColor`/`pmaColor`/`aboveColor`/`belowColor`/`equalColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showPma`/`showMarkers`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `pma`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_PASCAL_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/PRICE_COLOR/PMA_COLOR/ABOVE_COLOR/BELOW_COLOR/EQUAL_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLinePascalZone` type (`above`|`below`|`equal`|`none`); `getLinePascalFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value); `normalizeLinePascalPeriod(period, fallback)` (coerce to an integer >= 1; non-finite or sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLinePascalWeights(period)` (the binomial weight row, length = period, all integers); `computeLinePascal(values, period)` (the PMA, null through the warm-up; non-array -> []); `classifyLinePascalZone(pma, value)` (the above/below/equal/none rule); `runLinePascal(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, period, weights, pma, samples, pmaFinal, aboveCount, belowCount, equalCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, pma, zone}`; `computeLinePascalLayout({data, period, ...})` -- projects a single panel with the price line, the PMA line overlaid, one `priceDots` entry per bar, one `markers` entry per bar with a defined PMA coloured by zone, and the value domain spanning the price and PMA; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLinePascalChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Pascal Triangle Moving Average + binomial coefficients + window + the zone counts (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-pascal-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the PMA line + every price dot + every marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-pascal / -aria-desc / -svg / -empty / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -pma-path / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-pma / -tooltip-zone / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-pma-final`, `data-above-count`, `data-below-count`, `data-equal-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`, `data-empty`; markers expose `data-pma` and `data-zone`. The markers on the PMA line are coloured by zone (red PMA above price, green PMA below price, slate level). Tooltip on a price dot or a marker shows x, price, the PMA and the zone. Config badge in top-left calls out `PMA <period>`. The legend has two toggle items (Price / PMA). 71 vitest cases (getLinePascalFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + value** + non-array -> [] + empty -> [] + preserves order, normalizeLinePascalPeriod incl. floors a fractional + **zero / negative / NaN falls back** + the minimum of 1, computeLinePascalWeights incl. **a single unit weight for period one** + **row one for period two** + **row two for period three** + **row three for period four** + **row four for period five** + **one weight per slot** + **symmetric about the centre** + **sums to a power of two**, computeLinePascal incl. non-array -> [] + **the identity for period one** + matches length + **the warm-up window null** + **the exact binomial weighted average for period three** + **divides by a power of two exactly, including dyadic fractions** + **keeps a constant series at its constant level** + **all null when the period exceeds the series length** + **null for a window with a non-finite value** + **one value per full window**, classifyLinePascalZone incl. **above / below / equal** + **none for a null PMA** + **none for a non-finite PMA**, runLinePascal incl. not-ok short series + ok + **the default period 5** + **a custom period** + **the binomial weights** + **the warm-up PMA null** + **the exact PMA series** + **zone classification of every bar** + **self-consistent zone counts** + **the final PMA** + one sample per point + x-sorting + empty/null -> ok=false, computeLinePascalLayout incl. not-ok single point + **collapsed canvas -> ok=false** + ok + price/pma paths + **one price dot per bar** + **one marker per bar with a defined PMA** + **every marker inside the panel** + carries the run, describeLinePascalChart incl. **the indicator name** + **the binomial coefficient weighting** + the zone counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / **empty state** / **root data-period/above-count/total-points** / img-role svg / **the price + PMA lines** / **one marker per bar with a defined PMA** / **the zone attribute on each marker** / config badge `PMA 3` / **legend toggle hides the PMA line** / **showPma=false hides the PMA line** / **controlled hiddenSeries hides the price line** / onPointClick on a marker / ref) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. | 11.696 | UI: chart-line-stoch-rsi primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.714 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-stoch-rsi.tsx` ships `<ChartLineStochRsi>` -- a pure-SVG TWO-PANEL line chart with a **Stochastic RSI** panel applying the stochastic formula to the RSI series (the price panel on top, the Stochastic RSI panel below on a FIXED 0..100 scale). **The computation:** the Stochastic RSI applies the stochastic oscillator formula to the RSI series rather than to the price -- it measures where the current RSI sits within its OWN recent high-low range, an "indicator of an indicator" far more sensitive than the plain RSI. It is a close-only indicator, so `ChartLineStochRsiPoint` carries `{x, value}`. `computeLineStochRsiRsi(closes, rsiPeriod)` is Wilder's RSI of the close series. `computeLineStochRsiStoch(values, stochPeriod)` applies the stochastic oscillator formula to ANY array -- `100 * (value - lowest) / (highest - lowest)` over the trailing window; a window touching a non-finite slot, or a FLAT window (highest equals lowest), yields null. `computeLineStochRsi(closes, rsiPeriod, stochPeriod)` composes the two: the stochastic of the RSI. Each defined bar is classified against the thresholds: `overbought` above the upper, `oversold` below the lower, `neutral` between, `none` while undefined. **Worked fixture:** the Wilder RSI is transcendental for a mixed series, but the pieces have exact anchors. THE STOCHASTIC FORMULA is exactly testable -- the fixture `[20,60,40,80,0,25]` with a 3-slot window gives `[null,null,50,100,0,31.25]`, asserted with `toEqual`; the newest value topping the window reads 100, the newest at the window low reads 0. THE RSI has monotone anchors -- a strictly RISING series has no losses so the RSI pins at exactly 100, a strictly FALLING one pins at 0 (both asserted with `toEqual`). THE COMPOSED PIPELINE: a strictly rising price pins the RSI at 100, a flat RSI window has no stochastic, so the Stochastic RSI is ALL NULL for a trending series -- an exact end-to-end anchor. The run / component use a choppy fixture and are checked structurally: every Stochastic RSI reading is null or within 0..100, every zone is valid, the counts are self-consistent. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs `<ChartLineRsi>` -- the plain RSI measures the price's own momentum; the Stochastic RSI runs the stochastic on TOP of the RSI, normalising it to where it sits in its own range, so it cycles overbought/oversold far more often. vs `<ChartLineStochastic>` -- the classic Stochastic runs the formula on the PRICE's high-low range; the Stochastic RSI runs the same formula on the RSI series instead. vs `<ChartLineDerivativeOsc>` -- the Derivative Oscillator double-SMOOTHS the RSI then subtracts a signal; the Stochastic RSI does not smooth -- it stochastic-normalises the raw RSI. The defining trait: a trending market pins the RSI flat, so the Stochastic RSI is genuinely UNDEFINED there (null), not pinned at an extreme. All exported helpers carry a `LineStochRsi` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineStochRsiPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `rsiPeriod` (the RSI lookback; default 14) and `stochPeriod` (the stochastic lookback; default 14), each coerced to an integer >= 1; `upperThreshold` (the overbought level; default 80) and `lowerThreshold` (the oversold level; default 20), accepted when finite and in (0,100); `pricePanelRatio` (default 0.55, clamped to [0.3, 0.8]); `gap`; `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`priceColor`/`stochColor`/`overboughtColor`/`oversoldColor`/`neutralColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showStoch`/`showThresholds`/`showMarkers`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `stoch`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_STOCH_RSI_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/RSI_PERIOD/STOCH_PERIOD/UPPER_THRESHOLD/LOWER_THRESHOLD/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/PRICE_COLOR/STOCH_COLOR/OVERBOUGHT_COLOR/OVERSOLD_COLOR/NEUTRAL_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineStochRsiZone` type (`overbought`|`oversold`|`neutral`|`none`); `getLineStochRsiFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value); `normalizeLineStochRsiPeriod(period, fallback)` (coerce to an integer >= 1; non-finite or sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineStochRsiRsi(closes, rsiPeriod)` (Wilder's RSI, null through the warm-up; non-array -> []); `computeLineStochRsiStoch(values, stochPeriod)` (the stochastic formula, null for a flat window or a non-finite window; non-array -> []); `computeLineStochRsi(closes, rsiPeriod, stochPeriod)` (the composed StochRSI); `classifyLineStochRsiZone(stochRsi, upper, lower)` (the overbought/oversold/neutral/none rule); `runLineStochRsi(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, rsiPeriod, stochPeriod, upperThreshold, lowerThreshold, rsi, stochRsi, samples, stochRsiFinal, overboughtCount, oversoldCount, neutralCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, rsi, stochRsi, zone}`; `computeLineStochRsiLayout({data, rsiPeriod, stochPeriod, ...})` -- stacks the price panel above the Stochastic RSI panel (the StochRSI panel y-domain FIXED to [0,100]), projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `stochPath`, one `markers` entry per defined bar coloured by zone, and `upperY` / `lowerY` for the threshold lines; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLineStochRsiChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Stochastic RSI + stochastic + RSI + the zone counts (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-stoch-rsi-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the Stochastic RSI line + every price dot + every marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-stoch-rsi / -aria-desc / -svg / -empty / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -panel-label / -thresholds / -threshold-line / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -stoch-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-rsi / -tooltip-stoch / -tooltip-zone / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config); root mirrors `data-rsi-period`, `data-stoch-period`, `data-upper-threshold`, `data-lower-threshold`, `data-stoch-rsi-final`, `data-overbought-count`, `data-oversold-count`, `data-neutral-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`, `data-empty`; grid / axis / tick / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"stoch"`; threshold lines carry `data-level="upper"|"lower"`; markers expose `data-stoch-rsi` and `data-zone`. The Stochastic RSI panel carries dashed upper (red) and lower (green) threshold lines and zone-coloured markers (red overbought, green oversold, slate neutral). Tooltip on a price dot or a marker shows x, price, the RSI, the Stochastic RSI and the zone. Config badge in top-left calls out `SRSI <rsiPeriod>/<stochPeriod>`. The legend has two toggle items (Price / StochRSI). 76 vitest cases (getLineStochRsiFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + value** + non-array -> [] + empty -> [] + preserves order, normalizeLineStochRsiPeriod incl. floors a fractional + **zero / negative / NaN falls back** + the minimum of 1, computeLineStochRsiRsi incl. non-array -> [] + matches length + the warm-up null + **pins at 100 for a strictly rising series** + **pins at 0 for a strictly falling series** + **all null when the series is too short** + **every defined RSI within 0 and 100**, computeLineStochRsiStoch incl. non-array -> [] + matches length + the warm-up null + **applies the stochastic formula exactly** + **reads 100 when the newest tops the window** + **reads 0 when the newest is the window low** + **null for a flat window** + **null for a window touching a non-finite slot**, computeLineStochRsi incl. non-array -> [] + matches length + **all null for a strictly rising series** + **is the stochastic of the RSI** + **every defined value within 0 and 100** + the warm-up null, classifyLineStochRsiZone incl. **overbought / oversold / neutral** + **none for a null | 11.697 | UI: chart-line-smi primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.715 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-smi.tsx` ships `<ChartLineSmi>` -- a pure-SVG TWO-PANEL line chart with a **Stochastic Momentum Index** panel from the double-smoothed distance to the range midpoint (the price panel on top, the SMI panel below). **The computation:** the Stochastic Momentum Index (William Blau) refines the stochastic oscillator by measuring the close against the MIDPOINT of the recent high-low range rather than its low, and double-smoothing the result. It needs high, low and close per bar, so `ChartLineSmiPoint` carries `{x, high, low, close}`. `computeLineSmiRaw(bars, qPeriod)` is the raw parts -- per bar, the distance `D = close - (highestHigh + lowestLow) / 2` over the trailing q-window, and the range `highestHigh - lowestLow`. `computeLineSmiEma(values, period)` is an EMA seeded from the first finite value (a null slot carries the previous EMA forward). `computeLineSmi(bars, qPeriod, smoothPeriod1, smoothPeriod2)` double-smooths D and the range with two successive EMAs each, then `SMI = 100 * emaD2 / (emaR2 / 2)`; a zero double-smoothed range yields null. The SMI runs around ZERO in roughly [-100,100] -- positive when the close LEADS the range midpoint. Each defined bar is classified against the thresholds: `overbought` above the upper, `oversold` below the lower, `neutral` between. **Worked fixture:** the double EMA is transcendental in general, but two conditions make the fixture exact -- hold the RANGE CONSTANT and pick SMOOTHING PERIOD 3 so the EMA alpha is exactly `2/4 = 0.5`. The fixture is 10 bars that all share high 30 and low 10, so over any 3-bar window the range is the constant 20 and the midpoint is the constant 20; the close steps `20 -> 28 -> 12`, so `D = close - 20` is `[.,.,8,8,8,8,-8,-8,-8,-8]`. With alpha 0.5 the double EMA stays DYADIC, and the range being constant collapses `SMI` to `10 * emaD2` -- the whole series is exact: `[null,null,80,80,80,80,40,0,-30,-50]`, asserted with `toEqual`, zones `[none,none,overbought,overbought,overbought,overbought,neutral,neutral,neutral,oversold]` (4 overbought, 3 neutral, 1 oversold). The EMA helper is checked with a dyadic alpha (`[4,8,16]` -> `[4,6,11]`), and a constant series with the close at the high pins the SMI at exactly 100. A flat bar (high equals low) gives a zero range and a null SMI. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs `<ChartLineStochastic>` -- the classic Stochastic measures the close against the LOW of the range; the SMI measures it against the MIDPOINT (so the SMI is signed, centred on zero, not 0..100) and double-smooths both numerator and denominator. vs `<ChartLineStochRsi>` (11.696) -- the Stochastic RSI runs the raw stochastic formula on the RSI series; the SMI runs a midpoint-relative, double-EMA-smoothed stochastic on the price. vs `<ChartLineTsi>` -- the True Strength Index double-smooths price MOMENTUM; the SMI double-smooths the distance to the range MIDPOINT. The SMI is the smoothest, least-jittery member of the stochastic family -- the double EMA filters the whipsaw the raw stochastic shows. All exported helpers carry a `LineSmi` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineSmiPoint[]` (`{x, high, low, close}`); `qPeriod` (the stochastic lookback; default 10), `smoothPeriod1` and `smoothPeriod2` (the two EMA smoothing lengths; default 3 each), each coerced to an integer >= 1; `upperThreshold` (the overbought level; default 40, accepted finite and in (0,100)) and `lowerThreshold` (the oversold level; default -40, accepted finite and in (-100,0)); `pricePanelRatio` (default 0.55, clamped to [0.3,0.8]); `gap`; `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`priceColor`/`smiColor`/`overboughtColor`/`oversoldColor`/`neutralColor`/`zeroColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showSmi`/`showZeroLine`/`showThresholds`/`showMarkers`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `smi`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_SMI_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/Q_PERIOD/SMOOTH_PERIOD_1/SMOOTH_PERIOD_2/UPPER_THRESHOLD/LOWER_THRESHOLD/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/PRICE_COLOR/SMI_COLOR/OVERBOUGHT_COLOR/OVERSOLD_COLOR/NEUTRAL_COLOR/ZERO_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineSmiZone` type (`overbought`|`oversold`|`neutral`|`none`); `ChartLineSmiRaw` interface (`{d, rangeHL}`); `getLineSmiFinitePoints` (keeps only bars with finite x + high + low + close); `normalizeLineSmiPeriod(period, fallback)` (coerce to an integer >= 1; non-finite or sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineSmiEma(values, period)` (the seeded EMA; non-array -> []); `computeLineSmiRaw(bars, qPeriod)` (the D + rangeHL parts, null through the warm-up; non-array -> {d:[],rangeHL:[]}); `computeLineSmi(bars, qPeriod, smoothPeriod1, smoothPeriod2)` (the SMI, null through the warm-up and for a zero range; non-array -> []); `classifyLineSmiZone(smi, upper, lower)` (the overbought/oversold/neutral/none rule); `runLineSmi(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, qPeriod, smoothPeriod1, smoothPeriod2, upperThreshold, lowerThreshold, d, rangeHL, smi, samples, smiFinal, overboughtCount, oversoldCount, neutralCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, high, low, close, smi, zone}`; `computeLineSmiLayout({data, qPeriod, ...})` -- stacks the price panel above the SMI panel (the SMI panel y-domain dynamic, seeded with 0 and both thresholds), projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `smiPath`, one `markers` entry per defined bar coloured by zone, and `zeroY` / `upperY` / `lowerY` for the reference lines; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 bars -> ok=false; `describeLineSmiChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Stochastic Momentum Index + double-smoothed + midpoint + the zone counts (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-smi-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the SMI line + every price dot + every marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-smi / -aria-desc / -svg / -empty / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -panel-label / -zero-line / -thresholds / -threshold-line / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -smi-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-high / -tooltip-low / -tooltip-close / -tooltip-smi / -tooltip-zone / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config); root mirrors `data-q-period`, `data-smooth-period1`, `data-smooth-period2`, `data-upper-threshold`, `data-lower-threshold`, `data-smi-final`, `data-overbought-count`, `data-oversold-count`, `data-neutral-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`, `data-empty`; grid / axis / tick / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"smi"`; threshold lines carry `data-level="upper"|"lower"`; markers expose `data-smi` and `data-zone`. The SMI panel carries a solid zero line, dashed upper (red) and lower (green) threshold lines and zone-coloured markers (red overbought, green oversold, slate neutral). Tooltip on a price dot or a marker shows x, high, low, close, the SMI and the zone. Config badge in top-left calls out `SMI <qPeriod>/<smoothPeriod1>/<smoothPeriod2>`. The legend has two toggle items (Price / SMI). 78 vitest cases (getLineSmiFinitePoints incl. **keeps only bars with finite x + high + low + close** + non-array -> [] + empty -> [] + preserves order, normalizeLineSmiPeriod incl. floors a fractional + **zero / negative / NaN falls back** + the minimum of 1, computeLineSmiEma incl. non-array -> [] + **seeds from the first finite value** + **the exact dyadic EMA for a period-3 alpha of one half** + **keeps a constant series constant** + **carries the previous value across a null slot** + matches length, computeLineSmiRaw incl. non-array -> {d:[],rangeHL:[]} + matches length + the warm-up null + **the exact distance to the range midpoint** + **the exact high-low range** + **positive when the close is above the midpoint** + **negative when below** + **a zero range for a flat bar**, computeLineSmi incl. non-array -> [] + matches length + the warm-up null + **the exact SMI series** + **pins at 100 for a constant series with the close at the high** + **null when the range is zero** + **every defined SMI within -100 and 100** + **positive when the close leads the midpoint** + **all null when the period exceeds the series length**, classifyLineSmiZone incl. **overbought / oversold / neutral** + **none for a null / non-finite reading**, runLineSmi incl. not-ok short series + ok + **the default periods 10/3/3** + **custom periods** + **the default thresholds 40/-40** + **custom thresholds** + **the exact SMI series** + **zone classification of every bar** + **self-consistent zone counts** + **the final reading** + **the raw distance + range arrays** + one sample per bar + x-sorting + empty/null -> ok=false, computeLineSmiLayout incl. not-ok single bar + **collapsed canvas -> ok=false** + ok + **stacks the price panel above the SMI panel** + price/smi paths + **one price dot per bar** + **one marker per defined SMI bar** + **the upper threshold above zero above the lower threshold** + carries the run, describeLineSmiChart incl. **the indicator name** + **the double-smoothed distance to the midpoint** + the zone counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / **empty state** / **root data-q-period/overbought-count/smi-final** / img-role svg / **the price + SMI lines** / **the zero line + the two threshold lines** / **one marker per defined SMI bar** / **the zone attribute on each marker** / **both panel labels** / config badge `SMI 3/3/3` / **legend toggle hides the SMI line** / onPointClick on a marker / ref) all pass under vitest 4.1.5.
