| 11.698 | UI: chart-line-qqe primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.716 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-qqe.tsx` ships `<ChartLineQqe>` -- a pure-SVG TWO-PANEL line chart with a **Quantitative Qualitative Estimation** panel from a smoothed RSI and an adaptive trailing band (the price panel on top, the QQE panel below). **The computation:** the QQE works on the RSI -- it smooths the RSI with an EMA, then wraps the smoothed RSI in an ADAPTIVE TRAILING BAND whose half-width is the smoothed average true range of the RSI times a factor. It is a close-only indicator, so `ChartLineQqePoint` carries `{x, value}`. `computeLineQqeRsi(closes, rsiPeriod)` is Wilder's RSI. `computeLineQqeEma(values, period)` is a standard EMA (alpha `2/(period+1)`) and `computeLineQqeWilder(values, period)` is Wilder smoothing (alpha `1/period`), both seeded from the first finite value. `computeLineQqeTrail(rsiMa, delta)` is the ADAPTIVE TRAILING-BAND STATE MACHINE -- given the smoothed RSI and the band half-width it RATCHETS a long band up (it can only climb while the RSI leads it) and a short band down (it can only fall), flips the trend when the smoothed RSI crosses the active band, and emits the QQE line (the long band in an uptrend, the short band in a downtrend) and the per-bar trend (+1 up / -1 down / 0 undefined). `computeLineQqe(closes, options)` composes the pipeline: RSI -> EMA smoothing into the smoothed RSI -> the absolute change of the smoothed RSI Wilder-smoothed and scaled by the factor into the band width -> the trailing band. Each defined bar is classified by the trend: `up` or `down`. **Worked fixture:** the RSI and the EMAs are transcendental for a mixed series, but the DETERMINISTIC core has exact anchors. THE TRAILING-BAND STATE MACHINE is a pure deterministic function, hand-traced from `rsiMa = [50,54,58,56,48,44,50,56]` and a constant band half-width of 4 -- the band ratchets, the trend flips DOWN at bar 4 when the smoothed RSI drops below the long band and back UP at bar 6 when it tops the short band; the QQE line `[46,50,54,54,52,48,46,52]` and the trend `[1,1,1,1,-1,-1,1,1]` are asserted with `toEqual`. THE RSI has monotone anchors -- a strictly rising series pins it at 100, a strictly falling one at 0, a flat one at 50. THE EMAs are checked with dyadic alpha (`[4,8,16]` -> `[4,6,11]` for EMA period 3 and Wilder period 2). THE FULL PIPELINE: a flat series leaves the RSI at 50 and the ATR of the RSI at 0, so the QQE line is exactly 50 and the trend stays up -- an exact end-to-end anchor. The run / component use a choppy fixture and are checked structurally. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs `<ChartLineStochRsi>` (11.696) -- the Stochastic RSI runs the raw stochastic formula on the RSI; the QQE smooths the RSI then wraps it in a SuperTrend-style trailing band, so it is far less jittery and gives a binary trend rather than an overbought/oversold oscillator. vs `<ChartLineRsi>` -- the plain RSI is the raw momentum line; the QQE smooths it and adds the adaptive band as the actionable signal. vs `<ChartLineSupertrend>` / `<ChartLineParabolicSar>` -- those run a ratcheting trailing stop on the PRICE; the QQE runs the same idea on the SMOOTHED RSI, so the flip reflects momentum exhaustion rather than a price reversal. The defining trait: the band width ADAPTS to the RSI's own recent volatility (the ATR of the RSI), so the QQE tightens in calm RSI and widens in turbulent RSI. All exported helpers carry a `LineQqe` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineQqePoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `rsiPeriod` (the RSI lookback; default 14), `smoothPeriod` (the RSI EMA smoothing; default 5), `wilderPeriod` (the ATR-of-RSI smoothing; default 27), each coerced to an integer >= 1; `factor` (the band-width multiplier; default 4.236, accepted as a positive number); `pricePanelRatio` (default 0.55, clamped to [0.3,0.8]); `gap`; `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`priceColor`/`rsiColor`/`qqeColor`/`upColor`/`downColor`/`midlineColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showRsi`/`showQqe`/`showMidline`/`showMarkers`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `rsi` / `qqe`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_QQE_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/RSI_PERIOD/SMOOTH_PERIOD/WILDER_PERIOD/FACTOR/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/PRICE_COLOR/RSI_COLOR/QQE_COLOR/UP_COLOR/DOWN_COLOR/MIDLINE_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineQqeZone` type (`up`|`down`|`none`); `ChartLineQqeTrail` + `ChartLineQqeComputed` interfaces; `getLineQqeFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value); `normalizeLineQqePeriod(period, fallback)` (coerce to an integer >= 1; non-finite or sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineQqeEma(values, period)` / `computeLineQqeWilder(values, period)` (the two seeded exponential smoothings; non-array -> []); `computeLineQqeRsi(closes, rsiPeriod)` (Wilder's RSI; non-array -> []); `computeLineQqeTrail(rsiMa, delta)` -- the state machine, returns `{qqeLine, trend}` (non-array -> {qqeLine:[],trend:[]}, leading nulls skipped); `computeLineQqe(closes, options?)` -- returns `{rsi, rsiMa, atrRsi, delta, qqeLine, trend}`; `runLineQqe(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, rsiPeriod, smoothPeriod, wilderPeriod, factor, rsi, rsiMa, qqeLine, trend, samples, trendFinal, upCount, downCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, rsiMa, qqeLine, trend, zone}`; `computeLineQqeLayout({data, rsiPeriod, ...})` -- stacks the price panel above the QQE panel (the QQE panel y-domain dynamic, seeded with [30,70]), projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `rsiPath`, `qqePath`, one `markers` entry per defined smoothed-RSI bar coloured by trend, and `midlineY` for the 50 line; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLineQqeChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Quantitative Qualitative Estimation + smooths the RSI + trailing band + the trend counts (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-qqe-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the smoothed RSI line + the QQE line + every price dot + every marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-qqe / -aria-desc / -svg / -empty / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -panel-label / -midline / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -rsi-line / -qqe-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-rsi / -tooltip-qqe / -tooltip-trend / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config); root mirrors `data-rsi-period`, `data-smooth-period`, `data-wilder-period`, `data-factor`, `data-trend-final`, `data-up-count`, `data-down-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`, `data-empty`; grid / axis / tick / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"qqe"`; markers expose `data-trend` and `data-zone`. The QQE panel carries a dashed 50 midline, the smoothed RSI line, the dashed QQE trailing line and trend-coloured markers (green uptrend, red downtrend). Tooltip on a price dot or a marker shows x, price, the smoothed RSI, the QQE line and the trend. Config badge in top-left calls out `QQE <rsiPeriod>/<smoothPeriod>`. The legend has three toggle items (Price / RSI MA / QQE Line). 80 vitest cases (getLineQqeFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + value** + non-array -> [] + empty -> [] + preserves order, normalizeLineQqePeriod incl. floors a fractional + **zero / negative / NaN falls back** + the minimum of 1, computeLineQqeEma incl. non-array -> [] + **the exact dyadic EMA for a period-3 alpha of one half** + **keeps a constant series constant** + **carries the previous value across a null slot**, computeLineQqeWilder incl. non-array -> [] + **the exact dyadic smoothing for a period-2 alpha of one half** + **keeps a constant series constant** + matches length, computeLineQqeRsi incl. non-array -> [] + matches length + **pins at 100 for a strictly rising series** + **pins at 0 for a strictly falling series** + **reads 50 for a flat series** + **all null when the series is too short**, computeLineQqeTrail incl. non-array -> {qqeLine:[],trend:[]} + qqeLine/trend length matches + **the exact trailing QQE line** + **the exact trend sequence** + **starts in an uptrend** + **flips the trend down when the smoothed RSI drops below the long band** + **flips the trend up when it tops the short band** + **follows the long band below the RSI in an uptrend** + **skips the leading null slots**, computeLineQqe incl. non-array -> empty arrays + matches length + **holds the QQE line at 50 for a flat series** + **keeps the trend up for a flat series** + **smooths the RSI with an EMA** + **every trend value in the valid set** + the warm-up null + **derives the band width from the ATR of the RSI**, runLineQqe incl. not-ok short series + ok + **the default options 14/5/27/4.236** + **custom options** + one sample per point + **a valid zone for every bar** + **self-consistent trend counts** + **a flat series wholly in an uptrend** + the final trend + carries the rsi/qqe arrays + x-sorting + empty/null -> ok=false, computeLineQqeLayout incl. not-ok single point + **collapsed canvas -> ok=false** + ok + **stacks the price panel above the QQE panel** + price/rsi/qqe paths + **one price dot per bar** + **one marker per defined smoothed-RSI bar** + **the midline inside the QQE panel** + carries the run, describeLineQqeChart incl. **the indicator name** + **the smoothed RSI + the trailing band** + the trend counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / **empty state** / **root data-rsi-period/smooth-period/total-points** / img-role svg / **the price + RSI + QQE lines** / **the midline** / **one marker per defined smoothed-RSI bar** / **a valid zone on each marker** / **both panel labels** / config badge `QQE 3/3` / **legend toggle hides the QQE line** / onPointClick on a marker / ref) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. | 11.699 | UI: chart-line-wae primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.717 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-wae.tsx` ships `<ChartLineWae>` -- a pure-SVG TWO-PANEL line chart with a **Waddah Attar Explosion** panel from the MACD delta against a Bollinger band width (the price panel on top, the WAE panel below). **The computation:** the Waddah Attar Explosion pits a MACD-derived MOMENTUM against a Bollinger Band width "EXPLOSION LINE". It is a close-only indicator, so `ChartLineWaePoint` carries `{x, value}`. `computeLineWaeMacd(values, fastPeriod, slowPeriod)` is the MACD line -- the fast EMA of the closes minus the slow EMA. `computeLineWaeStdev(values, period)` is the rolling POPULATION standard deviation and `computeLineWaeBollWidth(values, period, mult)` scales it into the Bollinger Band width (`2 * mult * stddev`). `computeLineWae(values, options)` composes the pipeline: the WAE momentum is the CHANGE in the MACD scaled by a sensitivity factor (positive for an up push, negative for a down push), the explosion line is the Bollinger Band width. `classifyLineWaeZone(trend, explosion)` marks a bar `explosive-up` or `explosive-down` when the momentum MAGNITUDE tops the explosion line (the sign gives the direction), else `quiet`, `none` while undefined. **Worked fixture:** TWO conditions make the pipeline exact. The fast EMA period 3 has alpha `2/4 = 0.5` and the slow EMA period 7 has alpha `2/8 = 0.25` -- BOTH dyadic -- so the MACD stays exact. The Bollinger width is read at period 2, where the population standard deviation collapses to HALF the absolute close change (`|delta|/2`, no transcendental sqrt), also exact. The fixture has closes stepping `16 -> 32`; with sensitivity 4 the whole pipeline is exact -- the MACD line `[0,0,0,0,4,5,4.75,4.0625]`, the momentum `[null,0,0,0,16,4,-1,-2.75]` and the explosion line `[null,0,0,0,16,0,0,0]` are ALL asserted with `toEqual`, zones `[none,quiet,quiet,quiet,quiet,explosive-up,explosive-down,explosive-down]` (1 explosive-up, 2 explosive-down, 4 quiet). The zone classification is also tested directly as a pure function (explosive-up / explosive-down / quiet / none, including the equality boundary where the momentum equals the explosion line -> quiet). A constant series gives a zero MACD, zero momentum and zero explosion, so it is wholly quiet. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs `<ChartLineMacd>` -- the MACD chart plots the MACD line, signal and histogram; the WAE takes the MACD DELTA (the bar-to-bar change), scales it, and judges it against a volatility gate. vs `<ChartLineBollinger>` -- that wraps the price in the Bollinger bands; the WAE uses only the band WIDTH, as the explosion threshold. vs `<ChartLineElderImpulse>` -- the Elder Impulse colours bars from the AGREEMENT of an EMA trend and a MACD histogram; the WAE compares a MACD-momentum MAGNITUDE against a Bollinger-width THRESHOLD to gate breakouts. The defining trait: a bar only signals when momentum EXCEEDS the band width -- the BB width acts as an adaptive dead-zone that suppresses signals while volatility is contracting. All exported helpers carry a `LineWae` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineWaePoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `fastPeriod` (the fast MACD EMA; default 20), `slowPeriod` (the slow MACD EMA; default 40), `bbPeriod` (the Bollinger lookback; default 20), each coerced to an integer >= 1; `bbMult` (the Bollinger multiplier; default 2) and `sensitivity` (the momentum scale; default 150), accepted as positive numbers; `pricePanelRatio` (default 0.55, clamped to [0.3,0.8]); `gap`; `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`priceColor`/`momentumColor`/`explosionColor`/`explosiveUpColor`/`explosiveDownColor`/`quietColor`/`zeroColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showMomentum`/`showExplosion`/`showZeroLine`/`showMarkers`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `momentum` / `explosion`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_WAE_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/FAST_PERIOD/SLOW_PERIOD/BB_PERIOD/BB_MULT/SENSITIVITY/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/PRICE_COLOR/MOMENTUM_COLOR/EXPLOSION_COLOR/EXPLOSIVE_UP_COLOR/EXPLOSIVE_DOWN_COLOR/QUIET_COLOR/ZERO_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineWaeZone` type (`explosive-up`|`explosive-down`|`quiet`|`none`); `ChartLineWaeComputed` interface; `getLineWaeFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value); `normalizeLineWaePeriod(period, fallback)` (coerce to an integer >= 1; non-finite or sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineWaeEma(values, period)` (the seeded EMA; non-array -> []); `computeLineWaeMacd(values, fastPeriod, slowPeriod)` (the MACD line; non-array -> []); `computeLineWaeStdev(values, period)` (the rolling population stddev, null through the warm-up; non-array -> []); `computeLineWaeBollWidth(values, bbPeriod, bbMult)` (the band width); `classifyLineWaeZone(trend, explosion)` (the explosive-up/explosive-down/quiet/none rule); `computeLineWae(values, options?)` -- returns `{macd, trend, explosion}`; `runLineWae(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, fastPeriod, slowPeriod, bbPeriod, bbMult, sensitivity, macd, trend, explosion, samples, trendFinal, explosiveUpCount, explosiveDownCount, quietCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, trend, explosion, zone}`; `computeLineWaeLayout({data, fastPeriod, ...})` -- stacks the price panel above the WAE panel (the WAE panel y-domain dynamic, seeded with 0 and spanning the momentum plus the +/- explosion band), projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `momentumPath`, `explosionPath` (the +explosion edge) and `explosionMirrorPath` (the -explosion edge), one `markers` entry per defined momentum bar coloured by zone, and `zeroY`; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLineWaeChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Waddah Attar Explosion + MACD + Bollinger Band width + the zone counts (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-wae-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the momentum line + the explosion line + every price dot + every marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-wae / -aria-desc / -svg / -empty / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -panel-label / -zero-line / -explosion / -explosion-line / -explosion-mirror / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -momentum-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-momentum / -tooltip-explosion / -tooltip-zone / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config); root mirrors `data-fast-period`, `data-slow-period`, `data-bb-period`, `data-bb-mult`, `data-sensitivity`, `data-trend-final`, `data-explosive-up-count`, `data-explosive-down-count`, `data-quiet-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`, `data-empty`; grid / axis / tick / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"wae"`; markers expose `data-trend` and `data-zone`. The WAE panel carries a solid zero line, the dashed explosion line and its mirror at +/- the band width, the signed momentum line and zone-coloured markers (green explosive-up, red explosive-down, slate quiet). Tooltip on a price dot or a marker shows x, price, the momentum, the explosion line and the zone. Config badge in top-left calls out `WAE <fastPeriod>/<slowPeriod>`. The legend has three toggle items (Price / Momentum / Explosion). 84 vitest cases (getLineWaeFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + value** + non-array -> [] + empty -> [] + preserves order, normalizeLineWaePeriod incl. floors a fractional + **zero / negative / NaN falls back** + the minimum of 1, computeLineWaeEma incl. non-array -> [] + **the exact dyadic EMA for a period-3 alpha of one half** + **the exact dyadic EMA for a period-7 alpha of one quarter** + **keeps a constant series constant**, computeLineWaeMacd incl. non-array -> [] + matches length + **the exact MACD line** + **zero for a constant series** + **positive while the fast EMA leads on a rising series**, computeLineWaeStdev incl. non-array -> [] + the warm-up null + **half the absolute change for a period-2 window** + **zero for a constant series** + **the exact standard deviation of the fixture** + matches length, computeLineWaeBollWidth incl. non-array -> [] + **twice the multiplier times the standard deviation** + **the exact band width of the fixture** + **scales with the multiplier** + **zero for a constant series**, classifyLineWaeZone incl. **explosive-up / explosive-down** + **quiet within the explosion line** + **quiet when the momentum equals the explosion line** + **none for a null momentum / null explosion / non-finite input**, computeLineWae incl. non-array -> empty arrays + matches length + **the exact MACD line** + **the exact momentum series** + **the exact explosion line** + the first-bar null + **scales the momentum by the sensitivity factor** + **a zero momentum for a constant series**, runLineWae incl. not-ok short series + ok + **the default options 20/40/20/2/150** + **custom options** + **the exact momentum + explosion series** + **zone classification of | 11.700 | UI: chart-line-ssl primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.718 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-ssl.tsx` ships `<ChartLineSsl>` -- a pure-SVG SINGLE-PANEL line chart with an **SSL Channel** overlay flipping between the high and low moving averages on a close cross (the close line and the two SSL lines share one panel). **The computation:** the SSL Channel runs a moving average over the bar HIGHs and another over the bar LOWs. It needs high, low and close per bar, so `ChartLineSslPoint` carries `{x, high, low, close}`. `computeLineSslSma(values, period)` is the simple moving average run over the highs and over the lows. `computeLineSslHlv(closes, smaHigh, smaLow)` is the DIRECTION STATE MACHINE -- it returns +1 once the close crosses ABOVE the high moving average, -1 once it crosses BELOW the low moving average, and CARRIES the last direction between crosses; a bar without both moving averages, or before any cross, is 0. `computeLineSsl(bars, period)` composes the pipeline: in an UPTREND the SSL Up line rides the high average and the SSL Down line rides the low average; in a DOWNTREND they SWAP -- the crossover of the two lines marks the trend change. Each defined bar is classified by the direction: `up` or `down` (`none` while undefined). **Worked fixture:** the moving average is exact for integer windows that divide cleanly, and the direction state machine plus the line swap are exact comparisons and selections -- so the whole SSL pipeline is exact integer arithmetic. The fixture is 7 OHLC bars read with period 2 (every high and low EVEN, so the period-2 averages are exact integers); the close crosses above the high average at bar 1, holds, crosses below the low average at bar 3, holds, then crosses back above at bar 5. The direction state `[0,1,1,-1,-1,1,1]`, the SSL Up line `[null,35,45,19,6,45,65]` and the SSL Down line `[null,15,25,47,37,12,30]` are ALL asserted with `toEqual`, zones `[none,up,up,down,down,up,up]` (4 up, 2 down). The direction state machine is also tested directly -- it flips up on a close above the high average, down on a close below the low average, carries between crosses, stays 0 before any cross, and does NOT flip when the close only EQUALS the average (strict cross). **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs `<ChartLineHilo>` (Gann HiLo Activator) -- the HiLo flips a single trailing stop on a close-vs-MA cross; the SSL keeps TWO lines (the high-MA and the low-MA) and swaps which is "up" and which is "down". vs `<ChartLineSupertrend>` / `<ChartLineParabolicSar>` -- those compute an ATR-based trailing stop; the SSL uses plain moving averages of the highs and lows as the channel, no ATR. vs `<ChartLineDonchian>` -- the Donchian channel is the rolling MAX high and MIN low; the SSL uses the rolling MEAN of the highs and lows. The defining trait: the channel direction is a carried state -- it only flips on a genuine close cross, so the SSL Up/SSL Down crossover is a clean discrete trend-change signal. All exported helpers carry a `LineSsl` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineSslPoint[]` (`{x, high, low, close}`); `period` (the moving-average lookback; default 10, coerced to an integer >= 1); `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`priceColor`/`sslUpColor`/`sslDownColor`/`neutralColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showSslUp`/`showSslDown`/`showMarkers`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `sslUp` / `sslDown`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_SSL_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/PRICE_COLOR/SSL_UP_COLOR/SSL_DOWN_COLOR/NEUTRAL_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineSslZone` type (`up`|`down`|`none`); `ChartLineSslComputed` interface; `getLineSslFinitePoints` (keeps only bars with finite x + high + low + close); `normalizeLineSslPeriod(period, fallback)` (coerce to an integer >= 1; non-finite or sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineSslSma(values, period)` (the moving average, null through the warm-up; non-array -> []); `computeLineSslHlv(closes, smaHigh, smaLow)` (the direction state machine; non-array -> []); `computeLineSsl(bars, period)` -- returns `{smaHigh, smaLow, hlv, sslUp, sslDown}` (non-array -> empty arrays); `classifyLineSslZone(hlv)` (the up/down/none rule); `runLineSsl(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, period, smaHigh, smaLow, hlv, sslUp, sslDown, samples, hlvFinal, upCount, downCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, high, low, close, sslUp, sslDown, hlv, zone}`; `computeLineSslLayout({data, period, ...})` -- projects a single panel with the close line, the SSL Up line, the SSL Down line, one `priceDots` entry per bar, one `markers` entry per bar with a defined channel coloured by direction, and the value domain spanning the close and both SSL lines; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 bars -> ok=false; `describeLineSslChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions SSL Channel + moving average + cross + the direction counts (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-ssl-aria-desc"` span; the close line + the SSL Up line + the SSL Down line + every close dot + every marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-ssl / -aria-desc / -svg / -empty / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -ssl-up-line / -ssl-down-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-close / -tooltip-ssl-up / -tooltip-ssl-down / -tooltip-zone / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-hlv-final`, `data-up-count`, `data-down-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`, `data-empty`; markers expose `data-hlv` and `data-zone`. The SSL Up line is green, the SSL Down line red; the close line sits on top in blue, with markers coloured by direction (green up, red down, slate none). Tooltip on a close dot or a marker shows x, close, the SSL Up value, the SSL Down value and the channel direction. Config badge in top-left calls out `SSL <period>`. The legend has three toggle items (Price / SSL Up / SSL Down). 77 vitest cases (getLineSslFinitePoints incl. **keeps only bars with finite x + high + low + close** + non-array -> [] + empty -> [] + preserves order, normalizeLineSslPeriod incl. floors a fractional + **zero / negative / NaN falls back** + the minimum of 1, computeLineSslSma incl. non-array -> [] + the warm-up null + **the simple moving average of the window** + matches length + **null for a window with a non-finite value**, computeLineSslHlv incl. non-array -> [] + matches length + **zero on a bar without both moving averages** + **flips up when the close crosses above the high average** + **flips down when the close crosses below the low average** + **carries the direction between crosses** + **stays zero before any cross** + **does not flip when the close only equals the average** + **the exact direction state for the fixture closes**, computeLineSsl incl. non-array -> empty arrays + matches length + **the exact high moving average** + **the exact low moving average** + **the exact direction state** + **the exact SSL Up line** + **the exact SSL Down line** + **rides the high average with SSL Up in an uptrend** + **rides the high average with SSL Down in a downtrend** + **swaps the SSL lines when the direction flips**, classifyLineSslZone incl. **up / down / none** + up for any positive value, runLineSsl incl. not-ok short series + ok + **the default period 10** + **a custom period** + **the exact SSL lines** + **the exact direction state** + **zone classification of every bar** + **self-consistent zone counts** + **the final direction** + **carries the high + low moving averages** + one sample per bar + x-sorting + empty/null -> ok=false, computeLineSslLayout incl. not-ok single bar + **collapsed canvas -> ok=false** + ok + price/ssl-up/ssl-down paths + **one price dot per bar** + **one marker per bar with a defined channel** + **every marker inside the panel** + **the value domain spans the close + SSL lines** + carries the run, describeLineSslChart incl. **the indicator name** + **the moving averages + the cross** + the direction counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / **empty state** / **root data-period/up-count/total-points** / img-role svg / **the price + both SSL lines** / **one marker per bar with a defined channel** / **the exact zone on each marker** / config badge `SSL 2` / **legend toggle hides the SSL Up line** / **showSslDown=false hides the SSL Down line** / **controlled hiddenSeries hides the price line** / onPointClick on a marker / ref) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. | 11.701 | UI: chart-line-coral primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.719 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-coral.tsx` ships `<ChartLineCoral>` -- a pure-SVG SINGLE-PANEL line chart with a **Coral Trend** overlay from a six-pole Tillson smoothing of price (the close line and the Coral line share one panel). **The computation:** the Coral Trend indicator is a SIX-POLE Tillson smoothing of the price. It is a close-only indicator, so `ChartLineCoralPoint` carries `{x, value}`. `computeLineCoralCoefficients(cd)` is the four Tillson coefficients for the constant `cd` -- `c1 = -cd^3`, `c2 = 3(cd^2 + cd^3)`, `c3 = -3(2cd^2 + cd + cd^3)`, `c4 = 1 + 3cd + cd^3 + 3cd^2`; they recombine the third through sixth smoothing poles and SUM TO EXACTLY ONE. `computeLineCoral(values, period, cd)` runs the price through SIX cascaded exponential filters (the pole alpha derived from the period as `2/(((period-1)/2+1)+1)`) and recombines poles three to six with the Tillson coefficients; the poles are seeded from the first value, so the Coral line is defined from the FIRST bar. `classifyLineCoralTrend(coral, prevCoral)` marks each bar `up`, `down` or `flat` by the SLOPE of the Coral line. The Tillson coefficients give the higher poles a small NEGATIVE weight, which adds a touch of LEAD -- the Coral lags less than a plain moving average of the same length. **Worked fixture:** the six-pole cascade is transcendental in general, but two anchors are exact. `cd = 0.5` makes `cd`, `cd^2` and `cd^3` exact dyadic values, so the Tillson coefficients are exact -- `{c1: -0.125, c2: 1.125, c3: -3.375, c4: 3.375}`, asserted with `toEqual`, and they sum to exactly `1`. Because the coefficients sum to one, the Coral of a CONSTANT series is that constant -- with the poles written in the incremental form `pole + alpha*(input - pole)`, a constant input keeps every pole exactly at the seed and the coefficient recombination collapses to the constant; `computeLineCoral([8,8,8,8,8], 5, 0.5)` is `[8,8,8,8,8]`, asserted with `toEqual`. A rising series gives a rising Coral, a falling series a falling Coral, and the trend classification is tested directly as a pure function. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs `<ChartLineMovingAvg>` / a plain SMA -- the SMA is a single flat-weighted average; the Coral is a six-stage CASCADE recombined with the Tillson coefficients, which inject lead to cut lag. vs `<ChartLineT3>` -- the Tillson T3 IS this six-pole structure; the Coral Trend is the T3 used as a slope-coloured trend ribbon, classifying each bar by the line's direction. vs `<ChartLineHma>` / `<ChartLineZlema>` -- those are other lag-reduction smoothers (weighted-MA composition, zero-lag EMA); the Coral uses the specific GD-of-GD-of-GD Tillson cascade. vs `<ChartLineGaussian>`-style filters -- a Gaussian is a symmetric low-pass; the Coral is causal and lead-corrected. The defining trait: the six poles plus the negative-weighted recombination make the Coral a smooth-yet-responsive trend line whose own slope is the signal. All exported helpers carry a `LineCoral` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineCoralPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `period` (the smoothing length; default 21, coerced to an integer >= 1); `cd` (the Tillson constant; default 0.4, accepted as a finite number); `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`priceColor`/`upColor`/`downColor`/`flatColor`/`noneColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showCoral`/`showMarkers`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `coral`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_CORAL_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/CD/PRICE_COLOR/UP_COLOR/DOWN_COLOR/FLAT_COLOR/NONE_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineCoralTrend` type (`up`|`down`|`flat`|`none`); `ChartLineCoralCoefficients` interface; `getLineCoralFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value); `normalizeLineCoralPeriod(period, fallback)` (coerce to an integer >= 1; non-finite or sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineCoralCoefficients(cd)` (the four Tillson coefficients; non-finite cd -> default); `computeLineCoral(values, period, cd)` (the six-pole Tillson smoothing; non-array -> []); `classifyLineCoralTrend(coral, prevCoral)` (the up/down/flat/none rule); `runLineCoral(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, period, cd, coral, samples, coralFinal, upCount, downCount, flatCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, coral, trend}`; `computeLineCoralLayout({data, period, cd, ...})` -- projects a single panel with the price line, one `segments` entry between each pair of bars (coloured by the ending bar's trend), one `markers` entry per bar coloured by trend, and the value domain spanning the price and the Coral; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLineCoralChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Coral Trend + six-pole + Tillson + smoothing + the trend counts (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-coral-aria-desc"` span; the price line + every marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-coral / -aria-desc / -svg / -empty / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -coral-segments / -segment / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-coral / -tooltip-trend / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-cd`, `data-coral-final`, `data-up-count`, `data-down-count`, `data-flat-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`, `data-empty`; segments + markers expose `data-trend`. The Coral line renders as slope-coloured segments (green rising, red falling, slate flat, light grey for the first bar) with one coloured marker per bar; the price line is a plain grey reference. Tooltip on a marker shows x, price, the Coral value and the trend. Config badge in top-left calls out `Coral <period>`. The legend has two toggle items (Price / Coral Trend). 69 vitest cases (getLineCoralFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + value** + non-array -> [] + empty -> [] + preserves order, normalizeLineCoralPeriod incl. floors a fractional + **zero / negative / NaN falls back** + the minimum of 1, computeLineCoralCoefficients incl. **the exact dyadic coefficients for cd one half** + **the four coefficients sum to one** + **c1 is the negative cube of cd** + **non-finite cd falls back to the default** + **different coefficients for a different cd** + **the sum stays at one for any cd**, computeLineCoral incl. non-array -> [] + empty -> [] + matches length + **holds a constant series at its constant level** + **the value itself for a single-bar input** + **rises across a strictly rising series** + **falls across a strictly falling series** + **every value finite for finite input** + **responds to the cd constant** + a two-value input length, classifyLineCoralTrend incl. **up / down / flat** + **none for a null previous / null current value**, runLineCoral incl. not-ok short series + ok + **the default options 21/0.4** + **custom options** + **holds the Coral at the constant level for a flat series** + **classifies a flat series as flat after the first bar** + **rises through a rising series** + **self-consistent trend counts** + the final Coral value + one sample per point + x-sorting + empty/null -> ok=false, computeLineCoralLayout incl. not-ok single point + **collapsed canvas -> ok=false** + ok + the price path + **one price dot per bar** + **one Coral segment between each pair of bars** + **one marker per bar** + **every marker inside the panel** + carries the run, describeLineCoralChart incl. **the indicator name** + **the six-pole Tillson smoothing** + the trend counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / **empty state** / **root data-period/total-points** / img-role svg / the price line / **one Coral segment between each pair of bars** / **one marker per bar** / **a valid trend on each marker** / config badge `Coral 5` / **legend toggle hides the Coral segments** / **showCoral=false hides the Coral** / onPointClick on a marker / ref) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. | 11.702 | UI: chart-line-fdi primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.720 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-fdi.tsx` ships `<ChartLineFdi>` -- a pure-SVG TWO-PANEL line chart with a **Fractal Dimension Index** panel scoring path roughness over the lookback window (the price panel on top, the FDI panel below on a FIXED 1..2 scale). **The computation:** the Fractal Dimension Index scores how ROUGH the price path is over a lookback window. It is a close-only indicator, so `ChartLineFdiPoint` carries `{x, value}`. `computeLineFdiValue(window)` scores ONE window -- it normalizes the window into a UNIT SQUARE (x over [0,1], price over [0,1] of the window's own high-low range), measures the LENGTH `L` of the price curve in that square (`sum of sqrt(dx^2 + dy^2)` over the segments), and returns `1 + ln(L) / ln(N - 1)`, clamped to [1,2]; a flat window is exactly 1 and a window of two or fewer points is a straight segment, also 1. `computeLineFdi(values, period)` rolls the score over the close series. `classifyLineFdiZone(fdi, upper, lower)` marks a bar `trending` BELOW the lower threshold (a smooth path), `choppy` ABOVE the upper (a rough path), `neutral` between, `none` while undefined. A smooth, straight path has a curve length near the unit diagonal so the FDI sits near 1; a jagged, space-filling path has a much longer curve so the FDI climbs toward 2. **Worked fixture:** the FDI involves a square root and a logarithm and is transcendental in general, but two anchors are clean. A FLAT window has a zero price range, so every normalized step is zero, the curve length is exactly 1 (the sum of the equal x-steps, each `1/(N-1)`), and `ln(1) = 0` -- so the FDI is EXACTLY 1; `computeLineFdiValue([8,8,8,8,8])` is `1`, asserted with `toBe`. A STRAIGHT RAMP at period 5 has a curve length of `sqrt(2)` and `ln(N-1) = ln(4) = 2*ln(2)`, so the FDI is `1 + ln(sqrt(2))/ln(4) = 1.25`; `computeLineFdiValue([10,20,30,40,50])` is checked with `toBeCloseTo(1.25)`. A maximally jagged window clamps to 2, a jagged window scores ABOVE a smooth ramp, a smooth monotone window scores low, and the zone classifier is tested directly. The run / component use a flat-then-choppy fixture; the first full flat window scores exactly 1, asserted with `toBe`. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs `<ChartLineChoppiness>` -- the Choppiness Index also gauges trend-vs-range but from the ratio of summed true range to the high-low span; the FDI measures the geometric DIMENSION of the price curve. vs `<ChartLineHurst>` -- the Hurst exponent estimates persistence from rescaled-range scaling; the FDI estimates the box-counting-style fractal dimension directly from one window's curve length (and `D` relates to Hurst as `D = 2 - H`). vs `<ChartLineEntropy>` -- entropy scores disorder of the value distribution; the FDI scores the geometric roughness of the path SHAPE. The defining trait: the FDI normalizes each window to its OWN range, so it is amplitude-blind -- a tiny-amplitude zigzag and a large one score the same roughness. All exported helpers carry a `LineFdi` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineFdiPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `period` (the lookback window; default 30, coerced to an integer >= 2); `upperThreshold` (the choppy level; default 1.6) and `lowerThreshold` (the trending level; default 1.4), accepted when finite and in (1,2); `pricePanelRatio` (default 0.55, clamped to [0.3,0.8]); `gap`; `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`priceColor`/`fdiColor`/`trendingColor`/`choppyColor`/`neutralColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showFdi`/`showThresholds`/`showMarkers`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `fdi`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_FDI_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/UPPER_THRESHOLD/LOWER_THRESHOLD/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/PRICE_COLOR/FDI_COLOR/TRENDING_COLOR/CHOPPY_COLOR/NEUTRAL_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineFdiZone` type (`trending`|`choppy`|`neutral`|`none`); `getLineFdiFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value); `normalizeLineFdiPeriod(period, fallback)` (coerce to an integer >= 2; non-finite or sub-2 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineFdiValue(window)` (the FDI of one window, clamped [1,2]; non-array / sub-2 -> null; non-finite member -> null); `computeLineFdi(values, period)` (the rolling FDI, null through the warm-up; non-array -> []); `classifyLineFdiZone(fdi, upper, lower)` (the trending/choppy/neutral/none rule); `runLineFdi(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, period, upperThreshold, lowerThreshold, fdi, samples, fdiFinal, trendingCount, choppyCount, neutralCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, fdi, zone}`; `computeLineFdiLayout({data, period, ...})` -- stacks the price panel above the FDI panel (the FDI panel y-domain FIXED to [1,2]), projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `fdiPath`, one `markers` entry per defined bar coloured by zone, and `upperY` / `lowerY` for the threshold lines; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLineFdiChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Fractal Dimension Index + roughness + path + the zone counts (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-fdi-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the FDI line + every price dot + every marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-fdi / -aria-desc / -svg / -empty / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -panel-label / -thresholds / -threshold-line / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -fdi-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-fdi / -tooltip-zone / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-upper-threshold`, `data-lower-threshold`, `data-fdi-final`, `data-trending-count`, `data-choppy-count`, `data-neutral-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`, `data-empty`; grid / axis / tick / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"fdi"`; threshold lines carry `data-level="upper"|"lower"`; markers expose `data-fdi` and `data-zone`. The FDI panel carries dashed upper (red) and lower (green) threshold lines and zone-coloured markers (green trending, red choppy, slate neutral). Tooltip on a price dot or a marker shows x, price, the FDI (to three decimals) and the zone. Config badge in top-left calls out `FDI <period>`. The legend has two toggle items (Price / FDI). 71 vitest cases (getLineFdiFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + value** + non-array -> [] + empty -> [] + preserves order, normalizeLineFdiPeriod incl. floors a fractional + **sub-2 / NaN falls back** + the minimum of 2, computeLineFdiValue incl. non-array -> null + **null for a window shorter than two points** + **1 for a two-point window** + **exactly 1 for a flat window** + **1.25 for a straight ramp at a five-point window** + **clamps a maximally jagged window to 2** + **null for a window with a non-finite value** + **within 1 and 2** + **a jagged window scores higher than a smooth ramp** + **a smooth monotone window scores low**, computeLineFdi incl. non-array -> [] + matches length + the warm-up null + **1 for a fully flat window** + **all null when the period exceeds the series length** + **every defined value within 1 and 2** + **one value per full window** + **null at a slot whose window touches a non-finite value**, classifyLineFdiZone incl. **choppy / trending / neutral** + **none for a null / non-finite reading**, runLineFdi incl. not-ok short series + ok + **the default options 30/1.6/1.4** + **custom options** + **the first full flat window at exactly 1** + **classifies the flat window as trending** + one sample per point + **every reading within 1 and 2** + **a valid zone for every bar** + **self-consistent zone counts** + x-sorting + empty/null -> ok=false, computeLineFdiLayout incl. not-ok single point + **collapsed canvas -> ok=false** + ok + **stacks the price panel above the FDI panel** + price/fdi paths + **one price dot per bar** + **one marker per defined FDI bar** + **the upper threshold line above the lower** + carries the run, describeLineFdiChart incl. **the indicator name** + **the path roughness** + the zone counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / **empty state** / **root data-period/total-points** / img-role svg / **the price + FDI lines** / **the two threshold lines** / **one marker per defined FDI bar** / **a valid zone on each marker** / **both panel labels** / config badge `FDI 5` / **legend toggle hides the FDI line** / onPointClick on a marker / ref) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for the new files (conditional spreads honour `exactOptionalPropertyTypes`, `noUncheckedIndexedAccess` guarded). Exported via components/ui barrel. | 11.703 | UI: chart-line-wavetrend primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.721 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-wavetrend.tsx` ships `<ChartLineWavetrend>` -- a pure-SVG TWO-PANEL line chart with a **WaveTrend Oscillator** panel from the smoothed deviation of price off its average (the price panel on top, the WaveTrend panel below). **The computation:** the WaveTrend Oscillator measures the SMOOTHED DEVIATION of the price off its own moving average. It is a close-only indicator, so `ChartLineWavetrendPoint` carries `{x, value}`. `computeLineWavetrendEma(values, period)` is the EMA in the incremental form `prev + alpha*(v - prev)` (so a constant series stays exactly constant); `computeLineWavetrendSma(values, period)` is the SMA. `computeLineWavetrend(values, options)` runs the pipeline: the EMA BASELINE `esa = EMA(price, channelLength)`, the EMA of the ABSOLUTE DEVIATION from it `d = EMA(|price - esa|, channelLength)`, the CHANNEL INDEX `ci = (price - esa) / (0.015 * d)` (guarded to 0 when the average deviation is zero), the EMA of `ci` into **WT1** (`= EMA(ci, averageLength)`, the main WaveTrend line -- the smoothed normalized deviation) and a four-period SMA of WT1 into the SIGNAL line **WT2**. `classifyLineWavetrendZone(wt1, upper, lower)` marks a bar `overbought` ABOVE the upper threshold, `oversold` BELOW the lower, `neutral` between, `none` while undefined. **Worked fixture:** the EMA cascade and the `0.015` divisor make the WaveTrend transcendental for a varying series, but a CONSTANT series collapses to exact zero -- with a flat price the EMA baseline equals the price, so the absolute deviation is zero, the average deviation `d` is zero, the channel index is GUARDED to 0 (avoiding the `0/0`), and both WT1 and WT2 are exactly 0; the test asserts every WT1 reading is 0 and every defined WT2 reading is 0 for a constant series. The EMA helper is checked with a dyadic alpha (`[4,8,16]` -> `[4,6,11]` for period 3), the SMA helper with `[10,20,30,40]` -> `[null,15,25,35]`, and WT2 is verified to be the four-period SMA of WT1. The zone classifier is tested directly. The run / component use a swinging fixture and are checked structurally. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs `<ChartLineCci>` -- the Commodity Channel Index IS the `(price - SMA) / (0.015 * mean deviation)` channel index, but raw; the WaveTrend takes that channel index and EMA-SMOOTHS it (and uses an EMA baseline, not an SMA), so WT1 is the smoothed CCI-style reading. vs `<ChartLineStochRsi>` -- the Stochastic RSI runs a stochastic on the RSI; the WaveTrend runs an EMA-of-EMA on a deviation channel. vs `<ChartLineQqe>` / `<ChartLineSmi>` -- those also double-smooth, but the WaveTrend is unique in normalizing the price's deviation by its OWN average deviation before the final smoothing, then crossing WT1 against a short SMA signal line WT2. The defining trait: WT1 + WT2 form a crossover oscillator -- the WT1/WT2 cross plus the overbought/oversold extremes are the signal. All exported helpers carry a `LineWavetrend` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineWavetrendPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `channelLength` (the EMA baseline + deviation length; default 10) and `averageLength` (the final EMA smoothing of the channel index; default 21), each coerced to an integer >= 1; `upperThreshold` (the overbought level; default 60, accepted positive) and `lowerThreshold` (the oversold level; default -60, accepted negative); `pricePanelRatio` (default 0.55, clamped to [0.3,0.8]); `gap`; `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`priceColor`/`wt1Color`/`wt2Color`/`overboughtColor`/`oversoldColor`/`neutralColor`/`zeroColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showWt1`/`showWt2`/`showZeroLine`/`showThresholds`/`showMarkers`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `wt1` / `wt2`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_WAVETREND_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/CHANNEL_LENGTH/AVERAGE_LENGTH/UPPER_THRESHOLD/LOWER_THRESHOLD/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/PRICE_COLOR/WT1_COLOR/WT2_COLOR/OVERBOUGHT_COLOR/OVERSOLD_COLOR/NEUTRAL_COLOR/ZERO_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `CHART_LINE_WAVETREND_SIGNAL_PERIOD` (the fixed WT2 SMA length, 4); `ChartLineWavetrendZone` type (`overbought`|`oversold`|`neutral`|`none`); `ChartLineWavetrendComputed` interface; `getLineWavetrendFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value); `normalizeLineWavetrendPeriod(period, fallback)` (coerce to an integer >= 1; non-finite or sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineWavetrendEma(values, period)` / `computeLineWavetrendSma(values, period)` (the smoothing helpers; non-array -> []); `computeLineWavetrend(values, options?)` -- returns `{esa, d, ci, wt1, wt2}`; `classifyLineWavetrendZone(wt1, upper, lower)` (the overbought/oversold/neutral/none rule); `runLineWavetrend(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, channelLength, averageLength, upperThreshold, lowerThreshold, esa, d, ci, wt1, wt2, samples, wt1Final, overboughtCount, oversoldCount, neutralCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, wt1, wt2, zone}`; `computeLineWavetrendLayout({data, channelLength, ...})` -- stacks the price panel above the WaveTrend panel (the WaveTrend panel y-domain dynamic, seeded with 0 and both thresholds), projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `wt1Path`, `wt2Path`, one `markers` entry per defined WT1 bar coloured by zone, and `zeroY` / `upperY` / `lowerY`; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLineWavetrendChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions WaveTrend Oscillator + smoothed deviation + moving average + the zone counts (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-wavetrend-aria-desc"` span; the price line + WT1 + WT2 + every price dot + every marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-wavetrend / -aria-desc / -svg / -empty / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -panel-label / -zero-line / -thresholds / -threshold-line / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -wt1-line / -wt2-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-wt1 / -tooltip-wt2 / -tooltip-zone / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config); root mirrors `data-channel-length`, `data-average-length`, `data-upper-threshold`, `data-lower-threshold`, `data-wt1-final`, `data-overbought-count`, `data-oversold-count`, `data-neutral-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`, `data-empty`; grid / axis / tick / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"wt"`; threshold lines carry `data-level="upper"|"lower"`; markers expose `data-wt1` and `data-zone`. The WaveTrend panel carries a solid zero line, dashed upper (red) and lower (green) threshold lines, the teal WT1 line, the dashed amber WT2 signal line and zone-coloured markers on WT1 (red overbought, green oversold, slate neutral). Tooltip on a price dot or a marker shows x, price, WT1, WT2 and the zone. Config badge in top-left calls out `WT <channelLength>/<averageLength>`. | 11.704 | UI: chart-line-cyber-cycle primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.722 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-cyber-cycle.tsx` ships `<ChartLineCyberCycle>` -- a pure-SVG TWO-PANEL line chart with an **Ehlers Cyber Cycle** oscillator panel from a smoothed second difference of price (the price panel on top, the Cyber Cycle panel below). **The computation:** John Ehlers' Cyber Cycle extracts the cyclic swing of the market. It is a close-only indicator, so `ChartLineCyberCyclePoint` carries `{x, value}`. `computeLineCyberCycleSmooth(values)` is the four-tap Ehlers smoother `(value + 2*value[-1] + 2*value[-2] + value[-3]) / 6`, defined from the fourth bar. `computeLineCyberCycle(values, alpha)` feeds the SECOND DIFFERENCE of the smoothed price (`smooth[i] - 2*smooth[i-1] + smooth[i-2]`) into a TWO-POLE RESONANT FILTER -- `cycle = (1 - 0.5a)^2 * secondDiff + 2(1-a)*cycle[-1] - (1-a)^2*cycle[-2]` -- whose feedback turns the second-difference impulse into an oscillation around zero; it returns the cycle line and the TRIGGER (the cycle delayed one bar). While the smoother warms up (the first seven bars) the cycle uses the RAW price second difference, quartered (`(value - 2*value[-1] + value[-2]) / 4`), with the first two bars seeded to 0. `classifyLineCyberCycleZone(cycle)` marks a bar `up` ABOVE zero, `down` BELOW, `flat` at zero, `none` for a non-finite reading. **Worked fixture:** the resonant filter and the smoother's `/6` make the cycle transcendental past the warm-up, but two anchors are exact. A SHORT series (six bars, all inside the smoother warm-up) computes the cycle as the raw price second difference QUARTERED -- pure integer arithmetic; the fixture `[40,48,60,56,72,64]` gives `[0,0,1,-4,5,-6]`, asserted with `toEqual`, and the trigger lags it to `[null,0,0,1,-4,5]` (zones `[flat,flat,up,down,up,down]`). A CONSTANT series leaves every second difference -- raw AND smoothed -- at zero, so the cycle is EXACTLY 0 across both the warm-up and the resonant path; the test asserts every cycle reading is 0. The smoother is checked with `[6,12,18,24,30,36]` -> `[null,null,null,15,21,27]` (each `/6` of a multiple-of-six weighted sum), and the zone classifier is tested directly. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs `<ChartLineMacd>` -- the MACD is a difference of two EMAs (a high-pass); the Cyber Cycle is a two-pole RESONANT band-pass that rings at the market's dominant cycle. vs `<ChartLineRoofing>` / `<ChartLineSupersmoother>`-style Ehlers filters -- those are pure low-pass / band-pass smoothers; the Cyber Cycle pairs the band-pass with a trigger line for crossover signals. vs `<ChartLineDecycler>` -- the decycler REMOVES the cycle to leave the trend; the Cyber Cycle ISOLATES the cycle and discards the trend. The defining trait: it operates on the SECOND DIFFERENCE (acceleration) of the smoothed price, not the price or its first difference, and the resonant feedback gives it a far cleaner cyclic read than a raw oscillator. All exported helpers carry a `LineCyberCycle` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineCyberCyclePoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `alpha` (the resonant filter smoothing constant; default 0.07, accepted finite and in the open interval (0,1)); `pricePanelRatio` (default 0.55, clamped to [0.3,0.8]); `gap`; `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`priceColor`/`cycleColor`/`triggerColor`/`upColor`/`downColor`/`flatColor`/`zeroColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showCycle`/`showTrigger`/`showZeroLine`/`showMarkers`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `cycle` / `trigger`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_CYBER_CYCLE_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/ALPHA/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/PRICE_COLOR/CYCLE_COLOR/TRIGGER_COLOR/UP_COLOR/DOWN_COLOR/FLAT_COLOR/ZERO_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `CHART_LINE_CYBER_CYCLE_WARMUP` (the bar count below which the raw seed is used, 7); `ChartLineCyberCycleZone` type (`up`|`down`|`flat`|`none`); `ChartLineCyberCycleComputed` interface; `getLineCyberCycleFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value); `normalizeLineCyberCycleAlpha(alpha, fallback)` (coerce into (0,1); non-finite / sub-0 / >=1 falls back); `computeLineCyberCycleSmooth(values)` (the four-tap smoother, null through the warm-up; non-array -> []); `computeLineCyberCycle(values, alpha)` -- returns `{smooth, cycle, trigger}` (non-array -> empty arrays); `classifyLineCyberCycleZone(cycle)` (the up/down/flat/none rule); `runLineCyberCycle(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, alpha, smooth, cycle, trigger, samples, cycleFinal, upCount, downCount, flatCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, cycle, trigger, zone}`; `computeLineCyberCycleLayout({data, alpha, ...})` -- stacks the price panel above the cycle panel (the cycle panel y-domain dynamic, seeded with 0 and spanning the cycle + trigger), projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `cyclePath`, `triggerPath`, one `markers` entry per bar coloured by zone, and `zeroY`; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLineCyberCycleChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Ehlers Cyber Cycle + smoothed + second difference + the zone counts (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-cyber-cycle-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the cycle line + the trigger line + every price dot + every marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-cyber-cycle / -aria-desc / -svg / -empty / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -panel-label / -zero-line / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -cycle-line / -trigger-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-cycle / -tooltip-trigger / -tooltip-zone / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config); root mirrors `data-alpha`, `data-cycle-final`, `data-up-count`, `data-down-count`, `data-flat-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`, `data-empty`; grid / axis / tick / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"cycle"`; markers expose `data-cycle` and `data-zone`. The cycle panel carries a solid zero line, the violet Cyber Cycle line, the dashed amber trigger line and zone-coloured markers (green above zero, red below, slate at zero). Tooltip on a price dot or a marker shows x, price, the cycle, the trigger and the zone. Config badge in top-left calls out `CC <alpha>`. The legend has three toggle items (Price / Cyber Cycle / Trigger). 70 vitest cases (getLineCyberCycleFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + value** + non-array -> [] + empty -> [] + preserves non-finite**, computeLineCyberCycleSmooth incl. non-array -> [] + matches length + the warm-up null + **the four-tap weighted average of the window** + **keeps a constant series at its constant level** + **null for a window with a non-finite value**, computeLineCyberCycle incl. non-array -> empty arrays + matches length + **seeds the first two bars at zero** + **uses the quartered price second difference during warm-up** + **lags the trigger one bar behind the cycle** + **holds the cycle at zero for a constant series** + **holds the trigger at zero for a constant series** + **every cycle reading finite for finite input** + **runs the resonant filter past the warm-up** + **a non-zero cycle for a varying series**, classifyLineCyberCycleZone incl. **up / down / flat** + **none for a null / non-finite cycle**, runLineCyberCycle incl. not-ok short series + ok + **the default alpha 0.07** + **a custom alpha** + **the exact warm-up cycle for a short series** + **the warm-up cycle zones** + **self-consistent zone counts** + **a constant series wholly flat** + one sample per point + carries the smooth/cycle/trigger arrays + x-sorting + empty/null -> ok=false, computeLineCyberCycleLayout incl. not-ok single point + **collapsed canvas -> ok=false** + ok + **stacks the price panel above the cycle panel** + price/cycle/trigger paths + **one price dot per bar** / **one marker per bar** / **a valid zone on each marker** / **both panel labels** / config badge `CC 0.5` / **legend toggle hides the cycle line** / **showTrigger=false hides the trigger line** / | 11.705 | UI: chart-line-band-pass primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.723 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-band-pass.tsx` ships `<ChartLineBandPass>` -- a pure-SVG TWO-PANEL line chart with an **Ehlers Bandpass Filter** panel isolating the dominant cycle band of the price (the price panel on top, the bandpass panel below). **The computation:** John Ehlers' Bandpass Filter is a two-pole resonant filter tuned to a chosen cycle. It is a close-only indicator, so `ChartLineBandPassPoint` carries `{x, value}`. `computeLineBandPassCoefficients(period, bandwidth)` turns a centre `period` and a fractional `bandwidth` into the three filter constants: `beta = cos(2*pi / period)`, `gamma = 1 / cos(2*pi*bandwidth / period)` and `alpha = gamma - sqrt(gamma^2 - 1)`; when the discriminant `gamma^2 - 1` is non-positive (a degenerate band) `alpha` falls back to 1, which zeroes the drive term so the filter stays at rest. `computeLineBandPass(values, period, bandwidth)` drives the TWO-BAR PRICE DIFFERENCE (`price[i] - price[i-2]`) through that tuned resonance -- `bp[i] = 0.5*(1-alpha)*(price[i] - price[i-2]) + beta*(1+alpha)*bp[i-1] - alpha*bp[i-2]` -- seeding the first two bars at zero; the feedback turns the two-bar difference into a clean oscillation around zero AT THE CHOSEN CYCLE, rejecting the trend below the band and the noise above it, returning the bandpass array. A TRIGGER line (the bandpass delayed one bar) accompanies it; their crossover is the signal. `classifyLineBandPassZone(bp)` marks a bar `up` ABOVE zero, `down` BELOW, `flat` at zero, `none` for a non-finite reading. **Worked fixture:** the `cos` / `sqrt` coefficients make the bandpass transcendental for a general series, but one anchor is exact. A CONSTANT series leaves every two-bar price difference (`price[i] - price[i-2]`) at zero; with the first two bars seeded at zero the recursion `0.5*(1-alpha)*0 + beta*(1+alpha)*0 - alpha*0` stays exactly at zero for every later bar, so the bandpass is EXACTLY 0 across the whole series -- the test asserts every reading is 0 and the run is wholly `flat` (flatCount = bar count, up/down 0). The coefficients are checked structurally: `beta` against its defining identity `cos(2*pi / period)` with `toBe`, `gamma >= 1` for a valid band, `alpha` inside the open unit interval, the bandwidth/period normalization. A two-cycle twelve-bar sinusoid fixture confirms the bandpass rings with BOTH SIGNS (the filter resonates) and that the trigger lags the bandpass exactly one bar. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs `<ChartLineCyberCycle>` -- the Cyber Cycle runs a resonant filter on the SMOOTHED SECOND DIFFERENCE of price (acceleration of a four-tap smoother) with a single smoothing constant `alpha`; the Bandpass Filter runs on the raw TWO-BAR DIFFERENCE of price and is tuned by TWO inputs, a centre `period` and a fractional `bandwidth`, that set the resonant band explicitly. vs `<ChartLineMacd>` / `<ChartLinePpo>` -- those are the difference of two exponential averages (a high-pass that still drifts with the trend); the Bandpass Filter is a true BAND-PASS that rejects both the trend below the band and the noise above it, ringing only at the dominant cycle. vs `<ChartLineDecycler>`-style filters -- a decycler REMOVES the cycle to leave the trend; the Bandpass ISOLATES the cycle band and discards the trend. The defining trait: the `bandwidth` input narrows or widens the passband around `period`, so the same primitive can be tuned from a sharp single-cycle resonator to a broad cycle sweep. All exported helpers carry a `LineBandPass` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineBandPassPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `period` (the centre cycle period; default 20, coerced to an integer >= 2); `bandwidth` (the fractional bandwidth; default 0.3, accepted finite and in the open interval (0,1)); `pricePanelRatio` (default 0.55, clamped to [0.3,0.8]); `gap`; `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`priceColor`/`bandpassColor`/`triggerColor`/`upColor`/`downColor`/`flatColor`/`zeroColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showBandpass`/`showTrigger`/`showZeroLine`/`showMarkers`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `bandpass` / `trigger`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_BAND_PASS_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/BANDWIDTH/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/PRICE_COLOR/BANDPASS_COLOR/TRIGGER_COLOR/UP_COLOR/DOWN_COLOR/FLAT_COLOR/ZERO_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineBandPassZone` type (`up`|`down`|`flat`|`none`); `ChartLineBandPassCoefficients` interface (`{beta, gamma, alpha}`); `getLineBandPassFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value); `normalizeLineBandPassPeriod(period, fallback)` (coerce to an integer >= 2; non-finite / sub-2 falls back, fractional floors); `normalizeLineBandPassBandwidth(bandwidth, fallback)` (coerce into (0,1); non-finite / sub-0 / >=1 falls back); `computeLineBandPassCoefficients(period, bandwidth)` (the `{beta, gamma, alpha}` triple); `computeLineBandPass(values, period, bandwidth)` (the bandpass array, first two bars 0; non-array -> []); `classifyLineBandPassZone(bp)` (the up/down/flat/none rule); `runLineBandPass(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, period, bandwidth, beta, gamma, alpha, bp, trigger, samples, bpFinal, upCount, downCount, flatCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, bp, trigger, zone}`; `computeLineBandPassLayout({data, period, bandwidth, ...})` -- stacks the price panel above the bandpass panel (the bandpass panel y-domain dynamic, seeded with 0 and spanning the bandpass + trigger), projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `bandpassPath`, `triggerPath`, one `markers` entry per bar coloured by zone, and `zeroY`; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLineBandPassChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Ehlers Bandpass Filter + dominant cycle + resonant + the zone counts (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-band-pass-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the bandpass line + the trigger line + every price dot + every marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-band-pass / -aria-desc / -svg / -empty / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -panel-label / -zero-line / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -bandpass-line / -trigger-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-bandpass / -tooltip-trigger / -tooltip-zone / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-bandwidth`, `data-bp-final`, `data-up-count`, `data-down-count`, `data-flat-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`, `data-empty`; grid / axis / tick / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"bandpass"`; markers expose `data-bp` and `data-zone`. The bandpass panel carries a solid zero line, the violet bandpass line, the dashed amber trigger line and zone-coloured markers (green above zero, red below, slate at zero). Tooltip on a price dot or a marker shows x, price, the bandpass, the trigger and the zone. Config badge in top-left calls out `BP <period>/<bandwidth>`. The legend has three toggle items (Price / Bandpass / Trigger). 78 vitest cases (getLineBandPassFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + value** + non-array -> [] + empty -> [] + preserves order, normalizeLineBandPassPeriod incl. **keeps a valid integer period** + **floors a fractional period** + **falls back below two** + **falls back for zero** + **falls back for negative** + **falls back for non-finite**, normalizeLineBandPassBandwidth incl. **keeps a bandwidth in the open unit interval** + **falls back for zero** + **falls back for one or above** + **falls back for negative** + **falls back for non-finite**, computeLineBandPassCoefficients incl. **finite beta/gamma/alpha** + **beta is the cosine of two pi over the period** + **gamma at or above one** + **alpha inside the open unit interval** + **responds to the bandwidth** + **normalizes an out-of-range bandwidth**, computeLineBandPass incl. non-array -> [] + empty -> [] + matches length + **seeds the first two bars at zero** + **holds a constant series at zero across every bar** + **every reading finite for finite input** + **a non-zero oscillation for a varying series** + **oscillates across zero with both not-ok empty/null + ok + **the default period 20 / bandwidth 0.3** + **a custom period and bandwidth** + **holds the bandpass at zero and wholly flat for a constant series** + **a valid zone on each
