| 11.706 | UI: chart-line-highpass primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.724 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-highpass.tsx` ships `<ChartLineHighpass>` -- a pure-SVG TWO-PANEL line chart with an **Ehlers Highpass Filter** panel removing the low-frequency trend component of the price (the price panel on top, the highpass panel below). **The computation:** John Ehlers' Highpass Filter strips the trend (low-frequency) content of a price series, passing only the faster cyclic swings. It is a TWO-POLE filter and a close-only indicator, so `ChartLineHighpassPoint` carries `{x, value}`. `computeLineHighpassCoefficients(period)` turns a single cutoff `period` into the two-pole filter constants: `a1 = exp(-1.414*pi / period)`, `b1 = 2*a1*cos(1.414*pi / period)`, `c2 = b1`, `c3 = -a1*a1` and `c1 = (1 + c2 - c3) / 4`. `computeLineHighpass(values, period)` drives the price's SECOND DIFFERENCE (`price[i] - 2*price[i-1] + price[i-2]`) through that filter -- `hp[i] = c1*(price[i] - 2*price[i-1] + price[i-2]) + c2*hp[i-1] + c3*hp[i-2]` -- seeding the first two bars at zero, returning the highpass array. A TRIGGER line (the highpass delayed one bar) accompanies it; their crossover is the signal. `classifyLineHighpassZone(hp)` marks a bar `up` ABOVE zero, `down` BELOW, `flat` at zero, `none` for a non-finite reading. **Worked fixture:** the `exp` / `cos` coefficients make the highpass transcendental for a general series, but it has two exact anchors that ARE its defining property. The second difference of any straight line is identically zero -- `(a + k*b) - 2*(a + (k-1)*b) + (a + (k-2)*b) = 0` -- so a CONSTANT series (slope zero) AND a LINEAR RAMP (any non-zero slope) both leave every driving term at zero; with the first two bars seeded at zero the recursion `c1*0 + c2*0 + c3*0` stays exactly at zero for every later bar. The test asserts the constant series `[50,50,...]`, the ascending ramp `[10,14,18,22,26,30,34,38]` (asserted with `toEqual([0,0,0,0,0,0,0,0])`) AND the descending ramp `[100,93,86,79,72,65,58,51]` are ALL driven to exactly zero -- THAT is what makes this a highpass: the trend is removed regardless of its slope or sign. The coefficients are checked structurally: `a1` against its defining identity `exp(-1.414*pi / period)` with `toBe`, `a1` inside the open unit interval, `c3 = -(a1*a1)` (negative), `c1 = (1 + c2 - c3) / 4`. A two-cycle twelve-bar sinusoid fixture (which has curvature, a non-zero second difference) confirms the highpass PASSES the cyclic content with both signs. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs `<ChartLineBandPass>` -- the Bandpass Filter is a two-pole RESONANT filter tuned by a period AND a fractional bandwidth, driven by the two-bar price difference; it isolates one cycle band. The Highpass Filter is tuned by a single cutoff `period`, driven by the SECOND DIFFERENCE, and passes EVERYTHING above the cutoff, not just one band. vs `<ChartLineCyberCycle>` -- the Cyber Cycle resonates on the smoothed second difference; the Highpass does not resonate, it simply removes the trend below the cutoff. vs `<ChartLineMacd>` / `<ChartLinePpo>` -- those are a difference of two exponential averages (a high-pass that still drifts with a strong trend); the Highpass Filter drives a linear trend to EXACTLY zero, which is its defining and structurally distinct property. vs a decycler -- a decycler keeps the trend and discards the cycle; the Highpass keeps the cycle and discards the trend. The defining trait: a straight-line trend of any slope is removed to exactly zero. All exported helpers carry a `LineHighpass` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineHighpassPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `period` (the cutoff period; default 20, coerced to an integer >= 2); `pricePanelRatio` (default 0.55, clamped to [0.3,0.8]); `gap`; `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`priceColor`/`highpassColor`/`triggerColor`/`upColor`/`downColor`/`flatColor`/`zeroColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showHighpass`/`showTrigger`/`showZeroLine`/`showMarkers`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `highpass` / `trigger`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_HIGHPASS_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/PRICE_COLOR/HIGHPASS_COLOR/TRIGGER_COLOR/UP_COLOR/DOWN_COLOR/FLAT_COLOR/ZERO_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineHighpassZone` type (`up`|`down`|`flat`|`none`); `ChartLineHighpassCoefficients` interface (`{a1, b1, c1, c2, c3}`); `getLineHighpassFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value); `normalizeLineHighpassPeriod(period, fallback)` (coerce to an integer >= 2; non-finite / sub-2 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineHighpassCoefficients(period)` (the `{a1, b1, c1, c2, c3}` constants); `computeLineHighpass(values, period)` (the highpass array, first two bars 0; non-array -> []); `classifyLineHighpassZone(hp)` (the up/down/flat/none rule); `runLineHighpass(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, period, a1, b1, c1, c2, c3, hp, trigger, samples, hpFinal, upCount, downCount, flatCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, hp, trigger, zone}`; `computeLineHighpassLayout({data, period, ...})` -- stacks the price panel above the highpass panel (the highpass panel y-domain dynamic, seeded with 0 and spanning the highpass + trigger), projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `highpassPath`, `triggerPath`, one `markers` entry per bar coloured by zone, and `zeroY`; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLineHighpassChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Ehlers Highpass Filter + two-pole + trend + the zone counts (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-highpass-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the highpass line + the trigger line + every price dot + every marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-highpass / -aria-desc / -svg / -empty / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -panel-label / -zero-line / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -highpass-line / -trigger-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-highpass / -tooltip-trigger / -tooltip-zone / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-hp-final`, `data-up-count`, `data-down-count`, `data-flat-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`, `data-empty`; grid / axis / tick / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"highpass"`; markers expose `data-hp` and `data-zone`. The highpass panel carries a solid zero line, the violet highpass line, the dashed amber trigger line and zone-coloured markers (green above zero, red below, slate at zero). Tooltip on a price dot or a marker shows x, price, the highpass, the trigger and the zone. Config badge in top-left calls out `HP <period>`. The legend has three toggle items (Price / Highpass / Trigger). 76 vitest cases (getLineHighpassFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + value** + non-array -> [] + empty -> [] + preserves order, normalizeLineHighpassPeriod incl. **keeps a valid integer period** + **floors a fractional period** + **falls back below two** + **falls back for zero** + **falls back for negative** + **falls back for non-finite**, computeLineHighpassCoefficients incl. **finite a1/b1/c1/c2/c3** + **a1 is exp of minus 1.414 pi over the period** + **a1 inside the open unit interval** + **c3 is the negated square of a1** + **c1 derived from one plus c2 minus c3 over four** + **responds to the period**, computeLineHighpass incl. non-array -> [] + empty -> [] + matches length + **seeds the first two bars at zero** + **holds a constant series at zero across every bar** + **drives an ascending linear ramp to exactly zero** + **drives a descending linear ramp to exactly zero** + **every reading finite for finite input** + **a non-zero output for a curved series** + **oscillates across zero with both signs for a cyclic series** + **responds to the period**, classifyLineHighpassZone incl. **up / down / flat** + **none for a null / non-finite highpass**, runLineHighpass incl. not-ok short series + not-ok empty/null + ok + **the default period 20** + **a custom period** + **holds the highpass at zero and wholly flat for a constant series** + **removes a linear trend, leaving the highpass at zero and flat** + **a valid zone on each sample** + **self-consistent zone counts** + **lags the trigger one bar behind the highpass** + one sample per point + **carries finite filter coefficients** + carries the highpass array + x-sorting + **the final highpass reading**, computeLineHighpassLayout incl. **the zero line inside the highpass panel** + carries the run, describeLineHighpassChart incl. **the indicator name** + **removing the trend** + **the two-pole filter** + the zone counts + No data, | 11.707 | UI: chart-line-instantaneous-trend primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.725 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-instantaneous-trend.tsx` ships `<ChartLineInstantaneousTrend>` -- a pure-SVG SINGLE-PANEL line chart with an **Ehlers Instantaneous Trendline** overlay from a low-lag recursive smoother (the price line, the Instantaneous Trendline and its trigger share one panel). **The computation:** John Ehlers' Instantaneous Trendline is a low-lag trend follower -- it tracks the trend of the price with far less lag than a moving average. It is a close-only indicator, so `ChartLineInstantaneousTrendPoint` carries `{x, value}`. `computeLineInstantaneousTrendCoefficients(alpha)` turns a single smoothing constant `alpha` into the five filter weights `c1 = alpha - alpha^2/4`, `c2 = alpha^2/2`, `c3 = alpha - 0.75*alpha^2`, `c4 = 2*(1 - alpha)` and `c5 = (1 - alpha)^2`. `computeLineInstantaneousTrend(values, alpha)` runs the recursive low-lag formula `ITrend[i] = c1*price[i] + c2*price[i-1] - c3*price[i-2] + c4*ITrend[i-1] - c5*ITrend[i-2]`; the first bar seeds with the price, the second with the MIDPOINT of the first two prices, bars two through six use the short averaging seed `(price[i] + 2*price[i-1] + price[i-2]) / 4`, and from the SEVENTH bar the recursion takes over (`CHART_LINE_INSTANTANEOUS_TREND_WARMUP` = 7). A TRIGGER line, `2*ITrend - ITrend[i-2]`, extrapolates the trendline's recent slope to lead it; their crossover is the signal. `classifyLineInstantaneousTrendZone(itrend, trigger)` marks a bar `up` when the trigger LEADS the trendline (rising), `down` when it LAGS (falling), `flat` when they match, `none` for a non-finite reading (the first two bars, where the trigger has not warmed up). **Worked fixture:** the recursive region is `alpha`-dependent and transcendental, but the seven-bar warm-up is exact AND `alpha`-independent -- the seed `(price[i] + 2*price[i-1] + price[i-2]) / 4` carries no `alpha`. A six-bar integer fixture chosen so every weighted sum divides by four computes to exact integers: `[40,44,48,44,40,36]` (a rise then a fall) gives the trendline `[40,42,44,46,44,40]` (asserted with `toEqual`), the trigger `[null,null,48,50,44,34]`, and zones `[none,none,up,up,flat,down]` -- the warm-up region exercises EVERY zone, and the trendline result holds for any `alpha` (a separate test confirms alpha 0.07 and 0.5 give the identical warm-up itrend). The coefficients are checked against their defining identities with `toBe` and against the constant-preserving property: the price-side weights `c1 + c2 - c3` collapse to `alpha^2` and the total `(c1 + c2 - c3) + (c4 - c5)` sums to one (so a constant input is reproduced). A ten-bar constant series confirms the recursive region reproduces the constant (`toBeCloseTo`). **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs `<ChartLineHighpass>` / `<ChartLineBandPass>` / `<ChartLineCyberCycle>` -- those are Ehlers OSCILLATORS that strip the trend and ring around zero in a sub-panel; the Instantaneous Trendline is the OPPOSITE -- it KEEPS the trend and discards the cycle, tracking the price level itself, so it is a single-panel OVERLAY, not a zero-centered oscillator. vs `<ChartLineCoral>` -- the Coral Trend cascades six exponential poles and recombines them with the Tillson coefficients; the Instantaneous Trendline uses a single short two-pole recursion plus a three-tap price term, and its hallmark is a hand-tuned coefficient set arranged to cut lag while still summing to one. vs `<ChartLineMovingAvg>` / `<ChartLineWma>` / `<ChartLineEma>` -- a moving average lags the price by roughly half its window; the Instantaneous Trendline is built specifically to MINIMIZE that lag, which is the "instantaneous" in its name. vs `<ChartLineSsl>` -- the SSL Channel flips between high and low averages on a close cross; the Instantaneous Trendline is a single continuous low-lag line with a trigger. The defining trait: a low-lag recursive smoother that tracks the trend, paired with a slope-extrapolated trigger. All exported helpers carry a `LineInstantaneousTrend` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineInstantaneousTrendPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `alpha` (the smoothing constant; default 0.07, accepted finite and in the open interval (0,1)); `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`priceColor`/`itrendColor`/`triggerColor`/`upColor`/`downColor`/`flatColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showItrend`/`showTrigger`/`showMarkers`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `itrend` / `trigger`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_INSTANTANEOUS_TREND_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/ALPHA/PRICE_COLOR/ITREND_COLOR/TRIGGER_COLOR/UP_COLOR/DOWN_COLOR/FLAT_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `CHART_LINE_INSTANTANEOUS_TREND_WARMUP` (the bar count below which the averaging seed is used, 7); `ChartLineInstantaneousTrendZone` type (`up`|`down`|`flat`|`none`); `ChartLineInstantaneousTrendCoefficients` interface (`{c1, c2, c3, c4, c5}`); `ChartLineInstantaneousTrendComputed` interface (`{itrend, trigger}`); `getLineInstantaneousTrendFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value); `normalizeLineInstantaneousTrendAlpha(alpha, fallback)` (coerce into (0,1); non-finite / sub-0 / >=1 falls back); `computeLineInstantaneousTrendCoefficients(alpha)` (the five-weight set); `computeLineInstantaneousTrend(values, alpha)` -- returns `{itrend, trigger}` (non-array -> empty arrays); `classifyLineInstantaneousTrendZone(itrend, trigger)` (the up/down/flat/none rule); `runLineInstantaneousTrend(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, alpha, c1, c2, c3, c4, c5, itrend, trigger, samples, itrendFinal, upCount, downCount, flatCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, itrend, trigger, zone}`; `computeLineInstantaneousTrendLayout({data, alpha, width, height, padding})` -- projects a single panel with the price line, the Instantaneous Trendline and the dashed trigger overlaid, one `priceDots` entry per bar, one `markers` entry per bar coloured by zone, and the value domain spanning the price, the trendline and the trigger; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLineInstantaneousTrendChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Ehlers Instantaneous Trendline + low-lag + recursive smoother + the zone counts (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-instantaneous-trend-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the trendline + the trigger line + every price dot + every marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-instantaneous-trend / -aria-desc / -svg / -empty / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -itrend-line / -trigger-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-itrend / -tooltip-trigger / -tooltip-zone / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config); root mirrors `data-alpha`, `data-itrend-final`, `data-up-count`, `data-down-count`, `data-flat-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`, `data-empty`; markers expose `data-itrend` and `data-zone`. The trendline is a solid violet line, the trigger a dashed amber line, with zone-coloured markers on the trendline (green rising, red falling, slate flat). Tooltip on a price dot or a marker shows x, price, the trendline, the trigger and the trend zone. Config badge in top-left calls out `ITrend <alpha>`. The legend has three toggle items (Price / Instantaneous Trendline / Trigger). 75 vitest cases (getLineInstantaneousTrendFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + value** + non-array -> [] + empty -> [] + preserves order, normalizeLineInstantaneousTrendAlpha incl. **keeps an alpha in the open unit interval** + **falls back for zero** + **falls back for one or above** + **falls back for negative** + **falls back for non-finite**, computeLineInstantaneousTrendCoefficients incl. **finite c1 through c5** + **matches the price-side coefficient identities** + **matches the feedback coefficient identities** + **collapses the price-side weights to alpha squared** + **sums all weights to one so a constant is preserved** + **responds to alpha**, computeLineInstantaneousTrend incl. non-array -> empty arrays + empty -> empty arrays + matches length on both arrays + **seeds the first bar with the price** + **seeds the second bar with the midpoint of the first two prices** + **computes the exact warm-up seed for an integer fixture** + **extrapolates the trigger two bars off the trendline** + **leaves the warm-up region independent of alpha** + **reproduces a constant series through the recursive region** + **every trendline reading finite past the warm-up** + **responds to alpha in the recursive region**, classifyLineInstantaneousTrendZone incl. **up / down / flat** + **none for a null trendline / null trigger / non-finite reading**, runLineInstantaneousTrend incl. not-ok short series + not-ok empty/null + ok + **the default alpha** + **a custom alpha** + **the mixed fixture zones** + **the mixed fixture zone counts** + **holds a constant series flat after the warm-up seed** + **a valid zone on each sample** + **self-consistent zone counts** + **marks the first two bars none while the trigger warms up** + **carries the trendline and trigger on each sample** + one sample per point + **carries finite filter coefficients** + carries the trendline + trigger arrays + x-sorting + **the final trendline reading**, computeLineInstantaneousTrendLayout incl. not-ok single point + **collapsed canvas -> ok=false** + ok + price/trendline/trigger paths + **one price dot per bar** + **one marker per bar** + **the value domain spans the price and the trendline** + carries the run, describeLineInstantaneousTrendChart incl. **the indicator name** + **the low-lag character** | 11.708 | UI: chart-line-mesa-sine primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.726 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-mesa-sine.tsx` ships `<ChartLineMesaSine>` -- a pure-SVG TWO-PANEL line chart with an **Ehlers MESA Sine Wave** panel plotting the cycle phase sine and the lead sine (the price panel on top, the sine wave panel below). **The computation:** John Ehlers' MESA Sine Wave plots the dominant cycle phase as a pair of sine lines. It is a close-only indicator, so `ChartLineMesaSinePoint` carries `{x, value}`. `computeLineMesaSineSma(values, period)` is the simple moving average that detrends the price (null through the warm-up). `computeLineMesaSinePair(phase)` is the pure pair for a phase angle: `sine = sin(phase)` and `leadSine = sin(phase + 45 degrees)` (the lead constant `MESA_SINE_LEAD = pi/4`). `computeLineMesaSine(values, period)` detrends the price (`cycle = price - SMA`) to leave the cyclic swing, pairs that cycle with a QUADRATURE companion -- the cycle delayed a quarter period, a 90-degree phase shift -- so the dominant cycle PHASE is `atan2(quadrature, in-phase)`, and plots the sine and the lead sine of that phase; when the detrended cycle has no measurable amplitude (below `MESA_SINE_EPSILON`, e.g. a flat market) the phase, sine and lead sine are left null. `classifyLineMesaSineZone(sine, leadSine)` marks a bar `up` when the lead sine is ABOVE the sine, `down` when BELOW, `flat` when level, `none` for a non-finite reading -- the sine/lead-sine CROSSOVER is the cycle turning-point signal the indicator names. **Worked fixture:** the phase runs through `atan2` and `sin` so it is transcendental, but the indicator has clean exact anchors. The fixture `[8,16,24,32,40,32,24,16]` is chosen so its period-4 moving average is an exact integer -- SMA `[-,-,-,20,28,32,32,28]` -- giving the exact integer cycle `[-,-,-,12,12,0,-8,-12]` (asserted with `toEqual`); with a quarter delay of one bar the quadrature pair lands the phase on the CLEAN quadrature angles `atan2(12,12) = pi/4`, `atan2(12,0) = pi/2`, `atan2(0,-8) = pi`, so the sine hits `sqrt(1/2)`, `1` and the lead sine the values 45 degrees ahead (all `toBeCloseTo`). A CONSTANT series has no cycle: the detrended cycle is EXACTLY zero, the amplitude falls below the epsilon floor, and the sine and lead sine drop out entirely (every reading null) -- the exact "no cycle, no wave" anchor. The `computeLineMesaSinePair` helper is checked against its defining identities with `toBe` (`sine === sin(phase)`, `leadSine === sin(phase + pi/4)`, `sin(0) === 0`), against the lead identity (the lead sine of a phase equals the sine of that phase advanced 45 degrees), and both lines are confirmed to stay within the unit band. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs `<ChartLineCyberCycle>` / `<ChartLineBandPass>` / `<ChartLineHighpass>` -- those plot a single filtered AMPLITUDE line oscillating around zero; the MESA Sine Wave plots the cycle PHASE, mapped through `sin`, as TWO lines (sine and lead sine) both bounded in [-1,1], and its signal is their crossover, not a zero cross. vs `<ChartLineInstantaneousTrend>` -- that tracks the trend amplitude; the MESA Sine Wave discards amplitude entirely and shows only the phase. vs a plain stochastic or RSI oscillator -- those are bounded but measure overbought/oversold level; the MESA Sine Wave's two lines encode WHERE in the cycle rotation the market sits, and run flat/indeterminate when there is no cycle (a trending market) rather than pinning to an extreme. The defining trait: it detrends, derives a phase from a quadrature pair, and plots `sin(phase)` with `sin(phase + 45deg)`. All exported helpers carry a `LineMesaSine` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineMesaSinePoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `period` (the detrend / cycle period; default 20, coerced to an integer >= 4); `pricePanelRatio` (default 0.55, clamped to [0.3,0.8]); `gap`; `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`priceColor`/`sineColor`/`leadSineColor`/`upColor`/`downColor`/`flatColor`/`zeroColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showSine`/`showLeadSine`/`showZeroLine`/`showMarkers`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `sine` / `leadSine`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_MESA_SINE_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/PRICE_COLOR/SINE_COLOR/LEAD_SINE_COLOR/UP_COLOR/DOWN_COLOR/FLAT_COLOR/ZERO_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `MESA_SINE_LEAD` (the 45-degree lead, pi/4); `MESA_SINE_EPSILON` (the amplitude floor); `ChartLineMesaSineZone` type (`up`|`down`|`flat`|`none`); `ChartLineMesaSinePair` interface (`{sine, leadSine}`); `ChartLineMesaSineComputed` interface (`{sma, cycle, phase, sine, leadSine}`); `getLineMesaSineFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value); `normalizeLineMesaSinePeriod(period, fallback)` (coerce to an integer >= 4; non-finite / sub-4 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineMesaSineSma(values, period)` (the detrend SMA; non-array -> []); `computeLineMesaSinePair(phase)` (the sine/lead-sine pair; non-finite phase -> null pair); `computeLineMesaSine(values, period)` -- returns `{sma, cycle, phase, sine, leadSine}` (non-array -> empty arrays); `classifyLineMesaSineZone(sine, leadSine)` (the up/down/flat/none rule); `runLineMesaSine(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, period, sma, cycle, phase, sine, leadSine, samples, sineFinal, leadSineFinal, upCount, downCount, flatCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, phase, sine, leadSine, zone}`; `computeLineMesaSineLayout({data, period, ...})` -- stacks the price panel above the sine panel (the sine panel fixed to a [-1.1,1.1] band so the unit range always shows with headroom), projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `sinePath`, `leadSinePath`, one `markers` entry per finite-sine bar coloured by zone, and `zeroY`; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLineMesaSineChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Ehlers MESA Sine Wave + cycle phase + lead sine + the zone counts (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-mesa-sine-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the sine line + the lead sine line + every price dot + every marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-mesa-sine / -aria-desc / -svg / -empty / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -panel-label / -zero-line / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -sine-line / -lead-sine-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-sine / -tooltip-lead-sine / -tooltip-zone / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-sine-final`, `data-lead-sine-final`, `data-up-count`, `data-down-count`, `data-flat-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`, `data-empty`; grid / axis / tick / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"sine"`; markers expose `data-sine` and `data-zone`. The sine panel carries a solid zero line, the violet sine line, the dashed amber lead sine line and zone-coloured markers on the sine line (green lead-above, red lead-below, slate level). Tooltip on a price dot or a marker shows x, price, the sine, the lead sine and the phase zone. Config badge in top-left calls out `MESA <period>`. The legend has three toggle items (Price / Sine / Lead Sine). 79 vitest cases (getLineMesaSineFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + value** + non-array -> [] + empty -> [] + preserves order, normalizeLineMesaSinePeriod incl. **keeps a valid integer period** + **floors a fractional period** + **falls back below four** + **falls back for zero** + **falls back for negative** + **falls back for non-finite**, computeLineMesaSineSma incl. non-array -> [] + matches length + **leaves the warm-up window null** + **averages the trailing window** + **the exact integer moving average of the fixture**, computeLineMesaSinePair incl. **takes the sine of the phase** + **takes the lead sine 45 degrees ahead** + **a zero sine at phase zero** + **runs the lead sine exactly 45 degrees ahead of | 11.709 | UI: chart-line-corrected-ma primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.727 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-corrected-ma.tsx` ships `<ChartLineCorrectedMa>` -- a pure-SVG SINGLE-PANEL line chart with a **Corrected Moving Average** overlay iterating until the average sits inside its variance band (the price line, the corrected line and a shaded variance band share one panel). **The computation:** Andreas Uhl's Corrected Moving Average smooths the price and then "corrects" the result toward the simple moving average until it lands inside the variance band. It is a close-only indicator, so `ChartLineCorrectedMaPoint` carries `{x, value}`. `computeLineCorrectedMaSma(values, period)` is the simple moving average over the window (null through the warm-up). `computeLineCorrectedMaVariance(values, period)` is the POPULATION variance over the same window -- the mean squared deviation from the window average. `correctLineCorrectedMa(prevCma, sma, variance)` corrects ONE bar: starting from the prior corrected value `prevCma`, it HALVES the distance to `sma` each iteration until `(value - sma)^2 <= variance` -- the variance band, one standard deviation around the average -- and returns `{cma, iterations}`; a non-positive variance collapses the band to the average itself, and a value already inside the band is returned untouched with zero iterations. `computeLineCorrectedMa(values, period)` runs the pipeline bar by bar, SEEDING the corrected line from the average on its first defined bar and carrying it forward; each later bar starts from the prior corrected value and re-corrects against the new band. `classifyLineCorrectedMaTrend(cma, prevCma)` marks each bar `up` / `down` / `flat` by the slope of the corrected line. **Worked fixture:** the general correction iterates a transcendental count of times, but the indicator has clean exact anchors. A series PERIODIC with the window length -- `[10,20,30,40]` repeated three times, period 4 -- makes every period-4 window a rotation of the SAME set `{10,20,30,40}`, so the moving average is a constant 25 and the variance a constant 125; the corrected line is seeded at 25, and every later bar finds the prior corrected value (25) already exactly at the band CENTRE, so it pins to 25 with ZERO corrections -- `computeLineCorrectedMa(periodic, 4).cma` -> `[null x3, 25 x9]` and `.iterations` -> `[null x3, 0 x9]`, both asserted with `toEqual`. A CONSTANT series has zero variance, collapsing the band to the average, and is reproduced exactly. The `correctLineCorrectedMa` helper is checked directly: a value already inside the band is returned unchanged with 0 iterations; a far value (200 against an average of 5, variance 9) is proven to iterate (`iterations > 0`) and land inside the band (`(cma - sma)^2 <= variance`); the halving never carries the value past the average. The SMA and variance helpers are verified against exact integer fixtures (`[2,4,6,8]` -> variance 5). **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs `<ChartLineMovingAvg>` / `<ChartLineWma>` / `<ChartLineEma>` -- a plain moving average passes every small swing straight through; the Corrected Moving Average ITERATES the prior value back toward the average and stops once it is inside the variance band, so a calm stretch leaves the line pinned to the average (it filters the noise a plain MA carries). vs `<ChartLineInstantaneousTrend>` / `<ChartLineCoral>` -- those reduce LAG with hand-tuned recursive filters; the Corrected Moving Average instead reduces NOISE by a variance-gated iteration, and it is the only one of the family that exposes a per-bar iteration count. vs Bollinger / Keltner band primitives -- those plot a band as the headline output; here the variance band is the STOPPING CRITERION for the correction and is drawn only as a faint shaded backdrop. The defining trait: the corrected value is iterated until it sits inside `mean +/- standard deviation`. All exported helpers carry a `LineCorrectedMa` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineCorrectedMaPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `period` (the window length; default 20, coerced to an integer >= 2); `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`priceColor`/`cmaColor`/`bandColor`/`upColor`/`downColor`/`flatColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showCma`/`showBand`/`showMarkers`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `cma` / `band`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_CORRECTED_MA_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/PRICE_COLOR/CMA_COLOR/BAND_COLOR/UP_COLOR/DOWN_COLOR/FLAT_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `CHART_LINE_CORRECTED_MA_MAX_ITERATIONS` (the per-bar iteration cap, 100); `ChartLineCorrectedMaTrend` type (`up`|`down`|`flat`|`none`); `ChartLineCorrectedMaCorrection` interface (`{cma, iterations}`); `ChartLineCorrectedMaComputed` interface (`{sma, variance, cma, iterations}`); `getLineCorrectedMaFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value); `normalizeLineCorrectedMaPeriod(period, fallback)` (coerce to an integer >= 2; non-finite / sub-2 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineCorrectedMaSma(values, period)` (the moving average; non-array -> []); `computeLineCorrectedMaVariance(values, period)` (the population variance; non-array -> []); `correctLineCorrectedMa(prevCma, sma, variance)` (the one-bar correction -> `{cma, iterations}`); `computeLineCorrectedMa(values, period)` -- returns `{sma, variance, cma, iterations}` (non-array -> empty arrays); `classifyLineCorrectedMaTrend(cma, prevCma)` (the up/down/flat/none rule); `runLineCorrectedMa(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, period, sma, variance, cma, iterations, samples, cmaFinal, upCount, downCount, flatCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, sma, variance, cma, iterations, upper, lower, trend}` (`upper`/`lower` = the band edges, `sma +/- sqrt(variance)`); `computeLineCorrectedMaLayout({data, period, width, height, padding})` -- projects a single panel with the price line, the corrected line overlaid, the variance band as a CLOSED shaded `bandPath` (the upper edge then the lower edge reversed), one `priceDots` entry per bar, one `markers` entry per finite corrected bar coloured by trend, and the value domain spanning the price, the corrected line AND the band edges; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLineCorrectedMaChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Corrected Moving Average + variance band + iterates + the trend counts (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-corrected-ma-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the corrected line + every price dot + every marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-corrected-ma / -aria-desc / -svg / -empty / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -band / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -cma-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-cma / -tooltip-iterations / -tooltip-trend / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-cma-final`, `data-up-count`, `data-down-count`, `data-flat-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`, `data-empty`; markers expose `data-trend`, `data-cma` and `data-iterations`. The variance band is a faint violet shaded region, the corrected line a solid violet line with trend-coloured markers (green rising, red falling, slate flat). Tooltip on a price dot or a marker shows x, price, the corrected value, the iteration count and the trend. Config badge in top-left calls out `CMA <period>`. The legend has three toggle items (Price / Corrected MA / Variance Band). 81 vitest cases (getLineCorrectedMaFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + value** + non-array -> [] + empty -> [] + preserves order, normalizeLineCorrectedMaPeriod incl. **keeps a valid integer period** + **floors a fractional period** + **falls back below two** + **falls back for zero** + **falls back for negative** + **falls back for non-finite**, computeLineCorrectedMaSma incl. non-array -> [] + matches length + **leaves the warm-up window null** + **averages the trailing window** + **holds the moving average constant for a periodic series**, computeLineCorrectedMaVariance incl. non-array -> [] + matches length + **leaves the warm-up window null** + **the mean squared deviation of the window** + **holds the variance constant for a periodic series** + **zero for a constant window**, correctLineCorrectedMa incl. **leaves a value already inside the band unchanged** + **collapses to the average when the variance is zero** + **iterates a far value back inside the variance band** + **pulls the value toward the average, never past it** + **lands the corrected value inside the variance band** + **keeps the prior value when the average is non-finite** + **falls back to the average when the prior value is non-finite**, computeLineCorrectedMa incl. non-array -> empty arrays + matches length on every array + **pins the corrected average to the centre of a periodic series** + **needs no corrections for a periodic series** + **reproduces a constant series exactly** + **keeps every corrected value inside its variance band** + **keeps every corrected value finite where defined**, classifyLineCorrectedMaTrend incl. **up / down / flat** + **none for a null current / null prior value**, runLineCorrectedMa incl. not-ok short series + not-ok empty/null + ok + **the default period** + **a custom period** + **counts a periodic series wholly flat** + **carries the variance band edges on each sample** + **keeps every corrected sample inside its variance band** + **a valid trend on each sample** + one sample per point + carries the sma/variance/cma/iteration arrays + x-sorting + **the final corrected value**, computeLineCorrectedMaLayout incl. not-ok single point + **collapsed canvas -> ok=false** + ok + price/cma/band paths + **one price dot per bar** + **one marker per finite corrected bar** + **closes the band path** + **spans the value domain over the band edges** + carries the run, describeLineCorrectedMaChart incl. **the indicator name** + **the variance band** + **the iteration** + the trend counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / **empty state** / **root data-period/total-points** / img-role svg / **the price + cma lines** / **the variance band** / **one marker per finite corrected bar** / **a valid trend on each marker** / config badge `CMA 4` / **legend toggle hides the cma line** / **showBand=false hides the variance band** / onPointClick on a marker / ref) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. | 11.710 | UI: chart-line-ergodic primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.728 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-ergodic.tsx` ships `<ChartLineErgodic>` -- a pure-SVG TWO-PANEL line chart with an **Ergodic Oscillator** panel from a triple-smoothed momentum ratio with a signal line (the price panel on top, the oscillator panel below). **The computation:** William Blau's Ergodic Oscillator is a triple-smoothed momentum ratio. It is a close-only indicator, so `ChartLineErgodicPoint` carries `{x, value}`. `computeLineErgodicMomentum(values)` is the ONE-BAR price change (`price[i] - price[i-1]`); the first bar has no prior so it is null. `computeLineErgodicEma(values, period)` is an exponential moving average over a series that may carry leading nulls -- the nulls pass through and the average SEEDS from the first finite value with `alpha = 2/(period+1)`. `computeLineErgodic(values, options)` TRIPLE-SMOOTHS the momentum through three cascaded exponential averages (the numerator `num`) and the absolute momentum through the same three (the denominator `den`), then takes `ergodic = 100 * num / den` -- and because every exponential average is a weighted mean with positive weights, `|num| <= den`, so the oscillator is BOUNDED to [-100, 100]; finally it smooths the oscillator into the SIGNAL line (a fourth exponential average). `classifyLineErgodicZone(ergodic, signal)` marks a bar `up` when the oscillator LEADS its signal, `down` when it LAGS, `flat` when level, `none` for a non-finite reading (the first bar, where the momentum is null). **Worked fixture:** the triple cascade is transcendental for a general series, but the indicator has clean PERIOD-INDEPENDENT exact anchors. A CONSTANT series has zero momentum, so the numerator and denominator are both zero and the oscillator reads EXACTLY 0 (`[null, 0, 0, ...]`, `toEqual`). A LINEAR RAMP has a constant momentum `b`: the momentum series fed to the cascade is constant from its first finite bar, and an exponential average of a constant series is exactly that constant -- the `alpha*(b - b)` term is exactly zero -- so the triple cascade preserves `b` in BOTH the numerator and the denominator; the oscillator is therefore `100 * b / |b|`, EXACTLY +100 for an ascending ramp `[10,14,18,22,26,30,34,38]` and -100 for a descending ramp `[38,34,30,26,22,18,14,10]`, on every bar past the first, FOR ANY choice of smoothing periods (`toEqual([null, 100 x7])` / `toEqual([null, -100 x7])`). The exponential-average helper is checked with a dyadic alpha -- period 3 gives `alpha = 0.5`, so `[4,8,16]` smooths exactly to `[4,6,11]` and `[null,4,8,16]` to `[null,4,6,11]`. A varying series confirms the oscillator stays bounded to plus or minus 100 and produces a non-zero reading. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs `<ChartLineMacd>` -- the MACD is a DIFFERENCE of two exponential averages of PRICE (an unbounded spread); the Ergodic Oscillator is a RATIO of the triple-smoothed signed momentum to the triple-smoothed absolute momentum, so it is BOUNDED to [-100,100] and reads pure trend strength, not a price spread. vs `<ChartLineTsi>`-style True Strength Index -- the TSI double-smooths; the Ergodic adds a THIRD smoothing pass for an even cleaner momentum read (the dispatch's "triple-smoothed"). vs `<ChartLineRsi>` / stochastic oscillators -- those are bounded to [0,100] and measure overbought/oversold level; the Ergodic is bounded to [-100,100], centered on zero, and reads directional momentum -- +100 a pure uptrend, -100 a pure downtrend, 0 a flat market. vs `<ChartLineCyberCycle>` / `<ChartLineBandPass>` -- those extract a cycle; the Ergodic measures trend persistence. The defining trait: a triple-smoothed signed-over-absolute momentum ratio paired with a signal-line crossover. All exported helpers carry a `LineErgodic` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineErgodicPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `firstPeriod` / `secondPeriod` / `thirdPeriod` (the three cascade smoothing periods; defaults 12 / 6 / 3, each coerced to an integer >= 1); `signalPeriod` (the signal-line smoothing; default 3); `pricePanelRatio` (default 0.55, clamped to [0.3,0.8]); `gap`; `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`priceColor`/`ergodicColor`/`signalColor`/`upColor`/`downColor`/`flatColor`/`zeroColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showErgodic`/`showSignal`/`showZeroLine`/`showMarkers`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `ergodic` / `signal`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_ERGODIC_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/FIRST_PERIOD/SECOND_PERIOD/THIRD_PERIOD/SIGNAL_PERIOD/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/PRICE_COLOR/ERGODIC_COLOR/SIGNAL_COLOR/UP_COLOR/DOWN_COLOR/FLAT_COLOR/ZERO_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `CHART_LINE_ERGODIC_BOUND` (the oscillator magnitude bound, 100); `ChartLineErgodicZone` type (`up`|`down`|`flat`|`none`); `ChartLineErgodicComputed` interface (`{momentum, ergodic, signal}`); `getLineErgodicFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value); `normalizeLineErgodicPeriod(period, fallback)` (coerce to an integer >= 1; non-finite / sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineErgodicMomentum(values)` (the one-bar change, first bar null; non-array -> []); `computeLineErgodicEma(values, period)` (the null-tolerant exponential average; non-array -> []); `computeLineErgodic(values, options?)` -- returns `{momentum, ergodic, signal}` (non-array -> empty arrays); `classifyLineErgodicZone(ergodic, signal)` (the up/down/flat/none rule); `runLineErgodic(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, firstPeriod, secondPeriod, thirdPeriod, signalPeriod, momentum, ergodic, signal, samples, ergodicFinal, signalFinal, upCount, downCount, flatCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, ergodic, signal, zone}`; `computeLineErgodicLayout({data, firstPeriod, ...})` -- stacks the price panel above the oscillator panel (the oscillator panel FIXED to a [-110,110] band so the full [-100,100] range always shows with headroom), projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `ergodicPath`, `signalPath`, one `markers` entry per finite-oscillator bar coloured by zone, and `zeroY`; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLineErgodicChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Ergodic Oscillator + triple-smooths + signal line + the zone counts (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-ergodic-aria-desc"` span; the price line + the oscillator line + the signal line + every price dot + every marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-ergodic / -aria-desc / -svg / -empty / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -panel-label / -zero-line / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -ergodic-line / -signal-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-ergodic / -tooltip-signal / -tooltip-zone / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config); root mirrors `data-first-period`, `data-second-period`, `data-third-period`, `data-signal-period`, `data-ergodic-final`, `data-up-count`, `data-down-count`, `data-flat-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`, `data-empty`; grid / axis / tick / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"ergodic"`; markers expose `data-ergodic` and `data-zone`. The oscillator panel carries a solid zero line, the violet oscillator line, the dashed amber signal line and zone-coloured markers (green above signal, red below, slate level). Tooltip on a price dot or a marker shows x, price, the oscillator, the signal and the zone. Config badge in top-left calls out `ERG <p1>/<p2>/<p3>`. The legend has three toggle items (Price / Ergodic / Signal). 77 vitest cases (getLineErgodicFinitePoints incl. **keeps only finite x + value** + non-array -> [] + empty -> [] + preserves order, normalizeLineErgodicPeriod incl. **keeps a valid integer period** + **floors a fractional period** + **falls back for zero** + **falls back for negative** + **falls back for non-finite**, computeLineErgodicMomentum incl. non-array -> [] + matches length + **leaves the first bar null** + **takes the one-bar price change** + **null where a price is non-finite**, computeLineErgodicEma incl. non-array -> [] + matches length + **the exact dyadic average for a period-3 alpha** + **passes a leading null through and seeds from the first finite value** + **holds a constant series at its constant level** + **keeps several leading nulls null**, computeLineErgodic incl. non-array -> empty arrays + matches length on every array + **leaves the first bar null** + **reads zero across a constant series** + **pins to +100 on a pure uptrend** + **pins to -100 on a pure downtrend** + **tracks the oscillator with a signal line on an uptrend** + **keeps the oscillator bounded to plus or minus 100** + **every defined reading finite** + **a non-zero oscillator for a varying series**, classifyLineErgodicZone incl. **up / down / flat** + **none for a null oscillator / non-finite signal**, runLineErgodic incl. not-ok short series + not-ok empty/null + ok + **the default periods** + **custom periods** + **counts an uptrend wholly flat against its signal** + **a valid zone on | 11.711 | UI: chart-line-elder-thermo primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.729 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-elder-thermo.tsx` ships `<ChartLineElderThermo>` -- a pure-SVG TWO-PANEL line chart with an **Elder Market Thermometer** panel from the larger of the high and low excursions per bar (the midpoint panel on top, the thermometer panel below). **The computation:** Alexander Elder's Market Thermometer reads the market "temperature" from how far each bar reaches BEYOND the prior bar. It is an OHLC-band indicator, so `ChartLineElderThermoPoint` carries `{x, high, low}`. `computeLineElderThermometer(bars)` takes, for each bar past the first, the LARGER of the two excursions -- the absolute change in the HIGH and the absolute change in the LOW from the prior bar: `max(|high - high[-1]|, |low - low[-1]|)`; the first bar has no prior, so it is null. `computeLineElderThermoEma(values, period)` is an exponential moving average over a series that may carry leading nulls -- the nulls pass through and the average SEEDS from the first finite value with `alpha = 2/(period+1)`. `computeLineElderThermo(bars, options)` pairs the thermometer with its moving average (default period 22). `classifyLineElderThermoZone(thermometer, thermoMa, hotFactor)` marks a bar `calm` when the thermometer is BELOW its average (or exactly 0 -- a bar at rest), `warm` when it is ABOVE the average, `hot` once it crosses the average times the `hotFactor` (default 2), `none` for a non-finite reading. **Worked fixture:** the thermometer is pure `max` / `abs` / difference arithmetic, so it is EXACTLY computable from integer fixtures -- only the moving average is transcendental, and a dyadic alpha makes that exact too. A FLAT market (constant high and low) has no excursion: every thermometer reading is EXACTLY 0 and the market is wholly calm. A STEADY RAMP -- both bands rising by a fixed step (high +4, low +3 per bar) -- has a constant excursion `max(4,3) = 4`, so the thermometer holds a constant 4 and an exponential average of a constant series is exactly that constant (the `alpha*(4-4)` term is exactly zero), so every bar reads warm. A VARYING fixture `high [20,24,19,28,22,30,25,33]`, `low [10,14,12,8,15,11,18,14]` computes to the exact integer thermometer `[null,4,5,9,7,8,7,8]` (asserted with `toEqual`), and with a period-3 moving average (`alpha = 0.5`) the average is the exact dyadic series `[null,4,4.5,6.75,6.875,7.4375,7.21875,7.609375]` (also `toEqual`). The zone classifier is checked directly for calm (below the average), calm (a zero thermometer), warm (above the average) and hot (`classify(12, 5, 2)` -> 12 >= 10 -> hot). The bar that takes the LARGER excursion is verified -- `[{high:50,low:40},{high:51,low:30}]` gives `max(|51-50|, |30-40|) = max(1, 10) = 10`, the low excursion winning. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs `<ChartLineAtr>` / `<ChartLineTrueRange>` -- the Average True Range measures the bar's RANGE including the gap from the prior close; the Elder Thermometer measures the EXCURSION of each band past the prior bar's same band and takes the larger of the two, a different volatility read. vs `<ChartLineElderImpulse>` (11.691) -- the Elder Impulse colours bars from an EMA slope and a MACD histogram (a momentum permission gauge); the Elder Thermometer is purely a volatility "temperature", classified against its own moving average. vs `<ChartLineBollinger>` / `<ChartLineKeltner>` band primitives -- those bracket price; the Thermometer plots a dedicated volatility line and grades it calm/warm/hot. vs `<ChartLineHistoricalVolatility>` -- that is a standard deviation of returns; the Thermometer is a max-of-excursions read with no squaring or annualization. The defining trait: the per-bar `max(|dHigh|, |dLow|)` graded against its EMA into a calm/warm/hot temperature. All exported helpers carry a `LineElderThermo` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineElderThermoPoint[]` (`{x, high, low}`); `period` (the moving-average lookback; default 22, coerced to an integer >= 1); `hotFactor` (the hot multiple of the average; default 2, coerced to a finite number > 1); `pricePanelRatio` (default 0.55, clamped to [0.3,0.8]); `gap`; `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`priceColor`/`thermoColor`/`thermoMaColor`/`calmColor`/`warmColor`/`hotColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showThermo`/`showThermoMa`/`showMarkers`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `thermo` / `thermoMa`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_ELDER_THERMO_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/HOT_FACTOR/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/PRICE_COLOR/THERMO_COLOR/THERMO_MA_COLOR/CALM_COLOR/WARM_COLOR/HOT_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineElderThermoZone` type (`calm`|`warm`|`hot`|`none`); `ChartLineElderThermoComputed` interface (`{thermometer, thermoMa}`); `getLineElderThermoFinitePoints` (keeps only bars with finite x + high + low); `normalizeLineElderThermoPeriod(period, fallback)` (coerce to an integer >= 1; non-finite / sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `normalizeLineElderThermoHotFactor(factor, fallback)` (coerce to a finite number > 1; otherwise falls back); `computeLineElderThermometer(bars)` (the raw max-of-excursions, first bar null; non-array -> []); `computeLineElderThermoEma(values, period)` (the null-tolerant exponential average; non-array -> []); `classifyLineElderThermoZone(thermometer, thermoMa, hotFactor)` (the calm/warm/hot/none rule); `computeLineElderThermo(bars, options?)` -- returns `{thermometer, thermoMa}` (non-array -> empty arrays); `runLineElderThermo(bars, options?)` -- returns `{series, period, hotFactor, thermometer, thermoMa, samples, thermoFinal, calmCount, warmCount, hotCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, high, low, midpoint, thermometer, thermoMa, zone}`; `computeLineElderThermoLayout({data, period, ...})` -- stacks the midpoint panel above the thermometer panel (the thermometer panel anchored at a zero floor, the top dynamic with 10% headroom), projects `pricePath` (the bar midpoint line), `priceDots`, `thermoPath`, `thermoMaPath`, one `markers` entry per finite-thermometer bar coloured by zone; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 bars -> ok=false; `describeLineElderThermoChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Elder Market Thermometer + larger of + moving average + the zone counts (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-elder-thermo-aria-desc"` span; the midpoint line + the thermometer line + the moving-average line + every price dot + every marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-elder-thermo / -aria-desc / -svg / -empty / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -panel-label / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -thermo-line / -thermo-ma-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-high / -tooltip-low / -tooltip-thermo / -tooltip-thermo-ma / -tooltip-zone / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-hot-factor`, `data-thermo-final`, `data-calm-count`, `data-warm-count`, `data-hot-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`, `data-empty`; grid / axis / tick / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"thermo"`; markers expose `data-thermo` and `data-zone`. The thermometer panel carries the violet thermometer line, the dashed cyan moving-average line and temperature-coloured markers (green calm, amber warm, red hot). Tooltip on a price dot or a marker shows x, high, low, the thermometer, the moving average and the temperature. Config badge in top-left calls out `THERMO <period>`. The legend has three toggle items (Midpoint / Thermometer / Thermo MA). 80 vitest cases (getLineElderThermoFinitePoints incl. **drops bars with a non-finite x / high / low** + non-array -> [] + empty -> [] + preserves order, normalizeLineElderThermoPeriod incl. **keeps a valid integer period** + **floors a fractional period** + **falls back for zero / negative / non-finite**, normalizeLineElderThermoHotFactor incl. **keeps a factor above one** + **falls back for exactly one** + **falls back for below one / negative / non-finite**, computeLineElderThermometer incl. non-array -> [] + matches length + **leaves the first bar null** + **takes the larger of the high and low excursions** + **the exact integer thermometer of the fixture** + **reads zero for a flat market**, computeLineElderThermoEma incl. non-array -> [] + matches length + **the exact dyadic average for a period-3 alpha** + **passes a leading null through and seeds from the first finite value** + **holds a constant series at its constant level**, classifyLineElderThermoZone incl. **calm below the average** + **calm for a zero thermometer** + **warm above the average** + **hot past the hot multiple** + **none for a null thermometer / non-finite average**, computeLineElderThermo incl. non-array -> empty arrays + matches length on both arrays + **the exact integer thermometer of the fixture** + **the exact dyadic moving average of the fixture** + **reads zero across a flat market** + **holds the thermometer constant for a steady ramp** + **holds the moving average constant for a steady ramp**, runLineElderThermo incl. not-ok short series + not-ok empty/null + ok + **the default period and hot factor** + **custom period and hot factor** + **reads a flat market wholly calm** + **reads a steady ramp wholly warm** + **the fixture zones** + **carries the bar midpoint on each sample** + **a valid zone on each sample** + **self-consistent zone counts** + one sample per bar + x-sorting + **the final thermometer reading**, computeLineElderThermoLayout incl. not-ok single bar + **collapsed canvas -> ok=false** + ok + **stacks the price panel above the thermometer panel** + price/thermometer/moving-average paths + **one price dot per bar** + **one marker per finite thermometer bar** + **anchors the thermometer panel floor at zero** + carries the run, describeLineElderThermoChart incl. **the indicator name** + **the larger of the excursions** + **the moving average** + the zone counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible each marker** / **both panel labels** / config badge `THERMO 3` / **legend toggle hides the thermometer line** / **showThermoMa=false hides the moving-average line** / onPointClick on a marker / ref) | 11.712 | UI: chart-line-vfi primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.730 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-vfi.tsx` ships `<ChartLineVfi>` -- a pure-SVG TWO-PANEL line chart with a **Markos Katsanos Volume Flow Indicator** panel from cumulative signed volume relative to its mean (the close panel on top, the VFI panel below). **The computation:** Markos Katsanos's VFI reads the BIAS of volume across the lookback window. It is an OHLC+volume indicator, so `ChartLineVfiPoint` carries `{x, high, low, close, volume}`. `computeLineVfiTypical(bars)` is the typical price per bar, `(high + low + close) / 3`. `computeLineVfiSignedVolume(bars)` signs each bar's volume by the DIRECTION of the typical change from the prior bar -- POSITIVE when the typical rose, NEGATIVE when it fell, ZERO when it held (`Math.sign(t[i] - t[i-1]) * volume[i]`); the first bar has no prior, so it is null. `computeLineVfi(bars, options)` divides the cumulative signed volume over the lookback by the cumulative volume over the SAME window and scales to 100: `vfi[i] = 100 * sum(signedVolume[i-period+1..i]) / sum(volume[i-period+1..i])`. Because `|signedVolume[i]| <= volume[i]` by construction, the rolling-sum ratio sits in [-1, 1], so the VFI is BOUNDED to [-100, 100]: +100 when every bar in the window was an up bar, -100 when every bar was a down bar, 0 when up and down volume balanced; a non-finite reading or a zero cumulative volume yields 0. `classifyLineVfiZone(vfi)` marks a bar `up` when the VFI is positive (volume biased to up bars), `down` when negative, `flat` at zero, `none` for null. **Worked fixture:** the whole pipeline -- typical, signed volume, rolling sums, ratio -- is pure rational arithmetic with NO transcendentals, so an integer fixture computes to an exact integer VFI. A STRICTLY RISING typical (`close [10,12,14,16,18,20,22,24]`, half-range +/-1 so `typical = close`) makes every bar an up bar; the signed volume equals the volume, the cumulative sums match, and the VFI pins to EXACTLY +100 (`[null,null,null,100,100,100,100,100]`, `toEqual`). A STRICTLY FALLING typical mirrors it to -100. A FLAT typical (constant close 50, half-range +/-2 -> typical 50) gives every signed volume exactly 0 and the VFI exactly 0. An ALTERNATING fixture -- closes `[10,12,11,13,11,14,12,15]` (delta signs `+,-,+,-,+,-,+`), volumes `[100,200,100,200,100,200,100,200]` -- gives signed volumes `[null, +200, -100, +200, -100, +200, -100, +200]`; the period-3 rolling sums are `[300,0,300,0,300]` over volume sums `[500,400,500,400,500]`, so the exact integer VFI series is `[null,null,null,60,0,60,0,60]` (`toEqual`), with zones `[none x3, up, flat, up, flat, up]`. The typical formula is verified directly (`{high:12,low:6,close:9}` -> `(12+6+9)/3 = 9`), and the signed-volume zero-rule for a flat typical is asserted bar by bar. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs `<ChartLineObv>` / `<ChartLinePvt>` -- those ACCUMULATE the signed volume across the whole series (an unbounded running total); the VFI is a ROLLING ratio over a fixed lookback, bounded to [-100,100], reading the LOCAL bias of volume. vs `<ChartLineVolumeOsc>` (11.687) -- the Volume Oscillator is the percent spread of a fast and a slow volume moving average (measuring volume MOMENTUM, not direction); the VFI signs volume by PRICE DIRECTION and reads how lopsidedly volume falls on up vs down bars. vs `<ChartLineErgodic>` (11.710) -- the Ergodic Oscillator triple-smooths PRICE momentum signed by direction; the VFI takes a SINGLE rolling ratio of VOLUME signed by direction. vs `<ChartLineMfi>` / Money Flow -- MFI uses typical-price * volume as money flow and runs an RSI-style 0..100 oscillator; the VFI runs the cumulative SIGNED volume / cumulative volume as a +/-100 oscillator centered on zero. The defining trait: a bounded volume-biased oscillator scaled exactly to plus or minus 100 of the window's mean volume. All exported helpers carry a `LineVfi` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineVfiPoint[]` (`{x, high, low, close, volume}`); `period` (the lookback; default 26, coerced to an integer >= 2); `pricePanelRatio` (default 0.55, clamped to [0.3,0.8]); `gap`; `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`priceColor`/`vfiColor`/`upColor`/`downColor`/`flatColor`/`zeroColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showVfi`/`showZeroLine`/`showMarkers`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `vfi`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_VFI_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/PRICE_COLOR/VFI_COLOR/UP_COLOR/DOWN_COLOR/FLAT_COLOR/ZERO_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `CHART_LINE_VFI_BOUND` (the VFI magnitude bound, 100); `ChartLineVfiZone` type (`up`|`down`|`flat`|`none`); `ChartLineVfiComputed` interface (`{typical, signedVolume, vfi}`); `getLineVfiFinitePoints` (keeps only bars with finite x + high + low + close + volume); `normalizeLineVfiPeriod(period, fallback)` (coerce to an integer >= 2; non-finite / sub-2 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineVfiTypical(bars)` (the `(h+l+c)/3` per bar; non-array -> []); `computeLineVfiSignedVolume(bars)` (the sign-direction * volume; first bar null; non-array -> []); `classifyLineVfiZone(vfi)` (the up/down/flat/none rule); `computeLineVfi(bars, options?)` -- returns `{typical, signedVolume, vfi}` (non-array -> empty arrays); `runLineVfi(bars, options?)` -- returns `{series, period, typical, signedVolume, vfi, samples, vfiFinal, upCount, downCount, flatCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, high, low, close, volume, typical, vfi, zone}`; `computeLineVfiLayout({data, period, ...})` -- stacks the close panel above the VFI panel (the VFI panel FIXED to a [-110,110] band so the full [-100,100] range always shows with headroom), projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `vfiPath`, one `markers` entry per finite-VFI bar coloured by zone, and `zeroY`; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 bars -> ok=false; `describeLineVfiChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Markos Katsanos Volume Flow Indicator + cumulative signed volume + mean volume + the zone counts (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-vfi-aria-desc"` span; the close line + the VFI line + every price dot + every marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-vfi / -aria-desc / -svg / -empty / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -panel-label / -zero-line / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -vfi-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-close / -tooltip-volume / -tooltip-vfi / -tooltip-zone / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-vfi-final`, `data-up-count`, `data-down-count`, `data-flat-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`, `data-empty`; grid / axis / tick / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"vfi"`; markers expose `data-vfi` and `data-zone`. The VFI panel carries a solid zero line, the violet VFI line and sign-coloured markers (green positive, red negative, slate balanced). Tooltip on a price dot or a marker shows x, close, volume, the VFI and the flow zone. Config badge in top-left calls out `VFI <period>`. The legend has two toggle items (Close / VFI). 76 vitest cases (getLineVfiFinitePoints incl. **drops bars with any non-finite field** + non-array -> [] + empty -> [] + preserves order, normalizeLineVfiPeriod incl. **keeps a valid integer period** + **floors a fractional period** + **falls back below two / negative / non-finite**, computeLineVfiTypical incl. non-array -> [] + matches length + **averages the high, low and close per bar** + **yields the close when the half-range cancels out** + **null for a bar with a non-finite field**, computeLineVfiSignedVolume incl. non-array -> [] + matches length + **leaves the first bar null** + **signs the volume positive on a rising typical** + **signs the volume negative on a falling typical** + **zeros the volume on a flat typical**, classifyLineVfiZone incl. **up / down / flat** + **none for null / non-finite**, computeLineVfi incl. non-array -> empty arrays + matches length on every array + **leaves the warm-up window null** + **pins to +100 across an all-up series** + **pins to -100 across an all-down series** + **reads zero across a flat-typical series** + **the exact integer VFI of the mixed fixture** + **bounded to plus or minus 100** + **every defined reading finite**, runLineVfi incl. not-ok short series + not-ok empty/null + ok + **the default period** + **a custom period** + **counts an all-up series wholly positive** + **counts an all-down series wholly negative** + **counts a flat-typical series wholly flat** + **the mixed fixture zones** + **a valid zone on each sample** + one sample per bar + x-sorting + **the final VFI reading**, computeLineVfiLayout incl. not-ok single bar + **collapsed canvas -> ok=false** + ok + **stacks the price panel above the VFI panel** + price/VFI paths + **one price dot per bar** + **one marker per finite VFI bar** + **fixes the VFI panel band past plus or minus 100** + **the zero line inside the VFI panel** + carries the run, describeLineVfiChart incl. **the indicator name** + **cumulative signed volume** + **mean volume** + the zone counts + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / **empty state** / **root data-period/total-points** / img-role svg / **the price + VFI lines** / **the zero line** / **one marker per finite VFI bar** / **a valid zone on each marker** / **both panel labels** / config badge `VFI 3` / **legend toggle hides the VFI line** / onPointClick on a marker / ref) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. | 11.713 | UI: chart-line-vroc primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.731 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-vroc.tsx` ships `<ChartLineVroc>` -- a pure-SVG TWO-PANEL line chart with a **Volume Rate of Change** panel from the percent change of volume across a lookback (the close panel on top, the VROC panel below). **The computation:** the VROC is the volume analogue of the price Rate of Change. It is a close+volume indicator, so `ChartLineVrocPoint` carries `{x, close, volume}` (no high/low needed -- the rate of change reads only volume). `computeLineVroc(bars, period)` reads each bar's volume against its volume from `period` bars ago and returns the PERCENT CHANGE: `vroc[i] = 100 * (volume[i] - volume[i-period]) / volume[i-period]`. A bar with a non-finite volume or a ZERO prior volume (the divisor) yields null. `classifyLineVrocZone(vroc)` marks a bar `up` when the indicator is POSITIVE (volume expanding versus the prior window), `down` when NEGATIVE (volume contracting), `flat` at zero, `none` for null. Because volume cannot drop below zero, the VROC is BOUNDED BELOW at -100 (a complete volume dry-up) but UNBOUNDED ABOVE (a volume surge can read 200, 500 or higher), so the panel uses a DYNAMIC domain seeded at 0 with a 5% pad on each side so the zero line is always in view. **Worked fixture:** the whole pipeline is pure rational arithmetic with NO transcendentals, so an integer fixture computes to an exact integer VROC. A CONSTANT-volume series reads exactly 0 across the window (`computeLineVroc(constVols, 3) -> [null,null,null,0,0,0,0,0]`, `toEqual`). A DOUBLING-volume fixture (`v[i] = 2 * v[i-period]`: 100, 110, 120, 200, 220, 240, 400, 440 at period 3) pins the VROC to EXACTLY +100. A HALVING-volume fixture (200, 300, 400, 100, 150, 200, 50, 75) pins it to -50. A MIXED fixture -- volumes `[100, 100, 100, 150, 200, 50, 300, 100]` at period 3 -- gives the EXACT integer VROC series `[null, null, null, 50, 100, -50, 100, -50]` (`toEqual`), with zones `[none x3, up, up, down, up, down]`. The zero-prior-volume case is checked directly: with `v[0] = 0`, `computeLineVroc(data, 3)[3]` is null. A non-finite volume at the current bar also yields null. The zone classifier is checked for up, down, flat and none. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs `<ChartLineVfi>` (11.712) -- the VFI signs volume by PRICE direction and takes the cumulative-signed-volume / cumulative-volume ratio, a bounded `+/-100` oscillator that reads volume BIAS by direction; the VROC is a pure VOLUME momentum gauge reading the percent change of volume itself, no direction, no signing. vs `<ChartLineRoc>` style price-RateOfChange primitives -- those run the same percent-change formula on price; the VROC runs it on VOLUME, an asymmetric signal (bounded -100 below, unbounded above). vs `<ChartLineVolumeOsc>` (11.687) -- the Volume Oscillator is the percent spread of a fast and a slow moving average of volume (a smoothed cross-MA momentum); the VROC is a single-step percent change with no smoothing -- responsive but noisier. vs `<ChartLineObv>` / `<ChartLinePvt>` -- those ACCUMULATE the running volume total; the VROC reads a single point-to-point percent change, bounded below at -100. The defining trait: a percent-change momentum gauge on volume, bounded below, unbounded above. All exported helpers carry a `LineVroc` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineVrocPoint[]` (`{x, close, volume}`); `period` (the lookback; default 14, coerced to an integer >= 1); `pricePanelRatio` (default 0.55, clamped to [0.3,0.8]); `gap`; `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`priceColor`/`vrocColor`/`upColor`/`downColor`/`flatColor`/`zeroColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showVroc`/`showZeroLine`/`showMarkers`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `vroc`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_VROC_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/PRICE_COLOR/VROC_COLOR/UP_COLOR/DOWN_COLOR/FLAT_COLOR/ZERO_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `ChartLineVrocZone` type (`up`|`down`|`flat`|`none`); `getLineVrocFinitePoints` (keeps only bars with finite x + close + volume); `normalizeLineVrocPeriod(period, fallback)` (coerce to an integer >= 1; non-finite / sub-1 falls back, fractional floors); `computeLineVroc(bars, period)` (the percent change; non-array -> []; null in the warm-up, null for non-finite or zero-prior volume); `classifyLineVrocZone(vroc)` (the up/down/flat/none rule); `runLineVroc(bars, options?)` -- returns `{series, period, vroc, samples, vrocFinal, upCount, downCount, flatCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, close, volume, priorVolume, vroc, zone}` (`priorVolume` is the volume from `period` bars ago, useful in tooltips); `computeLineVrocLayout({data, period, ...})` -- stacks the close panel above the VROC panel (the VROC domain dynamic, seeded at 0 so the zero line always shows, with a 5% pad on each side), projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `vrocPath`, one `markers` entry per finite-VROC bar coloured by zone, and `zeroY`; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 bars -> ok=false; `describeLineVrocChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Volume Rate of Change + percent change + lookback + the zone counts (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-vroc-aria-desc"` span; the close line + the VROC line + every price dot + every marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-vroc / -aria-desc / -svg / -empty / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -panel-label / -zero-line / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -vroc-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-close / -tooltip-volume / -tooltip-vroc / -tooltip-zone / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config); root mirrors `data-period`, `data-vroc-final`, `data-up-count`, `data-down-count`, `data-flat-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`, `data-empty`; grid / axis / tick / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"vroc"`; markers expose `data-vroc` and `data-zone`. The VROC panel carries a solid zero line, the violet VROC line and sign-coloured markers (green expanding, red contracting, slate steady). Tooltip on a price dot or a marker shows x, close, volume, the VROC and the flow zone. Config badge in top-left calls out `VROC <period>`. The legend has two toggle items (Close / VROC). 66 vitest cases (getLineVrocFinitePoints incl. **drops bars with any non-finite field** + non-array -> [] + empty -> [] + preserves order, normalizeLineVrocPeriod incl. **keeps a valid integer period** + **floors a fractional period** + **falls back for zero / negative / non-finite**, computeLineVroc incl. non-array -> [] + matches
