| 11.722 | UI: chart-line-bayesian primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.740 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-bayesian.tsx` ships `<ChartLineBayesian>` -- a pure-SVG TWO-PANEL line chart with a **Bayesian Trend** probability panel from the posterior of three trend hypotheses given the observed returns (the price on top, the three probability curves below in a FIXED [0, 1] band with a dashed 0.5 midline). **The computation:** the Bayesian Trend reads three hypotheses about the rolling-window return generator -- `H_up: returns drawn from N(+delta, sigma^2)`, `H_flat: returns drawn from N(0, sigma^2)`, `H_down: returns drawn from N(-delta, sigma^2)`. It is a close-only indicator, so `ChartLineBayesianPoint` carries `{x, value}`. `computeLineBayesianReturns(values)` produces the bar-to-bar differences (the first bar is null; a non-finite bar propagates null). For each bar with a filled lookback, `computeLineBayesianPosteriorWindow(returns, delta, sigma)` sums the log-likelihood of each hypothesis (the normal-density constants cancel in the softmax so the implementation keeps only `-0.5 * ((r - mu) / sigma)^2`), then a softmax over the three log-likelihoods with EQUAL PRIORS gives the posterior probabilities `(pUp, pFlat, pDown)`. `computeLineBayesianPosterior(values, window, delta, sigma)` runs this per bar; the first `window` bars are null (the lookback has not yet filled). `classifyLineBayesianZone(posterior)` marks each bar by `argmax`: `up` if `pUp` leads, `down` if `pDown` leads, `flat` if `pFlat` leads (ties favour flat over up over down), `none` if any probability is null or non-finite. **Worked fixture:** the softmax exp is transcendental, so the posterior cannot be bit-exact for arbitrary inputs. The implementation has two algebraically exact invariants that survive the floating-point arithmetic. The SIMPLEX INVARIANT: `pUp + pFlat + pDown = 1` within ULP at every defined bar -- the softmax denominator may introduce ULP noise on the re-summed probabilities, but the underlying `s/s = 1.0` is exact in IEEE 754, asserted with `toBeCloseTo(1, 12)`. The SYMMETRY ANCHOR: for a CONSTANT_FLAT series `[5 x 12]`, every return is exactly 0, so `l_up = sum of -0.5 * ((0 - delta) / sigma)^2` and `l_down = sum of -0.5 * ((0 + delta) / sigma)^2` -- the squared term is the SAME bit pattern for both signs (IEEE 754 negation is just a sign flip and `(-x)^2 === x^2`), so `l_up === l_down` bit-exact, and therefore `pUp === pDown` bit-exact. Asserted at every defined bar with `toBe`. The MIRROR ANCHOR: a window of returns `[r1, r2, ..., rn]` and its sign-flipped mirror `[-r1, -r2, ..., -rn]` swap the up and down log-likelihoods bit-by-bit while leaving the flat one alone; `a.pUp === b.pDown`, `a.pDown === b.pUp`, `a.pFlat === b.pFlat`. The CONCENTRATION PROPERTY: a wider window over `+delta` returns adds more identical positive log-likelihood contributions per bar, concentrating the posterior more on `H_up` (`posterior(window=10).pUp > posterior(window=3).pUp`). The ORDERING ANCHORS: a RISING `[10..21]` series makes every return `+1 = +delta`, so `pUp > pFlat > pDown` at every defined bar; FALLING `[21..10]` mirrors to `pDown > pFlat > pUp`; CONST_FLAT `[5 x 12]` makes every return 0, so `pFlat > pUp = pDown` at every defined bar. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs `<ChartLineRsi>` -- the RSI is a FIXED `100 - 100 / (1 + sumUp / sumDown)` over the bar-to-bar price changes; the Bayesian Trend is a PROBABILISTIC classifier over three parametrised hypotheses with a tunable drift and noise. vs `<ChartLineAroonOsc>` -- the Aroon Osc measures the recency of the highest high vs the lowest low (a TIME-only signal); the Bayesian Trend measures the LIKELIHOOD-weighted evidence over the observed returns (an AMPLITUDE-aware signal). vs `<ChartLineErgodic>` -- the Ergodic Oscillator is a triple-smoothed momentum ratio (deterministic + frequentist); the Bayesian Trend is a posterior over a GENERATIVE MODEL (probabilistic + Bayesian). vs `<ChartLineCmo>` -- the CMO is `100 * (sumUp - sumDown) / (sumUp + sumDown)`, a SIGNED unsigned-share; the Bayesian Trend produces THREE CALIBRATED probabilities that sum to one. The defining trait: a per-bar three-way posterior over a generative trend model with equal priors, the first probabilistic primitive in the family. All exported helpers carry a `LineBayesian` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineBayesianPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `window` (default 10, integer >= 2); `delta` (default 0.01, strictly positive finite); `sigma` (default 0.02, strictly positive finite); `pricePanelRatio` (default 0.55, clamped to [0.3, 0.8]); `gap`; `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`priceColor`/`upColor`/`downColor`/`flatColor`/`noneColor`/`midlineColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showProb`/`showMidline`/`showMarkers`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `pUp` / `pFlat` / `pDown`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_BAYESIAN_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/WINDOW/DELTA/SIGMA/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/PRICE_COLOR/UP_COLOR/DOWN_COLOR/FLAT_COLOR/NONE_COLOR/MIDLINE_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `CHART_LINE_BAYESIAN_MIDLINE` (the midline constant, 0.5); `ChartLineBayesianZone` type (`up`|`flat`|`down`|`none`); `ChartLineBayesianPosterior` interface (`{pUp, pFlat, pDown}`); `getLineBayesianFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value); `normalizeLineBayesianWindow(window, fallback)` (integer >= 2; sub-2 / non-finite / fractional all coerced); `normalizeLineBayesianDelta(delta, fallback)` (strictly positive finite; zero / negative / non-finite falls back); `normalizeLineBayesianSigma(sigma, fallback)` (strictly positive finite); `computeLineBayesianReturns(values)` (bar-to-bar diffs; first bar null; non-finite propagation); `computeLineBayesianPosteriorWindow(returns, delta, sigma)` (the posterior for a single window); `computeLineBayesianPosterior(values, window, delta, sigma)` (the per-bar rolling posterior; first `window` bars null); `classifyLineBayesianZone(posterior)` (the up/flat/down/none rule with the flat-favoured tiebreak); `runLineBayesian(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, window, delta, sigma, posterior, samples, pUpFinal, pFlatFinal, pDownFinal, upCount, flatCount, downCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, pUp, pFlat, pDown, zone}`; `computeLineBayesianLayout({data, window, delta, sigma, ...})` -- stacks the price panel above the probability panel (the probability panel FIXED to [0, 1] so all three probabilities always show), projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `pUpPath`, `pFlatPath`, `pDownPath`, one `markers` entry per defined-posterior bar coloured by the argmax zone (positioned at the leader's probability), and `midlineY` for the 0.5 line; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLineBayesianChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Bayesian Trend + the lookback window + the three hypotheses + the final posterior (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-bayesian-aria-desc"` span; the price line + every price dot + the three probability lines + every marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-bayesian / -aria-desc / -svg / -empty / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -panel-label / -midline / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -prob-paths / -p-up-line / -p-flat-line / -p-down-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-p-up / -tooltip-p-flat / -tooltip-p-down / -tooltip-zone / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config); root mirrors `data-window`, `data-delta`, `data-sigma`, `data-p-up-final`, `data-p-flat-final`, `data-p-down-final`, `data-up-count`, `data-flat-count`, `data-down-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`, `data-empty`; grid / axis / tick / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"prob"`; markers expose `data-zone`, `data-p-up`, `data-p-flat`, `data-p-down`. The probability panel renders three coloured lines (green P(up), slate P(flat), red P(down)) with a dashed slate 0.5 midline and zone-coloured markers at the leader's probability. Tooltip on a marker shows x, price, the three probabilities and the bias zone. Config badge in top-left calls out `BAYES <window>`. The legend has four toggle items (Price / P(up) / P(flat) / P(down)). 90 vitest cases (getLineBayesianFinitePoints incl. **drops non-finite x or value** + null -> [] + non-array -> [] + preserves order, normalizeLineBayesianWindow incl. **keeps valid** + **floors fractional** + **falls back for sub-2 / non-finite / string**, normalizeLineBayesianDelta incl. **keeps positive** + **falls back for zero / negative / non-finite**, normalizeLineBayesianSigma incl. **keeps positive** + **falls back for zero / negative / non-finite**, computeLineBayesianReturns incl. non-array -> [] + matches length + **first return null** + **matches bar-to-bar differences** + **zero for a constant input** + **null at a non-finite bar**, computeLineBayesianPosteriorWindow incl. **simplex invariant within ULP** + **bit-exact pUp === pDown on a zero window (the symmetry anchor)** + **flat leader on a zero window** + **up leader on +delta returns** + **down leader on -delta returns** + **probabilities in [0, 1]** + **mirror anchor (signs flipped swap up and down bit-exact)** + **concentration property (wider window -> stronger leader)**, computeLineBayesianPosterior incl. non-array -> [] + matches length + **the warm-up window null** + **the simplex invariant per defined bar** + **the up leader at every defined bar of a strictly rising series** + **the down leader at every defined bar of a strictly falling series** + **the flat leader at every defined bar of a constant series** + **the bit-exact pUp === pDown per defined bar on a constant series** + **every defined probability inside [0, 1]** + **respects non-finite window via the fallback**, classifyLineBayesianZone incl. **null -> none** + **non-finite -> none** + **pUp leader -> up** + **pDown leader -> down** + **pFlat leader -> flat** + **tie favours flat then up over down** + **exposes a midline constant at 0.5**, runLineBayesian incl. not-ok single point + not-ok empty/null + ok + **the default window/delta/sigma** + **custom options** + **classifies a rising series as up after the warm-up** + **classifies a falling series as down after the warm-up** + **classifies a constant series as flat after the warm-up** + | 11.723 | UI: chart-line-monte-carlo primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.741 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-monte-carlo.tsx` ships `<ChartLineMonteCarlo>` -- a pure-SVG SINGLE-PANEL line chart with a **Monte Carlo forecast fan** overlay from bootstrapped return samples (the historical price line on the left, the forecast fan extending past a dashed divider to the right; a violet median line carries the center of the fan, the band between the lower / upper quantiles is shaded). **The computation:** the forecast is a NON-PARAMETRIC BOOTSTRAP. It is a close-only indicator, so `ChartLineMonteCarloPoint` carries `{x, value}`. `computeLineMonteCarloReturns(values)` pools the bar-to-bar differences (the first bar is skipped, a non-finite bar is dropped from the pool). For each of `simulations` sample paths and each of `horizon` future steps, ONE return is drawn UNIFORMLY WITH REPLACEMENT from the pool and accumulated onto the last historical price: `paths[s][step] = paths[s][step-1] + sample(returns, rng)`. The per-step quantile fan is collected from the simulated paths at that step: `lower` at `quantileLow`, `median` at `0.5`, `upper` at `quantileHigh`, plus the arithmetic mean across the simulations. `computeLineMonteCarloQuantile(sortedAsc, q)` is the floor-quantile on a sorted-ascending snapshot. The PRNG is a deterministic **Mulberry32** seeded from `seed` (`createLineMonteCarloRng(seed)` returns a `() => number` in `[0, 1)`), so two runs with the same input return the same fan BIT-FOR-BIT. The defining algebraic invariants survive the floating-point arithmetic. **Worked fixture:** the bootstrap is intrinsically stochastic, so a generic input cannot give a bit-exact fan. The implementation has three exact anchors. The ZERO-VARIANCE POOL: a `CONST_FLAT` historical series `[5 x 10]` produces a returns pool of all zeros, every bootstrapped sample is 0, every simulated path stays at the last price 5, so `lower = median = upper = 5` at every horizon step -- asserted with `toBe(5)` per step, for any seed. The SINGLE-VALUE POOL: a pool with one unique value `k` (e.g. `[1, 1, 1, 1]` or `[-1, -1, -1, -1]`) has every bootstrapped sample equal to `k`, every path at step `i` equals `lastPrice + i * k`, the fan width is zero -- asserted with `toBe(100 + step.step)` and `toBe(100 - step.step)`. The DETERMINISM ANCHOR: `computeLineMonteCarloForecast(100, [1, -1, 2, -2], {seed: 42, ...})` is `toEqual` itself on a re-run (Mulberry32 is fully deterministic from the seed). The DIFFERENT-SEED PROPERTY: different seeds on a variable pool produce different fans (`not.toEqual`). The QUANTILE-ORDER INVARIANT: `lower <= median <= upper` at every step by construction of the floor-quantile rule on the sorted-ascending snapshot. The PRNG contract is tested directly -- 1000 outputs all in `[0, 1)`, deterministic across two instances of the same seed, distinct across different seeds, all finite. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs `<ChartLineBayesian>` -- the Bayesian Trend is a posterior over THREE hypothesised parametric models, producing three calibrated probabilities that sum to one. The Monte Carlo Fan is a NON-PARAMETRIC bootstrap of the empirical return distribution, producing a quantile band over the projected path. vs `<ChartLineRsi>` / `<ChartLineCmo>` / `<ChartLineAroonOsc>` -- those are deterministic indicators over the existing series; the Monte Carlo Fan projects FORWARD past the last bar. vs `<ChartLineGauss>` / `<ChartLineButterworth>` -- those are deterministic smoothers over the existing series; the Monte Carlo Fan is a forecast. vs `<ChartLineErgodic>` -- the Ergodic Oscillator is a triple-smoothed momentum ratio over the existing series; the Monte Carlo Fan extrapolates. The defining trait: a per-step quantile band over `simulations` bootstrapped paths drawn from the empirical return distribution -- the first forecast primitive in the family, with a deterministic PRNG so the fan is fully reproducible from the seed. All exported helpers carry a `LineMonteCarlo` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineMonteCarloPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `horizon` (default 20, integer >= 1); `simulations` (default 200, integer >= 1); `seed` (default 1, non-negative integer); `quantileLow` / `quantileHigh` (default 0.1 / 0.9, finite in `(0, 1)`); `fanOpacity`; `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`historyColor`/`medianColor`/`fanColor`/`bridgeColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showFan`/`showMedian`/`showBridge`/`showMarkers`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `history` / `median` / `fan`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({step})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_MONTE_CARLO_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/HORIZON/SIMULATIONS/SEED/QUANTILE_LOW/QUANTILE_HIGH/FAN_OPACITY/HISTORY_COLOR/MEDIAN_COLOR/FAN_COLOR/BRIDGE_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineMonteCarloFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value); `normalizeLineMonteCarloHorizon(horizon, fallback)` (integer >= 1); `normalizeLineMonteCarloSimulations(simulations, fallback)` (integer >= 1); `normalizeLineMonteCarloSeed(seed, fallback)` (abs + floor, non-negative integer); `normalizeLineMonteCarloQuantile(q, fallback)` (strictly in `(0, 1)`); `computeLineMonteCarloReturns(values)` (bar-to-bar differences; non-finite bars dropped); `computeLineMonteCarloQuantile(sortedAsc, q)` (floor-quantile); `createLineMonteCarloRng(seed)` (Mulberry32; deterministic in `[0, 1)`); `computeLineMonteCarloForecast(lastPrice, returns, options?, lastX?)` -- returns one `{step, x, lower, median, upper, mean}` per horizon step (empty pool / non-finite lastPrice -> []); `runLineMonteCarlo(points, options?)` -- returns `{history, returns, forecast, seed, horizon, simulations, quantileLow, quantileHigh, lastPrice, ok}`; `computeLineMonteCarloLayout({data, horizon, ...})` -- projects a single panel with the historical line, dashed forecast divider, fan band, median line, dashed upper / lower lines, a bridge segment from the last history point to the first median point, and one marker per horizon step on the median; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 history points / empty returns pool -> ok=false; `describeLineMonteCarloChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Monte Carlo + the horizon + the simulations + the bootstrap-with-replacement procedure + the final median (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-monte-carlo-aria-desc"` span; the history line + every history dot + the median line + every marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-monte-carlo / -aria-desc / -svg / -empty / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -forecast-divider / -fan-band / -fan-lines / -upper-line / -lower-line / -bridge / -history-path / -dots / -dot / -median-path / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-median / -tooltip-mean / -tooltip-lower / -tooltip-upper / -tooltip-width / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config); root mirrors `data-horizon`, `data-simulations`, `data-seed`, `data-quantile-low`, `data-quantile-high`, `data-last-price`, `data-forecast-steps`, `data-history-points`, `data-animate`, `data-empty`; markers expose `data-step`, `data-median`, `data-lower`, `data-upper`. The fan band fills with `fanColor` at `fanOpacity` (default 0.18 violet); the upper / lower lines render as dashed `fanColor` strokes; the median line is solid `medianColor`. Tooltip on a marker shows step, median, mean, lower, upper, fan width. Config badge in top-left calls out `MC H<horizon> S<simulations>`. The legend has three toggle items (History / Forecast median / Forecast fan). 88 vitest cases (getLineMonteCarloFinitePoints incl. **drops non-finite x or value** + null -> [] + non-array -> [] + preserves order, normalizeLineMonteCarloHorizon incl. **keeps valid** + **floors fractional** + **falls back for sub-1 / non-finite / string**, normalizeLineMonteCarloSimulations same, normalizeLineMonteCarloSeed incl. **non-negative** + **abs of negative** + **floor fractional** + **non-finite fallback**, normalizeLineMonteCarloQuantile incl. **keeps in (0, 1)** + **falls back for zero / one / negative / non-finite**, computeLineMonteCarloReturns incl. non-array -> [] + single-bar -> [] + **bar-to-bar diffs** + **zero returns for a constant input** + **skips non-finite bars**, createLineMonteCarloRng incl. **outputs in [0, 1)** + **deterministic for the same seed** + **distinct sequences for different seeds** + **finite outputs**, computeLineMonteCarloQuantile incl. **empty -> NaN** + **single value** + **floor-quantile rule at multiple positions** + **clamps at the last position**, computeLineMonteCarloForecast incl. empty pool -> [] + non-finite lastPrice -> [] + **one step per horizon entry** + **the zero-variance anchor: every fan reading equals the last price** + **the single-value pool anchor (+1): every fan reading equals last + step** + **the single-value pool anchor (-1): every fan reading equals last - step** + **the quantile order lower <= median <= upper at every step** + **deterministic at same seed (toEqual)** + **different seeds produce different fans (not.toEqual)** + **every value finite** + **step index runs 1..horizon and x advances by step** + **a positively-biased pool trends the median upward**, runLineMonteCarlo incl. not-ok single point + not-ok empty/null + ok + **the default horizon and simulations** + **custom options** + one forecast step per horizon entry + **zero-return history produces a zero-variance fan at the last price** + **a rising unit history produces a strictly increasing median** + x-sorting + **the last historical price**, computeLineMonteCarloLayout incl. not-ok single point + **collapsed canvas -> ok=false** + ok + **non-empty history and median paths** + **non-empty fan band path** + **one history dot per bar** + **one marker per horizon step** + **forecast divider past the last history dot** + **every marker inside the panel** + carries the run, describeLineMonteCarloChart incl. **the indicator name** + **horizon and simulations** + **bootstrap with replacement** + **the final median** + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / **empty state** / **root data-horizon/simulations/seed** / img-role svg / **the history + median paths** / **the fan band** / **the forecast divider** / **one marker per horizon step** / config badge `MC H5 S50` / **legend toggle hides the fan band** / **legend toggle hides the median path** / onPointClick on a marker / ref / **the component renders for a falling unit series**) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. | 11.724 | UI: chart-line-mahalanobis primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.742 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-mahalanobis.tsx` ships `<ChartLineMahalanobis>` -- a pure-SVG TWO-PANEL line chart with a **Mahalanobis Distance** panel from the multivariate standardized deviation of each bar's BIVARIATE feature vector off the rolling mean (the price on top, the distance panel below with a dashed horizontal outlier threshold). **The computation:** the Mahalanobis Distance reads the multivariate standardized deviation of the current bar from the rolling-window mean. It is a close-only primitive lifted to two dimensions, so `ChartLineMahalanobisPoint` carries `{x, value}`. `computeLineMahalanobisFeatures(values)` produces the bivariate feature `f = [value, return]` per bar where `return = value[i] - value[i-1]`; the first bar's feature is null. For a rolling window of `window` features ending at bar i, `computeLineMahalanobisStats(features)` returns `{muValue, muRet, varValue, varRet, covValueRet, det}` from the sample mean and the `1/(N - 1)` covariance: `Sigma = [[varValue, covValueRet], [covValueRet, varRet]]`, `det = varValue * varRet - covValueRet^2`. `computeLineMahalanobisDistance(feature, stats)` then computes `d = sqrt((f - mu)^T * Sigma^(-1) * (f - mu))` -- the 2x2 inverse is `(1/det) * [[varRet, -covValueRet], [-covValueRet, varValue]]`. If `|det| < CHART_LINE_MAHALANOBIS_DET_EPSILON = 1e-12` (constant series, linear ramps -- the rolling features are collinear), the distance is left null. `computeLineMahalanobisRolling(values, window)` runs this per bar; the first `window` bars are null (the lookback has not yet filled). `classifyLineMahalanobisZone(distance, threshold)` marks each bar `normal` (below threshold), `outlier` (>= threshold), or `none` (null / non-finite). The DEFINING ALGEBRAIC INVARIANT: the Mahalanobis Distance is INVARIANT UNDER AFFINE REPARAMETERISATION of the feature vector. **Worked fixture:** the implementation has three exact-style anchors. The SINGULAR-COV ANCHORS: a CONST_FLAT series `[5 x 10]` has bivariate features all `[5, 0]`, the return variance is 0 so `Sigma_yy = 0` and `det = 0` -- the distance is null at every defined bar (asserted with `toBeNull` per bar). A RISING_LINEAR series `[10..19]` has bivariate features all `[value, 1]`, the return variance is 0 again so `det = 0` -- null at every defined bar. These are the cleanest failure-mode anchors: any series with no variance in returns (a constant or a linear ramp) is undefined under Mahalanobis. The SYMMETRIC THREE-POINT ANCHOR: the window `[[1, 0], [0, 1], [-1, -1]]` has mean at the origin and sample covariance `(1/2) * [[2, 1], [1, 2]] = [[1, 0.5], [0.5, 1]]`. `det = 1 - 0.25 = 0.75`, `Sigma^(-1) = (1/0.75) * [[1, -0.5], [-0.5, 1]] = [[4/3, -2/3], [-2/3, 4/3]]`. For the feature `[1, 0]` the diff is `[1, 0]`, so `d^2 = 1 * 4/3 + 0 + 0 = 4/3` -- asserted with `toBeCloseTo(Math.sqrt(4/3), 10)`. The TRANSLATION INVARIANCE ANCHOR: shifting every input value by a constant `k` shifts `mu_value` by `k` while leaving the return feature alone, so `f - mu` is unchanged and `d` is unchanged. Asserted within ULP (`toBeCloseTo(a, 10)`) for `k = +1000` and `k = -50` -- the result is not bit-exact because floating-point summation isn't order-invariant under translation (`(a+k) + (b+k) + ...` rounds differently than `(a+b+...) + n*k`). The SCALE INVARIANCE ANCHOR: multiplying every value by `c > 0` scales `mu` by `c`, `Sigma` by `c^2`, `Sigma^(-1)` by `1/c^2`, `f - mu` by `c`, so `d^2 = c^2 * c^(-2) * d^2_old = d^2_old`. Asserted within ULP for `c = 2`. The ZERO-DISTANCE ANCHOR: a feature exactly at the rolling mean of a non-degenerate window gives `d = 0`. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs `<ChartLineBayesian>` -- the Bayesian Trend produces three calibrated probabilities under three parametric hypotheses; the Mahalanobis Distance is an UNSIGNED multivariate z-score whose classifier is a single threshold. vs `<ChartLineRsi>` / `<ChartLineCmo>` -- those are UNIVARIATE shape statistics over the bar-to-bar returns; the Mahalanobis Distance is a TRUE multivariate measure that ACCOUNTS FOR THE COVARIANCE between value and return (a feature pair that drifts together is treated as one-dimensional). vs `<ChartLineMonteCarlo>` -- the Monte Carlo Fan is a FORWARD-LOOKING probabilistic forecast; the Mahalanobis Distance is a BACKWARD-LOOKING outlier metric over the existing series. vs `<ChartLineErgodic>` / `<ChartLineSnr>` -- those are momentum / noise statistics with a sign; the Mahalanobis Distance is a NON-NEGATIVE outlier metric (`>= 0` by construction since `Sigma^(-1)` is positive-semi-definite when `det > 0`). The defining trait: a multivariate z-score over a bivariate feature space that detects collinear-feature degeneracy as null, the first multivariate-aware primitive in the family. All exported helpers carry a `LineMahalanobis` infix so the `export *` barrel line cannot collide. API: `data` -- a single `ChartLineMahalanobisPoint[]` (`{x, value}`); `window` (default 20, integer >= 3); `threshold` (default 2, strictly positive finite); `pricePanelRatio` (default 0.55, clamped to `[0.3, 0.8]`); `gap`; `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`priceColor`/`distanceColor`/`normalColor`/`outlierColor`/`noneColor`/`thresholdColor`/`gridColor`/`axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots` (default false)/`showDistance`/`showThreshold`/`showMarkers`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility (`hiddenSeries` keys `price` / `distance`); standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({point})` / `onSeriesToggle({seriesId, hidden})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_MAHALANOBIS_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/WINDOW/THRESHOLD/PRICE_PANEL_RATIO/PRICE_COLOR/DISTANCE_COLOR/NORMAL_COLOR/OUTLIER_COLOR/NONE_COLOR/THRESHOLD_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `CHART_LINE_MAHALANOBIS_DET_EPSILON` (the singular-det guard, 1e-12); `ChartLineMahalanobisZone` type (`normal`|`outlier`|`none`); `ChartLineMahalanobisFeature` interface (`{value, ret}`); `ChartLineMahalanobisStats` interface (`{muValue, muRet, varValue, varRet, covValueRet, det}`); `getLineMahalanobisFinitePoints` (keeps only finite x + value); `normalizeLineMahalanobisWindow(window, fallback)` (integer >= 3); `normalizeLineMahalanobisThreshold(threshold, fallback)` (positive finite); `computeLineMahalanobisFeatures(values)` (bivariate `[value, return]`; first bar null; non-finite propagation); `computeLineMahalanobisStats(features)` (sample mean + `1/(N-1)` cov); `computeLineMahalanobisDistance(feature, stats)` (`null` when `|det| < EPS`); `computeLineMahalanobisRolling(values, window)` (per-bar rolling distance; warm-up null); `classifyLineMahalanobisZone(distance, threshold)` (the normal/outlier/none rule with at-threshold = outlier); `runLineMahalanobis(points, options?)` -- returns `{series, window, threshold, distance, samples, distanceFinal, normalCount, outlierCount, ok}`; each sample `{index, x, value, distance, zone}`; `computeLineMahalanobisLayout({data, window, threshold, ...})` -- stacks the price panel above the distance panel (the distance panel's domain starts at 0 and stretches past the threshold so the outlier line is always visible), projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `distancePath`, one `markers` entry per defined-distance bar coloured by zone, and `thresholdY` for the dashed threshold line; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLineMahalanobisChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Mahalanobis + the bivariate feature pair + the window + the singular-cov behaviour + the final distance (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-mahalanobis-aria-desc"` span; the price line + every price dot + the distance line + every marker `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-mahalanobis / -aria-desc / -svg / -empty / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -panel-label / -threshold / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -distance-path / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-distance / -tooltip-threshold / -tooltip-zone / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config); root mirrors `data-window`, `data-threshold`, `data-distance-final`, `data-normal-count`, `data-outlier-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`, `data-empty`; grid / axis / tick / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"distance"`; markers expose `data-zone` and `data-distance`. The distance panel carries a slate dashed threshold line and zone-coloured markers (slate normal, red outlier). Tooltip on a marker shows x, price, the distance, the threshold and the zone. Config badge in top-left calls out `MAHA <window>/<threshold>`. The legend has two toggle items (Price / Distance). 83 vitest cases (getLineMahalanobisFinitePoints incl. **drops non-finite x or value** + null -> [] + non-array -> [] + preserves order, normalizeLineMahalanobisWindow incl. **keeps valid** + **floors fractional** + **falls back for sub-3 / non-finite / string**, normalizeLineMahalanobisThreshold incl. **keeps positive** + **falls back for zero / negative / non-finite**, computeLineMahalanobisFeatures incl. non-array -> [] + matches length + **first feature null** + **bivariate [value, return]** + **null at non-finite bar**, computeLineMahalanobisStats incl. **empty -> zero stats** + **computes sample mean correctly** + **singular cov for perfectly correlated features** + **non-singular cov for varied features**, computeLineMahalanobisDistance incl. **null on singular cov** + **zero at the rolling mean** + **the worked sqrt(4/3) anchor** + **non-negative for non-degenerate windows** + **exposes a small singular-det epsilon**, computeLineMahalanobisRolling incl. non-array -> [] + matches length + **the warm-up window null** + **a constant series produces null at every defined bar** + **a linear ramp produces null at every defined bar** + **a varied series produces finite non-negative distances** + **the translation invariance anchor within ULP (k = +1000)** + **the negative-shift variant (k = -50)** + **the scale invariance anchor (c = 2)** + non-finite window fallback, classifyLineMahalanobisZone incl. **null -> none** + **non-finite -> none** + **below threshold -> normal** + **at threshold -> outlier** + **above threshold -> outlier**, runLineMahalanobis incl. not-ok single point + not-ok empty/null + ok + **the default window and threshold** + **custom options** + **a constant series classifies every bar as none** + **a linear ramp classifies every bar as none** + **a varied series produces defined distances** + **self-consistent zone counts** + one sample per point + x-sorting + **the final distance**, computeLineMahalanobisLayout incl. not-ok single point + **collapsed canvas -> ok=false** + ok + **stacks the price panel above the distance panel** + non-empty price path + **one price dot per bar** + **one marker per defined-distance bar** + **the threshold inside the distance panel** + **every marker inside the distance panel** + carries the run, describeLineMahalanobisChart incl. **the indicator name** + **the bivariate feature pair** + **the rolling window** + **the singular-covariance behaviour** + No data, component render incl. labelled region / accessible description / **empty state** / **root data-window/threshold** / img-role svg / **the price + distance lines** / **the threshold line by default** / **one marker per defined-distance bar** / **a valid zone on each marker** / config badge `MAHA 5/2` / **legend toggle hides the distance line** / **showThreshold=false hides the threshold line** / onPointClick on a marker / ref) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. | 11.725 | UI: chart-line-vama primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.743 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-vama.tsx` ships `<ChartLineVama>` -- a pure-SVG SINGLE-PANEL line chart with a **Volume Adjusted Moving Average** overlay weighting recent prices by the excess volume above the rolling-window mean. For each rolling window the mean volume is taken; every bar with volume above the mean gets weight `volume - volumeMean`, every bar at or below the mean gets weight zero, and the VAMA is the weighted price average `sum(price * weight) / sum(weight)`. If no bar in the window is above average (every weight is zero -- a flat-volume regime), the VAMA falls back to the plain SMA of the closes. The implementation has four exact-style anchors that survive the floating-point arithmetic by construction: the EQUAL-VOLUME FALLBACK ANCHOR (closes `[1..10]` with volumes `[10 x 10]` falls back to the SMA, asserted bit-exact as `[3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8]` at bars 4..9); the SINGLE-HIGH-VOLUME ANCHOR (one bar with above-mean volume in the window -> VAMA equals that bar's close, asserted with `toBe`); the TWO-WEIGHTED ANCHOR (prices `[10, 20, 30, 40, 50]` volumes `[3, 3, 4, 7, 8]` -> `sum(p*w) = 230`, `sum(w) = 5`, VAMA = 46 exact); the PRICE TRANSLATION INVARIANCE ANCHOR (shifting all prices by k shifts every defined VAMA by k bit-exact across the rolling series). Distinct from neighbours: vs `<ChartLineMovingAvg>` SMA weights every bar equally; the VAMA weights only conviction bars and falls back to SMA when there is no conviction. vs `<ChartLineWma>` / `<ChartLineEma>` those weight by TIME recency; the VAMA weights by VOLUME EXCESS. vs `<ChartLineVfi>` / `<ChartLineVroc>` those are volume-flow indicators; the VAMA is a volume-weighted smoother. The defining trait: the first conviction-weighted MA in the family with a clean SMA fallback for flat-volume regimes. API: `data` (`{x, value, volume}[]`); `period` (default 14, integer >= 2); standard sizing/colour/a11y props plus `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/toggle flags/controlled+uncontrolled `hiddenSeries` (`price`/`vama`)/callbacks. Pure helpers: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_VAMA_*`; `ChartLineVamaTrend`; `getLineVamaFinitePoints` (keeps finite x/value/non-negative volume); `normalizeLineVamaPeriod` (integer >= 2); `computeLineVamaWindow(prices, volumes)` (returns `{vama, weightSum, usedFallback}`; null on length mismatch); `computeLineVama(prices, volumes, period)` (per-bar rolling; first `period - 1` bars null); `classifyLineVamaTrend`; `runLineVama` (returns full `{series, period, vama, weightShare, samples, vamaFinal, upCount, downCount, flatCount, ok}`); `computeLineVamaLayout` (one panel with segments + markers slope-coloured); `describeLineVamaChart` (ARIA summary mentioning the indicator, the period, the fallback-to-SMA property, the trend counts). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"`, sr-only aria-desc span; price line + dots + markers `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node. Root mirrors `data-period`, `data-vama-final`, `data-up-count/-down-count/-flat-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`, `data-empty`; segments + markers expose `data-trend`; markers also expose `data-vama`. Tooltip shows x, price, volume, VAMA, weight share, trend. Config badge `VAMA <period>`. Legend has Price + VAMA toggle items. 74 vitest cases (getLineVamaFinitePoints 4; normalizeLineVamaPeriod 5; computeLineVamaWindow 8 incl. **all four exact anchors**; computeLineVama 10 incl. **rolling SMA fallback bit-exact** + **translation invariance bit-exact**; classifyLineVamaTrend 5; runLineVama 12; computeLineVamaLayout 10; describeLineVamaChart 5; component 15) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean (conditional spreads honour `exactOptionalPropertyTypes`, `noUncheckedIndexedAccess` guarded). Exported via components/ui barrel. Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/c4-mgr-auto. Worker auto-w707. Bump 1.11.743. |
| 11.726 | UI: chart-line-volatility-stop primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.744 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-volatility-stop.tsx` ships `<ChartLineVolatilityStop>` -- a pure-SVG SINGLE-PANEL line chart with a **Welles Wilder Volatility Stop** overlay flipping a stop on an average true range cross. The trailing stop is derived from the ATR: while long it sits at `high - multiplier * ATR` and trails UP only; while short it sits at `low + multiplier * ATR` and trails DOWN only. A close that crosses the stop FLIPS the position and seeds a new stop on the opposite side at the same ATR offset. The True Range is `max(high - low, |high - prev_close|, |low - prev_close|)` (collapsing to `high - low` on the first bar); the ATR uses Wilder's smoothing (an SMA seed of the first `period` true ranges, then `ATR[i] = ((period - 1) * ATR[i-1] + TR[i]) / period`). The position colours the stop segments and the markers (green long, red short, orange flip) so the regime reads at a glance. Point: `{x, high, low, close}`. The implementation has three exact-style anchors that survive the floating-point arithmetic by construction. The CONSTANT-SERIES ANCHOR: `CONST_FLAT [5 x 10]` with `high = low = close = 5` has every TR = 0, every ATR = 0 (zero arithmetic exact), every stop = close = 5, and `close < stop` is `5 < 5` which is false -> no flip fires. All defined bars are long, the long count is 8, the flip count is 0; asserted with `toBe(5)` for every defined stop and `toBe(0)` for the flip count. The RISING-SERIES ANCHOR: `RISING [1..10]` with `high = low = close = i` stays long throughout (close rises faster than the stop trails up) -- long count 8, flip count 0 exactly. The FALLING-SERIES WORKED ANCHOR: `FALLING [10..1]` with `high = low = close = v`, `period = 3`, `multiplier = 2`. Hand-traced: `ATR[2] = 2/3`, `ATR[3] = 7/9`, `ATR[4] = 23/27`. Bar 2 initialises long with `stop = 8 - 2 * (2/3) = 20/3 = 6.667`. Bar 3 stays long (close 7 > stop 6.667, trail candidate 49/9 below the stop). Bar 4 close 6 < stop 20/3 -> FLIP. New short with `stop = 6 + 2 * 23/27 = 208/27 = 7.704`. Bars 5..9 stay short, trailing down. Asserted: flip count = 1, flip bar = 4, position after flip = short, long count = 2, short count = 6. The initial long stop `20/3` is asserted to 12 places with `toBeCloseTo`. The trail-up monotonicity (long stop never decreases until a flip) and trail-down monotonicity (short stop never increases between flips) are tested directly. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs `<ChartLineMovingAvg>` and similar smoothers -- the smoothers ride alongside the price; the Volatility Stop is a DIRECTIONAL trailing stop that FLIPS regime on a cross. vs `<ChartLineBollinger>` / channel primitives -- those are symmetric bands around a central line that do NOT trail; the Volatility Stop trails monotonically in one direction until the cross. vs `<ChartLineKama>` / adaptive smoothers -- the smoothing adapts but the line still rides alongside; the Volatility Stop's stop sits OFFSET from the price by the ATR. vs `<ChartLineMonteCarlo>` -- the Monte Carlo Fan is a forward-looking probabilistic forecast; the Volatility Stop is a backward-looking deterministic trailing stop. The defining trait: the first OHLC-aware directional trailing stop in the family, computed from Wilder's ATR and flipping on a strict close-vs-stop cross. API: `data` (`{x, high, low, close}[]`); `period` (default 10, integer >= 2); `multiplier` (default 3, strictly positive finite); standard sizing/colour/a11y props plus `strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`tickCount`/toggle flags (`showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots`/`showStop`/`showMarkers`/`showFlips`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`)/controlled+uncontrolled `hiddenSeries` (`price`/`stop`)/callbacks. Pure helpers: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_VOLATILITY_STOP_*`; `ChartLineVolatilityStopPosition` type (`long`|`short`|`none`); `getLineVolatilityStopFinitePoints` (keeps finite x/high/low/close); `normalizeLineVolatilityStopPeriod` (integer >= 2); `normalizeLineVolatilityStopMultiplier` (positive finite); `computeLineVolatilityStopTrueRange(curr, prev)` (the scalar TR; `high - low` on the first bar); `computeLineVolatilityStopATR(bars, period)` (per-bar Wilder ATR; warm-up null); `computeLineVolatilityStop(bars, period, multiplier)` (per-bar `{position, stop, flip, atr}`; warm-up `{none, null, false, null}`); `runLineVolatilityStop` (returns full `{series, period, multiplier, atr, bars, samples, stopFinal, positionFinal, longCount, shortCount, flipCount, ok}`); `computeLineVolatilityStopLayout` (one panel with the close line + segments per same-position run + markers per defined bar + flip-highlighted marker); `describeLineVolatilityStopChart` (ARIA summary mentioning the indicator, period, multiplier, trail-in-direction property, flip count). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"`, sr-only aria-desc span; close line + dots + markers `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-volatility-stop / -aria-desc / -svg / -empty / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -segments / -segment / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-close / -tooltip-stop / -tooltip-atr / -tooltip-position / -tooltip-flip / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config). Root mirrors `data-period`, `data-multiplier`, `data-stop-final`, `data-position-final`, `data-long-count`, `data-short-count`, `data-flip-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`, `data-empty`; segments expose `data-position`; markers expose `data-position`, `data-flip` and `data-stop`. Flip markers render larger with a contrasting stroke when `showFlips`. Tooltip on a marker shows x, close, stop, ATR, position, flip. Config badge `VSTOP <period>/<multiplier>`. Legend has Close + Volatility Stop toggle items with `long N / short N / flips N` stats. 74 vitest cases (getLineVolatilityStopFinitePoints 4 incl. **drops non-finite x/high/low/close**; normalizeLineVolatilityStopPeriod 4; normalizeLineVolatilityStopMultiplier 4; computeLineVolatilityStopTrueRange 4 incl. **first-bar `high - low`** + **gap-up max against prev close** + **gap-down max** + **flat first bar**; computeLineVolatilityStopATR 6 incl. **CONST -> ATR = 0 bit-exact** + **the worked initial seed ATR[2] = 2/3 for RISING period 3** + **Wilder convergence**; computeLineVolatilityStop 10 incl. non-array -> [] + length + **warm-up bars in 'none'** + **initialises to long at the first defined bar** + **a rising series stays long throughout (no flips)** + **the worked falling-series flip at bar 4 (the exact anchor)** + **a constant series with zero TR never flips** + **the worked initial long stop 20/3 on falling** + **trail-up monotone on long** + **trail-down monotone on short**; runLineVolatilityStop 13 incl. not-ok + empty + ok + defaults + custom + **rising counts (long 8, short 0, flips 0)** + **falling counts (long 2, short 6, flips 1) - the worked anchor** + **constant counts (long 8, short 0, flips 0)** + self-consistent + one sample per point + x-sorting + final stop + final position + **final short after falling flip**; computeLineVolatilityStopLayout 10 incl. not-ok single + collapsed canvas + ok + non-empty price path + one dot per bar + **markers = 8 for RISING period 3** + value domain + **no segment across a flip on falling fixture (6 segments not 7)** + markers inside panel + carries run; describeLineVolatilityStopChart 5 incl. name + period+multiplier + trails-in-direction + **mentions flips 1 times on falling** + No data; component 15 incl. labelled region + accessible description + empty + data-period/multiplier/position-final/flip-count + img role + price line + markers count + valid position per marker + **data-flip=true marker on falling fixture** + config badge `VSTOP 3/2` + legend toggle hides stop + showStop=false + onPointClick + ref) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for the new files (conditional spreads honour `exactOptionalPropertyTypes`, `noUncheckedIndexedAccess` guarded via explicit `null` push on missing ATR). Exported via components/ui barrel. Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/c4-mgr-auto. Worker auto-w708. Bump 1.11.744. |
| 11.727 | UI: chart-line-rocket-rsi primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.745 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-rocket-rsi.tsx` ships `<ChartLineRocketRsi>` -- a pure-SVG TWO-PANEL line chart with a **Rocket RSI** panel applying the Fisher Transform to a double-smoothed RSI to sharpen turning points (the price line on top, the rocket panel below with a horizontal zero line and zone-coloured markers). **The computation:** the Rocket RSI is a Fisher-stretched cousin of the standard RSI that uses double smoothing to cut noise. The pipeline runs: standard RSI of close-to-close changes over `rsiPeriod` (a flat window with `sumGain = sumLoss = 0` yields RSI = 50 by convention); SMA smoothing of length `smooth1`; SMA smoothing of length `smooth2` (the double smoothing); normalize to `[-1, 1]` as `x = RSI / 50 - 1`; clamp `x` to `[-clamp, clamp]` past the Fisher singularity; Fisher Transform `rocket = 0.5 * ln((1 + x_clamped) / (1 - x_clamped))`. `classifyLineRocketRsiZone(value)` marks a bar `up` for positive, `down` for negative, `flat` at zero, `none` for null. The Fisher Transform stretches values near the boundaries of the input range -- a price stuck at an extreme RSI keeps producing extreme rocket-RSI readings, so reversals stand out sharply. **Worked fixture:** four exact-style anchors survive the floating-point arithmetic. The FISHER(0) = 0 BIT-EXACT ANCHOR: `(1 + 0) / (1 - 0) = 1`, `ln(1) = 0`, `0.5 * 0 = 0` -- asserted with `toBe(0)`. The CONSTANT-SERIES ANCHOR: a constant series `[5 x 25]` has every bar-to-bar change equal to zero, so the standard RSI by convention returns 50; two SMAs of constant 50 stay at 50 bit-exact (the SMA-of-constant identity); normalize gives `x = 50/50 - 1 = 0`; Fisher(0) = 0. The Rocket RSI is exactly 0 at every defined bar of a constant series -- asserted with `toBe(0)` for every defined bar after the warm-up of `rsiPeriod + (smooth1 - 1) + (smooth2 - 1)`. The WORKED FISHER(0.5) ANCHOR: `(1 + 0.5) / (1 - 0.5) = 1.5 / 0.5 = 3`, `Fisher(0.5) = 0.5 * ln(3)` -- asserted with `toBeCloseTo(0.5 * Math.log(3), 12)`. The SMA-OF-`[1..5]` ANCHOR: `computeLineRocketRsiSmooth([1, 2, 3, 4, 5], 3)` is `[null, null, 2, 3, 4]` bit-exact (integer arithmetic). The RSI-SHAPE ANCHORS: constant -> 50 at every defined bar; strictly rising series -> RSI = 100 at every defined bar (all positive diffs, `sumLoss = 0`); strictly falling series -> RSI = 0 at every defined bar (all negative diffs, `sumGain = 0`); WAVE -> RSI in `[0, 100]` everywhere. Antisymmetry (Fisher(-x) = -Fisher(x), RISING vs FALLING) holds within ULP (the IEEE 754 `log` is correctly rounded per input but `log(1/y)` and `-log(y)` differ by an ULP). **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs `<ChartLineRsi>` -- the standard RSI is bounded in `[0, 100]` and changes hands at 30 / 70; the Rocket RSI is a Fisher-Transformed signed reading whose extreme values stretch under the transform, so turning points stand out SHARPER. vs `<ChartLineBayesian>` -- the Bayesian Trend yields three calibrated probabilities; the Rocket RSI is a single signed reading. The Bayesian Trend is probabilistic; the Rocket RSI is deterministic. vs `<ChartLineCmo>` -- the CMO is `100 * (sumUp - sumDown) / (sumUp + sumDown)`, a signed unsigned-share with no transform; the Rocket RSI is the Fisher-stretched cousin of the standard unsigned share. vs `<ChartLineMahalanobis>` -- the Mahalanobis Distance is an unsigned multivariate z-score; the Rocket RSI is a signed univariate transform. The defining trait: a Fisher-stretched signed reading that sharpens turning points by mapping the bounded RSI input through the logarithmic transform. API: `data` (`{x, value}[]`); `rsiPeriod` (default 8, integer >= 1); `smooth1` (default 5); `smooth2` (default 5); `clamp` (default 0.999, finite in `(0, 1)`); standard sizing/colour/a11y props plus `pricePanelRatio`/`gap`/`strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`tickCount`/toggle flags/controlled+uncontrolled `hiddenSeries` (`price`/`rocket`)/callbacks. Pure helpers: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_ROCKET_RSI_*`; `ChartLineRocketRsiZone` type (`up`|`down`|`flat`|`none`); `getLineRocketRsiFinitePoints`; `normalizeLineRocketRsiInteger(value, fallback, min = 1)`; `normalizeLineRocketRsiClamp(value, fallback)` (in (0, 1)); `computeLineRocketRsiRSI(values, period)` (standard RSI; flat window -> 50; warm-up null); `computeLineRocketRsiSmooth(values, window)` (SMA with null-propagation); `computeLineRocketRsiFisher(x, clamp?)` (Fisher Transform with clamping; `Fisher(0) === 0` bit-exact; non-finite -> 0); `classifyLineRocketRsiZone(value)`; `computeLineRocketRsi(values, options?)` (returns `{rsi, smoothed, rocketRsi}`); `runLineRocketRsi(points, options?)` (returns full `{series, rsiPeriod, smooth1, smooth2, clamp, rsi, rocketRsi, samples, rocketFinal, upCount, downCount, flatCount, ok}`); each sample `{index, x, value, rsi, rocketRsi, zone}`; `computeLineRocketRsiLayout({data, rsiPeriod, smooth1, smooth2, clamp, ...})` -- stacks the price panel above the rocket panel (the rocket panel uses a SYMMETRIC domain `[-bound, +bound]` where `bound = max(1, 1.1 * max |rocket|)` so the zero line stays centred), projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `rocketPath`, one `markers` entry per defined-rocket bar coloured by zone, and `zeroY` for the zero line; degenerate canvas / fewer than 2 points -> ok=false; `describeLineRocketRsiChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Rocket RSI + Fisher Transform + the double smoothing + the lookback periods + the zone counts. ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"`, sr-only aria-desc span; price line + dots + rocket line + markers `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-rocket-rsi / -aria-desc / -svg / -empty / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -tick-label / -panel-label / -zero-line / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -rocket-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-rsi / -tooltip-rocket / -tooltip-zone / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config). Root mirrors `data-rsi-period`, `data-smooth1`, `data-smooth2`, `data-clamp`, `data-rocket-final`, `data-up-count`, `data-down-count`, `data-flat-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`, `data-empty`; grid / axis / tick / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"rocket"`; markers expose `data-zone` and `data-rocket-rsi`. Tooltip shows x, price, RSI, rocket value, bias zone. Config badge `ROCKET <rsiPeriod>/<smooth1>/<smooth2>`. Legend has Price + Rocket RSI toggle items. 89 vitest cases (getLineRocketRsiFinitePoints 4; normalizeLineRocketRsiInteger 5; normalizeLineRocketRsiClamp 5; computeLineRocketRsiRSI 8 incl. **CONST -> 50** + **RISING -> 100** + **FALLING -> 0**; computeLineRocketRsiSmooth 6 incl. **SMA-of-constant bit-exact** + **the [1..5] window 3 exact**; computeLineRocketRsiFisher 7 incl. **Fisher(0) = 0 bit-exact** + **Fisher(0.5) = 0.5 * ln(3)** + antisymmetry within ULP + clamp behaviour; classifyLineRocketRsiZone 5; computeLineRocketRsi 8 incl. **CONST -> Rocket = 0 bit-exact** + RISING saturates positive + FALLING saturates negative + antisymmetry within ULP + clamp magnitude; runLineRocketRsi 12 incl. **CONST -> flatCount full + rocketFinal = 0**; computeLineRocketRsiLayout 10; describeLineRocketRsiChart 5; component 15 incl. config badge `ROCKET 4/3/3`) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for the new files (used `ReadonlyArray<T>` over `readonly Array<T>` for the parser, conditional spreads honour `exactOptionalPropertyTypes`, `noUncheckedIndexedAccess` guarded). Exported via components/ui barrel. Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/c4-mgr-auto. Worker auto-w709. Bump 1.11.745. |
| 11.728 | UI: chart-line-vsa primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.746 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-vsa.tsx` ships `<ChartLineVsa>` -- a pure-SVG TWO-PANEL line chart with a **Volume Spread Analysis** panel highlighting effort-versus-result divergences from bar range against volume (the bar midpoint on top, the VSA index on the bottom with a horizontal zero line and dashed +/-threshold reference lines). **The computation:** the VSA reads the relationship between EFFORT (volume) and RESULT (spread). Each bar carries `{x, high, low, volume}`. For each rolling window of `period` bars, `computeLineVsa(bars, period)` computes `spreadMean = mean(high - low)` and `volumeMean = mean(volume)`; then for the current bar `effort = volume / volumeMean`, `result = (high - low) / spreadMean`, and `vsa = effort - result`. A zero-mean spread or zero-mean volume window is degenerate and leaves the values null. `classifyLineVsaZone(vsa, threshold)` marks a bar `no-demand` for `vsa > threshold` (effort with no result), `ease-of-move` for `vsa < -threshold` (result on light effort), `agreement` inside the band (the at-threshold boundary is agreement -- strict inequality), `none` for null. **Worked fixture:** three bit-exact anchors. The EQUAL-ALL ANCHOR: spreads `[4 x 5]` + volumes `[4 x 5]` at period 5. `spreadMean = 4`, `volumeMean = 4` (both exact). At bar 4: `effort = 4/4 = 1`, `result = 4/4 = 1`, `vsa = 0`. Asserted with `toBe(0)`. The EASE-OF-MOVE ANCHOR: spreads `[2, 2, 2, 2, 12]` + volumes `[4, 4, 4, 4, 4]`. `spreadMean = 20/5 = 4` (exact), `volumeMean = 4`. At bar 4: `spread = 12`, `volume = 4`. `effort = 4/4 = 1`, `result = 12/4 = 3` (both exact in IEEE 754), `vsa = 1 - 3 = -2`. Asserted with `toBe(-2)`. Zone at threshold 0.5: `-2 < -0.5` -> `ease-of-move`. The NO-DEMAND ANCHOR: spreads `[4 x 5]` + volumes `[2, 2, 2, 2, 12]`. `spreadMean = 4`, `volumeMean = 4`. At bar 4: `effort = 12/4 = 3`, `result = 4/4 = 1`, `vsa = 2`. Asserted with `toBe(2)`. Zone: `2 > 0.5` -> `no-demand`. The DEGENERATE-WINDOW anchors: every bar `high == low` gives `spreadMean = 0` and every VSA null; every volume zero gives `volumeMean = 0` and the same. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs `<ChartLineVroc>` -- the VROC is the percent change of volume over a lookback; the VSA CROSS-CHECKS volume AGAINST the bar spread, surfacing the relationship between effort and result. vs `<ChartLineVfi>` -- the VFI is a signed signed-volume cumulative flow; the VSA is a per-bar RATIO comparing two normalised series. vs `<ChartLineUpDownVolumeRatio>` -- the up/down ratio measures the share of volume on up vs down bars; the VSA does not care about direction, only about whether the volume produces a spread response. vs `<ChartLineMahalanobis>` over `[volume, spread]` -- the Mahalanobis distance computes a covariance-aware multivariate z-score; the VSA is the cleaner ratio-based effort-minus-result divergence that classifies by sign. The defining trait: a per-bar normalized ratio that detects when effort and result diverge in either direction. API: `data` (`{x, high, low, volume}[]`); `period` (default 14, integer >= 2); `threshold` (default 0.5, finite > 0); standard sizing/colour/a11y props plus `pricePanelRatio`/`gap`/`strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/toggle flags (`showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots`/`showVsa`/`showZeroLine`/`showThresholdLines`/`showMarkers`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`)/controlled+uncontrolled `hiddenSeries` (`price`/`vsa`)/callbacks. Pure helpers: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_VSA_*`; `ChartLineVsaZone` type (`agreement`|`no-demand`|`ease-of-move`|`none`); `getLineVsaFinitePoints` (keeps finite x/high/low/volume + `high >= low` + `volume >= 0`); `normalizeLineVsaPeriod`; `normalizeLineVsaThreshold`; `computeLineVsa(bars, period)` (returns `{effort, result, vsa}` per bar; warm-up + degenerate windows null); `classifyLineVsaZone(vsa, threshold)` (at-threshold = agreement); `runLineVsa(points, options?)` (returns full `{series, period, threshold, vsa, samples, vsaFinal, agreementCount, noDemandCount, easeOfMoveCount, ok}`); each sample `{index, x, high, low, midpoint, volume, spread, effort, result, vsa, zone}`; `computeLineVsaLayout` (two panels, midpoint on top, VSA below in a symmetric domain, dashed zero line + threshold lines, marker per defined-VSA bar coloured by zone); `describeLineVsaChart` (ARIA summary mentioning effort + result + no-demand + ease-of-move + counts). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"`, sr-only aria-desc span; midpoint line + dots + VSA line + markers `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-vsa / -aria-desc / -svg / -empty / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -panel-label / -zero-line / -threshold-lines / -threshold-line / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -vsa-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-spread / -tooltip-volume / -tooltip-effort / -tooltip-result / -tooltip-vsa / -tooltip-zone / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config). Root mirrors `data-period`, `data-threshold`, `data-vsa-final`, `data-agreement-count`, `data-no-demand-count`, `data-ease-of-move-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`, `data-empty`; grid / axis / tick / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"vsa"`; threshold lines carry `data-direction="upper"|"lower"`; markers expose `data-zone` and `data-vsa`. Tooltip shows x, spread, volume, effort, result, VSA, zone. Config badge `VSA <period>/<threshold>`. Legend has Midpoint + VSA toggle items with `agreement N / no-demand N / ease-of-move N` stats. 74 vitest cases (getLineVsaFinitePoints 4 incl. **drops non-finite + high < low + negative volume**; normalizeLineVsaPeriod 5; normalizeLineVsaThreshold 4; computeLineVsa 10 incl. **equal-all anchor VSA = 0 bit-exact** + **ease-of-move anchor VSA = -2 bit-exact** + **no-demand anchor VSA = 2 bit-exact** + **zero mean spread -> null** + **zero mean volume -> null** + effort + result non-negative; classifyLineVsaZone 6 incl. **at-threshold -> agreement (strict inequality)**; runLineVsa 14 incl. **equal-all -> agreement** + **ease-of-move zone on the anchor** + **no-demand zone on the anchor** + midpoint formula; computeLineVsaLayout 11 incl. **threshold lines stacked correctly**; describeLineVsaChart 5; component 17 incl. **zero + 2 threshold lines by default** + **no-demand marker on anchor** + **ease-of-move marker on anchor** + config badge `VSA 5/0.5` + `showThresholdLines=false` hides both lines) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for the new files (conditional spreads honour `exactOptionalPropertyTypes`, `noUncheckedIndexedAccess` guarded via `!` on internal-window indices and `?? null` for sample arrays). Exported via components/ui barrel. Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/c4-mgr-auto. Worker auto-w710. Bump 1.11.746. |
| 11.729 | UI: chart-line-confluence primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.747 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-confluence.tsx` ships `<ChartLineConfluence>` -- a pure-SVG TWO-PANEL line chart with a **Confluence** panel summing aligned signals from multiple lookback oscillators into a single integer score (the price on top, the score below with a horizontal zero line and dashed +/-threshold reference lines). **The computation:** for each lookback `L_k` in the lookback set, the per-bar signal is the SIGN of the bar's value minus the value `L_k` bars ago: `signal_k(i) = +1 if value[i] > value[i-L_k]`, `-1 if <`, `0 if =`, `null if i < L_k or either operand is non-finite`. The score sums the signals across the lookbacks into `[-N, +N]` where N is the lookback count. The score is null until EVERY lookback has filled (max lookback). `classifyLineConfluenceZone(score, threshold)` marks a bar `bullish` if `score > threshold`, `bearish` if `score < -threshold`, `neutral` inside the band, `none` if null. **Worked fixture:** pure integer arithmetic, no transcendentals -- every anchor is bit-exact for integer fixtures. The RISING-SERIES ANCHOR: `[10..34]` with lookbacks `[3, 5, 10]`. At every bar `i >= 10`, `value[i] > value[i-L_k]` for every `L_k`, so every signal is `+1` and the score is `3`. Asserted with `toBe(3)` for every defined bar. The FALLING-SERIES ANCHOR: `[30..6]`, mirror -> score = -3 every defined bar. The CONSTANT-SERIES ANCHOR: `[5 x 25]`, every diff zero -> every signal 0 -> score = 0 every defined bar. The FIVE-LOOKBACK ANCHOR: lookbacks `[2, 3, 5, 8, 13]` on RISING -> score = 5 every defined bar. The IS-INTEGER-IN-RANGE INVARIANT: on a wave fixture every defined score is `Number.isInteger` AND inside `[-N, +N]`. The warm-up rule (score null until max(lookbacks) bars have passed) and the non-finite propagation rule (a non-finite bar nulls itself and any future bar that looks back to it) are tested directly. **Distinct from neighbouring primitives:** vs single-lookback momentum primitives (`<ChartLineRoc>`, `<ChartLineCmo>`, `<ChartLineRsi>`) -- those each measure ONE lookback at a time; the Confluence aggregates the SIGNS from MULTIPLE lookbacks into a single score so a unanimous signal reads as a clear `+/-N` and a mixed signal lands inside the band. vs `<ChartLineBayesian>` -- the Bayesian Trend produces three calibrated probabilities under a parametric model; the Confluence is a DETERMINISTIC vote over deterministic lookback signs. vs `<ChartLineMonteCarlo>` -- the Monte Carlo Fan is a forward-looking probabilistic forecast; the Confluence is a backward-looking deterministic alignment metric. The defining trait: a multi-timeframe alignment vote that turns N lookback momentum signals into a single integer score with a built-in tunable threshold for majority confluence. API: `data` (`{x, value}[]`); `lookbacks` (default `[5, 10, 20]`, sorted positive integers; sub-1 or non-finite entries dropped; empty falls back to default); `threshold` (default `floor(N / 2)`, non-negative finite); standard sizing/colour/a11y props plus `pricePanelRatio`/`gap`/`strokeWidth`/`dotRadius`/`tickCount`/toggle flags (`showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots`/`showScore`/`showZeroLine`/`showThresholdLines`/`showMarkers`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showLegend`/`animate`)/controlled+uncontrolled `hiddenSeries` (`price`/`score`)/callbacks. Pure helpers: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_CONFLUENCE_*`; `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_CONFLUENCE_LOOKBACKS = [5, 10, 20]`; `ChartLineConfluenceZone` type (`bullish`|`bearish`|`neutral`|`none`); `getLineConfluenceFinitePoints`; `normalizeLineConfluenceLookbacks(lookbacks, fallback)` (sort, floor, drop sub-1; fallback for empty/non-array); `normalizeLineConfluenceThreshold(threshold, lookbackCount)` (non-negative finite; defaults to `floor(N / 2)`); `computeLineConfluenceSignal(values, lookback)` (per-bar `{-1, 0, +1, null}`); `computeLineConfluence(values, lookbacks)` (returns `{signals: Array<Array<-1|0|1|null>>, score: Array<number|null>}`; score null until every lookback has filled); `classifyLineConfluenceZone(score, threshold)`; `runLineConfluence(points, options?)` (returns full `{series, lookbacks, threshold, signals, score, samples, scoreFinal, bullishCount, bearishCount, neutralCount, ok}`); each sample `{index, x, value, signals, score, zone}`; `computeLineConfluenceLayout({data, lookbacks, threshold, ...})` -- stacks the price panel above the score panel (the score panel uses a symmetric domain `[-bound, +bound]` where `bound = max(N, threshold) + 1`), projects `pricePath`, `priceDots`, `scorePath`, one `markers` entry per defined-score bar coloured by zone, plus `zeroY`, `upperThresholdY`, `lowerThresholdY`; `describeLineConfluenceChart` (ARIA summary mentioning the lookbacks, the threshold, the score range, the zone counts, the final score). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"`, sr-only aria-desc span; price line + dots + score line + markers `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. data-section on every node (chart-line-confluence / -aria-desc / -svg / -empty / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -panel-label / -zero-line / -threshold-lines / -threshold-line / -price-path / -dots / -dot / -score-line / -markers / -marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-value / -tooltip-score / -tooltip-signals / -tooltip-zone / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-config). Root mirrors `data-lookbacks` (comma-joined), `data-threshold`, `data-score-final`, `data-bullish-count`, `data-bearish-count`, `data-neutral-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-animate`, `data-empty`; grid / axis / panel-label nodes carry `data-panel="price"|"score"`; threshold lines carry `data-direction="upper"|"lower"`; markers expose `data-zone` and `data-score`. Tooltip shows x, price, score, per-lookback signals, zone. Config badge `CONF <lookbacks joined by />/ t<threshold>`. Legend has Price + Confluence toggle items with `bullish N / bearish N / neutral N` stats. 80 vitest cases (getLineConfluenceFinitePoints 4; normalizeLineConfluenceLookbacks 6 incl. **sort + floor + drop sub-1 + fallback non-array + fallback empty**; normalizeLineConfluenceThreshold 4; computeLineConfluenceSignal 8 incl. **RISING -> +1 bit-exact** + **FALLING -> -1 bit-exact** + **CONST -> 0 bit-exact** + **non-finite propagation to looking-back bars**; computeLineConfluence 8 incl. **RISING -> score = N bit-exact** + **FALLING -> score = -N bit-exact** + **CONST -> score = 0 bit-exact** + integer in `[-N, +N]` on wave + **five-lookback -> score = 5 on RISING**; classifyLineConfluenceZone 6 incl. **at-threshold = neutral (strict inequality)** + **zero-threshold collapses neutral to exact zero**; runLineConfluence 14 incl. **RISING -> fully bullish** + **FALLING -> fully bearish** + **CONST -> fully neutral**; computeLineConfluenceLayout 11 incl. threshold lines stacked correctly; describeLineConfluenceChart 5; component 14 incl. **all markers bullish on RISING fixture** + **all markers bearish on FALLING fixture** + **all markers neutral on CONST fixture** + config badge `CONF 3/5/10 t1` + `showThresholdLines=false`) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for the new files (conditional spreads honour `exactOptionalPropertyTypes`, `noUncheckedIndexedAccess` guarded via `!` on internal-signal indices, OPTS test fixture uses an explicit mutable `number[]` type rather than `as const`).
